I feel bad for the elderly people who drive here every day to do their routine exercises having to pay $10 every day or pay $80 annually. Some can't afford that and some can. I suppose I'll start walking here back at home, it's nice too.  I would  have gave the park 5 stars but the parking fee is now the issue. Simply because I would only be there for 1.5 hrs.  Waa waa wa! I know im complaining.. lol.

We had our Annual Family Picnic here and we had an amazing time. The theme was MICHAEL Jackson,  we all showed up in our M.J. gear , tshirts or hairstyle.  What a blast. The park was very safe, clean and the scenery was absolutely beautiful

name: Brand Park
formatted_address: 1601 W Mountain St, Glendale, CA 91201, USA

google_reviews.text: Went Hiking on the brand lateral trail, it was a good workout. Very steep, I slipped, but managed to get through it. Incredible views with alternative ways to get to the top
Beautiful park with steep hiking trails.  I enjoy spending a few hours exploring this park with all its rich history
Beautiful roses, GREAT LIBRARY( art/performing arts) all around nice place to visit. Japanese tea house not available when I visited, but looking forward.

I came here at night and whoa it was still beautiful. As you enter you are greeted by a line of palm trees. Keep driving in for way kore parking lots. There are restroom that decent wise for park vibes but at least there are restrooms and running water. There are at least two restrooms throughout the park that I know of. It’s definitely not the Four Seasons so keep that in mindZ Free and plenty of parking. I shall have to come back during the day and check out the Japanese tea house, Doctor House, and the gazebo! And also of course the stunning views I have heard about. It’s safe to say it may take more than a day and the right time to explore this large park.

I had such a lovely time here with my wife, we just spread out a blanket, had a little picnic, and soaked up the sunshine. The park is bigger than I expected, with plenty of shady spots, open lawns, and easy trails if you feel like wandering a bit. It felt so peaceful and relaxed—one of those afternoons where time just slows down.

My favorite part of the park has to be the Japanese Friendship Garden and Shoseian Tea House. Walking through that little oasis with the koi pond, stone paths, and carefully tended greenery was so calming. We spent a few minutes just wandering and enjoying the quiet—it’s amazing that such a serene spot exists right in the middle of the park.

Beyond that, the playgrounds, fields, and historic buildings like the Brand Library & Art Center make it feel alive without ever being too crowded. I left feeling happy and calm after a hard day at work.

name: Marvin Braude Mulholland Gateway Park
formatted_address: 3600 Reseda Blvd, Tarzana, CA 91356, USA

google_reviews.text: Enjoyed a great hike across the Santa Monica Mountains!
Did the turkey trot half marathon trail run. Superb views of the ocean and city once you hit 5 miles from the trailhead.

Great and vast hiking and mountain cycling area - only true althletes! Plenty of trails and free parking (below yellow line!) The fires had scorched a good part of these Santa Monica mountains this January. Luckily, parts of them have started to re-grow:-)

Not crazy about this trail entry due to its relative accessibility and popularity (top of Reseda, with plenty of parking, so lots of hikers and bikers).  However, it does provide good access to a number of different single track trails that branch off of dirt Mulholland.  Posting pics here from some of those local trails, which are all easily reachable from this spot.

Dirt Mulholland on either side of the top of Reseda is closed due to the January fires. You can go off towards the left and head down towards the top of Caballero Canyon, but you can't go any further than that because the road is closed

name: Chantry Flat Parking Lot and Trailhead
formatted_address: Chantry Flat Rd, Arcadia, CA 91006, USA

google_reviews.text: Great Hike, Close by for SGV residents. Waterfall finally dumping incredible amounts of water unlike when it was just a trickle during the droughts !

Arrive early to get a parking spot and make sure that you have a paid park permit visible in your car window other than that, the hike is.
Fun, there are many directions to Go ,one will take you to a waterfalll. Another trail will take you to a pool where people can jump in,
Can be sketchy though. Another with take you to Mount Wilson, and the other will loop back above the parking lot.
There's a little cafe that serves food and drinks soda, water. A nice place to spend the day. Bring your own food and have a picnic. There is camping too.

Great place to take the kids. 10 yr and 12 year old. All dogs must be on leases. I suggest taking snacks and water. Backpacks recommended.
Shoes with grip, like hiking shoes or tennis shoes.
no flat bottom shoes. It's very cold this season, should warm up by April.

In desperate need of parking expansion, and the hike to Sturtevant felt a little like we were in someone’s backyard. Unfortunately, being so close to LA also means that there was a handful of influencers running amok and taking pictures in the middle of the trail.

A beautiful, relaxing hike with a little less privacy and peace as I had hoped.

Reopened after a 4 year closure, it’s an amazing hiking place with lots of trails to choose from. The most popular is Sturtevant Falls, I made a video for my YouTube channel lopezexplora that shows you step by step directions to it.
I’ll leave you some clips for you to enjoy!!

name: Hillcrest Park
formatted_address: 1200 N Harbor Blvd, Fullerton, CA 92832, USA

google_reviews.text:
What a nice park. N ceremony today. Thank u for ur services.
Buena Park H.S. ROTC Students
My handsome nephew n best friend
Loved it. Get in, do your workout and get out!!! Very nice views and it's nice and clean. Highly recommend.

This is a great park if you like walking around getting exercise that'll do it for you just wanna ride a bike and enjoy the scenery this will also be the place for you we enjoyed it so much laying under a tree on a warm summer day was so nice we planned on coming back here there's 3 pin news that you can rent for parties we definitely want to come back and rent when these venues we find a very appealing for our wedding Or retirement parties or any party for that matter great park must come see

For buns of steel walk the stairs. If you are looking for a beautiful place to walk or take photos this park has it all. Often there are promposals by the fountain. Take a Zumba class on the bridge by Harbor. Enjoy a picnic and a hike. The playground is also nice and well taken care of.
Great place to get some cardio,  not too crowded, and you can go anytime you want.

name: Westridge Trailhead
formatted_address: W Mandeville Fire Rd, Los Angeles, CA 90049, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a hike that has some nice viewd that are easily accessible form the city. The trail has two options, the fire road or the ridge hogbacks. This area has no shade because of the Palisades Fire burn, so it shoudl be avoided on a hot day. Parking is also limited ao arrive early.

The trail is open and gorgeous. I was mountain biking to Nike and then to Mulholland. Ran into several hikers and bikers. All dogs are without leash and few wondering off 20-30 yards away from the owners. Be aware, lots of coyotes and mountain lions.

The perfect spot to kick off a hike, trail run, or mountain bike ride in the Santa Monica Mountains. Westridge starts out as a large fire road, but there are smaller side trails along the road you can take as well. The views are absolutely fantastic, especially at sunset! There's a small lot and easy street parking. No bathroom though.

Come here during the day or in the evening for a beautiful  hike.
The drive is not too long and you are in a few minutes surrounded by the santa monica hills, the city out of sight. The hike has two trails, one that goes around the hills and one that goes up and down. That gives you the option of difficulty you want to have that day.
Probably my favorite hike in LA!

One of my favorite trails in LA. when I do hike here, I usually come in the evening and there is parking, but if there isn’t, then you could park in the neighborhood and walk your way up. it is such a nice view once you walk up to the peak! There are a good amount of mountain bikers, so be aware. also, my friend got pooped on by a bat when I first came here so keep an eye out for them.

name: Dante's View
formatted_address: Griffith Park Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90027, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a nice maintained garden along many of my hikes at Griffith Park. Although I've never rested here, it seems like a nice place to rest and enjoy. Also there are some nice views a few feet away on the Hogback trail.

I've been hiking this particular place for a while now, still trying to discover more trails *hidden ones or new ones* for first timers I would recommend going in the mornings to familiarize yourself with the trails warm days you'll see some rattle snakes *not always* but be wary.

Scenery always amazes me. Sunset, sunrise, they are both very enjoyable.

Dante’s View offers a breathtakingly beautiful landscaped garden in the sky. With its well thought designed layout and stunning panoramic view, this haven provides a serene and awe-inspiring escape, allowing hikers to immerse themselves in the beauty of the surrounding gorgeous nature. A must-visit destination for nature enthusiasts.  It looked like I was somewhere tropical, simply breathtaking.

I’ve been hiking for 6 weeks now and had to work up to this one, it was a bit steep in some areas, but taking it slow worked just fine.  It’s worth it!!

This trail system is very versatile. From the Greek Theater parking lot to Dante's view is about 3.5 miles (5.6km). You can certainly walk longer distances and modify based on your ability and time.
-Arrive early if you want to find parking ( 7am) and if you want to avoid extreme heat. Public restrooms are available close to the park next to the Greek Theater.
An easy drive.  Best seen at sunset   A great view as drop to valley is over 5.000 ft.

name: Glendale Peak Trail Head
formatted_address: 2715 N Vermont Canyon Rd, Los Angeles, CA 90027, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a great trail not far from DTLA. its got a great view from the top and not difficult at all. Amazing views of sunrise and sunset as well as the DTLA skyline. Only downside is that if you go at popular times (e.g. weekends and holidays) it is PACKED. Also used as a training ground for cross country runners and running clubs because of the size/width of the trails. Its a nice 4 mile loop takes about 2 hours if you go slow and take pics.
An easy trail with good scenic views along the way. Took us 2.5 hr to complete the loop, and get to see the Hollywood sign, Griffith Observatory, and DTLA
I love the views from Glendale Peak. It's a pretty easy hike from the west side of Griffith Park.

Nice View...
Great Place!!!
Good Trail To Hike...
Nice Place To Spend With Your Love Ones!!!
Keep A Look For The Snakes...

Found parking fairly quick on the street. Used the facilities next to main parking lot. Hiked thru a fire trail and then a somewhat steep uphill on a narrow trail a
& got to see some nice views. There are many trail options.

name: Griffith Park Observatory Trails Peak
formatted_address: 2715 North Vermont Avenue, Los Angeles, CA 90027, USA

google_reviews.text:
If you’re visiting the observatory, take the time to do this short trail as well.
It’s less than an hour round trip, but absolutely worth it.

You’ll get breathtaking views of the Griffith Observatory with the Los Angeles skyline in the background.
It was an absolutely amazing experience to watch the sunrise there. One of the most beautiful views I have ever seen in my life.

Great Hike to Mt. Hollywood!

We parked at the Greek Theatre and took the Mt. Hollywood Trail for a rewarding hike. The ascent was gradual, making it a pleasant and manageable climb. The trail is well-maintained, and the views at the top are fantastic—offering a great perspective of downtown LA and Griffith Observatory. There are multiple routes to reach this point, but the Mt. Hollywood Trail was a great choice. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a scenic and enjoyable hike!

Definitely take time to see the scenic view of Los Angeles from this place. It's free. Parking is free. When the Observatory is open at night, it is absolutely amazing and well-organized.  Even though the line is long, it doesn't take that long...plus the view is great!

Visited Griffith Observatory during a day trip to Los Angeles in late October 2024. Situated in Griffith Park, it has direct access to hiking trails. Perfect spot to enjoy sunrise and sunset, views of LA and the iconic Hollywood sign while learning a bit about science and astronomy. Definitely a must visit when in Los Angeles.

name: Playa Del Rey Beach
formatted_address: Playa Del Rey Beach, 7313-7351 S Marine Ave, Playa Del Rey, CA 90293, USA

google_reviews.text: Great open beach. A few shells.  Cement bike/ walking path. Lots of room. Volleyball  🏐  courts. Bathrooms. Huge sand dune to slide down.... Plenty of  free off street parking.

It may not be the biggest or fanciest of the LA Beaches but Playa Del Rey has a lot of charm and they’ve made great strides in cleaning this beach in recent years. It’s an excellent spot for bird watching!

No bun fires on the sand should be enforced. It was a good reason to have that rule up to a few years ago. Not fun to walk into firewood piles at sunset, then next day into charred wood pieces on the sand. There is Dockweiler beach for lighting fires. Playa del Rey should remain (one of the last) pristine and peaceful beaches in LA county.

I went on a Tuesday during sunset it was beautiful and peaceful hardly anyone there and the water was so warm great to swim I’ve never felt that in my life it was amazing because all the LA beaches I’ve seen to are freezing! Please keep this an uncrowded clean beach!
Lovely ! Bike trail clean and the perfect time to be there the weather was magnificent!

name: Redondo Beach Pier
formatted_address: Fishermans Wharf, Redondo Beach, CA 90277, USA

google_reviews.text: We visited the Redondo Pier for the Kite Festival. Ample parking options around the area. Fun and unique food and dessert offerings. Well thought out design offers a full visual and engaging experience. You feel like you're on a pirate ship (the good kind). Not to mention the clearest water I've ever seen for a SoCal harbor. Can't wait to go back.

Cool pier. Felt very local with people fishing and hanging out. There's a few restaurants. Many that will display their seafood. We saw some seals swimming a few hundred feet away and the water below was so clear.

Came to the annual 52 Kite flying event with my group. It was hot, but next time we will be prepared. We thought it was a small group, we didn't think the kite flying was an annual event. But it was actually a beach event. Lots of people. We got there early, 11:45 am, so we found parking. Left at 1:45 pm, the heat drained us and our car's ac replenished us.when we were leaving there were a lot of cars trying to find parking.

May not have all of the fanfare Santa Monica pier has. But it's a much more relaxed feeling. Seems more peaceful. Plenty of places to eat. If you looking for attractions, definitely not for you. But if you want an enjoyable walk or a night out, this is the place.

I have been here many times during the day and for sunset but never in the night
As a first experience, it was so nice and peaceful. I truly do love redondo beach. It is a great place to sit and think. You can come with friends, family, anyone. They have a great environment for everyone.

name: RAT Beach
formatted_address: RAT Beach, Redondo Beach, CA 90277, USA

google_reviews.text: As a photographer, I find Rat Beach to be a great spot for portraits. The environment is calm and scenic, and the light in the afternoon and at sunset is especially beautiful for photos.

Took a harder route down the cliff, you could use the walkway if ya want. The rocks are slippery but walking to your right will get you to RAT Beach. Don’t place your blankets on the damp sand there are bugs. Mostly a surfers beach it seems. Very nice view & waves were gentle.

One of the most beautiful beaches in LA, but it lacks a restroom. You can get to the beach from the parking lot above or by walking from the Torrance ramp. The beach is not groomed often, so it has a beautiful, wild feel. The Santa Monica bay at its best.

I haven’t been here in years, and it was great to walk along the beach for an early spring stroll on Saturday morning.  It wasn’t too crowded, and I was able to enjoy the ocean waves and watch some of the local wildlife.  Saw some dolphins in the water, a great white heron, and some little birds along the shore.

Just wanted to clarify for anyone wishing to go to RAT Beach that this is a duplicate location and is incorrectly placed on Google Maps! Navigation to this duplicate location will direct you to a gated, residential parking lot. The stair pathway to the beach from here is NOT for public access and quite steep. Public access to RAT Beach is actually from the Malaga Cove Trail, which starts at the parking lot at Roessler Point along Paseo del Mar/Via Arroyo (access from Via Corta off of Palos Verdes Drive North). Alternatively, you can park at Torrance Beach and walk on the sand to RAT Beach from there. Google Maps won't allow me to suggest an edit to remove this duplicate, so I'm leaving this review and photo to warn everyone in advance.

name: Sunset Beach
formatted_address: Sunset Beach, Pacific Coast Hwy, Pacific Palisades, CA 90272, USA

google_reviews.text: Nothing quite like parking along PCH on this area and just taking in the sunset

Sometimes in life, you just need to get out and forget about the things of everyday life. We all need to relax and enjoy time alone or together with someone you love. It helps to reflect on things you did or want to do. Forgive yourself for mistakes and learn from them. I admit I have made a lot of mistakes. I m not perfect. I continue to learn.
I have a lot of regrets. But I do not let those regrets trouble me. I admit to my mistakes and move on. Will I keep making mistakes? Of course, maybe. I do not know everything. I try to do my best to make things right.
If anyone feels the same, I hope you forgive yourself and make time for yourself. Love yourself even if someone else doesn't.

One of my favorite spots to watch sunset!
I love this hidden beach!

Chill spot. We parked on side of road. I sat on top of a rock because I'm injured but my sister climbed down and picked up trash. I recorded slow mo and hyperlasped video that both came out perfect. It was an awesome way to watch light fade. This day we were in May Grey...but I bet late in Summer the sunset is spectacular.
First time here. Another magical place in Southern California. Enjoying the sunset by all means. I love my love and my life. A lot of peace, beauty, and depth. Thank You!

name: Dockweiler Beach
formatted_address: 12000 Vista Del Mar, Playa Del Rey, CA 90293, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a community beach. It doesn't have  the fancy pier or touristy stores and/or restaurants. But, it does have what you really don't see on many beaches. It has an alcove setting with the back drop of mountains with an outlet for the Marina Del Ray harbor. There is limited free parking, bike and scooter rentals, and public restrooms. It offers a walking/riding trail that offers picturesque views to enhance your journey. You can enjoy viewing airplanes as they rise from their LAX departure  You might even see Billy Bob my wiser favorite crane. Be alert, that rock might turn out to be an eyeball! Either way if you are lucky enough to find a space to park..enjoy!
Sand is almost as white as what I’m used to in Florida and the waves are spectacular.  And you can see mountains and the ocean here simultaneously.  Definitely can’t do that in Florida

#Dockweiler Beach: LA's Unexpectedly Charming Fire Starter

Well, bless your heart! I might be a Southern girl, but I’ve got to admit, there’s a genuine, unexpected **charm** to **Dockweiler Beach** once you get past all the noise.

It's certainly not your typical quiet, luxury escape—we can all agree on that. But honestly, that’s what makes it great. It's the only place in LA County where you can have a full-blown **beach bonfire**, which means it’s the unofficial host of **everyone's best memories**.

The **fire pits** are first-come, first-served, and yes, that means you need to be **dedicated** to claim your spot, but that just creates a little **sense of community** out there on the sand. You see families and friends putting in the *work* to get their evening started, and I respect that hustle.

And as for those big jets from **LAX** soaring right over the water? Rather than just being loud, it's actually an **incredible spectacle**. It forces you to look up and realize you're having this pure, natural moment on the coast while the whole modern world is flying right over your head. It’s a pretty profound little juxtaposition, honestly.

Whether you're there for the dramatic sunset, the crackling **fire**, or just the simple joy of roasting a marshmallow with the roar of an engine as your background music, Dockweiler truly delivers a **unique, festive, and memorable** experience. It’s a little rough around the edges, sure, but that just makes it **real**.

---
**What's your favorite thing to cook over a beach fire, darlin'?**

Beautiful and clean beach with stunning ocean views and plenty of space to relax. The best part is watching planes take off right above the beach — such a unique experience! Perfect spot for sunsets and spending time with family or friends.

We came to do a bonfire for my sister's birthday the parking was cheap only 9 dollars and the vibe was great. The beach is clean and has plenty of fire pits and trash cans to maintain it clean. We were able to BBQ grill and enjoy quality family time. They were fully staffed with lifeguards, EMT truck and police so it definitely felt safe to be there with all emergency services ready for any emergency

name: Torrance County Beach

google_reviews.text:
Torrance County Beach is a hidden gem that offers a serene escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life. The beach features beautiful, soft sand and stunning views of the ocean, making it a perfect spot for relaxation or a leisurely stroll. The atmosphere is peaceful, with fewer crowds compared to more popular beaches, allowing visitors to enjoy the natural beauty in a tranquil setting.

The amenities are well-maintained, including clean restrooms and ample parking, which adds to the overall convenience. Whether you're looking to sunbathe, swim, or simply take in the breathtaking scenery, Torrance County Beach has something for everyone. The surrounding cliffs provide a picturesque backdrop, and the sunsets here are nothing short of spectacular.

Wildlife enthusiasts will appreciate the chance to spot various birds and marine life, adding to the beach's charm. Additionally, there are nearby picnic areas that are perfect for family gatherings or a romantic outing.

Overall, Torrance County Beach is an ideal destination for those seeking a quiet day by the sea. I highly recommend this beach to anyone looking to unwind and enjoy nature's beauty. I can't wait to return for another day of relaxation and fun!

I used to ride the long bike path that goes along these beaches about 3 times a week, locking my bike here and hiking up thru Malaga Cove, up to the Ranch Market for lunch and a health shake. Sometimes a quicker stop at the nearby Trader Joe’s in Redondo Beach, worked out just fine too!

Parking free on the streets may be acceptable, if posted signs don’t show restrictions. Beach street parking along the Esplanade to the north is metered and checked frequently.
There are also paid lots to the north at the Redondo Beach Parking and south  at Torrance State Beach pay and display lots.

name: Santa Monica State Beach
formatted_address: Santa Monica State Beach, California, USA

google_reviews.text:
I had a pretty funny experience visiting Santa Monica State Beach. The first time I ever saw this place wasn’t in real life — it was actually in **GTA V**. So when I finally came here in person, everything felt strangely familiar. The pier, the beach path, the wide stretch of sand… it honestly felt like I had already been here before.

For a moment I almost thought **GTA V was created before the beach existed** 😂 (obviously joking). But it really says a lot about how detailed the game is, because walking around the area feels incredibly similar to what you see in the game. If you’ve spent a lot of time in GTA V, visiting here is kind of surreal — like stepping into a real-life version of the map.

The beach itself is beautiful. It’s very wide, the sand is clean, and there’s plenty of space even when it’s busy. You get great ocean views, and the whole area around the pier has a lively atmosphere. There are lots of people biking, walking, jogging, and just hanging out. It’s a great place to walk along the beach path or sit and enjoy the sunset. The nearby promenade also has plenty of shops and food options if you want to grab something to eat.

One thing to plan for is **parking**. There are several parking lots nearby, which is convenient, but they can get quite full on busy days. Also, be prepared for the **exit traffic** if you park in the main beach lots. When the beach is crowded, getting out of the parking lot can take a while. When I visited, the line of cars waiting to exit was very long and it took **around 30 minutes or more just to get out**. It’s not a huge problem if you expect it, but definitely something to keep in mind when planning your visit.

Overall, it’s a great beach with an iconic view of the Santa Monica Pier and a very fun atmosphere. And if you’ve played GTA V before coming here, you might have the same strange feeling I did — like you’ve already explored the place long before you actually arrived.
This beach can be a bit busy but you can find a quiet spot to relax. It's nice if you want to go to the pier. There is a bike/walking path nearby. Parking can be difficult.

Beautiful ! Plenty of parking and. not free I paid $15 so....plan your trip Wisely. The beach is really nice and it's a little bit of a hike from the parking to waters edge. Come prepared to walk a bit. Also, choose your bike rental wisely too. I paid $95+ tax for a bike for a good 4hrs. Come to find out there were cheaper options.

At the end of 3rd Street Promenade, we headed towards Santa Monica State Beach and strolled along the beach front on the “top”. There are “2 levels” here which we never noticed before. The “top level” is the promenade walk which has views over Santa Monica. This level is about 4 storeys higher than the beach level which is great if a tsunami does take place – especially since there was a tsunami warning recently after a 8.8 richter scale earthquake struck off Russia. The “lower level” is the waterfront at the street/beach level. The promenade and the waterfront levels are separated by the Pacific Coast Highway...

🌊 Santa Monica Beach – Sun, Surf and California Vibes 🌊

Santa Monica Beach is one of the most iconic spots in California. From the soft sand to the sparkling ocean, everything feels like the perfect beach day. Walking along the shore, watching surfers ride the waves, and feeling the warm sun on your skin is pure relaxation.

Tip: Visit early in the morning or late afternoon for fewer crowds and amazing lighting for photos. The beach is ideal for jogging, biking, or simply relaxing under the palm trees. Nearby, the Santa Monica Pier adds extra fun with its Ferris wheel, games, and restaurants.

Verdict: Santa Monica Beach is more than just a beach – it’s a full California experience. For me, a perfect day filled with sun, sea, and energy, easily earning five stars.

Santa Monica Beach is one of the most stunning and enjoyable beaches in California, offering the perfect mix of relaxation, natural beauty, and activity. The beach stretches for miles with soft golden sand and clean waters, making it a great spot for swimming, sunbathing, or just enjoying a peaceful walk along the shoreline. The wide space allows everyone to find a comfortable spot, even during weekends when it gets busy.

One of the highlights is the bike and walking path that runs along the coast, connecting Santa Monica to Venice Beach. Renting a bike or scooter and cruising along the oceanfront is a must-do experience. The Santa Monica Pier is another iconic attraction right on the beach, with its Ferris wheel, arcade games, and lively atmosphere that’s fun for both families and solo travelers.

The beach is well-maintained, with lifeguards on duty, clean restrooms, and easy access points. The vibe is very welcoming and safe, with people from all over the world enjoying the sunshine together. Watching the sunset here is simply magical — the sky turns into beautiful shades of orange and pink, creating a postcard-perfect moment.

Parking can be a little pricey or crowded during peak times, but it’s worth it for the experience. Overall, Santa Monica Beach is a must-visit destination that captures the true spirit of California’s coastline — vibrant, relaxing, and unforgettable.

name: Will Rogers State Beach
formatted_address: Will Rogers State Beach, Los Angeles, CA 90272, USA

google_reviews.text:
Great roadside beach park accessible off the southwest end of Temescal Canyon Road and California State Highway #1.
This long, thin strip of a beach park is nicely tucked in below the Palisades Hills. Unlike other popular stretches of beach, residential ocean view lookie-loos have less chance of intrusion with public paying visitors.
Beach crowds are seemingly dependent on the seasons. Warmer weather will have lifeguards present at the stations watching over swimmers and surfers. Off-season crowds are less often in the colder water, without lifeguards present and more likely here for sand based activities, including beach volleyball. Paved pathways and large parking lots provide space for bicycling, roller skating or skateboarding.
There’s public restrooms available, although in the off-season, some may be locked. A restroom and shower facility is located at the south end of the park next to the famous Baywatch Headquaters.
I’d suggest asking the parking attendant when entering the park for availability.
The downside of the upside, is the togetherness of the parking area with the busy Pacific Coast Highway, aka California State Highway #1. While there’s a continuous separator preventing incursions, the almost constant traffic noise and nosiness of commuters may be too much for some visitors to pay for.

Nice beach with good parking. I really enjoy walking this beach. Cross the PCH inside the neighborhood to find more great views and beautiful side streets to wander. name: Santa Monica State Beach

google_reviews.text: great beach experience. lots to play and see. the pacific ocean is fantastic

Clean sand beach portion of waterfront south of the Santa Monica Pier and next to popular hotels, restaurants, parks and shopping!
I love the beach here as my resting spot after riding the bike path along the coast. It has the same great benefits as the stretch of beach north of the pier, tho without as much of the views of the Santa Monica Mountains and the Malibu coastline.
The biggest advantage here is the lesser crowds, especially on the weekends.

An absolute must-view place if you are in Santa Monica!
We rented some bikes with the local sharing service (close to the metro station on Colorado Avenue) and cycled from Santa Monica to Venice and back. It takes 20-30 minutes on a slow pace. We loved the views and the vibes.

Awesome and clean beach just next to Santa Monica pier. Not so many palm trees here as in Venice beach though.

Very interesting to go from the beach to under Santa Monica Pier where you get amazing views with the many pillars.
Huge-feeling , clean beach with nice weather. Breeze coming off the ocean was refreshing and comfortable.

name: Santa Monica State Beach
formatted_address: 930 Pacific Coast Hwy, Santa Monica, CA 90403, USA

google_reviews.text: Santa Monica State Beach is one of those places that reminds you how good it feels to just pause life for a bit. The wide stretch of sand feels endless, and the boardwalk path makes the walk to the water so relaxing. On a hot day, the ocean breeze feels like instant therapy. What I love most is how the vibe is different every time—sometimes it’s full of energy with families, volleyball games, and music, and other times it feels calm and almost peaceful. Parking can be a little tricky, but once you’re on the sand, it’s worth every step. The sunsets here honestly look like they belong in a painting. 🌅 Definitely a spot to recharge, whether planned or unplanned.

This is one of my favorite beaches, been coming here since I was a kid, so many happy memories from childhood, teenage years, etc. The beach is fairly clean, however you do have to watch out for the water report cause it can get dirty. Be sure to bring a sweater or blanket cause after 5 it does get cold. It can be hard to find parking, there is a bit of traffic, the bathrooms can get a little dirty, and it can get pretty crowded.
Santa Monica has always been a beautiful place to visit.

Stopped here during a day trip. Hard to find parking but worth it!
@inacincinnis
Beautiful beach. Lovely area. Lots to see and do. Good food available.

name: Ginger Rogers Beach

google_reviews.text: Super Gay and Lots of guys on Saturday and Sunday. Party groups with music in the middle, calmer vibes towards the rainbow tower and towards Santa Monica Pier for the calmest. Bring your Speedo! You can pay for the parking lot or another pay behind the gas station. Personally I never pay and always find parking the the neighborhood. Sometimes a long scenic walk though!

If you’re looking for a gay-friendly beach in LA, Ginger Roger’s Beach always delivers. There are volleyball courts right next to the parking lot, a liquor store and a gas station right across the street, and the vibe is always just overall very friendly here. ❤️
Very cool,...clean best beach bathroom with snack bar right there, and underground tunnel to safely cross under the busy Pacific Coast Highway to the liquor store and restaurants too!
Love  this beach, been coming here for many years and always have a great time
Very cool beach, but a little noisy

name: Penn Park

google_reviews.text: A beautiful quiet park with lots of shade trees and sitting areas.  I love the 3 American elm trees in a triangle hugging each other and creating a huge canopy of shade along with 3 picnic benches, one under each tree set in a circular fashion which promotes socialization.  A drinking water fountain is also present.

AUGUST 2025. Wow… this was such a surprise to me. Beautiful and well kept park. The bathrooms were so clean and well stocked. The grass and playgrounds were amazing condition. My kids had a great time here. We arrived around 10a on Saturday morning and had the playground to ourselves. This is only a small section of a very large park. The birds, the large shaded trees, the waterfall sounds…. It was just a relaxing day for our family. One of our favorites already. Parking was easy and had about two handicap spots. Cannot wait to come back and explore more!

I'm born and raised in Whittier.  And I have good memories of my cousins birthday party at Penn Park around 1985.

I decide to return after all those years.

It was beautiful.  The park is for anyone who loves peace. But especially for families and for couples.

Take a girl or guy or whoever you like here, and picnic, major points will rack up.

name: Morgan park

Playground needs to be maintained more often. Some swings were dirty and “use at your own risk”. Playground had graffiti and kids eating/drinking food all over (spills and trash in the playground). The playground itself is a nice playground. It’s just the parents and kids that are inconsiderate leaving trash around. My kids encountered some rude kids with no playground etiquette but that isn’t the fault of the facility. It’s just the people and area, probably will not come back in the afternoon/evening times. Would only recommend coming early in mornings to avoid and low chances of bumping into the kids with no playground etiquette and respect.

Located smack dab in the middle of the city, this park has improved through the years. Granted, at times there are homeless people hanging out, they keep to themselves though. Love the outdoor gym. The parents need to let their kids know that the exercise equipments are not for playing on. They have a kids’ zone with slides, swings, and such. Also a splash zone that’s on during the hot summers. The public restroom is a hit and miss. I just wish they would fix the door lock on one of the stall doors. Good park for walking or jogging. A lot of dog walkers too. Thankfully there is a dog waste station. Hopefully a lot more people would make use of it.
I love this place I've been coming over since summer and still now. Today's Tuesday I will be there today every Tuesday. game on kids

name: Benny H. Potter West Adams Avenues Memorial Park

google_reviews.text: Clean, big park. Toddler area, dog area and workout equipment. Bathroom open, one was dirty… other was clean but no TP. Basketball courts, grass and sand areas.
This place have a couple of the most beautifully,  old and huge trees ever... I love to go and just hug them to feel their amazing energy.💚💚💚

This is the big little park there is a gym set, playground and a walking path for mother's with strollers and dads that are working on their fitness. Most of the time I use the pull up bars at the very far corner of the park. There is a great mural with Historical Black Icons. I love it here!!!

A quiet park nestled in the Jefferson Park Community and blocks away from the peace awareness labyrinth and gardens. The park itself offers itself a meditative and spiritual cleanse through the peace and quiet of nature , with the cool breeze and shade of the trees which surround this beautiful park.
You might see your occasional walker, dog walker, or regular citizen enjoying the relaxation of the environment. Definitely glad this tucked away, perfect size park was discovered. And a guaranteed frequent visit location for all who discover it as well.

Nice big park, clean bathrooms, & a nice play area for the kids. The only true downside is that it has sand and no recreational office to speak with someone about their park programs, if they have any.

name: Dearborn Park

Nice for kids to play here.. specially on sundays after church.

The play structures are large and shaded and decent quality. I'm giving this a 5/5 despite the sand and nasty bathrooms. They also have a basketball court, 2 full tennis courts, and lots of green space and picnic benches.

I came to this park over the weekend for my friend baby shower that they were having here. Finding parking was easy since there are a lot of street parking. I was trying to avoid parking near cars but for some reason they wanted to park near me. The baby shower was set up near the kids playground area so it was a good spot. The weather was nice and there were other parties at the park as well. The kids play area is a decent size and they even have a mist area. We notice that they have some monitor workers walking around the park, I guess to make sure everyone follows the rules. I never seen that before at a park but it's a good thing to have.

the park has a decently long walking path with picnic tables and exercise equipment along it, and a fairly large gated playground. the trees are cool and i appreciated the street lights as i journaled at night.

name: Ladera Park

google_reviews.text: Great park, lots of space, clean and safe (though I'd advise against walking around at night), Wienerschitzel, liquor store and gas station in walking distance for snacks.

This park was great to come to and grow up near at a young age. It's huge, and I remember as a kid playing here all the time. Something I noticed on my return a few weeks ago is how deluxe this park. There are so many amenities. Seriously jam-packed, with beautiful park infrastructure. Infrastructure that was graciously made with care decades ago and has stayed the test of time.

Lovely little park in the middle of the city. Feels very clean and well looked after, restrooms, playground for kids tennis and basketball courts are available. You can bring your dog as long as they're on the leash and you clean after them. Lots of spaces and tables for picnic as well. Anice little place to stretch your legs in between trees and some green

Nice toddler playground. Adaptive swings. One sheltered picnic area. Many uncovered picnic tables. Didn't see a parking lot. Only street parking. Paved pathways to descend from street to park and playground. Basketball court on other side. Public bathrooms on each side of the (not too big, not too small) park.

Ladera Park in Inglewood is a total gem for anyone looking to chill or get active. It’s got everything: clean playgrounds for the kids, courts for basketball and tennis, and lots of space for picnics or just lounging around. The vibe is super community-focused, with lots of local events that bring everyone together. It’s always clean, feels safe, and there’s plenty of parking. Basically, it’s the go-to spot if you’re in Inglewood and want a cool place to hang out or meet up with friends. Definitely worth checking out!

name: Pearson Park
formatted_address: 400 N Harbor Blvd, Anaheim, CA 92805, USA

google_reviews.text: One of my favorite parks! Visited in June 2025 and really enjoyed my time here. Lots of beautiful green space to enjoy, and the walking paths were well-maintained and accessible. The ponds and cactus garden were my favorite parts. We saw many ducks and turtles in and around the water, and I loved seeing so many different varieties of succulents and cacti! Loved the huge palms along the walking paths too! Will definitely be back!

Clean, safe, well designed. There is a lot of duck poop towards the front, but the basketball court, baseball field, swimming pool and tennis courts are well kept. Love the police surveillance camera near the basketball courts and the tribute to Kobe and Gianna. It was a great place to meet up with my son and play basketball. Nice park to just kick it.

A small park in the middle of Anaheim, where you get easy access to the shopping center. On a less busy day, it’s actually pretty quiet here. There’s a little pond, an amphitheatre and various sport activities.

We had a great time at this park, even though a squirrel almost peed on me.  Very charming, lots of birds and plants.  Highlight was seeing the red shouldered hawk fly into their nest with food for their babies.
Really cool spot. Seeing lots of turtles, geese, squirrels and my favorite of all the Kobe Bryant and Gigi mural

name: Echo Park
formatted_address: 1632 Bellevue Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90026, USA

google_reviews.text: Echo Park in Los Angeles is a truly refreshing place to unwind. The lake, surrounded by tall palm trees and the skyline in the background, creates a peaceful atmosphere right in the city. You can walk, paddle on the lake, enjoy the ducks, or just sit by the water and relax. The neighborhood has a friendly, artistic vibe with cafes, small local shops, and plenty of good food. Whether you go during the day for the sunshine or in the evening for the lights reflecting on the lake, it always feels calming and beautiful.

Canonical landmark in front of the LA skyline, Echo Park offers the tranquility in the midst of downtown LA for walking around an artificial lake. Pedal boats are available for renting, and the lotus festival is not to miss. Highly recommended to relax and recharge after grudging days!
Stopped by on my way to the Echo and it was such a great looking park. Cleaned up great and always an LA Landmark. It was so nice to see the park.

Great walk around the lake, especially on a nice day. Lots of people watching and geese who want to get your food. Definitely some homeless around and the bathrooms are kinda gnarly but for walking purposes it’s a hidden beauty in LA.
Small but uber interesting iconic (locally) park in the Echo Park Silverlake L.A. area.  Swan paddle boats.  Fauna (birds) everywhere and the flora.....beautiful.

name: Story Park
formatted_address: 210 N Chapel Ave, Alhambra, CA 91801, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice little park that is unfortunately split in two by Woodbury Avenue. The north part has a playground, picnic area, and softball field. The south part has tennis courts and a community center. Both sides welcome dogs on leash, and there are poop bag dispensers if you run out.

This is a review of the tennis courts at Story Park. It's sad how the city has allowed them to be in such disrepair. They haven't been resurfaced in probably 50 years! There are cracks, chips, and the painted lines are now so very faint. The lights are very dim if you try to play at night. This seems to be common in Alhambra these days. Many of the other city courts are in similar shape. Unfortunately,  the city seems to be distracted by other things. Sad.

A Pre Post-Apocalyptic Park for every survivor 🤺🫥A nice park for kiddos and adults alike!! It is “conveniently” divided in half with the baseball field and playground on one side and a rec center with an abandoned pool🏚🏚 on the other. A food helping pickup station was in progress during the family visit🫴🏻🍕🍗🧇🍓🍌🍎

Great little park for families!
I brought my baby here and we had such a great time! Story Park has two separate playgrounds, which is perfect for kids of different ages. There's also a volleyball net, a baseball field, and plenty of picnic tables. It’s clean, well-kept, and has lots of space to run around. I’d definitely recommend it for a small kid’s birthday party or just a fun afternoon out. We'll be coming back for sure!

Story park is located in the prime area of Alhambra. This place is quiet, not too big so you don’t feel too intimidated, and the park gives a nice tree shade. When you lie down on the grass area, you feel relaxed and rejuvenate yourself.

When you live in between Alhambra and San Marino, stop by Story Park. You will have a quality time with your family.

Please Stay Healthy, Be Safe, and Be Well.

Until the next posting…

Bosco

Nice park for walking, swimming, basketball, football, exercising, dancing, playing, lunches, meetings, parties, protests...
This park is rich in history. It's cherished. Come and enjoy.

Signal Hill Park is perfect for family outings. The picnic areas, playgrounds, and open spaces offer endless opportunities for fun and relaxation. I can't recommend it enough for a day of quality time with loved ones.
This park is clean small and just for dogs
A beautiful sight seeing spot! Ice cream truck often pass here and you should get one. love the vibes here!

name: Prospect Park
formatted_address: Echandia St, Los Angeles, CA 90033, USA

google_reviews.text: The park is clean easy access, with a playground area, picnic area with tables and benches. Plenty of green grass and shade from large beautiful trees.

1. Long Meadow & Lakeside
- One of the largest uninterrupted meadows in any U.S. urban park
- The Prospect Park Lake offers boating, birdwatching, and peaceful views

2. Recreation & Trails
- Miles of paved and dirt trails for biking, jogging, and walking
- Baseball fields, basketball courts, soccer, and playgrounds

3. Family-Friendly Spots
- Prospect Park Zoo, LeFrak Center (ice/roller skating & boating), carousel, and nature programs
- Multiple playgrounds for all ages

4. Events & Culture
- Home to free concerts, festivals, and Smorgasburg food markets

It's a beautiful park, there's a lot of tree and a playground, for me it's a very relaxing place. The only problem is that there's no street light so in the night is very obscure and it looks a very unsafe place.

Calm, had a nice picnic here with some friends. Nice food in the area and it's pretty quiet, not pure silence but closer to public ambience. A simplistic moonlight on a beautiful day for some romantic scenery. The park itself is well kept, it's just the surrounding area that was a little dirty.
Small park but so cuts. Nice view of the city, clean tables. Love.

name: Polliwog Park

We've come back a hand full of times and there is this one standing swing apparatus which is supposed to be tied down with a little leeway to build a little strength and balance as you walk on it to get to the next one beside it. But when I noticed my daughter swinging on it. It seemed odd that it would swing out too far when the adjacent ones on either sides of it were properly tethered/chained down to prevent it from a) running into a walk way, b) colliding into the other c) tipping someone over.
They had not addressed it and taken care of that potential hazard yet.
I was raised in the 80's. So I figured," hey just let her be!" But when younger or less physically rambunctious children were trying to hang on and find their equilibrium her center apparatus banged into them knocking them off balance and often times right off 🤭.
So I pulled her right off to show some care for the safety of other people's children.
We moved on to other parts of that area as we watched in horror as some of the unregulated kids would just haphazardly an improperly use the particular playground equipment dangerously without knowing. And watching a Parent show how it's done and fall right off hurting their pride 🥹🤭. When the equipment has to be properly attached.
Moving right along, obviously, where there's ducks, and or geese, there will be their feces all over the place that's just the way it is. It's that's prolific. For the most part they stay by the water, but when a lot of them have ventured out further inland they leave their happy droppings of course.
it's a dog-friendly Park that has dog poop all over. I thought, hey, it's Manhattan Beach, surely some of these folks have a reasonable decorum of cleanliness and will pick up after their own dog. 🤷..
Maybe, it could be people not from around the area that are being disgusting not caring, usually is. That's not the parks fault. But it does need a little more attention to keep it from getting a little more out of hand which is coming close to.

We stumbled upon Polliwog Park and absolutely loved it. Such a beautiful park with a peaceful pond and lots of open space to explore. My granddaughter had the best time—she especially loved the ducks and learned how to say “duck” for the first time here! 🦆

The toddler play area is enclosed, which made it feel super safe and relaxed for little ones to run around. You can tell this place is very family-friendly—everyone we met was kind and welcoming. A perfect spot for a sunny afternoon with babies and young kids. We’ll definitely be back!

Beautiful community park with a lovely lake, plenty of open space, and free street parking nearby. The playground is very well equipped, and there’s also a solid outdoor workout station for adults. Bathrooms are available and generally convenient.
The main downside is the is the goose poop around the lake area — while the geese add to the scenery, there’s quite a bit of goose poop concentrated near the water, so watch your step.
It's a very well-maintained and clean park,  It's a large and wonderful park. The only inconvenience is that there aren't many restrooms.

one of the best chill parks in manhattan beach, hands down. super peaceful vibes with the pond, ducks just living their best life, and plenty of open space to relax or walk around. the amphitheater is a cool touch and actually feels alive when events are happening. great spot for families, casual walks, or just zoning out for a bit. the disc golf course is a fun bonus, and the park is clean and well-maintained. dogs are allowed, which is a huge win. parking is usually manageable too.

overall, perfect balance between calm and community energy. yeah twin, this place slaps

name: Live Oak Park
formatted_address: 10144 Bogue St, Temple City, CA 91780, USA

google_reviews.text: New playground, regular activities in the community center, wonderful and much needed 3rd space for a healthy and vibrant community. The city park and recs folks are great. They put a lot of work into events like campaloza (camping in the greens) Halloween events, walk events, summer camps, etc. Tennis courts, soccer fields, baseball field, pickle ball field. There are always members of the community getting sunshine, being active, and creating memories with family/friends/neighbors here. Please drive slowly around the park, kids might run into the streets chasing balls.

This park is at the junction of Temple City and Arcadia City, in a residential community, far from the main road, very quiet.  This park is a sports-themed park. The grass is beautiful and open.  To the north are tennis courts, which are numerous.  There is a parking lot on the south and north sides of the park, and there are many parking spaces.  To the west is the lounge area and barbecue area, where many people hold parties during the holidays.  Inside the sports field is a football field and a baseball field, where children's football and baseball games are often held.  This is a great place to exercise.
Very nice park the upgrade to the playground has made this even busier then before. They just need more lights for the playground. Amazing place to visit
Live oak Park is a wonderful, expansive open space in Temple City. There is significant renovation going on, building a moonscape playground which promises to keep little explorers engaged.
I just feel the number of buses to Temple City Live Oak Park has been eliminated on the weekends when we are off work.  The access for non-local residents to visit the Park becomes very inconvenient.

name: Abraham Lincoln Park
formatted_address: 300 N Buena Vista St, Burbank, CA 91505, USA

google_reviews.text:
Beautiful park with tons of shade and a great
enclosed playground area. Love this one because kids can’t run out of your sight easily when playing. Northwest Library is also at this location so you can spend hours of fun here with ample parking both in the library and on the street.

Great place to take your kids. Whether it's biking around the block, a causal walk, or playtime with the little ones, this park will be for you. The kids play area is gated, shaded and small enough to see your kids from the seat provided, yet big enough for them to run around and play. Parking around is easy. You can take your dog as well, but they are not allowed in the kids play area.

Love this park! It's super cute and usually has parents/kids playing or couples having a picnic. Great place to walk around, enjoy the grass and trees and chill out. The ooonly thing I would caution are the mosquitos. These things are mean and will go for your ankles. They come out mainly around sun set, so if you plan to stay longer, bring some repellent and change into those pants!

This is a cute little Park off of Buena Vista Road in LA it is totally gated around it so the little ones can't go off into the street. It's shaded so that's a plus a little kiddos don't get sunburned while they're playing. And it's right next to the library, very convenient.
This little park is located on the grounds of the  library and is super quaint . We went during the early afternoon and it wasn't crowded.  The kids all had a great time, and I'd definitely go back.

name: Almansor Park

google_reviews.text:
I finally visited Almansor Park even though it’s so close to my house, and it turned out to be a really nice spot. It’s a big park with plenty of space to walk in loops — perfect for getting your steps in. Dogs are allowed on leash, though I did notice quite a few off-leash, so something to be mindful of.

The park has a pond with lots of waterfowl, which adds a nice touch of nature, but it also means the main gazebo seating area is pretty covered in droppings. If you want a clean place to sit, the benches a little further from the pond are much better.

There’s also a neat “Alhambra” statue/sign that makes for a cool photo op. Overall, it’s a pleasant community park that’s great for a stroll, but I’d avoid the gazebo area if you’re planning to sit and relax.

A quiet park with a clean environment, offering many trails for leisure activities to the public. The park is well-integrated with nature and home to many small animals. The playground equipment for young children shows some age, but it still offers a high level of entertainment and uniqueness. Kids continue to have a great time!

Nature rating: 🌳🌳🌳🌳🌳

One of my favorite parks, the little duck pond is always fun to visit but please remember to not feed the wildlife. They have lots of designated areas for sports and the kids playground is rubber floor and has plenty of shading especially after 3pm which is great to bring the kids to play after school. Their bathrooms are clean and always stocked.

Almansor Park is one of the best parks in the area. It’s spacious, clean, and well maintained. There’s plenty of room to walk, run, picnic, or just hang out. The lake and open green space make it feel a lot bigger than most neighborhood parks, and it’s great for families. The playground area is solid, and there’s always a good community vibe on weekends.

The only downside is parking. It can be tough to find a spot during peak hours, especially on weekends or when there’s an event going on. You may need to circle a bit or park farther out.

Overall, a great local park and definitely worth visiting.

They even had a little pond where u can enjoy seeing different types of ducks and turtles🤩 I mean, it has it all. Just wonderful. Really enjoyed my day here.

name: Furman Park
formatted_address: 10419 Rives Ave, Downey, CA 90241, USA

google_reviews.text: Huge park with a walking path around the outside (2 laps are close to a mile)! And they have 3 tennis courts and 1 basketball court and 2 playgrounds and a "gym" station. Given how huge the park is I wish they had more courts for both sports but it's nice if you're able to secure a court.

It's on the smaller side, but it seems well maintained. There's exercise equipment and a track to run. A couple baseball fields, tennis courts, basketball courts and a couple playground areas and several areas to grill. I haven't met the staff here, so I can't comment on that.

I do know it closes at 10:00pm, which is better than other parks that close at sunset. The restrooms seem to close a bit earlier, but I don't know the exact time. My guess would be that they close between 6:00pm and 8:00 pm. Recently, the cops are fairly consistently checking between 10:00-11:00pm to see that people are out of the park after closing.

For those that play pogo, this is an ex raid location. It also has several spots to spin (I believe 6-7 without including the gyms).

Update: the lights at the park seem to stay on over night, and while I don't recommend walking there late, I occasionally do and the lights definitely help make you feel safer. I definitely don't recommend walking there alone. I've never had reason to believe it's dangerous, but it's best to exercise caution.

There are also 2 pay stations to charge electric vehicles.

This is my favorite place in Downey. Because it is walk distance where I stay with a baby. We almost every morning visit here and have great time. We walk and play together. It’s really peaceful place.

name: Wilderness Park
formatted_address: 10999 Little Lake Rd, Downey, CA 90241, USA

google_reviews.text: I enjoyed the visit to this park. I came for a walk with my dog. The park is very clean and well kept.  During the week you can park here for free, parking is hard to get in the main parking lot, I guess they close the parking area near the playground during the week.  Maybe I need to try coming earlier, I came at 930am and had to circle the parking lot and wait for a spot. This park has an access to the San Gabriel river trail and I saw lots of people riding their bikes. The walking path is level and free of debris, I felt safe coming here to walk, lots of other women and some men walking the pathway. They have garbage cans and dog stations with baggies for your dog, I did not see any water stations so bring water for you and your pup.

I love bringing my kids to this park, it’s beautiful, serene, and always calming. Their favorite part is feeding the ducks, squirrels, rabbits and I enjoy taking photos and videos of the stunning flowers, especially the colorful baby’s breath. Seeing their faces light up makes every visit feel even more special.

We went to spend our saturday evening but I saw people fishing as well. Although I do not recommend fishing there because the water was not that clean in my opinion, just take your stuff and see if you find it suitable for fishing then go for it, I would not. Overall, the park is lovely. My daughter loved the ducks. 💕

By far one of the cleanest parks I’ve been to. They have a play area for kids, a variety of picnic tables, they have a bunch of ducks, turtles and even fish. You’re also able to look for hummingbirds, dragonflies, bees, butterflies etc which was pretty nice! My kids loved this park. Very well kept.

I biked 40 minutes from home along the San Gabriel River Trail and ended up at Wilderness Park — definitely worth the ride. This is one of the most peaceful, well-maintained green spaces in southeast LA County.

The park features a scenic man-made lake with fountains, ducks, shaded paths, and wooden bridges that make for great photo spots. Interpretive signs along the path highlight local wildlife and plant life — including pollinators, migratory birds, and waterfowl species. You’ll see ducks, egrets, herons, and maybe even a dragonfly show if you slow down enough to notice.

What stood out most was how calm and walkable the layout is. It’s not a huge park, but it’s very well planned: paved trails, picnic areas, shaded benches, and wide-open views of the lake. It’s a great place to relax, observe wildlife, or just take a break from city pace. Clean, quiet, and clearly cared for by the City of Downey.

Whether you’re biking in from the river trail like I did or visiting with family, Wilderness Park is a great reminder that a well-designed park doesn’t have to be flashy — just thoughtful.

Leimert Park used to be a fun, SAFE, conscious Black space to hang out at, til the weeee hours of the morning!! We usta play chess with the elders while listeng to jazz at 5th St Dicks and World Stage!! The Vision theater usta be poppin with conscious activities and well. The drum circles, African dance classes, Black events, concerts, festivals, vigils, farmers markets, etc all used to be fun and safe.

It's gone waaaaaaay down hill since the 90's.
The vibe is gone.
Tooo many dope-heads and negativity. At night, its a cesspool for chaos.

But I DO love the Black businesses that are left and some the the events held there.
But the conscious, intelligent, respectful Black vibe is basically gone. Too much war with each other and disrespect.
Thats all I'll say for now, respectfully🙏🏾

If you are looking for opportunities to support the melanated Los Angeles community, this is where you want to go. Leimert  Park is in the LA  Crenshaw district and has always been the place to be/visit. It’s filled with melanated owned businesses and is also family friendly. There’s a community melanated American drum circle and a local vender market almost every weekend. There’s an abundance of good food, cafes, art, music, shopping, hair salons/beauty supplies, clothes, wellness stores, entertainment options. It’s always kept up nice, presentable, and clean. The Leimert Part neighborhood has always been considered as a more desirable upscale, and affluent neighborhood in South Central Los Angeles, CA. I always make sure I hit this area up every time I visit South Central LA, my hometown. It’s directly within the vicinity of the Baldwin Hills Plaza, Crenshaw mall. There’s so much variety, inclusivity, and diversity in this area. It’s a thriving melanated, cultural, entrepreneurial community. Don’t go to LA without visiting the Leimert Park plaza. The actual park was remodeled/revamped and is much more nicer now. The actual park, on its own, used to attract a lot of homeless people who were actually living there. That issue was fixed and is no longer a problem.  At the end of the day, no matter where you are, you should always have situational awareness/knowledge, monitor and pay attention to your surroundings for your own personal safety. It’s just the wise thing to do. Some people call it street smarts, while others call it plain old common sense.

Love, Light, Peace, Blessings, Community, Respect, Melanated, Care, Connectedness, History, Happiness, Family, Victory, Home

Cascades Park has been a part of Monterey Park for so long that its city logo is even based off of it. Even though years go by and the city itself undergoes changes, this park still stands as it decades ago and is definitely a great place to stop and rest while taking in the city view from up top.

You can find many stairs sloped in a way that'll be a fun and quick challenge to go through in order to reach the top, parking at the base as well as up on the hill which would require you to go through side streets first, and that beautiful mini waterfall that lights up during the night.

Note what you see is what you get. It's more of a sightseeing place rather than an actual park so no playground, no restroom, and no picnic tables (though I did see a group of friends chilling on the grass).
This park has stairs to work out 🏋️. And a nice view of the city .

A hidden gem of the LA area. If this park were in Los Angeles proper, thousands of people from all over the world would come here to take touristy pics, but as it stands Cascades Park stands in a relatively quiet suburban neighborhood and tends not to be very crowded on most days

name: Columbia Park
formatted_address: 4045 190th St, Torrance, CA 90504, USA

google_reviews.text: One of my favorite parks for walking my dogs. Very large park with plenty of different areas to walk. Inside the park there are various paths you can take.  There is a large community garden at the back of the park and on one side of the park there is a large field that runs the length of the park. This field is also part of the park and its used for various local sports events. The field is  open for everyone's use if its not in use for sports. There are three parking lots so parking is never an issue. There are various picnic tables throughout the park and one very large picnic area. The middle section of the park has a very large grassy area. There are two areas for kids to play. Many people come to this park to picnic, walk or jog, walk their dogs, play sports, etc. Even when it's very busy, I can find areas to be alone and walk my dogs. The park does contain quite a few overhead power lines.
I truly enjoy this park. It's huge! Places to have outdoor celebrations or family gatherings. Or if you want to sit under a tree and read a book, it's perfect. There's enough space for everyone.

You could reserve tables for birthday parties family reunions graduations there’s plenty of parking all the way around the park that’s how huge it is lots of beautiful trees well-maintained grass a dirt walking trail for exercising restrooms and very friendly people from the community and people that are not from the community so kind pet friendly I would recommend this Park whether you are having a small party or large gathering or even just to walk to get exercise or just kicking the soccer ball or throwing the football around barbecue areas as well as sitting areas The actual slides and playground area for the kids there are several of them going according to age so the littler kids have an opportunity to experience the fun!

name: Joslyn Park

google_reviews.text:
⚠️ watch out for Kona ⚠️

Love this park! Great for fetch and playing with other good dogs. Most are friendly and kind. Unfortunately, a dog named Kona is a regular here. I’ve seen her at all times of day. She is aggressive towards other dogs, somewhat randomly and without warning. Especially against huskies. Kona is a mix but is tall and skinny, with short hair that is a brindle pattern. I believe the owner said she is American bully, Doberman, and pitbull terrier.

She left my friend’s husky with a large puncture wound on his shoulder that got infected and had to be treated at the vet. He was just minding his business when she ran by and attacked. I think she thought he might take her ball. The owners refused to help cover vet costs. I’ve also seen her attack another shepherd mix, who was just playing with a lab that Kona likes. The shepherd’s mom said it was not the first time.

Other than Kona, I’ve never had an issue with anyone else.

So yes, El Dorado Park is awesome! I love it, every time I see it!

One look at the Google Maps satellite view here, and it’s easy to see why this park is so popular with locals and visitors!
Out of the huge urban sprawl of concrete and rooftops, here’s a big beautiful green spot on the map!
El Dorado Park is like several parks together as one, all within easy access of the freeways!
Park on the west side of the park, off Studebaker Rd for free and have access to all the amenities of the City of Long Beach side!
For spur of the moment visit’s, it’s a cinch to stop by one of the many available take-out food stops nearby to gather up lunch for a picnic.
Here, there’s park facilities, activities, events, open space and areas for just about all games to play!
The park includes several baseball, softball and soccer fields, courts for basketball, volleyball, roller hockey, and tennis. There’s a skatepark park, also used for BMX fun!
A cool, tree lined disk golf course is at the north end, a traditional golf driving range and an 18 hole course is at the south end. A portion of the traditional course continues to the northeast side.
The large white pavilion or marquee tent at the south end parking lot may be rented for private functions, weddings and receptions.
Overall maintenance of the park is good! Trash containers are placed around the park, so disposing of trash shouldn’t be a problem. Restrooms are available, tho may be a bit messy when the park is crowded.
Busy weekends and holidays may involve early planning for the west side of the park, especially securing a parking spot and table. Street parking is also available, tho may be few and far between!
Otherwise, the peace of mind toting supplies to family picnics and group gatherings may be made easier with the paid parking lots of the LA County operated Regional Park side. There’s both open and covered picnic tables, chairs and grassy spots and are usually within close reach of parked vehicles and more available picnic spots.

name: Siminski Park

google_reviews.text: I had some business at LAX and had some time to spare, so I bought an In-N-Out hamburger and headed to the nearest park. The park was small, but the surrounding trees provided shade, which was refreshing. There was a playground and restrooms, but I'm not sure if they were usable. The parking lot was also small. It didn't seem like a very well-organized park.

Functional but Lacking Critical Sanitation Supplies
Location: Siminski Park, Inglewood, CA
Rating: 2/5 Stars (Below Average)
This restroom facility at Siminski Park is technically functional but is severely lacking in the basic sanitation necessities required for public health.
On the positive side, the toilet was operational and flushed correctly, and there was a sufficient supply of toilet paper. The sinks also provided running water, allowing for hand rinsing.
However, the overall environment was uninviting and appeared poorly maintained, falling far short of acceptable standards for a public facility. Most importantly, the restroom was completely missing both hand soap and paper towels. While running water is available, the absence of soap makes effective hand washing impossible, and the lack of paper towels leaves users without a way to dry their hands.
Recommendation: If you must use this restroom, be sure to bring your own hand sanitizer and drying cloth/tissues, as the park management does not provide essential cleaning supplies. This facility requires immediate attention to maintenance and restocking of hygiene products.

Disgusting park but sorta beautiful. The moment we opened the car door to this park, all we could smell was human poo & pee. Like a really, really strong smell of pee. It’s almost impossible to not notice . Extremely overwhelming & the restrooms are atrocious. It seems safer to just pee outside in the parking lot (we did not do that but by comparison it seems safer). We came toward closing time on a Friday and there was an abandoned bench with a bunch of food and trash on it as if somebody had a picnic & then just left all the stuff there. There were two other cars in the parking lot but they seemed abandoned tbh. It was a little eery but this park seems to have potential. Me and my fiancé came to this park about a year ago when we were first dating & it was a lot better compared to now…we did come in the day time then so that may be the difference. I don’t know. If somebody just cleaned up around here, this would be a quaint little park.

Now for beautiful, this park has a lot of potential. I love the spaciousness and the trees. There are numerous fresh trees and the vibe is so lively. The general structure is very park-like and this could be a loving space for a community if it is maintained properly.
