It’s also easy to access, with nearby parking and walkable spots to grab coffee or a bite to eat before or after your visit. Whether you’re looking to unwind, take photos, or just enjoy the sound of the waves, Coronado Beach is a must-visit when in San Diego.

Nothing bad to say about such a beautiful place. I could sit at the beach there every evening and watch the sun set and die a happy man! Beach looked very well maintained and there’s so much stuff around to enjoy as well.

The only issue is parking. You will need to search for parking on the street and it could be far away from the beach. other than that, the beach was absolutely beautiful. Clean and family friendly. It’s really interesting when you see the sand at this beach because it looks like it has glitter in it. It’s very dark colored sand with a silver glitter on top.
This beach is clean and nice enough (the best I’ve been to in recent memory) to make the trip when you are in the area, parking will be a little tough during weekends.

name: Black's Beach
formatted_address: Black's Beach, 2800 Torrey Pines Scenic Dr, La Jolla, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text: Definitely go here! The parking is free and there's lots of it. You might have to walk far though. There are things to do at the top of the cliffs but you have about a 10-20 minute hike down the cliff to access the beach. Note, the only way back up is to climb the steps back up... It's mostly steps but parts of the hike are a little scary. I would only recommend doing this if you are a good walker and have a bit of stamina. I'm 6 months pregnant and had to stop multiple times on the way back up. Now... The beach... It is beautiful and a mix of regular people and nudist.... Mostly naked men but a lot of women do have their tops off. The beach was clean and the water was great! I highly recommend going and cannot wait to go back! Definitely bring a blanket and your bathing suit!!

The beach is wide and the water looks great!  Not really friendly, but people are respectful.

There is a long area of shallow water following wave break, but it can be difficult to make your way into deeper waters.  I was knocked back a few times, even when going “under” the wave.

The fact that paragliders also use the area makes it somewhat challenging for parking.

Also, the stairs!  It is LITERALLY a hike, so you’ll definitely get a workout.  Factor that obstacle into your beach time.
Gorgeous scenery and a short, but challenging hike up and down the cliffside. The views and beach are worth the effort! Great place to sunbathe and watch the gliders pass overhead.

Nice spot with good views. Went out for quiet time and fresh air and it was pretty peaceful. The cliffs overlook the beach bellow which you can access from trail and steps. There is also a restaurant and hang-gliding option if that’s your thing. Party is a little limited. I went on the weekend and during the week, the weekday is less crowded.

Beautiful beach. I like to take the stairs down the bluffs to the shoreline in the morning and run along the water. Note: this is a nude beach, so it is something to remember when visiting. Otherwise, I more often come here and stay atop the cliffs. Great place to reflect, unwind, and relax while gazing out across the mighty Pacific. La Jolla Cove, Mission Bay, and Coronado to the south. Immediately north is Torrey Pines. Simply spectacular.

name: Law Street Beach
formatted_address: Law Street Beach, 671 Ocean Blvd #601, San Diego, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text: As good as the Pacific gets, I’m sure that it’s a mess on the weekends, but weekdays no trouble finding parking. There is a decided lack of street people and that's refreshing.

Great views from the top of the park that overlooks the beach. Watch the surfers, kite borders and beach goers.
Since it's about 100 feet up you can see expansive ocean views; North towards False Point and South looking at the Pacific Beach Pier and if you look just right you can see the hills of Ocean Beach and pier.
Beach is nice and big, lots of open sand. Have to walk down from park to get to the beach.

Was amazing with the sun set and view, great place to go and just chill.
The parking there was difficult but when you find it then your set ... be sure to stay for sunset and be careful on the way down if you go as it is so rough ... we slipped and fall because we are clumsy, but thats us ... we were in slippers and not shoes ... so its all on us.

Low key side of Pacific Beach. Good walking path, lots of surfers and good sized green area for picnic. There is donation based yoga Saturday and Sunday mornings. Four star because parking was a challenge

This is a real quite beach. Very clean and the bathrooms and showers are easy to access. Parking is a bit rough. There is a group who does yoga and tons of swimming and surfing. In the early morning there are tons of shells on the beach.

name: South Mission Beach, San Diego
formatted_address: South Mission Beach, San Diego, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: May have to circle the parking lot a few times until a spot opens up, but great beach overall. Attentive lifeguards, plenty of space to spread out, bathrooms and rinse-off showers, and nice view of the coastline. There’s a pretty large grassy area, volleyball courts, and basketball courts. Visited many times on weekday evenings to skim board and catch the sunset. Parking lot is still pretty full around 6:00pm but usually we find a parking spot near the lot entrance or as someone is leaving. Weekends are more crowded and parking is trickier but if with patience, you’ll get a spot. If not, there’s another parking lot down the road (next to Belmont park) with a bigger lot and an additional lot across the street. From the times when I have visited, people at this beach were friendly and fun.

I was impressed by what a large park and beach this area is! You could easily spend a whole day here just chillin' and enjoying the park, the sports, the beach and swimming. There are even a few grills and firepits available to use. I was just here for a short while to sit on the beach and watch the sunset. It is overall a great place for that. The   beach is a bit messy with washed-up seaweed, but overall, it's still enjoyable!

Beautiful and large beach area with plenty of things to see, play, do, or just chill. Big enough that it won't look too crowded. There are many AirBNBs and rentals very close or on the beach.
There is also Belmont Park park right by the beach, and plenty of places to eat. Kids and adults can easily enjoy a whole day here.

This is the southernmost point of Mission Beach. It's a very spacious beach that attracts many people. There are several places where you can play beach volleyball. While there are public parking lots nearby, there are no restaurants, cafes, or shops right next to it.( Foods are few blocks away) It can get quite crowded during the popular summer months, especially during summer vacation.
Went unfortunately on a cooler rainy day (still trying to figure out how to control the weather lol) and it was very peaceful. Beach was beautiful and the boardwalk was nice!

name: Breakers Beach
formatted_address: Breakers Beach, San Diego, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: All the Cabana grills managed by MWR have holes in them, can't even grill properly.

I loved this beach! I plan to return. I love that it is more private than the public beach. I walked there from Building 572 easily. (if you're carrying stuff, maybe you'd want to drive) The beach was clean...no trash!

Cool beach on Naval Base Coronado. Was cloudy when we went but so much open beach space, beach equipment rentals and a large parking lot.  Will head back and take more pictures when we go and it’s sunny out.

LOVE THIS BEACH!!! I have been to this beach too many times to count now and forever see myself coming here. If I could afford to retire here one day, well then everyone would know where to find me!! If you are fortunate enough to to be able to access the beach beach from base side, it's never crowded and just perfect. Having been on both sides, I do like then both, but with a small child, the less crowded side on the base is the obvious choice. Waves are perfect and if you're someone who's interested in collecting sand dollars, well then come on down because they are endless!!

This is a nice beach. Because it is on base, parking is easier than the other beaches off base in Coronado. There are good waves for boogie boarding and surfing. The water is always a little cold but you get used to it. It is a little walk to the actual beach from the parking lot.

name: Tourmaline Beach
formatted_address: Tourmaline Beach, 601 Tourmaline St, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text:
Such a lovely beach! 🧜🏻‍♀️ It’s great for surfing (also for beginners) with a big parking lot, public restrooms & showers.
I really like it for beach walks too: You can start in PB and walk along the end of Tourmaline where you‘ll find some stairs so that you can enjoy a fantastic view from above.

Came here on a Monday afternoon for a pleasant walk with my wife. Low tide allowed plenty of flat, shiny sand to walk on. There is a little memorial for past and present surfers. I like the welcoming Aloha message. If you are not here for swimming or surfing, you can turn left and walk towards the pier. Take a day off if you can to enjoy it on a weekday, I am sure weekends are packed.
It's pretty easy to find parking here, even over Memorial Day Weekend (Seriously!). While it's not too busy, it's also not desolate either, and there is plenty of retail and restaurants close by.
I love to come here to just relax, read a book or catch the sunset.
Love this beach! We like to come here to surf, watch the sunset, or simply just walk down the beach. Just be aware that there is not much parking in the lot there.

name: Kellogg Beach
formatted_address: Kellogg Beach, Point Loma Way, San Diego, CA 92106, USA

google_reviews.text:
Awesome local spot. It's tidel so plan when to go.
A couple of safety things to know about
No lifeguard.
It's basically in a channel so there is a steep drop off.
There is always a current.
The doc that people swim too is covered in razer sharp shells under the surface Be CAREFUL CLIMBING ONTO THE DOC FROM THE WATER.

This is a lovely little hidden gem frequented by locals. It’s not too crowded, and because it’s a bay, it’s really perfect for small children because you don’t have to worry about big surf or undertow. The beach doesn’t have too many shells. The sand isn’t fine, but it’s not too dirty. Kellogg beach is located a few blocks from the end of a coastal pedestrian/bike path, so it’s a great place to visit if you’re staying in the area and can bike in. If you’re driving, parking is very limited, and the beach is a bit tough to find, which is why I think it’s best suited to locals.

Kellogg Beach might not seem like it’s a location worth 5 stars at first glance, but if you cut through the residential area, it also connects to Bessemer Path, which has a lovely water front walk with lots of scenic spots, places for dogs to run in the water, and if you look closely, quite a few flower species! The fact that the path is right by the water helps to keep it a bit cooler too :)

Small hidden beach at the end of Rosecrans. Street parking only which can be challenging. No bathrooms. But a fun place to sit and relax...enjoying the views of the marina. The water is calm. Our kids kayaked and had a blast. A sea lion swam up pretty close to shore for a visit! It was pretty busy for a weekday.
This is one of my favorite beaches in San Diego. By far the easiest to park at and never really crowded. The views are magical.  The Sail boats are so fun to watch.

name: Mission Beach Park
formatted_address: 3000 Mission Blvd, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: Mission Beach is spectacular — a true 5.0 in every way. The beaches are top notch, lots of guards (do take the time to thank them, it’s a huge responsibility and NOT an easy task during summer busy periods). Volleyball courts on the sand nearby are plentiful and a path from the South Mission Beach Life Guard station stretches a full 3.1 miles GREAT for roller skating or walking, running or just about anything. The people watching is also pretty great. Also, there are some really talented street performers here (musicians), and some amazing sunsets. These photos and video should give you an idea.
It was an awesome beach with surfing going on. And a lot of stuff to do. Saw beautiful parrots, shopping,, and eating as well. My favorite beach this trip.

Long, long, long BEAUTIFUL stretch of beach. Clean. Safe. Surfing designated areas. Great boardwalk with an enormous amusement park including a giant roller-coaster right at the ocean. Great restaurants, shops. Roller skating and bike friendly. Parks with lawns and big trees and picnic tables. It's amazing, friendly and CLEAN!

Beautiful public beach with miles of paved boardwalk to stroll. Sunsets are amazing. You can have a wood fire on the beach as long as it is controlled and the coals are placed in provided bins. There are public restrooms near the beach and there is quite a bit of free daily parking near Belmont Park.
Great place to experience the ocean beach life. Surfing and volleyball, or just walking, running or biking the boardwalk.

name: la Jolla Shores Beach
formatted_address: La Jolla Shores Beach, San Diego, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text:
As an avid paddleboarder, I have searched far and wide for the perfect spot, and I can confidently say that La Jolla Shores is it. This beach is a 5 star haven, offering a breathtaking and serene experience that is simply unmatched.
The calm, clear waters of the cove are what truly set La Jolla Shores apart for paddleboarding. Unlike other beaches with crashing waves, the gentle swells here create the perfect conditions for a smooth and peaceful glide. This is ideal for paddlers of all skill levels, from beginners still finding their balance to experienced riders who want to enjoy the spectacular scenery.
What makes the experience so memorable is the abundant marine life. While paddling just beyond the waves, you can look down through the glistening water and see fish, bat rays, and the famous, harmless leopard sharks that frequent the area in the summer months. I even had a pod of dolphins escorting my group on a recent outing near the pier an unforgettable moment!
Rental shops like Everyday California and La Jolla Surf Systems are conveniently located just a block or two from the beach, making the whole process incredibly easy. You can get your gear and be on the water in minutes. There is also plenty of sandy shoreline to launch from, so you're not battling crowds to get in the water.

It’s still one of the best, been visiting here for 5 years. It’s so serene and eye catchy.
Only complain is food restaurants around it. They need to upskill with fusion and eclectic menus for different seasons. Can’t just have same  bland foods all the time
Beautiful area to watch seals and birds and great view. Easy to get to, a bit crowded to find parking, lots of people visiting , but during the week is fine.

This is a coastal gem that offers something for everyone! The scenery is stunning, with dramatic cliffs, clear blue waters, and abundant wildlife.

One of the best things about La Jolla is the opportunity to see seals and sea lions up close. They often lounge on the rocks and beaches, making for fantastic photo opportunities. The tide pools are also great for exploring, revealing a variety of marine life.

Whether you're looking to relax on the sand, swim in the ocean, or explore the natural beauty, La Jolla Beach is a must-visit destination in San Diego!

Beautiful beach with crystal clear waters most of the time. I've seen lots of fish, some small leopard sharks, dolphins, jellyfish, and stingrays (beware!). It's family friendly with lots of sand, and a grassy area to play as well. They also have a small playground and bathrooms. I also like that there are many cute restaurants within walking distance from the beach. We like to walk over there after a fun day and enjoy a meal by the sea (not ocean view, but close enough)

However, the reason for the 3 stars instead of five is how crowded it gets in the summer. The parking lot is always full, so most people have to park on the side streets which are very narrow and hard to get through (cars have to take turns driving past where there are cars parked, like it's almost a one-way street except that it's not). During one of the weekends in summer, the bathroom line was over 20 people! I was so glad I didn't need to go. The only good thing about the crowds, is that so many people scare away the stingrays, so there's that.

Overall, I'd recommend this beach on a weekday. It's still a bit crowded, but obviously we go here often, so it's worth it.

name: Central Beach
formatted_address: Central Beach, Coronado, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: Been to many beaches… this by far is one of my favorites

Pristine, family-friendly beach known for its wide, sandy shores and sparkling ocean views. It's perfect for swimming, sunbathing, and beach sports. The beach is clean, with gentle waves ideal for all ages. Stunning sunsets and the iconic Hotel del Coronado make it even more charming 😎

Brought the kid to take a dip into the Pacific in late March 2024 (yeah cold, but he's young and his blood's on fire) and were disappointed to find "beach closed due to bacteria" signs up and down the beach. A lifeguard truck seemed to be pulling out towards the water and calling swimmers back from the surf. A little research and I see the Tijuana River is dumping millions of gallons of human sewage out near Imperial Beach and it's led to closures of Coronado pretty often the past two years. Gross. This is an environmental disaster, and such a shame for a beautiful beach. The hotel was also under construction on the facade, so the trip through all that bridge traffic was a washout. Should have gone straight to Point Loma, I guess, and we'll strike Coronado off our list until "swimming in human excrement" is not on the menu.

Beautiful, clean beach for families, picnics, and sunbathing! We parked off Loma (free unlimited-hour street parking) and walked over within a few minutes. We sat to the left of the little dip in the sand (closer to the hotel) and found there to be far fewer people, though we would’ve had plenty of space regardless - the beach is huge (wide and long!). There is a free public restroom/bathroom directly behind the lifeguard tower. If you’re on a sand dollar hunt, I found two that were fully intact! Great beach, highly recommend.
It's really beautiful when the tide is low! Try to go on a weekday to avoid crowds and find parking.

name: Mission Beach
formatted_address: Mission Beach, Surfrider Sq, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing beach with great waves, enough space good restaurants and desserts close by and an amusement park as well, so much to do with not enough time but it was a blast coming here definitely coming back 😎
Very clean, not as crowded as other California beaches, Great boardwalk, nice lifeguards and great waves for boogie boarding. We had an awesome stay
Beautiful beach to visit around sunset! Lots of sand/beach to lay out and just walk around.
Nice beach to watch sunset. There is plenty of parking. Restrooms were filthy.
Best beach in Southern California!

name: Pacific Beach
formatted_address: Pacific Beach, California 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful! What a wonderful place. Every time I’ve visited San Diego in the past, I always stayed closer to downtown near the Gaslamp District, but on my most recent trip I was lucky enough to stay in Pacific Beach. What an absolutely stunning area. Walking along the beach and the boardwalk is peaceful, scenic, and relaxing. Everything feels open and there’s just a really great energy to the neighborhood. I can’t wait to come back. From now on, I’ll definitely be staying in Pacific Beach. It’s simply beautiful.

Pacific Beach is quintessential California. Lovely beach, great boardwalk, tons of shops and restaurants and a small town vibe. It is also adjacent to Mission Bay which has a nice boardwalk and parks. Can’t wait to comeback.

Loved this Beach most. Very lively atmosphere. Lots of water sports option available and found a number of people were enjoying. Lifeguard personnel's were on duty. Enjoyed the vibe of this Beach. Resorts on the Pier on the sea were most attractive. Must visit Beach while in San Diego and one of the Best Beaches.
A great place to stay, or just hang out. And there’s the beach.

Aug 2025 . . . this is arguably the peak month for visiting a Southern California beach . . . lots of visitors on vacation, sunset is about nine o’clock, and of course the waves keep washing up on shore, providing a great playground for kids and adults alike. The sunsets are magical, and I loved to watch how so many people seem to gather, pause, and of course capture on their smartphones or cameras, but in truth, even though I did the same thing on a few occasions, it was better to just watch it and let the moment settle in my memory for recall anytime I want or need to purge the stress of life. The boardwalk is great fun too, skaters, skate boards, and of course a few too many e-bikes with folks who don’t seem to know how to ride them. Love this place.

name: Ocean Beach City Beach
formatted_address: Ocean Beach City Beach, San Diego, CA 92107, USA

google_reviews.text: Popular surfing beach well monitored by life guards (our visit was June/July). Swimming is not encouraged in the area parallel to Bacon. Three large fire pits - we were able to snag one about an hour before sunset and several groups asked about availability after. Save for the fire pits, the beach was fairly empty after dark - perfect for our needs.

#exploringmyworld #wilinwithwiley
Still a favorite place to enjoy the beautiful tidepools and rocky cliffline.
Cliffs are beautiful and less polluted than the beaches closer to the metropolitan area. These are very nasty with trash and there is a lot of displaced people that loiter in this area. Keep a close eye on your kids and dogs. Things on the ground can be a hazzard such as needles and other toxic items.  A lot of intoxicated people in the area.

Absolutely Beautiful! Beware getting too close to the cliffs. But the view and crashing waves against the rocks was picturesque. Parking is limited. But once parked. It was well worth the wait for a space. Beautiful at sunset.

Nice beach to walk around, lots of seaweed and the homeless hangout spot but still beautiful!! 🌊 Hate the pier is permanently closed hope they fix it one day, it's the longest concrete pier on the west coast and has damage so closed... 😢
Very nice usually not busy, and it's perfect if you want to relax or meditate.

name: La Jolla Cove
formatted_address: La Jolla Cove, San Diego, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text:
La Jolla Cove is one of the most beautiful coastal spots in Southern California. Located in San Diego, this small, picturesque cove offers stunning ocean views, clear turquoise water, and a peaceful atmosphere. The cliffs surrounding the cove create a dramatic landscape that is perfect for photography.

One of the highlights of La Jolla Cove is the wildlife. Visitors can often see sea lions and seals resting on the rocks, which adds a unique and memorable experience. The water is usually calm, making it a popular place for snorkeling and swimming. The underwater visibility is excellent, allowing people to observe colorful fish and marine life.

The most beautiful scenery and a fantastic experience with the seals and pelicans, and egrets, and seagulls.  A must see place if you are visiting San Diego or just live here.  Restore your energy and go as much as you can, 👍🥰

Beautiful view, proximity to sea life and overall a great place to stroll around (including the mini hike just north of it).

I can only imagine the sunset must be great there.
Beautiful place to walk, watch the birds & the seals, and see the sunset. We were able to grab a Lyft from our hotel downtown very easily and it was the perfect last day to our San Diego trip.

Beautiful place a must in San Diego tourism. The trail provides incredible views and scenery. The Cove it self harnesses an oceanic eco system with a variety of sea life. Don’t get to the seal ions or seals. The area is heavily protected by rangers and lifeguards. Great place to enjoy from unique sea adventure or just a stroll with the family. Restrooms areas are clean as wells as showers. Play areas are ideal for weekend getaway togethers.

name: Coronado Beach
formatted_address: Coronado Beach, Coronado, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful and clean beach with soft sand and amazing ocean views. It has convenient showers and restrooms, which is great after swimming. Good place to relax and watch the sunset.

Not a quiet beach! A very detailed review of what to expect.
This is not the place you want to come if you are looking for a quiet place to sit and read a book. Instead, it is bustling and fun and loud and boasts a world-Class hotel across the street! Because of the hotel, there are lots of opportunities for food and cocktails and fresh clean bathrooms. It seems it’s totally fine to get a cocktail and stroll around on the beach. Either there are no open alcohol restrictions or everybody’s completely ignoring them.
Because the hotel resort complex is so high-end,  there are also opportunities for:
1. bonfires on the beach (you pay for seating and firewood, and there are places to dispose of your hot coals).
2.  S’more packages that you buy from a vendor as well
3. Popsicle and other vendors
4. Private Seating for your group in the plastic igloos (again extra charge)

So the entire atmosphere feels festive and fun! We went in December, and the water was certainly swimmable, if not a bit chilly.

Not a big beach for finding shells. Although there are some.

This feels like a real family beach, and not a big party beach, even though people were walking around with open containers!

The waves are not surfable. I suppose some people could try, but this is not a good surfing spot.

The beach is very wide, so if you want to play football or throw a frisbee, it’s an excellent beach for that.

I imagine in the summer that it is really quite packed! Even when we were there in December, it was quite packed. Please note we were there on a festive holiday weekend, and the hotel-resort was really showing off its splendor.

Beautiful beach that stretches pretty long. Visited in September and found it to be a beautiful spot to enjoy a sunny day. Wasn't overly crowded at all, and although I understand there are issues with Tijuana discharging waste towards the U.S., the water seemed clean.

Watch the seagulls. They try getting in to people bags on the beach. They seem to have gotten pretty good at it
Great place to visit. Beautiful beach and stunning views.
Beautiful long beach, which keeps it from feeling too busy, and one of the best spots close to town to enjoy the ocean. Great view of the Hotel del Coronado too!

name: Windansea Beach
formatted_address: Windansea Beach, San Diego, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text: it was beautiful, not to mention that there were little spots you can hide from people, it looks like the caribbean, it’s also a beach that a lot of teens go
Loved everything about this beach.  Perfect for relaxing, and watching the waves, picture taking, and of course the Sunset 🌇💛

Favourite beach during our stay in San Diego. Calm, nice water entry and a bit more of a private feel than some of the larger beaches. Enjoyed watching the surfers and boogie boarders while relaxing. Parking was easy in November, but I can see how it would be tricky in the busy months.

Windansea Beach is the MOST beautiful little hidden gem! The giant rock structures make it supremely more unique than any other beach that I’ve visited. The shore area is fairly small allowing minimal people to gather there. There are several benches on the side walk to sit and overlook the ocean. The water crashes against the giant rock structures creating a beautiful and majestic scene. This place is really one of a kind and a must see beach.
Such a beautiful hidden gem. The beach was pretty quiet when we visited. Beautiful waves and sunset.

name: Ocean Beach Tide Pools
formatted_address: 1779 Ocean Front St, San Diego, CA 92107, USA

google_reviews.text: Some nice views here and kids enjoyed finding crabs along the rocks at low tide. Walk down stairs and nice paved path along the beach. Can even get down to the water in some areas. Parking is very limited will need to park on street.

It was an amazing experience with the kiddos. But there were way to many people that were not being respectful of the marine life. I made sure my children knew not to touch or disturb any living creatures, but to just enjoy the experience. I hope more parents teach there kids these simple guidelines to keep the the wildlife there for everyone to enjoy in the future.

Went to the tidepools under OB pier during -1.4 king tides. It was spectacular as you could see tidepools that are submerged year round teaming with life. Tons of crabs, lobsters, fish anemones and even a grumpy octopus. Flashlights kept the fun going well into the evening. As if that wasn't awesome enough once we were done poking around we still had plenty of time to watch the OB holiday parade. What a night we had!
This place is awesome!  Kids have buckets collecting clams and the pools even have octopus that can be seen from time to time

The sky is a different wall of beauty every single night. Long Beach lights up 360⁰and the view changes depending on where you stand. There's the Catalina Island  way in the back and layers of boats and moving water moving and reflecting the lights, sky and moonlight just for You!

name: Powerhouse Park
formatted_address: 1658 Coast Blvd, Del Mar, CA 92014, USA

google_reviews.text: Stingray season started, hundreds of people every year get hurt big time at the Delmar beaches and I reported multiple times, Lifeguard, always always take a report from the injured person and no sign still ever been posted. What is going on? City Of Del Mar? Are you afraid? people are not going to be coming to the beach as that’s what I was told before , this is ridiculous. You need to educate people, especially parents And kids. Just shuffle your feet. Please place the warning sign about stingrays

Heaven on Earth , A most tranquil , naturally  breathtaking Eden that promotes wellbeing and mindfulness. Murray Hidary performed his Mind travel personal piano concert as the sunset and the waves lapped the shore. Beautiful

This is a beautiful place to hang out, whether going to the beach or at the park or community center. There is a lot offered. The beach and ocean is beautiful and everything is in walking distance. We very much enjoyed visiting.

There's a large grassy area overlooking the ocean, great for picnics. There's plenty of benches for watching the surfers. Music events are common over the summer. Paid parking is available across the street, but there's also street parking if you get lucky. There are several restaurants walkable for lunch.
Beautiful ocean side park with a great beach, bathroom, beach shop, and kids playground. Parking along street, with closer spots metered, and a very expensive public lot right next to the park.

name: Windansea Park
formatted_address: 6600 Neptune Pl, La Jolla, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text: Windansea Beach is absolutely stunning! The sand is soft and clean, and the atmosphere is relaxed yet lively—perfect for a peaceful walk or watching surfers in action. The sunsets here are breathtaking, painting the sky with incredible colors. The rocky coastline adds a unique, photogenic charm. Even though it’s well-known, the beach maintains a sense of calm and authenticity that sets it apart from the busier beaches in San Diego. A must-visit spot!

This is a beautiful park even if others are careless of their surroundings. I enjoyed the view and the rocks were nice to sit in, but as we were sitting enjoying the sunset, a couple came up behind us (less than 4 feet away) and stood and talked loudly while their baby cried. Please just have a little courtesy.
It's the place that made me fall in love with CA. It's beautiful in day and in night. I will definitely come back there to enjoy ocean and sunset. I recommend it strongly to all. See you soon CA.

Beautiful beach. I think the algae bloom or something was making it a little stinky tonight, but this is where there are photo shoots happening every evening it seems. Plus it is just a couple blocks from an awesome church, La Jolla Lutheran.
Amazing beach with sandstone throughout to break it up and great surfbreak! Powerful Waves crashing up against the sandstone is spectacular!

name: Stan's Beach
formatted_address: Coronado, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: So worth the drive from Tucson!  I love any time I can put my toes in the ocean.

It's a great beach but my only complaint is the lack of public restrooms. I had to walk 15 minutes to find a bathroom from where I parked which is totally unacceptable. Lots of hotels but mostly private unless you're staying there.
Great place to walk and enjoy your personal space
Great beach to take a stroll for sunset or anytime of day really. Parking is relatively easy and convenient

Definitely one of my favorite beaches in San Diego area. Came in the middle of the week and there were a lot of families but it was still quiet and relaxing. I've been here at all different times of the year, but late summer is definitely one of my favorites. Parking is usually pretty easy and the water is always beautiful.

name: Silver Strand State Beach
formatted_address: 5000 CA-75, Coronado, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: Super fun location with the ocean on one side and the bay on the other.  So much sand and great sunsets.  I found sand dollars just south of the campground.  The campground is just a concrete parking lot with electric and water hook ups.  A sewer truck comes around for a fee to empty your tanks.  Otherwise there's dump station on site for $10. I loved the bike trails just east of the campground that run from Coronado south to Imperial Beach.  Hosts were friendly and check in a breeze.  The only bummer was the polluted ocean and no water allowed water contact that happens often i read.

Pretty great state beach: clean, not crowded, parking was affordable, bathroom was clean, water dispenser was working and water was pretty good for tap. My only wish would be that the vendor vestibules were open so I could have had a drink while I watched the sunset.
Lovely beach with great parking spots and incredible views up and down the coast. Another one of the magical California places.

One tiny sign throughout the whole beach stating it was hazardous to go in the water due to sewage and chemical waste!
We didn’t see the sign until we were leaving. Thankfully we didn’t swim as it was too cold, but we did walk through the water. The beach is beautiful, but why is this beach even open at all?
Disgusting!

We love this beach! 🏖️ It’s super family-friendly, with plenty of space to play, build sandcastles, and soak up the sun without feeling crowded. The kids can run wild, the adults can relax, and everyone leaves with sandy toes and happy hearts.

The only catch? Shopping is basically a myth here. Forgot sunscreen? Snacks? A cute beach hat? Better luck next time—you’ll either have to plan ahead or improvise. But honestly, the trade-off is worth it. Peace, sun, and waves over crowds and shopping any day! 🌊😂

name: Mission Beach Boardwalk
formatted_address: W Mission Bay Dr, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: This was a great place to visit on a weekday morning! The parking wasn’t too crowded. Got a nice walk in! Wish they had a juice or smoothie place but they did have a small coffee shop! Located right next to Belmont park. I definitely could see this as a nice family outing.

This is a great beach and boardwalk that my wife and I enjoyed.  First, It offers an amusement park with rollercoasters, rides and arcades.  Secondly, the beach is clean and beautiful.  It’s a great place to hang out and go for a swim.  Third, it has 3 miles worth of path for exploring and walking.  Fourth, this beach has plenty of space for people interested in biking and/or skating.  Lastly, there are a ton of restaurants and shopping.  Literally, anything you could possible want, you can find here.

10-25-2025
I came here to attend Salsa On The Beach (SOTB), last gathering of the year. Loved it! Hosted by the lovely couple, Josh and Kristen, his beautiful partner. What a great activity to bring family and friends of all ages to enjoy music, dancing, healthy (n not so healthy 🤣) food and the changing colors of the sky!

Enjoy dancing y’all! : )
I loved this beach. It represents a true Cali style beach. The boardwalk offers lots of food and drink and is perfect for people watching.
loved seeing all the rides at Belmont Park, the old roller coaster. very nostalgic. lots of great little shops, restaurants, coffee shops, souvenir shops, just an all-around awesome vibe

name: Mission Beach, San Diego
formatted_address: 3100, Ocean Front Walk, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: Mission Beach is on of my favorite parts of California!  It has an amazing vibe and feel.  The sand is soft and comfortable to lay in.  The boardwalk is active and wide enough for everyone of all types to enjoy. The waves are a great size for surfers to enjoy.  This is an ideal place to visit, vacation at, and live!

This place is great! the sunsets are awesome! The place is relatively clean the beach was a little dirty but not too bad the crowds were pretty heavy but not overwhelming the only complaint I would have is the weed smell totally run the entire day we will probably come back but it's not real high on our list because of the weed smell tired of being forced to smoke other people's drugs

Enjoyed one of my last trips ever visiting my younger brother shortly before he retired and then unexpectantly died of stroke.  Very nice place, great trip, wish we could have him back again.  The beach is clean, always seems to be right temperature, waters are fairly comfortably warmer there than farther north, loved the pelican and seagulls flying over.  And lots of stores, shopping, great restaurants all around near it.  My brother adopted and loved his daughter Grace, he loved and tried to help most everyone, was very smart at technology.  We now have his elder dog and cat for a little while before their ages get them.

One of the Most excellent places to visit year round. My Family and come all the time. The people,sights, sounds and it's always great with a Beach. At night is amazing. Camp fires,Bacon wrapped Hot dogs,churros ECT .I Reccomend World Famous Seafood,right next to The Surfer Hotel. 2 words Lobster Bisque. The Bread Pudding Dessert is Absolutely delightful. Needless to say, Mission Beach is enjoyable,relaxing and Fun

Whenever I come down from the Los Angeles area, this is a must place to stop. The vibes are great here and the people are awesome. Great places to eat, beach parties, volleyball, entertainment, family fun, amazing views, etc. Mission Beach has it all.

name: Imperial Beach Pier
formatted_address: 910 Seacoast Dr, Imperial Beach, CA 91932, USA

google_reviews.text:
Hey Google Fans, if you're looking for a nice stroll or morning/afternoon jog at a quiet beach, then Imperial Beach is the right spot to visit. The sound of the peaceful ocean waves, view is breath taking to capture photos, and beaming sun to get a nice tan. The neighborhood of Imperial itself is coming along beautifully with restaurants, outdoor spaces, and of course the ocean.
well maintained curb appeal. In addition, the well maintained curb appeal followed by the use of color scheme and exterior decorum is giving beach vibes. From the moment you pull up to park there is ton of street parking available with a few parking spaces closer to the beach (I'm sure payment is required). Overall, I would definitely recommend this destination to you to come out one day to enjoy.

We visited in the morning after breakfast. It is a beautiful spot for ocean views and we had a nice relaxed walk on the pier. We enjoyed watching the surfers. Had no difficulty getting street parking.

This was a very pleasant visit with some great ocean views. The pier offers a peaceful atmosphere and is a nice place to take a walk or enjoy the scenery. Be cautious around the seawater, as conditions can be hazardous. Paid parking is available for $2 for 8 hours, which is a great deal if you plan to stay a while.
Lovely place for a walk and watch the sunset. There is a variety of events happening. The car shows are really cool to attend. A relaxing vibe.

Best pier around here, good views,place to surf, lifeguard towers all around with lifeguards on duty, so swim with peace of mind. Kids love the area, they have bike rentals here, parking is a kinda tricky but not bad. The do have restrooms on the pier itself and showers by the beach. Also lots of fishing which is nice.

name: Shell Beach
formatted_address: Shell Beach, San Diego, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text: Lots of little creatures to see on the tide pools, rocks you can climb on for better view, nice clear waters. Really enjoyed our stop here
Nice views, near the area with sea lions.
Very pretty view over the pacific ocean! Especially for during the sunset!

Great spot to chill and watch the sun set. Open clean beach with lots of tide pools. Some stairs to go down . When u get to the bottom of them , it goes right to big bolders to walk on to the beach. Watch ur step at this point. Watched a lot of people lose there footing . Parking was ok for Jan. Some in the area

Busy location. Perfect to see the seals/sea lions and take pictures. Avoid going down to the beach when sea lions are present as they can attack you. Access down via stairs, highly recommend for simple seal watching and photos

name: Coronado Shores Beach

google_reviews.text: Awesome place. They have showers outside so to rinse off the sand/dirt. During covid 19 throughout M thru T is the best time to go as many people aren't out there or at least they are spread out. Water is cold/crisp at first but not freezing like pools especially when the temp is like 85+ Degrees Farenheit ! Pack a lunch and go enjoy. The seagulls are brave here as one tried to fly off with my keyssssss!
Nice beach. Not too crowded. Waves were nice from rains.
Astonishing sunrise perfect place for a magnificent walk and relax enjoying the nature with the wonderful birds.
Nice beach. Clean, not crowded, clean water.
I visit this beach in the late Fall and Winter when the tides is low. The whole beach looks like a reflection!

name: Coronado Dog Beach
formatted_address: Ocean Blvd, San Diego, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: I loved the sunset here, this beach was less crowded, good chill vibes, felt very laidback. I also saw some people doing bonfires n bbq which was dope!

Amazing beach!
We went on a Saturday around 5pm and it was still so open and free. Plenty of space and super clean!

Watching the sunset here was definitely worth it! Tons of friendly dogs off leash and everyone is respectful!

Also tons of cleaning stations to wash the sand off your feet! And clean your pups!
Coronado Dog Beach has shallow waves and plenty of space for the dogs. Trash cans are available. Lovely place for sunset, with clear view of the Coronado Islands.

Beautiful beach. They maintain it very clean.  Parking can be difficult, but worth the visit. Visited a few beaches in San Diego and I know technically this is not San Diego, but by far my favorite. Best part, you may see a fighter jet or 2.

Good weather good place.👍👍👍 It's a perfect place to unwind and enjoy the beauty of the California coast, while also letting your dog have the time of their life. The sound of the waves and the sight of dogs playing is incredibly soothing. A great place to visit!"

name: South Casa Beach
formatted_address: South Casa Beach, San Diego, CA 92037, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful place to watch the sunset or just enjoy the ocean. If you come in the morning you have a good chance of seeing some seals.

We enjoyed talking a walk along this area to La Jolla Cove. The stretch of side was easily walkable with great views of the ocean, wildlife, and the cliffs and beaches below. The beach at this stretch is not very big, but it is accessible. There is a staircase that leads from the sidewalk to the beach. There is a lifeguard hut here as well. However, I'm not sure about their hours of operation, so I'd check ahead if that is a concern for you. Also there isn't a formal parking lot nearby. From what I could tell, it was all street parking only.

We took a friend of ours visiting from Virginia there over the weekend. It was beautiful. Quite a few people there but not too bad. The Children’s Pool observation walkway was closed. Looked like part of the railing at the end had fallen.
Visiting from Maryland on business and went out to relax and enjoy the view. Absolutely spectacular!

Nice location, water full of kelp and beach full of seagulls, but very pretty, and we set up our towels in the shadow of the short cliff. Parking and driving around La Jolla was miserable, however. Possibly due to being between holidays (christmas and new years), possibly always like that? Gridlock upon leaving. Wanted to see the cave, but a line out the door. Whole town was super busy! Children's pool closed due to seals, which drew a crowd also. Overall, an experience. Beautiful area but too many people/cars.

name: Ocean Beach
formatted_address: 1484 Pescadero Dr, San Diego, CA 92107, USA

google_reviews.text: I am very happy to live here! The local scene is very laaaiiiidd back. Sure there are ups and downs anywhere you go but I'm gonna just mention some ups. You can't beat the weather, food, drink and music scene. Farmers market every Wednesday, musicians playing in the streets. Not to mention the beaches and the Pacific Ocean, which is the largest ocean on this planet. And Dog Beach is the first leash free beach in the United States. The pier, which is sad to have it temporarly closed, is the longest concrete structure off of the West Coast. Just some fun facts for ya. For a local it's all within walking distance. Good for stumbling home. Check it out! It's awesome!

OB is always good for the soul! Downtown is lively with plenty of food options, the beach is huge with lots of room, & whether you walk North to Dog Beach or South to Sunset Cliffs, you're going to have a beautiful day!

Ocean Beach is amazing as a beach, and visually stunning. The water is neither too warm nor too cold. The waves are just right, not too strong and not too calm. Unfortunately, the restrooms are extremely dirty and almost unusable. There is free parking, but you do need to find an available spot. You do not really have to worry about parking hours because the rules are not strictly enforced, so it is flexible. The vibe here is great, relaxed and laid-back. You can just hang out however you like. It is definitely worth a visit. Just keep in mind that the cleanliness of the area and the restrooms is terrible. You can make a fire if you find an open fire pit. The sunset is breathtaking and I highly recommend watching it.
There are places where you can take a shower.
Beach access and parking are free. You don’t have to pay for anything at all — not for showers, not for restrooms

I have lived there in the 80s. I've had the time of my  life!! I can now see some changes there, but it will always be a fun, hippy style town! I've never been happier! I'll come back, soon in a heartbeat 💓 love all my ' obeacians forever!!) I'll be back!! Hopefully real soon!! A memory that will never, ever, fade away!!....

Ocean Beach is a beautiful and relaxed beach with a laid-back vibe. The sand is soft and the water is perfect for swimming, surfing, or just relaxing on the shore. I especially enjoyed watching the sunset over the water and seeing people surfing. The beach is also conveniently located near many restaurants and shops, so you can easily grab a bite to eat or do some souvenir shopping after your beach day.

If you're looking for a place to escape the hustle and bustle of the city and enjoy some quality time by the ocean, Ocean Beach is the perfect spot!

name: Del Mar Shores Stairway to Solana Beach

google_reviews.text: Had a great trip to Solana Beach with our 2 dogs! Rules about dogs differ depending on the time of year….and time of day ~ posted signs attempt to explain! Pros: Great of you're looking for a beach that's not so crowded and you want your dog (on leash during the summer) with you.

Cons:  Little parking.  There 2 very small (12 car) public parking that's free.  If you cant find it in there, then good luck.  No bathrooms.  Lots and lots of stairs (not for the out of shape or if you have a lot to carry).

Keep in mind this is around the bend from Delmar dog beach.  So if you had a bathroom emergency then you could walk the 150 yards to the dog beach port a potties.
What a great happy 😊 place to take your pup!  The leash free dog park was wonderful 🐕 How could anyone be in a bad mood here?  Highly recommend for any dog lover 👍 👍
Beautiful beach that was not very busy on a Monday afternoon. Good place to dip your feet in the water.
I walked here on a cold, rainy morning and watched the dog walkers and surfers. 10/10 vibes.

name: Blacks Beach Park

google_reviews.text: Black's Beach in La Jolla is a stunning, secluded beach known for its dramatic cliffs and breathtaking ocean views. It’s popular with surfers and nature lovers, and offers a unique, tranquil atmosphere. Just be prepared for the steep hike down, but it's well worth the effort for the beautiful setting! Be ready to climb!

Great beach. Nice and quiet except for the fighter jets which was super cool. Seaweed was minimal. Great views too. Be prepared to get the ol heart rate up climbing back up from the beach tho. Not for the faint of heart.

Only off is, they should build more restrooms not just let people turn to back bushes OMG.

It's a free beach you can sit and watch the sunset, play, photo ops, ect the water is so clear you can see the bottom. It's a must see!!!

What a super cool place to visit if you're in the San Diego LaHolla area... Submerge yourself in the refreshing deep blue waters of the OP. Take a walk along the entire beach area. Sit and relax while you hear the sound of the crashing waves hitting the rocks. Take the perfect postal picture, and enjoy watching the different sea birds, and the great number of sea lions basking in the sun, and swimming very close to you. What a treat.

This is a tourist attraction?

name: Coronado Beach
formatted_address: 920 Ocean Blvd, Coronado, CA 92118, USA

google_reviews.text: A beautiful beach. The sand was nice and cool. A very large beach with plenty of lounge chairs and umbrellas. We went for a walk on the beach and it was simply beautiful.

Coronado Beach in California is a beautiful, clean, and breezy spot — perfect for a relaxing day by the ocean. The sand is soft and well-maintained, and there’s plenty of open space to walk, lounge, or play a game of beach volleyball. It’s peaceful and scenic, with stunning views and a laid-back vibe.

What’s missing is a bit of convenience — there aren’t many washrooms, and there’s no boardwalk or much in the way of food stalls or snacks nearby. It’s best to come prepared.

Still, if you’re looking for a calm, clean beach experience without the crowds or chaos, Coronado Beach delivers. Just pack your own snacks and enjoy the fresh air!  Overall a good experience . 4 stars ⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️
One of our favorite beaches in Cali. Very clean, not too crowded, and beautiful surrounding areas!

google_reviews.text: Such a beautiful beach! So many great seashell finds in the winter time. The beach is massive, great for a long run or walk. The waves were perfect for some buggy boarding adventures! Can’t wait to be back!

name: Del Mar Dog Beach

google_reviews.text: Del Mar dog beach is a fantastic place to go daytime or to catch a beautiful California sunset. The beach is clean, large enough both at high and low tide to fit a large amount of people and dogs comfortably. Most dog owners are responsible and take care of their dogs as well as cleaning up after them. Our dog always finds a friend to run around with and by far is his favorite place to go in San Diego. I would absolutely recommend this place to anyone looking to take their dog to the beach or someone who wants to enjoy the beach and the company of dogs running around. Whatever you decide, beautiful place to visit and capture some amazing sunsets.

Amazing dog park and is off leash rules for the winter months. My little furries loved it and met all kinds of nice people with their nice dogs. Only a few huskies that were out not listening and stirring things up of which we stayed clear. It was a beautiful couple days and we will definitely be back. Wow!

My year and a half puppy loves Del Mar dog beach! From 6am-8am parking is free, and there are a ton of sweet dogs to play with. Dogs must be on-leash during the summer but starting Labor Day, they are allowed off leash. My dog is deathly afraid of the water but the sandy area is huge, so he has plenty of room to run around and walk up and down the beach. Del Mar is beautiful and the beach is full of dogs that are having the time of their lives!

Sunday morning at Dog Beach is a joyful experience, particularly at low tide when the beach is even wider! If you drive, get back before your parking permit expires and end your stay refreshed. Parking is strictly enforced - I watched someone get a ticket as they were walking back to their car.
Wonderful area dogs everywhere lol Everyone and the dogs were all nice. Went around 10am parking good could see it getting harder to get a space, have port a Pottys, bring water have a grand day!

This is a really nice place and this is the first time that I came here. The huge trees are absolutely magnificent and it's very peaceful. Every so often a large plane flies over above.

We were only here a short while so I didn't get to walk the whole area but I think I got some pretty good pictures of some nice areas that aren't seen when just driving around on the road.

Nice little park. Great place to walk around. Nice neighborhood too.
You can hear and see the planes coming into Lindbergh Field.
Nice park, love the scenery and environment.

name: Ward Canyon Park

google_reviews.text: This lovely neighborhood park has a paved walking path and many shady mature trees to shade you from the sun. There is a fenced-off area for the dog park, and there are plenty of balls to play with (like an informal lending library).
Really nice for night walks, it gives me this old nostalgia I cannot explain.

This is a great park that can be enjoyed by everyone! Read a book. Soak in the sun. Walk. Jog. Play basketball. Yoga. There is a tot play area.. Tables. Grills...please be sure to properly dispose of your charcoal.  Spend a moment or spend an afternoon...great park!
This park is full of beautiful trees, has a large grassy area, playground, walking trail and restrooms! It's not a neighborhood we are in often and made for a pleasant discovery!
Such a cute little park. Great recreation areas with seating, tables, shade trees, beautiful flowers, grass, a concrete path around the perimeter, and even a small amphitheater.

name: Torrey Hills Park
formatted_address: 4260 Calle Mejillones, San Diego, CA 92130, USA

google_reviews.text: This little park is a lovely spot for a walk, with a great playground, a gazebo, and areas perfect for small gatherings. Restrooms are available too. Used to visit in March for the lupines, but right now it’s the yellow flowers in bloom—just as beautiful

A Great Spot for Kids and Dogs, but Some Facilities Need Repairs

Torrey Hills Park is an excellent place for both children and dogs to play and explore, but some of the facilities are showing signs of wear and tear. The park offers plenty of parking and features a large soccer field (though the grass isn’t in the best condition), a basketball court, baseball field, marble picnic tables, and a children’s playground with swings and slides.

Unfortunately, two of the slides have been boarded up with wood and chains, likely due to disrepair, and are awaiting maintenance. The metal walkway on the playground, however, seems to be a favorite spot for dogs to hang out.

There’s also a large staircase that leads up to a hilltop, offering a small hiking opportunity. At the top, you’ll find the Ocean Air substation, and you can follow a loop trail that connects back to the park through the streets behind the hill. On clear days without fog, the view from the hilltop is amazing—you can see not only the greenery and nearby houses, but also the Pacific Ocean in the distance.

One caution for dog owners: while the park itself is clear, the entrance area and the greenbelt along the streets behind the hill have some fox tails, which could pose a threat to dogs. Overall, Torrey Hills Park is a nice spot for outdoor fun, but it would be even better if the facilities were updated and repaired soon.
A hidden gem in the SD Parks and Rec. Truly my favorite park in town. Great playground, open space, good basketball courts and lots of walking trails. Such a great place for the whole family.

2025-1026: A great park for little kids, featuring a shaded playground under small trees. While the first walking trail was a bit of a disappointment as it leads to a busy road, the second trail offers a pleasant, moderately steep hike up a hill. The view from the top makes the climb worthwhile.
Great park! Nice walking trail around the perimeter that includes an uphill path. Well kept and clean at the playground and sports fields. Parks and Recs employees are friendly and informative.

"Google. Take me to the nearest park. Now!" This never failed. My nephews loved the park and had the best time. Safe park in a safe neighborhood. People here are friendly. Other kids here are well taught and respectful. Such a good experience to a new park. We will definitely explore more parks around.

Clean, peaceful, calm, and has clean restrooms that are always appropriately stocked with a Functional hand dryer that actually blows hot air!

I remember coming here as a kid. Now I'm taking my own children. We just went there for my grandmas birthday and this park has everything you need. Very big field, basketball court, bathrooms, playground, lots of tables and tress with shade. I need to learn how to get a permit here to get a jumper for all the kids.

name: Howard Lane Park
formatted_address: 1626 Dairy Mart Rd, San Diego, CA 92173, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to go walk your dog 🐕 The park has 2 basketball courts a big field for soccer and a nice playground

We love the park.
But no restroom here.

This park is good for kids and family. Sidewalk is large enough for bikes. In the morning it is great for walking but in the evenings it can get a bit fuller especially in the summer. I would not recommend coming here at night alone because I have heard some stories of it not being very safe at night. But if you are with someone you would be fine and I have never had a problem.

Playing basketball here is really fun especially when I'm hitting nothing but net with the exception of one or two rim shots. I'm still good at Ball after all these years that have gone bye. But stop on by with your wife and children and have a great peaceful time... Watching the San Diego trolley pass bye every 7 to 15 minutes. It's a beautiful scenery..

Very clean and well kept. Not giving it a perfect score bc there are no restrooms and thats one of the things I look for since I have three girls who can't hold it. Lol. Aside from that, I love this park. You get to see the trolley pass every 5 to 10 minutes as well. My girls love it but if you're looking to go for a quiet break then... you might want to look for another park.

name: Kensington Park
formatted_address: 4121 Adams Ave, San Diego, CA 92116, USA

google_reviews.text: We have been here B4 & the little park next to the Library is Cute but Unless the Library is Open there is No Bathroom. There is a Starbucks across the street & Many Cute Small Restaurants in this Nice Area! Mostly We Enjoyed looking for the Decorated  Yards & Houses! Some Went All Out & Our Granddaughter 4, Was Over The Moon for those Special Ones! Two We interacted with Went Out of Their Way for Her & I Wish We Would Have asked their Name to Say A Special Thank You! One was a Gentleman whose Vintage Car had Santa & His Reindeer Attached to It! She got to Sit on the Car & Touch the Reindeer! One House had Gifts Out for Any Kids that Came by, Candy Kisses, Stickers & little Stamps kids put on their hands! This home Even had TV Screens on the Roof with Video loops of a Snowman on one side & Santa w/his Reindeer Flying! The Rest was So Lit Up with Both Blow Ups & Tons of lights, it Was Spectacular! The Woman & Her Daughters arrived home just as we were walking by & while we were Complimenting the Hard Work, She said it was started on Nov. 1st I 100%  Believe it! That's when Her & Her Lovely Daughter's brought Her Goodie Table Out & Our Granddaughter Went Wild Over that Extra Nice Gesture Too! I Thank the People we Met & just Talked with along Our "Walk!" (My hubby & I have difficulty Walking, so we used Our "Scooters") Our Daughter, Granddaughter Walked with Us. We had So Much Fun & Can't Say Thank You Enough to All those Who We even just Enjoyed Wishing Merry Christmas & Happy New Year To!👍😁

This tiny park is adjacent to the public library and is a great place for small kids to play. Many other public parks are so large that kids tend not to interact with each other. This park is just the right size, offering kids an opportunity to learn how to share and make new friends. My daughter loves this park!

Lovely cutesy park. Great for my little one to run around because it is completely gated. Library is right next to it so free WiFi. Starbucks is across the street, and there is also a cafe, and little shops. The one way street is a tough spot to park so just park further and have a nice walk.

I love this community, people are friendly. Starbucks and the library are very close. The Starbucks is the best. The community has a park. Bring the children and Grandma/pa safe and exciting. I want to own real estate here. Great place for families.

Kensington Park is a fantastic little neighborhood playground. What it lacks in size and equipment variety, it makes up for in excellent event programming (both through the library and the Ken-Tal association) and proximity to other amenities (food, coffee, wine, beer, and ice cream to name a few).

This is an excellent location to bring your friends/family for a meal in the park. There is often music being played on the grassy side of the field. Bring a picnic blanket, a book and camp or for a few hours.

Tip: there isn't much seating, and very few tables, so be prepared to sit and eat on the ground.

Parking behind the playground can get scarce on event days, but there should be plenty down one of the side streets.

name: Memorial Park
formatted_address: 373 Park Way, Chula Vista, CA 91910, USA

google_reviews.text:
I have driven by this park hundreds of times, but this time I finally took the time to visit it while I enjoyed a locally roast cup of coffee from a nearby coffee shop.

The park offers a nice lush well maintained green belt. The park has a very nice amphitheater with a cool stage as well. It offers restrooms, plenty of mature trees and palm trees for shade, and a nice walking or running area that cuts through and around the park.

I did spot a few homeless people using the facilities and an individual crashed out in the shade of a tree, but who can blame him for that. Nice park.

just something about this place has incomparable energy. Whether you are Solo or with a significant other or just a family outing, this place always provides a great atmosphere and it's very well maintained. It's a hub for a vast amount of places to eat in the perimeter. Favorite park in the whole South Bay forsure

Memorial Park on Third Avenue is hands down my favorite park in Chula Vista. It’s well-maintained and constantly cleaned, and I have to give a special shoutout to the gardeners who do a fantastic job keeping the park beautiful.

One of the standout features is the open, ungated dog park. It’s bright and sunny, with lovely grassy knolls that make it perfect for laying out a blanket while your dog runs around and enjoys the open space. If your dog is on the wild side, just keep in mind that there are no fences to contain them. Fortunately, there are plenty of trash cans for easy waste disposal, which helps keep the area clean.

I do wish there were more benches throughout the park to sit and take in the scenery. If you’re lucky, you might even spot wild parrots flying through the park and perching in the treetops—it’s an amazing sight (and sound) to experience!

There is a public restroom, but unfortunately, it’s not the cleanest, so I’d avoid using it if possible. On the plus side, the amphitheater is a great addition to the park, and you can often catch small events there, especially on Sundays.

Another highlight is the lively farmers market held on the street parallel to the park. It’s a fantastic event that draws in a great crowd and adds to the vibrant atmosphere of the area.

Overall, Memorial Park is a gem in downtown Chula Vista—a great place to relax, bring your dog, and enjoy the community events. With a few small improvements, like more seating and cleaner restrooms, it could be even better!
Very clean park! Dog and children friendly. Every Sunday morning there is a farmers market and it’s such a perfect place to bring a blanket and people watch or relax in nature.
Great place to bring your dog for a walk.

name: Civita Park
formatted_address: 7960 Civita Blvd, San Diego, CA 92108, USA

google_reviews.text: Was hoping to find a FENCED dog park but it never made itself known, lol. However, the landscaping is gorgeous, many CNP, nice hidden statuary surprises, long viewing vista's, numerous picnic areas, great and EZ parking, amazingly clean and well maintained restrooms.  Worst part was we accidentally turned into the school driveway (immediately next driveway) while parents were waiting for kids to be released DONT MAKE our mistake!!!

It’s always a wonderful time at the stunning Civita Park. Lots of playground activities. Lots of field space to play sports. Many people host birthday and other types of events. Very clean and safe, lots of well attended to restrooms. Dog friendly on leash and there is a dog park at the very top of the stairs. Highly recommend, definitely one of the top San Diego parks if not number one.

Visited with the Walk It Out group — a truly beautiful and enjoyable experience.
The walking paths offer clear and easy progression through levels one to five, making it accessible for all fitness levels. The grounds are pet-friendly and exceptionally well-maintained, with vibrant foliage and scenic views throughout. A peaceful and inviting place to move, relax, and enjoy nature.
Beautiful and clean park. Not a lot of riff raff at this park which makes it a favorite for us. They have a farmers market on Saturdays as well.

Completely accessible. Built through a valley on a hill, so may be difficult for self-propelled wheelchairs in people with less stamina. Absolutely gorgeous park, lush with flowers and a whole vast variety of plants. Nice and clean, even clean restrooms. Quiet and peaceful for the amount of people there. Absolutely delightful.

name: Woodglen Vista Park

google_reviews.text: My son loves to come here. It's my local park. They'd get a 5 star is they'd cut the grass and fill the potholes. Someone could get hurt stepping in them.
This park is beautiful but the playground needs to be updated.

This park has vibrant life by it with a butterfly exhibit right next to it and tons of activities to do like tennis basketball soccer dog play area and a public bathroom and like always every public bathroom isn't good but it does its best. Plus they have a skate park and a playground which makes this area a great area for kids to roam freely without a danger of any dangerous acts. To finish this great park out it also has an ice cream truck that comes at 1600 to 1800, which I can say this is the most safest and peaceful park out there

Beautiful park. Lots of shade trees. Plenty of places have a picnic & places for kids to play. There is a section dedicated to dogs.  I found this park while playing Jurassic World Alive. Several EPIC dinosaurs spawned here.

Seems okay, lot of undesirables lingering around but it is what it is.
Not much lighting so definitely a daytime only park.
Skatepark is decent too but no lights as well.

name: Kimball Park

google_reviews.text: This park is amazing. It's large and great for the whole family. Baseball fields, skate park, picnic benches and also lots of trees and shaded areas. This place usually has more traffic than my picture show. I was here early this morning. They had a little leage game going on.
Veterans Day Parade ended at the park. Great band .. Food trucks.. Friendly people. Clean bathroom ( mens)

I would like to say thank you to those that over the grounds of Kimball Park I must say that you guys got the place looking really good. I see that the Lillie ones very happy to play around the park and make memories and that's what it's about families enjoying time with there love ones I have so many memories there when I use to take my kids there when they were small and they enjoyed every minute of it thank you Kimball Park
The park was clean and a great place to walk.

Went to the carnival 4th of July. Nice large park with plenty to do. Large grassy areas, skatepark and baseball. Went to the Holiday carnival and tree lighting. Exceptional fun for the entire family. Adjacent to the library.

name: Pioneer Park
formatted_address: 1521 Washington Pl, San Diego, CA 92103, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice park, very clean, very spooky at night. The owls played their parts real well during the tour. If you want to unknowingly walk over a thousand bodies its fantastic.
I love this park. expansive green space, good playgrounds for kids, an interesting if slightly eerie graveyard in the corner. Come set up with a chair and enjoy the sun, read a book.

Beautiful park that I visited for the very first time, but discovered the park has a very creepy secret given that it was a cemetery, and they just moved the headstones but not the bodies. This fact adds a bit of serenity to the area given its the resting place for a lot of people. So, it's just something to keep in mind if you're planning on going here, depending on what your comfort level is with that side of the park's history.

I'm giving 5 stars mostly because I LOVE the huge magnolia tree!!  I also love the mesquite trees because they remind me of home (Texas). I guess the trees just remind me of the south and I miss it a little. I was also very interested in the history there and seeing the gravestones, although its a little sad that the bodies were never moved and the park where kids are playing and people are walking around hanging out is still a graveyard with bodies (apparently only some of the headstones were moved to one side section). If you can get past that, its a good (small) park to bring the kids and/or dog, or just chill. There's a playground on one side thats kind of separated from the actual park area by the restrooms.
Definitely a cool spot for people who like large, old trees or people wanting to see or feel a presence at night (so I've heard).
I just wish the parents would keep their kids off the headstones!! Pretty disrespectful!

Best fact.  Park built over a old cemetary. There are dozens or tombstones toward the back. I guess there was a old school there and the faculty was put to rest there . Very pretty and slightly spoky.

Behind the tombstones there is a small open area hidden from sight . High school kids would frequent this area at night to drink and well.... you remember hopefully . This spot has fast access to Washington st going down the hill for quick getaway if cops come for you. Just dont be to loud especially in the summer two houses are ear shot distance ..

Scenic park with playground, exercise equipment and paved sidewalk.  There are some benches. Plenty of open grass ..

My only complaint is the homeless camp out in the day time and bathrooms are not up to Okay standards. Likely only cleaned once a day.  But hey at least they open. !! If the closed go pee in the hidden area behind tombstones

name: Crown Point Park
formatted_address: Crown Point Dr, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text: Connect English students LOVE the bonfires that we hold all through the summer at Crown Point Park. Many students say that it is/was their favorite experience in San Diego while they are in the USA studying.

I attended the JACL's anniversary event at Crown Point Beach in Mission Bay Park. We all ate, chatted and enjoyed the view of the sea together. This park is really beautiful. There are no big waves and it's very calm. It's very suitable for family gatherings. It's also suitable for children to swim and do water sports.
Great place for running, cycling, playing volleyball, basketball, or having a picnic. Two different public restroom buildings in the area and plenty of parking.
Beautiful area. Loved seeing all of the dogs playing in the evening!
Quiet open space with pleanty of parking & tables. A mix of grass, sand and lots of shade trees.

name: Wells Park
