google_reviews.text: I take my dog to the dog park and the big dog area has gone downhill with dogs digging and maintenance not filling or posting signage. I also have been using the new Pickleball courts and even got a membership so I can reserve courts. The City is making improvements hope they can fix the dog parks.

I bring my jack Russell here all the time. It’s a great dog park. It’s split between little dogs and big dogs. There’s three water fountains for dogs to be able to drink Wow rest rooms Yeah, he sounds like for you when you leave and everything is enclosed and enclosed inside the second fence, so the dogs can’t run away. Great open area for family friends, and your dog to get out and run rules are posted inside and outside for dog owners. Make sure and bring bags to pick uo your poop.

City of El Cajon latest and greatest public pickleball venue. Individually fenced courts. 4 open play, 1 challenge/reserve able court. Lights come on gradually before you notice its even dark. Super clean, new and nice. They have lots of parking and bathrooms. I posted the rules and hours in photos. No court monitor so good luck with the frey. If you want to reserve courts join the Parks and Rec Pro pickleball membership for a low price for a year membership. Then you can log into their reservation system and reserve courts upto 7 days in advance. You can reserve 1 court a day. It is very popular so be sure to get on there at Midnight 7 days in advance and best of luck because it is practically impossible now to reserve regular court hours. The same people have reservations everyday.

This place is amazing and very clean! So beautiful and the dogs loved it. So many people are here in the mornings it’s fun getting to know everyone and the dogs play like crazy the dog park is so big and all dogs are off leash so they can run and play together.

Wells Park, located in El Cajon now has a new public pickle ball venue! Facility consists of five fenced courts; four for open play and one court that is reserve able via the paid membership app. Very clean courts with restroom adjacent. Lots of benches, parking, shade clothes, water and bottle filler. Very small shop but does have cold water and Gatorade along with a few snacks available for purchase. If you want to reserve court the one court you will need to join via the Parks and Rec Pro pickle ball membership for an annual membership. Big plus is the lights at night. Courts are open from 8:00 am to 9:00pm.

name: Parkside Park
formatted_address: 6240 Parkside Ave, San Diego, CA 92139, USA

google_reviews.text:
Very clean with a wide flat grassy area. I think it's almost perfect for events. I don't see a restroom and if there is one it's out of sight of the playground. There's plenty of seating but virtually zero shade.
Add a restroom near the playground and more shade (artificial or trees). 😊

Beautiful park for kids (little and bigger), dogs, big open field area, basket ball courts, two play grounds for the different size kids and swing sets. I came here as a little girl and now as an adult can take my little sister.

No restrooms available and parking can be very limited during busy hours (4-6 p.m. on weeknights). Field is perfect for a quick soccer game or game of catch. Playground is nice and entertaining for a wide range of ages. Lots of swings. Basketball court usually has a busy vibe but people are usually pretty welcoming for a quick match.

This is a nice big neighborhood park. It has a lot of great equipment for children, a large grassy area for outdoor sports, picnic tables, and a basketball court. It is wheelchair accessible from the an upper tier housing area and also from the main entrance; which in itself is expansive. A long cement walkway winds through a large housing development. This walkway is great for bikes, scooters, running and walking.

Would love to see additional improvements but this park is always a joy it’s clean and many members from the community utilize it for sports exercise, kids playground, picnics and gatherings. I encourage everyone who wants to see more improvements and upgrades to contact parks and rec and ask for them to do the upgrades and repairs needed. Attending the community meetings at the rec center also helps !

name: North Park Community Park
formatted_address: Howard Ave, San Diego, CA 92104, USA

google_reviews.text: My adult daughter loves this park because it reminds her of her father who's no longer with us. We used to live in the area. We make it a point to stop by after seeing live music at the observatory down the street.

This is a small park in a really nice neighborhood. Pet friendly with a designated area fenced in for dogs to run around. Playground for small children. Bathrooms are usually locked. Water fountains, baseball and tennis courts here. Homeless people sleep here and nobody really bothers them at night. It's a pretty safe area. The have NA meetings here in the park everyday at noon.
Medium sized park, only one bench, too picnic tables, plenty of trash cans, two eucalyptus trees, other smaller trees, one chill homeless person. Mowed grass.

It is a good park but there are some things to be aware of. Review of the park itself, the dog park, and the playground

THE PARK: I live a block from this park and come every day for both my dog and 1 year old child.

The park does have nicely maintained grass. Tons of squirrels live here which entertain the local dogs. It’s nicely shaded with lots of trees.

Don’t come here if off leash dogs bother you. People use it as an off leash dog park in the morning and after work. Often the dogs will run up to my dog (who is leashed).

There are sometimes unhoused people here using it as a space to sleep. Mostly just during the week days.

DOG PARK: The dog park is okay, I’m glad it’s there but it’s definitely not worth making a special trip for it. It is a dirt park and it has tree roots that cause a major tripping hazard for the dogs.

PLAYGROUND: The playground is the best part in my opinion. Now that my child is 1 we go there and let him play. The baby swings are a nice feature. It has good stuff for a toddler to play with.

North park community park is my favorite park in the area!
Filled with green grass and large trees with squirrels, this park is great for an early jog through the morning dew, bonding with the  dogs, picnics or family celebrations on the weekend.
With two dog parks and playgrounds for kids, North Park Community Park is just that, safe for families fun for dogs and great place to meet the neighbors.

name: Spanish Landing Park (West)
formatted_address: 4200 N Harbor Dr, San Diego, CA 92101, USA

google_reviews.text: Spanish Landing Park on San Diego's vibrant waterfront boasts a sandy beach, public art displays and pristine marina views. Its unique location gives visitors an glance of an important moment in the history of San Diego: the 1769 meeting of the Gaspar DePortola and Father Junípero Serra expeditions.
Good place to chill while waiting for someone at the airport. Restrooms available. Water fountain and plenty of parking, and trash cans.

One of my favorite places to paddle board. It’ll cost you about a dollar an hour to park. Completely worth it for a sand beach, bathrooms, and shower. They have park benches as well. I love being able to do all my prep for paddle boarding on the grassy hill.

What a peaceful trail.
Easy to walk with kids, and you can have a break at the picnic table. There is a small beach next to the trail. Parking space is widely opened to the public.

This is a great place to park just to walk along the water, but it also is an amazing place to park if you have to pop into the airport on business. I parked here to get my global entry interview done and saved about 10 bucks.

name: Silver Terrace Park
formatted_address: Silver Terrace Park, 5550 Friars Rd, San Diego, CA 92110, USA

google_reviews.text: Such a cute little park that has parking and fencing for the littles! However there is no public restroom.
It's a very nice park it's very small it's a neighborhood park very clean nice and green good place to kill time while you're waiting to check in to a hotel like I did no restrooms
Small park with a good size playground. A fence blocks off most of the parking lot. The ground is covered with wood chips, no sand. Two infant swings. Everything seems to be in good condition.
It is weird that they do not have fence on the end of the park, it is like half finished.  Suggest to dog owner if dog is well trained, not run away type of dogs.
Very nice little park w kid friendly play area, lots of benches n a BBQ too!

name: Shadow Hill Park
formatted_address: Shadow Hill Pk, 9161 Shadow Hill Rd, Santee, CA 92071, USA

google_reviews.text: Husband and I went for the tennis courts about an hour or so before sundown. There were quite a few people there but it didn't feel noisy or crowded. Even with the kids there. Sad there's only 2 tennis courts but they have bathrooms and tables by the playground. Thinking about going back to do a picnic/tennis date 😊 Definitely wanna go back

We love this park. It's peaceful and fun for our kids! There is a concrete walkway around the perimeter. This is convenient for kids on scooters or people walking their dogs on a leash. They have a basketball court and covered area so that you can eat in the shade. There is plenty of parking.

We went there yesterday to check the park. My neighbor told me that it is a good park. My son was having fun running around. Nice park. Not so crowded when we went there.
Update. It's still one of the good parks to go to close to Santee and Lakeside. It still amazing place to go with the kids. My kids love it. It's very quiet,  not a lot of people here.

Beautiful park and playground has things we haven’t seen at many other parks. My son loved it and he gets bored easily. Swings and canopy above. My son’s favorite: a glider and monkey bars. It’s amazing how many parks don’t have monkey bars.

Playground had lots of seating for parents, yet another thing many parks don’t have.

Beautiful nature and trees and lots of shade, great place for picnics. Decent parking lot and bathroom.

Would definitely come back.
Park was not crowded. Benches and picnic tables are set apart perfectly to follow social distancing guidelines. Small park but spacious enough for kids to run, hike and sititing on grass area

name: Coronado Tidelands Park

google_reviews.text: A beautiful, well maintained park. It offers easy access to The Strand's shared ped/cycling pathway and a unique perspective of the Coronado Bridge.

Coronado Tidellands Park is my favorite. Very nice place 👌 best view, hotels 🏨 with best view of the ocean, take a walk 🚶‍♂️ along the beach with the view of the tall city buildings which adding to the beauty of area.

Beautiful Park. Park is next to the bridge. Picture perfect. The bathrooms are clean. Picnic spots are located surrounding the park. You can even have a small quiet picnic on the grass while watching the boats go by under the bridge  Nice play area for your littles. Enough space for baseball, football, volleyball and soccer.
Arguably one of the most beautiful settings for a park in the entire county, but undoubtedly one of the worst playgrounds!
We visited Coronado Tidelands Park just before sunset and had a wonderful time. The view at that time of day is absolutely perfect—highly recommend coming here for the sunset!

name: Torrey Meadows Park
formatted_address: 13499 Torrey Meadows Dr, San Diego, CA 92129, USA

google_reviews.text: Very clean park, it's a small park however plenty of space for running or sports. It has its own basketball court & playground, bathroom & gazebos. Their playground is also very well kept & modern fun. It's peaceful & the gazebos are very big with about 7 picnic style tables & grill. The gazebos must be reserved for parties through parks & rec phone calls only. Then they'll send you an email where you fill out info about your gathering & they'll reply with prices & rules.

*A Hidden Gem: Torrey Meadow Park Review*

Tucked away in a serene landscape, Torrey Meadow Park is a treasure trove of natural beauty and recreational activities. This park is perfect for families, dog owners, and nature enthusiasts alike. Here's a rundown of the park's fantastic features:

*Kids Playground:*

The playground is a haven for kids, with a variety of play structures, swings, and slides. The equipment is sturdy, well-maintained, and designed with safety in mind. The playground area is surrounded by shade structures, providing a comfortable spot for parents to relax while keeping an eye on their little ones.

*Dog-Friendly:*

Torrey Meadow Park is a dog lover's paradise! The park allows dogs, and the expansive grass fields provide ample space for them to run around and play. The scenic trails are perfect for a leisurely stroll with your furry friend.

*Picnic and Birthday Party Areas:*

The park offers designated picnic areas, complete with tables, benches, and grills. These areas are ideal for family gatherings, birthday parties, or casual get-togethers. You can reserve specific areas for private events, making it an excellent spot for celebrations.

*Grass Field:*

The lush green grass fields are perfect for outdoor activities like frisbee, soccer, or simply lounging in the sun. The fields are well-maintained and provide a beautiful backdrop for picnics or relaxation.

*Scenic Sunsets:*

Torrey Meadow Park offers breathtaking views of the surrounding landscape, especially during sunset. The sky transforms into a kaleidoscope of colors, making it a photographer's dream spot.

*Balloon Hot Air View in Evenings:*

One of the park's most unique features is the hot air balloon rides that take place in the evenings. Watch in awe as the balloons soar above the park, offering a bird's-eye view of the stunning landscape.

*Tips and Recommendations:*

- Arrive early to secure parking and a good spot for your picnic or playtime.
- Bring plenty of water, snacks, and sunscreen to keep you and your family comfortable throughout the day.
- Take advantage of the park's scenic trails for a leisurely hike or jog.
- Don't miss the hot air balloon rides in the evenings for a truly unforgettable experience.

*Rating: 5/5*

Torrey Meadow Park is a must-visit destination for anyone looking to spend time outdoors with family and friends. With its playground, dog-friendly areas, picnic spots, and stunning natural beauty, this park has something for everyone. Whether you're a local or just visiting the area, Torrey Meadow Park is sure to leave you with lasting memories.

Great structures for kids all ages with some that can't be found everywhere. Amazing flooring fo walking babies and a circle in the middle as sand box with climbable caves.

One of my favorite playgrounds.

There's also a huge green area, picnic tables, basketball court and bathroom.
Real nice park quiet and clean restrooms my dogs live it especially without a leash. Most dogs are loose and no problems will definitely be coming back.
Great park! Restroom is clean. Kids can get a lot of fun here.

name: Meadow Park
formatted_address: Meadow Park, Chula Vista, CA 91913, USA

google_reviews.text:
Meadow Park is a huge new addition to the Montecito neighborhood! With plenty of grass fields for everything from baseball to football, there is also a ton of space for picnics on the lawn or under one of the many covered spaces.

Surrounding the grass is a wide sidewalk for pedestrians, bikers, or skaters. There are also 6 built-in spots for a quick workout on the go without the need for additional equipment. A couple basketball courts are available too.

The playground structure is built with two age ranges in mind - one for smaller children and one for older kids. The entire area has built in shaded covering to keep parents comfortable while watching their kids play.

There is also a dog park attached. There is a section for large dogs and a separate section for smaller dogs.

At the top of the park are unshaded picnic tables with built in checkers and chess boards as well as some ping pong tables. You’ll just need to bring your own equipment to play.

It’s great that there are bathrooms at this park! So handy!

Chula Vista has another new park known as Meadow Park, it is located in the middle of a residential neighborhood and new housing construction as well.

The park features, built in chess tables, ping pong tables, two separate single basketball courts, a walking path, fitness equipment, a gated dog area, a large gazebo, plenty of open grass space, benches, and of a nature themed playground.

There are two separate playground areas for the smaller kids and the other is for the older kids. The playground is shaded and both structures have lots fun climbing structures, slides, bars, and bridges. It looks exactly like a tree house and it even says “ Kids Only” which is very unique.

Bathrooms are available with a baby changing table.

There is plenty of street parking on both sides of the park.

Amazing park and definitely worth the visit!

Always love my time there with my kids. My two year old can do nearly everything on his own and never tires!  If you are early, before 9 am, recommend bringing a towel to wipe any morning dew from the slides.

Great park. Great features such as playground, bathrooms, a gazebo for parties, nice walkway with workout stations to use around a well maintained grass area. Great for soccer or football, there are also basketball hoops and it’s handicap accessible.

Absolutely love this park.

name: Northmont Park

google_reviews.text: This is a great park for littles. Relatively quiet, has a basic bathroom, picnic tables and low walls. Bathrooms:  Men’s and women’s, one open stall which was clean and well lit.
Parking: Right next to the park in a free parking lot.
Location: Neighborhood park.
Safety: No unhoused, no trash on the playground. Lots of mosquitoes- there must be some standing water nearby because I had multiple mosquito bites despite it being mid morning.

This is such a quiet, beautiful park. Very peaceful. The park is very clean. It is located in La Mesa/ Grossmont area, I believe. There's a playground with clean bathrooms. There's food places nearby. Very good place for a nice picnic with the family.  Nice flower section. Lots, and I mean lots of shade at this park. It's not a massive park by any means. But it is a nice beautiful park with lots of nice lush green grass. I highly recommend you take the family there. For more on the places I go, be sure to follow my Instagram @Manbehindalens and follow my Google Maps as I'll be posting many more places. So feel free to ask any questions.

google_reviews.text: It was awesome to finally visit this historic park! We were passing by on the freeway when we spot it and decided to exit and visit it. Bright, vibrant colors, and very cool designs that will make you go “WOW” more than once! Parking in the area is ample and there’s even a small area right next to the place. Bring you phone or camera because you will want to take photos!

She told me that the bridges and freeway were built right through and over their community and that the park created around and including it was rich with culture and color. I am SO glad I went back and walked around so I could appreciate its beauty. I was alone and never once felt unsafe. I walked up to some of the little shops after and really enjoyed Barrio Logan more than any place I visited in San Diego. Great people, can’t wait to go back!

name: South Creek Park
formatted_address: 12249 Wickerbay Cove, San Diego, CA 92128, USA

google_reviews.text:
"Charming but Dated: South Creek Park Review"

South Creek Park, nestled in the Saber Springs area near Poway, San Diego, offers a nostalgic slice of childhood with its playground equipment, reminiscent of my teenage years some 25 years ago. Despite its age, the park maintains a clean and safe environment, supported by the polite and friendly nature of its visitors. This welcoming atmosphere makes it a lovely spot for families and kids to enjoy.

However, not everything has aged gracefully. The playground equipment, while holding sentimental value, shows signs of wear and tear, with some parts broken and disappointingly taped off. It's clear that these pieces hold historical significance but are in dire need of replacement to meet the safety and enjoyment standards of today's park-goers.

On a brighter note, the park boasts a well-paved concrete path that encircles the area, perfect for skateboarding, scooting, biking, or simply taking a leisurely walk. Additionally, the baseball field is a well-maintained feature, adding to the variety of activities available for visitors.

While the overall experience at South Creek Park is positive, highlighting its potential as a fantastic family destination, it's tarnished by the state of the restrooms, which were notably neglected and unsanitary during my visit. This, along with the need for playground equipment updates, underscores the importance of better maintenance to truly realize the park's potential.

In conclusion, South Creek Park is a gem that requires a bit of polishing. With some updating to the playground and improved upkeep, particularly of the restrooms, it could easily become a top choice for family outings in the San Diego area. Despite these areas for improvement, its charm and the warmth of its community make it worth a visit.

This park is quietly hidden away from the noisy Poway Road. We were pleasantly surprised how beautiful the play area was. The area was clean and had plenty of seats to sit. It had plenty of slides and bathrooms. The baseball ground had a walk area around the ground if you wanna go for a jog or walk. Definitely we will be going many more times.

name: Briercrest Park

google_reviews.text:
Not much parking but very modern, peaceful urban park. Tucked behind Sharp Grossmont Hospital and a memory care residential building, it could easily be missed. With the recent storms, the plants are a bit battered but will hopefully come back full force once we get back into our usual hot climate. Lots of shady places here though and places to sit with some interactive areas for mostly kids but adults will enjoy looking at least once. Also, popular rock hiding spot so bring some of your own or enjoy the hunt. I usually like to relocate one to a different park to give it some mileage. I’ll add photos in just a bit; technical issues but didn’t want to wait to do a write up.
Edit: Photos added which gave me a couple more what I would like to say are insights but opinions; park needs to be on a more regular maintenance schedule. It doesn’t need much but everything just looked like it had a coat of dirt over it. Yes, it’s a natural park, etc. but that doesn’t mean a good power wash and trim wouldn’t help.

name: Lincoln Acres County Park
formatted_address: 2717 Granger Ave, National City, CA 91950, USA

google_reviews.text:
Lincoln Acres County Park is a fairly new park that opened in late 2021 located in National City right near the Lincoln Acres Library. The park is fully gated with shade and features three separate playground structures, large grassy space, one large basketball court, and a white gazebo. This was our first time visiting this park and it was such a fun experience to spend all morning playing and reading some books at the library.

As mentioned before, the park features three separate structures; two structures for the toddlers and one large structure for the bigger kids. The structures has plenty of climbing equipment, slides, learning equipment, and balancing beams as well.  My little one had so much fun running from one playground structure to the other.

Right next to the park is a pathway that lead to a small park and pathway, there are also plenty of benches throughout the park. After the park, take a short walk to the library where they have awesome computer games for the kids and relax with a book while they kids explore.

There is a small parking lot area in front of the park but since there was an event when we were visiting some of the visitors were parking on the street.

Clean bathrooms are available along with drinking fountains.

Small Sheriff’s office right next to the playground and library (which came in handy when someone at the park had their car hit by another park visitor and the sheriff was able to document the incident rather quickly).

Overall this park is highly recommended and one of favorites mainly because of the cleanliness and safe guarded it seems.
Great playground, we celebrated a birthday party in their community salon, great atmosphere, party was great and their staff  friendly and helpful
Nice park with kids area . Small but clean

And in the summer months free lunches are provided for the kids. The library staff is unbelievably helpful, and friendly. Plus for anybody who want to throw a party they have cheaply priced hall rentals. The bathroom's  are  well stocked and clean. Over all I give this place an A+.

Hilltop Park is a fantastic spot for families, dog lovers, and anyone looking to enjoy a day outdoors. Located at 780 Hilltop Dr, Chula Vista, this park offers a little something for everyone. A beautiful waterway runs through the park, adding a tranquil touch to the landscape, and the playground is perfect for kids, with plenty of space to climb, slide, and explore.

Dog owners will especially love the large off-leash dog area, where pups can roam free until noon—great for early risers looking to let their furry friends burn off some energy. For those who enjoy picnicking or grilling, the park is equipped with bbq pits and picnic tables, making it a perfect place for gatherings or a relaxing lunch in the sun. With its wide range of amenities and welcoming atmosphere, Hilltop Park is a true gem in Chula Vista!

name: Ruffin Canyon Open Space
formatted_address: San Diego, CA 92123, USA

google_reviews.text: I entered Ruffin Canyon Open Space through the back door.  I wanted to enter from Sandrock Canyon Open Space but the San Diego Parks and Recreation map, which shows both properties, didn’t show a connection between them.  Google’s Satellite Map, fortunately, did show a connection.  That connection, however, is a long, dry creek bed full of rocks.  The creek bed heads in a south-easterly direction before connecting with a north-south creek bed.  Going north at this point, and still walking in a rocky creek bed, takes you to what the Parks and Recreation map says is the end of the Ruffin Canyon Trail (or in my case, the beginning).  Shortly after trekking north, you’ll have the option of heading east to an outlet on Shawn Avenue.  Continuing north on the Ruffin Canyon Trail eventually takes you to a fork in the road.  The trail to the left, which leads to Gramercy Drive, is closed due to a habitat restoration project taking place.  The trail to right takes you to Ruffin Road.  The trailhead at Ruffin Road appears to be where most people start their hike into the canyon.

Really enjoyed this little hike out there, don’t get me wrong it’s nothing crazy there isn’t like an amazing mountain top peak or like a deep valley but hey it’s in the middle of San Diego and it’s a nice little walk about 2 miles or so I ran into one couple with there dog and you are walking on a dried riverbed.
Such random and empty space. Can’t exercise here easily and not much space anyway for a good hike. Just let the wild animals roam free.

Not a bad little hike. Once you get down the hill, it IS very rocky to the point where you can't just passively walk; you have to be focused on your footing. This isn't necessarily a bad thing, but it'd have been nice to know before getting down there.

Starting at the Kobe Dr entrance, this 1.5 mile hike will connect you to friars road.  You’ll head along the west rim of the canyon for ~.5 miles, then take a left to the bottom of the canyon.  From there it is another 1 mile of passable trail and rocky arroyo walking to connect next to the Escala dog park.  Add another half mile to access the connection under friars road into the shooing center.

My child loves playing in nature. Hopping on logs. Playing with vegetation in his pretend out door kitchen. This was everything we hoped for. We will be back. Get these every where!

name: Maple Canyon Open Space
formatted_address: San Diego, CA 92103, USA

google_reviews.text: Such a unique walking path so close to downtown! This was a very enjoyable hike, and a way to experience some nature in the city. Only downside is that there's a considerable amount of construction going on, making the place louder than ideal. Note: it is definitely more than a walk in the park, I would suggest wearing sneakers!

Our new favorite place to hike with kids. The bridge is nice on top but the real fun is to hike down. Turn left right in front of the bridge if you’re on 3rd and Quince. You’ll see a small hidden path that goes down into the canyon. You can park on Quince no issues.

Everything right now is super green and looks amazing. The bridge structure is perfect for pictures. The second bridge looks a lot like the steel used on the Eiffel Tower. Really impressive.

We even found some natural clay they kids played with like play doh.

We didn’t finish the whole path because it was going to get dark soon. Maybe next time.

A lot of people walking around 6 pm on Sunday but not crowded at al. Feels safe.

We took out 4 year old and he did great. Not elderly or wheelchair friendly unfortunately.

Check my IG for more @artisticabysarah
Great place for a hike. It gets muddy after it rains. A section was under construction while I was there.
A good place for a short walk and so relaxing when you walk up to the bridge and go through the downtown. Good place for kids to explore nature and city lifestyle.
In urban area, between Bankers Hill, Mission Hills, and SD Airport/DT!!! Coyotes, birds, lots of cool nature, and a few old cabins! Amazing Bridges too!

name: Mission Valley Preserve
formatted_address: Friars Rd, San Diego, CA 92110, USA

google_reviews.text:
I would give this place more than five stars if I could!!!

The only reason I can't is because there are a lot of homeless camps and trolleys all over the place, making it feel unsafe and dirty. Especially if you want to go off trail. In one place there is even a metal sign put up saying don't go there because if may be unsafe, and graffiti nearby saying "rip e man"

Other than that this place is absolutely stunning to see during the April period when the spring bloom happens in and around San Diego! An absolute must see, there are poppies and flowers sprouting everywhere! And what's more all this is in the heart of San Diego! Unbelievable.

You can hike all the way to Cottonwood park where there are bikers taking the bike lane that goes all the way along the San Diego river all the way to Ocean Beach.

I wish they would clean this place up because it's an absolute diamond in the making.

To access there are multiple places but the official entrance is in the back of the parking lot of Mission Valley YMCA.
My feed is aspirational...  Place has a lot of potential once they clean up the neighborhood

It's a safe place if you stay on the Friar's Rd side and not cross the river that's the evil creepy side that's not safe. Most of the homeless who live there are just trying to get by day by day and they usually just leave everyone alone.

We went there for a Sunday hike with my 8 yr old daughter and my husband.  We thought it would be a great place to see nature, get some sunshine and do some exploring.  While it is a perfect place for that, we didn't feel safe and had to turn around after only 15 minutes.  The place is full of transients, homeless encampments, and sketchy activity.  Lots of trash left behind by transients and just an unsafe feeling.  We were there at 10:30 a.m. on a sunny day, and even my husband felt uneasy.  I wish the city could address this problem because it has such potential to be a cool little gem in the middle of our city.

I absolutely love with the San Diego river park foundation has been doing to make our river a more welcoming place for the public. The little pathways lined with stones and direction signs really add a nice touch. I would love to see this done with the entire length of the river if possible. As someone who spends much of their leisure time looking for new fishing spots along the river to catch and release it would be nice to be more clear as to where the game reserve begins and ends so as to avoid fishing in the wrong location.  Keep up the good work San Diego river park foundation!

name: Living Coast Discovery Center
formatted_address: 1000 Gunpowder Point Dr PARKING, Chula Vista, CA 91910, USA

google_reviews.text: The Living Coast Discovery Center is an amazing habitat sanctuary to visit just South of San Diego. They have a variety of wildlife with multiple birds, reptiles and marine life. I was lucky enough to arrive on time for see the Eagles being free which was a cool sight. There is also hiking trail out to the Bay which is nice as well. Great for kids and not as crowded as other places.

Our kids grew up coming on field trips here. Not much has changed, but it's still as interesting as it was then. This is a great place to bring kids to, to come as a family, to learn about nature and conservation. It's small enough to see everything in a day, and still go into some detail. They have many species of animals here. Their workers are very knowledgeable about the animals, their habitats and interactions.

There is a composting station/workshop, a shark and sting ray tank (for petting), as well as a sea anemone petting area, a sea turtle enclosure, several bird enclosures, a look out area with beautiful views looking over the marsh lands and the bay, and an area with picnic tables. The area is surrounded by native plants, chapparal.

The gift shop is well stocked and items are mostly affordable.

Pleasant staff who are mostly very knowledgeable. (We enjoyed talking to several employees.)

Clean bathrooms.

Expanded and closer parking lot compared to older one. Bus/shuttle van comes on a loop several times an hour. Also, there are several short hiking trails.

Definitely worth spending a day here. (Or more—when my kids were little we had annual passes. Worth the price if you plan to visit more often.)

Living Coast Discovery Center is a peaceful, hands-on wildlife spot with sea turtles, rays, birds, and beautiful coastal views. Friendly staff, fun exhibits, perfect for families or a chill day exploring. Loved it!
It's small but l really enjoyed my day at Living Coast. Guided presentations helps you to better understand the animals.
We went and enjoyed learning about all the animals they had. The Staff was so nice, they had tons of interesting facts and information about the animals. They made our experience wonderful!

This nature park is amazing, it's a mini hike down the trail towards excitement, it's not your normal playground, it has lots of activities for your little ones to explore, and cave into thier curiosity, and work on their motor skills. The suspension bridge is so much fun, the slides are sturdy, and fast, the steping logs, are creative. There is beach sand by the canoes, and the play area is cover with wood chips. Location: In a neighborhood.

Dylan taught us how to identify animal prints and scat, helped us explore native plants, and even provided binoculars for bird-watching—such a thoughtful touch that made the experience even more engaging.

We learned so much and left feeling inspired. Highly recommend Silverwood and especially Dylan for any families looking for an enriching outdoor education experience!

Daniel Neale.

name: Lily Pond

google_reviews.text: Beautiful pond with seagulls, ducks , koi fish and red slider turtles. Arrive early so you can enjoy the area without a crowd 😎 they keep the area clean and the animals look healthy ❤️
Lily pond at Balboa Park is amazing, fantastic display of top notch architecture and reflection of that in the pond which makes it even special during sunset

Balboa Park was such a joy to visit in San Diego!! Specifically this spot at Lily Pond is where you can take the most iconic picture for your SD trip!! I visited during this past Thanksgiving & the weather was perfect. Classic California landmark for San Diego, in the same vein the Hollywood sign is for Los Angeles & the Golden Gate Bridge is for San Francisco.
The Lily Pond in Balboa Park is such a serene and picturesque spot. Watching the ducks swim around was a nice touch that made the experience even more charming.

The Lily Pond, located adjacent to the Botanical Building in Balboa Park, is a serene and picturesque spot that enhances the beauty of the park. Known for its tranquil atmosphere, the pond is surrounded by lush greenery and features blooming water lilies that create a stunning visual display, especially in the spring and summer months. The reflection of the Botanical Building’s iconic latticework structure in the water adds to the charm of the scene, making it a popular spot for photography and peaceful contemplation.

Historically, the Lily Pond was part of the original design for the Panama-California Exposition in 1915, contributing to the park's overall aesthetic of beauty and harmony. Today, it serves as a peaceful oasis, offering visitors a place to relax and enjoy the natural beauty of the park. The gentle sounds of the water, combined with the vibrant colors of the lilies and surrounding flora, make the Lily Pond a beloved and timeless feature of Balboa Park.

name: Robyn's Egg Trail
formatted_address: Robyn's Egg Trail, San Diego, CA 92110, USA

google_reviews.text: Cute little trail with lush greenery. A wash goes  straight through the middle, during the rainy season it might be a little wet. Right now it's December and just rained a few days ago so there's some puddles and some muddy places. A lot of rocks for grips though. Like the other side toward the top of the hill I did get a little winded, I'm not that out of shape but I do have asthma so take precaution. Other than that it's really pretty both sides border people's backyards from mission hills at the top but they have very nice well landscaped yards so it's not unsightly. I climbed a tree branch I had fallen over into a little bridge; super fun! I would say it's about a 15 to 30 minute walk taking some time to explore.

Not too long, not so short but it's a rough trail. If you're not in the best of shape then you might want to use caution and do not go alone.

Even those in good shape will probably find themselves going up or down deep sections utilizing a three point contact with the ground.

It follows a dry most-of-the-year creek/arroyo. If you're starting at Titus street then you'll be heading uphill.

For days or weeks after the rains in the fall/winter it can become lush and green.

moderately washed out dry creek trail with some sections that aren't there anymore.  manholes here and there made me wonder if I was just in a construction area post cleanup.  If you're out of shape like me and started at the bottom, you might get some very "I'm gonna die down here" kind of vibes about halfway through.  2/5, will probably go around next time.

Great little trail. Wonderful sense of nature in a relatively urban area. Great for running and fast hiking. Few people are ever seen here. It has open scrub areas as well as a tight tree enclosed trail running along side a dry stream bed and can be easily slipped off from. Be careful.

Different than other sea shores in San Diego. It's rocky and full of vegetation and sea creatures. I felt very lively at this place. As you can see in my pictures, I got to experience sea anemone and shells. There were crabs, lobsters and other shell bearing creatures too. The rocks are slippery due to alagal growth so wear shoes and walk carefully. There is a low tide time when you can walk on these rocks. Parking could be an issue since it has limited space. If you manage to park, it is worth visiting this place.

This is a nice trail. The scenery was breathtaking and the atmosphere was peaceful. The trail was perfect for hikers of all levels, with a short and mild slope that made it easy to navigate.

name: Rose Creek
formatted_address: Rose Creek Trail, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text:
Rose Creek is a salt water Marsh and Estuary system that empties into Mission Bay in Pacific Beach.
A paved pedestrian/ bike path runs along the creek's east side, and a dirt path is on the west side.
There is an abundance of wildlife thriving here. Numerous birds including herons, ducks, hawks, hummingbirds, and songbirds are everywhere, as well as rabbits, squirrels and many reptiles.
While the eastern path has a bike path, I prefer the western side with the native plant area. Flowers of many kinds are everywhere.
While many people are just whizzing by, I prefer to hang out and watch this small natural area full of life.

The best time of the year is now to see all the wild flowers. It’s a good hike and not busy at all but there are power lines along the way.
Great area to hike and mountain bike 🚵‍♀️.

This is an incredibly fun mountain bike trail that weaves through a canopy of gnarly trees. Fun turns and the tree trunks keep you on your toes. I got too excited on my last lap through there and had a close encounter with one. The shade is a nice relief from the other exposed trails in the area. Way more crowded on the weekend, so keep an eye out when cruising downhill.
Greer Ranch is golden!! Best mtn bike park in the area.

name: Nature/Abstract L Star Ladies Who Paint Mural
formatted_address: 841 14th St, San Diego, CA 92101, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful huge murals. Several murals on the west and south walls.

name: Lake Murray Recreation Area
formatted_address: 5650 Kiowa Dr, La Mesa, CA 91942, USA

google_reviews.text: Lake Murray is a fantastic spot to spend a day with the family. It offers beautiful scenery, perfect for social events, picnics, and even fishing. With ample space, great amenities, and plenty of parking, it's a local gem. 👌🛵🦤

My first time walking at Lake Murray, and it was so easy and enjoyable! There are trash bins, bathrooms, and plenty of spaces to relax. You can walk, run, bike, boat, kayak, or have a picnic. There’s lots of parking too. I love this place, it’s perfect for relaxing and enjoying the surroundings.

We had a great time at the lake. The gentleman at the rental place was so kind! We rented a pontoon boat for an hour and just cruised around the lake before going on the hiking trails. Never knew there was so much to do here! You can rent pedal boats, kayaks, fishing boats too. Definitely a great way to spend a day when the beaches are overcast.

Lovely walk along the 5 fingers of lake, then you have to turn around to return as it is not a round-trip walk. A few bathrooms along the route, two are pit toilets.
Ample parking and easy access for individuals with special needs.
Fishing 🎣  and boat rentals are available.

Great outdoor family place. Wild life. No swimming but you can fish the lake. There is a small snack store where you can also rent water equipment or bikes to ride around. However, the trail does not go all the way around. Once you get to where it would be the overpass it's gated and you will be forced to go back the same way. Round trip would be about 6 miles. The trail is paved so bring your bike, skates or just walk/run and enjoy😁

name: Tijuana River Valley Regional Park Bird & Butterfly Garden
formatted_address: 2310 Hollister St, San Diego, CA 92154, USA

google_reviews.text: Handful of benches to sit in different areas of the garden and plenty trash cans.  It's not overwhelmed with flowers it's a bit more natural; still beautiful in a peaceful way. No bicycles or horses in the garden area and you are able to walk out of the garden to a different area where there is more tables and parking different trails less used by people so it's soft sand. Did get a couple of ticks so use bug spray.  Two of my pictures are of us leaving the garden with a sign saying butterfly garden and another with a different sign and trail marker which is a trail on the outside of the garden.

Miserable unkept trail with a poor map and unhelpful signs. If you're riding a horse, I'm sure this place is amazing, but otherwise, if you're here to bird, DON'T. Walking was awful as there was horse manure everywhere which caused a ton of bugs and a stinky path. Plants were all overgrown on trail and there was low visibility for birds. There ARE bobcats here so do not bring your dog. There is also an open and spewing sewer close to the edge of one of the trails. All birders, avoid this trailhead and place in general. Not worth it.
I love this area...lol minus the mosquitoes 🦟   I spent some time slapping them away

We were staying at the nearby campsite and wanted to visit this area. There was a closed building on the property and a short trail you can take. We saw a lot of humming birds. This area is popular with horse back riders.
Park vegetation has been renewed and coming back. good walking trails. Birds will return as plants grow. good dirt parking lot.

name: Point Loma Native Plant Garden
formatted_address: 2275 Mendocino Blvd, San Diego, CA 92107, USA

google_reviews.text: An excellent place to see mature California plants, or just to take a nice stroll.

I volunteered for community cleanup hours here last Sunday with Shane from SD River & Parks Association and it’s a very nice well maintained botanical garden I don’t know much about biological diversity but there’s cool little signs to teach you about the various species of plants throughout and park it’s kinda overlooking the highway a bit but there’s some nice benches you can sit and watch the bustle of life go by while learning about the native ecology of Pt Loma & San Diego in general and like I said they do community cleanup every Sunday it’s well maintained so I recommend visiting this quaint little park! 5 ⭐️

Wow, a little slice of nature heaven in a residential area. My daughter and I arrived around 3p.m. and spent about 1 1/2 hour's. All native plants and tree's with identified information. Poppies were blooming and the fragrance of nature was wonderful. A super clean park and a number of paths to meander. Very few people at this week day time. Only one negative as we were exiting I just missed stepping in dog ----. Come on people the park provides bags to clean up your dogs waste, why not try next time?
I cleaned it up my good deed for the day, haha.

A small, secluded Plant Garden with trails and benches. I enjoy walking around and learning about the plants native to San Diego. We are extremely fortunate to have such beautiful native plants. Best of all, if you go at sunset, you'll get a spectacular view. Sometimes I go to the park at night to feed some Skooma and Moon Sugar to the homeless people.
Beautiful little hidden garden. More people should know about this place. Lovely display of native flowers, trees, cacti, etc., and all publicly funded along with the help of volunteers.

name: Sweetwater Summit Regional Park
formatted_address: 3218 Summit Meadow Rd, Bonita, CA 91902, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice little quiet place to walk, see the reservoir,  mountains, freeway at some part. Think it connects to the Bonita trail and golf park. But there's a quiet campground there too. Close to the city but out far enough to be away.  Sometimes I get lucky and saw some coyotes roaming around

The best Campsite in San Diego County, clean and safe and the best restrooms of any campsites. Restrooms are private individual and they lock no stall restrooms where you have to look over your shoulder and make sure no one’s entering the restroom. When you’re occupying it. Nice walk, trails, clean roads, hot, running water and the restrooms. Nice park employees very helpful. Very comfortable For travelers.

This county campground is wonderful!  Big sites, lots of pull through sites, most with three-way hook-ups and big rig friendly.  There are bike lanes on the roads making it easy to get to multiuse pathways.
Overall great value at a moderate cost.

Nice campsite and definitely the best prices in San Diego. Tons of great trails to hike and close proximity to the city. If you're parking in an RV loop space, choose the inner one because I was on the outside loop and neighbors blocked me in with their car. For reference I was site 54 and should've chosen 53. Will definitely be back and try to find one of the spots on the outer edge overlooking the valley that doesn't share space with a neighbor (back in spaces).
This area was great! We got the day pass to hike up sweetwater summit, nice hike though very cakey and muddy. The playground was eh. Overall, great place to go camping with an rv or hiking!

name: Campland on the Bay
formatted_address: 2211 Pacific Beach Dr, San Diego, CA 92109, USA

google_reviews.text:
OVERALL GREAT PLACE BUT READ BELOW FOR BIG RIGS

*See "BIG RIG" notes below

Clean:  The place has a crew that goes around cleaning all day.  They rake and blow your lot off before you arrive.

Friendly:  Staff that greet you are very friendly, even a little sassy (I like that!) and very helpful

Location:  Great location, flat and level, and only minutes from everything important (Navy base, Sea World, Downtown, Hillcrest).

*BIG RIG notes:  I have a 45' coach with a toad.  San Diego is a big city with busy streets.  This location is handy as it's not far from the I-5 freeway.  However.....between the freeway and the RV Park, the streets are very busy, 2 lane or less and very narrow streets.  Everyone is in a hurry and know nothing about big rigs except we are "in their way" (lol).  Check your GPS when getting close.  I came in I-5 Southbound and took Exit 23 onto Mission Bay Drive no problem.
Point 1) But when turning right onto Grand Ave (map it first and see what I'm showing you) it is a HAIRPIN turn at a stoplight and you must be very careful.  You can make it! But you can't let the busy traffic intimidate you as you move slowly and across both lanes.
Point 2) After navigating this corner, you will be on Grand Ave for about a mile and you must turn on Olney Street.  This street is also very narrow, parked cars on both sides but the traffic is not heavy (it's a military neighborhood).  But my point is, it is narrow for a big rig.
Point 3)  My suggestion is you find a larger street West of Olney (Morrell or Lamont??) to turn on and then enter Pacific Beach Dr which takes you right into the RV park.
Point4)  I made it safely with the GPS directions I just spoke about.  So, you can make it too

Final Notes:  Great place, great staff, great location!  We have stayed here 4 times =)

A great RV Park with lovely views of the Pacific Passage. The park is very serene. It has all you need for your RV, and you feel very secure in the place. But for those who aren’t fond of cooking, the place might seem like it lack more dining options.

We enjoyed our stay at Campland on the Bay, and will be returning very soon.  We stay with our dogs and didn't have any issues.  The RV park is dog friendly! They have parks and runs all over the park. We were able to let our dogs enjoy the beach ⛱️ in the early morning and afternoon.  The cantina was dog friendly,  for the most part. All the staff, especially Travis, was welcoming and easy to talk to and were engaging. RV neighbors were close,  but that's ok, because,  everyone was super delightful and easy going. There are RV dumps within the parks, which makes it convenient. Overall,  we recommend Campland!

My family stays here when they come in from AZ, and we love staying here with them as locals. So many amenities, everything is well maintained and clean, and staff is usually so nice. Lots of families to socialize with and being on the bay is awesome. Easy bike ride to PB as well.

This was our first time staying. I think this place has reached its peak and is in decline. I booked a preferred site. They initially put us in the Q section with long term residents and the campsite next to us looked like a homeless encampment. The shower next to that site had a sign saying there was no hot water until further notice. The next morning we asked to move and they kindly moved us. Staff were friendly and helpful. But The whole place was empty so I’m not sure why they put us in the worst spot.  The place needs maintenance-the game room games were broken, there were piles and piles of sand everywhere, leaves and dirt on the skate park. There was a beer cup that sat on the stage the entire time we were there and we watched numerous employees walk by it without picking it up. The grass wasn’t mowed. The sewer caps were off or missing at most of the sites.
A group of unsupervised kids showed up at the empty campsite next to us and started a fire and then caught sticks on fire and were hitting them against the picnic table spreading embers all around. Security didn’t address it after a call.  The kids running around on scooters seemed out of control and unsupervised so I wasn’t comfortable with letting my younger kids out to ride around. I watched kids playing ping pong by throwing the paddles into the grass. It was chaos.

We thought it would be a nice time with the kid friendly things. We ended up leaving a day early. It’s very expensive for what it is. We will not be back. I can’t imagine people returning unless they have nostalgia for what it used to be.

name: Kumeyaay Lake Campground
formatted_address: 2 Father Junipero Serra Trail, San Diego, CA 92119, USA

google_reviews.text:
Kumeyaay Campground is truly one of the best campgrounds we’ve stayed at! The entire place is peaceful, well-kept, and surrounded by beautiful nature, perfect for a relaxing getaway. The campsites are clean, spacious, and thoughtfully laid out with just the right amount of privacy.

What really stood out to us were the showers and restrooms, spotless and well maintained, which is so important to us when camping. You can tell the staff genuinely care about keeping everything in great condition.

A huge shoutout to Scott and Mario, the campground hosts, they are incredibly hospitable, friendly, and always ready to help. They made our stay even more enjoyable with their kindness and welcoming attitude.

We’ll definitely be coming back! Highly recommend Kumeyaay Campground to anyone looking for a clean, comfortable, and well-run place to camp!

Honestly I was genuinely upset. I scheduled for a friday check in and it said it was available and a Sunday morning checkout. Me and friends traveled from Vegas and got here later than we planned and got there around 12am (technically Saturday now) and we camp all the time and have never come across a problem where if we show up later then check in time and we couldn't  get in because there was a gate covering the entrance. And even if the site was closed Friday, the site should have a true blocked window for when you can book. I even called Saturday morning and multiple times mid day to see if we could camp our Saturday-Sunday reservation and no answer or call back. So im left to assume they were closed but again the website said it was open to book for the days I needed. Bad communication, website could've been more thorough on booking dates, and could've at least had more signs at the entrance to state closure for that day. I stayed at lake Jennings last minute lost my money from this reservation but it was a terrible experience not going to lie. Never has happened before, but definitely disappointed. Ps there's no communication or explanation in the website / reservation confirmation email on what to do if you show up late. The other place i stayed at sent me a whole code and phone number on what to do.

Nice little campground. Tent sites available. RV sites available in different lengths. Fires are allowed, in fire pits, at each site. (Fires might not be allowed during some seasons as this is in a fire prone area.) Picnic table at each site. Generators allowed in certain campsites during non-quiet hours. Potable water available. Bathrooms available. Showers available. Trash dumpsters available. RV Tank Dump Site available.
Hiking trails, ranger station.
Friendly.
Small lake. Fishing allowed from shore only.
Good cell phone reception.
Don't count on any TV reception.

The camp grounds are Beautiful and the bathrooms are nice and clean  but the camp rangers make this whole experience not worth going. Talk about killing the mood. I get they are trying to keep the park nice. But we had 3 camp rangers come over keep in mind we have only been here for not even 5 hours. I had my phone music on he told us to turn it down and it was only 8:40pm. My music to my phone it not loud at all. He had me turn that down. It’s crazy. Not worth going. That will be the last time I go and camp there!!!! Horrible …… and no smoking no vapes. 🤦🏼‍♀️

Super peaceful, quiet and cute! Campgrounds in great shape. Fire ring was even clean upon arrival!!! 🔥

Re-visit 4 suuuuure

name: Lake Jennings Campground
formatted_address: 10108 Bass Rd, Lakeside, CA 92040, USA

google_reviews.text: We love camping at Lake Jennings! Rain or Shine its a very beautiful campground we spent my 50th birthday here, New Year stayed a whole week.. It was wonderful. Rangers and staff are Soooo wonderful! My family feel safe and secure here. The best campground in San Diego Area....

Lake Jennings is a great place to camp. Perfect for almost any sized rig. many sites have fantastic views and most sites are quite private. The staff is great, particularly a guy named Josh really went out of way to help us and to make our stay great.
The entire park covers quite a large area. (200 acres or more?) The campground itself is on top of a ridge with fantastic views.  It is a hidden gem. We will be back!

Lake Jennings is a BEAUTIFUL a campground, plus it has the Crew & Hosts that make the experience that much better! The Hosts are helpful, kind and accommodating! They keep the sites & grounds in beautiful condition. Some of the AMAZING Hosts include Josh, Sherry, Jenny, just to name a few. There are so many so many more we encountered and had a positive experience with ALL of them. Our experience was so positive we even applied for a "Camp Host" position!

They are pet friendly,  have hiking trails for days, have a wonderful playground for the kids and offer fishing at the lake as well!

Weather you're local or distant you should definitely check out this Campground!

We had a wonderful experience at Lake Jennings Campground! The restrooms were impressively clean and well-maintained, which is always a big plus when camping. The views of the lake are absolutely beautiful—perfect for relaxing and taking in nature.

The staff and rangers were friendly and professional, with regular visits to the campsites that made us feel very safe and well looked after. Quiet hours are taken seriously here, which made it peaceful and truly family-friendly.

One thing to note: there were quite a few ants and some cockroaches around, so if you're really sensitive to bugs, it might be something to consider. That said, it's nature after all, and with a little prep, it’s manageable.

Overall, highly recommended for a clean, scenic, and safe camping getaway! Campsite- 46.

Great campground! Fabulous spots for tent camping, tucked along the side of the hill overlooking the lake. RV spots are great too, we had spot 53 - highly recommend! It’s like a nice alcove in the campground. Bathrooms were a little smelly and felt like they could use a splash of bleach - but regardless enjoyed our time there!

name: Santee Lakes Campground
formatted_address: 9310 Fanita Pkwy, Santee, CA 92071, USA

google_reviews.text: This was our first time camping at Santee Lakes. We loved our lakefront RV site. Lots of places to bike ride. We loved the food at the Topwater grill but not the coffee. We will be staying here again.

I really enjoyed my time at Santee Lakes Campground on the outskirts of San Diego. It is a very large campground with accommodations for RVs, campervans, tents, and cabins for rent. Day use only available too for a fee.
Always peaceful. Clean restrooms.  Plenty of parking.  I don't mind paying a couple bucks to get in.  Keeps out the Riff Raff
The wife and I walk here 5 days a week after work. There is a nice breeze and it's a beautiful. We love feeding the ducks.

The place is HUGE!!!  200+ clean RV sites, lots of room, plenty of shade. Bathrooms and showers were clean and neat. Sites have full hookups too. They have several floating cabins, and about 8 on the land. There's a decent carryout food place right by the registration bldg. There's a nice store IN the registration bldg as well. There's a bigger, and a kiddie pool... The hot tub was a HUGE BONUS for us, used it nearly every day! We were able to get 4 mile + bike rides without leaving the park. Plenty of restaurants and shopping within a mile or two... At 75.00/day, not badly priced (at least for California)... Our granddaughter just loves this park, and it's less than a mile from where she lives... There are at least three playground areas, to go with the 6-7 lakes. BIG PLACE! 👌👌👌

name: Santee Lakes Recreation Preserve
formatted_address: 9310 Fanita Pkwy, Santee, CA 92071, USA

google_reviews.text: Santee Lakes is a beautiful, relaxing place to watch the ducks and other wildlife, and many people fish there. There is not a lot of shade for such a warm climate, but there are some picnic tables and benches scattered around. There’s a little store that sells drinks, ice cream and snacks, as well as camping supplies. No dogs of any size are allowed , even on a leash. If you are staying at the campground you are allowed to walk your dogs around all the lakes, which seems unfair to me. It gets very crowded on weekends and holidays with very limited parking, so plan to go early.

such a lovely place to walk and relax.  I want to thank whoever turned in my grandson's sweatshirt.  I am filled with much gratitude.  I was so happy to call the lakes and find out the items was in the lost and found.  Thank you so very much.

First time going i was impressed the ranger at the check-in shack waa very welcoming and gave us a lot of useful information park and bathrooms very clean great picnic areas saw lots of people catching fish will definitely return thank you for such an enjoyable experience

We just go to Santee Lakes for picnics, and sometimes to just walk around. There is an entry fee to park inside, but you can also walk in if you park out on the street close to the entrance. On some weekends it's actually easier to park on the street for free than to look for parking in the lake parking areas, so we usually just stick to the street parking.

It's nice here, the lakes look clean, grass is nice and well maintained, and the bathrooms are good. I enjoy coming here.
Very nice, peaceful and safe place for the family and kids. Your kids have a very nice playground to play and very nice view to enjoy walking around the lakes.

name: Tijuana River Valley Campground
formatted_address: 1942 Monument Rd, San Diego, CA 92154, USA

google_reviews.text:
Great place for dry camping.
There is potable water available onsite.
My only negative experience was the noise from the busy helicopter training site nearby.
Cell coverage was weak put usable.
The staff was very accommodating.
Yurts were clean and spacious. Me and my friends had a very fun stay here and I will definitely be back!

We had an AMAZING TIME!! We booked right when the park reopened after a massive flooding thankfully, it has been repaired and the park is in great shape. Everything has been sanitized and is very clean. We didn't have any issue with smells. The rangers were so friendly and it was quiet besides some helicopter traffic. We felt very safe. Bring bug spray. Showers are via tokens. Very kid friendly great for bikes nice and flat. Just keep an eye out for pokey plants.

One of the worst sites I’ve ever been to. Terrible bug and ant problems, border security helicopters flying overhead every two minutes at night, and the worst shower I’ve ever used. On top of that, I got yelled at by cleaning staff for briefly using an unused site’s table at 7:00 a.m. Overall, I did not have a good time and definitely won’t be coming back anytime soon.

I had an amazing experience in this secluded and peaceful campground. It was awesome to visit the border and walk around the area that is loaded with wildlife.

The campground was easy to access and affordable, very peaceful and relaxing. This area has many attractions if you're willing to be adventurous and keep your eyes open.

name: Willow Loop Campground
formatted_address: Santee, CA 92071, USA

google_reviews.text: Santee Lakes is always a treat. Clean and peaceful with large sites and plenty of fun things to do for all ages.  The staff is very friendly and helpful
Great campground we will definitely be going back

Would get 5 stars except for super high prices and poor internet. $55 night was based on $1650 per 30 day stay + electricity. The internet is provided through Tengointernet. When I first signed up for internet it worked ok but then went to very bad. Tech support through Tengo could not help and tried to blame it on my equipment. I was only 30 feet from the hotspot.

The sites are large and easy to get into. Nice park with lots of trees and grass. Upkeep is good. Security is great. Park is off all highways and quite. Fills up on weekends with lots of families and kids.

Monthly rates pretty much doubled this year. What is the point of full-time camping if you can find an apartment for less? Campers are parked on dirt with ant infestations in the summer/fall and puddles in the winter/spring. Even if you use electricity sparingly, you are charged anywhere from $120 to $300/month- Sometimes up to $500. The washers and dryers are always full and costs about $6 to wash and dry. A campround 10 miles away costs $2 to wash and dry. The Tengo wifi always goes out. The cellphone reception is terrible. There is also a free stuff pile on a ledge in the laundry room, but someone throws it all away like they don't even care about the environment. Still, if they fixed all of that, who willingly and happily would choose to camp for over $2000 plus electricity per month? This campground got greedy just like every other business after the pandemic and thinks they are better than they are. They are so greedy it just feels gross camping there.

One of our favorite stay-cation sites! If you just want to sit and enjoy the greenery or toss your line in the water, Santee Lakes is great! The RV campsites are clean and level. The hookups are well layed out and easy to connect to.  This area is an oasis in the middle of a city.

name: Sweetwater County Park
formatted_address: 3218 Summit Meadow Rd, Bonita, CA 91902, USA

google_reviews.text: The rangers are awesome! The handicapped sites are very nice! Some sites have beautiful views! For the most part the campground is quiet and peaceful!

Great campground. The rangers are super friendly. The bathroom in the newer section is nice and private for showers but the family facilities struggle with random trashing from guest children I guess. During the prime camping season it can be crowded.  Nice trails nearby and a view and breeze on the bluffs. I believe most spots are level and pretty easy to maneuver around. Vegetation is nice with shade. Picnic tables and fire rings at each spot. Near a golf course. Near most San Diego attractions. Low cost less than $40 a night.
Beautiful little rv park. Has a little water park running for kids to run and splash in. Does need maintenance. All hook ups.

This is one of my favorite place and I’m so glad that I met CJO, he is the love of my life, my best friend, my partner. We had such great times coming in late at night leaving early in the morning. Our journey together was awesome so thanks to this park. We had and made wonderful memories, and I will always cherish them. Charlie boy My BFF, my partner! THANK YOU!

Love this place and almost everything about it. Volunteers/staff are awesome and do a tremendously great job with the Rv sites. Not once have I come here and seen it with trash everywhere. They really keep everything up to standard. The rules can be a bit much like usual in Cali. However, if it's a rule, it's because someone has most likely abused the system or couldn't use common sense. I have seen both, so can't be mad at the park for that.

name: Sun Outdoors San Diego Bay
formatted_address: 825 E St, Chula Vista, CA 91910, USA

google_reviews.text: We just arrived yesterday, but this is a beautiful RV/vacation cottages resort with an excellent pool area, immaculate grounds, and amazing location that is central to everything you could want or need! Bar and café right next to the pool. Rv sites are equipped with Adirondack chairs and gas firepits. Very well maintained. Staff is super friendly and helpful. Looking forward to enjoying the next two months here!

We LOVED this place. My wife and I took our four teenage boys here, and it was just perfect. The little cottage was great, the grounds were clean and nice, and the facilities were awesome. We loved the pool. Highly recommend this place for families!

Sun Outdoors San Diego Bay is a beautiful, well-maintained resort with a lot going for it. The grounds are immaculate, the gym is nice for an RV resort, and the heated pool area is excellent. Most notably, the staff across the board were impeccable—friendly, professional, responsive, and clearly invested in maintaining a high standard for the property.

Stayed for one month for about $2,000 + Electric.
That said, many of the other reviews are absolutely on point regarding the massive levels of noise and guest behavior, especially on weekends and around holidays. There were multiple nights where children were running and screaming until 2–3 a.m., often with parents who appeared blackout drunk and completely disengaged. On top of that, the constant interstate noise of cars and motorcycles racing through the nearby exits, combined with frequent police sirens and train horns, made for an extremely loud and annoying stay.

Because of this, I would only recommend this specific location if you are comfortable sleeping with earplugs every night (as we did), can turn off hearing aids, or if noise simply doesn’t bother you. The resort itself is fantastic, but the surrounding noise and weekend chaos are significant factors that potential guests should be aware of before booking.

(Visited mid October, a Sunday through Friday morning) This is a very well maintained RV park. Pretty bay and sunset views 15-30 mins from major attractions. Great walking and hiking right outside the gate. The well marked sites are spacious and every site has a dining or picnic table and a gas fire pit with 4 Adirondack chairs. Water, sewer, and electricity hookups are good. Sites are really level but drain water well (rained one day). This is the only place I have camped in 5 years where I did not need any leveling blocks under jacks or tires.

Facilities are wonderful: pool, bar, cafe, hot tubs. Sky deck lounge. Restrooms and showers are clean and well lit. Secure gated entry for site and you need a key card for gym, restrooms, showers. There is an Amazon Locker on site. There is a camp store with essentials. The staff is super friendly. Landscaping is well looked after. We toured the cabins and they are first class, spacious for a cabin, well equipped for an extended stay.

We arrived on a Saturday, and the park was completely full up. There were only 1 or 2 empty spots in the whole place. We had never been here before so I had no idea what to expect. It was crazy busy with people running around, bikes, dogs, kids, scooters. It's definitely great for families with kids and dogs, but not so much for us older people who love peace and quiet. Overall it was a nice experience. 75% of the spots opened up by Sunday afternoon, and it was super calm and quiet after that. The biggest bummer though was the constant sewage smell, which got much worse in the evening. The bathrooms and showers were clean and always stocked. The food in the café was really good. The staff were helpful and friendly. If we come back, it will definitely be during the week.

name: San Diego Metro KOA Resort
formatted_address: 111 N 2nd Ave, Chula Vista, CA 91910, USA

google_reviews.text:
We had a great time at the KOA. The staff was super friendly and always helpful. We booked a deluxe poolside cabin. The kitchen had dishes and pots etc. The gas stove was a little finicky and the microwave only worked some of the time, but the staff was pretty responsive and tried to help where possible.
The pool was fun and we were very glad to have the hot tub. This time of year (February) the pool was cold, but it didn't keep the kids from swimming in it. The campground showers and bathrooms were clean and well kept.
We wished the jumping pillows were open longer hours. We enjoyed the fun family bikes they had for rent though.
Overall, great stay and we will be visiting again!

You must realize you are camping in a dense populated location. I think it's unfair reading some of the reviews on this page that people were upset about hearing the freeway. That's the cost of camping directly in San Diego.

Amenities were great, staff were VERY friendly and helpful (ask them were to eat around town), and sites were tight but manageable.

Cons - staff were very intense about a certain rules like at the bounce thing, but the pool was the Wild West with young teenagers. I felt I had to tell some of them off because they were rough housing around little kids.

Also, it gets pretty wild at night. Kids were riding bikes, being loud, playing on the basketball court until midnight.

The food place there was really good! And fair priced.

Had a terrible experience here. We were staying there 5 nights in a camping cabin. The second night our fire alarm started chirping. It was around 6 am. We let the front desk know and they said they would send someone down to take care of it. I didn’t see anyone come but were so busy running back and forth to the pool I hoped I had just missed them. That night around 1 am we found out it had not been taken care of when the chirping started again. We called and the after hours man came and removed the unit for us. The next day we were told it was fixed but that night at 3 am it started chirping AGAIN. This time we could hear other units around us also chirping. The man who came said the battery didn’t look new. the next day I went to the front desk and asked what the heck was up . Three nights in a row! She said well have you told anyone about this? As if I hadn’t. Have I ever. We had talked to people at the desk. We had texted their help line and called. She was brisk and didn’t really want to hear the whole story. They finally put in a new unit and that night fire alarm chirping did not keep us up. So 2 stars for the 2 nights we actually got some sleep. This was an issue that should not have continued more than one night and customer service and communication had huge room for improvement.

My godson wanted to go camping for the first time, it was his mother's worst nightmare. So we did the least outdoorsy camping and all were happy. This location is in the middle of San Diego, you can see the freeway and hear loud cars but it was great! It really did feel like we were out of town even though we only drove 20 minutes to get there. It is amazing for families, so many things for the kids to do and the best part for the tired parents, the pool and hot tubs close late. Perfect way to end the day after kid friendly activities all day. We were there for the fall festival, with a costume parade, pumpkin carving, Halloween decor prices. It was lovely. The community kitchen is very convenient, the baths and showers were clean. I'm very tempted to check out other KOA locations, maybe in an area with less light pollution to look at the stars

I highly recommend this place to literally everyone, no matter how high maintenance you are. I guarantee this to be a stay you won't regret or want to leave. I will always stay here when visiting San Diego. No matter the time of year. They have everything you want, need, and wouldn't expect a KOA to have. I can't wait to go back.

name: Tijuana River Valley Regional Park
formatted_address: 2721 Monument Rd, San Diego, CA 92154, USA

google_reviews.text:
Nice park right on the border between the US and Mexico.

It's incredible to see the wall between the countries and how the city of Tijuana is built right next to it, whereas San Diego is quite a bit further north.

If you want to see the border from relatively up close, when driving keep heading towards the wall as you approach this park, there's other trails where you can take clearer pictures after the water treatment facility and the winding road.

Beautiful campground,  everything looks pretty new, restrooms were clean,  campsite were spacious, almost semi private with some plants and space dividing each campsite. There is also a fun playground for the kids made all from wood and ropes, my kids loved it. We felt save,  park rangers are always there circling around. But there were many many many sand flies, we came back home with at least 50 bites so make sure to take a good brand of repellent because the brand I took didn't do much. We reserved for 2 nights but only spent 1 night, and came back home, the sand flies and the heat were too much for us. Also the water from the shower is too hot, there were only 1 temperature set which was really hot. There's a beach close to the campground but I think it's not safe for swimming because the water quality is not good. I don't think I will come back to this campground. Even though it's a beautiful and very clean campground,  the sand flies kills the moment

This is a great place to camp. Clean, quiet, super helpful/respectful staff. The weather can be extreme. Be prepared.  The firewood is a great deal at $5 a bundle. Hike 1 mile West you'll hit the ocean. No condos 7-11 Starbucks. Just OCEAN 🌊 BE SURE TO CHECK THE WATER QUALITY! No swim days are frequent. Thanks for the sewage Mexico! $$ at $24 a night this place is a bargain.

Such a beautiful place to come one of my favorites with plenty of trails to walk and hidden areas. Most trails are clearly marked. Came multiple times and still haven't walked all trails. Some benches to sit and relax, trash cans at the parking area and near the butterfly garden they will have maps of the trail systems. Annoying little flies bug spray works almost all the time.

We felt safe hiking up the hill near the border here. Well maintained trail, but maps could be improved. Great views of ocean and south bay.  - there is still flooding in lower area so we didnt explore trails in the valley

name: Fiddler's Cove Marina & RV Park (Navy MWR)
formatted_address: 3205 CA-75, San Diego, CA 92155, USA

google_reviews.text:
1. Park Quality - Lots of really great things about this park. Clean laundry, ($1.50 for wash and $1.00 for dry) Wide paved road keeps park noise down. Access to water recreation rental fun.

2. Sites - All sites are concrete pads that are fairly level. Utilities were good with the exception of extremely hard water. Sadly goat head thorns were in abundance.
