My dog had her paw punctured and it occurred on our pad, not in the gravel or on a trail, I used my leaf blower daily to ensure they didn’t reappear. They were also in the gravel surrounding our site. (one note; there is a wonderful trail out the back side of the park and I found no thorns here.)

3. Location - Its location to Coronado and San Diego make it amazing. But, with the park right on  CA-78, NOISE (see next item)

4. Noise - The noise from CA-78 was off the charts. It seemed like every vehicle was racing and all had modified exhausts to increase the noise level. Add in motorcycles and diesel trucks and I promise you peace does not exist. (Yes, I’m old and cantankerous)

5. Cell Service - Verizon was three bars of 5G

6. Wifi - I do not remember if there was park WiFi as I had no notes on it. Starlink was 220 Mbps down and 35 Mbps up.

7. Rates - $76 a day

8. Access - The park is not on an installation but there is a gate that requires card key access after normal business hours. (provided by the office at check in)

9. Dog Friendliness - Despite the presence of the goat head thorns, I would say this is very dog friendly. The great trail out the back of the park along, the Bayshore bike path out front and the off leash park at the entry parking lot, mean there are a lot of places you and your pup can explore. And, a short drive up the road is an off leash dog beach!

Wrapping it up. This was my final stop in California and of all the military “Famcamps” south of LA this was the best. I would definitely stay here again. (just knowing I need to be more vigilant over the goat head thorns)
Large full sevice campsites on the bay side of The Silver Strand.  The beach is great for walks.
Clean, well kept military rv park but there are no fire pits and people aren’t particularly friendly which we found odd.

Nice quiet campground for the military, veterans, and their dependents. Call MWR to make a reservation. Access to the bay as well as rentals, clean restrooms and showers, and helpful staff made this a very pleasant stay.

We love Fiddlers Cove. It is very nice, but the price has doubled in the last eight years. It is only for active duty military and retired veterans. I wonder how junior members of the military can afford it.

name: Oakoasis County Preserve Campsite
formatted_address: Lakeside, CA 92040, USA

google_reviews.text: This a nature preserve not a campground open 8 a.m. – sunset, daily, no overnight stays.

This campsite is only open to non-profits.  I attended a Cub scout campout here.  For San Diegans this place is extremely convenient.  For a weekend getaway with a pack of little ones the place is great.  You can see there was a lot of investment in this park.  Unfortunately the well system has struggled to remove microorganisms that make the well water not potable.  The bathrooms have operational flush toilets, but the sinks and camp taps have been taped off while they continue to work on getting the bromine treatment system to produce consistent results.  In the meantime the super friendly rangers help keep your jugs topped off.  All that said, just about anyone in San Diego could drive home and shower if so desired.

While being so close, it is nestled well into the canyon away from the sprawling housing and freeway noise.  The place just accommodates a single group, so little ones are free to roam the space without disturbing strangers.  There are day hikers passing through.  The trail is well maintained and brings you to a beautiful overlook above the reservoir.  Most of the trail is shaded beneath trees.  A great hike for small children.

There is a huge covered group dining area with an outdoor kitchen space.  No sinks or gas lines.  A good sized charcoal grill setup and lots of counter space.  There are plenty of picnic tables.  There is a 115V AC plug that may be accessed with an extension cord.  Requires ranger approval, I believe.

It's a beautiful space for a quick weekend outing with small children.  If only they could get that water system working correctly.
Good campsite. Plenty of space. Flushing toilets and one shower.
Amazing campground. Ranger Keith is super helpful. Highly recommend for any scouts
Nice area for hikes or just a nice walk with your fourlegged friends just keep them safe by keeping them leashed for their protection

name: Santee Lake rv Resort
formatted_address: Sycamore Canyon Rd, Santee, CA 92071, USA

google_reviews.text: We enjoyed the rv park. We were excited about fishing, but after 4 days and over $100 in permits and bait, not even a nibble. I did get a bass on a lure after a few casts.

We camped here in an RV for the month of March. PROS... Spacious sites, very clean, good playgrounds, nice lakes, fun feeding ducks, no one was catching any fish while we were there.  CONS... NO CELL SERVICE (We have Verizon and have never before had a problem)  We had to drive 2 miles to the front of the park to get any cell service. Horrible WiFi..... Nothing usable.  And their TV is a joke.... You only get a few stations and, especially in the evenings, the picture will just freeze with lines through it, prohibiting any further viewing (see photo attached).  The office will simply tell you to contact the WiFi company, or cable company.  But everyone else we talked with during our 30 day stay complained about the same things. We were told we were in a dead zone for cell service - between an airport and military base.   Sites are very sandy.  Even with a mat down, lots of it makes it's way into RV.

We would recommend this place for a day use... But not for any lengthy stay.

Great family getaway even if you live nearby.  We had such a great time at all the attractions they have to offer. Lakes, playgrounds, water park, trails.
All campsites are well maintained and clean. The staff could be a little friendlier but most are volunteer that live on-site.
Overall great trip.
Great place to camp. Serene, quiet, shade trees, and close to stores and restaurants.

Best RV campsite in San Diego for family spend weekends. More affordable and much better than those occean campsites. There are plenty of sites here. There are 7 lakes, ducks around everywhere, water playground, several playgrounds, boats rental… Riding bike will be a good choice.
Fin Fish restaurant provide delicious food.

name: Admiral Baker Park - RV Campground & Picnic Areas
formatted_address: 2400 Admiral Baker Rd, San Diego, CA 92124, USA

google_reviews.text:
1. Park Quality - I have very mixed thoughts about the overall park. There is a lot of beauty here with a nice proximity to the golf course. (a huge benefit if you golf) But, the noise from Friar Rd, the narrowness of the park road which make vehicles going by your site exceptionally loud, the goat head thorns, and just a general malaise from maintenance about the upkeep of the RV part of the park.

2. Sites - All sites are concrete pads that are fairly level. Utilities were good with the exception of extremely hard water. My site had loads of goat head thorns and I actually removed a half dozen plants. Spacing between sites was decent.

3. Location - The park is somewhat isolated and is not walkable to anything. Commissary and BX were a bit of a drive. (Mainly I went to Miramar)

4. Noise - While I was there, the motorcycles and diesel pickups were very disturbing as they left for work around 5:30 am. (speed also seems to be something they could care less about) Then there was Friar Rd. There was absolutely no peace and quiet as this road was a main thoroughfare for every loud vehicle on their way to and from work. And finally throw in the early morning golf maintenance and it’s a full blown sound nightmare. (I was in a site right along the golf course)

5. Cell Service - Verizon was three bars of 5G

6. Wifi - Park WiFi was 20 Mbps down and 3 Mbps up. For my Starlink, I got 178 Mbps down and 11 Mbps up.

7. Rates - $67 a day plus $3 a day for a dog

8. Access - The park is not on an installation.

9. Dog Friendliness - The presence of the goat head thorns and NO DOG signs make me say they are not very friendly to dogs. There is a small off leash park at the very back but it is overrun with gophers.

Wrapping it up. There are a lot of negative things I had to say about this place and the longer I am away the more I am convinced I would not return. If I did not have a dog, that might change my view of this park
A great place  for the whole family, for a large group, there is a play area and a  restroom , there is a large parking lot.

They are increasing prices again. Nothing has been done to the park to excuse the price hike.  The bugs are terrible.  There is no pool and the washer and dryers are expensive and do not work.  Campland or Sun outdoors is worth it.. not here

(We did not use for camping or stay here, we used the park) We did a party here recently. The location was close to the park and we were able to have a bouncehouse. They allowed us to rent out a complete area with easy access to clean bathrooms. Parking was really easy. The park was well maintained, and we didn't get accosted by bugs. I would recommend for bday parties or events. It was beautiful here. You do have to rent through their office, but they were really helpful when needed. They follow up on phone calls and assist when asked.
Quiet country setting. Next to golf course

name: Thousand Trails Pio Pico
formatted_address: 14615 Otay Lakes Rd, Jamul, CA 91935, USA

google_reviews.text: I love how this campground is spacious.  There are many sights but each sight has plenty of room.  Loved how we could open up our back patio in a field and still felt very spaced out.  People are friendly and the location is perfect as it is out in the boonies but still close enough to San Diego in minutes.  So quiet and peaceful.  The scenery is amazing.

We are new to the Thousand Trails community and we loved our stay at Pio Pico.  It’s beautiful and peaceful here. The grounds were very well kept and you could see some new landscaped areas have been recently added. The pool/spa facilities were nice and clean as were the bathrooms. All the staff we ran into were friendly. Cell reception (Tmobile)was no good and I never did figure out how to join the paid WiFi through Jabba. But we went up to the activities center for the free WiFi for school and it worked great. The shop at the entrance of the grounds was very handy, well stocked and had reasonable prices compared to most of the facilities we have had experience with. The long term residents keep their spaces well cared for and the ones we interacted with were very nice. Thanks for the great stay!

We want to start of by saying we have stayed here at least a dozen times now, and as of late it seems to have been a little more comfortable. But I do not want to speak to soon. Normally the woman workers harass the hell out of us making our stay so stressed that we hate it. They always knit pick and harass you for any minor issue or they may make things up. They also do not enforce rules equally. Our family comes here to camp, getaway and have a good time. Not to be scolded by the ladies that work here because they are so miserable that they take it out on everyone else.I say ladies because the few men that we have ran into have been so respectful and pleasant to us it's almost as if we are in a whole different RV park! I noticed they do not like ppl golf color ,ppl with kids ,ppl with animals ,or ppl. Our last stay felt a tad more laid back for some reason. The lady that rides around on the golf cart just to pick on people and write them up everyday for no reason was not making her rounds 3 times a day, their were no new rules made up our whole stay ( shocker) we actually had a decent time . Who is to say it's going to stay like that or go back to they're old harassing ways. I guess time will tell.

This location is a great place , they need and employee upgrade because these ladies are mean and treat you like a pile of dirt ,Maybe they are forgetting we pay for a service that they are not providing ,treating us like dirt is not in the contract i signed and does not happen at other 1000 trails . since the change of rules here by someone who knows nothing of running a park obliviously everything has went downhill. Now they went cashless you can no longer use USA currency at the pio pico campground whats next will you force us to stay insie at certain times. I read a previous review calling this place pio pico prison that is spot on the ladies treat this like boot camp and they are in-charge .You are not allowed to relax or have fun or they will penalize or retaliate and lie on you be carfull

The guys that work here are so nice. The females I don't know what's going on but they are so mean all the time or they just don't like people of color. They nitpick on us all the time every little thing they complain about we're supposed to be here camping having a good time that's not what's going on. Everybody else in the park is having a good time barbecuing camping we're doing the same thing but some reason these ladies find something to yell at us about every single day. It wasn't worth buying the membership to be harassed and discriminated against every single day our cars parked the wrong way our tents up the wrong way our animals barking too loud or animals barking the wrong way We have our animal outside the wrong way We drove too fast we drove too slow We looked at them the wrong way I mean I could go on meanwhile the full-timers here have full reign of breaking the rules or if they like you oh do anything you want. On top of this they add their own rules on to things Don't have any guests because they're going to charge $10 per guest Are you serious You're here camping you've already paid your fees now you want to charge more money because somebody comes to see you greedy people This is by far one of the worst thousand trails we stay at other thousand trails we do not experience any of this harassment these women have a power trip here. When the gentleman are working none of this happens only when the women are working for some reason. I thought it was because I was a man nope My wife tries to speak to them and they treat her worse. They offer us a free night off if we give them a five-star review sorry not going to do it people need to understand what's really going on here. Don't bring your children because they're not allowed to do anything This is Pio Pico prison and the rules change daily. I don't know why the regional office does not step in and do something about this place. I called corporate once and they said thousand Trails Pio Pico is Pio Pico's armpit of parks . Assuming it's the one they care about the least . Every morning a crabby lady rides around on a golf cart picking on certain people leave us alone let us have a good time at your park it's not picking on us stop making up a rules if it's in the rules we follow them stop adding your own to them please This is going to result in a lawsuit.

name: Mulberry Loop Campground
formatted_address: Santee, CA 92071, USA

google_reviews.text: Clean, responsive staff.  Cabins were reasonably well maintained and had everything needed.  Less than courteous reminder call to family members to vacate the rv grounds, and a less than courteous interaction from grounds keepers to keep dogs within a certain zone.  several other visitors were ignoring the notices about the pets and enforcement was spotty and inconsistent.
Stayed in a cabin. Weather was not good we still had a good time. Bedroom TV was very tiny, large fridge made it nice for all our food and drinks. Freezer had two ice trays. Bring trash bags.

Most expensive campground I have camped at in 3 years. Overpriced to be a sardine on a muddy site. Signage here is not great. Seems to be very loosely run. No contact from camp host. Appears to be a lot of permanent residents living here full time. Hook up are better situated than some sites, but this is not a repeat location for me.
We stayed in the cabin very beautiful. Fishing better in 1-3 no fish in 8. Walks are awesome, just very relax after day cleaning house.
Good access to lake, pools, and surrounding parks. The RV parking spots are in a parking lot, so it doesn't feel like wilderness, but the hookups are nice for an extended stay.

name: Iron Horse Area
formatted_address: Telegraph Way, San Diego, CA 92110, USA

google_reviews.text: This place located near Old Town trolley is a scetchy place to say the least. I wouldn't trust it. Or leave things in your car
Nice and clean grounds and landscaping make your switchover a lil more pleasant . And people here were very helpful on where I needed to go next
Old Town trolley station

Has gotten worse.
Basically a homeless camp.
Smells gross.
California has let this place down.
Good! 👍🏿

name: Whitewater Preserve
formatted_address: 9160 Whitewater Canyon Rd, Whitewater, CA 92282, USA

google_reviews.text:
Highly recommend. Just be prepared to park on the road and walk at least .75 miles to the entrance if you visit on the weekend.
There were a handful of volunteers and staff working who were all very friendly. Love the information center. Restrooms were clean. Great area for a basic camping trip if you can score a spot.

Beautiful preserve! Super refreshing coming up from hot Palm Springs! Very different from hiking in other places around. The visitors center area is beautiful and the river is nice and clear (at least the little bit of it that was there in late November). Lovely! And free!

This is a beautiful preserve! We were truly blessed to discover this place and spend 2 nights camping here. The kids loved it! The wading pond was super fun, and hanging out by the river was amazing. We were so lucky to have seen bighorn sheep near the entrance gate on day 2. The bathrooms were super clean! There are no trash cans provided so if you camp here please bring trash bags to pack out what you pack in. We loved the big green and open space for the tent camping. Just loved everything and will be back!

A nice escape from the desert, up in the hills, with some nice features, wildlife, and a picnic area with several tables (some covered). It's close enough to the Palm Springs area for an easy drive, although the parking is a bit limited. We arrived mid-morning and had to park down the road a bit from the visitors center. When we left, people were parking much further down the road. The visitor center has some good information, and several hummingbird feeders that were very popular. There are several water features near the center that make it kind of unique for the area, and you can even hike over to the main wash area and other areas as well. We did not hike extensively, but the trail markers seemed a bit ambiguous so you might want to come armed with an app or pre-loaded maps because cell coverage is limited there. We enjoyed our visit, the scenery and the nice staff members - one of which pointed out some bighorn sheep visible up on the mountain.

A beautiful oasis surrounded by incredible hiking trails and the perfect escape from the heat. I actually found this place by accident while staying at a nearby Airbnb, and I’m honestly shocked that more people in Palm Springs don’t know about it. Especially in the summer months. It’s such a pleasure to come here when it’s noticeably cooler, often around 20 degrees cooler than Palm Springs, and the scenery makes it feel like a completely different world. Hiking here before or after the peak of summer is an entirely different and much more enjoyable experience. I wouldn’t recommend hiking in the middle of summer because even though it’s cooler than Palm Springs, it’s still hot. That said, this place is absolutely beautiful and well worth the visit.

name: Andreas Canyon Trailhead
formatted_address: S Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text: Andreas Canyon is a peaceful hidden gem and a perfect trail for most hikers. The loop is just over a mile, making it an easy and enjoyable walk without feeling rushed. Lush palm trees line the canyon, creating a beautiful contrast against the stunning rock formations that surround you. The sound of water and the natural stillness make it feel like a true desert oasis—serene, grounding, and incredibly zen. A must-visit if you’re looking for beauty, tranquility, and an easy escape into nature in Indian Canyon.

Andreas Canyon is a brief yet captivating 2-mile round-trip hike. Rated as moderate, it's a family-friendly trail. The walk along the creek, complete with tiny waterfalls, makes this trail exceptionally scenic. With picnic tables near the parking area, visitors can sit back and enjoy the tranquil surroundings.

I'm not a hiker. I'm a pathwalker. This 1.2 mi loop trail to the tip of the canyon was doable for me. I was able to negotiate the uneven path and a few sandy rock areas where your shoe can slip on the grit. Start to the left if you need an easier path and only go halfway and turn around. We started to the right and I was glad we did the rougher part first and then the easier half as we returned to our car.
Not suitable for preschoolers or those who need walkers.

We just did the simple trail here and it was a pretty good hike around the oasis. Very scenic and we were not rushed. Nice to be able to take your time, sit, and enjoy nature and the sound of the stream. Also such a close drive from downtown Palm Springs.

This was a nice little loop that was perfect for something short and sweet if you're visiting the area! It's on a reservation and there is a fee to get in (about $15 per adult), the gates also close at 4pm, but it was worth it because the trails and roads are super well maintained it seemed. This loop had water running through and was really fun to go through and look around.

name: The Bump And Grind
formatted_address: Painters Path, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: Love this hike..though in my opinion the rating of "Moderate" under sell the difficulty of the hike! Recently I set out for what I thought was a 4 mile hike..after all that's what the app says..attached is what my hike ended up being a less than 2 mile hike..AND it took almost 2 hours..what you say?..This dude must be totally out of shape..Ha!..I'm not! Mile 1 had nearly 1200 feet of Elevation Gain..thats like climbing up a Ladder for an hour!..There are a # of trail options presented to you as you ascend..be mindful of the route you want to take!..great views from the top!
Highly recommend early morning hikes, it's less crowded so you don't have to stop or move over as much when people pass. It's a really great place to start your day!☀️
Really nice trail, but many areas have extremely narrow pathways and it does get crowded the closer you get to sunset.

Active and somewhat crowded trail, but it's always noce to see people getting some good exercise.  I just did a short 3 mile loop (there are several options), but it had almost 1,000 feet of vertical gain snd a steep area of descent.  Not for the faint of heart, but great views up top!

Best local conditioning hike. Trail is a 3.mile loop with 800 ft elevation gain, then seasonal add-on trail going another 200 ft elevation gain.  But shortcut up the middle ridge has an 800 ft elevation gain in just 1/2 mile, so it's good for exercising quads in your legs, then you can take gradual trail back down either side for 1.5 mile, or just go straight back down in 1/2 mile. In peak shape, you can do the shortcut trail in 15 minutes going up, and 15 minutes back down.

name: Palm Desert Cross Hiking Trail
formatted_address: Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a great place to hike! Multiple trails and amazing views of the surrounding desert. Be prepared. It is rocky and parts are pretty steep depending on the path you take. The main trail narrows as you get closer to the cross with two-way traffic so you need to step aside at times to let others pass. It was already pretty busy at 8am. You are out in the open, with full sun. Hat and sunglasses for sure! Dogs are allowed.

Great hike. The beginning has a very very steep start but once you’re past that it’s smooth hiking from there very exposed with NO shade at all so plan ahead and use good hiking judgement. Great views along the trail and at the top.
Great hike. Moderate difficulty. Total sun exposure. Well traveled, many nice people.

This is a great hike and workout. It's so refreshing and peaceful on your journey up to the cross and down. Amazing scenery! Fellow hikers are very kind and they really respect the natural landscape and wildlife. Highly recommend. You can go solo, bring your kids, friends, family...

If u want a fast and semi heart pumping hike. This is your spot. Easy hike, a little steep but one of my favorite hikes.
I've took my whole family hiking here and they loved it. If u time it right. It's a 30 min hike up and 20-30 min hike down. Only because I jog it down the mountain.

And an awesome view of the valley 🤩

Would recommend 100%!

name: Cactus to Clouds Trailhead
formatted_address: Skyline Trail, Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text: Nature is gorgeous, but the trail could have been marked better. We lost it a few times, and it was especially unfortunate during the steeper section right before the tram station.

Fantastic! This is a great trail with plenty of fantastic views whether you plan on a hike for a short while or an entire trek. Lots of rocks and gravel, and it’s rough on the knees, so be careful and come prepared. Bring water, and lots of it, because you’ll dry out quick!
Parking at the trailhead was easy, not many people attempting this every day. Trail was tough especially miles 8-10. After that the trail to the peak was not difficult. Absolutely beautiful
Fun trail with great views and if you really push you can end up on top of the mountain

Amazing trail, make sure to bring a friend or two, and a decent bit of supplies for it, I would personally recommend going in the early morning, so that you can avoid the sun, or go during winter. It isn’t the best marked, so I’d recommend using another app for directions.
Parking is a little tough, however if you go early, then you are able to park on the street near the museum and trailhead.

name: Art Smith Trailhead
formatted_address: Roy Wilson Memorial Hwy, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: This trail is a little steep at first but then is easier for miles...  With spectacular views.
Great hike in the desert and even saw a few bighorn sheep.  There are some steep parts, but overall the trail is pretty easy.
Amazing art work and talent. Fun thing to do with your loved ones and pets too. Awesome

We visited this trailhead on on a day in January. The temperatures were cool, and we enjoyed our walk along the valley floor and then up along the side of the mountain. Much to our delight, a herd of over 20 bighorn sheep came down the mountain across the valley from us, walked across the valley and then headed up the hill very close to us. It was a thrilling encounter.

This trail is very exposed with absolutely no shade. If it were a hot day it would be a brutal hike. The trail zigzags up the mountain to an overlook point and can be steep in places and is Rocky. I would exercise caution if you or anybody with unsure footing. The smartest of hikers along the path had hiking poles and that certainly would have made a big difference.
One of the most beautiful hikes in Coachella Valley.

name: Coachella Valley Preserve - Thousand Palms Oasis Preserve
formatted_address: 29200 Thousand Palms Canyon Rd, Thousand Palms, CA 92276, USA

google_reviews.text:
This natural area is beautiful. Walk along the trail to Simone Pond and then up to the vista spot on the ridge. Surreal landscape and photo ops.

However, I was very saddened to see funding dry up for this beautiful natural area. There’s a Native American gentleman who is living on the property and acts as a tour guide. His name is James and he enriched our visit with local history. It was a wonderful afternoon.
Beautiful place.  A literal oasis!  It's closed because of a flood just park on the street and walk under the gate like everyone else.  Also, stay off the restoration areas

Ww hiked about 5 miles along the ridge, down into valleys, into Oasis, up to a flat grassland area, and back down the ridge.
Great variety of terrain and views.   We got there around 7am, very few people.
It's worth the effort!

Well worth stopping here. You can go on some short walks or make it longer.  There was a volunteer to answer questions.
It had such a tropical feeling with the beautiful palms. Is also right on the San Andreas Fault!
Also free, but they will take donations

Coachella Valley Preserve is a treasure off the beaten path outside Palm Springs. A hurricane has damaged the preserve's board walk loop that had been a main attraction. However, what remains is still worth a stop. We hiked up one of the trails that overlooks the area. It was a nice one hour stop to see these palm trees that look like they are some how prehistoric and feels like a scene straight out of Jurassic Park.

name: Deer Springs Trailhead
formatted_address: Deer Spring Trail, Idyllwild-Pine Cove, CA 92549, USA

google_reviews.text: This was an intense hike, I read online easy/medium difficulty but I say Medium/hard difficulty level!! Great workout uphill pretty much the whole way. I only saw squirrels and I asked other hikers if they saw any animals and they said “squirrels “ too. But that was my first time and I shall return!! I’m a regular hiker and this brought me a better workout and gorgeous trees, tree roots, rocks of all sizes and huge pine cones! Now this hike could be less intense if you sit and rest on a rock or tree stump along the way. But just know you will be going up, up, and up from the start!! Great cardio and peaceful place for meditation and such. At the end of my hike I saw a crow perched on a tree and he let me snap a photo so enjoy my pics! -A

(Suicide rock 10/6/24) 🏔🏔

🔴Warning: You NEED a permit to go on this trail HOWEVER the permit is completely free. Just go to the ranger station down the road, go up to the board outside of the station, grab a permit from the "all other trails" box, fill the permit and leave the white paper on the board and keep the yellow. 🔴

Great trail. Was pretty rocky but we start at 7am and ended at 11am. The conditions were great and trail was easy to follow as long as you don't go off it. The views at the top of suicide rock were great also. ⚠️If you do this hike, definitely start early (5-8am). ⚠️There was no crowd really for being the most popular trail in idyllwild. If you come definitely do this hike. A moderate 7 miles.

Started around 6:30am to hike up to San Jacinto peak. There’s a small parking lot right at the trailhead that was empty.

Stayed on the trail 95% of the time and by the end I logged 18 miles total. So it’s a little more than AllTrails says but I definitely didn’t hit 20 miles.

Trail is pretty easy to follow but I needed to check the app a couple times when I had lost it.

Lot of solitude on this trail, didn’t see anyone till 5+ miles on the way up and then the other trails merge so there’s more foot traffic.

Views are beautiful. It gets pretty cold up there with the wind chills especially. I went through 4.5L of water and could’ve drank more. Bring plenty of food and water and layers and don’t be a doofus and forget your trekking poles like me. Luckily someone left one at the trailhead and I used a stick for my left hand.

Happy hiking and be safe!

The first approximately two miles are largely manzanita and oak trees (worth coming back for to see fall colors if you get the timing right), with a few peeks through the trees (the views are better going back down). The mile to Suicide Rock has better views of Tahquitz and the surrounding area, plus a couple of easy creek crossings.

Once you’re at the main viewpoint (which is a big payoff), I suggest walking around a bit and exploring the different views, which are quite spectacular and look down at Idyllwild. AllTrails noted a “true peak,” so I looked around for an unofficial trail of foot tracks, but couldn’t find anything leading up to what looked like would be blocked views by trees anyway.

The trail has plenty of shade. Expect an uphill hike with some big steps. However, unlike Devil’s Slide, this trail evens out every so often, giving you a few seconds to catch your breath. I would rate it as moderately difficult. If you can handle the elevation change, you can handle this.
Very calm trail. Not too extrenous if you have some conditioning. 3.4 mi up and another down for a total of 6.8 miles. Came early in the morning so no problem finding parking

name: Indian Canyons
formatted_address: 38520 S Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text: $12 entry fee gets you access to stunning scenery and gorgeous hikes of varying strain and skill levels. Gorgeous, though not all the trails are clearly marked or thoroughly kept. There are shaded ones that are beginner friendly near the beginning if you want something simple. Bring water and sunscreen, it gets hot. Parking is also a little wonky depending on how full the park is.

Honestly hate giving it a 4 star review as the place is beautiful. The loss came from the staff. Lady at the check in booth was rude to the point I mention it. Guys in the trail area were very nice and helpful tho. So dinging this for a start gives some idea of how much I appreciated the check in lady.
I'm any case, it's a fantastic place. It's not free, this, so expect to pay to hike. The views are fabulous and it's a great place to relax. For a fee.

The Oasis is beautiful, rugged. It is almost 1.5 miles inside the main gate. Not accessible. Plenty of Parking Blocks though it can get busy as this a very popular spot. The entry gate can have lots of traffic, it has 2 payment spots. We went on a weekday and had a wait time of 30 minutes on the main gate but parking was breeze. Surely for trek goers and a good photogenic location for instagrammers.

I attempted both the Andreas Canyon Loop and Murray Canyon to Seven Sisters Falls. Both trails are listed as 'easy'. The Andreas Loop was the easier of the two, though still had a couple narrow sections which skirt around one side of the canyon.  Murray Canyon required a short hike within the wilderness area to reach the trailhead. This trail is an out-and-back, and with the stream crossing at multiple points required a bit of balancing over rocks both to and from the trail. For me, I made it within 3/4 mile of the end before turning back to potentially avoid hiking with wet shoes 😅. When I'm back in Palm Springs I'll bring an extra pair and a towel to make it all the way 🙏

If you take longer hikes, be sure to plan to be back at your car by 5pm. There were multiple signs warning of towing after the canyon closes at 5pm.
Absolutely stunning place with short or long hikes. Well worth the entry price and time to drive - only 15 mins from downtown.

name: Mission Creek Preserve
formatted_address: 60550 Mission Creek Rd, Desert Hot Springs, CA 92240, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful hike. Amazing nature and a little stream of water at the moment. Various options with different hike lengths. We did the 3 miles, and it was enjoyable. It's best to submit the online form to be able to park inside. There's a dirt road that takes you to the actual hike.

Nice easy hike. You drive a little over 2 miles on a dirt road to main parking area which most car can do. Lots of parking available. The creek had cold flowing water and is the first thing you notice. On the hike you first hit the stone houses, which have benches inside to rest. You then hit the green wet lands, which are beautiful. We only went about 1.5 miles in and then went back. The 1.5 mile mark is the other camping area with bathrooms and more stone structures and benches. Definitely worth the time to check it out.

The Mission Creek preserve is an easy and good hike to go for the day. Parking is limited but this is not as popular so most of the time you will be fine. Getting here is a dirt road from the 62. The road is in good condition and any car will make it. There are a few points of interest like the cabins in the entrance or the stone house at the end. But the creek is the most fun. This is the desert and it is exposed so take a hat and water. It will not be enjoyable in the summer as temps are way too hot. There are restrooms by the stone house and you can cross the creek if you want to just keep going past that. The trail will even connect you with white water preserve if you want to extend the hike even further. Take snacks as there are tables inside the stone house for you to rest. Protect this area and keep it beautiful. Dog and kid friendly.
Great hike. Easy to moderate.

An easy and beautiful trail that can be enjoyed with children and dogs. There is a gradual elevation gain to about 300ft, but you hardly notice it as you’re busy admiring the mountain vistas.

Today there are still snow capped peaks in the San Jacinto range which can be seen from the trail. There is an abundance of yellow, white, blue and purple flowers, fully carpeting the ground in places. The normally dry, scrubby vegetable on the hillsides is brilliant green and mixed with patches of flowers.

Definitely a must do in the Palm Springs area.

name: Thousand Palms Oasis Preserve
formatted_address: Thousand Palms Canyon Rd, Desert Hot Springs, CA 92241, USA

google_reviews.text:
Luckily the rain held off just long enough to experience the area. If you plan on visiting when the sun is out I definitely recommend carrying water and a snack.
Always pack out what ever you bring in.  No dogs allowed.

Beautiful and peaceful. Gentle walk. Interesting little pieces of information dotted around on the wildlife and flowers etc. Such as beware of rattlesnakes! Great way to spend an hour or two. Be sure to take water and a hat!
This was an amazing place! You notice the temp drop very fast. Then the palms give off a golden glow in certain light. This is a place worth going to.
I've never been to an oasis in the desert. It's definitely straight out of the movies! What a cool place.
Interesting place for a short visit. It was unexpectedly beautiful and cool in the middle of desert.

name: Hi-View Trailhead
formatted_address: Yucca Valley, CA 92284, USA

google_reviews.text: Cute little trail we brought the dogs. When you first park if you go to the one side dogs are not allowed on that trail. Keep walking down a totally separate path opposite from that and you’ll find an unmarked trail that allows dogs. We went right at the fork and scaled a mountain. There was some seating and there was a book that you can sign at the top of the mountain. It was fun to read through that

An easy, short hike with a moderate incline. It’s full sun without any shade, so best to hike in the morning or late afternoon like most hikes in the area. It’s very quiet - I didn’t see another person on the trail when I visited. The trailhead parking is at the end of a short bumpy dirt road, but it was nothing my Corolla couldn’t handle.
Chose those trail for my first sunrise hike and it was great! There are stairs which can be challenging for some. If you do go for sunrise/sunset the view will be totally worth it!!🌅🫶😊

What an incredible experience! This is an easy to moderate trail with a decent grade. It is a short hike at only 1.3 miles. Make sure that you wear shoes with decent grip as the sand can get slippery. The views from the top are astounding!

This trail actually goes in to Joshua Tree National Park, as evidenced by the small National Parks Service sign along the trail. Payment is not required to use this trail, so you can see some Joshua Trees and save some money. The trail is great, with beautiful panoramic views of the mountains and lots of nice trees and scenery. There is a podium at the top of a peak that has a sort of guestbook you can sign, but unfortunately people think it's funny to draw inappropriate things like in elementary school. Otherwise though it's an awesome trail and well worth it.

Reception and GPS directions work fine, it will take up you towards a campground. Don't be deterred by the signs to register for camping, that isn't necessary if you are just hiking. Just follow the road the GPS tells you to the trail.

name: Santa Rosa and San Jacinto Mountains National Monument
formatted_address: CA-74, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful viewpoint and an absolute blast of a road on the way up to it. I’ve made the trip both during the day and night to see both spectacular views and they don’t disappoint.

Beautiful! Great trail system around the visitor centre, but the trail up top is closed. For more of a challenge, cross the road, and go up the Art Smith trail. Scenic, the first parts look out over Big Horn, and then go further up into the mountains. Very challenging, but no water stops so bring lots!
I highly recommend everything about this mountain: tram, tracking, cafe, mountain air, animals, etc.

Where do I begin?!!! This experience was stellar, amazing, breathtaking just to say in a few words. Some friends and I went on the tram on our way up and the views were captivating. The air is crisp and clean. You can't beat the scenic view up there. It was simply marvelous. The pictures I took didn't even do it justice. To see it with your eyes it is truly unbelievable!!! Definitely a place to come back too, the staff was out of this world, friendly, funny and made us feel at ease.

This is a must see and do if you are visiting the Palm Springs area. You start by driving up the mountain road to the entrance to the park which is located close to the downtown area. Then catch a ride up the tram to the top of the mountain and catch the beautiful views while you are riding up the mountain. By the way the tram floor rotates in a circle around inside so everyone gets to see the beauty. Be prepared for the odd feeling when the floor starts to move. It is very slow but does take some getting used to. There are many activities to do once you are on the top of the mountain. Take advantage of all the hiking trails coursing through the park. There are also shops and restaurants at the top that are worth looking into. We spent a good half day there and still could go back to see more. Definitely a Must-See for any family.

name: Oswit Canyon
formatted_address: QFH3+9J5, Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text:
The kind of hiking trail with quick roadside parking and access, easy loop, or connect to long range trails, and great desert flora and geology.  Fun place to enjoy for a walk.   Great place to keep natural and free of human debris!
Observed hummingbirds, raccoon scat, and excellent brittle bush all over, and many other interesting plant species.  Check it out!

Great hike up Oswit Canyon to the waterfall with the Great Outdoors Palm Springs group. A must in the area. Great views, varied trail terrain, the scrambling was fun. Very little waterfall this time of year but a cool place to rest before turning around. While trails are marked, easy to get off track. Easy street parking.
Moderate loop hike with great canyon and mountain views 🏜️ 🌵 ⛰️

Oswit Canyon is a fantastic short hike for the novice hiker. There is a bit of elevation gain, but the one mile loop makes it completely do-able. In spring, continue on to the waterfall. I, personally, haven’t gone that far, but about 20 minutes past the turnaround you can get to flowing water during the right time of year. Keep going past that for the scramble to the waterfall. Don’t forget your sunscreen or water. It’s exposed out there!

Excellent morning trip for someone visiting PS looking for some nature without too many people around. Gorgeous trail with lots to see.  Loved it!  The trail is not marked well at the South Lykken trail head entry - you have to search off to the south and in about 50 yards to see the first marker for the Oswit Trail.

name: North Lykken Trailhead
formatted_address: 799 W Ramon Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text: One of the most beautiful hikes in Palm Springs. Feels like ur on another planet. Steep in certain areas and easy in others. Best for those looking for moderate hikes. Not pets, parking is limited. Bring water.

This trail has beautiful vistas of Palm Springs at every turn! Bring plenty of water and a hiking stick because it is a moderate to strenuous, desert hike.
We saw birds, small lizards, and dragon flies.

Peaceful hike with beautiful city views
We got the North Lykken Trail recommendation from the Palm Springs Visitor Center as we were looking for a moderate hike with good views, and it definitely delivered. The trail was peaceful and quiet—we were alone the whole time, which made the experience even more special.

The scenery is beautiful, with a stunning view over Palm Springs once you get a bit higher. We didn’t complete the full trail but hiked for about an hour to a high point and back, which was perfect. A great option if you’re looking for a scenic and not-too-crowded hike near the city.
Great trail with easy pathway to find and not too much of an incline. Perfect for an easy / medium hike with great sunset views!!
So much fun and a good hike!  Follow the trail signs and you’ll be good to go!

name: Mount San Jacinto State Park
formatted_address: 25905 CA-243, Idyllwild-Pine Cove, CA 92549, USA

google_reviews.text: Wonderful state park known for it's large number of Joshua trees and rugged,massive rock climbing areas. Easy walking areas with amazing desert scenery. If you go on a weekend,  get there early, if not be prepared for a long line at the entry.

I loved the atmosphere of this campground with its incredibly tall trees.  I only gave it a 4 star because many of the tent sites were badly eroded as well as the parking places for them.
Electric sites are available however it appeared that the only site w/ water hook up was the handicap site next to the rest room.

Lots of great trails here. Make sure to pick up a free permit at the ranger station in town that covers the trail you're planning to hike. Mt. San Jacinto permits are picked up at the Ranger station and permits for other hikes can be picked up/self-issued at the State Park office. There will be a ranger checking and you will get turned away if you don't have the appropriate permit.
Overnight/Wilderness permits can be submitted up to 8 weeks in advance and cost $5/person, with groups up to 15 people.
You will also need a parking pass (America the Beautiful, Adventure Pass, etc.) for any vehicles parked at the trailhead.

The most convenient way to get to the park is via the Palm Springs Aerial Tramway. It will elevate you to 8,516 feet (about 2,6 km.)
$15 parking + $33 cable car will get you Incredible photo opportunities (plus a couple of hiking trails.)

This place is incredible .. we visited it in the month of November, we attended due to the snowfall, the place with this weather is amazing, everything surrounded by snow is very beautiful. Access starts with a cost of $ 8 dollars for parking, within the costs are 27 dollars for adults and 16 for children, they have a choice of 36 dollars and your walk includes dinner in their restaurant which has an incredible park view , although you can buy it only after 4:30 pm. You can bring your own food and eat on site, they have 2 restaurants and fast food with regular prices. It is very worthwhile, the best I think is to visit it early to reach the greatest possible daylight and thus appreciate the whole journey. It is an appropriate place for the whole family and a tip is to wear appropriate clothing, at least in winter to warm up well and wear appropriate shoes and water. I highly recommend it, I will definitely return.

name: Black Rock Canyon Trailhead
formatted_address: Yucca Valley, CA 92284, USA

google_reviews.text: Visited in mid-January, took Black Rock Canyon trailhead up to Panorama Loop and Mt. Warren Peak. Warm and sunny at the base of the trail, but definitely gets breezy and cool at altitude and along the crests, so strongly recommend not only sunscreen and plenty of water, but also a jacket or sweater if you plan on hiking up to the summits.
Well maintained trail. Volunteer rangers very nice. Trail is pretty easy, well demarked, and Mountian Bike friendly (after a dozen or so steps at the trail head)

Reached the trailhead at 3:45pm on a Sunday in February. Easy to find parking. Did the "Warren Peak via Black Rock Trail and Panorama Loop" hike from allTrails. It came to 8 miles total with 1800 feet elevation gain and took me around 2h40m. I did the Panorama Loop counter clockwise and got to Warren Peak just 5 minutes before sunset. Definitely recommend it as a good sunset spot and also that you should carry a headlamp for getting back to the car from there in the dark. Trail is easy to follow. For Warren Peak, you need to climb 500 feet straight up in 0.7 miles, and even the high point of Panorama Loop is a decent climb to get to, so please carry plenty of water if attempting this in hot weather. Great views from both summits.

One of our favorite trails at the park especially as “green” as it got after this years weather. This hike is recommended to add panorama loop for sure and Wareen peak as an option. Stunning views. Lots of elevation gain we marked 1200 ft but most of it was gradual until the peak on panorama. We did clockwise and really enjoyed it better this way. Fun hike. Highly recommend.

Second time in the area. Just beautiful. Many trail options from the campground or the parking area. Plenty of maps posted. It's free as well if you are on a budget and still want the Joshua tree hiking experience. Google maps works while you are on the trail too.

name: Araby Trail
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text:
Some of the reviews call this an easy hike which is so misleading. My Apple Watch clocked my heart rate at 140+ often. It’s about Lots of switchbacks with great views to catch your breath. Bring water. The only reason it got a 4 star was minimal parking and shade . Great views of the valley and rich people’s houses. Cute house at top for photos
I was really surprised how many people were out hiking with no water or hats. Even in milder temps we’re still in a desert.

Beautiful 6K out and back with small loop at the top. About an hour up, rolling terrain, not too steep, well worn easy to follow trail. About 2 hrs altogether and we stopped for short breaks to rest and refuel.

Not too crowded when we walked it from noon to 2pm.

Scenic views into the hills and the famous Bob Hope house, amongst other elegant mid-century residences. Down the same path back to the small free parking lot.

Not a highly technical trail but a little endurance helps for the first hour, tho by no means difficult or excessive. Recommend!

Great hike! About 7500 steps from trail head, to flat area above Hope house, and back to trail head. Roughly 3.69 miles give or take. I hike a lot so would say this is easy but my wife doesn’t and felt it was mildly challenging. I saw a wide variety of ability levels so definitely a hike for everyone. It is rocky and very little shade so want to be careful. Bring extra water!
The view the entire hike is amazing and Hope house is very cool. We ate lunch up there before hiking back down. Parking is limited depending on when you go. I got lucky that someone was pulling out as I pulled in.

Great first day hike! Loved passing by amazing architecture in the hills and hiking on up to 360° views. The peaks in the snow are stunning to see with sun shining on them. Will likely pass other hikers. Everyone is friendly and shared the paths well.

Nice, wide trail with some fairly steep inclines to get the heart pumping! There are lots of lookouts to get views of Coachella Valley on one side and views of Mt San Jacinto, and sister peaks, on the other side. The trail is completely devoid of shade, so hiking it in the middle of the day could be quite hot. There’s ample parking at the trail head off Palm Hills Drive.

name: Pushawalla Palms Trailhead
formatted_address: Thousand Palms Canyon Rd, Desert Hot Springs, CA 92241, USA

google_reviews.text: Interesting place. The trail starts with climbing to the top of a ridge with a great view of the Coachella Valley. As you leave the ridge, the trail drops down to a beautiful palm tree oasis, then a steep rocky climb (with a rusted-out truck shell along the way!) back to the foot of the hill whose ridge you just traversed. You can either walk the ridge back, or stay on low ground following a wash back to the parking lot. About 5 miles. Took us 2 1/2 hrs with stops to enjoy views and oasis.
Such a great, free hike! Park at the trailhead and download all trails to stay on path. 4.5mi completed in less than 2 hours. Would bring hiking sticks next time.

All Trails got this one wrong. We took the ridge path alllll the way to the end then dropped down into the Pushwalla river bed. You can see the palms against the canyon wall as your descending the hill on a very poorly tracked path, but you can see it if you’re looking for it. After walking around the river bed we came back up to flat ground and the came back on the Horseshoe trail side. We ended up hiking back up to the ridge after the last palm grouping but you can also walk around the ridges if you want an easier route back. The palms are amazing and worth the effort.

This 4-5 mile hike is one of the better hiking trails around Palm Springs. It has moderate uphills and super cool palm trees and trickling stream.
I recommend using the All Trails or Hiking Guy websites to research the trail. It’ll help make sure you take the best route.
Some fairly steep climbs. Some very rocky parts, but not too much. I fell going down because of loose sand. But the hike was otherwise very enjoyable. Some great scenery!

name: South Lykken Trail
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text:
This is 8.9 miles hike with elevation gain 2208. There’s practically no shade so in the summer do this hike at 5 or 6am. In the winter I do it around 10am, really always depends on a weather.
I never do the whole hike. I turn around at Josie Johnson Vista Park, where you have tables and can have a picnic. To this point and back to car is 5.9 miles with elevation gain 1200. It usually takes me 2:20 hours to complete it. First mile is uphill on the rocks. It’s easier to walk up than down. This part is hard on my hypermobile ankle joints. But I love this hike so much! Beautiful views and pleasant walk on narrow path. You can see the mountains on the other side, and Palm Springs. If you want to do the whole hike, you can walk back to the car 2 miles on the street. I’ve seen bighorn sheep’s, rattlesnakes, rabbits, and even fox. There’s some magic in these mountains.

Started at the south end. (Parked on road) Ended up walking all the way over to the North end by Mesquite Rd. Total walking time   2:45hrs. Total time including stops at 2 picnic areas and pic taking 4hrs. Saw bighorn sheep up close. The Mesquite end of trail is a bit more challenging. (Grabbed an Uber back to car $12)

This is a great hike. Some moderately strenuous sections, but totally worth it. I've only hiked it in the spring (several times) and the blooming cacti, bushes, trees, etc. are incredible. Incredible views along the entire trail. On my last two times going up I ran into a group of bighorn sheep at the top (near the picnic tables) and then another group on the way back down--even closer. It's such a cool hike--I don't know why it's not more popular. No parking at the trail head, but you can park on the street a block east of the trail head.
The trail was looking good.  About 20 volunteers working on the trail when we arrived.  Saw my first cactus bloom - beautiful.  Also saw eight long horned sheep!
Amazing sunrise. You need to start dark, but it Will pay off

name: Sawmill Trail 5E02
formatted_address: Pidgeon Springs Rd, Mountain Center, CA 92561, USA

google_reviews.text: Technically I was not on what's marked as the Sawmill Trail but was about a mile east, because my printout map of the Santa Rosa Mtns Natl Monument had me confused. There's an old telephone company service road that goes up into the mountains where Springcrest Drive ends. I hiked that and had a wonderful, and quiet, time. I am not giving 5 stars, though, because of the relative paucity of info on the NM's website map.

Really pretty, well-maintained trail with lots of desert plants, some mountain views and dry Creek bed crossings and a nice parking lot with plenty of parking. I had it pretty much at all to myself when I went and took the Cactus Spring trail to Horsethief Creek crossing, and would definitely go back to check out the other trail.

Starting at an elevation of 4,050 ft., the Sawmill Trail is located in a truly unique area in Southern California. Considered to be a sub-region of the Sonoran desert called 'the upper Colorado desert' - you will encounter lush desert plants like yuccas and prickly pair and wildlife like jackrabbits, mule dear, and bobcats as you meander up the grand switchbacks of the Sawmill Trail into the alpine forests of Toro and Santa Rosa Peaks. As you make your way up the trail to a distinct land mark called  'the incinerator' at an elevation of 6,261 ft., you will find unparalleled views of the desert, looking south to the Salton Sea, and northwest to San Jacinto and San Gorgonio peaks. From here, you can challenge yourself to keep climbing up to the peaks or call it a day and head back down.
Surprisingly beautiful hike. I suspect our elongated spring has helped, but we saw waterfalls, blooming cactus, and excellent vistas. Steady but very manageable grade
Fun hike, you walk past some old mining areas and a majority of the year the landscape is green and beautiful.

name: Earl Henderson Trail
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text:
My friends and I love this trail. During summer, we’ve been catching some of the most beautiful sunrises. It’s not too hot and by no means cold at all. We hike up and loop down to the wash where we finish off our hike with a nice run to our cars. Total time there and back is about 2 hours.. that includes us hanging out up top to enjoy the moment.

Highly recommend.

Henderson Trail is a great morning hike if you're looking for something reasonably quick, not too difficult, and with pretty desert views. We saw a few people out (one trail runner, one single senior with hiking poles, and a group of 5 in the distance) on a January Wednesday morning between 8-9:30 am. No dogs are allowed on this trail.

This trail is open for horseback riders and there are road apples along the way that show it's well used. Pay attention to where you step, but you'll need to do that anyway because the trail is sandy and rocky. I suggest to wear hiking shoes or something with lugged soles, or bring poles, if you're worried about stability.

Many reviewers said that Google sent them to a locked gate at the entrance to a private property and it did for us too. (This seemed to be because the trail goes past that property, which provides the only road in.) We looked up the hike on our All Trails app and discovered that there's a parking lot on South Araby Road across from the trailhead. (We confirmed that's the place to park with a local who was out walking his dog.) The parking lot is small and unpaved, so watch for the sign that says when not to park there (10 pm - 5 am). It's a bit counterintuitive.
I'm a hiking tour guide in the Palm Springs area. This is one of the trails I take people on as part of the Garstin Loop hike. You can find me by searching for, trail ahead hiking.
Hiked up Earl Henderson to Shannon to Berns and came back on the Gerstin trail, 3.6 miles with a 1000 ft gain, great hike!
Relatively easy hike near downtown Palm Springs with a great view of the mountains and Palm Springs.

name: Homestead Trailhead
formatted_address: Thrush Rd, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: Spent the late Sat. afternoon hiking during #CoronaQuarantine. So many people doing the same thing! Simple trail and lots of serious wash outs along the path. If you’re a bit less slight of foot you may not like the deep gouges you must hop over. But the scenery was wonderful.  There’s a great view of Porcupine Creek Golf Club about halfway up. AMAZING looking course in the spring at sunset. Worth the trip for sure.

It’s a good trail however there kinda thin, the trail to the left when the main trail splits is kinda short but the trail on the right goes for a while and meets up to a section with a huge! Incline to the top of a mountain

DONT TAKE THE LEFT TRAIL! We took the wrong way going up lol. It was uphill of the way! I advise you to take the trail to the right from the parking lot. Will take you about 1hr or less depends on your hiking speed. A good hike for the family.

A nice trail which climbs up the ridgeline of the hill. The trail is steep and is definitely a work out. There is a cross at the top which lights up at night. A decent trail but not the best or offers the best views.
Really lovely morning hike. Plenty of people around so I feel ok hiking alone. Tends to get busy mid morning on the weekends. No shade, sunscreen and hat recommended.

name: Murray Hill
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a GREAT hike. Moderate to difficult. 2400 foot elevation gain and a big chunk of that is the zigzag up the peak. The views along the way and at the top are amazing. Park at the east end of the Vons shopping center. Use an app to get to the Clara Burgess trailhead and it's clear from there.

About 10 km hike round trip to a high point overlooking the Palm Springs area.  Quite a bit of climbing…670 m of it.  The view at the end is worth it.  Make sure you pack a lunch or snacks to enjoy at one of the picnic tables on top.

Best view.... if your looking for more of a natural trail this is it... start at the base by vons... starts as a road but then... you'll love the hike and worth it... dont give up pace yourself... breath and enjoy not for beginners.
If you are up to it, take this hike from the trailhead in the Andreas Hills side! Beautiful views all around! There is a small strenuous part but once you get past it, it is worth it!
Murray Peak is a great workout, and it really gets your heart pumping.

name: Palm Springs Aerial Tramway
formatted_address: 1 Tramway Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text: Fabulous trip from 98 degrees to 66 degrees in just ten minutes. Short drive from Palm Springs to enjoy the cooler temperatures and stunning views. We purchased our tickets ahead of time online, but I don’t believe that is necessary. When you arrive, they will tell you how long the wait is to get on a tram. Having purchased tickets ahead of time did not give us any advantage.  Plenty of space inside to white. The actual trim trip takes only 10 minutes with wonderful views throughout. Several food options at various price points on the top of the mountain, which we enjoyed very much.  We’ll make this visit again.

This really is an awesome and beautiful place to visit. One gets to see so many things at such a great price.
The ride up is wonderful, the top view is just breathtaking then you can hike the parts of the mountain and forest that is accessible. And there’s no time limit either.
You can have all three meals here and at affordable prices.
Then there’s the museum and souvenirs shop (they had 🦂🍭!😄)
I had lovely time.
Just remember to bring some layers cos the temp changes suddenly up there. Also, it won’t hurt to wear comfy shoes you can hike/walk in cos you just be tempted to walk the trails between the wonderful trees and rocks that are there and see some wildlife.

A fun experience but the winter conditions made it too cold to go out once at the top   dress for the weather and check the palm springs tram app for top side conditions.  The museum and videos are well worth watching.  Also don't miss the counter weight room at the top right before you board to go back down.  Be aware that parking is $15 per car.  If you're going early buy tickets online to avoid the lines.

A great little gem in the desert.  Purchase tickets in advance with a ticketed time otherwise you can wait for upto 2.5 hrs.  Thankfully we had dinner reservations at peaks, and they were able to squeeze us on the next cable car up the mountain to make our reservation time.  Really cool viewing experiences, the bar topside had great festive drinks as well. Great couples date night idea , head up  around sunset and come down in the dark. Really cool.

This is a MUST do when you visit the Palm Springs area.  Escape the heat of the desert floor.  Walk amongst beautiful huge Jeffrey pines and other massive rock outcroppings. If you're not very mobile?  No problem.  Lot's to see & do from both the valley and mountain platforms. Very accessible for nearly all.  This was my 2nd visit in the past 5 or 6 years; and, I'll do it again, someday. 😃

name: Indian Vista Scenic Overlook
formatted_address: Banning-Idyllwild Panoramic Hwy, Banning, CA 92220, USA

google_reviews.text:
We ended our date night here and it was beautiful. It was a bit scary at night time as it is in the middle of nowhere and was pitch black.

Very short walk just a few steps and was all paved. It was well worth it just to see the city lights but if your thinking about going I would recommend maybe trying to go around sunset time.

A beautiful quiet spot to watch the sunset in the mountains!
I feel like this is an “overlooked” spot on the drive up or down from Idyllwild!
Such a beautiful way to break up a long journey!

It's an absolutely stunning destination with picturesque trails that wind through breathtaking landscapes. Whether you're an avid hiker or simply looking to soak in the panoramic views, this spot offers something for everyone. I highly recommend visiting San Jacinto Mountain if you find yourself in the area—it’s a place where nature truly shines.
Awesome place close by to meditate & explore.

name: Clara Burgess Trail
formatted_address: Goat Trails, Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a lovely hike to take when you have time to meander through the foothills.   I didn't plan very well and ended up hiking for three hours with no water or food!  Do not make that same mistake!  At times the trail is hard to follow because there are several paths to choose from.  I eventually made it back and I can't wait to go again!
Great hike
great place to go hiking

name: Warren Peak Trail
formatted_address: Yucca Valley, CA 92284, USA

google_reviews.text:
Found an ammo can with a visitor log at the summit - make sure you look for it and sign in!
Great add-on if you're doing the panorama loop trail. Came in January and it gets a bit breezy and cool along the ridge line, definitely not a bad idea to bring a jacket or sweatshirt, even if it's 60s/70s and sunny at the parking area.

In spite of the fact that this is also a six-mile, 1,100-foot elevation gain trail just like the Hollywood Sign trail, Warren Peak did not have the elevation gain gradually. The gain occurred all at once, toward the end of the hike. Much of the hike was perfectly flat, and it wasn’t until the last leg of the hike that we started climbing the mountain. This was made especially challenging because I had been in the ocean for eight straight hours the day before, not realizing I was getting a full-body workout the entire time. My legs were screaming all the way up that mountain, and as soon as I sat down at the peak I was afraid I wouldn’t be able to get up, again. I was going to die up there.

But, it was a beautiful place to die.

The views from Warren Peak were absolutely wonderful. Because the landscape around it was so low, you could see for miles and miles and miles. The lack of clouds certainly helped with this, too. I’m not even sure how far off I could see, but I saw peaks that were impossibly distant. In addition to amazing views (and company!) on the peak, there was also a little box with a notebook and photographs, filled with mementos from other people who peaked the mountain. My friends and I made sure to leave our mark, too!

This was a beautiful hike. I would definitely recommend it to anyone visiting our living in the area. It's 5k feet high,  which as you can imagine, is a 'leg day' challenge on the way up and a fun, skipping, faster downhill walk down (after the more cautious 'peak' is over).
Once you brave the top part, you'll have 360° views of the most beautiful mountains.
Must try! It's worth it.
Great hike with sweeping views of the park. We did the panorama loop in addition and enjoyed that portion as well. It was VERY WINDY at the top, but that did not take away from its beauty.
Mostly flat and sandy trail but it gets steeper and more of a rock scramble at the last stretch. Have shoes that have good grip but you will likely get sand in them.

name: Lake Cahuilla Veterans Regional Park
formatted_address: 58075 Jefferson St, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text:
You can camp here with hookups or just primitive tent camping.
The daily setting can be bustling; we were lucky to camp during the week.
It's just 16 minutes from La Quinta 'downtown', nice new development in Mexican style, well done with great coffee shops and restaurants

A magical campground on the lake where you can stay for night o few days and if you like to fish that is the place , near Palm Springs and all the Couchella valley.
Make sure to call them in advance to make your reservation and get your spot.
A certain time the gate is close and not walk in.
A quiet and beautiful place to watch the sun rise. Although its under construction its stil a great place. I think all the construction they are doing will only enhance this great this place.

Beautiful park and lake nestled by the surrounding rugged mountains with an unfortunate caveat…a police handgun range immediately adjacent to the RV sites on the northern most point of the lake that destroyed our Friday afternoon enjoyment and peace.

The staff, the sweet lady at the front office who checked us in can’t be complimented enough. Our view of the mountains and lake from RV site #1. (Equestrian & Group Campsites) is exquisite.

My only complaint is the discharging of firearms by the Riverside County Sheriff’s Department which sounded like a military operation in the canyons much of the afternoon on Friday, 3/6/26.

If you, your family, your pets, are not disturbed by the noise of discharging firearms, you will enjoy your stay here. FYI, law enforcement did complete their live fire exercises around 4:30 PM. If there are night fire exercises, we’ll leave.

Nice 2 Week stay with water and electric hookups. Black and Grey tanks are emptied by dump station. So using showers and bathrooms on site that are clean, allowing from not having to go dump every other day. The spaces are nice sized for great family camping gatherings and celebrations. Trails with  mountain sheep was a plus.

name: Panorama Loop Trail
formatted_address: Yucca Valley, CA 92284, USA

google_reviews.text: Challenging at times, but well worth it with some amazing views. I visited in January, and it can get quite breezy and chilly along the crest and backside of the peaks, so strongly recommend bringing a jacket or sweater, along with plenty of water, of course!

Incredible hike! My first experience at Joshua Tree and only time for one trail, so I’m glad I chose this one. My partner and I went at sunrise last Saturday and had the entire trail to ourselves. I couldn’t believe how quiet it was. The views were unlike anything I’ve ever experienced, the pictures could never do it justice. Just pure California magic.

We aren’t in the worst shape ever but I wouldn’t call us the most experienced hikers either, it took us about two and a half hours to hike the entire 6+ mile trail and that includes breaks along the way. We took the advice we saw from other Google/all trails reviews and went left once we reached the panorama loop/Warren peak fork. We brought several layers expecting lots of wind as we reached the vista, but we lucked out with great weather. Still didn’t regret having those layers however, especially at the beginning of the hike before the sun came up. It was quite cold! Packed some cliff bars, drinkable yogurts, plenty of water and Gatorade to replenish electrolytes and felt ready to take on whatever Joshua Tree wanted to throw at us! Can’t wait to go back and explore more!

We parked at the black rock camp ground and started the hike from there. We got tickets (30 USD per vehicle) as we went to Ryan mountain the previous day, but no one was there to check our tickets when we arrived at black rock camp ground so maybe you can do it for free? Not sure, but I felt it was worth it to pay because it allows them to maintain the place.

We did the hike in march and the weather / temperature was perfect! The initial part of the hike was a bit warmer, it gets a bit windy at the top so would suggest to bring a windbreaker, it really helps. We start off with sandy terrains, and there are more greenery as you start to ascend (we did it anti clockwise). I think both ways are fine

You may feel like there isn’t much to see during the initial parts of the hike, but the view at the top is worth it, trust me! Overall the hike wasn’t too difficult even though it is rated as “challenging” in their official website. You could do without any equipments but we are in relatively good shape. Many shades along the way to take a break

On human traffic: We started at around 845am and there wasn’t a lot of people. For the first hour I think we were the only ones, and I preferred if there were more people but the crowds starting to come in around 10+am, so perhaps we started too early. Met around 20-30 people along the whole 3.5 hour journey. It was a privilege to be able to experience this :) enjoy!

One of my favorite hikes. Trail has amazing views in all directions once you get to the loop summit. A couple of steep inclines. We went clockwise and enjoyed this direction better. With recent rains n snow some areas so green it looked like Africa with the contrast of Joshua trees and small grasslands it was not a usual desert feel. A must do loop to add on to black rock canyon.

I did this trail May 2oTh 2024, From Palm Springs with the aerial tram, then anti clock wise, along the creek. Some snow patches higher up. One of my best trails so far. crisp air, no people, creekside, some hard parts but mostly moderate. Get the free permit right at the start of the trail at the ranger station. Follow Alltrails directions. After the hike go for the steak at the top restaurant.

name: Herb Jeffries Trailhead
formatted_address: Edgehill Dr, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: This is an excellent hike. It's pretty much a 1.77 mile loop with a mild incline. There are a few merging trails to explore as well. You can start at the bottom of the bump n grind or on the opposite end near the Baptist church (closer to the cross).
Easy to kinda easy trails...good morning walk with wifey!
Was a fairly decent incline. Definately got the heart pumping. Nice view at the top.
Nice trail, connects to others to give you many options to enjoy the time with nature.
Wonderful hike, about 1.5 miles, lots of up and down hill. Bring a hiking stick.

name: Sunrise Park
formatted_address: 480 S Sunrise Way, Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text: Pretty nice park close to downtown palm springs. Nice updated playground for the kids. Restaurants across the street.  Pretty quiet at 9am

This was very close to downtown Palm Springs. My kids under 5 loved it. Quiet, shaded, and very well kept. The bathrooms were nearby and the parking lot was located near the library. Picnic tables were available. Would recommend to anyone with small kids.

Wonderful park in quaint neighborhood. Breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains. A big play area for the kids with lots of slides and swing unfortunately it was under some kind of renovation when we were there. Beautiful place to get some steps in, let the kids burn some energy and take in views.

Beautiful park. Having the community rec center there and outdoor basketball courts is great. The playground is large, there are two structures. There are also different options swings.
Very peaceful, lots trees and plenty of parking. Good spot to have a picnic as well.
Lovely and clean park with good equipment for kids under 12.

name: Miles Avenue Park
formatted_address: 82540 Miles Ave, Indio, CA 92201, USA

google_reviews.text: We love a playground with soft ground covering instead of the wood chips. Two different play structures (big kid, little kid) across from large dog park and next to ymca building. There was homelessness around but they keep mostly to themselves. Don’t recommend using the bathrooms. And I hear they do loads of activities here.

There's now a park "sheriff" at every park in indio to ward off homeless people! These parks just got 100% better with that said, keep an eye open on the city recreation website for free events like the videos I posted with this review!

Possibly the best Park in the desert! The open feeling, running on  the cement path is refreshing, with green grass for soccer, the sound of kids laughing, picnic areas with grills for barbecuing. The well established trees provide much needed shade. Restrooms work and are open during the day.  There is a 10 o'clock curfew as well. Safe place for kids!
Very nice park, the downside was bathroom was out of toilet paper and hardly any trash cans available
Nice park with a lot of trees, picnic tables, basketball courts, tennis courts and a playground!

name: Palm Desert Civic Center Park
formatted_address: 43900 San Pablo Ave, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: It's a beautiful park with lots of things to do. For us, it's our pickleball court of choice in Palm Springs and area. It can get really busy here especially on weekends. Good players from beginners to advanced. Very welcoming.

Beautiful park, plenty of free parking, playground for kids with spongy flooring so children do not fall and scratch end up with little band-aids, bump their little heads and injure themselves. Full size Basketball court, lovely little trails around the park for walking, jogging or running and picnic benches along with BBQ grills for a great family and friends events or gatherings. Huge park, beautiful grass to lay down on a blanket and read a book and just relax. I'm pretty sure this is a park that anybody and everybody can and will enjoy just as much as I have.

Beautiful park! Love the fountain with ducks, herons, turtles, and pidgins. Plenty of baseball and other sport fields. Lots of shaded benches. Perfect for hosting an event or having a picnic. Nice playgrounds for children. Plenty of well kept restrooms. Lots of parking. Honestly one of the best parks I’ve been to. Definitely recommend!

My wife and I enjoy the Civic part for all the things that it offers. Even watching a concert at night.  You can walk around the park as you’re going to play a game of Pickleball or tennis.  Palm Desert has done a fantastic job with our park. The crown Jewel, the Civic Park.
