Fantastic park. Huge! Filled with amenities, awesome pool, and very well cared for. Tons of FREE parking too! Love taking the family here. Well done Palm Desert!

name: Ruth Hardy Park
formatted_address: 700 Tamarisk Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text: Very recently remodeled with lots of fun playground structures. There's also a full basketball court, picnic tables with benches, a well-maintained restroom, lots of grass and sidewalks.

I have been enjoying new things about Ruth Hardy Park since my dog passed away a few months back. I have enjoyed going and walking through the park and just enjoying the beauty of the trees in the grass and laying down on the grass in looking into the skies and looking up toward the mountains 10,000 ft mountains just enjoying everything about what's around me and that's something that I find very exciting and very uplifting on a specially days when you need that and the park really helps provide that wonderful reality. The only thing I would suggest is add another restroom for men and women because there's only one for men and it's never clean or ready and women I probably don't have much more but nothing's been done to improve that

We love our parks 💙 however I wish we had more enforcement on people walking their dogs. Several people walk their dogs without leashes around here its a park for children not a dog park! They let them pee everywhere and poop.

The new remodeling looks great!

Beautiful large park with lots of grass & a nice play ground for kids. They have pretty clean restrooms & basketball/tennis courts. So much to do!!! Lots of things to see. There was an aerobics class with upbeat music going on today!! Super fun, made me join in!!! Love taking my fur baby Harlow here!!! He loves meeting new friends!!

Love this park! Used to come after school when I was in middle school. Glad that a lot of the original stuff like the Lost Mine is still here. Miss the turtles that shot water though haha
Brings back great memories of playing football here.

name: Rancho Mirage Community Park
formatted_address: 71560 San Jacinto Dr, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270, USA

google_reviews.text: Fantastic playground for kids of all ages. Here on a Friday and found the farmers market for lunch as well. Highly recommended.
Well manicured park in a quiet area, with play equipment, exercise equipment, lots of tennis/pickleball courts, a walking trail, restrooms and nice plantings!

Such a great park! Especially if you have children and a dog or dogs. There’s a place for the children to play- and grass area right by it for you to be with your pup. Please do everyone a favor And bring puppy poop bags just in case. There are also benches and picnic tables around for you to eat and or sit and watch.

Kids loved this place. Very nice and clean community park, with various playground equipments and very friendly and sociable people. The playground has a metal fire truck for kids to pretend play with.
The park has port amenities available too for tennis, basketball and other team sports.
Definitely recommend this park if you are visiting Palm Desert or around.

Incredibly clean! This is another favorite spot to take children.  Landscapers were trimming and cleaning up while we were there. A police SUV was parked for a while which made me feel safe. The restrooms are clean, but there were cars parked in from with individuals sitting alone. Otherwise, this is a nice place for families.

name: Century Park
formatted_address: 69900 Century Park Dr, Cathedral City, CA 92234, USA

google_reviews.text: Very pleasant park with wide lawn for kids to run. Small but good playground, a few covered picnic areas, a restroom, and an actual parking lot in case you want to unload the kids' tricycles or scooters or a big picnic spread. The view of palms and mountains is quite stunning. A very nice pocket park.
Love bringing the kids there for fun seems like everything is always fun and clean at the park
Hilarious!!! In one day rain in the desert we have a bloody lake!!! Awesome!! Real men took advantage of this sweet opportunity and road their canoes in the lake! Now it's historic.
Great place to chill and play piano at 3:00 in the morning while on rescue duty with my overnight delivery company. Just beware of the sprinklers If it's windy.
No dogs are allowed in this park except for seeing eye dogs. Otherwise it's small but nice with playground equipment some covered picnic tables with BBQs, a baseball diamond and a tennis court.

name: La Quinta Park
formatted_address: Corner of Blackhawk Way &, Adams St, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text: I took my daughter there for the first annual fall festival. City staff and volunteers were a real treat. So nice and helpful. We've also celebrated a number of her May birthdays there. She loves the splash pad!

La Quinta Park offers is a great escape into nature, with recreational amenities like park gym for both kids and adults. Nice views of the Santa Rosa Mountains.
Families appreciate the playgrounds and spacious picnic areas, ideal for gatherings and relaxation.  Has 2 softball fields. Enough room for soccer games and a paved track around the park.  You will also find a little skate/bike park near the basketball courts. There are restrooms and playgrounds too.
Check out my rental cabin near Big Bear through my profile. ;)

The splash pad here at the park is really such fun. A little area for smaller kids and the classic dumping bucket on the other side. This part of the park is really important as this desert heat is setting. We will definitely be back here many times.

We didn't use the playground this time but it also has a lot of neat equipment which also looks like it is accessible friendly. It also has lots of shade over it.

What a neat park now for the entire family! Not as super-sized as the Palm Desert city park but still fun. Recent landscaping improvements have increased the trees with new plantings and a much needed retaining wall along the paved sidewalk. The coolest part is the kids fountains area with waterworks that ca. be activated anytime!! Plenty of parking and beautiful grass areas.

Kids had a blast at the water fountain ⛲ area for the kiddos.
**Update** They have updated the water fountain area for the kids, all new area for the kids to play. I came here about 7 am to walk my dog, and it was a pleasant morning for it. It was, however, busy with LQ high school students being dropped off in the morning.

name: Ocotillo Park
formatted_address: 33300 Moreno Rd, Cathedral City, CA 92234, USA

google_reviews.text: My old stomping grounds that empty desert now has a  park that looks amazing with locking restroom doors  completely safe and clean. I was really impressed with the upkeep of the park.

Its a really nice park. The restrooms are clean, decent size grass field, baseball field, soccer goals, theres a few tables and benches, and the playground is a decent size. I like the fact that the park isn't on any main roads. Today my son & I visited it because of the fun-o-rama event to celebrate California clean air day. They had arts & crafts booths, games, free snacks for the kids and hosted a pretty funny tug a war game between police and firemen. We had a blast!
My kids first time here and they had a blast 🤣😂 There new faviorte park 💙
Love the skate park
Loved it. My daughter enjoyed the baby swing and the splash pad a lot

name: Town Square Park
formatted_address: 68700 Ave Lalo Guerrero, Cathedral City, CA 92234, USA

google_reviews.text: Lovely little oasis off the main highway where you can stop and get cool for a few minutes and enjoy the beautiful fountains in the park

Located directly infront of the city hall and Mary Pickford Theater, also the Fountain of Life. The fountain serves an attraction where kids as well as adults can play in the water. The park is surrounded with sculptures and murals around every corner.  Town Square Park can be rented for an event.
A beautiful small park with sculptures. It has an interesting fountain but it is not meant to play in as some reviews say. There are plenty of benches and shade. No public restrooms.

This is a great local for the locals to congregate and socialize.  Bring your kids they will be thrilled with the playground equipment just for them.  Theirs a⛲ and statues of local pioneers.  It's a educational experience.

I did a very large review under the part of the city Town Square Park that includes the fountains and this is alongside them and it's across the street from City Hall it's just another area there that can be used for people to hang out again and picnic and just enjoy being outdoors especially during the winter and spring months when the temperatures are so ideal for that and

name: Palma Village Park
formatted_address: -44554, 44550 San Carlos Ave, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a decent park. I’ve lived in the neighborhood for over 20 years and seeing it go through changes. There used to be a problem with homeless people spending most of the day there and camping out but seems like the city has cracked down on that. It’s regularly patrolled by local police. Bathrooms are maintained every morning. No graffiti.
Personally can't say anything bad about this park. Have been here plenty of time with family, and we seldom see homeless people here. Always clean. Thanks City of Palm Desert! 🌴
Very clean, serene, and the people who attend are far from mean. Very safe and accommodating place to take your kids for a day out. It also free of cost so I'm not sure if it could get better.

Very clean and well manicured park. Basketball courts, large grass play areas, stroller paths, playground for toddlers and playground for children over 5 are adjacent. Zero transient activity at the park or nearby. Restrooms are available but I did not go inside.
"The Little Playground" always quiet

name: Victoria Park
formatted_address: 2744 N Vía Miraleste, Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text: Buddy and I thank you for providing a respite in this beautiful park for us to rest. Thank you to all y’all Palm Springsonites. 🏜️🕺🐕🏖️
Beautiful, spacious playground. Lots of grassy areas shaded with trees. Foam ground for play structure. Great canvas sails to shade the playground.

Very clean very pretty. I was unaware this was even here until my car died out for a few hours in the heat. And all the happy dogs and their parents looked as if having a blast. Meanwhile the families with kids were at other end on slides and stuff also having a great time. Not once did anyone ask if I was OK it was obvious both my cat and I were way over heated but around 2am an officer finally came by and provided some comfort and when we tried to start engine it actually clicked over. I guess I did know what I was doing when tinkering earlier
Either way will be back to visit as intended
Beautiful park that your kids would love to play at.
Great park in a nice area. The grass is super green with all the rain we have had this Feb 2019. Pics are from my camera drone. Fun place to view the mountains and walk.

name: Baristo Park
formatted_address: 296 S Calle Encilia, Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text: The playground is under construction right now (Feb 2025). We chose a hotel close by so we could easily visit the playground, so that was disappointing to learn. Looks like it is a lovely shaded play area with some grass when it is not fenced off and having the play equipment replaced though!

Quiet and nice place to sit at picnic tables and eat.  Great little place to bring the littles.
Nothing like the laughter of a child.
It's okay. The playground is satisfactory and the water at the drinking fountain is potable. The basketball court is fine, and it's probably a good idea to have a fence between the park and the wash.

The park is great! My kids love going there. It's kept pretty clean and the play ground toys are fairly new! The only draw back is there is quite a few homeless people that camp out in the grass area.

The city installed huge canopies over the playground to keep the swings and slides and other kiddy toys so they don't get burned by the hot metal or plastic play yard things. They installed cameras and audio so they can view the goings on in the park at all times, they chased out the drugs and losers and returned Baristo park to is real name instead of Heroin Park which is been called for years now.

name: Adams Park
formatted_address: 78930 La Palma Dr, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text: Great playground. Just the right size. Very clean. Two shaded play areas with a wide variety of interesting play. Lots of seating. Easy walking path around the playground, with large green space alongside and sand volleyball. Feels safe. Several local families here with younger kids yet not too busy. Parallel parking in neighborhood. No bathrooms seen.
Remodeled Park! All new and clean.

Nice park! Two small wood chip playgrounds - one for younger kids (3 and under) and one for older kids with swings, slides, and things to climb, sand volleyball court, park benches and tables, garbages and recycling, a grassy field, and a gravel walking path around the whole thing.
I love the remodel.  Highly recommend this park for family and pets
Great and fun place to take the kids

name: Panorama Park
formatted_address: 28905 Avenida Maravilla, Cathedral City, CA 92234, USA

google_reviews.text:
Had a small dog and grand child. Nice well kept park. Better than most. Dog park for 2 sized dogs and a playground for 2 sized kids.
There was a homeless guy at the top of the big kids slide, but left when a mom said “my child wants to play and your on a kids structure”. He left right away without issue.
The dog area was nice as was the rest of the park. Enjoyed the splash pad as it was getting close to 90° at the time.

Nice dog park but kind of small. Also found two small puddles in there which I have to keep my pool away from because he loves to stand in these.   They should make the large dog area larger and the small dog area smaller than it is.
There are bigger parks around to take your dog to .
However, there is a convenient children’s park where you can walk your dog on the leash and hang around

A nice mid-size dog park. Has two fenced off areas for large and small dogs. The only complaint is there are usually TWO MUDDY AREAS that must be fixed. Perhaps reposition the sprinklers or add stones. But I guarantee your dog leaves the park dirty. That leads to a dirty car ride home. So bring a seat cover to save you the hassle.
Clean facilities. Ample parking. Park grounds in general are in good order. The ice cream guy nearby offers for a great atmosphere to take your kiddos out for a good, wholesome outing.

Gorgeous park in a very quiet neighborhood. Plenty to do, lush green field, dog walking, jogging, running, yoga all surrounded by mountains. Plenty of parking. Kids slides were closed for some renovations.

name: Dr Carreon Park
formatted_address: 82200 Dr Carreon Blvd, Indio, CA 92201, USA

google_reviews.text: Bathrooms were locked but the playground was pretty unique
Potential to be a nice park but unfortunately there was a lot of homeless people and lots of drug use happening. Not something I wanted my children around so we left.

Homeless people sleeping in the children's play area. Found a small knife laying casually next to the benches. Came at 10am and not a single child, not a single family. But there are cigarette butts in the children's areas. Park is absolutely gorgeous. Architecturally very rad. Lots of green grass. Absolutely will never return. Terribly unsafe.
It's a beautiful clean park!! Has a picnic area, restroom and dog friendly!

Great park! Well maintained. I stopped by to chill a bit in between meetings in the area while in town for business. Was pretty evident that the parks are well maintained in this area. The park was taken care of way better than the ones we have in San Diego county.

name: Eisenhower Park

This is a very beautiful little park!!

Cute little park but beware of the tall curvy slide. It seems a bit dangerous and its not tall on the sides to prevent a kid from flying off. It could've been cause I'm an adult and probably went down faster but even then it just doesn't seem the safest.

I saw one little boy almost fall off cause he was climbing the slide and his hand slipped. I love the whole play structure and park. Just not that slide.
The kid wants to go back tomorrow so I think it's 5 stars
Really nice park with a great overhead shade to keep the sun off the littles. We loved it!

name: La Quinta Community Park
formatted_address: 77865 Avenida Montezuma, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text: Perfect for the entire family. Street parking is available. Clean and well maintained. A basketball court, baseball field, and children's playground are available on first come basis.

Nice, quiet and clean park with a safe play area for kids and even a fitness area for adults. It has a baseball/ softball field, basketball court, eating benches with gazebos over them, a community fitness center building and even a concrete mini stage for performances with a decent amount of open grassy area to play soccer.

name: Demuth Park

We made lunch in the park. Really nice spot. People were playing volleyball, kids were playing and the restrooms were maintained!

Has alot of path for my youngest to practice riding his bike. What i don't like is when you want to sit down and have a little picnic and theres dog crap in the grass and on the sidewalk. Come on all you dog people. Clean up after your pet.
Awesome park, very well kept, connects to the local bike trail, and plenty of parking. Only complaint is there is loud noise from the airport that does destroy the peace and quiet.

This is a 2026 update the pickleball courts are shut down until further notice because they are more than doubling the size of the pickleball courts the number of Courts it's a master project. This is going to be under construction probably through next fall. They have finished the great work on the playgrounds for the children they are nicely covered they have so many new things new types of of places that they can use soft ground below rather than concrete is very very nice. There are eight baseball courts there are so many different soccer courts available to be set up anywhere between 12:00 to 15 when I see the baseball parks as well there is an enclosed small dog park and also the big dog park which is at the farthest west end and again there are coverings there for people when sitting down and bags for cleanup and it's really nice

This park is Clean, safe, not crowded.
Located across a residential    area   I enjoy quiet time here.
Quality of cleanliness in reference to man's best friend
(Dogs)  park provides you with pet dispensary trash bags , for your convenience.
Great idea!  Environment. & Park atmosphere is TRULEY enjoyable.

They just added 2 new basketball courts . Well taken care of and really well lit, in case you want to ball up at night. Very accessible from a traffic point of view definitely recommend going to this park. It has some jungle gyms for the kids .

Great little neighborhood park with a full size basketball  court, playground, benches and tables.  Lots of mature trees, shaded areas and grass. The playground has a curved tube slide, two twirl poles, and a rope spider web and bridge to test kids balance and coordination.  We brought 3 kids, ages 4, 5 and 6. The rope bridge was a challenge for all of them and we had to help them off if it.  They just needed more practice.

This is a nice park to relax and walk  around  using the path or to come play basketball or to bring the kids to cool off with the water area! Early morning or Evenings are best to beat the heat! No Restrooms available!

name: Hjorth Park
formatted_address: 81253 Ave 48, Indio, CA 92201, USA

google_reviews.text: Great park for smaller children and plenty of room for running. Also, this place has a volleyball court and outdoor charcoal grills.

Always drove by this park and never stopped. Since it is in the neighborhood I sort of think of it as more like a neighbor park not necessarily public. I stopped here tonight and it was great. My dog enjoyed himself and all the open space. Not too much grass but enough for a large dog. It was night when we arrived but it was well lit to be able to see our black lab and the 3 culdesacs to the park are great for parking and hopping off. Definitely will return. The park is also great for kids a small family was nearby at the time playing. Plenty of trash and doggy bag spots for pet lovers.
Lovely park, albeit not our favorite. Dog friendly park! Structures for older kids, not toddler friendly compared to other parks. 4/5. Super clean and safe.

Plenty of room for bringing your dog for some fetch or for taking a walk along the path surrounding the grass. Lately, people have not been wearing masks for the pandemic, so I'd be wary and make sure of that before stepping out. Beautiful park with a gorgeous view and even a volleyball court

name: SilverRock Park
formatted_address: 51900 Silverrock Way, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text: Very nice park and clean. This park is to bring your dog for a walk.

Quiet park where tons of dogs can be found romping around off-leash (which is normally illegal but the city doesn’t enforce it). Regular special events, ranging from rocket clubs, kite flying, concerts, walks, triathlons, and more,

Beautiful park. Restrooms are extremely well kept. Dog water bowl worked while we were there. Great scenery, plenty of shade trees with well placed benches and water fountains. For a casual visit, expect at least 45 minutes to walk all of the park and stream. As others have mentioned, not many other people. There are not many options for shaded parking but, with the lack of people, there where several options to keep vehicle shaded. It was approximately 97° during our visit, felt much cooler because of tree shade. Highly recommend visiting.
Park also has an amphitheater and a climate controlled meeting/ events building. Amphitheater appears to be similar size to "The Rock Yard" and meeting/events building appears to easily fit approx. 30 people.
Everyone with dogs had them on leashes.
La Quinta's newest park with a lovely outdoor music pavilion, walking trails, and other amenities. Located inside the gates of the SilverRock Golf Course.

Really nice dog friendly park, dogs are supposed to be on leash, but expect some friendly off leash dogs at times.  Only negative is the drinking fountains for the dogs are broken more often than not.

I only go here to let my dogs run for a bit over by the library. Nice Large grass area. Never have a problem with anyone like you will in Palm Springs. I have Giant dogs and people in PS don't like Giant dogs. I have had animal control & police called on me saying my dogs are malnourished. No cars are going by the dog park. Cars are on the road. A decent distance away. I will assume what they see is a distorted shape of my dogs which would make them think they may be malnourished. Both animal control & Police have said the same thing, They are definitely NOT malnourished!

We go to destiny church their it's a blessing. Service is so good.always great a clear understanding of the word of God tru the pastor's their. A loving welcoming church leading to the Cross. I definitely recommend.

Tejano event is kind of expensive for Cathedral City residents. It's not cover charge but don't buy a bottle a water for 3.95. It what it makes worse is that water has the label from Starter Bros. Don't take wrong Starter Bros I can buy a 36 bottles from the store for the money.
Is really nice to relax and have little eat

name: Machris Park

google_reviews.text: Pretty cool park, lived here for a couple of years and hadn't seen it until the other day. Bathroom is a bit sketchy at night but other than that great park.

In the area for Joshua Tree and came across this perfect park. Ty for putting this here for us. We love having grass to play on, exercise on and rest on. The added bonus of exclusivity was also perfect. Good memories.
Needs a little tlc...but it has a baseball field...playgroung...tables and bbqs.
Nice park took my kids here a few times

Public restrooms were clean, water fountains all worked, and the grass on the VERY LARGE baseball field was nice and GREEN.  The view from behind homeplate is awesome.  I had the opportunity to use the building/room available there (upon request/rental) for job training and although tight in space, had TWO restrooms inside and everything needed for a party/meeting.  I highly recommend this place.  It is also in a BEAUTIFUL neighborhood with fancy houses. Perfect place to go hike.

name: City of Palm Desert Cahuilla Hills Park
formatted_address: 45825 Edgehill Dr, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text:
We didn’t know what to expect of palm desert as it was just the location of our hotel on a road trip through, but wow the scenery here was lovely and we took some time to explore this local hiking park area. Definitely come early in the hot season and bring water and good shoes.  Great trail system. The map at the front isn’t great but all trails helps.

Parking is easy but not a ton of if.

Nice hiking trails did two different hikes up to the Cross the first time, then all the way to the Bump and Grind trail to the top. That was close to 2 1/2 hours and we took the short cut thru the wash back. Overall elevation gain was close to 1,000 feet.

Nice short hike, gets a little thin and windy in places but there is a great view at the top. We did a short hike loop but there are options to go further. Dog friendly too, and bathrooms at the bottom, bonus!!

A hidden gem! I have lived in Palm Desert for over three years, and didn't know about this place until a neighbor mentioned it. We took the Herb Jeffries trail, cut across through Hopalong Cassidy to end up back where we started. Parking is limited, but I think one could park on a residential street, walk across the bridge to the trail head. Our 6- and 4-year olds didn't have a problem, and while our two-year old was willing to walk it, I was concerned about him falling. We did encounter a couple of runners as well as mountain bikers, so keep that in mind. Bring water. And warm clothing if hiking during cooler weather. It gets chilly once the sun gets behind the mountains. There's a restroom at the entrance to the park, and some of the trails are open to canine companions, but check the posted signs.

Great hike. We did the Cross Trail Loop. There was a fair amount of climbing at the beginning, so the return was faster and primarily downhill. The peak rewarded us with gusty breezes and an amazing view. Took us about two hours, at a leisurely pace. Dogs are welcome.

name: Fritz Burns Park
formatted_address: 78107 52nd Ave, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text: There is big pool of players in the three morning session (Fri-Sun) I was there. Given there are 16 courts, the wait is still pretty short. I tried the evening session (Saturday after 6pm), there are far fewer players and many empty courts, so you might need to wait and ask around for a drop-in play. The pool and parking appears to be under reconstruction currently so there is very few parking spots on-site and I have park in the neighborhood across the street. beware of the no parking sign right across the tennis court, I see parking enforcement given out tickets Sunday morning.

My husband & I stopped by to play pickleball at Fritz Park ... excellent courts!  We drilled bc no one was available to let us join in. This park is under construction and parking is across the street. It'll be more grand once construction is complete.  Looking forward to playing there again cuz now I have a name!

The park is under construction until Spring of 2026, but the pickleball is always great here! Lots of players and everyone is friendly. You need to follow the paddle stacking rules or you'll get bumped to the end of the line. 16 pickleball courts.
People are friendly and very welcoming.   All ages and levels welcome.   Plenty of courts and good play.

This is the place for Pickleball.  Starts early just put your paddle up and play some games.  There is a 4.0 plus area as well so you’re bound to get some great games at every level. The construction is scarring some people away so it’s not as busy but still a great place for morning and evening Pickleball.

name: Downtown Park
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text:
The
Marilyn Monroe statues is better than life-size so much to do there’s a museum there. There’s actually a graffiti park which I found very interesting numerous restaurants. It just was busy and people were out shopping. It’s a place that you really have to take your time and wander around I can’t wait to come back.

A nice use of what was once a connecting street turned into a park, small band stage and seating area, unisex bathrooms with communal hand wash station.  Art displays. The Palm Springs Museum, shopping, dining,  and larger than life Marilyn statue are all adjacent.

This spot is a gem if you’re into public art, great photo ops, or just a peaceful stroll during sunset. The large metal sculpture is bold and modern — really cool to walk around and see from different angles. Right next to it is the famous Marilyn Monroe statue, which is massive and beautifully lit at night. Super fun and nostalgic!

The whole area is well-lit, clean, and surrounded by palm trees and mountain views, which makes for an awesome backdrop. There’s also a graffiti-style art area nearby that gives it a more urban and creative vibe.

Whether you’re visiting Palm Springs or just passing through, this is a must-see spot. Great place to relax, take photos, and enjoy some unique local art.

-Pedro

Stay tuned. Museum next!

Not an ideal spot for tourists - lacks fountains and activities. However, the park has a pleasant atmosphere, showcasing the characteristics of Palm Springs. Abundant palm trees and walls reflecting local soil features. Numerous seating options for relaxation.

This was a great place to stop and sit for a few minutes. The fountain is nice although there was a lot of trash at the base. We originally went to this park for the splash pad area but it wasn’t on. A little more shade in some areas would be nice too, but I bet it’s awesome in the winter and spring.

The 4 hour free parking garage that connects to the park is awesome, and there is a hiking trail across the street for the cooler months. It’s also within walking distance to the shopping and restaurant area.

name: Josie Johnson Vista Park
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text: It's a fun hike, beautiful scenery, not long at all. 1 mile up. It's a little sad that the waterfall is a deminished, but still a beautiful sight. It's a paid hike. Can be guided. Staff is super friendly and helpful. FYI. You are required to have water with you, if you don't bring some you can buy some or you can't buy a ticket.

The lady at the visitors center was very nice and super friendly. I asked her if when walking stick would do. I told her I had to artificial knees and she mentioned that some of the steps were about 20 in so I went back to the car and got my other walking stick. Is better with two walking sticks. The Rangers are super friendly and very informative. This is my first trip there it was a little bit advance for me. But I I know what it's like for next time. It was a beautiful day here in March. Good time of year for the hike. I went to the where the two waterfalls in the pond was I didn't go up to the main waterfall that was a little more advanced for myself. The great place and I saw a lot of families there.
Debatably the best high latitude trail you can take in the valley.

This trail is hidden and has 2 access points Viz., South Lykken and North Lykken trail heads. The South Lykken takes exactly 2 miles and 1000ft gain to reach this point. The views are stunning with cacti found all around the trail path. Stay on trails and don't climb on loose and withering rocks.
Great hike for all level of hikers. Ninety minutes to the top. Great views!

name: Guy J Tedesco Park
formatted_address: 12-800 W Arroyo Dr, Desert Hot Springs, CA 92240, USA

google_reviews.text:
Finding this gem was a refreshing surprise during our travels! The park is larger than it first appears.

The park had swings, a few slides, and unique rock climbing towers! There are lots of picnic tables with shade next to the playground.

There were a few large grassy areas for dogs to enjoy. The sidewalk went all the way around the park! The large skate ramp park area looked very fun!

My toddler loves this park more than any other. He’s made friends, perfected walking by himself, learned how to climb stairs, ran with puppies in the grass, watched the skateboarder in awe, and screams in excitement at the different big swing and roller slide. I’ve made friends and gotten great parenting advice. I’ve never seen homeless, drug addicts, or excessive amounts of trash & haven’t used bathrooms, (locked bc of COVID) but found a bullet casing by the slide recently (minus a star).

I've heard it called several things, veterans Day park, roundabout park, and something else I can't remember.

Not a lot to talk about, it's configured in a circle of picnic tables (6 or 7), very clean as it rarely gets used.

No swings, sandbox, bathrooms, or other amenities.
They do have 2 water spigots and they both work.

Located on the corner of Palm Dr and Mission Lakes Blvd.

Don't do anything illegal cuz you're right out in the open. Avoid going to the underpass as someone is living there, I like going there because they have a descent patch of really thick and lush green grass with clover mixed in, a natural deterrent for ants..

I'll try to remember to snap a couple photos next time I'm over there.
Very small dog park and we got soaked by sprinklers at around 5pm ish when we were there. So we ended up hiking up hill with dogs as quiet and huge, but a steepish hill.
Playground equipment new, nice skate and BMX park and picnic grass.  Basketball courts and community room that can be rented for events.

Its clean i like that theres plenty of benches and swings only problem is the neighborhood i hate the fact that i been here a few times and cars always come and park and there either drinking beer or smoking, ughh i mean come on its a kids park for crying out loud!
Decent place. But not during the summer. Too hot! Best go during fall or winter.
It was chill relaxed enjoyed some breakfast with my hunny at the park , enjoyed the morning breeze
Quiet little park with a padded play area, two basketball full-courts, and a few picnic tables.

Nice park with access to the Morongo Valley preserve trails.
Great public park,  softball field,  skate park, picnic area, play ground, basketball courts, and roght next to the Morongo Preserve hiking trails.
Great natural area, as well as play park for children. They even have skate boarding area and a wonderful Art co-op filled with unique items from Local Artisans

A funky park that is great for bird watching!
I visit once a week and always see a great variety of birds.

name: Michael S. Wolfson Park
formatted_address: Frank Sinatra Dr, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270, USA

google_reviews.text: Delightful little park that has several beautiful cactus.  It is very small about the silver of a football field or smaller.  There is a walking trail that goes along the Riverbed. The park is upkept and great to digest the day if you dont mind the sounds of traffic on Frank Sinatra.
Beautiful park! We met a photographer here for a quick family picture and were created with wonderful backgrounds with plants and mountain views.
Great little park to take a break… good facilities but no bathroom
New parking area and charge stations added. Lovely setting, walking paths, roses, views and trailhead

Decent area for a quick breathe of fresh air. The area seems small but it is so deal w . Has a trail attached so you can make a trek from one side of the neighborhood to the other. Nice area for lizards and birds as well as some bees and such.

name: City of Palm Desert Hovley Soccer Park
formatted_address: 74735 Hovley Ln E, Palm Desert, CA 92260, USA

google_reviews.text: Awesome place to spend your (and kids) extra energy. Or, to walk in a serene quietness

A fabulous park with so much open space for soccer,  walking or jogging. There are lots of big trees along the perimeter to shade your walk on those hot days and great mountain views! It also has a Frisbee golf course and playground area for kids plus a covered picnic area and restrooms.  Gets busy on the weekends but normally pretty quiet on weekdays.

Palm Desert Hovley Soccer Park is more than just a soccer park.  Yes, the park is bustling with soccer activity in its center. But, the park sports a disc golf course, horeshoes, and a basketball court.  Petanque is played on the east side of the park next to playground that my grandkids love.  You can belly up to the snack bar. Parking is ample, but filled during soccer ⚽️ games.  The path around the park is roughly .6 of a mile in length.  If you are trying to become a local legend, log your run on Strava. After your run, turn your head to the west and gaze up at the mountains, and thank God for this beautiful park.  😍 But, the important thing about the Palm Desert Hovley Soccer Park is: it is more than just a soccer park.
FUN field, easy access to parking, snack booth, and restrooms. Had a great time watching my niece play.  Tons of parking on three sides of the facility, which allows easy and close parking for all
Beautiful park to watch events, picnic, soccer and more... Very Clean with plenty of room for everyone.

name: Idyllwild Park
formatted_address: 54380 N Circle Dr, Idyllwild-Pine Cove, CA 92549, USA

google_reviews.text: Lovely small park in the shape of a circle, surrounded by trendy businesses and points of interest.  Do try the ice cream shop and the Red Kettle if you're in the mood for breakfast!  It's a very nice area.

Best mountain town on planet earth. If you've never been, you absolutely should go, and bring the family. Tons of cool little shops. Lots of neat places to eat. Great hiking, (if you're into that). It is a small town, with a community of people who love living there and running it and it shows. And if you're there on the weekend, you're sure to see mayor Max, the dog, (yes the official mayor of the town). It's an amazing place to go for a day that will have you coming back over and over for years to come.

We love visiting Idyllwild and meet friends there for monthly lunch dates. It is such a charming town with so much to offer - the cooler climate, mountain air, trails, vintage shops, great eateries, entertainment, farmer's market, peace, love and joy!

This place is cozy. We stayed the night up here a few weeks back after fresh snow and walked the shops. Ate some good food and met some real friendly people.

I’d recommend if you live in the surrounding area to stay here in the winter at least once in your life. It’s worth it.

Idyllwild was an Excellent little Town to visit for the day. Had some homemade pecan pie with some hot cocoa. People are really nice and friendly. We even got to meet Mayor Max of their hometown who happens to be a Handsome Golden Retriever! Lots of little mom n pop shops along with restaurants. Beautiful to see during winter time and thank goodness we didn't need snow chains. Happy safe travels everyone🙏

name: Esperanza Park
formatted_address: 30600 San Diego Dr, Cathedral City, CA 92234, USA

google_reviews.text: Hidden gem tucked in on the east side of the  Dream Homes neighborhood of Cathedral City is a beautiful park with a playground, workout equipment, picnic tables, restrooms, large grass field and separate fenced area for dogs. Parking lot is located on Mia street adjacent to the CV Link.

Beautiful new park! It has a soccer field, playground for the kids, (at this time) clean bathrooms. Plenty of water fountains and a dog park!
Not having any kids at home now I was interested in the dog park.

Not much shade as of yet, but in the winter time this is going to be a great place to bring the dogs!

Beautiful park with all new equipment, the kids loved the playground they had shade and so did we at the picnic tables which was nice. The dog park is very nice as well. Also has its own entrance to cv link.

This kids swing will be fatal one day...
The park was designed by someone who never had children. The only thing they seem to try to perfect was the dog park
Very cool for up coming practice for soccer

name: Hurkey Creek Park
formatted_address: 56375 CA-74, Mountain Center, CA 92561, USA

google_reviews.text: WOW, that’s all I have to say. This park is always amazing. My wife and I met 17yrs ago and we use to go tent camping for Easter here with the whole Fam. We had stopped going. Now that we have our own beautiful family we will not miss out on any more trips here, but it will during off peak. We just finished our trip from Feb 15 for 3days. Such a great time. Thank you Hurkey Creek staff so friendly and park is very very clean

We loved our experience camping here. We had both trailer and tent camping in our sites. The sites were well-maintained. Lots of bugs plants and adventures to be had at the campground. Lots of big groups camped there. The Rangers were good at getting the noise down during quiet hours. So close to Idlewild. we will definitely go back.

Currently in July 2025 there are not fires permitted. The creek was low, but we were able to find a nice place for young children to enjoy the water. Very hot in the day and cool at night during the time of our stay.
Great camp ground, helpful staff, recommend visiting Idyllwild to meet Mayor Max!

The sites here are pretty close together, and the creek was dried up when we visited. The group at the site next to us had a loud party going on past 10:00pm, and even though the ranger showed up, nothing was done since they continued to party. Overall it’s not too bad, though. The location is convenient, about 15 minutes from the town of Idyllwild and only 3 minutes from Lake Hemet.

Came for a RivCo parks event and had a great time! Camping was good, the Rangers were great, facilities clean.  A rattlesnake was spotted and the Rangers caught and relocated it so kids would be safe!  Highly recommend!

name: Veterans Park

google_reviews.text: Memorial Day at the park was very special and memorable. Thank you for putting all this together. It's a great way to honor the fallen on Memorial Day.
Very calm and peaceful.  Perfect scenic location to remember and celebrate our veterans.

Very simple and quiet place to rest while walking

name: Essig Park

google_reviews.text: Lovely small dog park with nice people. There is a big dog area too, we just happen to have small ones. One of the most beautiful and well-kept dog parks in the country. I travel in my RV with my dog, Pajamas, and we have been to ‘em all. The mountain views here are stunning! Nice people, grass and stone park, very clean…and did I mention those views? What a jewel.
Very well kept dog park.It was awesome!

My girl and I went there for a sushi picnic. We sat underneath the pavilion at one of the picnic tables that they have there.
We saw some children playing on the playground with their children, and people bringing their dogs for the dog area.
Overall, it is a nice, clean, and quiet Park.
This was our experience and I hope that you go here take your experience will be just as pleasant.
Lovely park with a playground for big kids and one for littles. Restrooms were clean and worked well, and there was a nice dog park too with lots of happy pups. And the view is great!!

name: Saguaro Park

google_reviews.text:
Small neighborhood park. They just remodeled it maybe two years ago to have new equipment, bark chips, etc. I love that it’s not accessible from Washington st. That helps keep it from being too busy.

The noise from Washington Street makes it very loud. All day. It is not a quiet, idyllic park.
Great little park, perfect for my 14 mo old to swing and play in the grass. Beautiful views of the surrounding mountains.

name: Memorial Park

google_reviews.text: This is different from the other parks I have visited in the city, not so much green but, more on the design in this park was made and crafted. For sure, neat & clean! It's a memorial of Office David Vasquez who was killed on the line of duty.
Good place to rest your feet if walking through Cathedral City Hall or Mary Pickford Theatre. Contains a bench and waste basket for trash.  Always well maintained, clean, and landscaped.

name: Desert Healthcare (Wellness) Park
formatted_address: Palm Springs, CA 92262, USA

google_reviews.text:
An absolutely wonderful wellness park surrounded by stunning, towering rock formations. The scenery alone makes this place special—you truly can’t ask for a more breathtaking backdrop.

Spending time here leaves you feeling relaxed, refreshed, and renewed. I highly recommend visiting, and I will definitely be returning.

This is one of the nicest parks in Palm Springs. In the morning you will find many dog walkers, and others. Just enjoying the cool mornings. Later in the day, you will find locals and tourists who want a nice, relaxing walk. It's adjacent to Ruth Hardy Park which offers many amenities, including a brand new playground for children. One of my favorite places in Palm Springs. Please come and enjoy

The wellness park at Ruth  Hardy Park is everything and anything you want it to be. Many dog lovers bring their four legged family members to the park for exercise and enjoyment and to socialize with others. People use the park for their daily exercise routines going from each permanent exercise point to the next, located along the pathways. It is also a very peaceful place just to sit on a bench, watch people, reflect and maybe throw in an afternoon siesta. There is also a Saturday morning yoga class led by an instructor in the core of the park as well as other health expos at various times throughout the year.
Very clean well maintained park with workout equipment. I saw many different birds and plant life. Good spot to hangout.
Just what I needed that day to do some grounding and mediation.

name: Dog Park

A nice breeze was blowing, snowy peaks in the distance, grass was green, the small trees provided shade, it felt safe, there was water, what more could I ask for?  My dog and I were happy.  Recommended dog park, we will be back!
I’ll never come here again! The dogs are great the scenery is amazing but 8 out of 10 people are judgy to newcomers or “hippies” as one old geezer referred to me as. 🥴

name: Pioneer Dog Park
formatted_address: 78695 Miles Ave, La Quinta, CA 92253, USA

google_reviews.text:
Update 3/2024:
A year later, still great.
*During dusk, the pathways are gently lit.
*More grass has filled in the dirt patches. *Less public toys to use.
*Metal poop scoop and rake to use (less plastic poop bag use = good)
*Dedicated parking lot

Out-of-towner Review:
* fully fenced with TWO separated areas (one for small dogs and one for large dogs)
* hours : dawn till 10pm
* ground cover : grass, dirt/sand, paved pathway for humans and three paved patios with benches and shade cover
* multiple trees (shade) and a hill create variety for dogs
* plethora of tennis balls and random dog toys on ground for public use
* poop bags provided (at multiple posts) and garbage cans

This was a great park for our four day use. We went morning and evening for our two pups and each time any and all dogs we met were great.

Playground nearby for human kids to play, too.

Palm Desert does a great job of providing safe spaces for dogs to run around. This is a prime example, there is ample free parking close by. My only issue was the amount of dog poo that others had not picked up. Come on people, pick up your dogs waste. It’s not like they don’t provide you with bags and garbage to dispose it in.

Dog park was not open when I arrived at 6pm today. No signs or anything says it’s closed but there were bike locks at the gate. And people use the playground as dog park, dog poop in play ground area where kids run around, not cool
Nice dog park with sections for large and small dogs. Well cared for and there is pioneer park right next to it. Our dog Nugget liked it.

A great place! Good people and good dogs. Self policing owners, if a dog can't behave, you gotta go. Poop bags on hand and watering stations. Good size for dogs to run. Separate big and small dog areas. We go every day. New folks and dogs are always welcomed enthusiastically.

name: Idyllwild Regional Park
formatted_address: 54000 Riverside County Playground Rd, Idyllwild-Pine Cove, CA 92549, USA

google_reviews.text: Super quiet, clean bathrooms. The shower steam is so strong that it was overwhelming. Nice area with plenty of beautiful trees and view. Good place to hike and climb rocks.

This is a really nice campground! Some sites are on a slant, so it helps to research before booking. We stayed at site #58, which was a great choice—it was close enough to the restrooms for convenience (especially helpful since we had a pregnant friend with us) but not so close that it was noisy. The restrooms are flushable and well-kept, though I wouldn’t recommend the sites directly behind them since you’ll hear a lot of flushing. It’s also super convenient that the campground is right in town, so we had quick access to anything we needed. One thing to note is that hammocks aren’t allowed, but overall we really enjoyed our stay.
We love camping here. Nice clean flush toilets and showers. We like #21 .  We save money on hotels by camping. We love getting coffee and pizza in town.

Needs to update payment system. Their online payment service doesn’t work, you would need exact cash. Bring your own pen. Not much to see for the $6 fee and additional $2 pet fee for day use. Recommend going elsewhere.

Tucked away in the mountains, Idyllwild Regional Park is pure magic. The trails weave through towering pines and massive boulders, with quiet pockets of shade that feel like secret hideouts. Campsites are clean, well-spaced, and you actually get some real peace and quiet without feeling packed in like sardines. Morning walks here are insane — crisp air, birds everywhere, and those slow-burn sunrise colors bleeding through the trees. If you want that old-school California mountain town vibe without the chaos, this place hits different.

name: Hot Springs Park
formatted_address: 10-901 Palm Dr, Desert Hot Springs, CA 92240, USA

google_reviews.text: Very chill park and nicely located next to the spa which is very convenient.

This Park is Beautiful, but the city is NOT MAINTAINING it well at all!

The drinking fountain, that should be allowing our visitors to taste the wonderful, healing, naturally chilled mineral water from our underground aquifers, is OUT OF ORDER, covered with GRAFFITI, and the back is covered with a loose sheet of plywood.

And, who designed this fountain?  The thing is full of non-galvanized, iron-based metal strips in full contact with water that are RUSTED and CRUMBLING and STREAKING ugly brown streaks.

How can our citizens be expected to take code enforcement and the clean up crews seriously when we can't even keep our public city landmarks from falling into disrepair.

We can do better!
The employees are great. There  customers service is great. Their food is awesome the hotel staff are very attentive to the guest I will be coming back

Watch out price is listed for $55 but they add another $50 for amenities.  FYI the clubhouse, pool and golf course (the amenities) is closed in September.  FAKE PRICING

google_reviews.text: Nice area with trails to suit everyone. While some of the boardwalk is still closed for repair, most is accessable. there many other trails up to 9 miles.

We went for a morning hike at Big Morongo Canyon Preserve, and were hoping for some good bird watching.  It was both a great hike and had great bird watching!  The trails are well marked and there are several - they are marked for distance and difficulty.  The bird watching on the trails was good, but it was really great not too far from the entrance were a fountain and various bird feeders had been set up.  We saw a huge flock of Western Bluebirds, it was fantastic.  We also saw hummingbirds, plenty of various finches, scrub jays, woodpeckers, and more.  It was great!
we had an AMAZING time here, the trails were quiet and beautiful. Definitely come here and try to donate a few dollars when you come

Worth the drive for a nice afternoon in the trees and fresh air.

Nice nature museum.

Note to staff.

Thank you

name: Prescott Preserve
formatted_address: RF5J+XM7, Palm Springs, CA 92264, USA

google_reviews.text:
Prescott Preserve isn’t just a park — it’s a statement. A bold, visionary transformation of land into something meaningful, restorative, and genuinely beautiful. What was once a golf course has been reimagined into a thriving desert sanctuary that perfectly balances preservation, accessibility, and purpose.

The trails are peaceful, well-designed, and easy to enjoy whether you’re out for a morning walk, an evening sunset stroll, or just a quiet escape from the city’s pace. The natural landscaping, water features, and emerging habitats create a calm, immersive experience that feels intentional — not manufactured. It’s the kind of place that makes you slow down without trying.

What truly elevates Prescott Preserve is the long-term vision behind it. This isn’t a “finished project,” it’s a living ecosystem in progress — with native plant restoration, wildlife support, and community education clearly at the core. It reflects thoughtful planning, respect for the land, and a future-focused commitment to sustainability that most developments only talk about.

Prescott Preserve delivers something rare: serenity, purpose, and authenticity in one space. It’s not just a nice place to walk — it’s a place that matters. A true asset to Palm Springs and a model for what modern land stewardship should look like.

This preserve is fairly new and needs time to develop. The former golf course closed in 2022, and it looks pretty rough now. Volunteer groups are slowly trying to transform it. Walking/biking trails are decent, but there isn’t much to please the eyes along the way. Much of it has gone to scrub. It is surrounded by private condo associations.
Beautiful preserve, so happy to hike around the preserve every time I visit Palm Springs. A birder’s paradise.

Good birding and walking area. A former golf course abandoned several years ago and turned into a nature reserve by the Oswit Land Trust. Easy to overlook. Enter on the east side of El Cielo Rd. at E. Mesquite Ave. Step over a low chain at the small signs pictured here. No wheels allowed. Google Maps gives a misleading location indicator as of March 3, 2024.

The Prescott Preserve is a stunning example of local land conservation. Oswit Land Trust is creating a beautiful nature preserve for wildlife and all members of the Coachella Valley community to enjoy. I love bicycling through the nature preserve! I love seeing the wildlife from a distance and knowing that they are safe.

name: San Jacinto Wilderness

name: Lake Hemet Forest Service Picnic Area

Lake Fulmor is beautiful right now. Wear mosquito repellent because there are lots and lots of the pesky critters here. Its a nice place to spend a day fishing, today it was just a few people it was very cold but will come back i spring time.

name: Bear Creek Oasis

google_reviews.text: One of my favorite hikes in the desert.  Each time I go I hardly run into any other hikers maybe due to the length and gain. The oasis is just a collection of palm trees and natural debris but there is no visible water reservoir that some of our other desert oases offer.  Definitely worth a visit!

I enjoy hiking!  This is no match for hikes in Washington state but it is beautiful in its own way.

Bobby approves this place.
