name: Smiley Park
formatted_address: 101 Parkwood Dr, Redlands, CA 92373, USA

google_reviews.text: Green grass and plenty of shade from old trees pepper this neighborhood park. Horseshoe pit in back corner is available to the public. They have public restrooms . Shaded seating on long little League style benches are here as well. There is a water fountain for drinking.

Smiley Park is as small a park as they come, with a public restroom available for your convenience. It does provide a cement walking path for strollers that leads from one side of the park to the other. There are no playgrounds, just grassy terrain, but there are plenty of benches and trash cans. My family made the most of it by walking the path of the park to the sidewalk, where we kept going to the next street, walking past the Redlands Bowl and then on to the Lincoln Musuem before heading back. The entire walk was stroller friendly.

The amazing and eclectic collection of trees in this small park is breathtaking. So many different species of all sizes, I found myself in awe of the blend of natural beauties. Wow! Check it out even if you are not from this area.

Smiley Park, located in Redlands, California, is a picturesque urban park known for its beautiful landscape and recreational amenities. Spanning across several acres, it offers lush green spaces, walking paths, picnic areas, and playgrounds, making it a popular spot for families, joggers, and outdoor enthusiasts. The park also hosts community events and concerts, providing a welcoming and serene environment for relaxation and leisure activities.

Smiley Park makes for a great day of fun in Redlands. Not only is it full of beautiful landscaping of mature green trees, it is always clean and immaculate. It’s one of the very few parks that offers much activity and history in one place. Walking through, you will find the A.K. Smiley Public Library which is registered with the National Historic Society, as well as the Lincoln Shrine which houses one of the largest collections of Abraham Lincoln memorabilia.  It is also home to the Redland’s Bowl which is an outdoor amphitheater that features exceptional music concerts and performances throughout the summer months. This is a great place to explore for the day, or just to visit one of the venues located there. I highly recommend taking some time to stop by and enjoy.

name: Aurantia Park
formatted_address: 29624 Greenspot Rd, Highland, CA 92346, USA

google_reviews.text:
​Aurantia Park is a fantastic community space—clean, well-maintained, and perfect for families. But for anyone with roots in this area, it's so much more than just a park. It’s a tangible connection to the history that lies just beneath the surface of our modern neighborhoods.
​As the son of a man raised by his grandparents on what was then Elder Gulch Road, this park holds special significance for me. I grew up hearing my dad's stories of life here when Highland was the citrus capital of the nation. He'd talk about having to get up on frigid winter mornings to help light the smudge pots in the groves to protect the oranges from frost before heading off to catch the school bus. That was life in the shadow of the mighty Gold Buckle packing house, which provided the company housing they lived in.
​I was just a four-year-old kid when the Gold Buckle fire lit up the night sky in 1971. In reality, I'm not sure if I truly remember the glow of the flames myself or if my memory is built more from the vivid stories my father has shared over the years. Either way, that event marked the end of an era.
​Setting that aside, it's incredible to think how many people living in the beautiful homes of East Highlands Ranch today have no idea their neighborhood sits on land with such a rich history. This park serves as a perfect tribute. Seeing a preserved portion of the old Plunge Creek Bridge here is a powerful reminder of the world that once was. Aurantia Park isn't just a place for our dogs to play. I's a place that helps us "remember when" and honors the citrus pioneers who shaped this community.

This park has workout equipment for adults and as of fall 2023 a new children's play structure. There are 2 swings and a gated area where the play structure is. A nice park with several shaded areas with benches, a dog park and a community garden. The little library there can always use some new donations. I've hosted meetups and gatherings here that were great. Port o pottie and no hand washing facility, plan accordingly.

Love the dog park!!!  Wish they would regularly stock the poop bags & send a cleaning crew for the inconsiderate folks who don't clean up after their dogs.   It sometimes can be quite disgusting & unsanitary.

Great park with grill, covered seating, and full play place. Plenty of parking. When covered with snow, the whole park is a sledding wonderland with small hills in the front and a large slope on the back half of the park.

name: Lillian Eaton Park
formatted_address: 34282 Yucaipa Blvd, Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text: It is a pretty park, with a rocky stream with bridges crossing it. Fountains and flowers. A chess board table top. But not much to do except walk through it, and there is not very many shady spots.
People enjoy protesting here.  In front of city hall. At least they stand on the sidewalk and in the park like traditional protesters, and not in the middle of the roadway.   So the park is busy.
Quiet and very well kept. Want a place to just be alone? This is the place.
Cute, quaint little park. Nice place to wait for local bus route, and meet locals.
Perfect Place to sit and Meditate with snacks and a little music!!!

name: Erwin Lake Park
formatted_address: 1140 Hatchery Dr, Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text:
Nice little park. Definitely check it out for the animals.

Cute loop to ride bikes on or walk around. On the backside you can get very close to the farm with animals. It's super cool for the kids.

Plenty of places to have a picnic.

Parking is easy and right out front.

Easy play for small kids.

Small but cute park especially for birthday parties or family gatherings. This is my family's go to place for warm weather fun and BBQ’S. There are live animals on the out-skirts (other side of the fence) and the kids love it.
A well maintained park. In closed by fence. Large area for riding a bike, playing in the grass, sitting under the canopy, play Volleyball just hanging out on the play ground. It's a great park.

Great little park. Not busy. Good for a party or a quick stop with the kids. Enough grass to play with a ball, winding path and small bridges for riding toys, sand, and a Llama next door.

Playground is great except that the baby swings are directional and facing the afternoon/evening sun.

I only rate low because of all the great hikes in the area, but,  if you're kids are really little it might deserve a better grade. Big Bear has invested some time& money.  Playground is safe and newer. Completely fenced! Bathroom & picnic area are immaculate!

name: Horsemen's Center Park
formatted_address: 24320 CA-18, Apple Valley, CA 92307, USA

google_reviews.text: Came out for a RC rock crawling event. The facilities are nice. No power or water hookups for campers. The event hosts had portable toilets brought in.

For over 30 years, this is one of my favorite places to go wander around and hike through the rocks with friends and family, even the little tiny ones.

When I was a teenager, we used to watch BMX races, and to this day, around the foot of the mountain, you can still see a lot of dirt bikes and off-road vehicle excitement. But don't worry, because of the rocks it surrounds, the dirt at the base is very heavy sand so it doesn't create too much dust.

And when I was in my twenties, I remember seeing rock climbing tie offs drilled into the side of the tall rocks... I traveled over 900 miles to visit my roots, and couldn't leave without going to Horseman Center, but I didn't go so far to see if the rock climbing tie offs are still there.
But YOU should go see if you can find them!
IT BEAUTIFUL THERE!

Enjoy! (And keep it clean!)
It's very clean and quiet place that we can walk or hike on the rocks

A warning to all future visitors, the park closes as soon as it gets dark, not 6:30 like it says on here. If you are not out of the parking lot by the time the sun is set, they WILL lock you in. And maybe even get mad at you as if you're a misbehaving kid, even if it's your FIRST time here. Regardless, it is a very beautiful place to hike and climb. I would still highly recommend it.
Awesome if you love climbing rocks, beautiful views, and love the outdoors.

name: Avenue A Park
formatted_address: 35145 Ave A, Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text: This is an interesting please to visit. The gentleman there told us all about the history of the first fire station in Yucaipa! It was actually the first in all of San Bernardino county! Lots of fun relics are housed there in the old fire house.
Good place to park my truck while I work. Wonderful birds singing and playing. I think parrots pass through on their migration south.
Well since it WAS my first time I'll give it 5 stars but if I were to base it on a friend's opion I would give it 3
Nice place to catch your breath.

name: Redlands park
formatted_address: 1200 E Colton Ave, Redlands, CA 92374, USA

google_reviews.text: So lovely!!! Beautiful experience when you visit.
The bathrooms are clean and the park is clean it is very nice place to go and relax get out in front of the TV
Beautiful day for an event. This Park has a lot of character .
Koo place to park n chill
Nice park

name: Sunrise Park
formatted_address: 3Q7C+G7 Loma Linda, California, Redlands, CA 92374, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice little park

name: Boulder Bay Park
formatted_address: 39098 Big Bear Blvd, Big Bear Lake, CA 92315, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a wonderful little park nestled in the corner of Big Bear Lake.   Beautiful rounded boulders frame the area and a couple small islands not far from shore.   Clean restrooms and a gazebo with plenty of seating.   Fishermen really love this place and there were a bunch of families and children enjoying the scenery.
Beautiful park, great overall scenery!  Saw the pair of eagles fly over with a fish that was caught!  What a great place to sit and soak in all the beauty Big Bear has to offer!!

Absolutely perfect park! Beautiful views of Boulder Bay and the lake. Excellent little beach to lay down a blanket and some pillows and chill out. Nice grassy area with picnic tables and public restrooms. You can even rent kayaks and paddle boards just be sure and get a lake use permit. Parking is limited so I would get there by 10 or 11 at the latest on a weekend.

We rented kayaks to use in the bay. We didn't realize we had to pull them down to the lake...but it was our agenda for the day! So pull them we did! Its not a hard walk but definitely not good if you have leg or hip problems.  The kayaking was nice and easy. We could see smoke from a wildfire, lots of nice homes, and plenty of boulders! Also, we saw some wildlife on the beach and while paddling around.  Something fun to do with the family or friends!
This park is fantastic. Super pet-friendly and has terrific views of the lake.

name: Pinehurst Community Park
formatted_address: Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text:
Pinehurst Community Park in beautiful Yucaipa,California is a neat hidden gem! There are trees and places to relax and sit or you can bring your own beach chairs,a piknik lunch and strollers.Next to the kids jungle gym play set there is a fun floor water splash fountain for the kids (you activate it by stepping on the round button at the front of the fountain)! For a family meal or fun time their is a great round bench and seating for about 6-8 people under a trellis covering for a gathering or birthday!
For convenience their is a nearby shopping center with in walking distance that features a Vons Market,McDonalds,frozen yogurt,a US Bank and other stores too-Enjoy!
.
.
.
.
#pinehurstcommunitypark #pinehurstdrive #family #piknik #parks #enjoy #godisgood #usbank #mcdonalds #vonsmarkets #staples #dennys #yucaipa #visityucaipa #iloveyucaipa
This little known Park is perfect, especially in summer with the splash pad, although it doesn't come on until 10:30 and it's summer only
Cute little park , glad it’s only for 2-5 years old , only downside no place to wash hands or use bathroom other than that my daughter loved it
Great place to relax in the summer with the slash pad & the playground set & pet friendly

Splash pad.nuff said
Other than that it's ok, don't sit on grass it is crazy damp

name: Crest Park
formatted_address: Lake Arrowhead, CA 92352, USA

google_reviews.text: Smallest of three picnic parks in the area. Closed until November 30, 2024, due to wildfire risk.
Gorgeous early morning views, atop cloud 9 ...
Small park, incredible vista, clean, 2 long-drop toilets. Nice place to breath in some mountain air, or something else  ;)
great views! nice little stop for a picnic
Was just driving the rim. Never even heard of crest park. Anything on the rim is nice though.

name: Wildwood Canyon State Park
formatted_address: 12241 Canyon Dr, Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text:
We will have to come back and do the actual trail. Today we walked the path along the park and found an entrance leading down into the wash which was a fun little walk. We saw horseback riders, dirt bike riders, and plenty of other people with dogs.

Update: Came back and completed the Oak Tree Loop. It's a moderate hike with scenic views. We saw a family of deer and they let me take pictures for a long time :)

Not a hike to be scoffed at if you're on foot in the middle of summer like I was. The wilderness is great and majestic and there are many trails to explore with some interesting stops and some wildlife to see along the way. I saw many deer and a lynx in the parking lot. Also the old house is a good place to rest in the shade and get some water.

Yo I came here today (4/18/2025) and it was my first time hiking this joint. My legs are tired so not sure if that’s good or bad to yall reading this review. Anyways, very nature low noise good trails. Some incline or you can opt out for the easier trails. People being their pets like dogs and horses. They walk the dogs and the horses carry their owners. I didn’t see anyone walking their turtle so idk if you wanna bring it here for a walk.

Anyways nature wise, I saw a bunch of birds and of many kinds (just check out the pictures I added and you know what add me on the gram @jluzphoto). There’s also deer and like other animals. We did come across a rabbit of sorts with no head, don’t know what did it but I’d take it as a warning to stay out?!

There’s a cool abandoned house, lowkey creepy if you ask me cuz there was a game cam pointing right at it and it makes me wonder if it’s haunted or some. I did add a photo of the house (add me on the gram @jluzphoto).

Anyways, I’d totally go back if I’m ever in the area specifically for hiking. I rate this place 14 out of 14. Always check me out on the gram if you haven’t done so after reading this wonderful review @jluzphoto

Easy, well maintained, and well marked trails. Lots of oak trees and other native plants. Interesting hiking destinations including old ranch buildings and an Oak forest. I have seen bears, deer, coyotes, bobcats,  hawks, and many other birds here.

This is the kind of place to go if you want to escape the crowds. I really liked this place for that reason. I only seen one other person on the trail at midday! There are two trails to take in order to get into the park. The first one is by the parking lot and the other one is up the road. You can take either one because its a loop trail. If you do take the one by the parking lot the trail is narrow and there's not much to see at first until you've gone maybe two miles, if that. After that it opens up. I seen a lone coyote, squirrels, acorn woodpeckers, and other birds. Be safe and have fun!

name: Firehouse Park
formatted_address: 2646 Whispering Pines Dr, Running Springs, CA 92382, USA

google_reviews.text: it's Running Springs farmers market,  very fresh vegetables,  assorted items to buy, great one of a kind gifts, I believe the last day is November 19th and restart next spring
There is a farmer/artisan fair every Saturday in the summer months. Lots of variety great people. We love going.
An OK small playground offering 4 slides. There is a section for toddlers and one for elementary level kids.
Cute park
I love the cranberry walnut bread. Great for French toast

name: 5th Street Dog Park
formatted_address: Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text: Awesome little dog park. Enclosed gated area for mingling dogos and there humans....or not. Very peaceful even though 5th St. is right there. Golf course views across the street. Trash can with poo bags right at gate of enclosure.

name: Creekside Park
formatted_address: 950 7th Pl, Calimesa, CA 92320, USA

google_reviews.text: Cute tiny park in Calimesa. Has a playground, calisthenics equipment, and a basketball court. (I skate here sometimes). Seen a coyote here the other day too. That was pretty cool.

name: Wildwood Basin Park
formatted_address: 36101 Wildwood Canyon Rd, Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text: If you're looking for a park with grass.. this is not it and you'll probably be disappointed. This is a scenic walking path suitable for people of all ages including dogs and horses with beautiful views of the sunset.

I came to this park thinking there would be water but it’s dry as heck. Ain’t no trees minus the ones in the parking lot and they don’t even give shade. Idk how it’s a park if no greenery. I didn’t like it.

I did see a coyote cross the road into the park. Little homie was skinny as heck. I didn’t walk it or anything because I had just come back from the state park down the road. Now THAT was a heckin hike. My legs are actually tired from hiking that one.

Anyways I rate this park 3 out of 7, don’t think I’ll come back unless the basin floods or something. Or if they add trees yo
I love walking my dogs here. Most of the time it's just my dogs and I on the trail.
We like to walk our dogs here. Great trails.
Love sitting and watching the sun set here

name: Amber Hills Park
formatted_address: 3294 Amber Hill Dr, Highland, CA 92346, USA

google_reviews.text: It's on the San Manuel reservation... Is it closed to the public???
I love this park

name: Caroline, Park
formatted_address: 32 W Sunset Dr, Redlands, CA 92373, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to walk around in Nature. Overall no complaints with this quaint park
Beautiful park

name: Oak Glen Preserve
formatted_address: 39611 Oak Glen Rd, Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text: Such a neat experience! More than we expected. We went for the fresh apples, got a nature walk thru giant sequoias, fresh apple pie and cider, and we got to snuggle some bunnies and feed emus! What a great day!

Really neat preserve to visit. They have short and long trails, botanic gardens, ponds, Giant Sequoias, small waterfall (man-made), a boardwalk, and more!! I visited Falling Waters, Sunflower Lane, Hummingbird Hill, Ruth's Butterfly Garden, Conifers of California, Floating Dock on Red-Wing Pond, and the Deciduous Forest.

Great place to pick apples, eat tri-tip, get fresh apple cider but we went for the beautiful hiking trails and a lot of shade and a stream.  The weekends get very, very busy! I was surprised how busy it was today, Friday!

Lovely walk, our doggies enjoyed it so much! You can pick apples in several spots; I didn’t check if you can go with a dog, the price or payment methods. This place is beautiful and quiet. Definitely not our last time here!

Went on a Monday, nothing was really open but we still had to pay $10 to get in for basically the small petting zoo and the playground. I don't remember having to pay to get in previously. Wish the slides had been open as my granddaughter loved it the last time. Probably won't be going back anytime soon.

name: Stanfield Marsh Wildlife and Waterfowl Preserve
formatted_address: 42300 Big Bear Blvd, Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text: Taking a leisurely stroll along the bridge to the lake is very pleasant, with beautiful scenery along the way. It's also suitable for cycling or jogging. There are parking spaces available at the beginning of the bridge. I highly recommend everyone to come here to enjoy the scenery of the lake area.

The bridge gives you a closer up view of the lake. You can see fish flopping in the water and flocks of birds skim across the lake and heron near the shore.  It’s a nice walk to the north side about a two mile plus walk to the discovery center.

The water smells bad though from across the embankment with pipes connecting it with the larger side of the lake. Could be from boats dumping their waste or just runoff from land.

The parking lot is hard to access if you’re coming from the south side. You’ll have to cut across double yellow lines. The parking on the bridge side is limited. It’s also not allowed on the bigger bridge crossing the lake during snow season beginning October 15.

I saw Jackie or Shadow when I went for an early evening walk. You don't always see bald eagles, but there are always interesting birds along the boardwalk. Nice place for an after work walk. Fairly level and paved/boardwalk, so an easy place for a walk.

The observation bridge made the place very nice. When you take photos, the bridge added for drama to the place. The background hills and the marsh and the clouds made it so cozy and nature. There is a good parking place and a good place to relax.
A beautiful and peaceful walk, just keep in mind that the mosquitoes come out at dusk.

name: Pebble Plains
formatted_address: 165 Dixie Lee Ln, Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text: A beautiful hike in the San Bernardino mountains. Definitely worth the visit while in Big Bear but please stay on trails. This is a fragile area and meant to be enjoyed by many. It is not wheel chair friendly, unfortunately. Dogs on leashes are going to have a great time! Bring water cause it gets warm quickly.

The best kept secret in Big Bear!!! This is a special place I take my husky. Please stay on the trail. Marked by pebbles. When there's snow, locals know where the trail is, so it's typically marked by foot prints. Please take care of our precious land.

The Pebble Plain habitat is amazing!  We didn't see another soul hiking in on three different days- crazy isolation in our countries most populous state! Tried to stay on the path and take only photos of this pristine and beautiful land. Posting pics during peak bloom time of four indigenous flowers that don't grow anywhere else in the world.

Needless to say, without the snow, it looks way different. We brought our dog and our kids here for some snow play at the end of our Big Bear trip and it was quite the winter wonderland. The gentleman who owns the gorgeous property that shares the fence with the preserve was quite nice too. When I find some friends willing to do a snow hike with me here, I'm coming back.
This place has numerous trails. Biking, hiking, or just walking your dog. The paths are wide and dirt. The paths traverse through a pine tree forest.

name: Big Bear Discovery Center
formatted_address: 40971 North Shore Drive/Hwy 38, Fawnskin, CA 92333, USA

google_reviews.text: This is such a great stop for everything if you just want to explore. Bonus points if you bring kiddos with you! We spent about 2 hours here and honestly? We had a blast. There’s trails everywhere, the center is full of animals and my daughter was so happy to see the same animals we see everyday here in big bear! We’re definitely coming back; Mainly because my daughter named the big grizzly “GooGoo”. So I guess it’s see ya soon GooGoo!

The center is located on the north side of Rim of the World Byway (Route 38) on the north side of the Big Bear Lake. Hours of operation vary, and they are only open from 0900 – 1600 hours daily and closed on Tuesday and Wednesday.
The center is the lynch pin for a variety of activities and educational programs centering around the local environment and history of the area. Inside you will find interactive and interpretive displays, hands-on activities and daily guided tours (usually two a day). There is a gift shop, information desk, picnic tables, restrooms and conference room inside the main building. Outside you can take in a free guided tour along a short natural trail, which is very easy and informative. There is a amphitheater, which is used to host the Music in the Mountains concert series and an interesting children’s nature discovery zone. There is plenty of parking available including RV parking. Finally, this is a fee-based parking lot, so do not forget to display a valid recreation pass anytime you park your vehicle.
Great place to stop in and explore.  Clean restrooms, gift shop, learning/information areas and trails. Definitely worth your time to visit!
We love it there; there are many shops and restaurants so you can spend the day there peacefully!
Friendly staff gift shop very well organized and lots of nice things you can buy to remember your trip to big bear

name: Children's Forest Trailhead
formatted_address: Keller Peak Rd, Running Springs, CA 92382, USA

google_reviews.text: Easy to find and plenty of parking on asphalt lot.  They do have restrooms as well but they are not always open.  The air feels so refreshing up here.  The views of the rocks and water reservoir are very appealing and great for pictures.   There are other nearby trails too.  Also free camping with no hookups.

I did this Trail on a weekday. The weather was amazing! 70F with sun! Perfect for a mild hike on the mountain!

I went off the main paved road after passing Camp #2 and reached the boulders and decided to climb to the top. For me it was a fun and easy climb to the top and you come to the amazing views all around.

I even took a little nap up top, relaxed and meditated for 10 minutes before heading back down. I was a little lost after coming down from the boulders but at the end I found my way back! There are good signals in the area of the park and that help a lot too.

It’s such a fun place to hike and explore up the mountain. Would go again definitely!

Very difficult to find with navigation!!  The visitor center was at the bottom of the mountain without clear indications of the actual site. We kept climbing and climbing to reach the site 2.8 mi up. The path had next to no shade but was paved asphalt so easy walking and would be possible for strollers or wheelchairs. Very few human encounters. Dogs are permissible with doo-doo clean up bags available.

Make sure to get a CA wilderness pass!!! Easy paths, though, also easy to get sidetracked 😁. There are a number of intersecting and twisting trails, and high bushes so you may want to use something to make sure you're headed where you think you're headed.

This is always worth walking when you are in the area.  I used to go here all the time as a kid.  It was kinda sad to see the disrepair it is in and how overgrown everything was.  Not as accessible as it should be to everyone  because of the disrepair.  The views are still great.

name: Skyline trailhead
formatted_address: Angelus Oaks, CA 92305, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful day for a dirt road.
Well marked trails for hiking and biking.
How can you go wrong spending time here? There’s always an adventure in the forest.
Great place to camp. Big areas, no fire pits but surrounded by nature. Right off the service road but quiet. I recommend.
Nice setting, cool bridge over marsh area. Sometimes lots of traffic but nice area nonetheless

name: Baldwin Lake Ecological Reserve
formatted_address: 44865, 44999 N Shore Dr, Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text: To start, I've lived in big bear for years and had never been. David, our walk guide, was a complete delight. Very knowledgeable, could definitely tell he loves his job. Was very informative and attentive to all of our questions and was happy to share all his knowledge to inquiring minds

Baldwin Lake Ecological Preserve is a beautiful spot to see the many faces of California. There are beautiful trees and open meadows and fields to explore. The road like path was running with water from the snowmelt, so bring boots for muddy gravel if its wet. It was a dog friendly trail so long as you pick up dog waste and keep them on a leash! The hike was an easy to moderate meander through beautiful trees and rocky slopes with panoramic views! Totally worth it. There are restrooms at the trailhead but they were locked on Wednesday mid afternoon when I was there. Not sure if they always are or not. The trailhead has a dirt parking lot and easy road access.

We went on a guided hike and it was really an incredible experience learning about local plants! I highly recommend this hiking area for people with young kids. It's a very easy hike, mostly flat. You can see very rare plants and a very unique part of the mountain!
A very interesting place to saunter around and get a feel for what the ecology of Big Bear was like before the tourists started frequenting the area.
Beautiful place to visit, we enjoyed the peaceful tranquil atmosphere, we plan on returning soon, and hope it'll be open as winter approaches.

name: Grout Bay Recreation Area
formatted_address: 39028 N Shore Dr, Fawnskin, CA 92333, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice view. Very short walk to the water but steep.
We took a drive around the lake and ended up at this point just past Fawnskin and it's just beautiful. There was still some snow and kids were able to slide down small slopes near road.
Winter Wounder Land....Beautifully Covered Inthe SNOW... ❄ ❄ ❄ Must Have Chains

Grout Bay in Fawnskin on the other side of Big Bear Lake is a beautiful little place to take in if you're just beginning to take in nature. My wife and I cautiously made our way down near to muddy marsh and discovered some animal paw prints in the mud. Could have been a dog or coyote. Also stumbled upon what looked like deer/fawn hoof prints. Glad we were able to see these before the snow comes.
Very peaceful.  Huge temperature difference between the picnic area and the lake's shoreline.  Bring a sweater.  The area has bathrooms available, too.

name: Bearpaw Reserve
formatted_address: 38801 Valley of the Falls Dr, Forest Falls, CA 92339, USA

google_reviews.text:
Stayed here with a high school camping club.
It is available to groups if you reserve ahead of time, not individuals.
The best campground experience ever. Got our own grassy area to ourselves, flush bathrooms, large fire pit area, spigot for dishes, a 10 min walk to a waterfall and an endless dirt road to walk for a long hike.
The views from the campground are spectacular. Plan to return with our group next school year

I have been to the other reserves associated with wild lands and have had no trouble with access. I didn’t realize this was by appointment only and therefore did not proceed after the sign directly off the main road saying it’s private property.
Fortunately it was just one stop of many on my weekend trip so i just sat by the water for a while and then continued on my way.
A wild place. Gem of a nature Preserve operated by The Wildlands Conservancy. Check their website for reservations and access. 🌲
Nice place to take the family

name: King Clone Ecological Reserve
formatted_address: San Bernardino County, CA 92356, USA

google_reviews.text: Definitely visiting this when I’m headed that way. SO OLD !!!
Amazing something can live so long!
Cool area and really interesting to see the clones

name: Stanfield Marsh Wildlife Boardwalk Park
formatted_address: Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice little walk after a nice weekend spent in big bear. I’d say the boardwalk is about 1/4 mile but then you can walk next to the lake and enjoy the scenery. Parking can be a little tough depending on the time of day.

Stanfield Marsh Boardwalk is one of Big Bear’s most peaceful spots to start the day. 🌅

It’s the perfect place to walk and catch the sunrise, with panoramic views of the lake and mountains reflected in the still morning water.

The wooden boardwalk is easy to stroll, surrounded by birds, reeds, and crisp mountain air.

Whether you’re out for a quiet moment, taking photos, or just enjoying nature before the crowds arrive, this spot never disappoints. A true hidden gem for sunrise lovers in Big Bear.

The long dock is so beautiful to walk. There are plenty of trash cans available but there's always some jerk who doesn't seem to care about the beautiful world they're living in. There are some benches to sit on with various views. The highway being right next to it though kind of kills the vibes but it's still a beautiful place. The bathrooms were very clean also!

This was a 2 mile walk up and back.  Not at all strenuous.  We walked during sunset and it was beautiful!  Be warned it's when the mosquitoes are out and there are swarms everywhere but we were able to avoid most of them just not in certain areas.

This is one of my favorite places to get my steps in, get some sun, a great view, and it’s a nice time and place to spend with family. Normally my family and I go on this walk every day but during the winter it depends on the temperature and whether it’s been snowing. I love that they’ve been doing construction and have added more parking (available April 2024), more benches to sit and enjoy the area, and they’ve also paved the path all the way down the the end. The views are amazing and I definitely recommend.

name: Juniper Point Picnic Area
formatted_address: 41365 N Shore Dr, Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text: What a fabulously scenic spot! Jupiter point Park was not too busy when we came before all the snow fell. Beautiful areas with picnic tables and outdoor grills although I’m not sure what times a year you’re actually allowed to spark them up and cook your food due to the fire dangers. A lot of people on the banks fishing with their families. Lots of pretty mallard ducks floating around honking their beaks adorably. Beautiful stunning views of snow, Summit and Bear Mountain and the lake! Beautiful spot to just pull up a chair and enjoy nature. Bathrooms were clean and everybody seemed to be respectful and didn’t leave trash everywhere. Beautiful, beautiful spot! I’m not quite sure anyone caught any fish though. I didn’t see anything on their lines.
Just passed by and greeted some ‘fishers’.. beautiful place and very ample parking if necessary, also great for a good hike! Can see Snow Summit at a distance with some snow on the slopes.

This is another rather large picnic area that can be found on the north side of Big Bear Lake. The area is accessible off the Rim of the World Scenic Byway (Route 38) with numerous picnic tables and BBQ grills, all within a lakeside setting surrounded by natural features – we especially like the tall pine trees. There is also a rocky path leading down to the lake/beach area and a paved walking, running or biking trail, which is part of the North Shore Biking and Walking Trail. There are additional trails and boardwalks extending into a marsh, providing opportunities for nature walks. Finally, there is a single-stall vault toilet, one porta-potty and one recycling bin in the parking lot.

Corn dog on Juniper Point. We had our "adventure pass" and we're able to find a spot for wheelchair ♿️ even though there's only one spot. Wheeled down to picnic tables avoiding large cracks in parking areas and path " Be careful " and enjoyed the Lake  and meadow views.

Parking is easy - never full, but you will want to ensure you have an adventure pass or annual pass. The trails here are easy and absolutely scenic surrounding the lake, you can see snow summit. I highly recommend showing up in time to entire a sunset view. There are plenty of tables to picnic at by the lake, but it's best to bring a tarp and blanket to enjoy being right of the water with the best view in Big Bear lake.

name: Thurman Flats Picnic Area
formatted_address: 9025 CA-38, Mentone, CA 92359, USA

google_reviews.text: Awesome hidden treasure! I just wish people visiting here would clean up after themselves! Respect nature respect the environment! Would like to see the gates remain open! I fixed a few steps leading down to creek beginning of last year. If your bring a cooler down those please carry them instead of dragging them down! Its a beautiful place keep it that way!!!

Thurman Flats has history and beauty. It is well maintained. The pavilion is a wonderful place for a gathering. It is first come to reserve. If the pavilion is taken, there are additional levels above to gather. The barbecue- charcoal cooking structures are some of this best engineered that I have seen. They are very safe and well created.

The hike to Mill Creek is not too long but would be difficult for anyone with balancing problems. The Creek was very strong when we visited so I would be very careful with children or pets.

The is oddly no water available so bring water. The restrooms are very clean, but although there are solar panels, there are no lights. Look out for snakes.

Be sure to have your National Park parking pass displayed for it is patrolled and you will get a ticket.

There is a bear safe trash dumpster. Please keep it clean for it is a beautiful and rugged place. Do not leave anything that would attract bears. Be alert bears abound in the San Bernardino National Forest.

Thurman Flats Picnic Area is open during daylight hours.
Nice area besides the litter and tagging. There is a nearby smaller stream with the ability to catch fish with your hands sometimes. And the berry's are delightful!

Usually my family and I go to another creek near Fontana.. we decided to expand our adventure and travel a little further out and to our surprise it was well worth it.  It was so beautiful and calming and fun.  The hike was awesome to the water. It's best if you wear shoes in the water to keep the rocks from cutting your feet. The picnic area was shaded and they also had bbq pits. A very lovely place
We loved everything about this place except the bathrooms, they were filthy. The covered concrete picnic tables were perfect for our Mother's Day brunch and the short hike to the river was wonderful

name: Meadows Edge Picnic Area
formatted_address: 40751 N Shore Ln, Fawnskin, CA 92333, USA

google_reviews.text: This is one of the largest picnic areas you will find within the Sand Bernadino National Forest near Big Bear Lake. Take Route 38 to North Shore Lane and follow the signs to the area. Like many picnic sites around the lake, they are open daily from 0800 to 1800 hours and this is a fee-based location so make sure when you park your vehicle that you display a valid recreation pass. The picnic area offers a wide variety of options, and there are plenty of grills and tables with adjacent parking spaces and several different walking trails that connect to the Pacific Crest Trail. You might also want to check the informative display board for additional background on this area. The only major complaint we have about the area is the lack of restroom facilities. Currently, the former restrooms are completely sealed off and we were only able to find two porta-potties in the area, which as we all know are not the best and these two fit the bill. Still, what we like about this location is the size and being away from the sounds of Route 38, and you just gotta love the smell of all those pines trees.

Beautiful surroundings and plenty of nature to enjoy. Walking trails and bike paths as well as picnic tables and lakeside views. You can get the adventure pass you need to park here at the discovery center just down the road. Highly recommended!!
quiet, nice view,near the lake. good for picnic
Really nice and calm place. Been there during summer, had a walk on the lake then took the Cougar Crest Trail which is pretty close and have an awesome view to the whole lake.

Fun day use area. Plenty is shaded tables. Nice trails & paved bike lane.
Restrooms are out of service but there's cleanish out houses to use.

name: Wildhaven
formatted_address: 29453 Pineridge Dr, Cedar Glen, CA 92321, USA

google_reviews.text: We visited Wildhaven Ranch this afternoon. What an amazing experience. An absolutely charming director. Knowledgeable staff. Spectacular ambassador animals, including bobcat, golden eagle, and of course the bears. And they do the good work of rescuing injured native species and releasing them back into the wild. We definitely recommend a visit. We will be back.

Wildhaven is a sanctuary for hurt or abandoned wild animals, where they are either rehabilitated and released back into the wild, or if that isn't possible, kept and cared for. It's obvious the staff (paid and volunteers) care deeply for the animals. They give tours by reservation, and the staff is very knowledgeable. I highly recommend a visit!

Loved the experience, they had the viewing of the animals set up nicely.
It was very interesting. Loved the howling coyotes and how we have a better understanding of them now. We live in coyote country and have always hated the howling now that we understand it better it won't be so bad. I would definitely visit again.

Such a cool experience! The animals were amazing and the tour hosts were super knowledge. Such a great cause for animals who would've died or been abandoned! We did the extra bear feeding and it was an awesome end to the tour.
Great place to take the kids to learn about bears.  and eagles. Followed CDC for social distancing during our tour.

name: Serrano Campground
formatted_address: 40650 N Shore Dr, Fawnskin, CA 92333, USA

google_reviews.text: Serrano is an exceptional campground in one of the most idyllic areas in California, America, the world. Consider yourself extremely lucky if you can snag #114 or any in that locale. I've camped there three times now and I am anxious to return.

I had the BEST time at Serrano Campground. The site is located on the north shore (10 min drive from Big Bear). Cougar Crest Trail is in walking distance from the campgrounds (highly recommend if you are looking for a nice hike), and there is a walking/biking path that takes you all the way to the lake.
The campgrounds were clean and in great shape. I was shocked with with how clean the restrooms and showers were. A small win is that the showers are free to use so you don't need to lug around a heavy coin purse.

Fire wood is available for sale on the grounds if needed and there are park rangers that monitor the entrances which made me feel safe. There are pumps around the sites with water that can be used for drinking or to wash your dishes. The campground is at a distance where it feels like you are out of the city, but there is still cell reception. I will definitely be coming back.

This is a really nice campground. The people are friendly and the place is clean and well maintained. They have free hot showers and they even sell wood at the camp entrance which is really convenient. The campground is very peaceful. Some large groups were a little loud and allowed to have fun without being bothered. It was a very chill atmosphere which is what you want when camping.

Beautiful campground. Spacious and very clean, great toilets and hot showers. There is a gorgeous bike path that leads to the lake, campground was very busy  but everyone was quiet and respectful. I gave it 4 stars because we saw mice running around our tent few times and it’s the only thing that might stop me from returning unfortunately:(

CONS:
Showers were not so great.
I had some unreasonably loud neighbors.
Unattended kids and families were continuously walking through my site.
PROS:
Plenty of spots to hang my hammock
Great cell service to get work done
Close to town and easy bikeway access

name: Barton Flats Campground
formatted_address: CA-38, Angelus Oaks, CA 92305, USA

google_reviews.text:
We have been coming to Barton Flats Campground for years. We specifically go here because the camping spaces are spread apart, its easy to bring our trailer.

The campground is usually booked, plan your trip in advance.

They have flush toilets & showers. Those rooms are clean enough for a campground. Every space we have camped at has a fire ring & a sturdy table w/ benches.

Great place to ride your bikes around the campground. We always take our dogs.

The hosts expect you to follow the rules, sometimes people don't. Don't be a jerk & respect others around you.

We like the camping spaces away from the highway, the spaces near the highway can be a little noisy from traffic.

Lots of shade, birds & small wildlife to watch. There seem to be enough restrooms distributed around the campground. You can buy wood from the hosts, you can pay a moderate fee to dump your RV adjacent to the parking lot near the entrance.

The campground closes during the winter.

Campground Review – Site #37 (Early June Stay)

The drive to the campground was absolutely beautiful and surprisingly accessible—any vehicle can make the trip without the need for four-wheel drive, which was a relief. The campground itself is very well-maintained, and the campsites are clean and thoughtfully arranged. I stayed at site #37, which I found to be ideal in terms of layout and location.

One of the standout features of this site was the generous spacing between campsites. We enjoyed a sense of privacy, with a large open field behind us and minimal road noise at night, creating a peaceful and quiet environment.

The camp hosts were friendly, professional, and attentive to the site’s upkeep and security. Bob, in particular, was welcoming and helpful throughout our stay.

Cleanliness was top-notch—I would rate it a 5/5. The restrooms were equipped with flush toilets and sinks, and new hot water heaters had just been installed for the showers. Our site was conveniently located near the restrooms and water access. It was level, offered a comfortable amount of shade, and was easy to set up camp on.

In terms of pests, I’d give it a 2/5 (5 being terrible amount of bugs). There were a few mosquitoes, but they weren’t much of a bother. We did have a couple of aggressive scrub jays in the area, likely due to a nearby nest, which might not be a common issue for all campers.

Overall, we had a wonderful experience and would highly recommend this campground to others looking for a clean, quiet, and well-managed place to enjoy the outdoors

This is a really cute camp site nestled in the pine forest. Clean bathrooms and showers and very friendly camp hosts.

I’m giving 3 stars because they need to have a mechanism for kicking out disruptive parties. The hosts hand out the rules but don’t enforce them. There was a double site close to us that disturbed 5-10 sites simultaneously. They partied all day and blared loud music, despite the rules that disallow music. They stayed up all night literally screaming at each other until 2am both nights we were there. They even had a projector showing a boxing match one night.  How is that allowed? The hosts went to talk to them multiple times but the guests were drunk and didn’t care. They should have called a ranger and kicked them out. They completely ruined our trip. I had to deal with a cranky 2 and 4 year old that didn’t sleep all weekend.  Totally awful people who should not have been allowed to stay.

Ideally located 90 min from LA, this is a breezy place in the mountains. Sites provide ample space to the neighbors. Facilities are clean and functional.
The fee policy is a bit odd: on top of steep $ 36 for a dry site,  RV dump is 10 $ even for tenants (no workaround), reservation is 8 $ (workaround available).
Barton Flats is a great place to camp. The sites are clean and well maintained with the river running for those who want to fish or just get wet.

name: Dogwood Family Campground
formatted_address: 26898 Rim of the World Hwy, Lake Arrowhead, CA 92378, USA

google_reviews.text:
One of our favorite campsites in SoCal! Beautiful and clean campground! Bathrooms are super clean and well maintained. Showers are free with hot water. Potable water stations are spread out through the campground.

The campsite hosts are friendly and helpful. The fire pits have a built in grill on them. Our campsite that I chose was huge with room for 3, 10 person tents maybe. I'm not going to divulge which one it is, but I'll say it's in the 80+ as a hint. There are trails nearby that you can explore. Firewood is sold near the entrance.

My only complaint is that some campers are very inconsiderate, especially during quiet hours which is 10PM - 6AM. Not a deal breaker but could get really annoying if you're a light sleeper. Overall, we enjoyed this campground so much that we plan on coming back! Maybe with more family members in tow.

Beautiful place, perfect for children to play and explore. It's much colder than the surrounding area, so remember to bring extra clothes. Most campsites have electricity, but no water. There are public water points, so you can bring some water containers. Book four to five months in advance to get the spot you want.

Dogwood camping is pretty cool. Lot of trees and the smell of the fire wood burning and seeing the stars above at night, just chilling with your friends & having some beers and some barbecue is a must for everyone! The best part is.... no one bothers you & everybody keeps quiet around 10PM.
The restrooms and the showers are pretty clean as well.

Loved our stay at Dogwood Campground!
We stayed in site#12. When we made the reservation it was noted that it could be difficult to back into, and indeed if our 21ft trailer had been any longer, it may not have worked, but we were prepared, and we appreciated the advanced warning. But after a few tries we got it, and we were extremely pleased with the results!
Our campsite upon our arrival was spotless, and had clearly been raked and tidied for us.
The bathrooms were nearby, but not right next to. Restrooms were clean, not stinky at all, flush toilets and even soap, which is not always provided, but very much appreciated!
The campground itself was quiet and peaceful.
Fire rings were in good working condition, and water spigot was outside the nearby restroom.
Arrowhead village was a short drive away.
We definitely recommend this campground!😃

Our favorite campground with our little teardrop trailer. Very large campground with varied sites- tent to RV with electricity. Surrounded by cedars and plenty of shade. Grounds well maintained and staff easy-going and friendly. Jennifer at the front booth was very helpful. Lots of trails in park and nearby. We’ll be back multiple times. Only 2 hours from San Diego.

name: Crab Flats Campground
formatted_address: Crab Flats Rd, Running Springs, CA 92382, USA

google_reviews.text:
Small campground, but surprisingly spacious. It's tucked a bit farther back into the mountains, which is honestly part of the fun. You don’t need a 4x4 vehicle, but high clearance is definitely recommended. I wouldn’t bring a Prius, Camry, Civic, or anything low to the ground, there are a few rough spots where you could easily get stuck or damage your car.
It’s far removed from the hustle of everyday life, quiet, peaceful, and surrounded by nature. Lots of shade and plenty of space between sites. Vault toilets are available and decent, depending on who used them last. There’s no water nearby, so be sure to bring plenty. Also, there’s no trash service, so pack out what you pack in.
As expected, no showers. (It’s camping 😁)
The area is dog-friendly and generally quiet, except for one issue (more on that below). We did have some wasps show up every time we were cooking or had water out, but they didn’t sting or cause any problems. Just something to be aware of.
Lots of birds and wildlife around, and when it's quiet, the sound of the wind moving through the trees is absolutely beautiful.
There aren’t many hiking trails directly at the campground, which might be a downside for some. However, Green Valley Lake is not far away and makes for a great trip to and from the campsite. There’s swimming, fishing, they have boat rentals, or you can just relax by the water. If you do swim or fish, theirs is a small fee since the lake is privately owned. Cool owners.There are also a couple of mellow, local restaurants nearby if you want a break from cooking. Very chill-mellow vibe type of place.
The Downside: Off-Road Vehicles
Unfortunately, the campground sits right next to an off-highway vehicle trail (OHV)So, depending on the crowd, you will hear engines and loud music, especially during weekends or holidays.
During our stay, there was a large group (20+ people) with several off-road carts and quads. They started early, around 7 a.m. blasting music and running engines throughout the day. At night, they were doing donuts near the entrance, kicking up dust that blew directly into campsites 1 and 2 (where we stayed). A couple of riders even kept on driving through out the campsite and in between the tent spots without any regards for others.
My neighbor eventually asked them to slow down near the sites, and they did, for a bit. But it didn’t last. They also ignored the numerous "No Campfires" and "Fire Danger High" signs and still started a fire, showing little concern for safety or other campers.

Despite the off-road noise and that one inconsiderate group, we still had a good time. Just be aware of the setting and know what to expect.Bring your own water, Pack out your trash, no fires unless explicitly allowed, be prepared for potential dust/noise from off-road vehicles, enjoy the nature, peace, and shade when it's quiet.

My son and I stayed here the last week in June. The weather was mid yo low 80s. I stayed at campsite 1 and there was only one other group there on the entire campsite. The campsite does require a high clearance vehicle to get there but most stock SUVs should be fine.

My son and I tent camped for two days. The site was big and had plenty of shade. The bathroom was a very short walk from our site and decently maintained. The site included water and a fire pit, but due to fires in the area/ weather they were both restricted.

My son and I hiked to the top of a tall rock in the site and watched the sunset which only took maybe 15/20 minutes. There are several trails located close by to drive on and explore. We also used the private lake about 20 minutes from camp.

For about $40 for me and my son, we rented a row boat and fished for an hour. The $40 allowed us to also fish from the shore. Unfortunately didn't catch anything but it was a good way to relax and enjoy lunch while our lines were in the water.

Overall I think the site was nice. I prefer less people om the site when camping so this was perfect. I think the time of the season and the dirt road to the site helped limit the traffic.
It’s definitely outdoor living. Toilet only. And the road to get there is very rough. Like you shouldn’t go if you have a Prius. But we had a great time.

Super fun offroad drive getting there, the girls loved it. Open yet plenty of shade, small hikes nearby, boulders to explore, will visit again. Make sure you bring plenty of water and wood as the water is very limited and also no one on site to sell wood. Very spacious between campsites.

Nice campground, just be aware of the rugged trail over to it. My 1998 ford ranger was able to get through it, but make sure you keep an eye on the gps. I got very lost trying to get to the campsite and my gps kept taking me down the trail for some reason. The site is good, just make sure you bring plenty of water and firewood. There are a few trails and dirt biking trails around the campsite too. There is a nice river and small waterfall area down the hill (southwest of the campgrounds). Lots of room between campsites and there are lots of trees that offer shade. Each site comes with a firepit, table, and a locker to keep camping equipment in. Great campsite for atvs and hiking.

name: North Shore Campground
formatted_address: 29102 Hospital Rd, San Bernardino, CA 92402, USA

google_reviews.text: We had our yearly camping trip with family, we went for north shore this time. We had amazing time, our camp host Mike and his wife Mary Ann were so welcoming and friendly. I saw that they talk to all their  camping families. The restrooms were always clean. They would always drive around and check on all the guests. I’ll define visiting here again!

Nice campground close to Lake Arrowhead. We actually just cruised through the camp to check it out. Briefly talk to the camp hosts, very nice. Campsites are spacious and have table and fire ring. A lot of spots have plenty shade. We went during the week and had plenty spaces available. We'll def use this camp on our next trip to Lake Arrowhead.
Beautiful place to camp, just the only downfall is there are no showers. Small campground very peaceful. Been coming here for over 16 years.

The camp hosts were lovely! Their kindness turned an already fantastic stay into something even more special. We had campsite 13 and I would definitely  recommend this one if you have a larger vehicle. We have a campervan with an awning and site 13 has the widest parking space and is the most level. We stayed 2 nights in the middle of the week and it was very quiet the entire time, although we had neighbors we couldn't even hear them. Overall a wonderful and very peaceful campground.

Well-maintained. Beautiful views. I'm late September I had no bug problems whatsoever and it was cool at night (upper 40s). I also saw a coyote about 100' away while I was making breakfast. For me, seeing wildlife like this is a plus, but it is definitely important to keep a clean campsite and keep your food secured while not in use.

name: Green Valley Campground
formatted_address: 33800 Green Valley Lake Rd, Green Valley Lake, CA 92341, USA

google_reviews.text:
A very quiet and peaceful campground. Occasionally, you can catch the pleasant scent of nearby plants in the air. The restrooms are among the cleanest I’ve seen at any campsite.
We stayed at Site 5, which had a large space and was conveniently close to the restrooms. The site could probably fit two or three tents, but there’s likely only enough room to park one car.
There are no showers in the campground, and Green Valley Lake is quite a distance away. It would take a while to walk there, so driving to the lake is a much better option.

I had an amazing weekend at Green Valley Campground with friends and family, and I highly recommend it! The campground is spacious, clean, and well-equipped with firepits, full working toilets, and fresh water. It’s perfect for kids to play safely, with campsites 4 and 5 offering great shade and seclusion. The nearby lake is ideal for fishing and paddle boating. The host, Donna, was incredibly kind and accommodating, checking on us regularly. When my wife accidentally left her purse behind, Donna went above and beyond to ship it back to us. This place is a gem for a peaceful, family-friendly camping experience!

2025 update: Still awesome.

Tucked away between Big Bear and Arrowhead is the nice, quiet campground at Green Valley Lake. I've been twice now and found the camp hosts to be kind and welcoming, the bathrooms to be excellently maintained, and the setting pretty perfect.

Most sites are sized well enough for a few tents, however there are a few with uneven, sloped terrain that might make a tent difficult: so van campers and RTT-users can claim those. Be sure to look at the available sites on something like campsitephotos, in addition to the ones on the reservation site, in order to gauge whether a site is good for your group.

Some sites are tucked away and offer extreme privacy, and some ... well, don't. Some are expansive but lack a lot of level ground, most have at least some tree cover. The family camp site (or double site) is all +++. Tucked away, expansive, level. You even get a little bridge and babbling brook.

There is a short 3ish mile hike available that starts above the campsites and loops around and ends nearer the town that has great views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. And a longer hike available as well.

This is the highest spot to camp in Southern CA, at an elevation of 7,200 feet.

The campground atmosphere is clean, serene, and rustic.

The sites are level, maintained, and uncrowded. A few are more secluded, and would be good for birding.

Roads inside the campground need maintenance, but are passable. Some turns looked challenging for  larger RVs. No hook-ups.  Restrooms available. A Camp Host was on-site.

The four season weather at this elevation is generally dry.  It rains approx. ten days annually.

It's a short drive to the tiny resort village.  There's a  private, non-motorized lake (pay for access), General Store, The Malt Shop, The Black Dog Social Club, museum, fishing, watercraft rentals, playground, swim beach, and the Fire Department.

From Hwy. 18, the campground is at the end of the only paved road leading into Green Valley Lake.

Enjoy the five mile scenic route of wooded, winding road. It slowly takes you back in time to discover the unspoiled nature and simple pleasures of camping in the San Bernardino Mountains.

Absolutely loved this campground! Just spent last weekend there. Our campsites were great. As a group of 7 we easily split 2 sites next to each other. But I will say it is hard to tell from the website how big each is. So if you have an opportunity scope out the different sites I highly recommend doing that. The bathrooms were kept very clean and well lit. The camp hosts are super nice and do an amazing job keeping an eye on things and keeping the sites tidy and ready for guests. Each site seems to be equipped with a fire pit and a bear box - which you definitely should use! We saw a bear while we were there. I would highly recommend this as a great place that feels far away and quiet but is honestly not too far of a drive from most of Southern California!

name: San Gorgonio Family Campground
formatted_address: 43064 CA-38, Angelus Oaks, CA 92305, USA

google_reviews.text:
We’ve been camping here for three nights and, for the most part, the campground itself is very peaceful and scenic. However, our experience was soured by the park ranger’s behavior. As the photos show, there is absolutely no one around our site — no other campers in sight — yet we were approached and told, quite rudely, that amplified music is not allowed.

If this is truly a rule, it would have been reasonable to inform us from the start of our stay, not suddenly on our third night after no one had said a word. The way it was handled felt unnecessary and unprofessional. A polite reminder would have been more than enough, especially since we clearly weren’t disturbing anyone.

It’s unfortunate, because the campground itself is beautiful and relaxing, but the ranger’s attitude left a very negative impression and definitely put a damper on our trip.
However, the showers were clean. The bathrooms were clean and we had great hot water.

One toilet of two was non-functional on a big summer weekend. This is outrageous!

There were large family groups. The policies of the campground were generally respected by all. And, when quiet time began, everyone seemed to respect it. Amazing.
Nice campground that is well maintained and most sites are reasonably private.  However, it has a significant amount of road noise, particularly early in the morning.

Great family campground with lots of double sites with room (more room then Barton).  Nice hosts, water, warm showers, hiking trails but close to 38 (you can hear it).  They sell fire wood.  Nearest convenience store is 15 minus away. I was here during the week and it was fairly quiet.

It was an amazing experience for us right from arrival... The forest service supervisor made us feel right at home and he and his family would drive and ask if we had any questions and it is how we found a lake a short drive from there ... Not for swimming but for kayaking and fishing.. Heading back this summer as well and highly recommended. Clean restrooms and surroundings. Bring a camera as there are plenty to see and photograph. Be a friendly neighbor and say hello to you fellow campers as it adds to the experience! Enjoy!
Ponce family.

name: Holcomb Valley Campground
formatted_address: Holcomb Valley Rd, Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text:
This campsite has beautiful views and horses! The service however was a downer on this whole experience. The camp host “Ed”, who introduced himself as the owner, drove into our campsite at a high speed with our small children running around the campsite. He walked up to my family and immediately started recording us with his phone, including our children, stating that there are no camp fires allowed and that he will be uploading this video to his “website” as proof of the fire and that we are being fined $500. He stated that this information was included in the confirmation email - which we did not remember reading in that email. We respectfully accepted and apologized and began pouring gallons of water to put the fire out. As we were doing this, Ed then begins accusing us of not having all of our guests registered on our reservation. We confirmed that we did in fact register everyone and had proof. He reminded us again about the fine that we will receive and then finally left. We felt extremely unwelcome and uncomfortable. We went to sleep early, woke up early, packed our things and left before 9am the following day.

Once I had service back on my phone, I rechecked our email confirmation. The email does not state anything about no fires being allowed. The email includes a link to a 15 page information booklet about the campsite. I opened that up and read through all 15 pages- NOWHERE does it say that fires are not allowed. There is even a “camp fire safety” section that states that you need to extinguish your campfire before going to bed. Additionally, the booklet states that you are able to add firewood to your campsite reservation and they will drop it off to your site. We also noted that there were not any signs anywhere in the campsite OR at the entrance that state that fires are not allowed. We are frequent and competent campers and would not have started a fire if it was not permitted.

I called the camp host Ed and asked him if he could remind me where I could find the information about no camp fires being allowed. He responded “I talked to the owners and they said you weren’t aware of the rules so we won’t fine you”. He continued to be rude and dismissive about it.

We will be back if and when a new camp host is hired. The views were 10/10 and our kids loved seeing the horses.

Camp host was incredibly helpful and friendly. Helped explain how the campsite worked and how to pay when I pulled up. Everything is well kept and is in a wonderful area with a lot of trails for dirtbikes or 4x4. 10/10

Save your money. This place doesn’t deliver what it promises!
We paid 202$ for a two-night stay at Holcomb Campground, and unfortunately, it was absolutely not worth it.

The hot showers didn’t work at all — only freezing cold water was available. We arrived around 7pm when the evening was already chilly, and the lack of functioning facilities made it impossible to freshen up. The restrooms were closed, with only a portable toilet available, which felt far below acceptable standards for the price.

When I contacted the host the next day, I got a dismissive response. He showed zero investment in resolving the problem or acknowledging the inconvenience.

Ironically, as we drove through the area, we saw many people camping for free — and they seemed just as well off, if not better. Next time, we’ll be doing the same.
Awesome campground 🙌 it is a dirt road to get to it but can be done easily with a car just go slow but we'll worth the 4 mile trip to get there.

We stopped here with my disabled wife.  Calvin is the campground host and he maintains the campground.  He is the friendliest and most efficient and most conscientious host we have ever met.  He deserves a raise.  :).

name: Hanna Flat Campground
formatted_address: 38198 Rim of the World Dr, Fawnskin, CA 92333, USA

google_reviews.text:
Hanna Flats is a great campground for those looking to really get back to nature. Beautiful area! ✨️ Clean campground. Really great sites. Ours had a huge, beautiful, oak tree! Prime spot. Lots of outdoor enthusiasts like mountain bikers, motorcyclist, and 4x4 clubs have trails to explore outside of camp.  Located in Fawnskin and very close to Big Bear lake. So much to do!
Just be aware, there are no bathrooms as you know it. No flush toilets or sinks. Only Vault toilets and spigots outside. This is true camping!  🏕.

We go every summer as a family. Sometimes, twice. We were just there this past weekend and weren't disappointed. Never are.
We stay in the same spot every time. As you can see, there's plenty to do. Especially off-roading. The color of the truck is gray in case you're wondering. There's also open shooting in designated areas so long as there's no heat restrictions in place.

Simply amazing place! We absolutely adore here. It was about 20 minute north west of big bear so not lot of people coming by, which for me was a huge plus.

They have this amazingly beautiful trail. One was 7 miles long, which we didn’t go lol. The other was 2.5 miles long, a perfect distance for your little kids!

It is very well kept and clean overall. They have this metal bin on every site that you can safely store food without animals trying to get your food.

They do have a lot of bird. I mean A LOT of birds. If you leave any snacks outside accidentally, they are gone 100%.

WILL COME BACK HERE.

Perfect spot for tent camping! Highly recommend this campground if you’re looking for a place that feels like you secluded but is still within 20 minutes driving distance of creature comforts in town. The surroundings are beautiful, the camp host was very helpful, and each reserved camping site is in close proximity of restrooms and potable water. The 2 mile dirt road off the main road is part of the charm!

We just stayed for Friday and Saturday night and had a great time!  Great campground!  The camp host made his rounds and was available, but wasn't intrusive.  The bathrooms smelled good on Friday, but were definitely smellier by Saturday night, but still clean.  No flushing toilets, as noted on the rec.gov website.  No sinks or mirrors, but a water faucet outside the bathrooms.  No showers, but we brought a basic one and it did the trick. The map makes it look like there is more space between sites than there is, however, our spot felt nice and roomy, spot 87.  There was enough space between neighboring sites though that it did not feel crowded.
Wood was available to buy for camp fires, we brought our own though, but some had been left behind by someone else and we left behind what we had left over.  The bear box easily held our medium cooler and other food items and dishes.  We did not see any bears though.
It felt like most of the people who camped there were there "to camp", not party, so overall, it was very quiet at night.
The dirt road to the camp ground is pretty long, probably about 5 miles.  Most of it is maintained, but there is a couple of miles that is not maintained and is very rough, especially if in a car.
We drove through the Serrano camp ground, near the lake and it also looked very nice and roomy.  Definitely a more convenient location to the town of Big Bear.  They had full bathrooms with stalls, sinks and soap and separate, individual shower rooms.  Easy push button showers, clean, but the water didn't get very warm.  I might try Serrano next time, but Hanna Flat was still a great place to tent camp!

name: Oak Springs Campground
formatted_address: 25656 Oak Springs Rd, Apple Valley, CA 92308, USA

google_reviews.text: I thoroughly enjoyed my time at this campground. Excellent, low key venue for my favorite, non-stop sounds. Thankful to have this place! ♡♡♡♡♡ Please be respectful and responsible so we can keep this campground open and thriving. Oh. Try not to be the Outbreak Monkey. We hate closures due to the virus. Stay healthy and have fun.❤️🙌
Great place.  A bit of a climb in the vehicle but worth the view and company.
The views from there are unparalleled in the High Desert, very friendly atmosphere.
Passed through road fire roads ohv green sticker tags only
This place is something special cant wait to go back

name: Yucaipa Regional Park Tent Campground
formatted_address: 34240 Oak Glen Rd, Yucaipa, CA 92399, USA

google_reviews.text:
For our annual family camping weekend ( 30 of us including uncles, cousins, brothers, nephews) we usually go to La Jolla Indian Reservation Campground ( By Palomar Mountain, San Diego County) but this year we wanted to try a different place. We tried this park and it was o.k.
The following is included in each camping site:
- Large camping sites ( size of a soccer field) for up to up to 30 people with a large fire pit.
- Large park shelter with 8 park tables, 2 extra large charcoal grills and a water spigot to wash dishes or hook up a hose.
- Bathroom and shower  buildings near by are very clean with cold and hot water. Bathrooms are on one side of the building and showers on the other.
- camping sites are separated by trees so you have some privacy.
-Each site has its own parking spaces.
- The only complaint is that you can't set up the tents under the trees and grass. Tents have to be in the center of the camping site which is pure dirt and there is no shade. So the sun hits the tents dead on.
The activities are plenty. fishing, walking trails, small fresh water river you can go in, and paddle boats for the lake. The big attraction however is the beach pool. One end of the lake was converted in to a beach pool with white sand and two large water slides. However you have to pay $12 to get in and no alcohol. Kids under 4 are free. In and out privileges. The pool runs from 10am to 4pm. Closed on Mondays. There is a big play ground area for kids outside the beach pool.
If you are camping check in is at 3pm and check out is at 11: 30 the next day. Park opens at 9am and closes at 5pm. If you are camping and get there after 4:30pm you are given an entry code to get in. There are in and out privileges so that is a plus. And if you need anything a five minute drive into town will take care of all you needs. We stayed there 3 nights and paid a total $320 for our group of 30. We plan to go back.

Some campsites need updates  was 100 degrees  and summer swim season wasn't over and pool wasn't open only sat and sun even though campground was full old benches where infested with black widows at night, 1 tarantula, 4 scorpions and they don't allow you to  put tents outside of circle  where the shade is we did have fun at pool for 6 hours that's how long its open,good fishing after stock day Thursday,good place for day use not for tent camping, also new shower bathrooms but never opened had to use old one infested with black widows

This campground is really nice!!! I got to get some good pictures of the wildlife there. Easy to find your campground, and there is enough space. Just lookout for the little spiders around the bathroom🕷.

I found this park through a friend that fishes, and thought I would give af try and take the trailer out for a couple days. Very nice and very spacious rv sites, which have full hookup. We walked all over the park with our dog and she loved all the new smells. They have a disc course plenty of picnic areas, water slides(not always open and not included in camping fee).  Everything about this park is a 10. However the bathrooms/showers around sites 1-14 was awful and hadn't been cleaned. And one toilet ran for 3days.
Amazing place for camping, or just a day of fun.

name: San Bernardino National Forest Serrano Campground
formatted_address: Big Bear, CA 92314, USA

google_reviews.text: Absolutely the best, most beautiful campground on the north side of lake. Going there again in 2 weeks to show the hubby  👍

We spent two days one night camping here.
The camping places are so clean, each place has a big table and big enough. The restroom is clean also, shower places is a little bit small but still ok. You can see kids run around here, pets friendly, some people do exercise in the morning, likes ride bikes, running, walking. Very beautiful and peace. It was very cold in the night, you should keep warmer. We wanna come back again.

RV DUMP/REFILL STATION REVIEW ONLY:
We came for a dump/refill and the stations are very worn down
The non potable water did not work and the hose has no fitting on the end anyways, it's just a raw cut hose. It floods out of the bottom of the pipe, so don't bother.
The drinkable water (potable) only worked on the station to the left and it was literally a trickle. It took almost 5 full minutes just to get 2 gallons! We gave up, there is a 7 eleven in Big Bear that has free potable water.
It's only $10 (card only) to dump though, and there are dumpsters at the dump station also so you can unload your garbage. No refunds, so read the instructions!
The ranger at the station was very nice though.
Our go to campground in SoCal. Peaceful, beautiful forest, clean facilities, great staff.
Beautiful mountains and places to camp and go 4x4 trail driving must see and go

name: Holcomb Valley Ranch
formatted_address: 34300 Holcomb Valley Rd, Fawnskin, CA 92333, USA

google_reviews.text:
We had our wedding at HVR at the end of July 2026...safe to say, it was quite literally the BEST DAY EVER!! The bride and grooms suites were clean and set up nicely. The shower's were clean, and many of our family members used them and said they were great! Ed, the camp host, was FANTASTIC!!! Super accommodating, and helpful! He and some staff even joined us during the reception lol
We've heard from so many friends and family members that our wedding was the best/coolest/most emotional/beautiful wedding they've ever attended and I truly think HVR had a huge roll to play! 10/10 could not recommend them more.
Here's pics from the wedding day and the rehearsal :)

Visited Holcomb Valley Ranch in Big Bear while attending the Young Men’s Weekend event, and the setting was absolutely incredible. The location itself is impeccable — wide open, peaceful, and surrounded by nature. It’s easily one of my favorite camping spots. There’s something special about waking up to the sound of wild horses in the morning, a rare experience that adds to the atmosphere.

I camped here for 3–4 days, and it was remarkably quiet and relaxing. The only noise came from a road behind the property where off-roading vehicles passed, but it was minimal and never distracting enough to take away from the overall peace of the place. The land is vast, giving everyone plenty of space to spread out and enjoy without feeling crowded.

For anyone looking for a spot that combines natural beauty, serenity, and a touch of the wild, this ranch delivers. Five stars without hesitation — highly recommend for group events or personal camping trips.
