This is one of our favorite spots to frequent. It opens earlier than other local hiking and you can easily watch the sunrise from the top of one of the small peaks. We do about a 4 mile loop and it’s the perfect way to get out and about in the early morning before it gets too hot.
Decent family friendly hikes of all difficulty levels
Many loops. Several steep climbs. Beautiful views. Parking on public streets. Most can complete the hike in 1-2 hrs, so there's enough people coming and going.

Pleasant Park. The main "loop" is mostly flat with a couple of steel hills so it's enough to get you sweating. The views are nice enough, the park is very skinny so it's a trail with several meters of chaparral habitat on each side and housing/roads beyond that. You certainly won't feel immersed in nature but you can hear the birds and smell the plants. I especially enjoyed the short creek trail, where we saw some crayfish and tadpoles. We went on an overcast day so we didn't struggle with heat.

name: Crystal Cove Perimeter Loop
formatted_address: E Coastal Peak, Newport Coast, CA 92657, USA

google_reviews.text:
It’s a great place to to hike the large loop clockwise is about 7 milesa bit tough the trail overlook. The ocean is very beautiful and enjoyable to walk. They’re beautiful scenes. I’m good in bathroom facilities before the hike or after the hike down at the Crystal Grove ocean. Don’t miss Shake shack after long height.
You will be disconnected from everywhere. This reception is so poor in this area. If you like biking, it’s a good good trade for bikers too although there’s no shades through the hike.

Not a place for young kids even for hiking (little to no shade) But a great place for adults!  Long Hikes or Bike (mtb or gravel). Depends on whether you like single track or fire roads.  Very steep.  Gravel some chunky sections, ups and downs across the ridges. Single track is fun and fairly techy.  Not some killer stuff but definitely need to be on focus.(lizard and old emerald are the highlights) there are more hidden gems but you have to look for them. There  is some ruttage out there, especially since this last winter.

Cyclists: If you have a 46 rear cassette at minimum this would be sufficient again some steep sections, choose your route wisely.
Always a good day out on the trails, especially with it still being so green and lush.  If you go far enough u can even see the ocean!! :)
Very nice area to run ,ride bikes or hike thru that area  very pleasant views of the area
Love this place,so peaceful and plus I get a good work out!

name: Dana Point Preserve Trailhead
formatted_address: 34385 Dana Strand Rd, Dana Point, CA 92629, USA

google_reviews.text: The CNLM Dana Point Preserve Trail offers a truly relaxing experience, perfect for a leisurely walk. The trail's scenic beauty, coupled with stunning views of the ocean, creates an ideal backdrop for a peaceful outing. As you stroll, the gentle ocean breeze enhances the experience, making it a refreshing escape from the hustle and bustle. It's essential to always stay on the trail to preserve the natural habitat and ensure a safe and enjoyable visit for everyone. While the trail is great for walking, it's not suited for jogging. Whether you're looking to clear your mind or simply take in the beautiful surroundings, this trail is a delightful escape.

The trailhead gate is located at the end of the street. The gate remains closed but is unlocked during regular hours. Parking is allowed on the street, except on Thursdays from 10 AM to noon due to street cleaning. Additional parking is available at the interpretive center, which also has another entrance. The trail does not loop.
This is an incredibly well-maintained trail. There is easy access to lookout points over the harbor and you can also go from this trail to walk down to Strands beach. I do this one on repeat!

This is one of the greatest trails that I have trekked in a long time. It was very calming and collective. There was lots of informational boards telling us about the natural wildlife surrounding this place. We got a great view of the ocean.

It spans roughly 60 acres and offers stunning ocean views, serene walking trails, and a chance to experience Southern California's unique coastal landscape. The preserve is known for its cliffs overlooking the Pacific Ocean, native coastal plants, and wildlife, including migrating gray whales visible during certain seasons.

name: Top of the World
formatted_address: W Ridge, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text: I love hiking this place the views are 360 at the top, and mostly panoramic everywhere. Parking is plentiful. There are some easy hike and hard hikes. I recommend doing the hard hikes early in the morning to beat the heat, make sure you have great shoes and a good stamina you will need it as they are short but steep(grades in high 20’s i think).

Great lookout at the peak.  Our daughters enjoyed the 360 view!!! The park next door to this is perfect for families looking for a place to rest, grab some water, use the facilities or have a snack after their trek on the hill!!

Been here multiple times. Always have fun hiking up there and then soaking in the views. Also easy to walk down a couple of miles from here and get to Laguna Beach downtown. Can get here from Parking lot 16 along the 133 freeway, or from Canyon View Park via West Ridge or Wood Canyon or Rock It trails. Can also easily park at Alta Laguna Park and just walk 5 minutes, but that won't give you any workout. There are restrooms and a playground right next to it in Alta Laguna Park.

Not just the vista point, but the "Top of the World". It's located at the end of the W. Ridge road, leading you to the high point of the Laguna Beach area, where you can see the endless sky and sea, and the entire Laguna Beach.

Definitely a go-to if you're looking for a quality time between you and the Pacific Ocean 🌊.
The best views come from the hardest climbs. Unless it’s cloudy, then you are out of luck. Still a great trail on a cool day. Majestic sites abound. Highly recommend.

name: Aliso Summit Hiking Trail
formatted_address: 29292 Ridgeview Dr, Laguna Niguel, CA 92677, USA

google_reviews.text:
One of the more accessible and most rewarding hikes in this area. The views are unbeatable at almost any time of day - sunset comes in first place though!

Make sure to check for ticks post hike and bring a hat because there isn’t much shade.

Such a great walk/hike. The trail is wide and clear, some of the path is paved but it is mostly hard dirt. The view of the ocean between the mountains and the view inland are both gorgeous. There is some shade, but it is mostly a sunny trail. Gorgeous homes line the inside of the trail. It isn't super nature-y, but it is a comfortable walk/hike with a gorgeous view. I'd say it is great for a stroll and do able for someone getting back into walking or wanting to start small to move into hiking.

Also, there are cute little gnome doors painted on the bases of some of the trees along the trail! That was super cute to see.

Dog friendly and parking in the neighborhood at the start of the trail is very convenient.
You can connect Aliso summit trail to Laguna Nigel top of the world park and Seaview park (easy). From Seaview park, you can hike down to Laguna Beach (difficult).
Great views. Some burnt parts. We did halfway because the smell was still a bit strong from burnt house/soil/plants or chemicals they use.
Great hike leading to a great view of the ocean

name: Bosque Trail
formatted_address: Follyhatch, Irvine, CA 92618, USA

google_reviews.text:
Bosque Trail is close to our home, and it’s one of our favorite spots for family strolls in the evening. When our kids get curious about a flower, we use an app to look up its name—it turns a simple walk into a fun learning moment.

My wife enjoys showing them the caterpillars munching on milkweed. It’s a great way for the kids to connect with nature and learn something new each time we go.

Bosque Trail is a long, paved walking path. It feels private because it's planted with tall native grasses and fragrant shrubs. It's located in Irvine's Great Park neighborhood, but isn't located inside "the Great Park" proper. The entire trail is about 2 miles out and back. Leading off the paved path are some side paths with decomposed granite. There are small hills which makes for a nice elevation change. It's perfect for walking, jogging, or biking. There are two large tunnels that take you underneath the roads. There are a couple small parking lots on Bosque with typically plenty of spaces available. There are 2 restrooms, drinking fountains, dog bags, and a playground. The path is well-lit and safe for night walking.

We love this place. It’s local and there is playground for kids and trails to walk and enjoy the nature and wild flowers although be aware of coyotes because they also lives there. It’s dog friendly and u should keep your dog on the leash all the time.
Beautiful and quiet trails ( I went on a Friday morning)  smooth paths. Not much shade recommend bringing water. Lots of bathrooms & rest areas .
Easy and kid-friendly trail to bike or walk on!

name: Gum Grove Park
formatted_address: Crescent View &, Avalon Dr, Seal Beach, CA 90740, USA

google_reviews.text: The place where the breeze from the nearby Seal Beach is fresh. The trails that even the elderly can easily take a walk, and the wildflowers blooming everywhere are beautiful. Dogs are welcome but must be on a leash. If you want to escape the hustle and bustle of the city, come to this place and enjoy being alone.

Great little walking trails, quite a bit of sound pollution.. airplanes, gardeners, construction. It seems like it is a different house every single time. There are some amazing nature sounds if you can tune out all the garbage. The best time to visit is a couple weeks after a good rain, everything is green and the wildlife is active. Free parking, no restrooms.

Being in the middle of a busy city can get exhausting and this little hike was such a nice escape from it all. From beginning to end I want to say it was less than 2.5 miles. This is almost completely dirt trails, nothing up hill, and not very shaded if you're going midday. While you visit you will most definitely run into some frogs, lizards, and lots of cool birds.

Parking: can be a little tricky because it's in a residential neighborhood. Park on the street (check which day street sweep is on)!

Dog-Friendly?: Yes 👍🐶 your dog must be leashed (although it seems like a lot of people don't follow this) and please just pick up after them. There are a few trashcans along the way, so pick up the poop! There are lots of coyotes in the area, so just be aware of that (also why it's a good idea to have your dog on a leash. My dog really enjoyed all the little critters he saw scurrying around (squirrels/lizards) and he was able to get a lot of energy out "hunting" lol.

This is a small gem located between the Los Cerritos Wetlands and housing tract.  This trail loop is about 2 miles round trip and can easily be managed by a family with little ones.
There are two entrances. The westerly entrance is located off PCH and Mar Vista Ave., at the end of Avalon Dr., past it's intersection with Crestview Ave. The easterly entrance is at Seal Beach Blvd. and Heron Pointe (across from the main entrance to the Naval Weapons Station).  Each entrance has parking, with moreso available at the westerly entrance. Like most public trails and parks, it is open from dusk until dawn.
The best time to visit is springtime when all the blossoms are out, blanketing the floor beneath the towering gumtree eucalyptus. The best way to enjoy the trail is through the westerly entrance, of which there are three levels of trails. The lower trail is the flattest, but still offers great views. The mid-slope trail takes you up & down amid the gum groves and in between the greenery and blossoms.  The upper trail runs along the top of the slope and abuts the fences and walls of the northerly end of the neighboring housing tract.
Continuing easterly will give you more options as you come to a fork past a gate. Proceeding straight and to your right will careen you through a much wider path between housing tracts. This path eventually leads to the easterly entrance at Heron Pointe, at Seal Beach Blvd. Taking the left in the fork will give a more rewarding trek, in my opinion. Once you go down some stairs (maybe eight steps), you will come across the first of ten monument stones with a plaque affixed. Each plaque explains the history of the surrounding area from the native people, to the ranchos, to the land purchases of Abel Stearns, Isias Hellman, and the Bixby family. Onward past these ten stones is a circular seating area with four informative signs. Unfortunately, they are weather-beaten and nearly eligible. Forging on will soon get you to a dead end at a locked double-gate.

Fun for a short and flat hike. The trail is in between protected wetlands and residences. This year's heavy rains brought a lot of wildflower blooms and frogs!

Entrance is in a neighborhood. Follow the road down the hill and you can park in a small lot at the start of the trail.

There are no bathrooms.

name: Buck Gully Reserve
formatted_address: San Joaquin Hills Rd, Newport Beach, CA 92657, USA

google_reviews.text: Not crowded at all. Out and back Trail.  Watch out for overhead spider webs and spiders.  I don’t recommend kids on this trail, but to each his/her own.  Nice easy trail. Peaceful. Nice neighborhood area to park however I feel bad for residents dealing with the foot or car traffic. I live near a visited area and parking is and could be an issue.  Overall pretty clear of trash.

Are you looking for a good hiking spot?

Not used to hiking? No problem. Start slow and build your confidence. This is a good beginner hike.

Hiking with a group is a great way to not get lost on the trails lol 🤣🥾

It’s a beautiful trail.

.
Tip:
Newport Coast Shopping Center - park there and walk to the trail
Starbucks (use the bathroom before hike)
21181 Newport Coast Dr
Newport Coast, CA 92657

Pretty amazing deep cove with steep mountains, lush growth and trees with a nice stream in the middle with miles and miles of paths which are bike and people friendly. At the top of the trail you can panoramic views of the Newport Beach harbor and a wonderful view of the seascape.  Trails range from easy to difficult.

The trail is well signed and well maintained. There is a year round stream in the gully and a few metal bridge crossings. On a weekday morning, I saw a few hikers and no cyclists. Bikes should only hike one direction; hikers can go either way. You see houses and power lines but you also see trees, vegetation and birds, plus hear the stream. A nice hike within the city.
This is another hidden gem in the middle of Orange county. We enjoyed the bobcat trail within the reserve. It has lots of shade through out that made the trek very enjoyable.

name: El Modena Open Space
formatted_address: N Cannon St, Orange, CA 92869, USA

google_reviews.text:
One of my favorite hiking spots. Starting from the south, you are met with a pretty steep climb about 5 minutes in, which has grades around 20-35%. After that, you get some nice ups and downs along the few hills there, and towards the end a very steep (and tight) descent down to the road and bike path, giving you an option of doing that steep climb back up, or following the road back to the start.

The views you get of the cities are amazing once at the top too. This hike is my favorite for a quick 1 hour hike that gives you plenty of opportunities to get your heart rate maxed.

We went stargazing and got to see the amazing sunset and city view. We street parked at nearby neighborhood and walked a few minutes to get there. Just be careful when crossing the street. We witnessed many cars ignoring the stop signs, and it's downhill too so 👀
A lovely open space area relatively untouched by civilization. You can find CA Gnatcatchers if you're quiet and go at the right time.

Great hike! Lots of steep hills and loose rocks and some point. Did ok in sneakers but if you're klutzy like me I'd wear boots. Don't follow the Google directions they'll take you to a school where there is no access point. Park off of Canyon and Stillwater. Lots of lizards and some birds. Lots of cactus
I've walked/ran this trail since around 2012. Three hills that will forsure get your blood pumping, each summit gives you an amazing view over OC, and on clear days the ocean is visible.

name: Bommer Canyon Preserve Cattle Camp
formatted_address: 11 Bommer Canyon, Irvine, CA 92603, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice and easy trails for the family. Some historical points of interest and regional flora to see. Bathrooms at the trailhead and Irvine police randomly pass through.

A fairly simple hiking attraction in the middle of Irvine. The set of trials vary in difficulty: at first the trials are easy, but if you're athletic, you can hike more rigorous challenging trials later in the hike. A believe the canyon is free of charge and that's always a plus side.

Not the most beautiful trail in Orange County (partly because Orange County has tons of beautiful trials), but sill a gorgeous place to hike!

Be warned: there is seldom any shade on the trail, be ready to get a lot of vitamin D!

An island of wilderness in the middle of Irvine. Many trails for walking, biking, running--open every day. Other trails open during special events--on the third Saturday of each month. Area is particularly verdant in April 2019 after winter rains.
evening hike, nice to be out here and avoid the crowds
Awesome park, with good hiking trails.

name: Laguna Niguel Regional Park
formatted_address: 28241 La Paz Rd, Laguna Niguel, CA 92677, USA

google_reviews.text: Cool park!  Just wish the Park Rangers could get a handle of the vehicle speeds!  Lots of people walk in the park but the vehicle drivers refuse to SLOW down 😩.  Pickle-ballers are the biggest offenders (most in Teslas!).

First time visiting the park on Easter Sunday. Restrooms very clean in the early morning. The park rangers were very communicative about the rules of the park and let us know if we need garbage bags for trash to please wave them down. Since it was a special day First come first serve kinda deal so make sure to get there early to set aside your grill/tables. Overall beautiful open areas perfect for family and pets. The trails are beautiful to walk around and people fishing by the lake. Just caution when crossing through because of the smaller areas and incoming cars.
What a lovely place with tall trees, water ponds, and green sitting areas! 😌🌳
I miss it already!! I’m from Seattle but I spent a lot of time down there. Going back in 6 months!!

A great park for parties, walks and fishing. Great for wildlife viewing throughout the year. Safe trails for exercise or leisure. Plenty of open space and a playground for younger children. The lake is often stocked with Trout and Catfish, but also has Bass and Bluegill. Not many spots around the lake are fishable, so it's tough when it's crowded.

name: Sycamore Creek Trail
formatted_address: 33856-33998 Del Obispo St, Dana Point, CA 92629, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a great walk any day, and it’s perfect to take visitors.   Eat a big Thanksgiving meal and saunter back and forth on this trail.  You’ll feel good about it.
Great place to walk your dogs

I can't quite put my finger on,  but it doesn't seem the way it used to be; the taste of the chili dog and  the chili stuff; it tastes different, not worse or any better, but just different. Anyway, that's my opinion and that's no change they're still have  great customer service as always and a good smile! I'll keep going back but I'm still a little indifferent, of the taste or change in the chili sauce, I really don't know if there is any, thanks covid!  I don't know, you tell me any change, better, worse, or nothing at, you be the judge!

Cool little park, if you want to get away from the madness of life, lots of birds,  green grass, rabbits and squirrels. If you like to exercise they have a good circuit routine. Benches available for a relaxing moment or BYOL ( bring our own lunch) cheers!

The Sycamore Creek Trail in Dana Point is well cap, the bathrooms are nice and clean oh, the office has pamphlets with information on local animals that live in the area Sycamore Creek Trail they grow plants that are indigenous to the area, the trail is marked well also they have Lookout areas that Overlook Dana strands Beach and Salt Creek, there is a Ranger at the office that has a lot of information they could take with you and they also have nice clean drinking fountains to fill up your bottles I hope you have a great time and Dana Point

name: Peters Canyon Regional Park
formatted_address: 8548 Canyon View Ave, Santa Ana, CA 92705, USA

google_reviews.text:
Moderate trail can take up to 2 hours.
Lovely view! Parking is easy and costs $3.

Good trails for families, easy notrth loop abd difficult east edge trails. You can take the trail to your needs. A lot of parking places.
Round trip can be around 2.7 miles to 5.8 miles, inclines of 2° to around 15° rolling hills, long hill climbs, many rest area with benches and delightful picturesque sceneries.  Great for bike riders,  joggers runners hikes and walks. Pet friendly with leash. This trail can extend towards the north,  Irvine park or to the south towards the river paths in Irvine.  Water station is great! BLUE SKy, good Sunshine, Mountain View's, you can see the other side of building in Irvine, which is far far away, enjoy!

An enjoyable hiking experience with hills, climbs, drops, dips, twists and views at most every turn! Exciting for first timers like myself and inviting for return visits when the time is right.
Since much of the trails are unshaded and I’m sensitive to the sun, I chose to go on a cool May Grey day, if not for the June Gloom. Besides, once the summer kicks in, it can get downright hot here!
As an Orange resident, I’d still want to hike and ride here with the seasonal changes, tho perhaps early in the morning. A park notice also advises the rainy day closures, as the dirt trails may turn to mud!
The main entrance has paid parking, with picnic tables, ranger station, restrooms and lookouts towards the lake.
Trail information is available at the kiosks. I also relied on my Google Maps app in satellite view for a continuous verification of my location progress in real time.

1.	Scenic Trails: Offers a variety of well-maintained trails for hiking, running, and biking.
2.	Great Views: Features beautiful scenery, including hills, a reservoir, and native flora.
3.	Difficulty Levels: Trails range from easy to moderate, catering to different skill levels.
4.	Popular Spot: Can get busy during weekends and early mornings.
5.	Wildlife: Opportunity to spot birds, rabbits, and other wildlife.
6.	Amenities: Limited facilities; restrooms and water fountains are available near the entrance.
7.	Parking: Paid parking lot available, but it can fill up quickly; street parking is an option.
8.	Dog-Friendly: Allows leashed dogs, making it a great spot for pet owners.
9.	Seasonal Beauty: Looks especially vibrant after rain, with lush greenery and wildflowers.
10.	Overall Experience: A peaceful escape offering both relaxation and physical activity.

This was our second time hiking here, and this time GPS took us to the north side of the trail near the lake. We were a little confused at first since we’re more familiar with the south entrance, but we decided to keep going and it ended up being a great change of pace.

For more experienced or frequent hikers, Peter’s Canyon offers an excellent mix of wide fire roads and narrower single-track trails, with steady elevation gain, rolling hills, and exposed sections. The north side near the lake has a more gradual start, but don’t be fooled because once you move away from the water, the climbs pick up and the terrain becomes more demanding. Expect packed dirt trails, some loose gravel in spots, and very little shade, so sun protection is key, especially mid-day.

Compared to the south entrance, the north side feels a little more scenic and peaceful, with open views of the reservoir and fewer bottlenecks early on. The south side, which we personally prefer, feels more direct and workout-focused, with climbs that hit sooner and a more familiar loop rhythm.

Important tip: there are two main entrances north and south, so double-check where your GPS is taking you. Both entrances charge for parking, but we actually ran into a local runner who frequents this trail and mentioned that street parking in nearby residential areas can be an option if you know where to look (always check posted signs).

Overall, this is a great trail for hikers who want a moderate-to-challenging workout, open canyon views, and flexibility to customize distance and elevation.

name: Canyon Park
formatted_address: 970 Arbor St, Costa Mesa, CA 92627, USA

google_reviews.text:
More than just a park, but an adventure for bikers Joggers Walkers runners and family's. The park is interconnected with canyon park plus talbert regional park..together,  creating a huge place to enjoy your day..in addition,  it offers acces to the water canal water trail that can lead to the city or to the ocean.
The trail at canyon are small hilly steep and filled with brush.  The talbert park is flat and brushy,  but offers ling stretches of scenic views. Plus, talbert park offers a bmx sheep hills bike enthusiast area. They are connected by a thin stretch of park and both locations can be accessed by multiple entry points.  The canyon park has to parking lots, upper and lower. Plus bike tie up stations.  For the kids, a park with 2 playgrounds. Clean and ample space, many trails to delight everyone.  The walk runs and bike paths are easy to moderate level in skill. Neighborhood is super quiet and friendly,  so please,  be polite and pick after yourself and have fun!

Good morning walk with my dog. The flowers and butterflies 🦋 are starting to become more abundant lately. 2 weeks till spring and warmer weather as the rains have given all the plants a drink. Water is flowing in the canyon! I love ❤️ it

Nestled in the coastal hills of Costa Mesa, here’s a great place to go as an alternative nature spot!
Canyon Park was obviously formed from runoff during the rainy season’s, thus carving downhill paths. Then there may be a babbling brook to enjoy.
When dried out, the park can be accessed more so across and thru the paths, for a nice little natural recreation area.
The park entrance is off of Arbor St. in an upscale neighborhood. There’s a community playground area fronting the street here, where walk-in access is permitted. There’s also a small trailhead at the opposite end of the playground.
Parking may be found deeper in the park, further along the entrance road.
I had to find a tree, as public restrooms were nowhere to be found!
Trails are also accessed thru thru Canyon Drive at Sea Bluff Drive and the more open Talbert Regional Park.

This is a hidden gem with several walking trails and a large pond, among other things. It's close to neighborhoods and easy to get to, but once in the park it feels like you're out in the wilderness.
Lots of short, easy dirt walking trails and pretty nature views. These trails connect to a larger network of walking/hiking trails, which makes this a good starting point for longer walks.

Dogs are allowed in the park, and the park has a children's playground area and good parking.
This is my favorite park in Huntington Beach/Costa Mesa/Fountain Valley so far. Feels like you're secluded in the woods. It's beautiful and a nice hike as well.

name: O'Neill Regional Park
formatted_address: 30892 Trabuco Canyon Rd, Trabuco Canyon, CA 92679, USA

google_reviews.text: Great park for camping. We stayed for a couple weeks other than a couple days precautionary evacuation for flooding. Staff is helpful and knowledgeable. Houses are nice except for the first one as you come into the park is a bit dated. Beautiful area. The drive into the canyon can be a bit treacherous but just take it slow.  Dumpsters throughout the park. Some sites also have water spigot at them. A lot of tent camping here also. We were in site 26. It was a bit snug. There are a couple corner sites for pull through for long RVs. Nice that each campsite has a picnic table also.

We camped here 3 days/ 2 nights. Had a huge pull through site that was level and shaded. Water on our site was easy but no electricity. Hikes were easy to access but no dogs on the trails except the 2 trails. So limited dog friendly trails. We enjoyed the location and rangers were nice.

Nice regional park.  There is a funny homeless man who sits at the back of the park and yells funny stories. You have to check him out if you are there.  I do not like how they added conststion cones in the trail to watch out for obvious low hanging branch.  It takes away from the ambience.
The Rangers are so very professional friendly and helpful. I highly recommend this campground. I've been visiting here always in an R.V. since I was a kid with mom and dad so many beautiful memories.

One of my favorite local regional parks, bathrooms and showers if you need them. Lots of trails to explore, very kid friendly. And if you don’t want to cook your own meals there are plenty of options close by in Rancho Santa Margarita.

name: Turtle Rock Nature Center
formatted_address: 1 Sunnyhill, Irvine, CA 92603, USA

google_reviews.text: Best short walk for toddlers. My 18 month old walked it. Cute nature center with toys and books too.

If you live in Irvine or are just passing through, you have to swing by the Turtle Rock Nature Center. It's five acres of pure awesome tucked right into the city, but you'd never know it. It seriously feels like you've found a secret, peaceful little world!

It’s super chill and perfect for families. The trails are easy—totally stroller-friendly—and they have cool little sections showing off different types of California nature, like desert plants and woods. My kids loved trying to spot lizards and little birds near the creek.

The Nature Center building itself is small but packed with fun, hands-on stuff. It’s a great spot to let the kids wander and learn without them realizing they’re learning! They even have this cool, quiet outdoor labyrinth path for when you just need a minute to chill and walk. It’s well-kept, free, and just a perfect, easy outdoor escape. Seriously, skip the mall and hang out here instead!

My son's wedding at Turtle Rock was a beautiful celebration of love. The staff's exceptional service and the stunning venue created an unforgettable experience for all. We couldn't have asked for a more perfect day.
Perfect little trail to take your little ones. We love the nature center.

It is a small nature walk, but the continue to make improvements.  Staff were willing to help educate us on some of the new things and it is right next to a public park that has a good amount of equipment

name: Borrego Canyon Overlook Park
formatted_address: 21 Viaggio Ln, Foothill Ranch, CA 92610, USA

google_reviews.text: I went down to the trail after lookout on the top of mountain. Walking along the trail and then I found it’s connected to the whiting ranch trail. I chose the mustard trail and it’s quite challenging but I made it. It’s out-and-back and I spent 1 and a half hour on it! Great trail! You walk further and you will have a beautiful lookout at the Four Corner spot.

Nice small park in the corner overlooking the canyon. It has a nice hiking trail with nice some with good playgrounds for kids up to the age of 10. A lot of shades and a grilling area. Plenty of seating. I would recommend though if you’re in the area.

I've been hiking the Borrego trail below this park and saw this park on google Maps. I thought I'd check it out. It's a pretty nice little park. It has a nice playground. Some tables with barbecues next to them. Open grass area for kids to play. Or other activities.

Beautiful park! I hiked down the trail that was nearby and sat on a bench overlooking the lovely valley. The bench had a message tucked under it so naturally I was curious. Kind of loved that little surprise with some words of wisdom. 🙌

Beautiful park with lots bbq grill, only problem I see is no water just as all other LA area park, but very pretty park. Also I was able to walk down to trail even other people commented you can Not walk to the trail. It is little rough and steep but you can walk to trail.

name: Huntington Beach
formatted_address: Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text:
Visited Huntington Beach with my family on January 10, 2026.

The beach was clean, calm, and very well maintained, and in my opinion it’s one of the best beaches in Orange County. I personally liked it even more than Newport Beach.

Even in January the weather was pleasant, and the beach was lively. People were enjoying surfing and beach volleyball, which added to the vibrant atmosphere.

My daughter had an amazing time chasing seagulls and playing in the sand, which made the visit even more memorable for us.

Beautiful beach area. Designated picnic areas with concrete tables and wooden awnings over head right off the parking lot and further down the beach. Volleyball courts. Parking is 15$ for all day use and they do not take cash only a credit or debit card to pay. There is saugus pizza located inside after you park and many other places close by to eat before you head over. Restrooms available during beach hours are decently clean and handicapped accessible, as is parking.

Huntington Beach in November was calm, clear, and beautiful.
The water was bright blue, the waves were steady, and the weather was perfect for walking the pier and watching surfers. Even in late fall, the beach was clean, wide, and full of classic Southern California views. Great spot for photos, sunsets, and a laid-back day on the coast.
Wow, Huntington Beach, beautiful, clean, and unique. I took a rest day here after climbing Mount Whitney and had a great time. I will absolutely come again.

Fabulous beach with lots of sand and lots of water. And the sunsets are unforgettable.

Looking forward to the day when the oil platforms will be removed.

name: Laguna Beach, California
formatted_address: Broadway St & Pacific Coast Hwy, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text:
Laguna Beach is truly a slice of paradise! The sunsets alone are worth the trip fiery orange skies melting into the ocean, with the reflection dancing on the water like a painting come to life. The coastline is peaceful and full of natural beauty, from the rocky outcrops covered with seabirds to the calm, endless horizon. Whether you're taking a quiet evening walk, enjoying the sound of the waves, or just soaking in the colors of the sky, Laguna delivers pure serenity.
It's the perfect place to relax, reset, and appreciate Southern California's coastal magic.

Laguna Beach is a beautiful place to visit, especially during the golden hour when the houses on the hills start glowing. The beach is clean, peaceful and has a great mix of scenery with the ocean on one side and mountains on the other. There are volleyball and basketball courts nearby, and the area feels lively without being crowded. Public parking is available (3$/hr) across the street in several lots, which makes it easy to spend more time by the water. A great spot to relax and enjoy the views

Laguna Beach Pure Coastal Magic! 🌴🌅💙

Laguna Beach is honestly one of the most breathtaking places in California. The moment you arrive, it feels like stepping into a postcard — crystal-clear water, golden sand, charming streets, and that peaceful ocean breeze that instantly clears your mind. Every corner has its own beauty, whether it’s a quiet cove or a busy art-filled downtown.

What I love most is how easy it is to find everything. Parking is convenient, the signs are clear, and the whole town feels thoughtfully designed for both locals and visitors. You can explore art galleries, unique boutiques, and beachside cafes all within walking distance. It’s the perfect mix of laid-back and luxurious.

The staff and locals are incredibly kind and welcoming. From shop owners to restaurant servers, everyone treats you like you belong there. You can tell that the community and management take real pride in keeping Laguna beautiful, safe, and clean. The beaches are spotless, and everything feels well cared for from the public restrooms to the walking paths.

Whether you’re catching the sunset at Main Beach, hiking through Heisler Park, or enjoying fresh seafood with ocean views, every experience here feels unforgettable. The art festivals, the coastal trails, the tide pools it all blends into this perfect balance of relaxation and inspiration.

Laguna Beach isn’t just a destination it’s a whole mood. Peaceful, creative, and full of life. Every visit leaves you refreshed, grateful, and already planning your next trip back.

If paradise had an address, it would be Laguna Beach.

Even people who live near the area talk about the unique difference of Laguna. The magic is palpable in so many ways in every season. Every morning the sun crosses those low-slung hills and kisses the sea. Waves crash ashore like a tonic, setting an easy tone for the day. It's a cliche, but this place is practically perfect...mmm...in every way.    😎👍

With unforgettable sunsets, charming streets, and an elegant art and dining scene, Laguna Beach offers a timeless experience that’s both relaxing and inspiring. A must-visit destination that leaves a lasting impression.

name: Huntington State Beach

google_reviews.text: Can't say enough about this beautiful location. Ive lived here now for 2 years, I ride the boardwalk several times a week. 10.3.25: Flew down to visit a friend who lives here and we had lunch on the beach at Na Ka Oi. That didn't stop me from enjoying the sunshine, toes in the sand, beach waves, and a mini air show as the planes were doing practice runs. It wasn't as crowded as I expected but there was a pretty good amount of people on the beach and in the water. There were a field of volleyball nets, but oddly enough not a single person was out there playing. I noticed a lot of police and beach patrol, but I attribute that to the air show activity.

name: Corona Del Mar State Beach Park

name: Dana Strands Beach

Dana Strands Beach is a top-notch site, notable for its cleanliness and vast sandy beach, where one can enjoy miles of walking paths by the shore, accompanied by beautiful views.

Dana Strand Beach has been our go-to summer spot since the 1990s. Its pristine sands, crystal-clear waters, and serene atmosphere make it the perfect destination for a relaxing day by the ocean. Our family loves spending time here, whether it's soaking up the sun, playing in the waves, or taking leisurely walks along the shoreline.

One of our favorite traditions is enjoying lunch at the nearby Ritz Carlton after a fun-filled morning at the beach. The luxury and quality of the Ritz Carlton dining experience never disappoints, providing a delightful contrast to the casual beach vibe. The stunning views from the Ritz, coupled with their exquisite cuisine, create unforgettable family memories.

Dana Strand Beach is also great for walking, jogging, and exercising. The long stair steps at both ends of the beach offer a fantastic workout with a view. This combination of natural beauty and physical activity makes our visits even more enjoyable.

Dana Strand Beach holds a special place in our hearts, offering a perfect blend of natural beauty and convenient amenities. It's a cherished destination for our family, and we highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fantastic beach experience.

name: Newport Beach Municipal Beach

If you’re looking for the ultimate SoCal beach day, Newport Beach is where the magic happens. The moment you arrive, the salty breeze and glistening ocean practically dare you not to smile. Stroll down the iconic pier with an ice cream cone in hand, watch surfers tackle the waves, or rent a beach cruiser to glide along the boardwalk like a local. The vibe here is effortlessly cool with families building sandcastles, friends tossing frisbees, and couples soaking up sunsets that look like they were painted just for Instagram.

What really sets Newport Beach apart is the mix of relaxed charm and upscale fun. You can grab a casual taco at a beach shack one minute and be sipping a craft cocktail at a waterfront restaurant the next.

Whether you’re here to surf, shop, sunbathe, or just people-watch, Newport Beach delivers. It’s clean, lively, and endlessly photogenic, making it the perfect spot for a weekend getaway or a spontaneous day trip. Bring sunscreen, your favorite shades, and an appetite for adventure because you won’t be disappointed.

It is a Nice area to exercise walk on Pathway. Can shops at cute little stores and beach ware.

OUR FAVORITE BEACH in LOS ANGELES !!!!

Just being able to set down a beach towel may be all that’s needed for many visitors, once they make it down yonder!

This review is long overdue as LCdM has been my favorite local beach for over a decade.

- It’s small and beautiful with plenty of texture, including tide pools
- Parking is residential and as a result free but also limited
- There are restrooms onsite

We also appreciated the family-friendly atmosphere—lots of other kids and families enjoying the day, but it never felt overcrowded. It’s such a relaxed environment, and parking was simple too.

After our whale watching adventure and a quick lunch, we went to Balboa Beach in Balboa Village. There were plenty of parking lots, and paying with the app was easy.

The kids enjoyed chasing seabirds and playing in the sun on the sandy beach. I took a walk along the trail and saw many people staying at vacation rentals. Balboa Beach is a great place for family fun with lots of conveniences.

Another beautiful beach in the LA area. Good parking with a easy way to pay using an app. Lots of food, liquor and ice-cream places around here. Cycling is very popular. We rented some bikes from a local shop and biked the whole beachfront road along all the pretty houses along the way with families hanging out in the front yard. Lots of kids and younger crowds.

name: Heisler Park Beach Stairway
formatted_address: Heisler Park Beach Stairway, 361-399 Cliff Dr, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text:
Heisler Park is one of the most beautiful oceanfront parks in Southern California. The views here are absolutely stunning.

There are scenic paths along the cliffs, palm trees, tide pools, small hidden beaches, and incredible sunsets — all in one place. It’s a perfect spot for a relaxing walk by the ocean.

I especially like coming here around sunset. The light over the ocean is beautiful and it’s a great place for photos or just to sit and enjoy the view.

There are also grassy areas that are perfect for a small picnic while watching the ocean.

Parking is usually metered nearby, but the park itself is free to visit. Definitely one of my favorite spots in Laguna Beach.

As a photographer, I recently had the absolute pleasure of capturing a beautiful wedding Heisler Park ! The setting here is truly ideal for such special moments. Beyond weddings, I've also done many other sessions here – from lively family portraits to heartfelt engagement proposals. Heisler Park offers a stunning backdrop for any kind of photoshoot. Highly recommend this location for its natural beauty and versatility!

One of the beautiful places in Laguna Beach. Beautiful sunrise with an empty rocky beach and wonderful sunset (for that better choose the weekdays). And be sure to watch the high and low tide, because at high tide on this beach the water rises and floods almost the entire beach. But in terms of beauty, especially for photos and videos, this place is one of the best.

California's coastline is well known  for its stunning natural beauty, and there are several beautiful parks along its shores. Our favorite is Heisler Park in Laguna Beach. It's located in California's Orange county coastline between pacific coast highway and the Pacific ocean. This park offers breathtaking views of rugged cliffs, Beautiful beaches. The combination of coastal vistas, lush Flowers, and abundant views makes it a paradise for nature lovers and photographers alike. Whether you're hiking along scenic trails or simply soaking in the coastal scenery, Heisler Park is sure to leave a lasting impression.

This is a must-visit spot in California! Laguna Beach offers a stunning boardwalk and a beautiful park right by the beach. The views are absolutely gorgeous, and there are picnic tables and seating areas to relax and soak in the scenery. I loved everything about this place—it's perfect for a day out!

name: Pier Playground | Seal Beach

I've been visiting Seal Beach since I was a young girl – this charming stretch of coastline, though technically north of Orange County and closer to Los Angeles, holds a special place in my heart. This was, however, my first time photographing a beach wedding here, and I must say, it's every bit as beautiful as our other stunning beaches and piers along the coast.

I highly recommend Seal Beach as a wedding or event location. Parking was surprisingly easy, and it wasn't terribly crowded, which was a huge plus. This allowed us to capture truly intimate and beautiful photos without significant intrusion from other people. I was thrilled with the results!
Very nice clean pier. Great shops nearby. Peaceful for sunset.

name: San Clemente State Beach
formatted_address: 225 Avenida Calafia, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text:
This campground has very clear pros and cons.

​The Pros:
​Breathtaking views and a beautiful, easy access point to the beach.
​The sound of the waves breaking at night is absolutely awesome.

​The Cons:
​Facility management is lacking; the majority of the shower facilities were out of order.
​It is extremely difficult to book a spot because it is highly competitive.

​Overall Verdict: Despite the maintenance issues, the location makes this campground well worth the stay.

Amazing spot. View from the cliffs is stunning on a clear day like today. Cite historic and sandy beaches to avoid the cobblestone in other spots
The natural habitat displays and the history in the park story only adds to the charm.

Beautiful & clean! We camped here for 4 days & 3 nights. The view is remarkable. There's is a path that takes you right down to the ocean. It is a VERY steep path! It was a little tricky getting down & especially so coming back up! The campsite is clean with a fire pit and covered picnic bench at each site. There are multiple bathrooms & pay to use showers that are well maintained on the grounds.

Pretty cool camp ground. With a short walk down to beach. Although the walk could be somewhat hard for some people, and definitely not wheelchair accessible due to the fact that it's a bit of a decline walking path down, which ends at the metrolink train track which you have to cross to get to the beach. But the camp ground has a spectacular view from the top of the walking path before the decline which is a huge open plato with picnic benches placed all around with trees for shade. Great fir large groups or for many small groups. The beach was clean and their were life guards on duty. But make sure to pay attention to the red flags when deciding on your spot because red flag means rocks which let me tell you, IT MEANS ROCKS! like big boulders that are only visible when waves revise bak for only a moment until another waves comes in and crashes over it. And beside them the waves crash right at shore creating a huge trench full of tons of rocks so your legs get beat up for sure. and you cannot swim where the flags are because it's very difficult to get pass the rocky trench since it's again wherr the waves crash. So from my experience it's not the best beach to go swimming at. But if your surfing or boogie boarding maybe it's more fit for that. Also I don't know how it was in other areas where there wasn't a red flag. I'm sure much better. I'd definitely return here again to find out!

One of the best camping site with full RV hookups and awesome view of the Pacific Ocean!!! Very dark evenings allowing for perfect view of all the constellations..on full moons, its so bright you don’t need a flash light to hike to the beach. Shore fishing is great as they have grunions runs, plenty of sandcrab  bait, and super secluded beach. Wave are mesuim awesome for surfing and boogie boarding. Great plac to have family parties until 10pm.

The waves are beautiful to watch, and the sand is soft and perfect for laying out or taking a walk at sunset. Whether it’s a quick afternoon trip or a quiet night under the sky, Calafia never disappoints. 🌊✨

name: Huntingon Dog Beach
formatted_address: Huntingon Dog Beach, Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text: Our dog loved Huntington Dog Beach! It was easy for us to social distance since there weren’t too many people. But there was still enough dogs for her to play with.

I live in the High Desert and my housemate wanted to go to the beach to celebrate his birthday, but we couldn't leave our 7 month Malinois. Our pup has never seen a body of water before and we never had her around a lot of dogs. What to do? Where could we go?  We wanted to be in OC and after some web browsing I came across info for Huntington Dog Beach.  Not knowing what to expect, we went praying for a positive experience and what did we get?
1) A beautiful, clean beach!
2) Ample amount of parking (download the Passport app to make payment a breeze)
3) Super friendly, beach-loving  dog owners who made us feel welcome and understood where we were at with our pup.
4) A HUGE dog-friendly beach where you can choose to be in areas around a lot of dogs or find a more remote area where you can test to see how your pooch reacts off the leash.  Our pup loves people and has been playful with other dogs at the Vet or on walks, but how would she interact with everything in this big new world? So many people understood and offered their hospitality introducing their dogs and giving us encouragement.
5) Awesome waves and a great place to kitesurf! (We didn't see any dogs participate, but plenty were watching!  😀 )

All thanks to the community of Huntington Beach for making this  place a dog paradise!

name: San Onofre State Beach
formatted_address: Old Pacific Highway, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text:
San Onofre (Sano) is literally my favorite beach — hands down. It’s one of the few places in Orange County that still feels totally secluded and untouched. There are no houses, no crowds of tourists, and when you’re there, it honestly feels like you’ve escaped OC completely.

It’s an amazing spot for surfing — especially for longboarding and mid-length boards. Most days the waves are mellow and perfect, but on the bigger days, it can get really fun. It’s not always great at high tide, and when there’s a negative tide it can get super rocky, so timing is key.

There’s so much more to love about Sano than just the surf, though. There are volleyball courts, BBQs, and tons of space to hang out with friends and family. Dogs are allowed, which makes it even better, and the lifeguards there are top-notch — super friendly and always keeping an eye out. The park staff does an incredible job keeping the bathrooms and grounds so clean, and there are even outdoor showers to rinse off after a long day in the water.

The only real downside is the long line to get in, especially on weekends, and the rangers don’t always check for open spots, which makes it move slower. But I usually e-bike in, which skips the wait completely — and honestly, that’s part of why I love it. There’s just something about Sano: the mellow vibe, the sound of the waves, the community feel, and that sense of being totally off the grid. It’s pure magic. 🌊☀️🔥

Honestly, we just stopped here as a quick pit stop, and we were pleasantly surprised to find a stort hike/beach trail. Really great views, and right off the highway. There is beach access, but it's a bit of an incline
beautiful place, the beach is big and extremely pretty, great place to spend the day and maybe catch some waves

If you're looking for a great beach to do some camping, plenty of beach trails, lots of people to meet who are also camping, swimming, and hiking, then this is a pretty good spot. I thought there were some really good scenic views at this particular beach and the way the rock has worn has some unique striations. I've never been to this beach before but it was super peaceful and plenty of room so you don't feel overcrowded like it's some beaches. The speech feels like you go here more for nature than to socialize. Peaceful spot!
I love walking around here especially when I can show up at low tide which exposes tide pools that you can check out as well.

google_reviews.text: This nice little spot was pretty quiet the weekday morning we were there, which also meant we easily found a spot parking along the street at the top of the hill. The beach got pretty busy by lunchtime. Bring water shoes; the tide pools are fun but the rocks hurt my bare feet. I also scraped up my feet playing around in the waves because of the rocks. They make such a beautiful sound when the water moves them in and out with the tide, but -- ouch.

We love the tide pools, they are full of sea creatures. This area is nice and quiet. I like that the bathrooms are close and most of the time clean. Downside is that there are lots of rocks, so when the big waves start coming they tend to pick up the rocks and bring them In as the tides come in, little ones can get injuries.

Our favorite beach in Orange County!

This beach has it all - nice soft sand, comfortable waves you can swim in, rocks and arches for beautiful pictures, and a nice cliff view to get you inspired.

We discovered this place incidentally while on our way to Corona Del Mar. However, we loved it so much that we now go here way more! There’s street parking at the top (which can be difficult to find at times) and then a wide paved walkway to the bottom. Overall a must-visit while you are here in Orange County :)
