The whole place just has a welcoming vibe that makes it a standout spot in the Santa Ana community. Definitely 4 stars!

It's ok, great playground equipment but it's somewhat dirty with a lot of homeless people roaming around. A bit scary to have the playground open to the traffic since there's a lot of little ones. Playground would be better if it was fenced off for kiddo safety. Parking is ok, never had an issue finding parking.

name: Estancia Park
formatted_address: 1900 Adams Ave, Costa Mesa, CA 92626, USA

google_reviews.text: One of the best parks I've ever gone to. There are lots of open spaces and relaxing places that make this place ideal for walking.   There are many different directions and locations that differentiate this park from others. If you walk your dog here, be respectful and clean up. I've encountered many instances where pet owners disregarded, and disrespected the park. The park is humongous and has lots of animals. This is undoubtedly, one of the best parks in California.

I take my dog here to play ball. He has been trained to be off leash or on leash. I never let him off when other dogs are around to be respectful of other dog owners. He's a well behaved well trained boxer. But he is 5 years old and needs to release energy. Because I have rheumatoid arthritis it's hard for me to take him on long distance walks. So a couple times a week I will come here and play ball with him. People are always very friendly and the kids absolutely love him. It's a nice park for the family and for sports.

Awesome place to run around with the kiddos. There's the upper grass, lower grass area and playground by the parking lot. It's great for letting them loose and feel untethered haha bathrooms on location and decent 👍🏽

This park is deceivingly large. Park by the Balearic Community Center to be close to the kids Playground and the soccer fields. Good spot to stop for an hour or so. There is another upper parking lot at the top of the hill that takes you to another large open grassy field.
It is a great place to walk up on the hill. The best thing for me is the old 1800 home. I look at my home and... where is my micro or washer! And you can see something about how they lived.

name: Kings Road Park
formatted_address: 1801 Kings Rd, Newport Beach, CA 92663, USA

google_reviews.text: So gorgeous at sunset! One of my fav hidden gems of newport 🤫🤗
Very nice place with breathtaking views.

Love this park!
Benches and grass nice views. But more for quiet moments. We watched the boat parade here

Small park.
Great view.
Limited street parking.

A beautiful view of Balboa Island and the channel. You can even see Catalina on a clear day. Three benches or the grass to sit down. You can park on the street. Bring a sweater, because it's breezy once the sun starts to set.

name: Edna Park
formatted_address: 2140 Edna Dr, Santa Ana, CA 92706, USA

google_reviews.text:
This park could have been a 3 star, but benefit of the doubt ill go with 4 stars. Parking is easy, and overflow to the street is fine. Well lit, well traveled by those visiting as well as the bike path that run alongside.  It's an official break stop for biker runners joggers walkers... ect. Facilities are available,  and from the most part, are clean. Jungle gym available,  large grass areas, and picnic table are open to the public. Clean sand clean grass clean 95% of the time. Weekends are busy, and many bikers arrive and use as a pit stop. Kidd are many.
The negatives, occasionally you'll see vagrants- just ignore  from time to time you'll see homeless in the restrooms - just ignore and sometimes you must be careful when walking into the bike path - riders are fast... ve careful.
Small park but serves for a great start for a run walk or bike journey
Beautiful Park, nice place to walk, jog or simply relax enjoying the views.

It's a nice little park with plenty of parking. It's more of a pit stop for the bike/walking trail. It has restrooms that are clean most of the time. Downside is you will sometimes see homeless hanging around and inside the bathroom.

Nice little rest spot if you're jogging/biking the river trail. It's adjacent so you do a quick detour. There's a small playground for the locals. No swings though. It has restrooms, but they wreak of urine. They'll do the job though. It has parking, a couple picnic tables, a couple shade spots, and a lot of bike racks.
@Azlan.thebraveheart (IG) loves this place because it's perfectly situated on the bike path.

name: TeWinkle Park
formatted_address: 970 Arlington Dr, Costa Mesa, CA 92626, USA

google_reviews.text: Spacious, well maintained with a large playground with play equipment for toddlers to older children.  Love that they have inclusive swings and wheelchair friendly areas.  My kids also enjoy the lake and squirrels, ducks, geese and other birds.  They have shelters to use to throw parties and decent bathrooms but they are still park bathrooms.

Lots of shade, ample parking. Great spot to set up a picnic and enjoy the lakes and water features. Tons of Geese (and I mean *tons*), Ducks, Squirrels, and a few Turtles, so keep that in mind when you're choosing where to lay your picnic blanket. Lots of winding paths and bridges for lovely walks, rocks for climbing, large grassy areas, athletic fields, picnic areas, restrooms, and a playground.

It is a beautiful park, it has beautiful sighting, lakes, ponds, squirls, ducks, cute small bridges, hills and the playground is perfect for little kids. I have a 21 month old who is so energetic and daring. He isn't afraid of heights, and this playground was perfect for him. I didn't have to be all too cautious and worried. I was able to get on the playground with him. And so did many other parents. The only thing is the restrooms were very dirty and the diaper changer was broken.

Our favorite park in Costa Mesa! Lots of grass, tons of ducks and geese. Nice (huge) playground for the kiddos. Walking pathways that are fun to walk. Lots of trees for shade (great for pick nicks with the family)! One of the few places to see Mandarin Ducks!!

This park is definitely beast mode as far as parks go; skate park, rock climbing hill that's gotta be irresistible to bmxers, sprawling greens the fountain the duck part and the several sports venues stuffed in next to it... All patroned by the surrounding neighborhoods inhabitants giving the feel of a family community even down to the local confections vendor. The music, the laughter, the levity the happiness all add to create a feel that this gem of a park Will be appreciate it by all the generations to come aesthetically pleading to the eye and so much greenery that it seems to stay in a constant state of coolness. I've been to a lot of States, lot of cities and towns. Places seen a lot of parks, but the Tewinkle park should be used as a standard in family entertainment. Bring the ice chest, the football, the dog, and the BBQ slathered staples... Bring a blanket, lay in the cool, soft grass... And breathe in the this grassy knoll, and breathe out the bustle of Costa Mesa.

name: Rosita Park
formatted_address: 706 N Newhope St, Santa Ana, CA 92703, USA

google_reviews.text: A good walk with Luna🐶 & also a good place to workout and chill
Cool fitness station!!!   Another cool place for me to visit for my workouts!! 👍🏽👍🏽

This is the best park to play and do  a lot of cool stuff at this park you get to workout while before playing soccer! The workout station is so nice and clean and you ca do a lot of exercise at this workout station! There is a big field at this park and you get to play baseball and futboll at this park come to this park if you want to play futboll with your friends!
This park brings back wonderful memories that I will always cherish. It’s now even more beautiful and highly recommended for family visits.
Homeless / crazy people drama when I arrived. Have your tazer ready. Where is the grass?  Water the place once in a while.

name: Memorial Park
formatted_address: 2102 S Flower St, Santa Ana, CA 92707, USA

google_reviews.text: I grew up in the 70's playing pop warner,an baseball there,the ball fields look the same bringing back some good memories.im glad to see the kids are still being able to there,seeing how the world these days isn't very nice.
It's a nice place to go with children. The excercise area is pretty cool as well. The community pool is currently open for summer.
Very nice park not just because I used to maintain it back in 1990. This was a pleasure why not mention it!
I love doing Pull Ups here!
Has an excercise section behind those trees.  There is a public pool and a Pokemon Gym.  However, you can find people smoking weed or people drinking every once in a while.

name: Knollcrest Park
formatted_address: Irvine, CA 92603, USA

google_reviews.text: From April through September, this pool is filled with kids in the afternoon,  probably from as young as a baby with diaper to 6th grade. They pee in the water and occasionally I see poo at the bottom and pool side too. From June through July, they have swimming school in the weekday afternoon, and no space for you to swim. Avoid those hours. Of course the water tastes ammonia and strongly salty chemical. I don't know the swimming school is commercial or not, but kids can learn swimming safely with adults, that's good. Just don't pee in the water.
The park is great. Good to spend some time out.

Great park for families.  There is a large lovely well manicured lawn for a picnic or quick game of catch while walking the pup. There are soccer fields at the school next door. Unfortunately there isn't much parking for large gatherings. Bike lanes and no parking zones exist on both sides of Knollcrest and Quail Hill Pkwy. There's also a grill next to 2 picnic tables.

Great park to take kids. My kids and I really enjoy living in the Quail Hill community and one of the reason is the parks like this. It's huge and there are tons of fields everywhere for playing sports or playing with the dogs. Always really well maintained too.

Lovely local playground with space for eating and a nice pool, too. This is a sand playground, and the walking areas have soft rubber flooring. Nice climbing and sliding structure for little and big kids.

name: Heller Park
formatted_address: 257 E 16th St, Costa Mesa, CA 92627, USA

google_reviews.text: Its more of a grassy patch with a playground than it is a "park." My first visit here was about a week ago. I'm aware of the leash laws, just as every other dog owner is. When you have a dog that is obedient and properly trained, unhooking the restraint to allow them the opportunity to run and interact with other dogs doesn't seem as though it would be enough to warrant Costa Mesa's finest to intervene. It hadnt been more than 5 minutes after I arrived, when an officer arrived, drove his vehicle INTO the "park" threatening to arrest all of the dog owners. He stated that we were all being recorded, and if any of us were seen returning in the next 24 hours, we would be arrested. I am unaware as to how often this happens, as this was my first visit, but there is definitely a grouchy individual keeping a close watch on the area, and has CMPD on speed dial. I wonder if the drug users and underage drinkers get reported too.

Smallish Neighborhood Park with a very small children’s playground— a few swings, and a small structure with slide and climbing things. Street parking around the park. Restrooms are available. A few Picnic tables, and a couple of benches. A nice green space for your dog to run around in. Beautiful trees for shade. Convenient location.

We got there around 3:30 pm on a weekday, boy was it crowded! No more tire swing😭 The playground has sand, 2 playsets with monkey bars running in between and about 4 slides. 2 baby swings and 2 regular swings. Restrooms, picnic benches, and a huge grassy area for birthday parties, sports practice, or to bring your furry kids🐶
Nice little Park! Park structure probably should be updated but place always seems to be full of kids and kids playing soccer

First time visiting. Went on a late night stroll on a Saturday night. The area itself is beautiful and quiet. However, the park an odor that was very unpleasant. Maybe it’s more of a dog park because the odor was very bad. I immediately left the area. I will not recommend and I’ll not return to this park.

name: Carl Thornton Park
formatted_address: 1801 W Segerstrom Ave, Santa Ana, CA 92704, USA

google_reviews.text:
Carl Thornton Park in Santa Ana is absolutely beautiful and family-friendly — the perfect place to celebrate a child’s birthday. I hosted my daughter’s party here, and everything went so smoothly. There’s plenty of open space for games and decorations, and the scenery with the lake in the background made for such a peaceful and pretty setting.

We set up near the playground, which kept the children entertained for hours. The play areas are clean, safe, and well-maintained, and there’s a great mix of equipment for different ages. There’s also lots of shaded picnic tables, restrooms close by, and plenty of parking, which made it super convenient for guests.

What I loved most was how relaxed and happy everyone felt — the park has this calm, natural vibe that makes any gathering feel special. Between the lake views, the walking trails, and the open grassy areas, it felt like the perfect blend of fun and nature.

I’d definitely recommend Carl Thornton Park for family gatherings, birthday parties, or even just a sunny afternoon picnic. It’s a beautiful spot that really brings people together.

This is a decently sized park with plenty of parking. There are 2 parts of the park with 2 playgrounds for the kids. There is a basketball court and a new fitness area. A huge lake with lots of ducks, geese, and sometimes rare, unique birds show up. Also turtles will appear every now and then, sunbathing on top of rocks or peaking their heads atop the water lol. I love the sunsets here!
Absolutely Beautiful and Serene!!! Seeing all the different birds enjoying each other’s company was so nice for my kids and I. The park is so well kept. Love it!

Love walking in this park but after the rain it was super muddy, and areas on track and in grass areas are flooded...playground is completely flooded. I would wait until it dries before coming out or if you want to walk around wear shoes you don't care to get muddy and dirty.
Super fun movie at the park.  Thank you to the City of Santa Ana Parks and Rec for hosting!  The Sandlot was the cutest movie, perfect for a summer park showing!

name: Heroes Park
formatted_address: 25420 Jeronimo Rd, Lake Forest, CA 92630, USA

google_reviews.text: Another great park 👍 👌 👏 to practice and watch the baseball ⚾️ games. Nice installations, beautiful fields. Different parking lots around fields. Only need to add restrooms 🚻 around the field as well. Enjoy your visit!
A nice baseball park. Also good for a walk.

Excellent fields for baseball and soccer. Not sure about softball. Batting cages. Easy free parking right by fields. Great views, cement walkway all around the fields, dog friendly with fountains for dogs and people.
Crap.  Reserved this for morning soccer game.  No lines, no goals.  Horrible field conditions.  Bald and patchy grass and overall terrible field.
Beautiful park, in the morning i seen people enjoying a noce walk and exercise runs, but a couple transient guys hanging around not so good. But beautiful park has restroom and plenty of space

name: William R Mason Regional Park
formatted_address: 18712 University Dr, Irvine, CA 92612, USA

google_reviews.text: A gem of a park to visit and stroll around and great for picnics. Watch the many birds and tiny turtles in the lakes. Plenty of restrooms and areas for bigger gatherings and activity areas for children and adult fitness. There is a small entry fee for vehicles which is included in your regional park annual pass if you have one.

Mason regional park is your standard community area for entertainment.  It has a lake thats vibrant and filled with life - fish and ducks... it has a walking and jogging paths that extend for miles. In fact, its two parks.in one. On the east side, the park is mostly for walkers joggers bikers and people looking to just stroll.  The west is mostly for families,  for groups and large scale activities.  Of course the west side has all the restrooms,  playgrounds large fields and is the main entrance.  You have the standard picnic tables, jungle gym, shaded trees, sand courts for volleyball a lake to stroll by, many sections outlined by parking areas and rolling hillsides that complement the park. The east side is coordinated by the turtle rock 🪨 trail system ..its long, curvy and built most.for people trying to do exercise by means of walk jogs and runs. There are a few area for water stations and that for the little critters also. Generally speaking,  if your look for a peaceful ✌️ day, to enjoy a simple park, thats clean safe modern and visited by then local community... this would be it. Overall, I give it a strong 3.5 to 3.75

Negatives... the east side could be developed a bit more... by inputting lakes or exercise stations and or a nature center . Plus, it would help if the east side had a parking site, which it doesn't.

A plus, part of the turtle 🐢  rock trail system on the east side. However,  this area is mostly leveled paths and east walk ways. Continue south for more challenging trails .

I truly enjoy visiting William R Mason Regional Park and spending quality time here with my children. It’s always clean, surrounded by lush greenery, and features a beautiful pond where turtles make their home. The park has great playgrounds for kids and offers a wonderful setting for celebrating birthdays. My only small drawback is the $5 entrance fee.

The park was extremely clean and very spacious and very breath-taking. The views were extremely nice and the people around were extremely respectful of my space. I had a really great time here with my family. I was able to picnic and walk my dogs all around the park. And across the lakes and over the bridge is because the park is so spacious and so nice. I had a great time in the air. Was extremely fresh and I will definitely come back here again even though you have to pay for parking. But that ISO k because parking is extremely cheap. I definitely recommend anyone who is just even passing by 2 just drop by here. Just even for 1 hour or 2 because this is.  A great place and a great place to relax and take a breath from all the hard work.

We absolutely loved this park! We were in the area for a show and stopped here beforehand. It was a beautiful day to walk around, enjoying the lake and nature. Perfect place for taking pictures, having a picnic, playing outdoor games, going for a walk/jog, or just napping in the grass. There are loads of turtle, geese, ducks, and also some frogs. (Please be mindful not to feed them or leave trash, it really isn't good for them.)
There is a small entry fee. The park is wonderfully maintained and we spent a good few hours there, just enjoying the afternoon. I do recommend wearing sunscreen or a hat on sunny days.

name: Pattinson Park
formatted_address: 6200 Palm Ave, Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text:
This park has plenty of space and ♿ parking available and plenty of shade from trees 🌳.

Sitting in a triangular shape the park has maximized its potential. Here you'll find large grassy areas, a 🏀 basketball court, 2 playground areas and a covered picnic table area. Benches and 🗑 trash cans are also scattered throughout.

Both playgrounds rest on rubber padding. One playground suitable for younger children  has some stairs with a could of slides, including one that fits 2 kids together side by side. The larger playground has a suspended bridge, VERY tall slide, pole, ladder and spiral slide.

Plan accordingly there is no bathroom at this park!!
Great park just 1.2 miles from PCH. There is a big jungle gym and a small jungle gym. A nice basketball court, a gazebo for picnics and a good size lawn to run the dog(s), or the kid(s).

Pattinson Park is a brand new park in Huntington Beach that is perfect for families and young children. The park features a playground with all-rubber surface and plenty of open picnic tables throughout the grass area. The park is well-maintained and clean.

The park is located in a quiet neighborhood, just a short walk from the beach. There is plenty of parking and thee park is easily accessible. Pattinson Park is a great place to spend a day with your family.

Pros:

New and well-maintained
Playground with plenty of features for kids
Quiet neighborhood
Close to the beach
Easy parking

Cons:

Small park
Not much shade
No restrooms

Overall, Pattinson Park is a great option for families looking for a new place to play. The park is clean, well-maintained, and has plenty of features for kids to enjoy. The only downside is that the park is small and can get crowded on busy days. However, if you are looking for a quiet and peaceful place to relax with your family, Pattinson Park is a great choice.
One of the better parks for older kids, only negative would be that there is no bathroom
Cute little park for kids to enjoy a clean outdoor space. Also, a decent grass area to play fetch h with your dog. We loved it. Went on a Sunday mid morning.

name: West Newport Park
formatted_address: 5700 Seashore Dr, Newport Beach, CA 92663, USA

google_reviews.text: This park is absolutely amazing for young children to burn off excess energy while having a great time! Very clean, kind people, clean restrooms, and racquetball courts.
A great place for families. Beautiful, clean, big beach, pay parking, bathrooms.

Upscale neighborhood park that’s situated close enough to the beach to offer additional beach parking, restrooms and sports activity areas for visitors. Good place to take the kids in addition to the beach!
If I can’t find street parking, then paid spaces are usually available without getting stuck in a tight spot!
Plus the upscale homes across the street offer a sense of security, as residents may witness potential problems outside their windows!
Nice to bring the kids for a fun day of play at the beach. Clean and easy to get to. Enjoyed the time here and the kids had fun too.

Easy access to and from the beach.  I'd recommend getting to there early to get a parking spot.  Bathroom facilities are well maintained and easily accessible.  Great location and easy to get to.

This park is safe and great to spend time at.  It's also a short 3 minute walk from the surf.

name: Oak Creek Community Park
formatted_address: 15616 Valley Oak Dr, Irvine, CA 92618, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice small park with soccer fields, playground, swings, BBQ area, clean bathroom facilities, with plenty of parking including handicap parking.

Very clean and beautiful place where kids can play multiple sports. Has a playground, shelter, two soccer fields, and separate fields. Bathrooms are well maintained and well cleaned. Shelter has grills and so as the tables located in the park. Great place to play sports and to have a fun time.
This is a great peaceful fun park for all ages

The park itself it nice, but parking during a soccer game is very difficult. Thankfully we found that you can park at the business office next to the park and there is a path that leads in to the park. The soccer fields looked pretty bad from the satellite view on Google Maps - but they have been redone and they were very nice. The grass around the field is still bad...but the fields are good.
A well kept park on a very nice part of Irvine. Was there to watch a youth soccer game. The parking is kinda small, and I didn't see alot of places to eat nearby. Otherwise it was nice.

name: Linda Lane Park
formatted_address: 400 Linda Ln, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text: This park was a great find. I got lost trying to find the pier and turned right too so on and ended up at this park. It's very nice, has monkey bars for the kids, benches to sit on, a great view facing the ocean and a 3 minute walk to the pier. A great place to walk your dogs too.
Great place to bring little kids. My son had a blast at the playground. Then you can walk right to the coastal trail looking at the Pacific.

Great place with children's playground and beach access. Bike and walking trails available. Bathroom and shower near the beach with a nice view to the ocean and train route. Perfect rest area specially if you are driving north or south with children and pets on Freeway 5.

Great place for beginners who want to learn surfing.  Great private area along the beach during the year that doesn't get too crowded.  San Clemente is our 2nd home from Littleton,CO, and this is one of our favorite places along the San Clemente Trail.

Rather small park, with a relatively urban and uninteresting beach. Close walk from the (paid) parking area to the beach. I enjoyed it greatly as an out-of-the-way niche with interesting water drainage pipes and concrete for toy photography, but as a "park" it was a bit underwhelming.

There were a couple of picture tables with an ocean view on a small, neat lawn, and things were clean and well kept overall. Right next to a train track.

name: Goldenwest Park

google_reviews.text: There's a fitness zone with machines for exercise for adults and teens.

Really love this park. Been here multiple times on various days of the week and every time we’ve come here it’s pretty chill & quiet, the playgrounds are clean and it’s in a good area. No problems with homeless or any trouble.
This park was great in the morning I took my son for a while here and he absolutely loved it , I would definitely be coming back here again very nice area

Love this park! It's small but perfect for families with children...don't have to worry bout someone snatching your kids cuz there's only two ways to go and both are visible...for those worried parents out there like me... and it's very quiet and quite beautiful and in a nice neighborhood
Nice little park next to a school. Sometimes I drive over here to let the kids play for a bit. A couple tables are here as well. Very shady. Street parking only. Never seen any rifraff there.

name: Peppertree Park
formatted_address: 230 W First St, Tustin, CA 92780, USA

google_reviews.text: i was surprised to find a performing area for concerts at this park and thought it'd be nice to attend. there were plenty of picnic tables available and space to have your own blanket and enjoy the park. i did not notice any homeless people when i visited. the restrooms were being cleaned at the time of arrival though. there is a huge baseball field as well as a playground for kids here.

Great little park and my granddaughter lives it here. They have interesting things that are different from other parks like falling pellets to resemble rain in a turning wheel. She really loves that. She likes to look at all the pellets fall.  There is a green oblong thing that turns in circles and swings that are made for the parent to sit with the child and it's secure. Definitely will come back here.

I love this place i come here every time I can, take my gran kids, my dogs, just walking around. Is very nice for partying or  just gathering for not reason, but is always crowded I think you have to reserve the canopies because they are always occupied.
Overall I love this Park.

Came here for a park concert. They had security walking around, which is a good thing, but security never did anything about the kids playing kickball.  Balls almost hit my head a few times! Not a safe environment if this is permitted close to where people are sitting, most of those people being seniors.  Had this happened, I'd be suing the city of Tustin. Also lots of large dogs and their owners couldn't keep a handle on the dog. Dog close to me got off its leash more than once and lunged at me. Tustin, having a concert in the park is a great idea, but it is not a safe environment. Kids need to play in one specific area and dogs should not be allowed.

Great senior center, good playground for kids, great summertime schedule in the park.

Bad bathroom upkeep; bad supervision, overly strict, limiting use of facilities, bbq's, picnics, etc... Tell Tustin PD to slow it down and all can relax in the park.

name: Handy Park

google_reviews.text: Cute park with a nice playground for kids. The volleyball courts seem nice too.

Large park with baseball fields, archery, soccer, football, hiking,  running,  biking, trails along 55 perimeter.  Dogs 🐕  have lots of fun. Kids area has huge playground set with swings, slide, hiding area, and much more. Sand is clean and park is very well maintained.  We Always feel safe. Parking lot is private just for thr park. Large area for picnics and full restroom facilities.
Highly Recommend

Nice park with a volleyball court, 2 playgrounds one for big kids and one for smaller kids, and about 3 cgarcoal grills next to the playgrounds. A big area for any ball sports ( soccer.... 3tc)
they have a picnic area with enough tables and seats for a big party.
The problem I encountered is that the restrooms floors were full of "P", and it's very noisy as the park is located @ a location right next to the school and the 55 freeway.
Other than that the place is enjoyable.

Update March 4th 2022 - there are literally two trees to give you shade on one side of the parking and sadly they seem to be cutting it down today. What a shame and for what reason? There are Little Critters that seek shelter in the shade as well.  I'm baffled? Now they are butchering the 2nd tree.

Handy Park is pretty nice.  I have a few times met nice people, as many are walking their dogs like I am.
It's usually not too busy.  It's located at the end of a subdivision.  Bathrooms I think are open till 10 or 11pm.
I only gave 4 Stars for one reason ~  I like to go during the day, there is absolutely no shade in the parking lot except for one small section usually in the morning hours.
Also, there's hardly a tree in the grassy park area itself so no shade to toss a blanket in the grass and enjoy the land.
I haven't seen anybody post up with umbrellas yet😲  (I've been known to be the first to make a stand⛱tho😬 lol lol) by

This is a great playground for toddler's and older kids. Nicely maintained baseball field and Boys & Girls club has summer camp here. We attended the Veterans day event today with a live band/orchestra and hundreds of flags representing our soldiers.

Nice park with an awesome playground that seems brand new.

Great medium sized grass area with a small but awesome playground area. A few trees and some pergola shade thing that’s cool.

No bathrooms, but it’s Costa Mesa so you can probably poop in the corner and no one will bother you.
Started learning yoga there from Mina Hashiguchi!

Quiet, peaceful park located between commercial and residential neighborhoods.  Not too crowded but my experience wasn't very pleasant as I was intruded upon by a small group of foul mouthed teenagers as well as a large, very busy trail of ants battling for my picnic lunch. This park is small and quiet. It’s really a local park as there isn’t much here, but it’s quite pretty. There is a lot of shade, two benches and a drinking fountain, and a playground (no bathrooms).

name: Rossmoor Park

google_reviews.text: Convenient street parking is usually available. I go here for the pickleball courts which are usually full during prime hours (evenings). So it's best to make a reservation. Nice courts and permanent nets. Also there's a water fountain and restroom right next to the courts.

Only have this park 4 stars b/c I have yet to see the inside of the building dead center but gets 5 stars regardless due to the fact they have some very clean men's restrooms! I do feel like these restrooms are hidden from the public because of area it belongs to but nonetheless they are clean and available!

Nice park, seems clean. The grass was very wet and there is fruit squished all over the floor, it was also pretty stinky. The playground is fun. I think I saw a building for bathrooms but I didn't walk over to inspect. Lots of shaded tables and benches.

A nice park in the middle of a bunch of houses. It's a fairly large park, so just watch the kiddos. There are plenty of options for the kids to climb, swing, and run around on. The only downside I can see is that the little kid's play area (2-5) is a bit far away from the big kid's play area (5-12 years old). Also, if you're inclined to fix things, like I am, bring some grease and put it on the squeaky swing (the second one from the left)

Beautiful park with plenty of space for kids to run around, including a playground. Lots of trees for shade. Good place for a birthday party. We visited on a Sunday which was not crowded and had no problem finding parking.

name: Fairview Park
formatted_address: 2525 Placentia Ave, Costa Mesa, CA 92626, USA

google_reviews.text: A fun place to go for a variety of trails. There is lots of benches, trash cans, bathrooms, and picnic areas. I drove there and enjoyed visiting the park with my dog. The other dogs there were nice and pleasant to see.

This is an amazing park for all types of visitors. There is a large open field to do many activities with groups and it is dog friendly. I highly recommend coming here with friends and families alike. The views this place has are great for photos as well. Thank you, and God bless Farview Park!

Lovely park for hiking. Landscape varies from plains to hills but it is considered easy. Parking available as well as restrooms. There is a paved track for cycling inside the park. You can also use your mountain bike to drive on the gravel trails
This is a great hike! It is the popular hiking spot but not overly crowded at all. It's an enjoyable peaceful walk in a well-managed nature environment.

Great time with the family.  Park was very clean. There were people but not crowded at all. The sunset view is beautiful and makes you want to come back again. Definitely a must to go visit with your family or by yourself.  Also great to walk your pets. There are spots where you can have a picnic. Highly recommend bringing child water and oxygen tank if you out of shape.

Give me a like if you find this post good. Thanks

name: Oso Viejo Community Park
formatted_address: 24932 Veterans Way, Mission Viejo, CA 92692, USA

google_reviews.text: Such an amazing park! It has everything you could ask for. Clean and well-maintained sport courts, great outdoor workout equipment, and a huge playground where kids can play for hours. There’s plenty of open space, shade, and seating areas, making it perfect for families and fitness lovers alike. Truly one of the best community parks around!

super fun symphony in the city! They had food trucks and games and even a bulk to ride on! i hade a great time with all my friends and the music was so fun! great opera singers and a few silly songs! the food was good but expensive. great hangout. somebody was writing in the sky!

Love the walking trails here for walking dogs! Bags provided, plenty of trash cans and water drinking fountains. Green space and play structure. Can get pretty busy, but it’s because it’s a great space!

This vast and seemingly never-ending park boasts beautiful scenery, a refreshing breeze, and plenty of shade. The trail includes stairs, hills, and ramps, making it perfect for dogs and both walking and biking.
#weightlossjourney #walkingchallenge
Love this park. Great playground (huge slides and swings), wonderful paths and a great river area. Update! They've done some really nice upgrades recently. Such a gorgeous place

name: Cordova Park
formatted_address: 26931 El Retiro, Mission Viejo, CA 92692, USA

google_reviews.text: Cordova Park in Mission Viejo off Felipe is my kids favorite park in Orange County this year due to the various more exciting playground equipment that children over 8 seem to really enjoy. Most playgrounds in Orange County are pretty tame but this one has quite a few that will spin fast and let you ride pretty high. The field next to it also has a baseball field and volleyball court. There were plenty of park benches and a walking trail for the dog with poop bags. It had beautiful flowers along the park and is overall just a wonderful park to take your family to.
Very nice greenbelt park with trees and flowers. They provide doggy bags by the trash cans to keep this park clean. Lots of friendly people walking there dogs.

I took a short walk to get a look at this park. It has a great playground along with a few picnic tables, benches and trees. Please note: the restrooms sure across the street at the Felipe Recreation Center.

Great local park to take the kids because of it's unique playground activities. There is a small sandbox, swings, spinning ball, jungle gym, a hanging see saw, and a strange zip line thing that doesn't work properly. There are also signs about insects, birds, and plants around the park. There are restrooms across the street at the tennis/pickleball courts.

Straight out the gate, I took 3 stars away because of little to no shade and how hot the playground equipment was in February in 68° weather. If you're from that area, you know how freaking hot Mission gets in the summer. I can't imagine having my kid play there during the warmer months, and it's not really a "little kid" friendly playground. It's definitely a "big kid" playground. 2 stars for the neighborhood being safe and parking is easy.

name: Harbour View Park
formatted_address: 16600 Saybrook Ln, Huntington Beach, CA 92649, USA

google_reviews.text: Great clubhouse to host parties. There's enough space to have an open dance floor, and tables arranged at one end of the venue. Plenty of space for kids to run around, both inside and out.
Great place to take the kids especially after school the only thing that's park needs to make it perfect is a restroom lol

Great park with two playgrounds, lighted tennis courts, a huge field, basketball courts, & handball courts. There are drinking fountains and adequate parking, but no public restroom. (It's somewhat attached to Harbour View Elementary School). There's a nice grassy area with lots of trees that a lot of people walk their dogs in. Very nice, well maintained park!

Nice park with clubhouse to rent, play ground, base ball diamond, side walks, tennis courts , shade , good vibe. Not an 18+ park as noted by another reviewer here. Open to all ages. There is the elementary school that is part of the park. If walking your dog during school hours dogs need to be leashed. On the downside , not many trash cans, open roads run along the southwest borders , grass condition is OK at best , loud during school hours , heavy traffic during school pu and drop off hours. Enjoy + if you bring your pets please clean up behind them
It's a very safe and spacious local neighborhood public park located in front of a school. Although small, it's a great place for the little ones to play and run around.

name: Plaza Park
formatted_address: 610 Paseo Westpark, Irvine, CA 92606, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice park, very safe for younger kids. Climbing stuff for older kids, but no big slides. Ended up having to kill some widow spiders, and they're seemed to be a lot of webs. City needs to spray or hose wood off maybe.

This nearby park is my preferred location for children's activities, parties, and family picnics.  It is characterized by cleanliness, tranquility, and well-maintained landscaping.
There is a close parking lot and restrooms.
Very clean beautiful park. Has 2 playgrounds 4 baby swings and big swings on the other side.  There is a huge grass area with paved trail. Very busy family neighborhood  park. No bathrooms here.
great green space to chill with a lot of shade and a playground for kids
So much green, enjoy the tree and play ground. And most important is baseball and or foot ball

name: Del Mesa Park
formatted_address: 2080 Manistee Dr, Costa Mesa, CA 92626, USA

google_reviews.text: My Daughter lives this place. Clean restrooms.
Good dog friendly Park to stop at before you get on an airplane with your service animal. The park could be lit a lot better. Not much light in the early morning.

Pricker Park! Not taken care of at all! Gopher holes everywhere!!
But when I'm playing with Mannix, all is well.
A small park perfect for a small party it has restrooms and a playground
Small  cute family park. A little windy...nice though

name: George Upton All Access Park
formatted_address: S Linda Way, Santa Ana, CA 92704, USA

google_reviews.text:
We LOVE this park! It’s clean, it’s big, it’s shaded. There are multiple different structures, swings, bathrooms, and a basketball court. It is next to Carl Thornton park, which has another playground and a pond with ducks and geese.

The activities and structures at this park are clean, nothing seems to be broken. It is newer, so the structures themselves are still well kept. There is also a smaller structures for children 2-5 years old.

Beautiful, clean, super cute and fun park. Minus one star for a very small parking lot. The entire park is gated, which makes it extra safe, but it does limit the places where you can park to get easy access to the playground. The neighborhood next-door has some confusing signage in terms of ability to park as certain portions require a permit.
This playground is amazing! My daughter had an absolute blast. We spent two hours there and she still couldn’t get enough. We only left because it was lunchtime. We’ll definitely be coming back!

Updated: The playground's recent renovation has made it a fantastic place for the kids. It’s super clean and filled with fun activities. Has restrooms, tables, plenty of shade, and even a basketball court, it has everything you need for a great day out! On the other side of Thornton Park, you'll find another renovated playground, an outdoor workout area, and a scenic spot to fish and watch ducks, turtles, and birds by the lake.

If you have children (especially younger ones) and are looking for a playground that combines safety, accessibility, and good amenities, George Upton All Access Park is a strong recommendation. It’s especially notable for inclusive play (children with mobility challenges or special needs can participate alongside other kids). The connection to the larger Carl Thornton park means there’s room to extend the outing beyond just the playground. there’s a woman that lives in a camper on site. Her name is Katie and she is wonderful. She keeps the park very clean. My kids really enjoy this park and we can spend hours here.

name: Springdale Park
formatted_address: 23572 Springdale, Aliso Viejo, CA 92656, USA

google_reviews.text: My son really liked it here. It has a lot of open space, picnic tables, shade, and nice playground equipment.  Oh, and it has a pretty good view of the baseball field. 🤫 It wasn't overcrowded. Seems like this is a little hidden gem tucked away in a neighborhood.

Great open space for families and gatherings.  Been here twice and had bounce houses and dog friendly activities.  The bounce house were private however the space is open to all.  The play space has plenty of options for little kids and big kids alike
Nice park, no restrooms, plenty of climbing, kids call it "Spiderman" park
Covered playground with sand. Covered tables. Covered swings. Unique nets for climbing. No restroom. Very little parking.
Nice park, no bathrooms.. lots to climb, kids call it " spiderman" park

name: Heritage Park
formatted_address: 4812 W Camile St, Santa Ana, CA 92704, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful place to bring the pups or just to picnic and rest.
HARDER THAN HELL TO FIND THE 1 ENTRANCE IN,  BUT WORTH IT WHEN YOU DO. SERENE,  WELL KEPT,  VERY MELLOW. IT WAS EASIER GETTING BACK OUT 🙏
A good walk with Luna🐶 & also a good place to workout and chill

There is a new work out station which is still very nice and has not been vandalized/tagged all over. Though it would be very nice if they would fix the plumbing since ANYTHING THAT GETS FLUSHED (YUCK!!) COMES UP out of the ground less than 100 yards away from that spot.  Shows how lazy the gardeners/maintenance workers are.  There is no possible way they can't see or smell it when they mow once a week! What a waste of taxpayers money paying their salary between that and the filthy restrooms!!!

IT IS SO CLOSE TO MY HOUSE 🏠 I love it its  kid friendly and dog friendly 😊  wow it even has tennis classes and courts and the same with basketball it has pond wif fountain!?! ⛲️ 😀 😊 🙂 😄 👍 ⛲️ 😀 😊 🙂 😄 👍 irvine high n Greentree close by.

name: La Veta Park
formatted_address: 3705 E La Veta Ave, Orange, CA 92867, USA

google_reviews.text:
Love this park. It is small but has enough rides to suit different ages. There is plenty benches fir parents to use. Picnic tables with BBQ grills. It's also has covered area with tables. Bathrooms which are a plus when you are with kids. The most important it has a sun cover over playground so it is great to come even when it's hot.
Tge only negative is parking. The only parking is on the street next to the park.

The park is big enough for jogging, running or to play soccer with the family, they have covered picnic area with enough tables for a big party, there are charcoal grills all over the park and at good distance from each other.  The problem I see in this park are that the bathrooms looks very dirty and there're no doors for privacy. It looks like a bathroom from a horror movie. There was Flys flying around the toilet. It needs a deep cleaning. There's not enough parking and la veta is a busy street. Other than that if you don't mind the restrooms and the parking it's ok.
Great park. Small but fulfill its purpose. Restrooms are always decent they are also conveniently located.
Nice clean park.  Large playground to entertain the kids.  I was a guest from out of town attending a baby shower.  They have a nice covered picnic area with tables.  Quiet neighborhood.

Nice big park, however, there are quite a few homeless, addicts, etc. that like to hang out there, mostly when the sun has gone down and it's dark out, and they just kinda make you feel quite uncomfortable because they all know one another, and then a stranger comes around and you know no one there, and vice versa......I don't ever wanna feel like I'm a stranger, who's in danger, at a public park... especially if I have nephews, nieces, etc with me...

name: Centennial Park
formatted_address: 3000 W Edinger Ave, Santa Ana, CA 92704, USA

google_reviews.text: This a wonderful little park. Very easy walking trail to loop around more than once. Pretty little lake. Very pet and kid friendly.  Parking is not bad.
Pretty nice expansive park with lots of wildlife! 3 separate playgrounds for small kids. Plenty of picnic areas and restrooms as well.
They have cleaned up the park it is beautiful and not stinky. They even try keeping up with cleaning off the sidewalks , a few too many geese, hope they can relocate some.
Beautiful park with a lovely pond, cool skate park, lots of places to picnic.

Came for the Alltrails walking track around the lake. The park has this centralized lake, shady areas with lush green trees, seats and tables to rest/snack, restrooms, many bins for trash and recyclables. Some other features are kids' playgrounds, basketball courts and soccer fields.

Do not feed the animals.

A myriad of birds come with scads of litter. If you don't particularly enjoy that or the stench of algae, the lake wouldn't offer you a great experience.

name: Heritage Community Park
formatted_address: 14301 Yale Ave, Irvine, CA 92604, USA

google_reviews.text:
I had such a fun time at Heritage Community Park in Irvine! I went with a few friends and we played a really great game of pickleball — the courts were in awesome shape and plenty of space to move around. It’s such a friendly, welcoming park where you can really enjoy being active outdoors.

Besides the pickleball, the park has nice open areas, shaded spots, and walking paths, so it’s easy to just hang out and relax after a game. The vibe is super casual and inviting — perfect for a fun afternoon with friends or family. I definitely left feeling energized and happy, and I can’t wait to go back for another round of pickleball soon! 🏓☀️
Heritage park has the most facilities for us to enjoy family time. Plus the animal friends are quite cute and unique.

This beautiful park is a great place for families, it has a very large palyground for toddlers and bigger kids, there is also a water features (motion activated) super fun, our daughter LOVES IT! The playground is fun, and very well kept. There's basketball courts for adults and a small court for kids. Overall this is a great place to spend some family time. Our favorite part is the giant lake in the middle, it's full of ducks, turtles, geese, etc. The park also features lots of parking spaces. Fun place to visit

Nice park to walk around the lake. Also a good place to play sports like basketball, tennis, pickleball etc. Also have a kids playground area. Good to have a picnic with family, as it caters to every age group. One minor inconvenience is at times, the pathways are full of duck poop.

About three weeks ago, I had the chance to watch several concert bands from high schools in our district, and I was honestly so impressed with this park near the place. The ducks left a strong impression on me. It was such an enjoyable experience, and I’m really glad I got to see it.

name: Hart Park
formatted_address: 701 S Glassell St, Orange, CA 92866, USA

google_reviews.text: I was there to photograph the American Legion Baseball Tournament with a friend. The baseball field is really nice, the bleachers good and strong and look well maintained. The park has a lot of beautiful trees. The parking is in a wash basin. A very nice place.  ;)

We were there to watch college baseball. There is plenty of bleacher seating. The weather was quite warm so it was nice to have mature tree to sit under to get out of the sun.
The bathroom was clean with several rolls of tp.

Hart Park is a great park. It has a swimming pool open during the summer, a volleyball court, horseshoe court, multiple baseball fields, and a football field. It has a lot of barbecue grills and picnic tables around the park. The parking is also huge.

The baseball diamond is a true hidden gym. You wouldn't know from the main street it was even there. I've driven past the park dozens of times. I see why it's the home field for Orange Lutheran baseball. Great facility with wonderful amenities. Parking is limited for game day so arrive early.

I grew going to this park. Never really felt the need to complain but this visit was bothersome.
It would be nice to see a park employee address the homeless hanging out at the playground and bathrooms and begging for food from the people that paid to use the park. We had my grandson's 1st birthday there and we paid to use the tables and it was extremely uncomfortable watching one guy fighting the leaves 6 feet away from us and another guy asking my friends and family for food.
Oh, we absolutely loved stepping in the dog crap in the grass by the playground and then finding several piles throughout the area.

name: Tustin Heritage Park
formatted_address: 2350 Kinsman Cir, Tustin, CA 92780, USA

google_reviews.text: Great hidden park nice and shaded and rolloer hockey rink
Great park, kid friendly, clean, hockey rink, basketball courts, clean restroom, picnic tables, everything you need for a funday at the park
A nice shaded spot for kids to run around! We really enjoyed the basketball courts, too!

We love this park! There are lots of shade trees and benches to sit on. My daughter is almost 4 and the main play structure, which is pirate themed, is perfect for her without me being too fearful of her falling. There are three separate play areas, one for bigger kids, one for little ones and a very small sand area  as most of the play area is wood chips.

It’s definitely a little hidden gem in Tustin that your kiddos would love.
Great inline hockey rink. It's not too worn out or busy. I highly recommend playing here for a pickup game, shooting, or skating around.

name: Lions Park
formatted_address: 570 W 18th St, Costa Mesa, CA 92627, USA

google_reviews.text:
This park is legit!! Definitely stop by.

Clean bathrooms (especially if you use the ones inside the library — yes, there’s a library 😄). There’s a kids’ area inside, and honestly as an adult, it’s clutch when it gets too hot outside because you have a nice indoor space to cool off.

Outside, there are cool zip-line style swings, play areas for both younger and older kids, and plenty of shaded spots. Major bonus points for no woodchips — huge win.

The library also has fun music exploration activities, which was a pleasant surprise. Plus, there are easy food options nearby, so you don’t have to overthink meals.

Parking was easy for me, though I can see it getting tricky during busier times. And to top it off, there’s a nice boba spot close by, which always seals the deal 🧋

Great family-friendly park with thoughtful details — we’ll definitely be back.

It's a really fun playground for kids of all ages. It has a big slide, seasaw, obstacle course, and zip line. If you have little toddlers, make sure to keep an eye on them all the time so they don't run into the zip line path. It can be unsafe for the little kiddos. There was plenty of parking and it wasn't crowded when we visited. Definitely recommend this playground.

One of my favorite Costa Mesa parks. There’s lot for kids to do and pleasant to take a lunch and have the kids run around. I love the musical instruments and swings. The kids (sometimes teens) line up to use the zip line swings on the play structure so make sure your kids don’t crash into the zip liners. Kids can also enjoy climbing up and crossing the big bridge. We love to end the park day in the library. Lots of books and cute little reading nooks for the kiddos to enjoy a book.

This is an excellent park.  My kids were really playful during more than two hours.  They really enjoyed the sliding swing.  Also, there is a huge plane where kids can pretend they are flying.  There is a double swing that is very safe, cause kids can be seated comfy.  I give it a 8 out of 10.

A uniquely themed park around airplanes with clear sightlines to SNA take offs out towards the ocean. Bouncy rubber covers most of the park with a small sand pit area. 3 restrooms - used one that was less than ideal for family or changing a baby diaper and couldn't see any hand wash sinks. Not very shaded either save for one covered seating area on the far side.

The main attractions are the swinging zip line and full scale jet to climb around. The zip line is a lot of fun, but requires adult help to get back up to the top and careful supervision to avoid collisions along the path. There is a marked area on the ground but no physical barrier so it was a bit nerve wracking having younger unaware kids wandering through the area.

Overall a decent park worth visiting once.

name: El Camino Real Park
formatted_address: 400 N Main St, Orange, CA 92868, USA

google_reviews.text: El Camino Real Park is a great park for kids and adults. Great for birthday parties, family and or friend's gatherings. This park has hand ball, baseball fields and a large playground area for kids. This park also has plenty of restrooms and it even has a small club house for rent for parties. Finally this is a great place to take your pet(s) for a walk. If you have not been to this park yet, I highly recommend that you stop by.

This is a pretty good park. There are basketball courts, tennis courts, four baseball fields, handball courts, and a volleyball court. There is a good amount of parking. There is a good-sized playground. There is a good amount of picnic areas and barbecue grills. This park is full of activity, with a lot of people active. The park is also pretty clean. This is a good park to go to any day of the week.
I really enjoyed this park I walk my dogs here it's very clean and safe and.
Nice park, they even got a small party hall you can rent out.  Only issue is the occasional homeless person walking around the park.   Gotta take care of your kids.
For the kids playground, I wonder when the city will remove that dirty and nasty wood chips and replace with a cleaner material.

name: Tustin Sports Park
formatted_address: 12850 Robinson Dr, Tustin, CA 92782, USA

google_reviews.text: This is an excellent public park. The restrooms are well maintained. The park is free of litter. It is a very safe space for children to play around. I did not see any graffiti. There are plenty of fields. I brought my nephew here for an event ran by the OCSC, the professional soccer team from Irvine. My nephew had a great time and thoroughly enjoyed the experience.

We love this park!

It’s especially great because there’s plenty of parking and fairly clean bathrooms. It’s even better when the on site bagel shop (Bageled) is open!

There’s everything from swings, sand box, climbing structures, patterns, and there’s some ADA friendly equipment as well.

Pro tip: come here during the weekdays before 2pm to avoid the weekend and after school crowds.

Tustin Sports Park exceeded our expectations! 🌟 The facilities were top-notch, with well-maintained fields and courts for sports, and a super fun playground for the kids. We loved the picnic areas with shade and BBQ grills - perfect for a family outing. The walking trail was a great bonus, and the staff were friendly and helpful. Clean, green, and full of activities - we'll definitely be back!
Nicw size but not too big but it is under shade and that is nice.  Many benches are in the shade too which is nice for parents.  There is even a little cafe.

The tennis courts has become a private court here now because of Lock & Roll. I'm a Tustin resident paying taxes in this City and the only court availability here is only 1 for public most of the afternoons where almost everyone is out of school and work. I play with my kid too, not against the program I'm all for it but this is selfishness. I have to go to Irvine and pay for clubs just to get access to tennis courts. Ridiculous! City of Tustin do something about this!!!

name: Ridgecrest Park
formatted_address: 25354 Eastwing, Aliso Viejo, CA 92656, USA

google_reviews.text: I have been coming to this park for over 4 years ever since I came here. There's a  basketball court, tennis courts, a big playground, and a sidewalk that's about a quarter mile all around the park. On the sidewalk, there's outdoor gym equipment for exercise. In this park, you could also host birthday parties or events at the shaded tables near the field area. You could even play football or soccer on the field. Finally, the bathrooms are surprisingly clean considering that its a bathroom park. Overall, I definitely recommend this park for anyone of all ages.
Beautiful park, fresh breeze all year round, especially in hot summer days. Has nice picnic areas, kids play ground, basketball courts, tennis courts, soccer fields. Dog friendly!
Beautiful part. Playground has a huge shaded  area safe to play anytime of the day.

Path to walk and run, 2 tennis courts, a basketball court, swings for kids, exercise areas (like parallel bars, push-up bars etc.) around the path, nice views. You'll find a lot of rabbits too. There is also parking and restrooms.
Our grandson is playing soccer. Like the rest of the parks we've been to in this beautiful city, parking is impossible to find, not to mention the restrooms.

name: Farquhar Park
formatted_address: 898 12th St, Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text:
Farquhar Park is a nice sized (3.52 acre) neighborhood park right off Main Street. It was parking on 2 of the 3 sides at no cost and backs up to another park named Lake Park which is also the 1st park in HB.

🚻🚫No restrooms so plan accordingly if you don't live nearby

🐕 This park is ideal to bring your dogs to!!

📷 perfect for taking pictures

Lots of benches and tables for picnics!!!

It is a great park for it's purpose!!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We grabbed our pizza from the crazy (but iconic) Main Street — The Ria never misses! 🍕✨ Took it to go and headed to Farquhar Park where Majestic Mika got to run wild and free off-leash with other friendly pups 🐶🐾. We met some really kind people, chilled at the clean cement picnic tables, and soaked in the peaceful vibes under the majestic shade of the old trees 🌳😌. It was so relaxing — just the perfect little lunch date with my man and my fur baby. 💕 Highly recommend for a chill afternoon with good food, good company, and dog-friendly fun! 🐕🌿

One of my favorite parks in HB! Love the views, sea breeze, proximity to all things cool what I don’t like about it are the lack of restrooms,misbehavior of dogs as the community tries so hard to make it into a dog park with canines running around with no leash. Lastly since  there use to be a lake at the adjacent park there seems to be a lot of hungry mosquitoes in the summer

Lovely park. There are kids. If you bring your dogs, keep it a safe, comfortable, and respectful place for all--please take good watch of them and/or have them on a leash. Many times, I've seen dogs run around and chase children that are scared of dogs, and the owners do nothing. Parents are rightfully upset, as it is not legally a dog park.

Love this park so much! The trees are so big and beautiful and provide a lot of nice shade. There are tables and trash cans so picnicking is v easy.  People love to bring their dogs here too. Great area.

name: Bluebird Park
formatted_address: &, 90 Coralwood, Irvine, CA 92618, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice clean park fun for most ages. Has a sandbox and is pretty quiet.
Nice small park with swings and slides for kids all age (3+). The sand adds another fun stuff for kids to do. There are picnic benches and trash cans which makes it easier with kids.
Relaxing walks in the sun, in the Nature, flowers, trees, singing birds and friendly neighborhood.
Small park for the kids to play
Nice quiet small park

name: Santiago Park
formatted_address: 600 E Memory Ln, Santa Ana, CA 92705, USA

google_reviews.text: It was a great place to walk around and bird watch. They have a park for kids as well as tennis courts and an archery range. I had a great overall experience and will most definitely continue going back.

It was very nice and gives you a feeling of serenity! We walked our dog, Snow, with my husband & I. We both thought that the park had a nice sized doggie play area. Good for kids & family gatherings as well as lite exercising.
I love the upgrades, the kids are is awesome, and the trails are so clear and clean. Playground is much better now and seems safer too.

Great park, very relaxing place and great place to get in touch with nature, feels like a real woodland area that you would find in the country, yet right in the heart of the city. City does truly an amazing job of cleaning and maintaining this big park. It runs along the Santa Ana creek there are however limited restroom facilities one restroom is located across the street from the discovery cube. The next open working restroom is located in Hart Park next to the 22 fwy the total extreme opposite end of the park. Again as mentioned by others the need for bathrooms here is a real issue. Unless you don’t mind people doing that right out in public or in the bushes. I mean they are not going to soil themselves so that’s the only other alternative. Otherwise this park is amazing and I highly recommend it to families and the kids will love it. Pets too i witnessed many people walking dogs and there is room to exercise.

I’m taking an trip down E Memory Lane to the Santiago Park in Santa Ana! It’s located near the big business of Orange County, Main Place Mall and Discovery Cube O.C.
This beautiful piece of stream fed urban oasis land provides a quick getaway for the business community and families of the county seat.
It’s a good sized park extended from the Santiago Creek Wildlife and Watershed natural area.
It’s designed as a natural place with the amenities of a common city park. Bike riders and hikers can get a scenic passage on park trails and have a picnic at the tables on the lawn and under the trees!
Lawn bowling, tennis and baseball play are provided for, with the courts and baseball field.
The west end of the park is under redevelopment, including a playground, pathways and restrooms. I’ll look foreword to the opening of this new addition with an update!
Parking is accessible off of E Memory Lane and Santiago Street.
The outer fringes of the parking lots may by inundated with vehicular hang-out’s, tho step(s) side into the lots are usually more associated with actual park users.

name: Santiago Hills Park
formatted_address: 8040 E White Oak Ridge, Orange, CA 92869, USA

google_reviews.text:
Good suburban neighborhood park, nestled within a residential community on the east side of Orange! It’s a relatively midsized park, tho has advantages of larger parks!
There’s a good amount of public parking and lots of open space for field sports. I like all of the wide, paved pathways around the fields, great for walks and fitness jogs! The basketball court is well maintained. There’s also a sand volleyball court. Both courts include good nets.
There’s plenty of picnic tables and grills.
There’s public restrooms, water fountains and trash containers for our convenience.

Rented the pavilion through the city office. Beautiful park, beautiful day. Was able to bring two 6ft tables with chairs since the 3 concrete tables weren't enough, and they still fit under the shade of the pavilion. Here's some helpful measurements for anyone else renting! Table measurements are 95"Lx35"W; there's 16 ft between posts; distance from ground to outlets is 68-70in.

This is one of our favorite family parks. It has a playground, basketball court, sand volleyball area and a big baseball field. Bathrooms are always clean and have soap. Good amount of picnic tables and trees.
Basic affordable, otherwise great. BYO pillow if you're picky.

Perfect view and food.
Highly recommend.

name: Sunset Ridge Park
formatted_address: 4850 West Coast Hwy, Newport Beach, CA 92663, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice park with a beautiful view of Newport and the Pacific Ocean. Its a nice small loop with a clean bathroom by the playground. And the field was really green. Dogs must be on leash but lots of places for them to smell and ours were happy doing a few loops.

One of the best parks in Newport Beach. Be warned that if you do not live in walking distance from here, it is a mission to get to this park. It is located off of Pacific Coast Highway and superior. This park is a great place to play soccer, take kids to the park, and get your exercise on with their stairway on Superior. Definitely a great place to take visitors and be outside.
It is a beautiful park! I had a great experience going there! I would 5/5 recommend going!

This is a very beautiful park. Right by the beach.   It's quite and regardless of the weather it's a great place to go walking,  jogging,  or just unwind.  There was a group playing soccer, some riding their bikes,  and some people walking their dogs.  It's super clean definitely a place to visit more often.

All I can say is wow, what a gorgeous breath taking view of Newport Beach. Also looks like a great soccer field for honing your skills. The park is within walking distance to numerous shopping, restaurants and of course THE BEACH.....

name: Centennial Park
formatted_address: 14722 Devonshire Ave, Tustin, CA 92780, USA

google_reviews.text:
I love this place! Centennial Park is another hidden gem in Tustin, tucked away in a quiet residential area that many people might overlook. However, once you discover it, you'll be glad you did!  this park is a great place to visit especially for families. One of the standout features of this park are the 5 different playgrounds, each designed for different age groups. Whether you have toddlers or older kids, there's a play area that will suit their needs and keep them entertained for hours :)
There are plenty of other amenities such as the BBQ stations, clean bathrooms, large green area perfect for picnics, outdoor meals, running, biking, volleyball, basketball, or just playing catch, there's room to do it all.

Another great feature of this park (one of my favorite) is its dog-friendly policy. It is a great spot for dog owners to take their pets out for a run or a walk, and there are plenty of doggie bag stations to help keep the park clean. It's great place to take your dogs for an early morning walk. I take mine several times per week. I love it

Great commnuity park. It waa closed from July 2024 it just oponed July 2025. New playgrounds, some strenght training sectuon. Multiple playgrounds. Peaceful park. My favorite place to walk and take kiddos.
Love all the new fun stuff to do!
Nice park, kept clean, flat paved trails. Quiet on a weekday morning. Several different small playgrounds
Awesome for dogs, grill on a Sunday afternoon, walk the trails. Amazing

name: Cypress Grove Park

There’s a fun playground and courts for volleyball, basketball, and baseball. It’s easy to watch the kids while they play, and it’s not too crowded. A nice spot to relax and let the kids burn off energy.

Very close to Cypress village elementary school, this park has better kids play area than other nearby parks. They have a limited car parking space but most people park on road. There is court for big kids too. Neatly paved walk space is good for adults and kids

This park is very good and safe for the kids. A lot of trees, and large playground. Parking is not so big.
First time here and definitely a nice small park

name: Irvine Terrace Park

google_reviews.text: Lovely little Park a little bit for all the kids. Nice playground to choose from and a nice little seeing area for adults. Big huge grass area!

Beautiful Park! Green grass, good playground, picnic tables are clean and spacious. The washrooms are clean, track around the grass is nice.
What a great find!  Would never know it was across the street from the fashion Island properties.

Mom went to Newport Center for shopping, and Dad took the children to exercise, play and have a picnic in this park. You can play tennis, football, and baseball. The bathroom is relatively clean and parking is on the roadside. The facilities and equipment for children to play are also relatively complete. All in all very good. You can see the sea, which is great!
About what you’d expect in the land of G wagons and Range Rovers. Well manicured and just a great place to take kids.

If you have really little kids, avoid this park. There is a small play structure, but if your kid is anything like mine they will go straight for the gigantic play structure that will give you an instant panic attack. It has extremely steep stairs and several openings on the sides that they can easily fall from. Aside from that, it's really clean and in a quiet area with an ocean view.

name: Camino Real Park
