google_reviews.text: This park is nice with large grass area and fun playground. There is also a basketball court. But you have to park by the street and there was some trash in the playground.
Great place! Clean community park with all the amenities BBQ, Clean restrooms, party area, benches, basketball court, play  are for kids, large grass area.. quite little spot

People were friendly and the park is dog friendly. Decently clean bathrooms, good-sized playground, basketball court and lots of grass for activities. Kids were getting restless during a long car ride during traffic and found this life-saving gem on the map.

Beautiful and big place for young and old!
Kids always enjoy coming here,

Came here for a birthday party.  The park is located in a residential area.  It was clean and well kept. Perfect place to bring your family to enjoy.  Playground equipment seemed to be well maintained and appropriate for small children. One picnic shelter, perfect for a small gathering.

name: Edison Park
formatted_address: 21377 Magnolia St, Huntington Beach, CA 92646, USA

google_reviews.text:
Edison Park and Community Center is a large 39.69-acres park settled across the street from Edison High. There is accessible parking, a community center building, a playground area and a handful of amenities surrounded by a grassy area.

🚻 Restrooms

🐕 Bring your dogs

🧺 Benches for picnics or sitting.

🏞 Large Grass areas

🌳 Many Trees for shade

🚗 Parking on the street around the park or in the lots

💧 Water Fountain

🗑 Trash bins

🎾 Tennis Courts

🏀 Basketball Courts

⚾️ Baseball Diamonds

⚽️ Soccer Field

🚲 Trail or path thru the park

🛝 4 Playground Units

The playground area is rather large sand area with rubber matting at the landings of the 2 newest playground units, with swings! There are 2 for infants and 2 full sized swings.

The older red/ blue/ beige playground structures are surrounded by sand and intended for ages 2-5 and another for 5-12 years old. Both structures offer slides and stairs to run along. The playground structure intended for 5-12 years old offers some climbing ladders, monkey bars, 2 slides, an off-center spiral pole and Fireman Pole. There is also swings with 2 for infants and 2 full sized swings.

Then there's the teal/ beige/ red/ blue playground structure on a rubber landing for 5-12 year olds. It offers some climbing ladders, monkey bars, 2 slides, an off-center spiral pole and Fireman Pole.

There are many events held here including groups like the local Friends of Edison Park group which meets to keep the park tidy from volunteer efforts. Despite their efforts the park continues to need update and servicing and there tends to be vagrants living here. Tred carefully as you go.

Otherwise its a great place to visit and get involved with the many offerings of the community! Check out HB Sands for more on what's available...
Friendly pick up pickleball every day. Go before noon. Some trash and bums. Clean toilets.

Definitely good for teens, skaters, and people who are home-challenged.

There's plenty of space to fly a kite or race RC cars.  Not a bad area for outdoor space, especially older kiddos.

If you are looking for a playground-kid-focused type of park to take 7 to 12yr old children, try Bauer Park, just around the corner 0.4mi away.

Edison Park is a bright idea!

Great park with lots of well manicured landscape. You can sit on a beautiful bench along the sidewalk under a whispering willow tree that softly dances in the wind.

Great for pets with plenty to sniff. Conveniently placed pet poop bags and disposal stations add to the parks pristine grounds. A great place for casual canine socializing.

Feeling active? Have sone energy to burn? Want to practice you skills? Edison Park has an abundance of opportunity! Several baseball diamonds, tennis courts, handball, lots of basketball courts, play grounds for the kids.

Picnic beneath the mighty shade trees. Enjoy the day out doors!

Another good, good sized park in the City of Huntington Beach!
The large city park has just about everything. There’s a community center with restrooms. There’s tennis, handball and basketball courts. There’s playgrounds, a skatepark, baseball fields, and plenty of open green space to play an impromptu game of football or soccer.
This park for it’s size seems to be lacking unsheltered picnic tables. The few roof covered spots tend to get taken on busy days, so additional shade tree or open space tables may be in short supply. There are park benches however, tho it may be preferable with some to just plop down here on the grass!
Also, for the popularity of the skatepark at the times I’ve seen it, there was a lack of bowl space that could’ve been dug out for more experienced skaters.
Parking is available along Hamilton Av or in either of two lots off of Magnolia Street.

name: Peninsula Park
formatted_address: 100 Main St, Newport Beach, CA 92661, USA

google_reviews.text:
We stumbled into Gaucho’s. It was awesome. I’ve been to Argentina many times before and I can tell you the food is authentic, the empanadas were awesome.
Service was friendly yet chaotic. It seems like nobody staffs restaurants properly anymore. Shame walk!

A Scenic Gem in Newport Beach
If you’re looking for the quintessential Southern California beach experience, Peninsula Park is a must-visit. I recently spent some time here, and it truly earns a full five stars for its perfect blend of relaxation and recreation.
The first thing you’ll notice is the incredible atmosphere. The park is situated right against a wide, clean stretch of sandy beach with stunning views of the Balboa Pier. The videos I took really don’t do justice to how beautiful the water looks when the sun hits it—it’s like a field of diamonds.
Why It’s Great:
Family & Pet Friendly: There is a fantastic playground area for kids and plenty of green grass for a picnic or a game of soccer. It's a very dog-friendly spot, making it a favorite for locals and visitors alike.
The Views: Whether you’re looking out at the pier, watching the waves roll in, or admiring the palm-lined horizon with the mountains in the distance, every angle is a photo op.
Convenience: The park is well-maintained with clear signage and easy access to the beach. Having the pier nearby means you’re never too far from a quick snack or a scenic walk over the ocean.
Whether you're coming here to let the kids burn off some energy at the playground or you just want to sit on the grass and watch the surf, Peninsula Park offers a peaceful yet vibrant escape. It’s the kind of place that reminds you why the California coast is so iconic. Highly recommended for a sunset stroll or a full day of family fun
Great place with paid parking and not too crowded (Thanksgiving weekend). Beautiful beach especially at sunset. Restrooms, playground, restaurants and dessert shops…we really enjoyed.

Very beautiful beach and park, with areas where children can play. The sea is gentle and has small waves. The pier is nice to walk and do some fishing. Parking is charged by the hour or full day and restrooms are free and are in good shape. I would definitely recommend spending a beach day here.
At the end of the pier you have Ruby’s to grab a bite. Amazing, indeed

A great spot for kids! We biked here most days while on vacation, my kids (2.5 and 1) loved the variety of play structures and the sand was nice to play in toys with. Appreciated the public restrooms right there too with showers! Right on the other side of the berm of the ocean, can go back and forth to water and play. Lots of benches to sit on as well. Only complaint was lots of trash in the sand (especially cigarette butts) in the sand and the “bouncy boat” was closed one of the days due to a broken spring. One morning they were doing tractor work on the berm, which was fun to watch for little boys too.

name: Mile Square Regional Park
formatted_address: 16801 Euclid St, Fountain Valley, CA 92708, USA

google_reviews.text: This park is well known in the community that has everything, from Boys & Grils Club, tennis courts, basketball inside & outside courts, golf course, outdoor amphitheater, picnic areas, soccer courts, baseball courts, and so forth.  Plenty of parking lots and street parking.  Nearby restaurants, markets, shops, hospital and freeway.  They got it all in one place.  Also they do have differ fairs throughout the years and lessons too.

Easily one of the most spacious parks in Orange County. So many activities here. Great for families and parties. Running, walking, biking, swan peddle boat rentals, tandem bikes w/cover rentals (2 and 4 seaters), golfing, a small fishing area, lots of grassy areas overlooking beautiful lakes where I’ve practiced yoga and acroyoga with friends. A few bridges to walk across and enjoy the ducks and geese. A major playground was revamped. There is an archery club. Multiple basketball courts. A wedding and special events club as well. There are plenty of exercise amenities scattered throughout the park. Plenty of areas for special sporting events like soccer and baseball. There are some quaint, hidden trails to explore. Restrooms are ok. The park extends from Euclid south to Warner, then west to Brookhurst, north to Edinger, then east back to Euclid.

There are some further renovations going on right now so you may see a lot of orange construction zones up.

Mile Square Regional Park, named for its nearly perfect square shape with each side measuring approximately 1 mile, where you can find your own tranquility amidst the city. Half of the park's area is an 18-hole golf course, making it a pleasant place to walk. The park has two lakes, Palm Island Lake is near Freedom Hall offering swan boat rentals, kayaks, and 1-4 person surrey bikes and single bicycles.

Fishing is allowed here, but anyone aged 16 and above must have a fishing license as uniformed personnel patrol the area will check for it.

The park is also home to many ducks that are remarkably unafraid of people, but be cautious while walking around the lake or on the grass to avoid stepping on duck droppings.

The grassy areas are perfect for picnics, and usually after 4PM, many people jog or bike along the park's trails. A full loop around the park, or a walk by the lakes can easily help me reach over 10,000 steps per day and I really enjoy strolling around here.

Restrooms:
There are public restrooms within the park and they are clean.

Parking:
I parked along Edinger Ave but you can park inside the park.

There are some good restaurants nearby in Fountain Valley Town Center.

📍 Mile Square Regional Park
16801 Euclid St, Fountain Valley, CA 92708
Been going here from the age 5 and I'm 45 , absolutely love this park!

Mile Square Regional Park is a fantastic place to enjoy the outdoors! The park is spacious, clean, and well-maintained, with plenty of walking trails, sports fields, and picnic areas. It’s perfect for families, exercise enthusiasts, or anyone looking to relax in nature. The staff is friendly, and the park offers a peaceful escape while still being close to the city. Highly recommended for a fun and refreshing day outdoors!

name: Sweet Shade Neighborhood Park

google_reviews.text: One of the best playgrounds in Irvine. Lots of different entertainment: slides, climbing frames, sand, swings, basketball hoops. Definitely recommend!

This is a great neighborhood park! Their is a play area for kids, swings for small children that have an attached seat for the parent to swing with them, miniature golf style basketball hoops (different shaped backboards to bounce the ball off to make it in the hoop), multiple sand Cornhole boxes, multiple full court basketball courts, BBQs, picnic tables, bathrooms, it's all their to enjoy. The only complaint I have is the field is ALWAYS soaked! I've been to the park at least 50 times the last 2 yrs and the field is always over watered. It seems to be a theme in Irvine with sprinklers. During the day it's not as bad but you'll still sink a little due to the over watering. At night forget it, your shoes will be trashed. Other than that everything else is perfect!

This is one of my favorite park in irvine. The shades are great, so if I want to avoid the crowd and hit the park at noon and post, the shades help my toddler from sunburn. Pretty clean and well maintained. Happened to use the restroom to wash hands, and was impressed by the cleanliness, more than  what a public park should offer. I always find a place to park. Has two basketball courts. I wish people don't let their dogs poop on the grass where the kids play and have picnics.

Remodeled and lots of activities for kids and plenty of parking. Clean amenities and bathrooms, probably one of the best parks out there for kids. Has barbecue stands and picnic areas, and adult basketball hoops as well and a small trail outside of the new playground!
The brand new playground just finished construction and it is fun. A lot of new entertainment for kids.

name: Irvine Ranch Outdoor Education Center
formatted_address: 2 Irvine Park Rd, Orange, CA 92869, USA

google_reviews.text: We had the opportunity to go ziplining. All the staff were great. Though, I'd like to extend an extra shout out and much appreciation to John. My child was especially anxious about the adventure, but he wanted to try. However, when we suited up and he got close to ziplining, he became very fearful and didn't want to go. John was an amazing coach and mentor. He assured my child that no one would make him do something he wasn't comfortable with, but also explained and demonstrated exactly what to expect. He took extra time to build trust and rapport, while giving my child the opportunity to make the decision on his own. And after some time and watching others go, my son took the "leap" and went ziplining! We are all so proud of his progress. And a lot of his confidence is due to John's expert coaching. We are so grateful for the experience!
The kids had so much fun!! Your staff was great!! They would not take our tips, they were very gracious!! Will definitely book again!

Amazing campsite, and activities for all ages!
For camping there is flushable toilets and showers pristine camp sites, and views of our beautiful hills.   There is activities for all ages (zip lining, ropes course (high and low), service projects to name a few). There is always something fun and adventurous you can do at the IROEC.
I went here for outdoor ed in 2013. Very nice facilities and I enjoyed my time. On the other hand I did have bubble guts in the middle of my stay :/

I went there for 3 days and it was INCREDIBLE!!! I had never been that happy after a camping trip. I would love to go back there and experience that all over again. The rope scope, zip lining , digging for coal, sifting out a different type of gold (Yeah for real!), the geodes we got to crack open, the food, the people, the energy, nature, beautiful views, the bravery, the courage from the staff to make this place comfortable just for you, and many more! I RRRREEEEAAALLLYYYY recommend you to go there,
From, A person who LOVED that place.❤️

name: Nature Park

There are signs that tell you the different trees and shrubs along the way. I would recommend this spot for a family picnic or to get away from people.
So much nature! Saw baby frogs, lizards, butterflies, squirrels playing in trees, terrifying spiders. All the things you hope to see walking through a nature park. Lovely visit
Nice and shady park, also leads to the Serrano creek trail

Surprising little oasis in the middle of lake forest. The Nature Park trail isn't well maintained, i had my dog and had to turn around. But, the park is beautiful and connects to Serrano Creek trail.

name: Dana Point Nature Interpretive Center
formatted_address: 34558 Scenic Dr, Dana Point, CA 92629, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to bring the kiddos. Our grands (5 & 7) loved walking the trail, looking for plants & animals to cross off the chart given them by the hospitable volunteer. Lots of hands-on, interactive things packed into the small space in the center itself too. We'll be back for sure.

The Dana Point Nature Interpretive Center is a fantastic destination, featuring a knowledgeable and friendly team dedicated to educating visitors. It's a wonderful place for kids to learn about nature in an engaging and fun way. The center offers various exhibits and activities that make learning enjoyable, ensuring a memorable experience for the whole family.

After exploring the center, you can also enjoy the nearby trail, which offers stunning views and a relaxing atmosphere. It's essential to always stay on the trail to preserve the natural habitat and ensure a safe experience for everyone. Whether you're looking to expand your knowledge or simply have a good time, this center and trail are great choices.

Me and my group stopped here to check it out and go to the restroom. They were very welcoming and happily invited us inside. They had many little exibits, each with their own information about the land and animals around them. Many fun kid activities. The is a trail path that you can walk to find animal information and have an amazing very of the ocean. It's a silent and calming place to hang out, and very clean. 10/10
This is a nice visitor center here, that has a lot of information about the surrounding areas here. This is also the trailhead used to access the trails in the preserve area.
The place is nice and clean. The trail is well maintained, easy hike with beautiful ocean view. Lucky to see a hawk chilling on the bushes.

name: Harbor View Nature Park

google_reviews.text: Lovely place, lots of flowers and animals.

A nice beautiful and peaceful stroll in this magnificent park in between the port streets community and the Gelsons shopping center. You can also stroll some hill by the nice stream and walk over the bridge to arrive to MacArthur blvd and San joiquim drive. A short cut.

When I'm in the area I go to this part for short walks and get the fresh air that it has by the ocean I really like it's pretty nice. Not bad to just relax and walk around for a while. It's really good location and it's not far from the beach which is the best part
Tranquility can be found here. Beautiful sounds from tiny waterfalls in babbling stream.
Fun place to take a walk. Nice little creek

name: Shipley Nature Center
formatted_address: 17851 Goldenwest St, Huntington Beach, CA 92647, USA

google_reviews.text: Lovely spot for a stroll and a chat, even got some meditation in! 🚶‍♀️🧘‍♀️ Just a heads up, leave your fancy shoes at home – it can get a bit muddy! 👟🌧️ #Walk #Talk #Meditation

The Shipley Nature Center is such a wonderful place to visit and take a nice long walk. I took my toddler and she enjoyed the scenery and nature walk. There are lots of places to sit and just enjoy the surroundings as well as various paths to take throughout the park. There were a large variety of trees and plants and we stumbled upon lots of animals that my daughter was so excited to see. There is a learning center where you can see all the types of animals that are native to that area. They have many fun learning resources, including a shop where you can buy toys, books, various gifts, and even plants and compost. There are classes offered as well. I highly recommend parents take their kids here or just go on their own for some quiet relaxing walks.

This is a wonderful place with more natural animals and plants. This place has many protrusions so pedestrians can go deeper and explore. Volunteers provide full support with documents and maps to identify and confirm objects. Many colorful flowers of many sizes and shapes, with many diffused scents, perennial trees growing in the forest, and nuts. Animals can see most birds, butterflies, squirrels, and insects. This place is suitable for children to explore and learn about nature. There are also many attractive souvenirs if guests need to celebrate

Free nice nature reserve located in Huntington Beach. They do ask for a $3 donation which helps maintain the area. It is open on a walk in basis Tue, Fri, and Sat from 9am-1. You can schedule a field trip for children outside of the walk in hours listed. There is a garden section which certain items are available for purchase. There is also a building with artifacts and other nature related items on display. They are always looking for volunteers to ensure the safety and care of the habitat to allow it to remain open. There are many well maintained trails located around the reserve with signage provided for information based on the area you are in. No pets, drinks, or bicycles are allowed in the reserve.

Shipley Nature Center is a wonderful nature experience with paths, learning presentations about gardening, plants and the native animals, birds and butterflies

There  are presentations, events, musical events  and kids classes.  It is delightful for all ages.

The only times you can get in is Fridays and Saturdays between 9 am - 1 PM.

It’s only $3 to explore and enjoy Shipley nature center.  Bring friends and family and help support this local treasure

In summer the gates open at 4:30 and close at 7:30.

name: Bolsa Chica Ecological Reserve

google_reviews.text: Very nice visitors center and amazing trails with birds and fish and other wildlife. When the flowers bloom, it’s so pretty.

Easily a top 3 bird watching spot. They have so many species it it impossible to keep count. Getting there is super easy and also parking. I was blown away and captured some amazing shots of some migratory and not typical birds. I highly recommend this spot.
Great place for bird sighting. Good variety, plenty of spots for close viewing, not crowded.

Great place to watch birds and go for a hike. Runners, birders and walkers share the trails. Plenty of clean, informative signs at every lookout point. Most of the scenery is near ocean side with oil rigs on the northern end. It cuts off on eastern end for mating birds. Some pretty pink flowers there to mark the spot.

A crosswalk at the parking lot on the east side for pch would be helpful for those who want to cross to the beach side.
A really great place to go birding (a lot of birds I seen here) and a great place for my photography!! Also, this place is a great job if keeping birds safe and able to see over 500 species

name: Talbert Regional Park

This large, open space park borders the Banning Bike Trail and is within close access to Canyon Park, where public parking may be found. I like to park there and bike in thru to the Balboa Street entrance, off 19th Street.
The naturalness of the park is a coastal asset, with dirt sand paths meandering thru past a seemingly endless variety of local shrubs.
I’d recommend a dirt bike or scooter with knobby tires, to easily ride thru the sandy soil and on thru the BMX Dirt Trails!
The downside with some of the trails is hiking and riding without appropriate footwear, bike or scooter wheels, when using that which is intended for pavement.
My favorite time to ride or hike here is on overcast days, as it is a largely open area without much sun cover.
Restrooms are off the beaten path, as the adjacent Vista Park by the Victoria Street Bridge is close for comfort!
Access to Santa Ana River trails may be at Victoria Street or off the Balboa Street entrance, although the latter may turn out to be haphazard thru multiple paths and a slippery dirt slope up to the paved river trail. Unless familiar with the more westerly pathways, I’d suggest a study of Google Maps Satellite View before going this way.

This place has been become A very special place the herbal scent on the trails and the fact that the tongva
cousin tribe of the Aztec mexica lived and called it home.

Came for a nice afternoon walk with my husband and 1.5 year old but quickly ran into a homeless man and decided to leave due to the narrow trails a deserted feel. Didn’t feel like a safe place to explore with kids in a bit of a valley surrounded by tall trees and thick brush.
Talbert Regional Park is a hidden gem for a chill afternoon hike—plenty of shade, and I even spotted a family of ducks by the creek! Perfect spot to escape without going far.

name: Nix Nature Center
formatted_address: 18751 Laguna Canyon Rd, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text: I meant to go to Laguna Coast Wilderness, but I saw this place 1st & thought it was it, never been to either plc. But OMG how amazing I spent hours here hiking taking in sights & pictures. Wow I hiked to Barbara’s lake. & got some amazing photos this is the only natural occurring lake in all of Orange County, so it’s just meant to be there for all of the wildlife that needs it to thrive. It’s absolutely serene here, breath taking!!
Informative exhibits and fun scavenger hunts for kids. Sign up the kids to become a Junior Ranger!

For hiking, this is about as scenic as it gets. Based on my experience doing Boat Road Trail (parking is free, but in a neighborhood), I would definitely come back to check out other trails in Laguna Coast Wilderness Park. It was a short, sweet trail (2 miles each way) with mostly rocky terrain and a good balance of going up and down. Laguna Ridge has a nice lookout spot, as does the Joseph Sweany Bench. Gorgeous views of Laguna / Newport with a side of exercise! I'd bring visitors who want to hike (but don't do it regularly) here for sure.

Visited the Nix Nature Center for the Hawks and Raptors educational event. Very friendly and informative staff! The Center itself is beautiful and a great starting point for many hiking/biking trails. They host many free events throughout the month, so check out their website! (Note, the events may be geared slightly more towards children, but they are still enjoyable and informative for adults.)

Parking is $3 but if the lot is full, they'll direct you to park along the dirt road and it's free.
Beautiful , We come often with my kisds we always see many animals and snakes 🐍

name: Peter and Mary Muth Interpretive Center
formatted_address: 2301 University Dr, Newport Beach, CA 92660, USA

google_reviews.text: Friendly and knowledgeable staff. The center is small, with a few exhibits. Nice way to learn about plants and animals of the back bay. Pretty and easy walking tails surround the center. No shade, nice views, wasn’t too busy even on a Saturday. Plenty of parking.

A beautiful lagoon trail with one of the friendliest visitor centers in Newport, located near the John Wayne Airport landing path. The serene nature trail and picnic area make it a perfect weekend outing for families. Bring your kids for a wonderful, hands-on nature experience.

Beautiful nature preserve where I go to plein air paint.  I was here to participate in an art show held every year.  You have the prettiest hiking trails where you can explore, walk you dog 🐕 and enjoy seeing animals and birds.

This is my second year doing the Backbay Plein Air quick draw.  It is always a great time with so many talented artists!  The trails are so peaceful and it’s fun to spot the horseback riders as well!  The center is set up beautifully with a large tank housing a very energetic ray and a shy shark( might have been his nap time haha).  The gallery is filled with amazing artwork ranging from well known masters to emerging artists.  I wasn’t sure if I was allowed to post photos of other artists before the show opened, so the one shown below is the painting I entered.  For the quick draw you only have a few hours to complete it from start to finish.  I arrived a bit late so I completed mine in an hour and 8 minutes.  I can’t express my gratitude for the Muth center and their contribution to the artistic community and nature reserve enough!

Lovely place in orange county. Lots of trails and vista points. The interpretive center is fantastic. Tidy collection of animals, live and also some which are neatly restored via taxidermy.

Lots of literature and a place for lil ones to have some activity as well.

name: Environmental Nature Center
formatted_address: 1601 E 16th St, Newport Beach, CA 92663, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to take kids. Lots of hands on activities for kids, beautiful nature, variety of different areas in the center. I’ve never seen so much green in Newport Beach lol. A great place for a field trip as well. Would happily come again.

The Environmental Nature Center in New Port is an absolute five-star gem! My wife, daughter, and I had the most incredible time exploring this hidden treasure. The serene natural surroundings and well-maintained trails provided the perfect setting for a leisurely family adventure. The educational exhibits were both engaging and informative, making it an ideal place for kids and adults alike. The staff was friendly and knowledgeable, enhancing our experience. We left feeling more connected to nature and with hearts full of gratitude for this fantastic resource in our community. A must-visit for anyone who appreciates the beauty of the outdoors!

What a great place to getaway from the craziness of the city! We are in love with this place and will probably be back soon. Great for kids. Super clean, safe and the staff very friendly and helpful. The place is beautiful. A little peace of nature hidden by the coast.
A fun little getaway for the family to walk, explore, & have a picnic lunch. That's what we did and had a great time. Staff was accommodating, and location was clean and well taken care of 😃
Great place to take your toddler for a nature walk. There is a creek and winding walking trails that loop around so you can’t really get lost. But only open to the public on weekends.

name: Niguel Botanical Preserve
formatted_address: 29751 Crown Valley Pkwy, Laguna Niguel, CA 92677, USA

google_reviews.text: Absolutely recommend coming here for a time to get in touch with nature. The smell of various plants and flowers makes it so relaxing aside from all the shade that the trees provide. There is plenty of areas to stop and sit while you begin your hiking adventure. Restrooms are clean and the garden is very well maintained. Would definitely come here again if I’m near the area. I visited from La Puente, CA.
Such a beautiful, well kept preserve. You could smell the mint, rosemary and pine! All the unique plants and trees made evey step a pleasure. The views are also a must see.

This place has so many different things to do  and there is parking close to bathrooms. There’s trails everywhere as they go up by stairs and down. The trails are dirt and gravel so a little slippery when walking down. The higher you travel up is where there’s more flowers but on the way up there’s plenty of trees, shrubs and benches. There’s also shaded areas when walking. The area also has wonderful views. There’s also a swimming pool in the community center with lifeguards. Next to the returned are regular and water play areas. We saw soccer fields and a bike trail, as well as some large grass areas with some with areas to sit and eat or just relax. This is a very nice and clean facility and garden. They also have EV charging stations.

This was a wonderful discovery.  The gardens are well maintained offering plants in a hillside setting with many meandering pathways, steps and bridges. There are lovely views,  sculptures and seating throughout the grounds. A fantastic way to spend time soaking up nature-plants, flowers, trees, birds, butterflies, squirrels! Free entrance and parking are a bonus.

Nice little community center with a small playground & quaint botanical garden. It is built on a slope, so some areas may be hard to access if disabled or difficulty walking. Many benches to rest/meditate. Not too busy on a late morning school day. Dogs are allowed on a leash, be careful of rattlesnakes. Can walk about a mile to the nearby regional park/lake.

name: Talbert Nature Preserve
formatted_address: 1299 Victoria St, Costa Mesa, CA 92627, USA

google_reviews.text:
This is a lovely park with trails throughout the preserve and one that goes to the beach! Always wildflowers here and many people hiking, biking, or walking their dogs.

There is also a picnic and day-use area that is always full of people enjoying the day. Some of the trails are paved making it easier for walking and biking.

This is a lovely place to spend part of your day!

When the long days of work are over, I always enjoy walking around the Talbert Nature Preserve for some fresh air and good energy. A great place for a walk with family or friends, or even with a pup or by yourself. Beautiful smells of sagebrush and saltwater wherever you go. If you make a trip here, try to pick up some trash during the walk. It will make you feel good about yourself knowing that your helping the beautiful place that your enjoying. This is a place for selflessness and positivity, so leave that city mentality behind for a minute and just breathe it in.

The fishing use to be great. Way too many gross homeless people walking around and camping near the lake. I found meth while fishing a couple months ago. Stick to the path, don't go too deep into the bushes.
Had a nice walk. It's not very big so you can't get lost. Cool bike track for bmx. Sad to see so many homeless looking for refuge here. I wouldn't go at nite
Typical walk. Nothing particularly cool to see but just nice to walk leisurely. Careful of the bikes that seem to fly by.

name: James Dilley Greenbelt Preserve
formatted_address: 350 Artisan Dr, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text: And head towards the lakes when you get there, you’ll realize from the signs that the lakes here are the only natural occurring lakes in all of Orange County. You’re not fed by men they are naturally occurring. It’s a beautiful hike. Make sure to get a map because you can come back the wrong way, but you’ll never get lost.

Really beautiful preserve. Parking situation is very easy; park, pay, sign in and you're good to go. The trail is very well taken care of and even 1/8 of a mile into it you feel like you're far away from where you really are. Gate closes around 5pm so make sure you plan accordingly but ti's a beautiful spot

Trails are well cleared and markers are easy to follow. Hiked the Blackjack trail to Ridgeline and followed to Edison, then Lake trail back to parking. About a 90 minute hike, moderate terrain. Hiked with my 14 year old. Bring plenty of water, there is very limited (almost non-existent) shade.
Good place to spend a few hours enjoying the wild life and the foliage indigenous to the area
Great, local hiking trails close to Irvine, Mission Viejo and Laguna Beach.

name: Limestone Canyon Nature Preserve
formatted_address: Silverado, CA 92676, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing experience I went with family and i didn’t expect seeing a yeti with family. Best experience ever would recommend. Just be careful because he did chase us through the forest.

You must register online and make a reservation in order to get in. I followed a tour today and the group of 25 people hike together and we visit one side of the sink. I plan to visit the other side on a open pass access day. The park is beautiful and worth the 10.5 miles hike in my opinion. If you can walk that long at a moderate pace with confidence, you should come and see it. You should have some hiking experience or otherwise the entire group have to wait for you.
Just went, cool sutff. Irvine looks great this time a year

This area is managed by OC Parks and the Irvine Ranch Conservancy. This is only open by appointment. It is a beautiful part of the old ranch that has been preserved in all its wild beauty. Visiting is a must if you like the wild outdoors.
A great place for mountain biking. Lots of options for someone just getting started all the way up to the advanced rider. I don't recommend for small kids though.

name: Santiago Creek Wildlife & Watershed Center, Santiago Park
formatted_address: Santiago Park, 600 E Memory Ln, Santa Ana, CA 92705, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a beautiful area to explore. I came here to do some birding (which was nice!) and to find a nice spot to read, but there weren’t many places to sit and unfortunately, the amount of plastic litter at this park was hard to ignore :(
Beautiful wild nature spot to hike, bike or walk. Lots of rocks, trees, vegetation and small wildlife to see.

This is a beautiful picturesque park and sanctuary. You can walk out here all day and explore or just sit in the quiet shade by the water. There are hiking trails and plenty of areas to have a picnic. Bring your family and friends and spend the day. Just remember to clean up your area before you leave so this beautiful place will be ready for the next visitors.

Great park to walk and observe nature in all its wildest forms. It’s nature in its finest form. This is a great place to bring the kids to show them more if a wilderness side of city living. Also great spot to walk the dog, or take a great stroll with your favorite human.

We came here for birding. We are sad that the creek has lots of human garbage. There are plenty of shade and parking. There are homeless camps in the park too but we feel kind of safe during our day walk. Free parking.

name: Ecological Reserve of Upper Newport Bay
formatted_address: 2482-2498 Mountains to the Sea Trail & Bikeway, Newport Beach, CA 92660, USA

google_reviews.text: There’s a back road/bike path that goes behind a trailer park before it leads to the nature center. You can look back at the bay and beach area that was blocked off from the trail. It offers a good ground level view of the bay.

One of the best places I have visited to inline skate! You will enjoy the nearly 10 mile round trip from here to UC Irvine. Please note that if you want to complete this route you have to be comfortable skating up and down hills, which I enjoyed. What a blast! Will definitely come back!

Went on a beautiful day. Nice ocean breeze. Well maintained trail. Lots of water fowl. About a 2.5 mile trail we went on although there are longer ones if you get closer to the water line. We were alerted of 2 coyotes near, we didn't see them. Great for camera buffs. Beautiful birds to see.

One of the favorite bike trail.
Beautiful scenery, easy access, and it connects the other side so you can enjoy both sides.

There is not enough shade so take your shades and water with you in summer.
What a beautiful oasis!! I went here on Super Bowl Sunday and for a small donation enjoyed what felt like a big park in a small space.

name: Nature Center - Irvine Regional Park
formatted_address: 1 Irvine Park Rd, Orange, CA 92869, USA

google_reviews.text:
Nice little free place to explore the history and display animals that live in the park. There are areas for the kids to do interactive hands on like digging for bones, touching animal fur, grinding materials in stone bowls and etc.
Jesus Christ Loves You!!!
Well put together. Good history lesson and cool taxidermied animal exhibit
Loved it! Free and informative.
Good stop to gain some history and knowledge about the local wildlife.
A beautiful park, we spent time there with friends, a very picturesque and calm place. We met peacocks, the ideal place to rest, there are walking areas. I will definitely come here again

name: Bolsa Chica Basin State Marine Conservation Area
formatted_address: Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text:
Great beach front getaway for RVs and travel trailers. Tent camping is not allowed. Water/electricity at each space. There is no direct  sewer hookup, but a no fee dump station is offered on site. Front gate and camp hosts are very friendly and stay on top of things, keeping everyone in check.
Live entertainment! You can relax and have a  cocktail/beer while you listen to great music.  The stage is located the area outside the  small general store/gift shop and snack bar. The evening Bonfires in the fire pits on the beach are a great way to end the day

Love this beach.  They have Fire rings,  but the parking is expensive.  Beautiful beach though with lots if little sea shells and tiny sea life.  They waved are pretty huge,  lots of surfers,  not a place for small children.  Perfect for surfers.

Nice place to walk. Paths are well maintained,  there is plenty of wildlife to view. The nature center has  a nice variety of creatures to see and things to touch. A good place to take young nature enthusiasts!

We camped with our trailer there and we loved it we were right by the beach and people around here are for the most part usually friendly there is bathrooms and showers we were hooked up but we had to dump our water tanks there a little ways up before we left of course which is pretty easy the road can be a little loud because its really close but its gated. Overall we always enjoy Bolsa Chica as we get to camp bbq swim and bike ride.
This is an absolutely beautiful and dynamic place. It always seems different everytime you visit. Different birds, different wildlife and various weather conditions make for a fun day anytime you go.

name: Upper Newport Bay State Marine Conservation Area
formatted_address: Unnamed Road, Newport Beach, CA 92660, USA

google_reviews.text: Really love this nature preserve.  Great biking through here with nice spots to stop and watch birds.
This is a nice viewpoint and you can see some wildlife here. You can only drive here one way, but you can walk/cycle here both directions.
Good lake view. It’s a nice place to watch airplanes taking off.
Beautiful 😍 🤩 relaxing place to come read a book 📖 or just and scap from day to day activities
Great area for walking. Not too much car traffic, great scenery and great fresh air.

name: Black Star Canyon Wilderness Park
formatted_address: Silverado, CA 92676, USA

google_reviews.text:
First time visiting Black Star Canyon Wilderness Park to check out the "falls" and it was just as expected. By doing a little bit of research, we learned it was best to go early - my husband and I got there at 7:45am in October on a Saturday and the lot was already starting to fill up. When we left at 11:30, the lot was very full. There is technically plenty of parking but the busier it is, the farther you would have to go just to get to the trailhead.

We had a very good weather for hiking. It was overcast for the first hour or so, and there is shade when hiking along the creek, and it was partly cloudy on our way out. If hiking during the afternoon, I would recommend bringing twice as much water as you think you would need.

Overall, the trail was long but not too difficult. The first 3 miles are fairly easy/moderate and only the very end being somewhat difficult because it is steep. We think the total hike was about 7-8 miles round-trip and took 3.5-4 hours at a moderate pace.

In October 2025, the falls and creek were dried out after a hot summer and only one storm in months. It made crossing the creek a non-event (only a little mud and puddles in a few areas), but it also meant the waterfalls were non-existent. The trail was still very scenic and a fun hike. The end of the trail is still very beautiful when dry.

Note, there are lots of mountain bikers so be alert and stay to the right side of the trail. No bathrooms, trash cans, or water fountains but still very clean; thanks to the regular hikers who help to clean up the trails. 👏

Please be kind to others and respectful to nature. Pack it on, pack it out. ✌️

I finally got to hike at Black Star Canyon on an Open Access day with the Irvine Ranch Open Space. I did the 6 mile loop which was provided on a paper map and on a poster at the trailhead. I arrived at 7:45 just before rangers open the trail at 8am. The trails are well-maintained and clearly marked. FYI this is a mountain lion area. I heard a warning growl on the Silverado Creek Trail and followed the instructions I read on the paper map. I stopped, didn't run, made loud (but soothing) noises, I waited about 2 minutes, then continued to walk. I didn't see it but it was a very obvious growl and I notified a ranger that I saw on the trail. This was an absolutely beautiful hike. It was a perfect mixture of some incline, downhill, and flat paths. I saw a few turkey vultures, beautiful yucca plants, and it got a little busy later in the later morning/early afternoon. I was also told that the Black Star Falls and historic Indian site is not part of open access day and is part of thee Cleveland National Forest system. Would definitely like to return to the area to do the Falls hike and visit the Indian village site.

Best hiking biking gravel trail very well maintained no water crossings super clean  8 to 14 miles one way one of the best gravel  dirt compacted trail out there beautiful views mountains all the way  trail starts at Santiago canyon behind Irvine lake and travels all the way to Corona double wide watch for mountain bikers keep on your side  watch for rattle snakes this is designated mountain lion area kids friendly fog friendly watch for waterfall trail  head thats whole different a difficult trail but this goes all the way to top two thumbs up 👍
Great day in wilderness, lots of hikers ( some with dogs), mountainbikers, enjoyed a lot...

Lovely place to hike, great exercise at a steady incline. Would recommend in spring for sure, followed by fall and winter. Could get pretty warm in summer with sparse waterfall. The main treat of the whole trail was seeing a beautiful peacock!

name: Fremont Canyon Nature Preserve
formatted_address: Silverado, CA 92676, USA

google_reviews.text:
Following this recommendation, I decided to check it out this morning so, I followed Google-Map and ended up at a gated private road that is always closed. No car can go through it without a key or code. From that gate it's about 8+ Miles one-way to "Fremont Canyon Nature Preserve" it's all dirt road and you need 4-wheel drive.
The only way to get there from that point is by walking. The next big question is - Where does the 3.5 miles begin!?
Is there any other way to get there?

name: Cedar Grove Park
formatted_address: 11385 Pioneer Rd, Tustin, CA 92782, USA

google_reviews.text: Really nice park overall, well maintained and clean. Everyone was nice we had no issues with other guests. I do wish the playground was a bit bigger and had more activities for smaller children but over was still a fun and great time.
A very nice park. Pet friendly  walking trails. Good for bikes. Nice palace to bring the family

This park in Tustin is the best place to visit if you are looking for quiet and relaxing places where you can go on trails , run  ride a bike,, lots of playgrounds for kids and field for a dog training , amazing path with trees and bushes, beautiful neighborhoods!

Good trail takes you out to Peter's canyon. Great spot to come out to. The times I've gone, it's been pretty chill and not too congested. I really like some of the benches near the trees. Good places to have a quick picnic. I don't remembered if they have grills out for public use.

Nice park with fitness equipment, 2 play areas for kiddos, and lots of shaded picnic areas. I especially love the ones under the beautiful cedar trees, but if you're planning a birthday party there's a large shaded picnic area with 2 bbqs next to the playground. I wish the path through the cedar grove were longer, but great walk with  little ones.

name: Newport Bay Conservancy
formatted_address: 2301 University Dr, Newport Beach, CA 92660, USA

google_reviews.text: I love coming to the bay and seeing the beautiful sights. I went one time and they were having some sort of display or show. Checked it out and had lots of cool animals and things to show off. Whole area is beautiful and would recommend for walking or studying. Awesome to see the planes fly across too.

Great easy to find trail! Trail itself is not very difficult. It is very family and dog friendly. If you want a nice stroll, this is the place. There is a paved section for runners and cyclists too. I appreciate that there are trash cans and dog poop bags along the trail.

Imagine yourself in the Cretaceous Period.
At this location, dinosaurs can be seen flying away every few minutes.
Apparently there's a dinosaur nest nearby.

You will never get bored, not only with the scenery, but also with the various objects flying in the sky.

Bring your binoculars for great fun.

Visiting from Salt Lake City for a quick weekend getaway.  Found kayak tours online and decided, sure why not!  Well, when my wife and I got there, we found out were there the only 2 signed up!  I fully expected them to cancel the tour, but NO!  There were 4 guides and us and it was an AMAZING and fun trip!  We were so appreciative of the guides taking time out of their day to volunteer and do these kinds of tours for the public.  Thank you!

This is such a great space, I remember exploring it as a kid and now my grandchildren have the chance to enjoy it - while my BFF of 40 years also still enjoy exploring it’s beauty.

If you’re a local, you know. If you’ve never been- it’s time to explore this beautiful, easy and ample hiking trail.

name: El Dorado East Regional Park
formatted_address: 7550 E Spring St, Long Beach, CA 90815, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing park!!! I love this park. It's my favorite park and I try to go as often as I can. It's HUGE and very beautiful. It's very well maintained. Once you enter most of the roads inside the park are one way with a white line divider for cars to park. You can drive to any area you want in the park and park your car and then go to the adjacent walking path. The park has various areas. There is youth camping and area 3 has many various events. Sometimes this area is closed to drive into and park because of an event, but you can use the walking path and walk anywhere in the park. There are lots of picnic areas. Many families come here to picnic for the day. There are lakes with ducks, geese, etc. There is an archery range and nice size dog parks. The dog parks are also very well maintained. You do have to pay for parking, but you can easily see where the money goes by how well maintained this park is. Note their website states they accept cash at the park entrance, but unfortunately they were robbed so now they only accept debit and credit cards to pay for parking. One of my pics has the parking prices. It's truly a wonderful getaway and I highly recommend.

This park was awesome! Lots of space to hang out and enjoy nature, some cool spots for cruising with your ebike some spots for atv carts other spot for small remote control aircraft and some nice walkways all around the park and some duck ponds and they have restrooms as well and it’s super huge parking was 6$ and they don’t accept cash just a heads up, definitely recommend checking it out if you love parks

🌴It was nice that there were big trees and wide lawns. The clean air and cool breeze were refreshing. 🌿🚶‍♂️It's a good place to take a walk and relax with your family. 🏵Archery range and 🐕dog park👍
Next to it🏄‍♂️?? There is a long trail road with the San Gabriel River, which is great for 🚵biking and 🏃‍♀️running. 👍

My family been to this park many times. You have the nature center that is very beautiful and then across the street you have the park where you can go and picnic play around in the grass and also you have the The frontier where you can go right on trains. Next time we go I look forward to doing some archery as well. My family will be back many many more times. Hopefully in the future the park take care of the bathrooms better far as cleaning and making sure they have supplies as well as homeless individuals that walk around shirtless and ask for food.
My first time in this beautiful nature preserve, well maintained trails to walk and visit. Clean and has ADA compliant trail for wheelchairs.

name: Gum Grove Nature Park

No restrooms, no picnic tables, no benches, no playground.
Great little nature spot
Dried up not kept up
Cute little park

name: Calico Canyon

name: Wetland & Riparian Habitat

Fairview Park, Costa Mesa, is lovely, with 3 distinct areas: the bluff, the ponds, &, down the stairs, Talbert Park, which is more a traditional park with benches & tables, restrooms, water fountain, squirrels & even foxes running through it.

Great place for a walk!!!

name: Los Cerritos Wetlands

Check out the walking tour put on by the Los Cerritos Wetlands Trust at 8 am on the first Saturday of every month.  They lead you onto the wetlands property that is normally off limits to the public.  Very informative and nice views you don't from Pacific Coast Highway.
Walk with the Audubon Society was so fun, we saw many different species and the work being done to restore has made it so green and nice for the wildlife
I love the dry natural landscape blended with the oil wells.  They come together nicely. If I was a painter I would paint this unique location a masterpiece.

This is the last bit of indigenous land that I inhabit with dear concerns we need have a little Indian program for understanding the symbiotic of what nature life I'll be spending this weekend and most weekends to June also get the sweetest Cerritosyacht club is where you can find me a and support Little Google land

name: Moro Campground

google_reviews.text:
Nice, well maintained campground. The views are awesome but depends on your campsite. We were lucky to get one with the best views, especially during sunset!

Park staff were friendly and informative. Everyone was considerate of quiet time and we had a restful sleep especially with the ambient sound of waves crashing.

You can only use a propane fire pit, no tent stakes either. It wasn’t windy so it wasn’t a huge deal. We just used our stuff to anchor the tents. We will definitely be back if we get lucky enough to snag a good site!

It’s hard to find a campground like this anywhere near L.A.
Reservations are extremely difficult to get, which says it all.
The restrooms and showers are outstanding—hot water is available, which is rare for campgrounds, and everything is very well maintained.
That said, the coin-operated showers don’t always work, so make sure to check beforehand and bring an extra coin just in case.
With these facilities and views, there’s really nothing lacking.
Note that you can’t drive stakes into the ground or make a campfire, so bring a gas fire pit if needed.

Excellent camping location for snorkeling at Crystal Cove State Park and El Moro beach. Great to swim, snorkel and relax. I snorkeled along the huge ocean cliffs towards Avalon point, which is further south around the cliff. There is abundant sea life there, I left some photos to showcase the marine life, it is protected, not touch or take city policy. I feature my adventure on YouTube @hikebytes called Snorkeling Crystal Cove at El Moro Beach. One of the best unknown snorkeling in SoCal coast line in Laguna Beach. Check it out for sure, we loved it 😍 Enjoy 🙌
Beautiful campgrounds, clean, including clean restrooms and showers, great staff at a location with views of the water that are just incomparable.

This beach is truly something special. Just a little north of Main Beach in Laguna, it offers a peaceful, scenic escape that feels like a hidden gem—especially for those in the know.

What I love most is the long stretch of sand. Even on weekends or during the summer, you can often find a quiet spot to yourself. On my most recent visit (you can see from my photos), I practically had the whole beach to myself. The views are absolutely breathtaking—classic Southern California beauty at its finest.

You’re allowed to bring food and drinks, which makes it great for picnics or full beach days. Parking is generally manageable, with an upper and lower lot plus a few in-between spots. Just keep in mind it’s a state beach, so parking is $15 for the day, and they accept card only—no cash.

One important note: to access the beach, you’ll need to walk through a short tunnel that sometimes floods. It’s a quick walk, but be prepared to take off your shoes and lift your items if needed. Also, dogs aren’t allowed on this beach, which is good to know in advance.

Despite the minor quirks, this is hands-down my favorite beach—and I say that as a California native. It’s serene, spacious, and stunning. A perfect slice of coastal paradise.

name: San Mateo Campground
formatted_address: 830 Cristianitos Rd, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text: A great camp ground. We are here during this time and it’s raining but we love it. The camp grounds is almost empty except for the few who are braving it through the atmospheric river 😆. The hike to the beach is amazing, about a good 4 miles round trip, great exercise for you and your dog, my husky loves it. Dogs not allowed but still worth the hike. Pretty huge camp ground with lots of sites. One of favorites to date. The drive to go into town is not long either, San Clemente is a cute little beach town.

Clean and well organized campground on the East side of the freeway.  It can be a bit noisy overnight due to freeway traffic, otherwise it is okay and feels safe.  Restrooms are clean and well stocked.

The trees here get five stars but everything else was just okay so three stars overall. The sites are nice size and the trees on either side give the illusion of privacy from your camp neighbors. Unfortunately, noise here goes completely uncontrolled even though there are strict policies in the paperwork you are given about keeping noise contained to your own campsite regardless of the time of day. My husband and I booked a full RV site Thursday through Sunday. Thursday was lovely actually, not too many people and we were able to enjoy sitting outside peacefully. All that changed on Friday when the campground filled up. Tons of kids running around screaming (yes playing, but still screaming uncontrolled) into the later hours of the night. Camp hosts just kind of shrug and say hopefully they'll stop soon. The other issue is once you've walked around the campground or taking a bike ride around it, there's really not much else to do. The 1.5 mile trail to the beach is so steeply inclined at various points that it becomes a real hike on a hot day, there and back. We brought old school pedal bikes with us not knowing, no way you can make it without an e-bike. You definitely need an e-bike or a vehicle outside of your RV to get around and really do anything fun. For $70 a night, we did not feel we got our money's worth. We would have if we had been able to sit out at our campsite and enjoy a peaceful nature experience without all the noise, but that was not to be.

The campsites themselves are clean, spacious, and there’s plenty of parking, which was great. However, the facilities were a big letdown—especially over Labor Day weekend when you'd expect everything to be fully operational. Out of several shower stalls, only one was working across all the loops, and more than half of the water stations were out of order. On a positive note, the one working shower had excellent water pressure. Just be aware that showers are token-operated (50 cents per token, good for 2 minutes), so come prepared.

We have been camping for years so there are things that are essential to us when we search for campgrounds and this campground didn't meet them after we arrived. 1- soap in the restrooms.  There was none. 2- cleanliness. The restroom/showers seem like they are pressured wash each morning however they don't smell or seem like any soap is used to disinfect.  3- Site cleanliness.  Our site was infested with rats. There were 2 rat nests in the high bushes. According to the host this is known and nothing is done. We found out our last night there. 4- noise. it was louder that we expected. Overall not a place we will be returning nor recommending.

name: Arroyo Campground
formatted_address: 92678, 30892 Trabuco Canyon Rd, Trabuco Canyon, CA 92679, USA

google_reviews.text:
We stayed 3 nights; Sunday - Wednesday. First night, the people at spot #13 were very loud: playing loud music well after 10 pm and talking/loudly laughing up until after midnight. Extremely rude considering everyone else was courteous of the quiet hours. The park rangers stopped and talked to them on Monday, they were quiet that night and left on Tuesday (thankfully). We didn't see any patrol on Sunday but there was a patrol that came through the next two nights.

Most of the spots have plenty of shade. We were at 53 and it had a couple great shade trees plus plenty of space. Spot 54 is a little close but the person camping there was only in their car so it never felt like we were on top of each other. Not sure how comfortable it would have been with another family there.

Included some pictures of this spot, along with some of the spots nearby after they emptied out.

Spot 53 has a path directly across from it that goes to Bathroom #2. It's nice having it there so it doesn't feel like you are walking through Spot 14 which is a pull through. Each person that stayed in the pull through spot didn't seem to use their campfire/picnic spot on the other side but hung out by their trailer near the walkway which almost made it uncomfortable walking by them.

The bathrooms are cleaned midday and even with a full campground the first night, it never got extremely bad. Mostly just toilet paper on the ground and a few items on the shelf behind the sinks because for some reason people can't pick up after themselves. The shower is free but you have to hold the button down for water; it lasts for a few seconds with each button press. The water gets hotter the longer you are in it.

Great paths for biking, hiking, and horse back riding. We did some biking and saw several horse riders. We tried to visit the Nature Center but it's only open on the weekends. It has a great playground behind it.

Firewood was $5 a bundle and the staff was very friendly. Especially the kid we talked to on Monday.

No camp store but Trabuco General Store was a short bike ride away; our teens hit it for Dipping Dots each day. And we rode over to grab some charcoal because we didn't bring enough; it was $13.44 for the tiny bag.

We had a great time and would definitely camp here again!

This is truly a top notch campground. The little creek added so much fun! Kids can build little enclosures for tadpoles for a whole day!😅 There are ample men and women's restrooms, which are always kept clean. And the free hot water showers!
Will definitely come back again!

My 1st time camping here. Flush toilets, showers. Bathrooms were clean. Campground was beautiful! Rangers patrolled on a regular basis mainly keeping eyes on peoples fires. We were lucky to be able to have camp fires, which was appreciated because it was in the low 40’s at night. Wonderful little general store less than a mile from where we were. No water in the creek bed. I’ll definitely return with my grandkids!!

I had a great first experience here. Lots of trails nearby for all types of adventurers, the facilities were very clean, camping spots were large and well kept; was an overall great experience. So cool to have this place right outside of Irvine. Will definitely be back.

Super clean and well organized campground with clean bathrooms that has running water and shower
Grounds are very well taken care of
A little close to the public road so some sites can hear cars driving by but at least not a ton of traffic.
Plenty of space per campsite and ground is flat for easy sleeping

name: Doheny State Beach Campground
formatted_address: 34381 Pacific Coast Hwy, Dana Point, CA 92629, USA

google_reviews.text: Very convenient beach campsite in south OC. One can walk to nearby restaurants and cafes. However, it is overdue for maintenance. Beach shower are out of order and roads and pedestrian pathways need paving and renewal. The campsites are very tight or closely spaced. Thankfully the temporary neighbors we had were friendly and quiet. This would be better operated by OC Parks.

Really nice campground.  Can hear the waves at night. Restroom are cleaned each day by the staff.  Make sure to check out the beach right around sunset. You will see calm waters and a beautiful sunset.

Very nice campground right within town. Beach front spots available but fill up fast and early. Staff and security on site is really friendly. Spots are great for a tent, small rv or van. Large beach with plenty of private space
Well used, older amenities. However, the beach and California Beach life was fun and inviting. Live music at DoHo Cafe, good food.

My stay here was fantastic. The beaches were beautiful, and bathrooms were cleaned every morning along with the trash. Campsites are pretty close to each other and there's a few trains that run through the night that may wake you up but in all it's completely worth it. Each campsite had a concrete picnic table and a fire pit. I loved my stay and can't wait to go back!

name: Huntington Beach RV Campground
formatted_address: 103 PCH, Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text: I love it here! There is a reason it is hard to get a reservation and that reason is LOCATION, LOCATION, LOCATION. Activities are never-ending You can bike ride all the way to Newport Beach on the bike path. You can have a huge bon fire on the beach at night. There are plenty of places to eat and a CVS is located on the corner in case you have forgotten something. On one of my stays I did hear the loud music from the hotel - but I was able to deal with that.

What's the best RV spot in Huntington Beach.
Amazing views.
Homeless population decreased.
Close proximity to countless restaurants and shopping.
No car needed.
Love this place!

It is basically a parking lot with stable 20/30/50 amp service and water. There is free access to one dump site for the 50 sites. The back-in sites are limited to 30' RV lengths and are the closest to the beach while the pull-thru sites are limited to 50' and are in the center of the lot. There is additional parking for toads and additional guests. Maybe it was just our luck, but we had no issues with homeless, just be mindful of the e-bikes and scooters, they don't seem to adhere to the posted speed limit or other rules. We camped at Huntington Beach RV Campground in a 25' Class C Motorhome.

Perfect location and just as advertised on their website. Water and Electric Only - No sewer. The dump station is located at the end of the park and was easy to get to. I was located in the back in spots against the boardwalk. Lots of activity and noise, so make sure you like to be part of the action or choose another spot. You can't beat the views and environment. Took bikes to all Huntington Beach and it was amazing!

The location, right at the beach just across from a bike/walking path could not be any better.  The spaces are roomy, wider than most.  You are basically in a parking lot, one with water and power.  No dump at your site is why I did not offer up 5 stars. Over the weekend, it was busy here, but during the week, not so much.  All day long you’ll see people running, walking or riding along the path.  Many dogs being walked.  Close, very close by are grassy areas where you can take your dog.  They do not supply dog pick up bags so bring your own. Plenty of garbage cans located throughout the park.  There is a public bathroom near the park, one with toilets only, but we did not use them.  Easy walk across the PCH to the downtown area to do a bit of “tourist” type shopping and plenty of places to find a bite to eat.  We sleep with a small fan running at night (white noise) which made it easy to get a good nights rest.  We are here currently, early February.   I definitely recommend this rv park.

name: Ronald W. Caspers Wilderness Park
formatted_address: 33401 Ortega Hwy., San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675, USA

google_reviews.text: Stayed at Caspers Wilderness Park in our Airstream and had a great experience. The full hookups made our 5-day stay comfortable and easy. The park has beautiful hiking trails and a really interesting wilderness center. Nights were peaceful with coyotes howling in the distance, and we even had a fox wander through our campsite. Such a unique nature experience. We’ll definitely be back again.

Very low key outdoor area. Multiple day camp areas, plenty of spacing and ample bathrooms.

They have a wilderness center that’s good for kids - stuffed animal replicas, magnification, games, a little theatre room and little crafts.

There are two campsites that are open now, make sure to ask if you need electric hook ups, one has it and the other doesn’t. It’s a $6 difference $20 and $26 for the electricity.  The area without electricity seemed way more busy but not packed, and there are a lot less spots in the non electric area.

Went couple of times and some cub scouts were here having a good time but our family still had plenty of space to have a good time at the park and playground.

Gorgeous Wilderness area with plenty of shaded and sun hikes. Perfect for a day outdoors with family or your horses. There is a large horse camping area which was being upgraded while we were there. No dogs allowed. The rangers are outstanding and friendly.

Not much to do at this park. Fire ants always all over the place, consistently. Bathrooms are scarce. Hikes can can be an option or visiting the Nature Center. fun if you have a big group but remember to camp away from the ants.

Have no idea how this location is since it was fully booked when I arrived.   It looks lovely on the maps.

This one's popular enough that it needs a reservation.

Also of note,  it is a wilderness preserve and thus does not allow domesticated animals on site.

name: Bolsa Chica Campground
formatted_address: Pacific Coast Hwy, Huntington Beach, CA 92648, USA

google_reviews.text: Cement parking spots with restrooms at the end of the loop. Must be fully self contained. They don't have any spots for tents. Nice campground, but please, please beware of stingrays.
Beautiful spot off Pacific Coast Hwy, between Bolsa Chica Wildlife Reserve and the Pacific Ocean.  Spots are close together,  but the views make up for it and other campers tend to be friendly.
What an awesome place! Staff was super friendly and accommodating. We found a great spot for our 40' MCI on the beach side.
I absolutely love this beach. It's definitely my favorite, always clean, parking is super close. There's plenty of place to set up your spot and not be too close to people around you.

Literally 3 downfalls.
1. They could use more fire pits.
2. They could regulate the pits better by actually enforcing the, "you must be present to save a fire pit" (however its worded).
3. Is horrible.
The last 3 times we went we barely found a pit and it was because people would put chairs or an umbrella next to it and assume that's they way to claim it. The 2 times the people that "saved" a pit NEVER showed up. Both times we showed up as the gates open and left at 10pm and not one person show up.
The pros -
1. It's a beautiful California beach!
2. It's pretty clean.
3. It's a beautiful California beach, what more needs to be said 😂

name: Live Oak Campground at Caspers Wilderness Park
formatted_address: 33401 Ortega Hwy., San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675, USA

google_reviews.text:
Just went here over the weekend and had a great time.
I do recommend booking much in advance because it does get full.
The campsites are decent sizes and can definitely hold a couple of large tents
There are fire pits and usually a seperate grill as well. But the fire pits do have grates over them too. They sell firewood for cheap also $5 a bundle.
Some campsites had good trees for shade but not all so be prepared it gets hot in the summer time.
Tons of critters around to welcome us 😄 squirrels, chipmunks, wood peckers but there are also red ants just be careful if you walk barefoot a lot or have children.
The park rangers are so sweet and helpful.
There is a nature center that has cool interactive spaces for kids and they do this Saturday night group for kids mostly, it’s fun and educational.
They have a HUGE playground by the entrance of the park.
They also have tons of trails to hike from a few miles long to like 20 miles it’s pretty cool.
They have water spigots some are switched off to conserve water but there are usually a couple close to each campsite.
The bathrooms are in one area you do have to walk to for vault toilets but there are portable potties closer if you’re okay with that.
The showers have been closed though.
All in all it’s a great place to camp and especially if you have kids
There were tons of kids playing around it was nice to see all the families out.

love this camp ground! Ronald Caspers Regional Park is far enough from civilization to see some serious stars but close enough to run and get pizza for dinner and coffee for breakfast. try and get a campsite along the usually dry river bed.

4 stars because they haven’t had water for the past 6 months. we camped here summer and winter 2021, and just visited today January 2022. Some equestrian group came through some months ago and emptied their reservoir and left nothing for everyone else.

nature center has great info on the park and helpful informative staff. the playground and the nature walk are perfect for kids. woodpeckers are everywhere and we’ve seen red tailed hawks, barn owls and great horned owls.

no pets allowed.

Spent amazing time at Live Oak Campground and honestly, it was such a peaceful escape. The whole area is clean, well maintained, and surrounded by beautiful oak trees—it really feels like you’re tucked away in nature, but not too far from home.

The campsites are spaced out enough for some privacy, and the bathrooms were way cleaner than I expected for a campground.

Perfect spot if you’re looking to unplug, relax, and enjoy some quiet time outdoors. Definitely bringing the family back again.
Live oak is an absolute beauty while still being close to home. Authentic “out there” camping with spacious sites, wildlife, and friendly rangers. Highly recommend for a get away with the family.

No dogs...no outside firewood... no alcohol, no fun. I went to camp and enjoy some of my taxes I have paid into the coffers of OC. I was due for some fun sun and a nice nature hike , but the fun police were on patrol. So I had to lay low like a criminal with my favorite after hike beer, whiskey and cigar. A very nice park and a great place for hiking and hugging trees...

name: San Onofre Beach Campground
formatted_address: Old Hwy 101, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text: Best beach camping in Southern California! The campsites are close together which is the main downside, but they’re all within walking distance to a bathroom with running water and beach trails down the cliffs to a rocky beach with great surfing. Each campsite has a picnic table and reliable fire pit. The staff is welcoming, informative, and maintains your privacy.
Beautiful ... campsites r teeny tiny. Looks like they r upgrading..hike to beach

Beach access campground. Fishing and surfing are also available. Campfires are available, children-friendly, dog friendly. But no beach access is allowed. RV, Tent, and Group camping are available. Electricity is not available. Daily visits are from 6 am to 10 pm. The gate closed at 10 pm.

San Onofre Military Beach camping. Have been coming to this area for years.  Always beautiful!!!  Unfortunately, the ocean is claiming more and more.  Some beach site are gone because of high tides.  The Lifeguard tower is on shaky legs. I hope they can save it. Campsites need some love, but the main hall and check-in is looking good.  I hope they can bring it all back to the grand showplace it really is.

Campground has great amenities, real bathrooms with showers, lots of RV hookups, big group campsites, on the beach. Unfortunately the beach access was closed due to sand erosion, do we had to walk a ways down the road to actually access the beach. Be we had a great time and I don't know why you wouldn't have a great time also!

name: San Onofre Beach Camp Pendleton
formatted_address: Beach Club Rd, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text:
Park Review

1. Park Quality -  I give the park a grade of fair for a number of reasons. First off, the entire park is open to anyone with base access, meaning, people (mainly surfers) can come and drive though/park wherever they want. I understand that there are day use areas and the beach is public but if I’m paying (and a high daily rate for a military park) I should have some sort of “exclusivity”. Showers, bathrooms and laundry were the worst I’d experienced. Anyone that comes to surf or otherwise hang out, has access. (seems that everyone knows the codes to access the showers) The laundry was always open and most machines were broken. (signs claiming they would be replaced in Nov 25 but someone said the signs were there a long time and doubted replacement)   Chances are I would not return to this park.

2. Sites - I stayed at two different sites, #41 which was in the lower area directly across from the beach. Most of these sites are narrow and back up to a hill that is infested with ground squirrels. I say this because it drove my dog nuts and I could keep my shades open. The office was very accommodating after about a week and let me move to sites #103. This is on the cliff overlooking the ocean and was a much better option for my dog's sanity. Most of these sites are much deeper and wider and felt more private. Also, it is away from the main access road, so much quieter. I would stay at these sites again if I were to return.

3. Location - Just down I-5 from San Juan Capistrano which gives you lots of options for restaurants, walking trails, and shopping. (Amazon locker is very close for shipping)

4. Noise - The ocean is loud and masks almost all noise on the lower sites. The upper sites will experience train noise, as freight and passenger trains run just outside the park to the east. Only once was I jarred awake by the trains. (it does often feel like a low level earthquake)

5. Cell Service - Verizon was two bars of 5G

6. Wifi - Park WiFi does exist but you’ll need to rent a box from the office. My T-Mobile 5G Gateway and it was 70 Mbps down and 43 Mbps up.

7. Rates - $79 a day plus $3 a day for a dog

8. Access - Of course you need to be a qualified person to make a reservation and utilize the park. Follow the directions in the confirmation email as Google will lead you on a boondoggle.

9. Dog Friendliness - The walkability is great for dog but as mentioned, if your dog chases things you can expect it to be a constant battle while walking the park.

Wrapping it up. If I were to return, as mentioned I would stay up on the upper level.

Security Alert
Some guard gates ask for civilian real ID, others do not.  SAN Onofre state beach has free, easy, no ID required, access onto Camp Pendleton, with anything you wish to carry, or do whatever they want.  Please be alert for children at the campground, housing, anywhere , as anyone can come over from the state beach and do anything they want.

On another note, the campground is a beautiful location,
front desk isn’t very accommodating

This was a great experience! We stayed in cottage 4.

We were worried that it is a rocky beach but they cleared a good portion and added soft sand. The cottage itself is sitting in the perfect spot to view the entire beach. With that, it is a very short 10-30 second walk to the beach itself. Some things to take note but not let it deter you. No a/c in the cottage and you must open the screen door and window in the bedroom. I suggest to bring some extra fans to help air flow. I also suggest to bring an air mattress.
