There is only one roll away bed on the entire site that can be rented for $22 a day. There is also train nearby but it isn’t bad. Well at 2am it does get really loud and long. I don’t know why it changes around that time. There is a playground and laundry just down the road. The reservation team was very professional and the front desk people were very helpful, even during our tsunami.

If you have been viewing this spot, just go for the booking! This was great!

San Clemente is nearby and that is a fun spot as well.

San Onofre Beach at Camp Pendleton is an incredible place to relax and enjoy the ocean. The beach is clean peaceful and perfect for a day of sun surf and family time. The camping area is well maintained and makes it easy to stay right by the water. The atmosphere is calm and welcoming which makes every visit feel like a getaway. It is one of the best spots on base to unwind and enjoy nature. I highly recommend this beach to anyone with access.
Paid $100 to sleep in the dirt for a so nights and enjoyed every second!  Bring earplugs, the train can be loud at 1:00 am.

name: Hickey Creek Camping Ground
formatted_address: Trabuco Canyon, CA 92679, USA

google_reviews.text: Best camping experience with our cub scouts. Great camping grounds. Neat restrooms including flush toilets and hot water  shower available.
Nice campground, near home, good to introduce the scouts to the outdoors.  Very nice clean facilities with showers.
Family friendly had a great time
the best place for camping
Nothing Really Fun

name: Upper Moro Campground
formatted_address: Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text:
We had a great stay at Upper Moro Campground in Crystal Cove State Park. The rangers were super helpful and friendly, which really added to the experience. It was nice to have both a pit toilet and a trash can available — makes things much more convenient for a backcountry site.

The pit toilet was a bit smelly, but that’s pretty expected for this type of setup. Each campsite had its own picnic table, which was a great touch, though a few of them were a little wobbly. The pit toilet at Lower Moro Campground was definitely in better shape, but overall, Upper Moro is still a beautiful, peaceful spot to camp with great views and easy access to the trails.

Would definitely come back

It's a primitive campsite. Not much to have as far as luxuries but it was something my husband and I knew hiking in. We stayed 1 night and it was peaceful and amazing however:
- LOTS of mosquitoes. I got bit all over my ankles even while wearing leggings & hiking socks.
- The reservation said it was pack in pack out but there are actually tons of trash cans near the bathrooms all along the upper & lower moro campgrounds so that was a nice surprise.

We had a very nice time up here and plan on coming back once it gets a little cooler. Super beautiful hike.

We started from the lower parking lot. Went up right at the first cut to end up on Moro Ridge Spur / Road. From there just go straight to Upper Moro Campground. The campsite have no water so take your own. They have a outhouse / pit for restroom. Mosquitoes 🦟 are ruthless anywhere and I don’t know why thought up here was the exception. Take your measures to prep against mosquitoes. I took us 1 hour and 45 minutes to ascend 974 ft, distance 3.15 miles. Make sure if your backpacking to use the rainfly as the fog will come in and wet everything. Will be trying Lower Moro next time. Have fun and Happy Camping
Great for bikepacking. We started from the parking lot. Be aware of mosquitos.
Very hard to determine spots. Some are marked, some are not,  and some have a million picnic tables. Lower moro had better bathrooms, views, and sites.

name: San Onofre Bluffs Campground
formatted_address: 8FWJ+P3, Agra, CA, USA

google_reviews.text:
Very nice beach view campsite - both sunset and night moon are wonderful.

Restrooms between each section of campsites. 4 rooms plus 2 sinks and a outside shower tower.

Be wearing that not all the path can reach the beach and it takes another 10-15 walk down from the beach parking to the beach. Parking is very limited ( about 10-12 cars).

This was a great place to go camping especially if you’re a person that needs to fall asleep to sounds. I actually enjoyed the sounds of the freeway and the occasional train that would pass as well as the sounds of the bugs and ocean. The toilets were so clean! There were places to wash my feet! Took off a star bc the walk to the beach far and the trail/hike closest to us eroded. So we can only view the beach from above. I’d definitely come back!

The Bluffs Campground is a mix of good and bad. The good being the proxemity to the beautiful beach, a short hike down the trail, and the view from the bluffs over the ocean. The sites are more pavement than dirt, each having a fire ring and table. The bad being the filthy stinking restrooms, the absence of hot shower facilitites, the constant noise of the 5 fwy and the occasional roar of passing  trains. No water at any of the campsites, so come prepared. Some modest upgrades are sorely needed. The location is beautiful however, and this could be a premier camping/rv campground if time, money and a plan were ever brought to bare.

This place is great for surfing, camping, seasonal wildflower viewing, day trips and more. The beach looks amazing while nervously standing atop the crumbling bluffs and the bluffs are an aesthetic bonus while down on the beach. This place, like many beach locations, can get busy, so book or arrive early. The camp sites are perfect for couples or small groups. Note there is not much space between camp sites so be prepared to be respectful of your neighbors if you are camping over night. Or be prepared to get to know your neighbors pretty well if they are not respectful of the close proximity. Wood fires are ok. Bathrooms are water wise. Surfers are abundant. Get out there and enjoy!

Considered Primitive camping but this is a hidden jewel.  Been camping here since I was a kid.  People will talk about freeway sounds or the trains... These can be background to what is a great place to relax and camp.  What is old Pacific Coast Highway serves as mostly level spots for RV's and Trailers.  Most sites have some dirt to pitch tents on.  Staff is friendly and the bathrooms are maintained daily.
There are three trails to the beach with Trail 6 being the longest.  The bluffs can provide amazing views.

Note:  Watch out for the ants... Demetrious Earth (DE) is excellent at preventing their entry to your food storage.  It is organic and non toxic.

name: Lower Moro Campground
formatted_address: 8471 N Coast Hwy, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text: Love the views!!! Great hike for any time of the day, would be a great sunrise or sunset hike.  A few little spots to stop on the way up. A well traveled path from the trailhead parking lot to the lower campsite. Tons of great trails back in here!!

Walk or bike in primitive camping with almost no trees, but spectacular views. Access on foot or bicycle by several steep dirt roads. Can be windy since it’s on an exposed ridge. Advanced reservations required for overnight.

Totally worth the hike. You should backpack in and camp here. The hike is not too bad but there are 3 steep sections that could be taxing if you're carrying a lot of gear. The views of the ocean and surrounding area from the top where you camp are incredible.

Cousin and I spent 2 nights at Lower recently and had some great mountain bike trail hopping fun. Site was super clean and as always we made sure to leave it cleaner than we found it for the next crew! Highly recommend
Best site on the entire mountain . Get there early 😋

name: Camp Sycamore
formatted_address: 16801 Euclid St, Fountain Valley, CA 92708, USA

google_reviews.text: A great place for a church or youth group to tent camp. The pavilion is large and nice. Plenty of flat spaces for tents
Great place to camp without going out of Huntington Beach. Quiet and secluded. Nice bathrooms and running water. Super pretty wildflowers and wilderness feel.
Nice place.
The kids went there for a day care over nighter and the had a good time there
CLOSE BY TO DO EXERCISE, AND OF COURSE A PICNIC WITH FAMILY AND FRIENDS

name: San Juan Meadow Group Campground at Caspers Wilderness Park
formatted_address: 33401 Ortega Hwy., San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place for group camping. Has bathrooms with flushing toilets and run ing water for sinks, lights on a timer. Park rangers come by frequently. Large firepits and bbq grills available

The actual site is nice, it also gets pretty cold during the night during the winter though, I went last night and it got as low as 24 degrees.  Also, there are a lot of gopher holes, so take caution when walking at night. Putting a tent down has no problems, as it's flat everywhere. The fire pits are also nice, and have space to put your legs in when the fire is burning. Overall, one of my favorite sites

If you have a large group, this is a fantastic place to come camping. Our YMCA group has been here several times throughout the years, each time having a blast. There are hiking trails nearby of varying length. There's a nature center up the hill that has great examples of native wildlife. The bathrooms have been out of order for some time, but they do provide porta johns for use by the campers.
Bring ear plugs if you want to avoid nearby traffic noise from waking you up at night. Bring thick sleeping pads because the ground is really bumpy. Also, no shower is available.
Favorite camping place. Highly recommended:)

name: Lakeview Park Camping Area
formatted_address: 5305 Santiago Cyn Rd, Silverado, CA 92676, USA

google_reviews.text:
Extremely poor choice for large events.
This venue is completely unfit for handling large crowds. It took nearly 1–2 hours just to get out of the parking lot—an unacceptable delay that reflects a lack of proper planning and infrastructure. The location is remote, with no reliable cell reception, which poses a serious safety risk in case of emergencies like wildfires. Surrounded by mountains and wildlife, the area feels isolated and unsecured. I would strongly advise against hosting or attending any large-scale events here. I won’t be returning.
I come here for the Over the Hump mountain bike racing.

name: Deer Canyon Campground
formatted_address: Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text: The surrounding chaparral is beautiful, and home to many native animals and plants. But this camp is accessible from the road by around a mile. And the sites are very close to the trail. Also weird is that even from one hundred feet away from the site we camped at we could hear other campers very clearly - despite the fact that they were not loud.  We arrived later in the evening, set up and were awoken around six or seven in the morning by at least one hundred hikers, all talking loudly. (Not an exaggeration) It doesn't feel safe or secure. The trail should route around it, but it doesn't. Don't camp here for more than a day and expect it to be safe to even leave your tent up. We booked two days but did not stay the second evening. This camp does have nice picnic tables. And, although hot during the day - it is pretty. But the hoards of people make it less than idyllic. Also - we're in the middle of a pandemic. Just another reason to avoid crowds. So, what is this site good for? Maybe taking your kids out for a quick camping evening?

Nice, accessible, place to test out your backpacking gear locally in Orange County before taking a bigger trip. 3.5 mile hike up from the beach parking lot. Relatively clean pit toilet, available trash cans, most sites have a picnic table, no running water.

Downsides include that it is right along a trail used by local exercisers (people were hiking by and playing loud music in the evening), and there are an abundance of rats that we could see and hear in the bushes near our site. Secure your food! Also not a lot of separation or privacy between campsites, and at least two sites are on a slope. Checkout is also 9am, which could be a problem if you are a late riser...but the hikers and runners would probably wake you up before that!

From the Visitor Center parking lot, along "no dog" and "no name", then down "Ticketron". 2.5 mile hike that is strenuous at times. The campground was clean. Two trash cans and a clean vault toilet stocked with tp.
No water. No real flat spot. Lots of poison oak.
Totally worth it.

Site #2
Trash was strewn all over the campsite due to overflowing trash in the cans nearby. It had to be at least 2 week's delinquency in taking it out. Ants were swarming everywhere because of this. Hopefully it's dealt with more diligently in the future and the ant problem subsides because of it. A very cool in-season back-country spot if not for the above.

Primitive camp site on a hiking trail.  Had a clean pit toilet, a trash can and bench tables.  Too bad there isn't running water.  In the morning, if you sleep late, you will have hikers and bikers going by your place.

name: Ortega Flats Campground at Caspers Wilderness Park
formatted_address: 33401 Ortega Hwy., San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675, USA

google_reviews.text: Small campground with individual camping sites. Good for tent camping, although having a recreational vehicle is likely going to be a better option. They do have a dumping station on site. You can go online to reserve the spots even booking them several months in advance. One can't help but compare this venue with O'Neill Regional Park which is also located within Orange County. While I feel that this is the lesser of the two campgrounds, it's still worthy of a drive-by to check it out.

If you pay for day use beware park rangers will ticket you immediately after "sundown" which is subjective but it seems they go by Google sunset time. Ranger Matchett was rude and unprofessional. Won't be coming back to this park. This is a picture I took on the way back from the trails around the time she issued the ticket. Sunset seems to be pretty subjective.

this is a great campsite! Ronald Caspers Regional Park is far enough from civilization to see some serious stars but close enough to run and get pizza for dinner and coffee for  breakfast. try and get a campsite as far from Ortega highway as possible.

4 stars because of the road noise they haven’t had water for the past 6 months. we camped here summer and winter 2021, and just visited today January 2022. Some equestrian group came through some months ago and emptied their reservoir and left nothing for everyone else.

nature center has great info on the park and helpful informative staff. the playground and the nature walk are perfect for kids.

no pets allowed.

UPDATE 08-17-23
We are back again this time in spot #10 this shade is perfect!

Water is still running in mid April in the creek I haven't seen this in quite a few years. We love this campground and stay as often as we can.

Great affordable place to camp in Orange County. Removed 1 star due to there being no water at the park. So you cannot fill up water or use the dump stations. The staff at the front desk are not very nice.
Plug ins for 110, 30a, and 50a.

Two week maximum stay at a time.

name: Group Campsite #2
formatted_address: San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text: Right on the bluff, one of the best in the whole park, lots of tent spots and a good grill

Our family and friends loved it. We had a memorable time during memorial weekend in 2021. This is our first time camping experience. We thoroughly enjoyed it
I would recommend this place for first time campers and beach lovers. This place is easily accessible to Walmart, Ralphs, and Costco within 10 mins drive.
Pros:
Nice shaded place with reserved parking for campers. Easy accessibility to the near by restrooms and it has flushable toilets.  Conveniently available near the camp sites.
Beach ⛱️ access is within 5 mins walk.
Paid Showers are available.
Cons:
Restrooms didn't have hand wash liquids or disinfectants.
Some toilets were ran out of bath tissues and not replaced in late hours.
Beware of SKUNKS during dark hours.
Shower areas can be maintained well.

Very nice park to rent and stay only only concern is the foxtail weeds for your pets when breathed in to the nostrils they can get stuck in there...
Just be real careful where you take them to do their thing and walk around the property...

Omg...I have never had so many ants so fast anywhere I have stayed. They obviously know its an issue because they sell natural bug powder for 10 bucks a small bag 🤦‍♀️. I spent the first day trying to kill all the ones that had gotten i to my camper and the next day putting my camper back on early so I could leave here without taking a billion ants with me. Worst experience.  Its sad, too, because its such a beautiful place...especially to watch the sunset. They need to do something about this ant problem. If I ever think about coming back, I will bring a lot of bug killer and not drop my truck camper.

I almost forgot, some of the bathroom doors do not lock, making them unusable, and a lot of the water spouts in this area are broken and have bags over them. Water was the one thing I needed and had to drive to get some in a separate container to fill up while here.

My family loves this campsite! They clean regularly and the people around are kind and quite of those around them. We have been going to this campsite every year for 5 years now and it has been the best family vacation we have all had! I suggest bringing some ant spray to spray around your tent because there are ants everywhere and baby powder just hasn’t been working but if that bothered us enough we wouldn’t keep coming back! I suggest taking the family on a fun camping trip on the beach so you can sleep with the sound of the ocean and wake up to the best smell of the ocean breeze!

name: Moro Campground Entrance
formatted_address: Unnamed Road, 8471 N Coast Hwy, Laguna Beach, CA 92651, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful camping on the bluff. Great views of ocean and cliffs. Lots of hiking and wasy access to beach. Well not so easy when we visited. Recent rains flooded the tunnel access to beach. No wood or charcoal fires allowed at campsite.
Views are fabulous. Facilities up to date. Staff and campers very friendly and helpful. Three nights here definitely worth the price. $75/night.
Definitely worth it to camp out here... View is 5/5.

Beautiful scenery, stunning sunsets. Normally I would rate this with 5 Stars but our last experience last week we had a terrible problem with ants getting in our camper.  We put diatomaceous earth around everything that touched the ground but unfortunately we did it after the ants invaded. We were never able to get rid of them 100%.

Also one of our neighbor campers, 2 sites over, played their music with lots of base so loud we could hear it inside our camper. We talked to a park employee at the entrance and he told us there was nothing he could do, you're allowed to play your music as loud as you want! We walked back to our campsite wondering how we were going to camp the next 4 days listening to loud music.  Fortunately for us they only camped 1 day!!

We camped for a week. There were quite a few empty campsites but according to reservations every site was booked. Must be lots of no shows. It would be nice if people would cancel their reservations if they can't make it, to give other people a chance to reserve the spot.

All in all we enjoyed our camping experience and definitely come back.

My wife and I stayed here during Covid and we were glad it was still open. Everything was great and the ranger made a special exception for us since we got there early. REMEMBER, check-in is 3pm and not 2pm like most others. The late check-in is because of the school getting out at 2pm.  We won't make that mistake again, but the Ranger was really cool about it.  We really enjoyed the electrical and water hook-ups too. It's great not hearing generators going all day charging up batteries.

name: Group Campsite #1
formatted_address: C92X+95, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text:
What a beautiful campground to stay at! The sound of the ocean overnight is a delight you don’t get in other places…
There’s a beautiful hiking path that leads to the ocean. It’s not a great swimming spot due to the rocks and pebbles entering the water but it’s a great place to get wet and hang out and play.
There are multiple restrooms all around the area as well.
We loved it!

This is truly a great site! Ample shaded tables, close to restrooms and showers, let alone the fantastic views! The only thing that can be improved is to add at least one more fire ring: one camp fire is simply not enough for over 40 people! :D

It has an awesome view and beach access to camp here but some expect any water or sewer hookups and good luck finding your campsite as there are no markings of any kind on the streets or at the individual campsites.  Impossible to tell people how to get to your campsite and forget getting Door Dash delivered as they can't find where your are located.  California can do much better than this.  Very disappointing 3 day stay.
Very beautiful and amazing campsite. Hopefully we can get it again some day!
Weather could have been warmer but never a bad day at the beach!

name: Campsite #63
formatted_address: 21522 Trabuco Canyon Rd, Trabuco Canyon, CA 92679, USA

google_reviews.text: Wide open space for multiple tents. Great sun exposure if you bring solar panels for your solar power stations

name: San Clemente State Beach Campground Entrance
formatted_address: 2642 Avenida Del Presidente, San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text: Genuinely one of the most beautiful campgrounds on the beach as far as LA or OC go, even as far up as Lompoc past Santa brabara.. the closest thing to compete is jalama. But though jalama has the benefit of being able to camp om the beach, it's not half as clean. San clamente is a beautiful site, with stunning views and the beach, including all nearby beaches are stunning. Highly recommend the premium sites, you can see the beach at all times. If not the beach is but 10 to 15 minute walk or a 30 second drive..

I stayed o/n 1/21/26, took a walk on the beach in the morning, came back to campsite124 before 11 am and found someone stole all my firewoods and a water bucket. Never thought the fellow campers could do that. The campground and staff were great.
No mention of the locked gate if you get into your reserved site late. No way to get in. Spikes in outgoing lane.
Nice campground, easy access to the beach with lifeguards present. Good surfing and body boarding spot. Campground was nice and clean and quite.
Place is great! Will be back

name: Seabreeze RV Resort (Military Campground)
formatted_address: Chapel Dr, Seal Beach, CA 90740, USA

google_reviews.text:
Sea Breeze RV Resort on the Seal Beach Naval Base is an excellent place to stay. The location is unbeatable—peaceful, secure, and just minutes from the beach and surrounding attractions. The resort is clean, well maintained, and very easy to navigate, making it a relaxing spot whether you’re staying short-term or longer.

The views and coastal breeze really live up to the name, and the overall atmosphere is calm and welcoming. It’s clear that a lot of care goes into keeping the grounds in great shape. If you have access to the base and are looking for a safe, convenient, and enjoyable RV resort near the coast, Sea Breeze RV Resort is highly recommended. We would absolutely stay here again.

Being our first time here, we were very pleasantly surprised to see how well maintained this park is....then we realized how cool the Nature Reserve is that covers one side of the park.  There is a field and trees where the Great Blue Herons and Snowy Egrets live.  These large birds and their babies were flying frequently to and from their 3' diameter nests in the trees right next to our RV.  Besides the park, there are plenty of nearby places to visit like Seal Beach Pier, Long Beach Harbor, and LA.  There is no NEX or Commissary; however, there is a Mini Mart and a nice Laundry room available.

BRING YOUR BIKE! This place is AWESOME and lovely! It’s a military spot, so only military folks (active duty, retirees, disabled vets, DoD civilians) can stay here. There are armed gate guards. There are so many things to see off base. Note: there is no base exchange or commissary, although there is a very, VERY small shoppette. No gas station on base, either. Beautiful views all around, long, spacious concrete pads with concrete parking for a tow vehicle. Plenty of trails for biking and walking. We biked the Pacific Coast highway for 26 miles. I LOVE that CA has dedicated bike lanes everywhere! We biked all over: Huntington Beach (where there’s a large Rad Bicycle center that you test ride, rent, and purchase RAD ebikes and accessories and get your bike serviced), Seal Beach, Long Beach, and other adjacent towns. Less than an hour to L.A., too! The weather during our visit was sunny and never got above 78 degrees. Highly recommend this very clean and well-maintained Rv park!

Military & Retired Military only.
Clean, level spaces. Car wash, laundry, bathrooms. Quiet.

It's your typical military base RV park. Well kept and spacious with basic amenities. Obviously safe and secure since you're on base but then you have the hassle of dealing with gate entry procedures every time you want to go somewhere. And it seems like they open/close gates randomly so understand there are two entrances and exits to this part of the base where the park is. This was our first stay at a Naval installation (we typically stay at Air Force bases) so we were caught off guard when they wanted to inspect the interior of our trailer before bringing it on base, so that's another thing to be prepared for. Other than that, its a great option for the area. Prices are high when compared to other installations but fair when compared to anything else in that area of California.

name: Sea Summit Trail - North El Camino Real Trailhead
formatted_address: San Clemente, CA 92672, USA

google_reviews.text: Whenever you're driving along North El Camino Real, this trail always catches your eye—and my curiosity finally led me to explore it. I found it absolutely beautiful! You get a unique view of cars passing by on the road while also enjoying the stunning beach scenery. Loved everything about this trail—it’s a perfect blend of nature and urban vibes.

I love this trail system! I happened to stumble upon it on my first day exploring the area and have revisited everyday since! It's a nice mix of well groomed trails, stairs and ocean views. It connects to a couple of different parks, public washrooms and even the outlet shopping area and restaurants nearby. There's tons of benches to stop at as well as tons of garbage cans along the way.

It was awesome, a great hike where we watched the waves in the morning. The view is incredible and the sound is relaxing. The ambience of the waves is always there and it just looks so good. Don't skip it, it is awesome. Not wheelchair friendly at all, there are a LOT of stairs. Be prepared to go up and down many many times. Try and spot surfers on the waves, it's really fun

Spectacular views on this amazing well marked trail! Plenty of access points with free parking and easy plentiful paid parking at North Beach San Clemente also. Decent clean Public restrooms available along the trail. This trail is easy, but can be a workout if you want, with all the great stairs along the trail. The views are what make this trail pure joy and sum up California's spectacular beauty.
I enjoyed walking this trail lots of great views could see for miles and it is dog friendly.

name: Hidden Trail Park
formatted_address: 25332 Stageline Dr, Laguna Hills, CA 92653, USA

google_reviews.text: Great quiet place to walk your dogs. They provide doggy bags! Nice trail, beautiful well kept grass, kids playground was super clean, a water fountain to drink from, fill your water bottles, and even super clean water bowl/ fountain for your dogs.

This is about the footpaths/horse trails around the hill.  The trails are well maintained and conditions are very good to excellent for walks, except for one stretch that's very steep and makes sensible shoes mandatory.  There's tyre marks on the wide and flat parts.  More worrying are coyote or large stray dog tracks.  Bring a walking stick or golf club.

I’ve been going to different parks in the area with my dog and this is one of my favs! Great grass space with a pathway all around it, several benches and tables in the shade along the path, and a playground + basketball court. Has trash cans and free doggy bags. 9/10
Beautiful park with great new playground equipment and shade structures.
Nice park. Clean and spacious. Baby loved the play equipment.

name: Arroyo Park Trail
formatted_address: Arroyo Park, 31372 Via Parra, San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675, USA

google_reviews.text: My grandparents donated the trees in this park back in 1981. Andy and Marie Cook.  Lots of good memories here!

This is a great place to exercise, walk your pet. I myself use it to dance at. It's close to home. When things are heated up. That's where i run. I put my ear phones on and get lost in the sound of my music. Most every weekend you will find a youths game going on. The greens are always kept up.
Nice little open area for the kids or dogs to run
Love parks
Rad place to walk.

name: Salt Creek Trail
formatted_address: 31535-31543 Street of the Golden Lantern, Laguna Niguel, CA 92677, USA

google_reviews.text: Paved trail. The lady with adorable fur babies saw me reading the sign about cyclists. She was right when she said bikers won't listen to the sign. Most bikers don't have a bell and are way too fast - scary! There are lots of surfers. There were awesome volunteers cleaning up the beach.

My daughter and I decided to take a walk down the Salt Creek Trail. We really enjoyed the sunset along with the scenery. It was nice to see that Alejandro Turnbull with his Eagle Scout Project, put in a bike repair station. The trail is very well kept and there are benches along the way for some of us who need a break. I'm grateful to live in this area and hopefully will someday walk the length of the trail. Parking is easy and plentiful as we went at sunset. I'd recommend this to anyone who wants to take a evening stroll or to runners and cyclist too.

Beautiful trail. I walked it with my wife from golden lantern to marina hills. Very peaceful and relaxing. It’s a paved trail. The only place to use the bathroom is by the baseball field in Chapparosa Park.

Beautiful trail, well maintained.
It is not a loop. Not sure how long it is , I  got to run 3 miles from the beginning at Chaparosa Park and came back up, I run out time. It is very hilly, beautiful views.
3 miles, cool wind. nice trail.

name: Arroyo Trabuco Trail
formatted_address: 26772 Avery Pkwy, Mission Viejo, CA 92691, USA

google_reviews.text: In mid October, there was plenty of water in the creek and reservoir. There were so many rabbits and squirrels. The slopes are quite steep, but it’s great to walk around the area.
Very nice place for a hike.
Beautiful views great hike lots of  water in creek
No dogs allowed on the trail
A beautiful place to get away and reconnect with nature.

name: Red Rock Canyon Park Trail
formatted_address: 26701 Portola Pkwy, Foothill Ranch, CA 92610, USA

google_reviews.text: Very enjoyable! I recommend going the back way at the North Whiting Ranch entrance and then going on the Billy Goat trail to get to the Red Rock Trail. Then you can take this other trail back for a full loop. This loop is a lot of elevation gain, approximately 5-6 miles. Great views and you really get high up! Highly recommend doing it this way if you want more of a challenge. If you go the typical way than it's shorter and much easier, up to you.
Beautiful and straightforward hike. The water fountain didn’t work when I went, so bring a supply of water.

One weekend afternoon, we took our family for a hike at Red Rock Canyon Trail, located in Lake Forest. We spent a few hours doing the round trip, and it was worth it for the breathtaking views and peaceful atmosphere. Highly recommended!

been wanting to go for a year and finally went, completely worth the almost two hour hike. we parked in the shopping center next to ralph’s for free. the trail was so cool with different sections that felt like you were in a rainforest then a desert then arizona. pretty much all flat and not strenuous except for uneven rocky path near the end. incredibly that this formation is 40 million years old and right in our backyard!

A total hidden gem! Love this trail because it’s just the right length and you’ll encounter a ton of different scenery.
I started the trail via Borrego park, and my one piece of advice is to not pay for parking at Borrego. Plenty of other free spots around. It’s crazy how by just walking/hiking 2(ish) miles, you can see these awesome red rock formations! This is an out-and-back trail.
The lizards were out in numbers!

name: Turtle Rock Trail
formatted_address: Via Novella, Irvine, CA 92603, USA

google_reviews.text: It's a decent hike I guess. More of a stroll, walk around to look at nature. Mostly flat, and great for beginners.
Favorite local workout trails on the weekdays and great views for sunset & sunrise
Good for daily walk, fresh air, top of everything. Take your Jacket after 6 Pm
Beautiful trails with amazing views of Orange County.
Nice place for hiking, good for beginner.

name: Aliso Summit Trail
formatted_address: 30775 Aliso Summit Tr, Laguna Niguel, CA 92677, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice hiking spot with terrific views of the canyon and ocean. Be careful of the edge as the ground falls off very steeply- but the path is plenty wide and hard packed earth.

The Most beautiful hiking trail in all of South Orange County.  Breathtaking views of Pacific Ocean and Catlina Island. Along with some of the most beautiful homes and mansion you've ever seen i  your life! The whole trail like is about 2 miles or so round trip. Takes about 1 hr. To walk it.  See my photos attached.

I parked on Ridgeview Drive and enjoyed the views from dusk to sunset.
The trail is engrossing as you enjoy the changing light, but after sunset it is really dangerous without lights. The walk ends with a night view of the city.

No restrooms, no place to hide. We learned about the dangers of nature.

It was fun. Next time I will bring a change of clothes.

You can walk down to the CA #1 route or PCH in the Monarch Beach easily and the view is breathtaking! Some people say it is the most spectacular trail in the Aluso Niguel, if not at least it is the one of best one

This is the easiest and perhaps the best ocean view trail out here! Has tried a lot this this one is a total chill! Most recommend for sure. You can take further trails and a nice bike patch at canyon club and niguel blvd straight to dana point beaches! Superb!

name: Santa Ana River Trail- mile marker thirteen
formatted_address: Santa Ana, River Trail, Anaheim, CA 92806, USA

google_reviews.text: This is the SART mile marker closest to the Anaheim transportation center. As both Amtrak (reservation required) Metrolink (no reservation necessary) trains allow bikes this makes for an ideal point for a combination trip that involves different forms of transportation. It is also close to Angel Stadium, The Honda Center, Karl Strauss Brewing, and many other places.

name: Seaview Trail
formatted_address: 22632 Ocean Way, Laguna Niguel, CA 92677, USA

google_reviews.text: foggy and gorgeous view in the Saturday morning! It is a short trail only about 30 minutes walk. After dropping down a steep creek route, it only takes about 15 to 20 minutes to the peak. The view is awesome, you cannot see the ocean above the clouds, you can hear the sound from the ocean.  When you watch back to the road you are coming from, you see the Reeds alongside the rim of the hills. You may hear the music from the Nutcracker by Tchaikovsky, Dance of the Reed Flute

Words can not describe the experience you will have. This trail is magical. First date best top 5 of OC discussion over. I'm glad its low key main stream. You see professional photographers there everyday... its this glow at sunset that's a sure fire instant cover shot. You can see almost every mile of the beautifulest coast line in the world. 10💣💣💣💣💣💣💣💥💥💥💥💥💥💥💞😍😍😍🥰🥰🥰🥰🥰🤦‍♀️🤷‍♀️ guys take notice.

This is a short trail that offers great views of the Pacific Ocean. Access the trail at Badlands Park. It is a very short hike, which makes it a great choice for various hikers who want a nice walk with great views. More adventurous hikers can drop into Badlands Park and onto that trail.  Very adventurous hikers can follow Seaview Trail til is plays out and follow the unofficial trails down to Laguna Beach and PCH.
Best ocean view of any trail in Laguna Beach. You are hundreds of feet above pch, overlooking everything in the city.  Truly a must see during sunset!
Just an amazing trail especially if you take it down to the beach.

name: Doe Trail Park
formatted_address: 1 Doe Trail E, Irvine, CA 92604, USA

google_reviews.text: Play with my friends there. Clean lush grass and pristine pool.
Nice and well maintained small park, there's a small swimming pool and a jacuzzi
Watch out for poop
Relax, jog or run

name: Santa Ana River Trail- mile marker eight
formatted_address: Santa Ana, CA 92704, USA

google_reviews.text: This is between 1st Street (north side) and McFadden (south side)
the PAVEMENT is a great ENGINEERING project

name: Arroyo Trabuco Trail
formatted_address: Trabuco Creek Rd, Ladera Ranch, CA 92694, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful and has lots of little trails to explore.

This a tough hike due to distance and sun exposure. Take plenty of water and sunblock. While close to the stream it is cool and shaded, but it has long stretches along the edge of the Canyon where it gets unbearably bright and hot. There are many stream crossings which still have some running water so hiking poles are recommended. Great views of Santiago Peak and other local peaks. The streams are green and shaded with interesting flowers to look at. At the end of the trail there is a nice park with restrooms and drinking fountains. Lots of mountain bikers so keep an eye out for them.

A very nice trail for hiking or mountain biking up the canyon. Big trees and at times it's hard to tell that you're in the middle of the suburbs. Some creek crossings and I would not recommend going after rain.
#CashApp $TnAcoins1
Nice trail. Love being away from it all ... yet right in the heart of town. Quiet, very much “alone” out there.

name: Turtle Rock Preserve and Trail
formatted_address: University Trail, Irvine, CA 92612, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing preserve park, you enjoy the nature and the trail with all these trees and flowers around.
Nice shaded trail with a huge puddle of water that was fun to ride through.

I live literally right near the trail lol, It is great having coyotes coming to your house when you try to throw away garbage but yeah a bunch of dead bunnies, a bunch,
Also really convenient to go to and between schools as its where many people bike to go to the middle school or high school near the area.
Love it!
A scenic hiking trail with great route options and excellent hiking opportunities.

name: Rancho Sierra Vista/Satwiwa
formatted_address: 4121 Potrero Rd, Newbury Park, CA 91320, USA

google_reviews.text: Great Easy hike through vast meadows with mountains views leading to a serene 100’ waterfall cascading gently over sheer rock faces into natural pools, that are teaming with life. We found frogs, newts and tadpoles. Recommend this hike in temps lower than 85 degrees. The meadows and trail are full sun until you start down into the canyon where it is fully shaded. We did it in 90 degrees, it was rough. Great family outing, bring a picnic lunch to enjoy on a perfect huge flat rock just in front of the falls, in a shady covering of oak trees and ferns. Please keep it clean and pristine, pack out everything you brought in and more if you see something left behind. 🙌 We made a video on YouTube show casing this spot and how to follow the trail markers to get to the waterfall and pools. It’s called “Serene 100 foot waterfall hike in Santa Monica Mountains, a sycamore canyon adventure”

It's beautiful and perfect! You choose how intense you wanna make it!!
Awesome spot to take a 1st date if she's into trails. Nice and clean, very open!
Respect mama nature and Careful with the wildlife if you're blessed to come across it!
❤️‍🔥🌄💯💫

I recently visited Rancho Sierra Vista for a hiking and walking trail, and I must say that I was thoroughly impressed. The beautiful scenery and well-maintained trails made for an unforgettable experience. The trails offered a great mix of challenging terrain and scenic vistas, with something for everyone.
The trails are well-marked, making it easy to navigate and explore the park. Along the way, I was treated to stunning views of the surrounding mountains and valleys.
the park had plenty of parking, which made it easy to access.
Overall, I would highly recommend Rancho Sierra Vista to anyone looking for a beautiful and peaceful place to hike and explore. The park offers a unique and unforgettable experience that is not to be missed. I give it a solid five stars!
This is a very beautiful area to hike with easy to moderate trails.cespecially beautiful after the rain.

Update: Such variety, and if you're interested in native plants, a chance to see them full grown or in their different shapes throughout the season. If you plant them in your yard, you have the chance to take care of them all year round, keeping them more attractive, not dried out like they are in the wild.

Excellent walking trails, especially in winter. I didn't know about this place, just the Wendy Trail, but there are loads of California native plants and many birds. Just gorgeous. Get a plant app to find out what species. I found loads of coyote bushes in various blooming stages. Also toyon, popular food source for wildlife.

name: Point Mugu State Park
formatted_address: 9000 Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Awesome to come out here and the drive is amazing, closest part of PCH to LA where it feels very unreal to drive here alongside cliffs and all that, Sandy Dune is also a very nice experience, beautiful at night to see the stars and during the day. It is steep tho so it will be a workout to get to the top!

Beautiful. Breathtaking view. Plenty of parking. No restrooms. Be ware of seagulls. They are agressive and try to take your food when I was there. Also saw dolphins and pelicans enjoying the nature not far from the seashore.
Peaceful. Hardly anyone there. Nice picnic tables and shade trees.  We saw orcas and dolphins! Watch out for puddles of tar that washes up. Nasty to get off your feet.

A fairly easy hike with a graduate incline to catch some amazing views of the coast.
There's a paid parking lot and porta potties at the start of the Ray Miller trail, or park along the beach and walk into the Ray Miller trail. Watch out for swamp of mosquitos during the rainy season. Definitely need mosquitos repellent.
The trail is often quite narrow, so single file only. No dog or bike allowed.
Wear hat and sunscreen and bring plenty of water because there's very little shade. You'll catch some welcoming cool ocean breeze as you hike thru the switchbacks. The view is worth the hike.

🐬 Point Mugu State Park is a gem along the California coast, offering stunning ocean views, scenic hiking trails, and a peaceful beach escape. The variety of landscapes—from rugged mountains to sandy shores—makes it perfect for both outdoor adventurers and relaxation seekers. Just watch out for the dolphins; they’re always showing off with their flips, making the rest of us look bad!
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️.

name: Arroyo Verde Hiking Trails
formatted_address: Arroyo Verde Park Trail, Ventura, CA 93003, USA

google_reviews.text: Love the trails at Arroyo. The trails are along the outside of the park. There are easy trails as well as a couple of steep trails that really make you work.
This is a wonderful location for hiking, offering various levels of difficulty to suit all abilities. The view from the summit is truly incredible.

Nice park with grass areas,  tables, shade trees, and hiking paths.  You can set up grass volleyball nets, pop ups and have family gatherings.  Park closes its gates at dusk.  The entrance fee is $3.00, cash only.
Hiking at Arroyo Verde brings me joy. I love to see nature. Like I've stated before I enjoy what God has giving us. So, my brothers and sisters God bless and enjoy the fresh air.

It's such a nice park and a hiking area with many trails! Spring is the best season to go hiking with dogs. Beautiful flowers are everywhere, like the sea of flowers. There are many people and events in the park during weekends, only 2.5$ for parking. It was cloudy and cool yesterday,my golden girl loved it so much!

name: Ray Miller Trailhead
formatted_address: Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: I love this trail. You can just make a quick hike for about 2,5 miles or go all the way up if you have time. The view is spectacular

Absolutely incredible views. You'll see rolling hills, wildflowers, and ocean vistas galore. This is definitely one of my favorite hiking spots in southern California. There are remarkably few people since it's fairly remote. That also means there's no signal, so be prepared!

Water and restrooms are at the trailhead. Park on PCH for free or in the lot for a fee.

Awesome trail, 5 miles up and down, takes about 2.5 hours. Gorgeous view, bring water and sunscreen. Watch out for SNAKES!

Parking fee 3/hr or 8 all day, recommend all day! You can use the same ticket on nearby trails.

Great trail with great ocean and Mountain Views, almost no shade but the incline is not too steep, less than 10%..
You can pay for the La Jolla walk-in campsite at the parking lot. You have to make one transaction for each camper, otherwise the machine won’t work and just shows a message to use another card.
Day parking works fine.
As always in California, bring plenty of water!
It’s a slow incline and very easy to hike up there. I found it very enjoyable.

name: Wheeler Gorge Nature Trail 23W15
formatted_address: CA-33, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a awesome little hike right now the streams have plenty of water 💧 alot of beautiful things to see alot of mosquitos 🦟 so I would take mosquito repellant definitely worth the drive

We had an amazing time here! Our site had a good amount of privacy, a fire pit, grill, picnic table, and easy access to the creek. A great "primitive" camping experience. The staff was very nice and helpful. The loop trail up the hill is a great hike, but does need to be more clearly marked (when you see marker #2, cross the river, lol it took us a minute to figure that out). The creek is beautiful, clear and cold. Perfect for getting refreshed. There is only one pool like area that goes knee deep, but there are plenty of places to sit and relax by the creek. Definitely will be returning!

Nice campground with personable hosts who take great care of the property. Pit toilets are kept clean and not as smelly as you'd expect. We reserved site 68 but the host suggested 28 for us instead which cannot be pre-reserved and happened to be available. Right by the creek so we slept to the calming sounds of creek and crickets. Highway noise is a bit of a bummer but not enough of a deterrent to not come back. No running water so bring your own. We brought a propane firepit so we could still enjoy the comforts of camping and not disobey the fire restrictions.

Becky and Bob are so great! Nicest camp hosts I've met.
Campgrounds are large and well maintained, beautiful area

All campsites are near the road, no bear boxes, no potable water on site, campsites are close to each other, many campers stay up late to party. Nearby nature trail is very short and currently very overgrown with poison oak. Not much else to do here. More bugs than any other campground I've visited due to the nearby Creek. Disorganized camp host. But the bathrooms were reasonably clean. I wound up cutting my trip a day short to just get outta there. Campsite #38

name: Matilija Trail - Trailhead
formatted_address: 3760-3998 Matilija Canyon Rd N, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: About 10-15 spots but fills quickly. Arrived at 7am on a Sunday and plenty of room. The first couple miles of trail are through a residential area so stay on trail. We went to the falls which is treacherous but fun! Would recommend only for rock hopper dogs and children old and brave enough to scramble.

Beautiful hike with some rough spots (likely from the rain this year) and overgrown bush. It’s lovely right now bursting wit colors but I was concerned with rattlers. Didn’t see one but bring a walking stick or it could be a short hike. Wide open sun, heavy sunscreen.

Amazing views, 4.7 mile hike in so 9.5 round trip.
Bring pants has a lot of bushes and trees.
There's a lot of rocks so bring comfortable hiking shoes. Super peaceful and quiet out here

After a huge rainfall and a landslide in the beginning of 2023 the entire landscape has changed. In order to actually get to the trail, you need to cross the river twice and make your own way. Unfortunately the lower campground is completely demolished. The trail is overgrown now and some areas are hard to reach.

Awesome views, almost no other hikers.
This place rock!  Trails could use some maintenance but otherwise a fun time.  About 2hrs round trip, the way back is faster.

name: Franklin Trail Trailhead
formatted_address: 4769 Foothill Rd, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Great ride but no emtb which is ashame but great views with beautifulwood benchesto sit on,  good work out and a few off beat trails that were challenging.

This place should 6 stars if I could. Far and away exceeded all of my expectations. Easy access to the trail, plenty of parking in the community, no adventure pass required, cell signal throughout the hike for easy communications, beautiful beach scenery and lovely greenery. This hike doesn't offer many tall trees or shade but when I came in the afternoon a couple hours before sunset, it was absolutely perfect. With just enough sunshine for a clearly lit path, stunning sunset colors splashing over the mountainside leading to the ocean, it was an absolute joy for all my senses. Not to mention, extremely secluded and only passing a couple people for the 2 hours on the trail.
Popular trail, dog friendly, flat for the first part then a steady climb, views of ocean along the way and plenty of benches to rest at
Great local trail! Gets busy over the weekend so if you plan to ride it be sure to take a bell!
Beautiful trail! Wish I had gone earlier to hike up more of it but still made for a gorgeous sunset hike.

name: Santa Paula Punch Bowls
formatted_address: Creek, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: I’ve been on this hike 2 times so far! I’ve only made it up to the first punchbowls which are 4 miles up. It’s beautiful! The terrain is quite easy with the exception of a few steep inclines. The water is up this time of year in Jan so when we had to cross the creek we were a little above knee level a few times. Parking is a little scarce. I’ve had to park in the side of the road both times. Definitely a fun hike! Go check it out!

The pool was fantastic! Let's get that out of the way. 20 something crowd. 30% have a bt speaker in nature. 5% of hikers litter. Wait till the flow drops a bit if you're not cool with 8 water crossings at a brisk pace.
Quite challenging but it was so fun via the entire hike. Following the Diamond signs and you will be fine. The trail is very fun. It was an adventure!

Very hard rocky hike. Takes 2hrs to get to the water ponds. Some one got lost, it could be dangerous at night. Bring some flash lights and make sure you dont pass the petroleum pumpjacks on you way out. You can bring a glowing spraycan to identify the exit with arrows & sings. Remember when your coming back down,the exit will be on left hand side. Have fun and enjoy this great hike
So nice to hike. The view so enjoyable.  Highly recommend it.

name: North Fork Matilija Creek Trail
formatted_address: Matilija Canyon Rd, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Always a great time. Be courteous of the residents as you're walking through their long driveway. Hike worth every step!

The trail is overgrown in some locations with bushes that can scratch your legs if you're not wearing pants. Overall, the trail is not too difficult. It's level most of the way to Matilija middle camp. There are several streams crossing that weren't too difficult to cross in November .  There are some great views of the canyon once you get to the top of the switchbacks. Be careful though the trail does have some poison oak in some spots.
Trail has changed some. It was a great hike!
Nice, well marked trail. Many campsites for backpacking. Always check to make sure the creek is flowing if you plan to filter water
This place is absolutely amazing

name: Big Rock Preserve
formatted_address: Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Cool place, park is close by also if you want to go swim.

Great little preserve ran by the Ventura Land Trust (VLT). Entrance is off the bike path that follows Hwy 33 towards Ventura. Park under the 33 Hwy and Casitas Vista Road. Hide any valuables out of sight in your car so nobody walking around gets any ideas. Walk towards the park and then down the bike path a few minutes and you'll see the trailhead sign on your right. The entire trail(s) is only a mile or so but you'll find the river back there. According to the VLT you can enter the river and explore just be safe and respectful of the nature there. There can occasionally be vagrants near the area so I'd always go with a buddy. Have fun!

What a beautiful little spot to see nature and the beautiful vegetation that grows along it and all the the more beautiful that is a protected area and steps from the Ojai Valley/ Ventura river bike path
I came here on a field trip with my 9 yr. Old daughters class. It was hosted by volunteers who take students on nature walks n the explore the  preserve.
We did some tree planting there today. Great place and they are doing great work.

name: Ojai Meadows Preserve - Lomita Ave Entrance
formatted_address: 398 S Lomita Ave, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Always a beautiful place to see some wildlife and talk a walk on a cool day.

This is a lovely little place to walk. It can be short and easy or you can use it to access the Saddle Trails for a more strenuous workout.
I frequently walk dogs there and my only complaint are the many out-of-control unleashed dogs I have encountered. It can be at best, really annoying and at worst, downright dangerous.
Nice little loop walk. Very flat, not a lot of rocks in the path.
Great place for a casual hike. There are a few benches to stop and sit

One of best places in town To take a after work stroll or walk around on the weekend and enjoy the pond when there’s water in it and the trails seeing and experiencing all the new native said have been planted in established over the years it’s really great that they’re doing here with developing habitat and water keep up the good work Ojai!

name: Carpinteria Bluffs Trailhead
formatted_address: Bailard Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text:
One of the sweetest wildlife experiences on the Central Coast. 🦭

Visited during pupping season (Feb–April) and saw dozens of fluffy baby seals resting with their moms from the bluff overlook — like a live nature documentary. The views are beautiful and it feels peaceful and special. Nice trails, bike path, and sunset!

Beach is closed to protect the seals (please respect it), but the overlook gives you a perfect view. Bring binoculars if you have them!

This place is beautiful and worth visiting more than once… was nice to visit in the afternoon to later have dinner in the evening with local eats, we chose Teddy’s which was great!  Looking forward to our next visit and to have better footage of the seals and the wonderful surrounding coastline.  It was nice too without the seals being in their sanctuary area.

This is such a beautiful morning walk. We entered the park through the neighborhood on Calle Ocho, and walked from the Tar Pits Park area to the south end of the nature preserve and back. There were gorgeous yellow and purple flowers blooming, and lots of docile honey bees foraging for pollen. The path roams along the bluffs, sometimes more inland, sometimes right against the edge. It wanders past the Seal Sanctuary, which is a must see. You can see seals sunbathing, playing in the waves, and bouncing on the sand. We saw pups nursing, and following their mums around. There are some bikes, but everyone was riding slowly and considerately. It's a lovely, easy walk. I was just fine in Crocs, carrying a cup of coffee.
This is such a beautiful spot! The sunset was so pretty and there were so many shells and sea animals.
A very nice and small hiking area. A great place to stretch your legs while heading up the coast. A few short trails with some great ocean and mountain views.

name: Arroyo Verde Park
formatted_address: Foothill Rd, Ventura, CA 93003, USA

google_reviews.text:
For a few months each year this is one of the most beautiful parks in California, and an absolute gem of trails of varying difficulty and perfect for hiking and trail running.

There is plenty of parking, facilities of all kinds including very clean bathrooms, and fields and picnic areas so it's great to take the family for an afternoon.

This is a wonderful place for a medium hike. I like to come here, look at the scenery and have fresh air. I like the nature that our God has bless us with.  See my pictures and judge for yourself.  Like always my brothers and sisters enjoy what God has given us.

Arroyo Verde is a great park. A lot of ppl use the trails for hiking. If you happen to bring a dog, keep them on a leash there are snakes, coyotes and mountain lions (I've never seen one). If you go all the way in back there are baseball diamonds. But because park is surrounded by hills, sometimes phone reception is nonexistent. Bring water because there are only a few water fountains.
Love arroyo. Been hiking and picnic’ing here for years. Dog days are fun. And now there is an area for kids in the back. Enjoy the hikes keep it clean please.

Plenty of open space for the dogs to run around in as well as open trails to walk around , trails are easy to moderate with good views of surrounding areas. There is a small fee to get in like around $3  but there is also restrooms and a playground for younger kids

name: Camarillo Grove Park
formatted_address: 6968 Camarillo Springs Rd, Camarillo, CA 93012, USA

google_reviews.text:
What a nice park location with a one mile trail and surrounded by beautiful mountains and the nearby Camarillo ranch property. There is a dog park area and plenty of room for kids to play and playground equipment.
Watch out for rattlesnakes as signs are posted all over the park.
Highly recommended

Nice park and picnic area. Hiking trail was beautiful and the nature and scenery made us feel like we were far from the town/city. Love the information located along the trail. Made a perfect opportunity to teach my kids about the nature and birds that you see while hiking.

This is a wonderful little park that has an area for dogs to roam, some picnic tables, a children's play area, and a party area, and some really nice shirt hiking trails! There are plenty of signs with information about the wildlife and nature in the area. You do have to pay to park, although it seems to be on an honor system. There is a really nice view from the bench at the top of the trails, it would be nice if there was a trail map but it's pretty easy to follow if you use satellite view on Google Maps.
$3 fee during the week and $5 fee on the weekend. Off leash park. Easy short hike around the hills. Great views. Lots of picnic areas where you can bbq on grill.

This park is small, but beautiful. Seems to cater more for people who have dogs. When I visited this park, I had been in my truck driving all day and just needed a break from driving and the heat. This park had plenty of open areas and parking and SHADE!! I will definitely visit again. There's a daily fee of $3 for weekdays and $5 for weekends. It's worth every penny.

name: Ojai Meadows Preserve
formatted_address: 1601 Maricopa Hwy, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: This place so peaceful and beautiful! Thank God for all who have helped keep the Meadows healthy & alive. I Lucy, love going on walks with my dog Charlie he is a three pawed heartbreaker say hi if you see us there. Have a great day and thanks for reading.
Cute nature Oasis just steps from shopping center. Child and elderly friendly, flat ground but option for a nice hike too.
A great place to take the kids or your dogs, lots of potential for photography or condos. 10/10 would do again.
Always a beautiful hike. Lots of horses use this trail so please keep dogs on leash. Good parking but it can be overwhelming on the weekends.
Nice, easy nature walk. Very chill and relaxed. Dog friendly.

name: Ventura River Preserve - Meyer Rd Trailhead
formatted_address: 1290 Meyer Rd, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice little spot to hik around and explore the river! Easy flat ground, and it looks like you can do some loops up into the canyons, although I have a sprained knee right now so I was keeping it easy. Will definitely explore more of this area when I'm healed! The canyons are intriguing....

Really awesome hiking. We followed a fairly easy trail, but at the time the river was flowing pretty well so we didn't cross it. I'd recommend coming here prepared to cross the river if you want to get the full experience. Not too busy and really nice open spaces. The trail was well kept.

Flat path along the riverbed. Many different trials in the area- can be as long or as short as you want.  The river is seasonal, sometimes fairly strong, sometimes weak. Lots of horses along path, so keep dogs on leash.
Me and my family enjoyed the hot hike it was adventurous the river was so dry I’m not sure how long it was like that other than that it was beautiful.
Awesome hiking trail and water crossings! Safe and convenient parking!

name: Romero Canyon Trailhead
formatted_address: Los Padres National Forest, 1101 Romero Canyon Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: Hiking the Romero Canyon Trailhead is one of those experiences that feels both peaceful and energizing. The trail winds through a mix of shaded canyon paths and sunlit ridgelines, offering a steady climb with just enough challenge to keep things interesting. Along the way, you’re surrounded by coastal sage, oak trees, and quiet stretches where the only sounds are birds overhead and water trickling down the creek. As you climb higher, the views open up—Montecito below, the ocean stretching beyond, and on clear days, the Channel Islands on the horizon. It’s a perfect blend of effort and reward.
Great hike! Pleanty of unique features and shaded areas to make it worth the drive up here!
Great hike, you can make it as hard or easy as you like depending  on the trail you choose to use. Interesting option after have being on most of not all los Padres National park hiking trails
Romero trail. Many variations and trails available if you have the time and the energy. Great conditions and views.  Always be curtious to fellow trail users
Leg burning 6.6 mile climb and technical downhill. Hold on for dear life!

name: Valley View Preserve
formatted_address: Shelf Rd, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing hike with scenic views.  On-leash dog friendly.  Very challenging.  There are a few challenging switch-backs.  3.5 miles & 900ft elevation gain.  Highly recommended.
We went hiking on The Lucy trail and then on to Foothill trail. It was pretty steep and plenty of switchbacks. Great exercise and pretty good views of geology!
Bitchin' and beautiful. Stout trails that illicit concentration whether hiking or biking. There was a reason why I lived in the Ojai Valley for 8 years!

This is a trail that is actually off of the Shelf Rd trail (which is not shown on Google Maps). While hiking up Shelf you will find to your left Luci trail as well as Fox Canyon trail, both trails are much longer and difficult than Shelf.  If you stick with Shelf Rd you will get to see an overview of Ojai at the top, there is even a couple of benches for you to rest and enjoy the view (night time hikes are amazing here!) This is one of the more popular hikes in town and it's also a favorite of mine. (parking is limited and please respect the locals)
The views are spectacular and the trails range from advanced to very easy. There are miles of trails for all levels of hikers and walkers to discover and enjoy

name: Foster Park
formatted_address: 37 Casitas Vista Rd, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
As of today (January 2026) park is open to pedestrian and bicycle traffic only. You'll need to park off the road and under the bridge. The parking lot is being redone.

I'd say that the best time to enjoy this place is now while the parking lot is closed and most people that know of this place still think it's closed. On weekends you'll have the odd family picnics there and there, and people frolicking in the cold, shallow river water. Bathrooms are open and functional. There's even a brand new filtered water dispenser installed! Water tastes great. The park isnt kept pristine like before -- some overgrown grass and leaf litter -- but certainly there isn't excessive overgrowth.

Nice campground between Ojai and Ventura. Make sure to make reservations ahead of time. It’s just really close to the main road so there’s a lot of road noise. Bathrooms are decent and maintained well (no shower facilities though).

It's currently(as of 01 Nov2025) closed while undergoing renovations, but it can be accessed via bicycle and on foot. It is a lovely park with beautiful Sycamore trees. I don't know when it will officially reopen.

This park is beautiful! Lovely cascading brooks along dense beautiful Oak, Sycamore Eucalyptus, Cypress, & other Native Beauties! Playground area for the kids is stunning, littered with backdrops of Autumnal Maroons along the Sulfur Mountain (along the east Hwy 33). Lush green trails grant walkers & horseback riders access to the Ojai Valley Trail! & don't forget to check out Foster Bowl, which is located just North past the park on the left hand side along west Mountain! Foster park is a site to see!
Space 16 and 17 are difficult to back into if either space is occupied. No Showers. This park is too shaded with trees to utilize solar charging.

name: Serra Cross
formatted_address: Father Serra Cross, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Father Serra Cross – Ventura, CA

Father Serra Cross (The Cross) is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places in Ventura. The views are absolutely breathtaking — you can see the ocean, the mountains, and the entire city all at once. It feels like you're looking at a postcard come to life.

One of my favorite ways to get here is by taking the trail from the Ventura Botanical Gardens. The hike is gorgeous, filled with native plants, ocean breezes, and stunning vistas the entire way. Whether you hike up or drive, the destination is worth every step.

You can have weddings up here, enjoy the trails, or simply sit on one of the benches and soak in the view. I come often just to sit quietly, pray, and look out at the ocean. It’s truly a magnificent spot that brings a sense of calm and gratitude.

There’s usually plenty of parking, and the area is easy to access. To me, this place represents Ventura at its finest — peaceful, beautiful, and full of heart. In my opinion, it’s one of the most beautiful places in the Ventura County.

I’ll include pictures so you can see just how incredible it really is.

If I was a few years younger the hike up would of been better but the drive was more what I needed. Their is a few turnouts on the way up but if you have time to wait for a top park, its worth it to watch the sunset

Serra Cross is a beautiful place to reflect on the goodness of God and His creation. Here, one has a full view if the city of Ventura from the Pacific, up the Westside toward Ojai. Here, one can sit and watch the view, see the sunset, or have a picnic. There are also nearby trails for hiking.

Perched high above the city, the park offers breathtaking panoramic views of Ventura, the coastline, and the Channel Islands on a clear day. Sunset is pure magic up here!

The park is small and simple & parking can be a little tight, especially at sunset, so be prepared to park further down and walk up the hill. But honestly, the short hike is worth every step.

I’d definitely recommend bringing a light jacket since it can get breezy, even on warm days

Amazing view
Very clean and friendly place
Have a good parking spots

name: Toro Canyon Park
formatted_address: 576 Toro Canyon Park, Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: Trees canopy the drive up overlooking Santa Barbara. Very clean and maintained. BBQ, picnic tables, restroom, playground, dog park, easy to moderate hiking trail up to a gazebo that offers stellar views

I came hoping for an easy to medium hike to great views from the Gazebo. The hike on a Sunday in late September was dry/dusty, mostly in the sun...I was the only person on the trail on the way up which was nice. At the top there is a gazebo and a plaque in memory of some firefighters who were injured and perished in a 1971 fire. The view is really just over the park and up the hillsides, nothing too spectacular or interesting.
The rest of the park has some nice looking play structures for kids and campsites. The restrooms were clean and stocked which was appreciated.

This park is amazing and has 4 different areas that can be reserved for groups up to 200 people. There is a dog park, playground, grills, common area, restrooms, a beautiful hike, plenty of parking and the drive to Toro Canyon is very scenic. No camping.
Really cute park. Lots of places to walk the yes playgrounds are really cute. The hike is really steep but short and sweet. The gazebo was cozy and we had a snack
Beautiful scenes for our eyes. Peaceful environment close to the noisy city. A place to breath clean air and listen to nature.

name: Scorpion Canyon Loop Hike
formatted_address: Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: It's a heavily visited, short trail. It's got reasonable vista points. Compared to other trails on this island the rating
The view is really beautiful on the mountain. The trail is not hard but sometimes it’s narrow.

The best of all the trails to see the island interior that you can easily do in a day. About 5 miles with 500' elevation gain. Definitely, hike up the canyon first as it is steep and a lot easier to go up than down.

I recommend you hike the smugglers rd last because it’s very steep uphill otherwise and you see more views at the end as a reward

Mondo's Beach: A Surfing Gem (with a Catch)
Mondo's Beach is a beautiful stretch of coastline, a popular spot for surfers, particularly those learning the ropes.  The gentle, rolling waves make it an ideal location for beginners to hone their skills.  The views are simply stunning, offering panoramic vistas of the coastline, perfect for enjoying a sunset or just soaking in the natural beauty.  It's easy to see why this spot is so beloved by local surfers from Ventura.
However, a word of caution: the localism is alive and well at Mondo's. While the waves are inviting, this is definitely a spot where the established surfers hold their ground. If you're not from the area, it's best to be respectful and observe the unspoken rules of the lineup.  Dropping in on someone or not taking your turn will likely earn you some glares and maybe even a few words.  While the locals are generally friendly, they are protective of their break.
So, if you're a beginner surfer looking for a great spot to learn, Mondo's is fantastic – as long as you're a local or go with one.  If you're visiting from out of town, it's probably best to stick to your home break to avoid any potential friction.  The beauty and surfability of Mondo's are undeniable, but it's important to be aware of the strong local presence.

Great beach! Took my 8 year old and husband here for a surf lesson. Weather was perfect and parking is along the road.  My oldest daughter is disabled,  so it was a little difficult to get her down to the beach because you have to climb down some rocks. But was well worth it!

Oxnard Beach State Park is truly a slice of coastal paradise. Nestled between Ventura and Malibu, this park offers a serene escape from the hustle and bustle, with its wide sandy beaches, lush greenery, and tranquil ocean views. From the moment you arrive, you feel a sense of calm as the cool sea breeze greets you and the sound of waves gently crashing fills the air.

The park is perfect for a relaxing day outdoors, whether you’re strolling along the well-maintained pathways, enjoying a picnic with friends and family, or simply sitting on one of the benches to take in the breathtaking views of the Pacific. The sand dunes and ice plant-covered landscapes create a beautiful, natural backdrop that makes every moment feel like a postcard.

What makes Oxnard Beach truly special is how uncrowded and peaceful it is. You can walk for miles along the beach, with palm trees swaying overhead, and not feel overwhelmed by crowds. It’s a great spot for families, couples, or solo adventurers looking to unwind and reconnect with nature. The sunsets here are magical—watching the sun dip below the horizon, casting hues of gold and orange over the water, is an unforgettable experience.

Whether you’re biking, jogging, flying kites, or just enjoying a peaceful walk, Oxnard Beach State Park offers something for everyone. It’s a hidden gem along the coast, where the beauty of California’s beaches can be enjoyed in a quiet and relaxed setting. I highly recommend visiting this magical place—you’ll leave feeling refreshed, recharged, and connected to the natural world.

Other than that the kids had fun.

No working public restrooms. City workers frequent the property but never fix the bathrooms, this is likely to target the homeless community, and may have to be dealt with via a class action lawsuit against the City of Oxnard.

They ignore the clearly posted signs prohibiting off-leash dogs, and neither Animal Control nor the police seem to care.

VENTURA STILL NEEDS A REAL SKATEPARK!

There are picnic tables and benches scattered throughout but best spots are under the trees so perfect for bringing a picnic planner and a book. name: Constitution Park

google_reviews.text: Cute park. Was able to get in a mile by walking the gounds as well as around City Hall. The roses and trees are so beautiful. Concert in the park is always fun, the bands are great. The atmosphere is perfect, especially the pink sky as the sun sets.

Constitution Park is honestly one of the most peaceful spots in Camarillo. It has this calm, steady energy that just makes you want to slow down and breathe a little deeper. The grass is always super green and soft, and the trees give just the right amount of shade—like nature’s own canopy. It’s the kind of place where you can just sit, zone out, and feel grounded.

It’s also perfect for playing Pokémon Go. The layout is great for walking loops, and there are plenty of PokéStops and spawns to keep it fun without feeling overwhelming. I love how quiet it is—just the sound of birds, a light breeze, and your footsteps. No chaos, no rush. Just chill vibes and good catches.

Whether you’re walking with someone, playing solo, or just escaping for a little peace, this park really hits the mark.
