name: Ventura Community Park
formatted_address: 7RC5+PF, Ventura, CA 93003, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a great park for walking. It even has mile markers if you like to keep track. There are bathrooms towards the edge of the park which have been very well taken care of each time I've visited them lately. The walking path is dog friendly and there's plenty of those baggie dispensers and trash cans to clean up after them. I haven't seen too many messes left behind. I have seen lots of bunnies, squirrels and a snake lately so dogs on a leash are a must for runners' and wild life safety.

Ventura Community Park is a large, clean, and beautiful park that offers something for everyone. It has wide open spaces, nice walking paths, and plenty of areas to relax or enjoy outdoor activities. The park is well-maintained, safe, and perfect for families, friends, and individuals.
The playground is big and fun for children, with lots of equipment to keep them entertained. Restrooms are available and kept in good condition, making visits more comfortable. The park is also dog-friendly, so it’s a wonderful place to bring pets for exercise and fresh air.
There are picnic areas where families can gather, eat, and enjoy the outdoors. With so much space and a welcoming atmosphere, it’s also great for sports, exercise, or just a quiet walk.

Ventura Community Park (known still by locals as Kimball Park) is my favorite place to walk. I've been going almost daily for three years. Most people are nice, (some are grumpy if you're wearing a red ball cap) and greet you along the path. I wish an annual pass for the pool was available but you can go for $10 last time I checked if there's no swim meet that day. The Star Jasmine bushes on one side of the pool smell awesome a few weeks out of the year. Two restrooms, three if you count the pool area. As mentioned before the E-bike kiddies can be annoying but haven't been racing around the walking area as much. Police come through often and the fruit vendors (and some times Mr. softee) are there when soccer ⚽ games are happening.

Tons of off leash dogs with poor recall and irresponsible, inconsiderate owners. It blows my mind how people think just because their dog is friendly they can leave them off leash. Other people need to walk their dogs and train them too and your “friendly” dog running up to reactive dogs is putting them 10 steps back in training. If you want to do that go to off leash dog parks or beaches so we can avoid you.

Kimball Park offers convenient amenities, including restrooms situated in the main swim facility lobby, one just north of the same facility, and another near the soccer fields on the southeast corner of the park. Both the soccer and baseball fields are impressively well-maintained, ensuring a great experience for sports enthusiasts. The park boasts a 1.25-mile walking path that's perfect for leisurely strolls, invigorating runs, or enjoyable bike rides. What makes this park even more appealing is the seamless connection of its walking path to another route parallel to the 126 freeway. This connecting path opens up exciting opportunities to explore the government center and venture further west towards Barnes and Noble. If you're seeking a well-equipped and scenic park for outdoor activities, Kimball Park is a fantastic choice.

One thing the park does not have is a good play set area for children.  There is a small one near the swim facility, but definitely not the size you would expect for such a large park

name: Encanto Park
formatted_address: 5300 Avenida Encanto, Camarillo, CA 93012, USA

google_reviews.text: While visiting Camarillo, I've made an effort to play with my grandchildren at the many family parks. The management of the city parks is impressive! Clean and well maintained grounds at each park. Dogs have been present at some of these parks, however, the owners I've run into have been most respectful and even asked if I was okay with their dog being off leash. I am happy that these pet owners care about community.

Unfortunately, more and more people are bringing large dogs off leash. Family Parks like these have small children and toddlers. They also have small dogs and puppies on leashes learning how to play at the park... Leash on Parks like this are designed this way for families to feel safe small dogs on leash or large dogs on leash that need to be on a leash can go there to play and be safe....
Not a place for large dogs off leash.

-There needs to be more signage notifying people that this is NOT an off leash dog park.
-I love this park, it’s beautiful, but there are many off leash dogs and many do not fully listen to their owners. I have had multiple off leash dogs run up to my leashed dog and disobey their owner’s commands. I’ve even had one attack my dog and I at this park.
Small park in great neighborhood. One playground with sand and swings. Good for ages 2+. Lots of grass for running and even playing sports. No restrooms which is a bummer

Very nice. Its close to home and if I get there
Mid day there is no one there. So my dog can run free, without other dogs or kids playing on the equipment.

name: Fremont Tot Park
formatted_address: 1130 N M St, Oxnard, CA 93030, USA

google_reviews.text: What a cute park my granddaughter loved it here plenty of places where she can climb on and I loved the open concept whereas she won't bump her head, nice swing sets.
Good place to play soccer, you can go there with the family and enjoy the day

This review is only for the playground, not the soccer area--the soccer area is in a separate section and I haven't been there. My 5 year old son loves the playground and that's important. He is always asking to go here. The playground is small, but it has enough things for children to do. And it's nice that they have a water fountain. But the eating area is very small and there is no bathroom. And the upkeep with maintenance and cleaning is very poor, especially the cleaning. I was very disappointed the last two times I was there. The first time, there was a lot of vulgar graffiti, mostly in one area. My son can't read yet, so I didn't have to explain anything to him, but I still found it disgusting. The second time, someone had brought what looked like a smoothie or something and the cup and spoon were on the ground and the smoothie was all over one of the play structures, so I couldn't let my son play on it because it was sticky. More cleaning is needed, but also for people reading this, please throw your trash away in the trash cans and if you bring food, use the eating area to eat. I won't say anything else about the graffiti because I doubt the people responsible for it would be reading this.

It's a good little park for the neighborhood. It's nice for the families that live in the apartments. And mostly a million thank yous to my neighbor Marvin who has walked this neighborhood for years picking up the trash. For the most part this park is safe and has a good little variety for the children to exercise.

Saturday the best soccer entretainment for all . the crew who is incharge of organizing this events are well dedicated , early in the morning start all the arrangements to serve our community with the most delisious HOT DOGS & SNACK IN TOWN ,.ice cream and it makes it a fun place for families with the great attitude people have they make everyone feel like Family

name: Community Center Park
formatted_address: Oxnard, CA 93030, USA

google_reviews.text: Avoid! Homeless encampments and trash strewn about. Knives, razors, needles, and various other objects are everywhere. One of the worst parks in town…

Has great play areas. Good for children and pets. There is a  building with bathrooms very clean.
Great place to watch movies in a big screen.  They do have a play ground for tiny ones.

Beautiful park, I believe your referring to the park that I only know as " Lions Park." It's across from our church "Gold Coast Fellowship," a Freewill Baptist Church  ~"Where the 'SON' always shines!"~
I  am only rating so low, do to reason being of the occasional type people that sometimes frequent the park. Which are sadly, drug addicts that are also sometimes homeless! If it wasn't for this reason, the park will be a beautiful place to take your children to play and have picnics, but of this apparent reason, this is why I rate the park {w/(2) stars-☆☆} so it's so low! Hopefully this problem some day will change, for the better again! To be a park of
(5) star ☆☆☆☆☆} quality once again, for the children and their families to play and have picnics, I hope and pray!

An okay place to go hang out before sundown. A lot of homeless people sleep at this park day and night. I've always known this park as lion park or bum park.  Churches feed the homeless every Saturday or Sunday . Playground needs an upgrade . My kids and I go here when the park across the street is full.

Hidden gem of a park with tall beautiful trees providing plentiful shade. Large swathes of open fields for frisbee or a little game of catch and play ground with slides & swings for the kids. Picnic tables with grills and trash receptacles ideal for an outdoor meal. Popular place with the usual crowds found in any American city. Generally avoid the pavilion and bathroom. Plentiful parking both on-site and at the adjacent Arts building.

name: Libbey Park
formatted_address: 210 S Signal St, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: A wonderful, wonderful park. Plenty of shade, a nice water fountain and plenty of seating in the entrance courtyard, several parking options, one of the best playgrounds I’ve ever seen, short hiking paths, nice tennis courts at the back, clean toilets and water fountains available.

Libbey Park had an international dance show today. A lot of dance shows were scheduled throughout the day.

I liked the park. It is filled with trees and has nice walkways amidst the grass and dedicated areas - small bowl, small stage, fountain, and restrooms. I took 1 picture of a performance and the day's schedule.

The apparatus overall is very nice and modern. So many slides, swings, and even a grassy hill with cardboard boxes nearby to “sled” down. Some of the circular swings looked rusty and old but overall, the park was modern and very clean.

Nice park with a small plaza with a central fountain. A playground, sure to provide plenty of fun for the kids, is located here. Tennis courts with grandstands and the Libby Bowl amphitheater are major attractions, as is the old jail.

It's a cute park with tennis court where some world famous tennis players played. This small town Ojai according to the locals is for weekends. We found it odd that shops did not open until 11 or 12. Some open only Thursday through Sunday.
But it was very sunny unlike the gloomy seaside.
Traffic must be awful on weekends.

name: Veterans Memorial Park
formatted_address: Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: Decent park.  I took my toddler son here, and he enjoyed the small play equipment but was a little disappointed that there were no swings.  We went in broad daylight on a Monday morning and felt safe, especially given that the police station is around the corner, but there were still some interesting elements around.  There are bathrooms, but we did not use them so I can’t comment on their condition.
Restroom at 10th and Ventura, children's play area, lots of trees and shade. Great place to have an aid station for the Ride for the Red this year. Seagulls love this park.
Went to a church service with my neighbor awesome people great srv.

Everybody on the premises of the park or drinking high or on drugs playing the playground with your kids do not let them see anybody drinking or sleeping or just sitting there yelling and remind everyone in the 805 and all over This is right across the street from where the Santa Paula PD had its headquarters outside Nobody gets any citations and right across the street from the city hall. WoW
Great skateboard park w playground w several benches. Good for picnics...no BBQ grills

name: Chumash Park
formatted_address: 750 S Petit Ave, Ventura, CA 93004, USA

google_reviews.text: Ventura parks and rec fails pretty badly with upkeep on this park. We do go, the playground is decent for age 3-6. But it’s not clean, the bathrooms are a no-go for us.

Single mom to 7 children and this is one of our favorite parks in the county. Clean park of rubbish and debris. Bathrooms are kept clean and trash is never over full. This park allows you to enjoy a variety of activities with your children like soccer, catch, excerise  and family reunuins or birthdays.
Cute little park, kids practice soccer here & people are always walking their pups. Just not the best place to be after hours.

This is a nice park. Lots of smaller kids sports games/learning sessions are held here as there is a lot of open ground areas with plenty of sun plus quite a few trees for shade.

Playground equipment (swings, slides, etc.) for smaller children and some for a bit older. Wish there were a few more benches with shade.

It is very dark at night unless you stay on the sidewalk by the street, they could use more lights. But they do provide doggy bags and  though we bring our own have had to use so appreciated.
Take a little walk after dinner or bring kid to play in the little playground

name: West Park
formatted_address: 450 W Harrison Ave, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: West park is a community center with a gymnasium,  sports fields, 2 playgrounds, nice cement path, tetherball poles and has a large cement play area too. It's next to Sheridan Way elementary and the skate park. The bike trail runs right behind it and there is access. There is nice large grass area and a picnic bench. The playgrounds have soft padding underneath. One side has toddler swings and the other side has big kid swings.
Nice little park! Big grassy area to relax in. Areas for activities. Easy access to the bike/walking trail. Always a nice breeze. Next to the freeway so it is sometimes noisy.

Looks nice from pictures but I have never been there . I'm not sure why or how but my phone was detected there and very late at night for a half hour.
I guess my phone grew legs and ran there on its own because I have never been there and would not go there at 10:30 or 11:00 PM.
Makes me think that's for sure.

This park is one of the only parks available to the Avenue area, luckily it has undergone some work in the last few years and isn't half bad. If you're ever in the area, it's great for a picnic and sports with its sizeable grass field.
Remodeled new grass new plants beautiful beautiful. Its a dog park too

name: Blanche Reynolds Park
formatted_address: 450 Valmore Ave, Ventura, CA 93003, USA

google_reviews.text: Excellent neighborhood park. Areas are spaced far enough between each other, we usually setup large combo bounce houses at this park.There are a few picking tables scattered around the park so if you plan on celebrating a party plan to bring in tables and chairs. It’s a first come first serve basis. Playground is for kids 5 and under and there are two picnic tables, if you want to do a small party get there early, Restrooms are nearby within 50 ft
Arroyo verde hills are here next to the park. The views and hiking are wonderful!!!! There are gorgeous views of the ocean and shoreline from the tops of the hills.

Such a great little park. Its close to the street which offers ease getting in and out of the car and it has a fence around it which is nice for keeping the kids from running into it.  It's a sand based park with play equipment, swings and a spinning merry- go-round style ride. There are public restrooms. There is a large grass area and sports fields. Rainbow bridge park is just behind it but there is no access when school is in session.  The park is clean and in a nice area. It feels safe and peaceful.
The park is located in a safe neighborhood. It's fun for young kids.

Great neighborhood park.  It’s a good way to get to know your neighbors and find some local kids to play with.  The homeless has been a problem in the past, it seems to have died down in the past year.  I don’t use the public bathrooms unless you give them a good visual first. Homeless tend to sleep in the bathrooms from time to time.  Easy parking the park is clean and trash is dumped a few times a week.

name: Thompson Park
formatted_address: 201 Imperial St, Oxnard, CA 93030, USA

google_reviews.text: This park has character! A nice path to do laps around and a great playset for kids to have fun too. Loved the trees and the outside eating area was really nice too.
Best park ever I went there last week it's a very nice park
Love this park.. has a walk path, has a basketball court, has benches, tables for picnic, has grills,  clean bathrooms, has a playground for kids to enjoy.. a very secure park for families to enjoy.
Nice park to relax and to take your kids the only thing is that there's alot of homeless people hanging out at the park
Love this park. Only meant for good times with pleasant ppl

name: Surfers Point at Seaside Park
formatted_address: Parking lot, Shoreline Dr, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
Very picturesque! Beautiful park with a view of the Marshall Islands.  Lots of big rocks, washed up bamboo and driftwood along the shore.
Several memorials in honor of surfers that passed away.
Lots of people walking, jogging, walking their dogs and a cat or simply sitting on the benches enjoying the sea breeze. . Everyone is nice and friendly.

The park is next a Casino and Ventura County Fairgrounds.

A beautiful jewel in the heart of Ventura. White sandy beaches and breathtaking sunsets. A great location to begin new traditions with family and friends. The newly renovated walking paths are highly patrolled.

Surfers Point Park is that perfect Ventura blend of laid‑back and lively. There's energy without chaos, nature with amenities, and community presence with plenty of room to find your own spot. Whether you’re riding waves, enjoying a scenic walk, or simply reflecting by the sea, it's a place that feels both alive and serene. I left with sandy shoes, salty hair, and a genuine sense of calm. Great spot to catch sunset as well!!
Nice place to watch the amateur surfers but kinda dirty. Not the best beach to lounge.

This beachside memorial exudes a profound sense of peace and reflection, standing as a gentle reminder of both the beauty and fragility of life. Nestled between palm trees and overlooking the vast ocean, the tribute is simple yet deeply touching. The surfboard, adorned with “Rest in Peace,” and the surrounding stones painted with heartfelt messages and mementos speak of a community’s love and remembrance for a lost friend.

The tranquil setting complements the solemnity of the memorial. The rhythmic crashing of waves, the soft rustling of palm fronds, and the warmth of the sun create a harmonious backdrop. It’s not just a place to remember, but also a space to find solace and healing by the sea. It serves as a meeting point for memories and emotions—a sanctuary for all who wish to pause, reflect, and honor.

Beyond the memorial, the beach itself offers a serene escape, with its golden sands, inviting walkways lined with towering palms, and the distant pier stretching into the horizon. It’s a quintessential Californian scene, blending the laid-back atmosphere with a deep sense of connection and community spirit.

Visiting this place feels like stepping into a quiet, sacred space on the edge of the world—a beautiful fusion of nature, remembrance, and peace.

name: Via Marina Park
formatted_address: 3301 Keel Way, Oxnard, CA 93035, USA

google_reviews.text: We come here at least once a year with a group of people to barbecue, to play corn hole*.To just visit with family and friends.Very enjoyable beautiful area

This park is great. It's clean and surrounded by lots of grass and a nearby full basketball court. It has swings for both little toddlers and older children. There is a slide for toddlers and a nice little jungle gym. My child is 18 months and lives coming here.
Always a beautiful place, you everyone or just the 2 of you, and even if you want to just enjoy it all, alone.

So much space! Our dog loves our walks to the park. Picnic tables, drinking fountain, bathrooms, and a Little Free Library make this park even better.

Wish everyone cleaned up after themselves and their dogs, though. There were fries all over the floor under one of the picnic tables. There was also dog poop on the grass that I almost stepped on.
It definitely hasn't changed much after I went to school next to it but it's still nice. Nephews had soccer practice. Great place for team practices and games.

name: Sycamore Neighborhood Park
formatted_address: 198 Via Katrina, Thousand Oaks, CA 91320, USA

google_reviews.text: This park has a basketball court and kiddie structures as well as small sitting areas.  There is a flat area of grass where kids can run around.  Parking is on the street as the parking above is private.  It is a small little park.

This is an excellent park for people of all ages. Be you a young toddler exploring the world for the first time or a child looking for a place to hang out within walking distance from home or an elderly person who just wants to get out and go for a walk at the park, this is the place to go.
Small park with a few playground equipment options for kids. The park has a basketball court and a grass area big enough to run some energy out.

Follow the trail north around the east side of the park to the tunnel under Borchard that will connect you to the trail to Dos Vientos Community Park, and to the use trail that will take you to the hairpin turn near the top of the Powerline Trail.
Nice quiet park has bbq picnic tables.  Gazebo.  Playground.  Basketball court.

name: Riverview Linear Park
formatted_address: 1851-1999 Montgomery Ave, Ventura, CA 93004, USA

google_reviews.text:
This place is my personal Utopia. Throughout life I have been fortunate enough to have returned to enjoy the view.
I have spent two days pulling up dead dry brush from the Plato. I'm very aware that this place is in a burn zone and I believe that clearing this brush out can protect the neighborhood close by and also give chance for new growth to occur. I enjoy watching two humming birds one green on red and also the local rabbits. This is my favorite place on earth so I am doing my part to enhance the quality of the environment. I hope to be able to invest in some plants a new fence line and a possible playground for the local youth.

We call it “the green belt” and take it from Montgomery Ave/North Bank all the way to Rio Grande/North Bank. Dog friendly, wide, paved path. There is a playground on the east end and it’s a great place to go for a run or take the kids.

One of the weirdest parks I've seen before, but really nice. As the name suggests, it's really long and narrow, spanning along the side of multiple neighborhoods. It's a nice addition because it could have easily been a large pathway with nothing nice about it, but it's clean and used by lots of families. Nice for a walk to overlook the riverbed and the farmland.

Great place for daily exercise. Walking, riding, or running. Lots of folks walking their dogs. I like the fact it's close to homes, but not in the middle of the neighborhood. It's a little private, yet feels safe. Everyone is polite and respectful of distance, due to covid.
Great park to walk the dogs, jog or sit at benches or patio chairs and view the northern Oxnard - Santa Clara River farmland area.

name: Camino Real Park
formatted_address: Dean Dr & Varsity St, Ventura, CA 93003, USA

google_reviews.text:
I love this park!

It’s well maintained although the road in the park really should get repaved. If you drive further back there is a sink near picnic tables that has running water and there’s another faucet near tables past the playground.

Not sure if the water is potable so I can’t recommend drinking it.

There’s a dog park that has a fence around it and it looks like dogs enjoy it. I don’t talk to any of the dogs in person but they sure look happy.

There’s courts (I’m assuming tennis) located to the left when you first drive in and they look well kept. The bathrooms are clean and always had toilet paper when I had to use them.

The fields are really big and I’ve been here during little league and it gets packed! Not all the time but sometimes.

Lots of shade to park under and great place to just chill.

Very beautiful Park in a nice neighborhood, with restrooms, plenty of parking, and hardly any homeless people. The park appears to be dog friendly and there is a section just for dogs. Most of the people in the park are playing ball, enjoying family time, walking or jogging. There are lots of picnic tables and trash cans. There's a nice playground section by the tennis courts for the kids.

A great park to take your furry friends and enjoy some out door time. Separate area available at back of park for small dogs and large dogs without leashes.
And a ton of walking area with leashes.

Beautiful park. I take my dog there frequently. There is an area for small dogs and large dogs. My dog can play and socialize. He is very happy about that! The parking to get to the dog park may be too far to walk for some people. In the small dog area there is only one bench and bring your own water. If new, you may not understand how to open the gates.
WARNING: There is a woman on the back side drive with a van she lives in with aLOT of small dogs she puts outside in a pen. At least one dog can scale the fencing and will attack. She knows this but doesn't always pay attention as one of her dogs easily got over the fence and made it to my dog and attacked him. Animal control has been out there.

Title: A Canine Haven in Nature's Embrace

Rating: ★★★★★ (5/5 stars)

Camino Real Park truly stands out as a gem for dog lovers and their furry companions. Nestled in the heart of nature's embrace, this park offers an exceptional experience that caters to both dogs and their humans.

The highlight of Camino Real Park is undoubtedly its spacious and well-maintained dog park. The area provides ample room for our four-legged friends to roam, run, and play freely. What sets this park apart is the thoughtful inclusion of mature trees that generously provide shade, creating a comfortable environment for both pets and owners, even on hot summer days. As a dog owner, I appreciate the foresight of the park's planners in considering our pets' well-being.

Picnic tables thoughtfully scattered throughout the dog park offer a perfect spot for human companions to relax while keeping an eye on their playful pups. It's an ideal setup for socializing with other dog owners and sharing joyful moments with fellow pet enthusiasts.

One of the highlights that stood out to me during my visits was the presence of a doggy drinking fountain. This small yet significant feature emphasizes the park's commitment to ensuring the welfare of our beloved pets. Having a readily available water source for our furry friends is a testament to the park's dedication to creating a safe and welcoming space for all visitors.

Beyond the park's amenities, the community of dog owners that frequent Camino Real Park is incredibly warm and friendly. Everyone seems genuinely invested in ensuring a positive experience for their dogs and other park-goers. It's heartwarming to witness how quickly friendships form between humans and their pets, fostering a strong sense of camaraderie among the visitors.

Camino Real Park has undoubtedly become a go-to destination for me and my canine companion. The park's commitment to providing a comfortable, safe, and enjoyable environment for our furry friends is evident in every aspect. I wholeheartedly recommend this park to any dog owner seeking a serene place to bond with their pets, surrounded by the beauty of nature and the camaraderie of a wonderful community.

google_reviews.text: Great place for the little kids. Family friendly neighborhood.

name: Cemetery Memorial Park
formatted_address: Main Street &, S Crimea St, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful views of the Channel Islands  in a peaceful respectful setting. On Ventura's scenic drive. Lots of open space, benches and shade. One of local canine favorites parks.🐕
Favorite spot for dinners, reading, and park ocean views. Love the huge open field and always so many cute dogs here. Great for sunsets as well!
Great place to watch the sunset bring a little towel to sit on the grass because there’s a lot of dogs. If you like dogs, sometimes they come up to you while you’re sitting there.

We love coming here. Great view. I can throw the ball as far as I can for my dog to chase, she loves it! We walk around and I get my steps. Great views of the ocean and islands. Go for sunset hours as well! Also a great spot to read a book and chill! Enjoy!

One of the most beautiful places for a picnic in town, perfect spot to watch the sunset. Lots of dogs, so only major downside is when people don't clean up after their pups or the dogs are rowdy. And it is an actual cemetery, so just know that there are headstones here and there. Absolutely gorgeous place.

name: Grant Park
formatted_address: 398 Ferro Dr, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: This park is very special.  It's always beautiful any time of day.  A wonderful place to stop and reflect.
Beautiful overlook of Pacific Ocean, Channel Islands, and Ventura beaches with Botanic Gardens trails nearby.

I absolutely love this place. Best views of Ventura in all of Ventura! Perfect for a sunset photo shoot or afternoon picnic. There's even benches at the top to sit and enjoy the views or a picnic table on the grass to enjoy your lunch with a view. Especially right now in spring the flowers are beautiful and blooming.

Such a beautiful view. Every time my new workers check in I tell them to go there and have a picnic. Absolutely astonishing how far you can see and all the awesome views you can experience looking in any direction. Mountains on one side, ocean and pier on the other, city as far as the eyes can see. If you don't go at least once during your time in the are, you're missing out.

Trail Series, Part 12
What is known as the “Hike to the Cross” is located within Grant Park in Ventura, California.

It offers 360 degree views that provide you with an opportunity to visually drink up and absorb everything from the hills around Ventura to the stunning Pacific Ocean.

The “hike” itself requires very little physical effort, but meanders nicely amongst the expanding trails within the adjoining Ventura Botanical Gardens.

Grant Park offers sufficient parking (although you can expect some company for each glorious sunset).

Each visitor here seems to soak up the experience in an individualized manner, but no matter how you choose to enjoy this experience, we’d recommend that you take a moment to stand, sit, or kneel at the foot of the cross…and reflect.

And that’s…Henry’s Take.

#hiketothecross

name: Walter B. Moranda Park

DOUG out

name: Steckel Park

google_reviews.text: I visited Santa Paula after 15 years,I went to check out Steckel Park to rake a walk near the creek,but the maintance of the Park was neglected for many years,my grandson wanted to play on the swings but the soft area is all damaged and is nit a safe are for children to play.
Really big! It's a bit closer to the road,  but there are capo sites that are shielded from it. Dog friendly. Wonderful spot. The creek was great.

TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE WITH STAFF

From the moment we arrived the staff was incredibly disrespectful. She seemed so eager to get us off the site even though we had a reservation. Two people visited us in the beginning and were very abrasive. The website to reserve a spot doesn’t specify that if you rent a campground that you can spend the night only if your camping but if you are there to hang out for the day you must be gone by 10. Tell me how that makes sense. I’m paying the same price as those who are sleeping overnight but not allowed to stay past 10?? We followed the rules but I thought it was unfair so be aware of that NEW rule. Lastly we started cleaning up and heading out at 10. By 10:10 PM another park staff member came and very rudely expressed that only one person needs to stay to put the remaining flame/fire out, she said we needed to be gone already. I was so upset mainly because me and my group felt like we couldn’t enjoy our time there. This is not how anyone who pays for a reservation (gives business) should feel. I’ve been coming here for years and never had these issues. So incredibly rude ,so disappointing, and disrespectful. Will not be coming back and telling all of my friends to stop coming here. Please be aware that if you visit here it’s guaranteed that you will be met with the same attitude as we were as I saw them visiting each site with the same approach.
Cool bird sanctuary. Parrots and a lot more. Great stop, briefly or as long as you need

It was nice just one of the hostess made you feel uncomfortable she rushed up to people including us and my guess. and ask what they want and tell them she just checking to see if there homeless. name: Dos Caminos Park
formatted_address: 2198 N Ponderosa Dr, Camarillo, CA 93010, USA

google_reviews.text: It's nice, peaceful, easy to park, flat walking surfaces; mostly green, shaded area with picnic benches and a playground. NO restrooms available, they remain locked during all hours.

Great small park.  Kiddo loves it. Best time is afternoon because the pine trees shade the whole play area.  Morning is when sun is right on the structure.

Lots of friendly kids and parents come here, and for the most part Camarillo keeps it clean and maintained.  Saturday we see some people play Ultimate frisbee each week, and there's a big grass area to run around.
Great small park. Smaller  grass area. There is playground, swings for kids. Also has lots of trees.  6 benches , bathroom are locked.  Great place for short visit. Peaceful.

The playground is somewhat sparse and there usually are not a lot of kids there. The playset seems to be geared around younger children. The park is also on a busy street corner so there is a lot of car noise there. There are much better parks in town I would go to first.

I dont know how many people read posts about parks but I will say that this park is relaxing and big enough for family picnics.
I have lived here most of my life and the park has pretty much stayed the same. The grass is green and the trees are big. It always reminds me that I am home

name: Adolfo Park
formatted_address: 3601 N Adolfo Rd, Camarillo, CA 93010, USA

google_reviews.text: My kids love this park, and I hate to be this person but this is absolutely not an off-leash park for dogs. Maybe it’s fine when children aren’t present, but there is nothing separating the play structure from the open space and too many times owners have let their dogs into the play area off-leash when children are present.

This park has a new playground. The park does not state age appropriate for the equipment but my guess is over 3. However, the slides and swings are good for toddlers. Note no swings for older children only two baby swings. This park has two small picnic tables and plenty of green grass. Park has no bathroom and very close to a 40 mph road.

Wonderful place to take a walk, let your children play and your dog run around. I'm not sure if your supposed to let your dog off leash but I do and haven't had a problem. This is my dogs favorite place to stop on his walk!

My dogs favorite park. I love this park because we're usually the only ones there in the morning. One of my dogs isn't always great with other dogs, so I can let him off leash when we're here. And, it's close to our home.

It's a nice park but 4 or a 5 stars?, No Way ! The park, like so many of the older parks in Camarillo, has NO RESTROOM FACILITIES. So you can't stay for long (if you know what I mean !). But I guess if you don't eat or drink anything while you're there you'll be ok. Put in a Port-a-potty and I'll give it a 3.

name: Trailside Park
formatted_address: 5462 Cherry Ridge Dr, Camarillo, CA 93012, USA

name: La Jenelle Park
formatted_address: Oxnard, CA 93035, USA

google_reviews.text:
When I was pregnant with my daughter,  my husband and I  would walk along the beach for exercise. We walked hand and hand watching until sunset. We would walk to where the La Jenelle is located. Back in 1988 we were able to walk along the ship. And on a building, someone painted what the ship use to look like, with a memorial plaque to read about it. At that time we hadn't picked out a name for our baby. We agreed that if it's a girl, my husband got to pick the name, and I got to for a boy. As we got further along to full term and still haven't picked a name for a girl. My husband told me that years ago where he lived there was a cute little girl, four or five years old that was a neighbor and her name was Janelle. I felt in love with the name. So when our baby was born, we were both so proud to call her Jannel.
Yes, we did spell it different. Today, I still love to say her name. That's my love story and how we named our daughter.

Delightful spot, but not really a park in the traditional sense. More like a lovely beach by a breakwall, where the breakwall includes visible pieces of a large shipwreck from the 466 ft cruise ship La Jenelle, which hit the shore in 1970. I've been in the area many times, but finally made my way out to see what it was really like.

Getting to the spot proved to be a challenge, because Google Maps navigation directions kept trying to take me through the military base, which isn't a thoroughfare for civilian tourists. After putting my faith in one of Google's finest apps twice, I gave up, looked at the location, backtracked to my starting point and found the spot. Look for "199-101 Sawtelle Ave, Oxnard, CA 93035" in Google Maps for parking, then once you arrive, walk along the paved path to the ocean, then across the sand to to the breakwall, passing numerous painted murals along the wall fronting the military base along the way. Once you get to the breakwall, meander along the rocks, looking down to the water to see the remains of the shipwreck. The other parts of the shipwreck that didn't become part of the breakwall were towed out to sea and dumped, making for interesting scuba dive sites.
What a beautiful place,  great piece of history. Taking a ship and making a breakwater.
I went here recently and it was a really pretty and secluded beachfront park. There is not much park, per se, but I really enjoyed it. There is a pretty mural as well!
Wonderful wide beach, attractive to both surfers and sunset watchers. Consider yourself lucky to find parking.

name: Carpinteria Salt Marsh Nature Park
formatted_address: 5775 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing hidden gem, parking and entry can be a bit tricky. Recommend the beach down the street south of here ~5mins
Peaceful place! I went there with my friends, we had a good time for walking the trail. There was very fun! We saw many wild animals and blooming flowers.
Trail is wide and flat. I enjoyed sitting and just listening to the birds. I recommend coming in the morning, birds seem more active.
Beautiful place to walk at sunset. Close to beach access. Would love to visit again.
Beautiful, stunning, calm, peaceful. I’ve been down this trail a few times and always enjoy it.

name: Seaside Wilderness Park
formatted_address: Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Bathrooms are very clean. Grass areas and campgrounds are uneven but not overgrown.  Pay for parking via website. It was great for our group camping trip. TrainS pass really close; the freeway is also audible. Ocean waves are faintly audible.

Entering through Emma Wood SB, I followed the bike path in and went down under a bridge where it was kind of swampy and continued on a dirt path out onto the beach. I was able to get my bike and kid trailer all the way to the site of the beach. I locked them together when we went to the water since there are many thieves in the area and it's not 100% safe area. It was overcast but not too cold. The weather is deceitful on the coast. It was a beautiful day.  We got there at low tide. The tide pools we covered in seaweed and didn't appear to have any animals or sea life in them. We did watch a crane and the seagulls for awhile and played in the waves as the tide came back in. We saw a helicopter fly over. There are always neat planes and such flying by rather low so you can get a good look. My 5yo. loves it.  It is a special place to explore and very enjoyable.

Beautiful beach with island views and a nice, wide stretch of sand (if you go at low tide 😉). No easy access to bathrooms, and you have to walk a bit to get there, but it's totally worth it because it's not usually too crowded, even in the summer.

Native vegetation along the beach. Going from the grasslands at the camping sites to trees and shrubs nearer the Ventura river. The small trails are great. The wider roads are strewn with trash and wandering homeless. You pass under the railroad tracks to reach a rocky berm and then the sandy beach.
Great park by the sea.  10 dollars for campsites,  toilets and showers.  Right on the ocean. Great deal and beautiful sites.

name: Carty Park
formatted_address: Carty Park, 3900 S F St, Oxnard, CA 93033, USA

google_reviews.text: Park is small but its used mostly for baseball. Has a snack bar, kids area and lots of grass area has 3 baseball fields

name: Delphinium Dog Park
formatted_address: 128004028, Ventura, CA 93004, USA

google_reviews.text:
New to this Dog Park, really enjoyed it! It’s very chill. My dogs loved it, didn’t see a lot of doggos. Would love to get together with someone who frequents this one so my pups can get more exposure.

We are new to dog parks in general been to one by the Collection in Oxnard have had mixed interactions there.

Very sandy and a bit of mulch. No grass. Plenty of trees. There are water fountains for the dogs and plenty of room to run. Park provides waste bags and trash cans. Always clean. Would love to have some grass here. Would suggest to put up signs that this is not a picnic area but a dog park only.

Well kept off leash dog Park. There are trees, water, and picnic tables. The surface is all bark. There is no area for small dogs but small dogs mingle regularly with the larger dogs. I've never seen an aggressive dog here

Nice neighborhood park. Water, poop stations, tables, trees, double gated. Walking trail adjacent. Nice size for neighborhood. No separate areas for different size dogs. Three foot high fencing, bark surface.

Nice park. No grass, but the mulch isn't that bad at all. Kinda deserted mid day and early afternoon as far as I can tell. I have gone four times.

Clean, well managed. double-door entrances to prevent accidental escape. Entrances on both sides.

name: Brock Linear Park
formatted_address: Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice shady walk in a neighborhood park

name: The Robert J. Lagomarsino Visitor Center at Channel Islands National Park
formatted_address: 1901 Spinnaker Dr, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Had booked a roundtrip with Island Packers for a Friday in September. Got there around 8am because I needed to check-in 45 minutes in advance for the 9am ferry to Scorpion Cove. The visitor center opened at 8:30am, so took a quick walk over to buy some souvenirs and postcards. Liked the setup inside, and the items were easy to find. Checkout with the staff was quick, and I was able to get back to my car, drop off the items, and walk over to Island Packers before they started boarding.

We always enjoy visiting the National Parks.  This one did not disappoint.  From watching the very informative short video to the helpful staff.  We also took the boat trip out to the Channel Islands.  On the way, we got to see Dolphins and Whales.  The islands and their history were just amazing.
It is a pleasure to visit, learn, explore, and experience this amazing center, which is lovely, beautiful, and family-friendly.

Easter at the harbor. Andrias chopinno then a nature walk around the building; exhibiting plants from the channel islands. Finished our afternoon with an elevator ride to the observation deck simply gorgeous! FREE ENTERTAINMENT

A neat visitor center for those visiting CINP.  Get your Natl Park passport booklets stamped here as well as t-shirts, caps, trinkets and books!  Their book selection on the marine ecology & environment of CINP outstanding btw!

FYI - just remember that for those taking the ferry boat road from the harbor rides nearby, the boats typically leave shortly after the visitor center opens, and if you catch the last ferry back, the visitor center will already be closed or closing.

CINP is a wonderful place, but remember that MORE THAN HALF of CINP is the Federally Protected marine sanctuary underneath the surface!  Imagine seeing the largest pod of dolphins just minutes from CINP - we did!  The boat captain said it had to be 5000+ dolphins!  And a pod of Orca whales teaching their young how to hunt seals, with an actual seal carcass!  ONLY IN THE MARINE SANCTUARY OF CINP!  MAKE SURE THE GOVT KEEPS THIS PROTECTED!

name: Summerland Greenwell Preserve
formatted_address: 2710 Montecito Ranch Pl, Summerland, CA 93067, USA

google_reviews.text: Looks like it used to be a really nice place.  Nice little start to the trail (1/10th mile long maybe) that had plant nameplates out along it and then it fairly much dead-ended at a sign pointing left and right that said trail. Both directions were pretty overgrown
Beautiful place. Calm & lovely.
Nice access to hiking trails

name: Santa Clara River Preserve
formatted_address: 1368 Mission Rock Rd, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: The Santa Clara River Bottom Preserve offers a peaceful escape with nice trails and plenty of natural scenery. It’s a good spot for a casual walk or birdwatching. However, some areas could use better maintenance, and signage was a bit sparse, making navigation tricky at times. Overall, it’s a decent place to visit if you’re looking for a quiet outdoor spot, but there’s room for improvement in amenities and upkeep.
Nice peaceful ADA accessible area with one porta potty, river access and room to roam.
Easy, flat hikes thru scrub and wildflowers.  Great place to bird watch along the river trail.
Great place to help you understand the importance of conservation balance.  The needs of farmers, drinking water, and biodiversity are being addressed here at this cool preserve.

Grand opening of this beautiful Santa Clara River trail. It is an easy hike for all ages. Bird watching is a must. The view to the river bed was breathtaking. Making this location one of my favorite places to visit.

name: Santa Clara Estuary Natural Preserve
formatted_address: Oxnard, CA 93001, USA

name: Ventura River Estuary
formatted_address: Good, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: There is a shallow point that is ankle to knee deep depending on when you cross. I run a loop.
Relaxing spot near a bike path. Picnic, listen to the waves, watch the birds, or take in a sunset

A great place to stroll, bike or run during the pandemic. Parking lot is to the north at Main and Peking. Porta Potty is there as well. Head south along the trail along the Ventura river and end at the see where it enters the ocean. Great sandy beach, though very windy when we were there.
Good Relax trail, excelent view
I love visiting this place. A great spot for borders and for bringing kids to tide pool or play where the river meets the ocean. There can be a homeless presence but I have never had a problem.

name: La Jolla Valley Natural Preserve
formatted_address: Ventura County, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Stunningly beautiful with almost nobody around. Just amazing.

name: Carpinteria Salt Marsh Reserve
formatted_address: Estero St, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Signs to explain the geology, and the marsh life. A restored marsh habitat close to the beach.
Great area to walk and see birds. No dogs because of the sensitive habitat. Bathroom at one of the entrances. Many interpretative signs.

I am in love with this area. The walkway, the bridge, watching the fish jump near sunset, and seeing the diverse salt water friendly plantings and the ebb and flow of the tides is endlessly fascinating.
Well maintained walking trail. We enjoyed exploring more of this beautiful beach town. We got more beauty than we expected.
Wonderful nature respite out of a busy day. The short walk is interesting including a bridge. I saw cranes, herons, and rabbits! If you want to make a donation to keep the place up, it is voluntary.

name: Tar Pits Park
formatted_address: 5669 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: It was such a lovely way to take a morning walk and explore the route pools. It wasn't crowded. People were friendly and gave each other information about sightings of sea lions.

This is one of my favorite stops along the California coast.  The seal viewing and tar pit trail are so unique and always memorable.  My favorite time to visit is sunset or sunrise. Don't be surprised to see other wildlife along the way.  And if you need a cup of caffeine,  stop in to the Lucky Lama before you head to the cliffs!
Beautiful park in between natural reserve and sea lion sanctuary! But amazing morning walk!

Warning: There's tar in these pits!

Parking: plenty. If you are a civilized, good and decent person you will pay to park at the state beach. If you did minimal research and you have a CA ID you know how to get a free pass to park.  If you are a savage like me, who doesn't count steps or carbs, you will park in the near by city streets with plenty of spots and just jump over railroad tracks to get to the tar pits faster, because for all you know this is the last these ancient tar pits are in business.

Preface: You drove here on untold tons of what is essentially solidified tar because they didn't clench your insatiable thirst for tar in its unrefined pure form. You are just a few miles from beautiful, prestine, historical Santa Barbara with her drive on pier, 200 year old mission, classy Spanish architecture, but you didn't care for that at all, instead you left refined St Barbara for gritty, blue collared St Joseph the worker to behold it's prehistoric, black, tarry goodness to what has essentially been an ancient trash can and death pit since the dinosaurs reigned. Being the town of the carpenter, pits like these would have been used by the Chumash to seal their boats for their seal hunting expeditions and trips to the channel Islands.

Most of the tar is on the cliff just above the beach.

The pits are a little underwhelming. Nothing like La Brea's. Here at least you can poke the tar and ruin your shoes with it. They have a few tiny ones that just ooze from the earth from the ground or rocks. One rock was oozing down the sides with it, at first it looked like some contemporary art piece an accomplished artist with a 1st grade education squirted black acrylic on and called it a day.

The first mini Pit I saw I assumed at first was made by a hobo's bonfire but then I poked it with my handy-dandy poking stick and saw the tar in all it's dark, molasses viscosity, and glory.

The sign I included said this was once an asphalt yard.

If this park didn't meet your drunken tar lust, then by all means drive 90 miles south to the Miracle Mile and see swimming pool sized bottomless pits of your black gooey idol along with the adjacent shrine to its existence . Or better yet go to an asphalt plant.
The view just beautiful! Seals 🦭, the rock formations... i love it. The marsh is full of little birds. Nice to walk and relax

name: Twin Ponds
formatted_address: Via Oviedo, Newbury Park, CA 91320, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful lookout point. Finding a place to park was a challenge and aggravating. We ended up parking in the entrance way to one of the gated communities directly across the street from the south entrance to the trail, (although I'm not sure if that was OK or not). It took me, my wife and three kids (ages 7,5,2) about 30 min at an easy walking pace to make it to the ponds. It was worth the trip in my opinion because of the lookout point, just watch where your walking, passed a local hiker who said they had seen a rattlesnake the week prior. It's a wide trail so as long as you stay in the center of the trail, you shouldn't have any issues. Water, sunblock and proper attire as always!
Both Ponds still have water - mid October. They support greenery in the surounding area. Reflections in the pond make for a great view. It was a nice hike to start the day.
Nice walking area.  Good place to walk dogs.  On the way there are nice views of ocean and Channel Islands.
Couldn't get to it. It's like inside gated communities. Definitely should be stated somewhere that it's a private location.

name: Willoughby Preserve
formatted_address: Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: We saw some big birds here when we were biking.

name: Ventura River Steelhead Preserve

name: Danielson Ranch Multi-use Area
formatted_address: Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Great spot for a rest after a long hike.

Accessible from either side, Sycamore Canyon campground or Rancho Sierra Vista, this is a great spot to have a picnic or take a break and use the facilities. This spot could be reserved to camp overnight by contacting State Parks.
Excellent for group or family camping, this site has running water, restrooms and showers. The area is home to some of the best trails in LA, great for hiking, mountain biking or horse back riding.

Kind of a remote country park, with some nice big oaks, picnic tables, fresh water, toilets...very peaceful spot, about halfway between the beach and Newbury Park.  Only problem is that in the residence there , there is a dog that barks sometimes, it was barking at me for awhile, kinda wrecks the mood but oh well.  The whole canyon (and beyond) is classic California, with lots of native plants and grasses, streams, big sycamore and oak trees, some kind of wild parrots (beautiful green birds, in at least one large flock of 80-100 birds)...  you can hike to a very pretty beach and camping area...you could spend weeks or months exploring this wild area of the coast...and best of all is that few people are around at least during weekdays..

I've been here a few times and it was hot and filled with mosquitos. Lol. There are bathrooms and running water though. Better then as lot of camp sites I've been to with the Scouts because of the amenities. The hike in from PCH is about 4.5 miles and very flat and easy. Just bring water!

The camp site it pretty big and go flat. There's also a bunch of places to hang a hammock if you have one. Highly recommended.

I loved a night hike and seeing all the stars despite the light pollution. Much better then being in downtown Los Angeles! Lol.
A great place to pause on your trek. Sit, relax, enjoy the huge trees, and fill your water bottle. Enjoy nature at it's finest.

name: Viola Fields
formatted_address: 6145 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: First time parking here great atmosphere and safe and secure area Great spot to go hiking and bike riding and watch the beautiful sunset
Nice, quiet sports field with lots of dogs and space to walk. There are adjacent hiking trails that go all the way to downtown Carpinteria.
Nice field to play some ball. Great views and walking track.
It a nice walk next to water
Great view. Great park.

name: Lake Casitas Recreation Area
formatted_address: 11311 Santa Ana Rd, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
Lake Casitas Recreation Area is a large campground with a lot to offer, but it comes with some limitations to be aware of. Most of the sites are geared toward tent camping that have access to the lake. However, RV sites with full hookups are very limited. We stayed in site E2, which was level and had electric and water hookups, so that worked well for us.

I travel with inflatable stand-up paddle boards and had to check them at the gate because the lake is not open for human contact, so no swimming or body contact. The on-site water adventure park looks fun, but it is not included in the camping price, and we were told by a ranger that prices will be increasing with only 1/2 day access this season (open Memorial Day weekend) due to high demand.

It’s a very large campground, so bringing bikes is a great idea to make getting around easier. Overall, it’s a decent place to camp if you know what to expect, but the restrictions and added costs brought it down to a 3-star experience for us.

Great place for camping, fishing, disc golf and more! We’re lucky to have access to this amazing lake! There are lots of camping options for groups or quiet camping in the Lakeside overflow (pro tip). I’m sure it’s full all of the time, during the summer, when water slides are open, so plan accordingly. Don’t forget to play the disc golf courses! Get out and check it out!
Leo and Evan are the BEST! We had engine trouble and the very friendly and HELPFUL Rangers towed us in. Thanks again gentlemen. Much appreciated!

Great lake. If you want to take a moring run or gust go fishing. You can rent a boat or or fish from shore. The campsites are very nice there is a lot of room to spread out. The bathrooms were very stinky and they felt dirty.  I would bring your own water to the lake just because the faucets look very used in my opinion. Over all it is a great lake to do a day trip or to camp out at.
Cool place to camp but since there are like 200 campsite it is very very smokey from all the camp fires. Most smokey camping experience ive ever had

name: Garden City Acres Park
formatted_address: 5302 Cypress Rd, Oxnard, CA 93033, USA

google_reviews.text: Garden City Acres Park is a beautiful and peaceful spot to relax and enjoy nature. The park has a well-maintained playground for kids, plenty of open grassy areas for picnics, and shaded spots to sit and rest. It is also dog-friendly, making it a great place to take your pets for a walk. Restrooms are available and kept relatively clean. Overall, it’s a great family-friendly park with something for everyone.

This a beautiful quite, family park. The graffiti is a little upsetting but otherwise the park is well maintained. The people are friendly. My dogs love going and the waking track is really nice and covered in soft wood chips.

Small park. The playground is smaller than others.
Has some grills with tables.
One basketball court.
Large area for soccer or football (there are no nets)
Some grills and tables. Good for a small picnic. Good for walking around.
Afternoons tend to get busy with people playing soccer and/or basketball.

Parking is limited to one side. They use to open the gate that allowed parking around the whole park.
Nice place to take the family, or go for a jog around. It has it's own fitness center also. Vary low-lying on the outskirts of the city. Well maintained and clean.
Great little Gem in the south of Oxnard.  A Solid Street Hoops Spot.

name: The Hummingbird Sanctuary
formatted_address: 15925 Ojai Rd, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: Had a wonderful day at the Hummingbird sanctuary! There were many hummingbirds flying around us. Our host was very knowledgeable and answered all our questions. Learned so many surprising facts! Connected nicely with my group and had a great time!

We had a great time. The host was knowledgeable about hummingbirds across North America and gave us tips on how to care for our own feeders at home. The hummingbirds were plentiful and very docile to sit with. I would recommend this place to anyone who loves to sit and admire the small wonders of nature.
Absolutely wonderful experience! A fun time to spend in a beautiful garden with several types of hummingbirds flitting about. Wish I could have stayed all day!

The entire hummingbird and tea experience was phenomenal for both myself and my wife.  We had came across this as a recommendation during a recent Airbnb stay in nearby Montecito and this made the perfect conclusion to our trip.

Zsilvia was a great host and very knowledgeable about these amazing little birds.  We left with so much more knowledge and appreciate for hummingbirds and seeing then in this setting was quite magical.  To see the apologetically close and under the live oak trees just hlgojbg about their business while you site mere inches or feet away was an experience we will not soon forget.

I highly recommend this experience if you are in the Santa Paula area and are interested in seeing these magnificent creatures up close or just want to know more about them and their uniqueness.

Thank you for the magical experience

This place is so amazing. So incredible to be so close to so many hummingbirds. Wow!! The host Szilvia also knows so much and is a lot of fun to talk to. I’ve loved hummingbirds for many years and I’ve never seen one that close up! Let alone hundreds. The tea was also so tasty. A very magical and memorable experience.

name: City of Ojai Community Demonstration Garden
formatted_address: 415 S Ventura St, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Dry for now which is what one would expect during a drought. That said, the garden is well laid out and has a number of learning and growing areas. Writing this July 2021 and was delighted to find a small pond with beautiful water lillies in full bloom. Wonderful.
Awesome place, easy walk down to the creek, oaks, majestic palms and eucalyptus line the creek. The garden itself is rustic and beautiful with flowers. Very few people around. Great nature walk.
A few parking spots out front, small trail around the area. A few benches to sit and enjoy the peace and quiet. 2 small ponds.
Love going here..beautiful place to sit for a spell, under nice trees..good work being done.
We are big fans. Lots of folks working hard to improve the park!

name: Toland Park
formatted_address: 525 Toland Rd, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: A great place to get those steps in for the day. Quiet and peaceful and lots of trails to explore.
Dry & hot, picnic areas are not maintained, and rattlesnake warning. Great views though. Too bad it is being neglected.
An old paved road loops through recovering native habitats, including fields of purple sage. Very restful.

This park is a great place for a relaxing picnic surrounded by nature. It's usually quiet and not too crowded, which makes it easy to find a peaceful spot to enjoy the outdoors. There's plenty of parking available, so you won't have trouble finding a place to park, even on the weekends. The park can get a little overgrown at times, but it adds to the natural charm. Overall, it's a wonderful spot if you're looking for a calm and scenic escape.
We just wanted to stretch it legs for a bit, so we entered by car and we're going to pay the entrance fee but we saw that the area just past the gate was very overgrown. We turned around.

name: Carpinteria State Beach
formatted_address: 205 Palm Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: This was my first time to Carp State Beach. It was 10 bucks for the day pass. Plenty of tables and Bbq’s. People are camping in tents and Rv’s. Real nice day. If you are thinking of going. Just go.

A nice place to camp! We did dry camping with our 28' trailer. Very easy to back the trailer in our site (123). Bathrooms and showers were very clean. A lot of fun trails that are very easy to walk/ run or ride bikes on. A four minute walk to resturants and breweries from our site. We will be going back to try a full-hookup site in the future!

Carpinteria is one of my favorite places in the country, and like all CA State Beaches, the views are incredible. But with that beauty comes everyone wanting to be there as well. The campground is challenging to book a spot at, but once you secure one, you’re set with the sounds of waves, beautiful beach path, and short walk to town w great dining options. The downside —- the sites are essentially a closely packed parking lot and, if you’re not on a beach front spot, outdoor seating is non-existent. Spots are so close together, you’re not opening your windows and pulling in/out is extremely difficult on a busy day. Worth it for a night if your focus is enjoying the town, but any longer would feel claustrophobic.

Great beachside state campground.

Hot showers need one or two tokens
Free cold showers
Flush toilet and running water sinks

The sites are priced from $40 which are further from the beach to $60 immediately next to the beach but no hookups
It’s $80 for a site with full hookups

Great for a beach vacation!

Carpinteria State Beach is wonderful for camping. Very mellow surf and great for walking. The campground itself is relatively quiet, but the train tracks are adjacent, so you hear several Amtrak trains and a couple of freight trains per day.

name: Wheeler Gorge Campground
formatted_address: 17017 Maricopa Hwy, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text:
Great place to get away, peaceful relaxing, CLEAN!!
Nice welcoming hosts, spacious sites,clear river water, only downside was the position oak but hey to be expected in the woods. Highly recommend

10/10 would recommend. There are so many great sites to choose from. Try and get a spot by the creek, some sites look bigger than others! Campground offered a lot of shade too. The vault toilet was clean and the campground was quiet and the stars were OUT! There is no cell service (att) and bring your own water too.
Our site was spacious, private, and we also had our own little private beach in the creek, it was magical.

Depending on your site this place can be really cool. Sites along the river are better- some are right along the highway which seemed terrible. No running water and poison oak along the sides of most sites. Cool creek with some bigger areas to wade in. Close to town if you forget anything but highway noise wasn’t a problem with the creek side sites. Might just be the weekend we were there but tons of loud kids made it not super peaceful.

Great location close to Wheeler Gorge, Mosler Rock and serval other deep Rock pools to swim and Boulder jump in, it close to many easy access park and swim spots. Great summer destination that is with 5-10 minutes away from rock pools galore. We feature many of these best SoCal swim holes on YouTube on our channel hikebytes. Check out our Playlist called best park and swim destinations. Showers and bathrooms and camp sites for reasonable fees.
It’s nice to camp. I’ve had a lot of fun with my friends. There’s a a stream close by and nice to take a hike!

name: Sycamore Canyon Campground
formatted_address: 9000 Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Very nice place, good facilities. You can take a cold shower for free and need to pay for hot water shower but that's fine. Nice hiking trails straight from the campsite. Friendly people.

Loved this campground. Went for one night only and very much enjoyed it. We were close to the restrooms which was convenient and there was a safer trail to get to the beach. We also did a ~1 mile hike that overlooked the ocean. Overall great time

Hidden gem with bonus hiking trails and a fantastic beach just across the street. Campsites are close to each other but bathrooms were very clean, unlike the beach side. Tip, if your visiting for the day, just park on PCH (ocean side). If ya go into either parking lot, it'll be a few bucks. Bonus, there was water available at the site.

I live in Redondo Beach, south of LAX and this is one of my favorite Campgrounds, close by, in Malibu near Point Magu. Just across Pacific Coast Highway from the beach in a canyon with coastal access safely under PCH. Sites are $45 per night with a one time service fee for your entire stay under $9. Each site has a picnic table and fire pit with a grill. Nice clean restrooms and pay showers. No hookups but there are clean drinking water spouts available near your site. Very friendly rangers. They even drove around each campsite offering bundles of firewood for $10, cash only.

The campground is close to the beach, has large campsites, has large parking spaces at most campsites, has fire pits, and is clean. It's a great place to camp. My kids loved meeting new friends and playing from morning until well into the evening. If you want to get to the beach you either have to cross highway 1 or go under it at low tide.  It is rocky under the bridge making it a little difficult to pass to the other side.

name: Rincon Parkway Campground
formatted_address: 4668 Pacific Coast Hwy, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Great camping area, small spaces, but beachfront views and access. I have been camping there for years as a local from Ventura. I have never had a problem there until recently. There are individuals scoping the campgrounds seeing what they could steal. They come at night, check car doors and around your campsite grabbing anything that they can. Lock everything up.!!!!! The Ranger told me that’s part of camping here it happens. Maybe Ventura County should step up their patrols of the area. Especially now that they’re charging $45 a night and having stricter regulations for camping there.

Rincon Parkway is an incredible value for what you get. This is definitely not a resort and doesn’t try to be one, but the ocean views are absolutely amazing. We could hear the waves all night, which made for a really memorable stay.

The campground feels like an old roadside pull-off along the coast, with very minimal amenities. Trash cans were the only real amenity, so come prepared and know what you’re signing up for. There is a train line and freeway running alongside the park, so there is traffic noise, but for us the sound of the ocean easily outweighed it.

If you’re looking for a simple, no-frills coastal spot with unbeatable views at a great price, this place delivers. I would definitely stay here again and would absolutely recommend it to anyone who values location and scenery over luxury.

Beach is perfect dày off with the cats. Sunny and clear. Quiet and relaxing. Purchased some food and snacks and had a couple of snuggly blankets. Watched a movie on my phone and took a long nap. Very safe too.

Having lived in Carpinteria in the 70’s, 80’s and 90’s the Rincon “Parkway” was a forlorn spot on the coast that no one bothered to stop at, much less want to spend a night at. Just a funky stretch of highway 1 next to the railroad tracks and dismal beach area. Every 4 AM the freight train blasts through guaranteed to wake you up! But now that the masses have invaded California it’s considered a “slice of Heaven”, just because it’s on the water and it’s equated with Malibu homes that go for millions.
It’s fine… I get the fantasy folks have created in their minds over this stretch of Highway 1… the trouble is the area is over-run with thieves that will steal anything that isn’t brought inside, ground squirrels with the plague and when I camped their with my RV for 4 days (only because every other campground was full), my RV was overrun with ants. Millions of ants.
Lower your expectations folks and bring bug spray and sun screen. Who knows, you might be front and center for the Big One… the earthquake and tsunami that is overdue by 50 years !

Camping in a parking space parallel to the beach. $45 a night, must reserve online. Trains run next to the road but the sound of the ocean waves 24/7 is worth it. Fantastic sunsets. No frills but exciting to be so close to the beach.

name: Faria Beach Park
formatted_address: 4350 Pacific Coast Hwy, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Wonderful place to camp and enjoy the ocean! Camp sites are tight, as to be expected in such a popular location, but we fit our 30’ camping trailer and pickup into site 33 just fine. We were there during a full moon, so the waves were huge and even splashed over the rocks into our campsite, but that was part of the fun. Showers were clean and hot. Bathrooms were clean. I’d definitely camp here again.

Overpriced, noise competes with ocean waves; and no privacy! My two over neighbors decided to congregate right in front of my parking space - you cannot call it a campsite. This campground functions more like an RV parking facility than a traditional camping experience. Spaces are tight, privacy is minimal, and there is constant traffic noise throughout the night. Lights remain on after dark, and there are no campfires allowed anywhere in the park.
Because open flames are not permitted at all, the campground also enforces a strict no-smoking policy, even within personal vehicles. For campers looking for quiet, separation, darkness, or a more nature-centered experience, this may not be a good fit.
Bathrooms are available, showers appear to be paid separately, and there are picnic tables at each site. However, those seeking a traditional camping atmosphere with space, natural sound, or nighttime calm may want to look elsewhere.

This is a really nice park, but there are only a few day use parking spots that go fast. Steps lead down to beach and end in loose rocks. The tide makes the beach seem very narrow between the water and wall near PCH. Restrooms are really clean and nice.

It’s a great place to be right on the water with hook up camping for your trailer or RV. It was rainy, so the gravel was better than I expected it to be, but the rain does pool in some sites. Campsite pictures are from campsite # 22. There are also non-hook up RV/Tent campsites.
Great place to hang and enjoy the beach. Nice little snack shack with a decent menu and cold craft beer.

name: Rose Valley Campground
formatted_address: Rose Valley Lake Rd, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text:
I went for the Rose Valley Falls Trail and loved it! Great little hike with some awesome views. I highly recommend checking out the trail.

I saw little designated campsites that I later found out you can reserve.

Rose valley campground is a great spot to spend some nights and hike, the waterfall is the main attraction and is only about 10 min walk from the parking lot, very easy to do.
Don’t forget to pay your dues, the campground manager checks often if everything is in order.

There is no shade in this campground, bring tarps, summer months gets lots of sun and some insects that look like flies or gnats are around. There is only one site that offers tree shade but is easily taken, coveted by many.

Latrine style bathrooms available

August 2019
$20 per night
$10 day use
Beautiful hiking trails, I thoroughly enjoyed the trek to Rose Valley Falls. It's not a particularly difficult hike, and the reward is well worth it. It feels almost magical.

Super tiny campground with one vault toilet that gets very used with all the hikers coming through. It's quiet and peaceful at night but lots of foot traffic during the day with people looking for the trails. There isn't any shade coverage so be prepared to bring easy ups if you want shade.

This campground is currently closed (Jan 2021), reservation is available starting in February 2021 online.

The waterfall trailhead is through the campground, on the far LEFT side as you walk in. Easy trail, kids and family friendly, may need to hop over some small creek and mud.

name: Hobson Beach Park
formatted_address: 5210 Pacific Coast Hwy, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Friendly and helpful hosts.  Kind and considerate park rangers.  A little food shack with fresh salada, burritos, sandwiches.  Acess to a beach.  I hooked up to power/sewage but there are other sites as well.  Really good experience.

I would love to give this five star review because I love having the ocean break right behind my RV. Very very tight spots. They allowed my rv reservation, 35 ft long fifth wheel,opposing slides. We maneuvered into the site, put the slides out. One slide is maybe 3 inches over what they consider "the line". The park ranger on duty Sunday, when we arrived. came and checked it out and told us it was fine. The site next to us has more room than any other site in the park. We arrive back to our RV on Monday evening after a day on Santa Cruz Island to a note on our door that says we have to move our rig because it is over the line. We wait till the ranger arrives today, Tuesday, tell him that the other ranger left a note in our reservation that we were fine and did not have to move. Today's ranger tells us no such note exists even though we saw him write it down and that we have to move our camper over. These rangers need to communicate! We are extremely frustrated,  ruining our plans for part of the day just to inch our camper over about 5 in. The fifth wheel two sites over has been there since Friday. Never been told he's over and the ranger today said he has to move his over about 2 in. Ridiculous. Moral of the story is park your rig with your slides out so you make sure you are not a half inch over any lines. Super frustrated.

"The line" you're supposed to stay in, btw, Is eyeballing between a line on the driveway and a cement bumper at the back of the site.

Great spot on the ocean; spots are pretty darn crammed together, but that was known in advance and not uncommon on the ocean.  Ocean waves can be LOUD, but we appreciated that.  VERY hard to park larger/longer rigs if/when others are parking their TV’s along the road, but nothing I couldn’t manage…. Attendant at the food shop was super rude and in the middle of an argument with someone on speakerphone.  Entering the park requires an undoable uturn for most rigs, you want to come in from the south and heading north to enter to avoid the uturn of doom….
Camp host is rude. Never been unwelcomed upon arrival like this before. The food is bad and the place was dirty Would kit be back

I was only visiting friends who were there. So I have no idea what it costs but I will say it was very nice! It's a little tight and I think only smaller trailers and smaller motorhomes can get in there. It would be a real tough one for like a 40-footer. But that's my opinion, I do not own a motorhome or a trailer so I'm not the guy to be telling you how it is. All I can tell you is that I thought it was very nice, everybody was very friendly and everybody seemed to be having a good time!

name: Thornhill Broome Campground
formatted_address: 9000 Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: One of our favorite places. 45" spot on asphalt, clean sandy beach, fire pits, cheap, recommend Starlink, no hookups. 31" max, separate your tow car before entry. Marine Corps Air Station Point Mugu , North 2 miles.
Great spot, camping right on the sand, 35$/night, with picnic tables, fireplace, bathrooms, a park ranger who patrols, quiet hours, and a mile long stretch of beach just for you. Well worth it.

Very comfortable camping in our van. Porta Johns were clean. (sanitation company came through on Monday). Plenty pf fresh water spigots. Definitely a windy spot--we hunkered down here during a Santa Ana event, and it was epic.
Right on the beach. Porta-potties were clean. Water spigots available. Wind can be stong in the afternoons. Park Rangers were great. No LifeGaurds,if swimming, buddy system or have someone watch you.
