Beautiful campground. Sleep right next to the ocean clean spot.

name: Santa Cruz Island Scorpion Canyon Campground
formatted_address: Santa Cruz Island, Potato Harbor Rd, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
Stay here as your base for a Santa Cruz Island adventure! The campground is a short walk from the ferry. The lower campground is shaded by lots of tress. There is water available which makes things so convenient and the pit toilets are alright. Watch out for the foxes!

The only downside is that there are no campfires allowed, but it's still so worth it to stay here. Also plan on having no cell signal.

We started out by calling this place "Great value Maui" by the end of it we had an entirely unique experience that has cemented this trip and hike as one of my favorites in Ca. We stayed 2 nights which was enough. Arrived on the 3pm ferry set up camp cooked dinner had a couple drinks on the beach and then went to bed. We woke up the next day took in the kayaking tour (a bit pricey 150 but worth it if you are new to kayaking and caves) we finished that had our lunch and set out for a 9.5 Mile hike that was challenging but really pretty. The final day we broke down campafter breakfast and hung out on the beach until 230. The first boat did not leave until 330. It would have been much better at 10, 12 or 2. It could be an efficiency thing, but it really was not a convenience thing because we drove home to Sacramento. We didnt get back until midnight. Which was fine but then you are tired for the next day.

Lovely campground. Lots of water spigots and toilets. All in good working condition. Lots of sites to choose from, some closer to each other than others and some without shade. Lots of eucalyptus trees and shrubs throughout. Very minimal bugs; only saw one mosquito in two days.

Things probably have read before if coming to camp but reinforcing:

Weather; gets cold even on warm days. Windy often.

Trash; pack it in, pack it out. Impressed how respectful everyone is. Didn’t see much of any trash.

Swimming/Snorkeling; awesome snorkeling from Scorpion Beach. Water shoes are a must, very rocky beach.

WiFi/Cell Service; barely available.

Foxes; yes their cuteness lives up to the hype.

Trails; lots of great hikes, various lengths and all go up the mountain.

This is a great campground on Santa Cruz Island and depending on your camp site is between 0.2-0.6 miles from the boat dock. You should pack like you are backpacking as you need to transport all of your gear from the docks to your site and you don't want to spend half your trip walking back and forth. Many of the sites have eucalyptus trees for shade and the campground's location in a valley helps protect it from the high winds that are typical on the channel islands. The endemic island foxes frequent this campground and are very cute and fun to watch run through the camp. From the campground there are beautiful hikes along the cliffs along the ocean, ocean kayaking, and the park rangers usually have a short movie or program on weekend nights.

We camped here during late November with two kids.
The Del-Norte lower campground. We were on site 20.
This is our 2nd trip to the Channel Islands itself and we do really love the experience.
* You need to take an 1hr+ish boat ride to get here from Ventura.
* Pack a wagon/cart if you need to haul a "boatload of stuff" it's $10 extra and worth it
* You need to pack out what you take including garbage so be prepared.
* The luggage if lucky will be moved on the boat with a "bucket brigade" style if lucky and ample campers are onboard else you could be on your own.
* The weather was variable during our stay and unexpectedly more windy than we had anticipated. Note there are no windbreakers.
* The restrooms near the pier in general smelt less. They are reasonably well maintained but there is heavy camping and visitor traffic on this island.
* The campsites are well maintained. Surprised to see eucalyptus trees (invasive) all around us.
* Small foxes (smaller than the ones in Santa Rosa) thrive in this campsite. It was like witnessing Darwin's theory...
* The campsites.is central to many trails on this side of the island.
* The water outlets have had a lot of yellow jackets.
- There were very few other insects that were bothersome. We did observe some giant insects but they came out.at night and were fun to observe.
* There was no place for.cleaning utensils, it seemed ad-hoc and could create campsites where people washed and left remnants in different places. A defined spot like Santa Rosa would be great.
* Not all campsites are the same, so pick and choose carefully. At the end of the day the island is fabulous but still...
* lastly, the campground is relatively near the boat drop-off zone and the Kayak rental spot.
Have fun camping!!

name: Camp Comfort Park
formatted_address: 11969 N Creek Rd, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text:
I’ve been coming to this campground for years and have always loved it — the peaceful atmosphere, the scenery, and the overall experience have kept us coming back time and time again. Unfortunately, this most recent visit completely changed how we feel about the place. The new camp host is, without question, one of the most inconsiderate and disrespectful people we’ve ever dealt with at any campground.

Within minutes of arriving — literally less than ten minutes — he approached us in a rude, confrontational manner, cutting us off mid-sentence and talking over both me and my wife as we tried to answer his own question about payment for our spot. My wife was in the middle of handling the payment process, trying to be polite and cooperative, but instead of giving her a moment, he kept interrupting and raising his voice as if we were doing something wrong. The whole exchange was uncomfortable, unnecessary, and completely out of line for someone representing the campground.

We’ve been loyal visitors for years, bringing friends and family here and recommending this place because it used to be welcoming and well-run. Sadly, that’s no longer the case. The attitude and lack of basic respect from this new camp host ruined the experience for us before we even had a chance to settle in. It’s disappointing to see a place with such great memories go downhill because of one person’s behavior.

As long as this current host is running things, we won’t be coming back — and from the sound of it, we’re not the only ones who feel this way. Rest in peace, Camp Comfort, until they find a new host who actually understands what hospitality means. 😢

Great place but they double booked my campsite which was strange. We worked it out with the people next to us but I suggest booking only from the county website so you know you have a reservation. The other people booked through another site and only got one of the sites the wanted and then I was in the other. The ranger tried to help but we just split the site with the people it felt rude to make them leave even though it felt rude that they put their tent up in my site with a note to move my car, it worked out. The creek is right off the camp site, not a lot of water but enough that the kids were amused

This smaller campground is awesome. The camp host is friendly. He maintains the campground well. People are pleasant. The restroom is clean and it has coin operated hot showers and laundry room. Each campsite has electrical, water and sewerage hookup.  There is creek with running water behind the campground. There is a clubhouse and a park for dayuse people.  We would come back to camp.

Great camping experience, clean restrooms (with showers), next to a really nice, lush park with a playground for kids. The RV area seemed nice as well, although pretty packed.

Campsites are past the RV area in a more secluded area, although most campsites, minus the two on the ends, are close to each other.

Also, this area is haunted by a few spirits, and there’s a wild rooster that hangs out.

We had a great weekend at Camp Comfort!  With four toddlers in our group, the clean and quiet campsites by the excellent playground made for an excellent and enriching experience enjoyed by all. Not sure where the camp host was, but the park ranger was very friendly, as were the other camp patrons (including the rooster).

name: Ventura Ranch KOA Holiday
formatted_address: 7400 Pine Grove Rd, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: Good experience. Nice large spot for our 42 foot fifth wheel. Peacocks were so fun to watch! Looks like they have a ton of activities and amenities for kids and such a big park. No complaints.

We could not have been happier with our home away from home this holiday. Ventura Ranch KOA truly felt like a dream campground, offering all the amenities and activities of a high-end resort. From tipis, wagons, and cabins to scenic hiking trails, an epic zipline course, and a beautiful pool, there was something for everyone to enjoy.

Its location, just one hour north of LA and centrally positioned for easy day trips throughout California made it even better for this family. We were impressed and cannot wait to return.

Overall it’s fun for the kids with the jumping pillow and just being outdoors but the drain in our cabin’s shower was plugged and made taking a shower very gross.
Also, make sure you physically call and lock in all sites on one phone call. I tried very hard to make sure my group was together MONTHS before my visit and was very frustrated to find out one family wasn’t close at all. After all my early emails and calls to ensure we would be together, I was shocked the staff didn’t tell me exactly what I had to do in order to make that happen. I know for next time but it made this trip a flop.

My wife and I were in space #5 and the site its self was nice. However the majority of of our stay we felt we were camping inside an Andy Gump. The sewage smell was so strong and prevalent the entire time of our stay, that we put our fire out and went into our RV EVERY NIGHT.  TOO BAD, THE AREA ITSELF IS NICE, but will NOT BE BACK. The staff was very nice.

Rooms = spaces!  Great spaces,  roomy,  clean, nice firepit/grill. Beautiful location.   Staff are wonderful!!!  Very helpful,  cheerful and knowledgeable.  This is my second trip to Ventura Ranch KOA.  Great experience,  both times!

name: Dennison Park
formatted_address: 7250 Santa Paula Ojai Rd, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful sunset, there is a nice overlook of ojai. The camping spots don't have a lot of privacy or shade but there was only one other camper when I was there so things were pretty quiet. Not a lot to do in this park, it has a small playground and picnic area. Camp host was very nice and helpful. It is a good place to stay a night or two as it is quite close to town and some beautiful hiking but not really a destination in of itself

It was out first time camping in Ojai and ny first time since my boy scout days almost 30 years ago now. So we wanted to pick a place that had all the amenities we wre looking for, one being clean and accessible restrooms. We got site 5, which had a great view of the mountain. The site was large enough for our three person tent, and mid-sized SUV. Site also came with a bench and fire pit. Ours had a tree which was a plus for shade. The campgrounds is on a hill and the sites are situated progressively in higher areas. The campgrounds are very peaceful. The stars are elite. Everyone has great camping etiquette. Dane, the campgrounds managet was the best part of the experience. He was very attentive, checking in on us throught our stay (2 nights). He takes great care of the grounds and it shows. Overall, 10/10 experience. We will be back. Thank you Dane for a great experience!

Great campground and an awesome host to match. Dane helped us get setup with a great  campsite after we had accidentally picked a not so great spot. The camp ground was quiet and serene with beautiful views of the valley. You felt remote enough to unplug but still had great service and clean bathroom nearby. It was perfect long weekend for us and our dog. Just what the doctor ordered.

Such a great experience with beautiful views. The camp host was so nice, stopped by a few times to make sure we were good 👍, gave us good camping tips. He also helped the people who were camping next to us set up their tent. We got a nice shady spot & really enjoyed our time there. We also made a new friend, this praying mantis hung out with us the whole time. We will definitely be going back next time

We camped here for one night and booked 15. Great spacious site for the dogs to roam around! We were able to see the rocket launch at night so that was pretty awesome! It’s a bit dusty but at least there are wood chips mixed in so it wasn’t too bad. Bring a brush if you have dogs. The ground keeper was friendly and reminded people to come watch the rocket launch. It’s pretty closed to Ojai downtown.

name: Cross Campground
formatted_address: Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: Love this place.  Been coming here since I was a kid.  Plenty of cool stuff to do.  Natural water slides, cliff jumps, bouldering, camping, hiking you name it.  The vibe is chill and everyone is always friendly.  The hike there is uphill and back down is a breeze.  Make sure not to pack too much as it can be sketchy at times.  Wouldnt recommend doing this hike if you have balance issues or are not very mobile.  There are spots where you need to hold on to rocks and climb on the edge of a big 10 foor drop.  Had my 11 year old and was pretty scared he would loose his grip.  My buddy and I had hands on him around the presupus but also worried about our own footing as we had 80 pound packs like knuckle heads.   Next teip we will be packing much lighter.
Love this camp site...maybe about 35 minutes from the water slide..
You need to walk like a hour,  but that's really good...
Nice

name: Foster Residence Campground
formatted_address: 59 Casitas Vista Rd, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Great site super friendly camp host. Very welcoming.  Clean site and restrooms . We stayed at site 5. Within 7 min from main street beach and pier. Definitely camping here again.Thank you!!!!

Small, nice, quiet, clean, easy access. Highly recommended. The RV sites have all necessary hookups (including cable TV). Toilets are very clean and well maintained. Group picnic area available. Great views!

We came here to stay while I had to go into work there in Ventura. The campground is a nice place to camp/hike with a lot of history in the area. Although, spot #2 and #1 at the campground are "not" big rig friendly. I don't care what the their website or other sites post. We had to maneuver our way back into the spot and made it work but I would not suggest it for big rigs.

Our stay was nice but cut short due to the drastic rainy weather coming our way. This spot sits at the bottom of a sloped road or washy area so we were not about to stay there and have anything come barreling down behind us and cause us any harm to ourselves, our pets or our truck/rig. So, we messaged the park about us leaving and explained our safety concerns and they were happy to refund us the two days early we had to leave. We were very grateful and anxious to leave to get ahead of the impending storm.

As you all know when California gets rain it gets rain and you need to stay clear of these areas for your safety.

We would go back and stay at Foster Residence Campground and would recommend.

This review is about the tent camping at Foster Residence Campground.

We visited in April 2023 and had a great stay. We stayed in site 16. Sites 11-13 are all grouped together, do not have much tree cover, and are on a slight slope. Sites 14-16 are more private and are along a creek (we could hear the creek but not see it, and I don't think you'd hear it during dry season). Imo site 14 and site 16 are the best for tent camping - flat ground, tree cover, space between you and your neighbor. These sites are also tucked back away from the road so you only hear nature. I believe all the other sites have a concrete slab for trailers and RVs. We paid $37 with online reservations for one night plus one dog.

The bathrooms were well maintained with flush toilets and running water. The camp host is tucked behind a huge fence but on our visit the host had a Ring doorbell set up in case you have questions.

They did not have any firewood on site, so be sure to pick some up on your way in.

We stayed one night with our Jeep Wrangler (two people / tent on top) and paid $34 (tax incouded)
The place is neat and well organised. Very quiet and full of camper vans and people camping with tents. You pay online through an website.
There are parking lots with tables, fire pits and restrooms and common areas. Plenty of shade and
wood to burn. The park host lives in an RV, very nicely she let us stay when we arrived at 7 pm.
4 stars because There are no showers or hot water.
There is little light (bring flashlights with you).

name: Ventura Beach RV Resort
formatted_address: 800 W Main St, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
It is a beautiful RV Park and very well maintained.  Amazing concrete pads, good size and nice healthy grass at each site. We knew going in that there would be a pretty good amount of road noise and we were not wrong. What we did not know is if you happen to book on a holiday weekend (we spent 3 nights during Veterans Day weekend)or literally anytime a group books multiple sites, they pretty much have carte blanche to make as much noise as they want.  That includes playing music at concert levels until 1:00 AM and shouting F bombs at each other. We like a good time as much as anybody, but common decency and courtesy calls for taking the noise level down at say 10:00.  Not so here, we asked if there is a quiet time and were told sort of but if a group wants to party we kind of just let them.   We were two sites away from the main party location and believe me sleep was not an option, and the policies of the park are no refunds for any reason. I can say with certainty that we won't be booking here again.  Two stars for the beautiful amenities and no more for the lack of sleep. Ventura is a really great town even when you are forced into zombie mode when trying to enjoy it.
So, with all that said book at your own tolerance levels.

What a great RV park location- you can ride your bike from the RV park to the beach without ever being in the road.  And then ride  on the boardwalk, past the pier and the beach and through a city park and back.  Watching surfers run barefoot with their boards from their cars on Main Street to the surf was a favorite of mine.  And Cody, told me to check out the end of Main Street where u can walk to and that was such a long blocked off area of the historic district protected from cars, and patrolled; an area to feel safe, shop, walk, bike, and eat and drink, and then walk back to the park.  Ventura is simply an awesome historic town.  I met a woman there named Martha who owns Ecugreen and now I have a new friend for life. She greets you for the first time as if she knew you for a lifetime. Great store with Ecuadorian goods and such a lovely person she is.
The park is so pretty and I love the long wide roads and the many beautiful trees.
Great stay- Great staff.  We always say RV parks are only as good as the people at the front desk.  Of course we had a couple of issues like anywhere else but the difference is, it is run by a man named Kylie, and he could not have been more accommodating, respectful, and just a nice person.   We would definitely stop in there again when on the west coast.
Special thanks to Kylie, Adrianna, Cody, Beverly, Sam, Tilia, Christine, Megan, Ventura, Juda, Ceaser, Rob, and I apologize, but I can’t recall the women’s name in admin who passed me off to Kylie, after she validated our minor complaint about our neighbor.  Spike (?) working the security was a great guy too.  So if you ever want to park and party, or park and relax on weekdays, this is a wonderful place to do so!

This place feels like a small community in the best way. Good neighbors, very welcoming atmosphere, and staff that is always positive and helpful. The mountain views are beautiful, the trees smell amazing, and the palms make it feel like a true California getaway.
Ventura Beach RV Resort has propane refill station.You can get everything you need in store.
Only 5 minutes to the ocean and walking distance to grocery stores and restaurants. Highly recommended.

Ventura Beach RV Resort has become one of my absolute favorite places to stay. As the owner of Seaward Brewing, I’m constantly looking for spaces that inspire creativity, offer comfort, and allow me to recharge — and this resort delivers every single time.

From the moment you arrive, you can feel how much pride the team takes in keeping the property clean, welcoming, and truly guest-focused. The sites are well-maintained, the amenities are convenient, and the location is unbeatable — peaceful, beautiful, and just steps from everything Ventura has to offer.

What I value most is the genuine hospitality. Every stay feels personal. The staff always goes above and beyond to make sure we’re comfortable and enjoying our time. As someone who runs a business built on community, I really appreciate that same energy here.

If you’re looking for a relaxing getaway, a place to unwind between workweeks, or just a well-run resort that treats you like family — Ventura Beach RV Resort is the spot. Highly recommended.”

— Monique, Seaward Brewing

First-time customer traveling in a 45-ft Class A.

I would not recommend this park for larger Class A coaches (40 ft+) unless you don’t mind scratches. Trees are not trimmed up, and when combined with cars parked on the roads, you’ll often end up hugging one side and scraping branches down your rig.

The park is also extremely strict, with rules that seem more about making money than hospitality. Even family members stopping by for a few minutes have to check in and pay for a pass. I understand the need for check-in, but charging for a quick visit or drop-off feels excessive. If you have friends or relatives in the area who might want to swing by, this is not the place to stay.

For the price, the restrictions are disappointing. On top of that, the freeway noise is constant, so it’s not exactly a peaceful stay.

That said, there are positives:
•	The landscaping is beautiful.
•	The park is very clean.
•	Concrete parking pads are a nice touch.
•	Extra water hookups at the site are convenient.

Overall, while it’s a clean and well-kept park with nice features, the strict rules, freeway noise, and lack of attention to tree trimming make it a poor choice for big rigs.

name: Middle Lion Campground
formatted_address: 19786 Forest Rte 6N31, Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Small, beautiful campground.  I would have given five stars, but a couple of the spots are so small and slightly at an angle, not sure where you'd even pitch a tent.  However, overall we had a great time.  There's one bathroom, but no running water.  Make sure you pack in all your water needs, and pack out your trash.

It was a fun time, me and my nephew went and did a day hike and camped 1 night. It was 20 dollars to stay. The road was very bad though getting there. You need a high clearance car or SUV if you want to go there. When we were leaving we saw someone in a car had hit their oil pan and we're losing oil because of it. The whole road back to the top had a stream of oil that had come out of their car. So be very careful and go slow down the road. Very holy road and not in a good way. Other then that and pretty much each camp site was in the dead heat of the sun from about 10 a.m. until 7 p.m. wasn't so comfortable after a few hours of not finding shade. So bring a tarp or expect a good sun burn. One last thing there is no cell service, except by Ojai, and 1 tiny part at the top part of one of the mountains driving there. So make all your calls and download your movies ahead of time. Or just be like us and get out of the heat of the day and drive back to town to download a movie and cool off at the same time. Lol. I hope this helped others to plan a good camping trip. Enjoy camping.
Great campground! Hard to tell which sites are which so I added some photos.
Loved the camp site river hiking trails small camp ground shaped in a circle some are close to each other some are bigger depends on which one you get a lot of young couples an families .
Love it best location to camp with kids and pets secured and safe.

name: La Jolla Group Campsite
formatted_address: 9000 Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Hot showers (no tokens needed), the rangers cleaned the bathrooms every day. Lots of hikers going by. Good value.
The Best location for the family. Clean restrooms, hiking trails, wonderful view, and secluded.

Great group campground.  There is ample parking for your group.  There are flushing toilets, sinks, and showers.  You can hear the waves from the two camp elevations; and can see the ocean.
There was hot water when we were there in October 2023.  There are a lot of mosquitos, but you are in the woods.  One negative is that there is no cell service fro Verizon, T-mobile.  There may be one bar for At&t at the parking lot.

probably one of the best campsites in the area. it was clean, had great wonderful bathrooms and showers. no drinking water but i loved hearing the ocean nearby and being able to cook meals with the amazing grill!!

I came here as part of a Boy Scout outing and I must say it was quite nice. There was plenty of room for several tents and a picnic area complete with tables and a water faucet. You do have to keep an eye out for the local wildlife as we found a nice sized rattlesnake eating rats by the tables but the rangers were able to come by to chase it off. My one complaint is that the showers didn't have hot water but at least they were free of charge.  The beach is just one mad dash across the PCH just mind the traffic. I would for sure come back with the troop.

name: Sycamore Cove
formatted_address: 9000 W Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Such a beautiful beach!  Parking space is limited I recommend going in before is open at 8am to wait in line and reserve your favorite spot ! Very clean and well maintained place

Sycamore cove is beautiful and perfect for families.  There are barbecues available for use. There is a large log to sit on for people with limited mobility (like myself).

Parking is $3/hr or $12/day here and is by Credit/Debit card only.

The biggest downside was the porta potties. There were 3 normal size and 1 handicap accessible.  They were all beyond disgusting, they are NOT maintained at all.

Even with that,  when we come back to CA, I could see visiting here once again.

We took a 5 minute video of the waves crashing.  Our YouTube account is StormyDuo if you'd like to watch it.
Great little beach located across from the campground. Restrooms, picnic benches, barbecues and firepits.
Beautiful spot,  firepits, picnic tables

A quiet beach to lay down in the sand and watch the waves and the magnificent sunsets. Not overly crowded, not a show-offy beach. Bathroom facility is under equipped and dirty. Park first and then go to the kiosk to buy a timed ticket from the machine and put on your dashboard..

name: Foster Red Mountain Campground
formatted_address: 247 Casitas Vista Rd, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: I've camped here a couple of times, most recently about a week ago. I really like coming up here because it's so close to my place, especially this time of year. It's super pretty after it rains, everything's all green and colorful. The campground and bathrooms are clean too. My only gripe is how close it is to the road. You can always hear cars driving by, so it kinda takes away from the camping vibe, you know?

At first, this place seemed decent, but then came Monday. Most of the days were filled with loud noises from tree cutting and jack hammering from the street workers. Then there is also the highway right next to you, and that can get pretty loud as well.

Then, along with all that, there was a couple that were staying there days on end drinking and fighting with their two dogs pooping at everyone's sites. (Thought there was a leash rule?) They also burned all their trash the whole time all day and night, including  plastic, which was horrible smelling and made the entire campground a smoke hole.

The place is also all dirt and lots of wind, so expect everything, including you, to be dirty the whole time, for there are no showers available here. There is also no electricity and very random water piks.

My site was covered in litter, including broken glass, bottle caps, cigarette butts and so on. Rarely do you see the camp hosts checking the grounds.

There was what I thought was a hiking trail, but that's a bit of a joke, for it is a short trail to neighboring Foster Park Campground. This trail is also a dangerous mess, and you can see that in the photos I posted.

For the cost to stay here, I'd say go to the other campground next door that's called just Foster Park, not this one.

Also, know there are a ton of mosquitoes and flies in this area. It's truly out of control.

Additionally, bring your own wood for camp host does not supply it on site. Trust me, you'll need that wood to make fires to help from getting bitten so much.

We had a great time this past weekend at Foster Red Mountain campground. Dane was the camphost and is literally the best ever! He helped us so much, kept the bathrooms clean, and was always available if needed, also very nice and friendly.
The kids loved to play on the swings. We did a little hike to the one attached to the campground. And we went across the street to enjoy the river.
It is nice quite and peaceful place to get away. I stayed there for a week, and enjoy every minute of it. Just me and my two little doggies.

We used to camp here with a lot of kids because it is easily accessible from LA and there are almost no mountain roads. There is a recreation area nearby where we could play in the river, but when we went on July 14th 2023, the recreation area on the other side of the river was under construction and closed, so we could not play in the river. Also, the men's restroom is broken, and from the cleaning inspection form, it appears that it has been broken since about May.

Sites on the side near the road should definitely be avoided because of the noise of cars.

name: Middle Matilija Campground
formatted_address: Ojai, CA 93023, USA

google_reviews.text: Great fall season in Matilija. A different look and feel from spring

Great campground, big enough for a medium size group. There's a stream nearby where you can get water. It usually runs year around. We were there this November and the temperatures were in the mid 30s at night and in the '60s during the day. There's an established fire pit for you to use. There's a lot of shade from nearby trees that can help you in the summertime.

Nice spacious campsite, room for several tents. Two fire pits, two grills. Large tree and creek through camp.

*WARNING*
Be careful anywhere on that trail, especially right alongside the creek. There is poison oak all over the place. Before you go, familiarize yourself with what poison oak looks like and avoid it at all costs. I've been pretty miserable since my trip.
It feels so distant from civilization. The stars are brilliant.

name: Big Cone Campground
formatted_address: E Fork Trail, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful, plenty of sites (primitive, no water or toilet) but close to a creek/waterfall. Continue down to the creek and back up to the Cross campground for a greater challenge. 2 grills and 1 fire ring plus plenty of rocks for sitting. 3.5 miles out to the site from the trailhead

12-12-23 camp is a little dirty, if you have extra time, space, please bring a garbage bag. I couldn't clean it all cause I was double packed carrying stuff for me and my little daughter. There is still water further down the trail, about 10 minutes away

Waste of a trip. If you dont trust this post go to the KOA campground while ur there for nothing.Big cone campground is unreachable. Property is placed along a old roadway and the roads are closed. If you dont trust me, go see. Their is a non reservation needed and spacious KOA campground for $25. Be out by 11am if you dont wanna waste the day driving some where else
Walked through it to goto the Punch Bowls.  We will definitely backpack and camp here.
kind of lacking because of the drought right now, but when things subside it's definitely the most awesome place in the county

name: Emma Wood Group Camp
formatted_address: Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
This is about Emma Woods Group campground, which is open. Entrance is off west Main Street and has an office cubicle, where you can check in to the camp host. Its a wonderful campground and a path will lead you to one of the most beautiful natural beaches. However after a big rain the ground will be flooded when you walk underneath the railroad bridge, so not on rainy days
Please note that the State Park Emma Woods is and will be closed.Their entrance is
over the bridge, coming down. It has been a challenge for me to differ between the two parks
One is closed, but the group campground which is a different entrance is open.

Close to town and beach. You could technically walk. But bikeably easy. Bring shade, there isn't much. Low dun from freeway actually helped me sleep good. Has firepit and tables.  Close bathrooms and a path to the beach. Was pleased!

I'll be honest. When I first pulled past the guard stand I was a bit skeptical. It was clean but desolate. HOWEVER,  once you take the path to the beach or walk the trails it's a completely different experience. I literally had the beach to myself. A few people came and went through the parking lot but left without getting out of the vehicle...their mistake. It is like having your own private beach. Listening to the waves crash on the rocks was like music and the sunset made me feel bad for those landlocked on the freeway...get out there and explore you might just find a gem.
A fairly decent place to host an event even if they need to be more flexible about the camping spots.

Do not go here if you’re planning to stay overnight. There is no indication to pay for Night Use  anywhere in their website so I chose Day use.

The Natl Guard kicked up off and threatened to give us a citation before I could even show him the ticket I paid for. They deserve to be fired. I REPEAT. DO NOT COME HERE.

name: Fox Campground
formatted_address: CM75+XH, Mira Monte, CA 93001, USA

name: La Jolla Valley Walk-In Camp
formatted_address: Oxnard, CA 93033, USA

google_reviews.text: Fun spot. Sites come with a table and food box. Pretty overgrown and some sites are better than others. Generally overgrown. If I don’t mind the extra brush to create some more privacy. Overall nice for an overnight trip as this locale is pretty stunning in terms of views and not too challenging to hike.

Very overgrown after the incredibly wet winter of 2022-2023. The silver lining is that means there is more shade. Our campsite felt nicely secluded by the surrounding brush. It costs $10 per night per camper. Pay at the automated pay station at the Ray Miller Trailhead, GPS coords 34.08607, -119.03700. We took the shorter route which starts at the Chumash Trailhead. It is very steep for the first 0.8 miles but shorter overall at about 3  miles compared to starting at the Ray Miller trailhead. There is a toilet but it was almost full and very dirty. Be sure to bring your own toilet paper. Still no water, no dogs, no campfires.

Good walk-in Campsite with a toilet, but no water. All sites offer good privacy, have a Picknick table, bear box and space for one tent. Some sites are a little overgrown but no problem if you got a knife ;) no shade or almost no shade.. And no fires allowed!!
You pay for the campsite at the ray miller trailhead parking lot. The machine only allows one camper per transaction, otherwise you get the message to try another credit card..

There are 8 walk-in campsites and 1 group site, all sites have a picnic table with bear box attached, and room for 1 tent. No fires are permitted. There is one pit toilet, and therein is the reason for the drop to 3 stars, the pit has not been emptied, it is full to the top.
Each of the sites in and of themselves is great. Very secluded from each other. This was my second time camping here, and I will return, even though the La Jolla trail is currently closed and you must take the long way around.

A great spot for a quick backpacking trip & great for beginners. I started from the Ray Miller trailhead. There's an electronic pay station where you select your trip type (day hike / La Jolla group camp/ La Jolla hike-in / etc), number of days, and number of campers, then pay with cash (no change given) or credit card. It's about 3 miles uphill along the Ray Miller trail then another 2 along the La Jolla fire road and down into the valley. Based on the map near the pit toilet there are 7 sites: 5 on the trail by the pit toilet and 2 sites on a trail maybe 2 minutes before it. Great to go in the late winter after the rain -- everything was so green! Note there's no water, no dogs, no campfires.

name: Waypoint Ventura Vintage Trailer Hotel & Campground
formatted_address: 398 S Ash St unit e, Ventura, CA 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
I've stayed at Waypoint a couple of times now, and have really enjoyed both stays. It is a unique place, perfect for those who love something different from the usual. The concept itself is fabulous, and on each stay we chose a different trailer so as to experience a new space each time.

The most important thing for me is that I stayed both times as a solo mama with my son, when he was age 2 and again at age 5. Both times I felt completely safe, and he loved staying somewhere so interesting, especially right by the train tracks where he could watch a train go by now and then. He was even the one to request to go back the second time. There is a gate to access the community that locks each evening, and all trailers have solid locks on the front doors.

The welcoming details of the experience are what really stand out to me. Each trailer is very clean on arrival, and features locally-sourced coffee and tea, and a very cool local guidebook. They aim to reduce waste while still offering high quality bathroom items, which is appreciated. The front desk and common areas are equipped with great features, from fire pits (s'more kits at the front) to hot cider and games. The atmosphere is just very pleasant and welcoming overall.

You have to go into the experience anticipating that the trailers are old (even though they are redone very well), so there are aspects of each that reflect that age.

When we stayed the second time, the bridge to the beach was closed, but when it opens again it provides very easy access to the pier and beach. The only downside of location is that it is adjacent to a relatively recently built block of apartment buildings, blocking what surely was a great view before the build. But it is close to downtown, restaurants, the highway, etc, so is very convenient.

Overall, both experiences were great, and I would definitely stay here again.

What a unique place to stay.  Vintage gulf stream trailers renovated but with still keeping the authenticity was quite impressive.   We stayed two nights and could have stayed longer had time permitted us to do so.  The grounds are well laid out as well as being clean and neat.  The staff is very amiable, warm and accommodating. One of us was having a birthday and the staff surprised her with a gift of a Waypoint coffee mug!
It was an easy walk into town where we found some hidden treasures in the way of restaurants and great food.
Also, the beach was about a 15 minute walk away and we took advantage of the closeness.  If you are into staying at novel places let Waypoint Ventura be on your list.  Oh, and the home made cookies were amazing!!!!!

We stayed two nights in the crown. Modern and very clean. Bathroom was very nice. They had everything we needed for a comfortable stay. Loved the spot in the park in the round courtyard - other trailers (looked like the bigger ones) were more individual in their placement.

The front desk was always accommodating and offered nice touches to help with morning and evening ways to enjoy the grounds.

Only con with the location is it is next to train tracks- trains do go by and do blow their horn and yes it is very loud. But walkable to downtown and the ocean.

Highly recommend this unique way of spending the night in Ventura.

We stayed at Waypoint for one night and wish it were longer. A fun and unique place to stay with exceptionally friendly staff. We loved the attention to detail and little extras- like cocoa and hot apple cider in the evenings and freshly baked cookies in the morning. The train does run right past the property, but this didn’t bother us at all during the night. Highly recommend this property if you’re passing through Ventura!

I can not say enough about how fun this place is.  We attended my Grandsons wedding, it was so nice to have everyone in same area. Our trailer was SO cute! We rented “life’s a Beach.  Beds were so comfortable, amenities were perfect!  Loved fresh squeezed OJ and fresh baked goodies in the morning.  My husband is using oxygen so the front desk brought Ice, water and rides to our events as needed.  A+++ to the staff service.

name: Tombstone Rock Santa Paula Creek
formatted_address: 4850 CA-150, Santa Paula, CA 93060, USA

google_reviews.text: The creek is a short trek from picnic and playground areas which look maintained. Very mellow with plenty of parking and shade.
Very pretty and very relaxing. I loved the sound of the water and the cool layers in the rocks, as well as the rushing water.

This creek the really pretty it has a small trail to go along it, clear water, and almost no sign of people. It’s a nice place to get away from the busy and messy campsites.  Be aware at night because coyotes often run through parts of the trail.
Grandkids love this place.
It's very nice, I recommend it. You only pay $5.

name: Upper Las Virgenes Canyon Open Space Preserve
formatted_address: Western end of, Victory Blvd, Woodland Hills, CA 91367, USA

google_reviews.text: Pretty cool if you’d like a mostly flat area to run through. Not much of an incline, but great landscape views! Especially now that everything is all green. Oooh and cute goats to go visit from a short distance! Pretty easy so you can bring your doggo (but leash is recommended because of other dogs). No easy access to restrooms. Parking is paid, but you can use street parking for free!

steep trails going up and down, some may need a walking stick. lots of people but not crowded! i did see a coyote here once, but it did keep it’s distance. lot of birds, hills and different hiking and bike trails. parking for $3 at the place, or free if you don’t mind adding 1-2 mins to your hike!

Amazing preserve, mind blowing views during spring time. Easy to moderate hikes. If you’re doing loops plan accordingly since the fastest one at least 3 miles long (you can do out and back though).

Paid parking lot ($3 cash left in the envelope). Plenty of free parking prior the main gate. Dogs are welcome on leash. No public restroom, no water fountains.

Upper Las Virgenes Canyon Open Space Preserve is the kind of place that makes you feel like you’ve stumbled into a nature documentary. Tucked away in the West Hills area, it’s got sprawling trails that wind through golden hills - or vibrant green with poppy blooms after a good rain - dotted with stately oaks. Perfect for hiking, biking, trail running. Pro tip: bring water and a hat, because the sun is for real.

The views are absurdly gorgeous, especially when you hit the higher points and realize LA’s chaos is just a distant memory. You might spot a hawk soaring overhead or a rabbit darting off. The trails can get dusty, and the inclines sneaky, but the peacefulness is worth every step. It’s raw, untamed, and gloriously free of city noise.

The entire area are covered with the beautiful wild yellow mustard flowers right now. The sight is spectacular. Good to know that there are parking spaces in the area. Couldn't do the walking trail at that time. Took some pictures so to share with friends who love to do outdoor walking/hiking.

name: Malibu Creek State Park
formatted_address: 1925 Las Virgenes Road, Calabasas, CA 91302, USA

google_reviews.text: New to the State, decided to mark off another item from my Bucket List. Always wanted to see the MASH filming location. I was not disappointed. What a beautiful State Park, the views are just spectacular here. The hike to the location took me just over an hour, but I was stopping to take photos of various spots along my hike too. My only regret was not having more time to truly explore more of the area. I will be returning again this summer to hike around more spots. I would like to remind anyone coming up here to be EXTREMELY mindful of where you are, this is a wilderness area which is home to a lot of wildlife. You are in their home, think before you decide to approach that friendly looking Forest Puppy, or that cute cat w the murder mittens. It won't end well for either of you. Lastly,  the rattlesnakes,  I was in the park about 6 hours total, I encountered 4 of them, almost stepped on one of them. Be careful where you step or where you decide to put your hand if climbing up a ledge. Aside from that, enjoy this truly beautiful place and relax.

Abundant parking. Beautiful views and very easy and nice trails. Nice waterfall with varying amount depending on rainfall. Has a nice information center with local native history and taxonomy of local flora & fauna.

I loved it, I went for new years day.i fished. I didn't catch anything. The hike was cute. I felt terrible I got there later. It was fine though. I stayed till dark like around 6 I was back at the parking lot. I made a small fire. I turned the grill on , grilled some meat , ate some tacos. Yum.

Really beautiful park! Highly recommend checking out the swimming hole at Rock Pool. Right now the water is really deep and you can jump from pretty high at your own risk. But there are lots of hidden rocks so make sure you check the depth of the area you plan on jumping into.

The water is a biturky and very cold and refreshing.

There's usually always people there so don't expect a nice private swimming hole but theres plenty of places along the creek you can go if you want more privacy.

Great camping spot. Nice flat and level pads. Plenty of tent space and big fire ring. Bathrooms were very clean and well stocked, bring hand soap. Camp sites are dry but water at bathrooms. Settled in nice area, lots of peaks around campground and easy access.to hiking trails.

name: Solstice Canyon
formatted_address: 3455 Solstice Canyon Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text:
This was a really easy and beautiful hike. Parking was hard to get however we managed, very popular spot.

It’s nice to have toilets at trail heads to use before and after, however these toilets were absolutely terrible unfortunately. Two were full to the toilet seat and the other two were not far behind.

The trail loop is about 3 miles and took us a very leisurely 75 minutes with a dog. Go clockwise, starting with the canyon/the waterfall so that you face the view on the way down. No sun shelter on the way down.

Very well maintained trail; easy to follow. We went on a Sunday morning and it was not busy at all. Parking was not a problem at 9am.

Great views. Happy to do this again.

The one element that makes Los Angeles worth all the challenges of living is the stunning array of state parks. Solstice Canyon is a great place to bring friends and family of all ages visiting from out of town.  It's a perfect hike for older folks or younger kids because it's mostly flat and the scenery is diverse.

The terminus of the main hike ends at a small waterfall where you will often see children of all ages grilling and enjoying the beautiful view. If not for the natural beauty of California, ideal year round weather, it's gorgeous coastline, sprawling deserts, giant sequoias, snow capped mountains, it wouldn't be the most desirable place in the US to live. Be sure to explore the natural riches of the state or you haven't really "lived" or "seen" California.

Solstice Canyon is one of my go-to hikes in Malibu. It’s the perfect mix of shaded canyon trails and beautiful ocean views, and the hike itself is moderate and manageable for most people. I love coming here to recharge and get fresh air between real estate appointments. There are two parking lots, so parking is usually easy unless it’s a busy weekend. The trails are well maintained, peaceful, and offer a great glimpse of Malibu’s natural beauty. Always a favorite.

Solstice Canyon was peaceful and full of natural beauty. I went with someone and explored the trails, waterfalls, and old ruins hidden in the hills. Teenagers were hiking in groups, taking pictures near the water, and enjoying the quiet and scenic atmosphere. The atmosphere is perfect for teens who love nature and adventure because it is calm, beautiful, and full of interesting places to explore. The service shows in how well the trails are maintained and how safe everything feels. The value is amazing because the experience feels rich and memorable without costing anything. The quality of the scenery is high with clean paths, beautiful views, and unique historical structures. The selection of trails and photo spots gives teenagers plenty of ways to enjoy the area. I have only been once but I would go again. Teenagers would love the peaceful adventure, the nature views, and the creative photo opportunities and I highly recommend it.

name: Vicente Bluffs Reserve
formatted_address: 31501 Palos Verdes Dr W, Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90275, USA

google_reviews.text: This trail is absolutely stunning! If you love walking and soaking in breathtaking landscapes, this is the perfect spot for you. It’s bursting with nature, and we even stumbled upon a hilarious herd of goats! 🐐 It’s funny for kids and anyone who adores the great outdoors. ! 🍃🌊 🍃 🌊

Enjoy the beautiful 1.5 miles trail with friends, families and your pups🐶It's easy and the road is flat. Free parkings are also available. Make sure to check the road situation after rainfalls(It may close after the rain.

Nice walk with a beautiful view. Not very long but it's very nice and clean. Lots of small lizards and rabbits moving around. Nice little museum at the end. This address takes you to the residential area with two openings. If you want to get to the parking lot  search or map to the  Point Vicente Interpretive Center.... The trail path will be to your right. Not crowded (Tuesday).  A sign for rattlesnakes. I didn't see any. Be careful and enjoy

Beautiful local spot in the Palos Verdes Area to visit. Perfect place to go in a short walk / run, the walking trail isn’t long itself and it’s more of a place to take a nice stroll/ walk/ meander and saunter around, enjoy the views and the sunshine, the open ended air. It’s gorgeous on a sunny day, wouldn’t recommend on a cloudy or rainy day. The parking is easy to find and there’s always room, there’s a gift shop and restrooms located near the lighthouse with a nice lawn and picnic areas. Its also located across a Trader Joe’s / shopping plaza which is nice! This isn’t a touristy area but I would recommend if you’re in the LA area and looking for a place to show family or friends visiting, or if you want to spend some time with a significant other. Wouldn’t recommend for tourists because it’s more quiet and far out.

Probably my favorite place in Rancho Palos verdes. Great views for the sunset and sunrise. Usually other places in Rancho Palos verdes you can’t really see the sunrise right as the sun is coming up but this location you can see the sunrise as soon as the sun comes up. So I love coming here for both the sunrise and sunset.

name: Santa Monica Mountains National Recreation Area
formatted_address: Calabasas, CA 91302, USA

google_reviews.text:
Santa Monica Mountains National Recreation Area is a breathtaking natural escape just outside Los Angeles.
With miles of hiking trails, scenic overlooks, and abundant wildlife, it’s perfect for outdoor enthusiasts and adventure seekers.
Whether you’re hiking, biking, or just soaking in the views, the park offers peace, fresh air, and stunning landscapes.
A must-visit for nature lovers and anyone wanting to disconnect from city life. 🥾🌲

Enjoyed a relaxing trip with some co-workers at the group site this week. The facilities are wonderful with plenty of picnic tables, clean restrooms, hot water, and showers. Enjoyed the hike out to the MASH site  while others did some climbing at the Planet of the Apes Wall.

Fantastic experience in Santa Monica. I went there for training at Channel Island Navy Base. I met great people and ate delicious food. The Pier is wide, you can find many vendors along the pier. You can listen to music play by local musicians. The public restrooms are clean and overall is a good atmosphere for family, and friends.

Beautiful scenery along with well maintained roads. There a few spots you can stop and take videos or pictures of the surroundings. There is a city named Calabasas on the way to Malibu which is pretty. Just drive slow and be careful, a lot of curves and blind spots. Take caution please.
Absolutely beautiful place

name: Portuguese Bend Reserve
formatted_address: Burma Rd, Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90275, USA

google_reviews.text: Parking was insane and absolutely meant to keep non-locals out. Classist anti-poor infrastructure. I called the city to complain and no one called me back. You have to reserve a parking spot an hour in advance. The parking lot and spaces were completely empty because the app doesn’t let you pay upon arrival, only in advance. You can park half a mile away, but what are people with disabilities supposed to do? I hope someone sues the city of Lomita for ADA violations. This also applies to the nearby park for the cliff views and easier walk. PBR is strenuous and steep

The parking situation is sketchy. If you want to park close to the trail you have to pay about $10-$11 through a mobile app.  I parked 2 blocks down from the entrance of the hike and thankfully I didn't get a ticket. After walking for about 10 minutes straight, I ran into 3 different trails. I explored 2 of them and the scenery was amazing. You get to enjoy the mountain view on one side and the ocean in the other. It's definitely a workout. I thoroughly enjoyed it and will definitely be back to explore it further.

It was kind of confusing on where to start and where to park. We found a parking lot that was $6 a car (which I thought was reasonable). And then we just started walking. There are a lot of different trails to go on and a lot of steep paths, as well. We walked by the beach that was full of rocks of all shapes and sizes. They had such cool markings on them! I guess depending on which trails you take, depends on how difficult the hike is. If you’re in the area and are into hiking.. check this place out!

Wonderful system of hiking trails with an seaside view. Wouldn’t call this an easy hike by any means as there are plenty of inclined stretches, including a glute intensive set of stairs. But definitely a beautiful area with plenty to explore. Walk through the nearby neighborhood too if you want to see some cool homes. We ended up stumbling upon an estate sale!

Parking - you can do street parking for free alongside the road on Crenshaw Blvd, north of Crest Rd near St. John Fisher church. The designated parking requires advanced reservation and is $10.75 for 2-3 hrs slot, so may not be worth it. The street parking May add 10 min walk before you enter Portuguese bend reserve but worth it.

There are so many trails inside but marked well at the crossings. You can keep walking as much as you can and just follow trails on google maps (zoom in to see trail names) to guide you back. I took the Burma road trail and came back on Ishibashi trail making it a 4.3 mile hike total. Burma is wide and ishibashi is bushy and narrow but beautiful with all the flowers. Great views of PV covered in evening fog and we saw lot of hares on the trail.

name: Stoney Point Park
formatted_address: On Topanga Canyon Blvd, between Chatsworth St &, 79FX+W9, CA-118, Chatsworth, CA 91311, USA

google_reviews.text: This is an excellent place to practice rock climbing with safety and pads! I was able to walk on the trail with fall safety gear on and feel safe. I’ve seen others walk to more complicated trails and have a great time. Please be safe and wise!

Truly enjoyed my day of adventure at Stoney Point Park. The climate was perfect for this kind of calm and not so much of a crowded hike. I started off on a 2 hour walk up to the park by way of Canoga Avenue. Spotted the hike entrance right adjacent to the 118 freeway. It is true...as you begin the hike the trail is wide. As you near the decent area, where you'll see a lot more of the huge boulders, narrow paths will appear. The view is expansive and you will appreciate the scenery of all the Valley! Great residential area surrounding the park as well. Highly recommend this location for a peaceful hike!👍🥾🧗

Got some hiking trails and rock climbing here. Lots of classic bouldering routes and a good share of top roping and trad. Check mountain project for all the route details. Some routes are a bit hard to get to.

Fun place to go rock climbing or rappelling. It was my first time there so we only tried the tip of the iceberg. It helps if you can go with someone who knows the various routes and climbing spots or do that research in advance.  We took our kids rappelling down a wall while 2 guys next to us did a 5.10 climb right next to us.

Not super busy while we were there but the remaining chalk marks on handhold on the rocks suggest this is a well known and visited place!

We couldn't find any restrooms so one star off for that. Looking forward to going back!

Nice and quiet place to really enjoy your horse  they feed them well  and keep the stalls nice and clean .there is good round pen and diferent turnout  arenas. Good prices  as well and they offer ridden lessons

name: Trippet Ranch
formatted_address: 20828 Entrada Rd, Topanga, CA 90290, USA

google_reviews.text: Lots of parking space with $3 for an hour , $10 for full day car parking(free for annual state parks holders). Restrooms and picnic benches are available. Did Musch trail to Eagle rock to parking which is a loop trail. I suggest you to do it clockwise. It’s greenery with splendid views all over. And also somewhat muddy due to recent rains, suggest to wear hiking / waterproof shoes. Some parts of the trail is overgrown with bees and flies. Came across Musch camp on my way to the rock. So many bicyclists on the     path.

Update: the main parking lot is closed with the reopening planned for 9/2025

Great place to hike. Parking is pay per day. From the parking lot, you can head North on the paved road to walk past the reservoir and connect with many dirt roads to the hills to the East and West. You can also take the fire road to the Trippet Ranch house and then on into the hills where you can eventually hike to Will Rodgers State Park. Either way there are always lots of flowers, birds, reptiles, squirrels, rabbits, and sometimes deer. I saw a bob cat once.

It's a proper spring with rolling hillsides of lush green, tall grass. There are lots of streams if you take the Musch trail. It's a good option to take because otherwise you will never see them ebb and flow after the rainy season is over.

Great location and trails for all types of hiker: easy to expert, short and multi mile trails. Get there early as parking is limited; pay to park in the lot at the vase of most trailheads or find street parking for free. There are trails along the street that will take to the main lot as well. Highly recommend!!

This is a great starting point to some of the Topanga canyon trails. Eagle rock is just a couple miles (uphill) from the parking lot. Musch trail is very scenic and pretty. Except for a very small portion of Mush trial, most of the trails here are along rigdlines so it means there is basically no tree cover, in the middle of the day you will be directly exposed to a lot of sun, so wear lots of sunblock and bring a hat. If you get here early there is usually very little people on the trails. There is a parking fee for parking at the trail site, its like $8 maybe. Many people try to find parking in the neighborhood around the trail site and just walk in so they don't' have to pay. I think its worth the small parking fee, the restrooms are really well kept and it helps support the parks programs. They used to only take cash but now they have a parking machine that takes card so its pretty convenient.

name: Zuma Canyon Trailhead
formatted_address: 5721 Bonsall Dr, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Zuma Canyon is an easy to moderate trail, depending upon which trail you choose. The main trail is nearly flat, heading alongside a usually dry stream bed. A ocean view trail to the right heads up steeply toward Kanan Road, with spectacular ocean views of Zuma Beach. The main trail continues up the canyon, where it connects with the Zuma Ridge trail to the left. This is a moderate hike, with some climbs and nice views of the canyon. If you continue straight, the trail moves further into the canyon, where the stream appears, requiring several stream crossings, before ending right before Zuma Dam. Zuma Canyon is a very nice set of trails with something for everyone and a very large (free) parking lot. Dogs and horses are allowed, but no bicycles.

It was nothing but a dead end. We couldn't cross the river so had to back track. Not fun. Won't go there again. It's beautiful for the walk. But my gf. Git stressed cause we had to walk back the way we came
I took the Ocean View Trail and while it was quite challenging, the views were worth the effort to reach the summit.

We got here very early and we had the trails all to ourselves. We started left on Zuma Loop trail, unfortunately there were too many horse dropping and it smelled very bad. When we were finally half way through the hike, the odor went away.  So we looped around merge into Canyon View Trail and then into Ocean View Trail. It was a 4.5 mile loop around. We had the best views. When you start hiking on Canyon Trail it gets a little steep. Prepare yourself in going up and dealing with a few inclines. Overall a beautiful place to hike and you get to enjoy the ocean views.

I climbed on the Ocean View trail and looped back on the Canyon View trail. Very nice hike, not difficult at all. Lots of spring flowers along the trail. Bring plenty of water as it gets hot and dusty!

name: Tuna Canyon Park
formatted_address: Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: The road is a bit narrow but it has great views throughout the journey. It has nice stop points to enjoy the views. Would recommend if you like the roadtrips and do a quick one around a mountain.

Great Feel at Malibu as so close to the sky, the sea and almost parallel as the clouds. So grateful to see the sunset at summer time. Many dogs and their owners are passing here. Interesting as the places are back gardens for the local neighborhood.

My daughter and I came out here since my daughter will be going back to Tennessee in a few days I wanted to bring the dogs and walk them it's such a beautiful Wednesday morning and we absolutely love this place even though this is the first time that we have come to visit

May 26/2023
It had a lot of flowers due to rain season, looked very good!
We saw a lot of bugs & lizards, we even saw a big SNAKE!(Watch the video)
It has very rewarding views no matter what path you take!
I recommend to take a left after the white gate then another left up.
There was this weird formation of rocks at the top,
Parking is very limited so go early!
Its a difficult drive up small roads in a residential area.

The biggest problem with it is it’s an awful pain in the butt to get to. The road there is straight up and incredibly windy. I parked next to a fire access road on Tuna Canyon and took that to the trail. There are trails running all over the place and there are absolutely no trail signs anywhere. I had to rely completely on google maps. The views are spectacular and there are very few people on the trail most of the time. It’s dog friendly and I took the pup. There are quite a few significant hills but they aren’t so bad that you can’t handle them. This is one of the SoCal hikes you can go on and actually feel like you’re in nature.

There are tons of flies for some reason. They really are all over the place. A lot of lizards and I did see a rabbit. Didn’t see any mosquitoes but I was there during the day.

name: Filiorum Reserve
formatted_address: Pacifica Dr, Rolling Hills Estates, CA 90274, USA

google_reviews.text: Very good 👍 5 stars my suggestion is if you come at sundown there might be mosquitos bring OFF / bug spray. & there's ticks !!!!! I got bit after I got home it was huge.  Its teeth was in my bone in my shin. Don't walk by bushes the wait and stick on you then bite can cause alot of problems years later.

Fun hike! Only did about 1 mile but the loop is longer. Will want to come back  again. Nice sights and very quiet. Hear alot of rustling from animals and birds chirping. I would  say its moderate level hike
Fun and quiet walking trail.Having an overlooking pacific ocean view is additional bonus. During springtime, there are lots of snails on the ground.Do be careful not to step on them.
This is so nice place for hiking. i met lots of hiker in the morning of saturday.
This hike has a beautiful ocean view and is a great workout.

name: Cave of Munits
formatted_address: 24501 Vanowen St, West Hills, CA 91307, USA

google_reviews.text: We came to hike on a Saturday afternoon. Free street parking available near the Trailhead. The hike to the bottom of the cave was easy, probably took 45 min. The climb to the cave was difficult. You don't necessarily need gloves but your definitely need some anti-slip hiking shoes. You will need to use both hands to hold on to the rope and lift your body up. There weren't too many people in the afternoon and there's plenty of shades by that time of the day, so I'd recommend doing an afternoon hike here. You should be able to finish the hike in 2 hours.

Brought gloves thanks to one of the reviews, and so glad we did. With sheer willpower and a lot of help from the rope, we got to the top. It's more difficult going down the rope than it was to climb up, so be aware of that. Bring water and hiking shoes with good grip. This is the view from within the cave. Nature is beautiful. #leavenotrace
Great place for a walk. The hike is not difficult, but it can be a bit hot in the summer. In February, there was a wasp nest in the cave at the top, so be careful!

It was one of the nicest hikes I ever done especially at night went with two friends and they also like it too.. I definitely recommend it going to it at night and bring a flashlight with u and u would enjoy the view from the top..

Getting to the caves is moderate difficulty. The rest of the hike is insane. There is a metal rope to help you climb up the steep mountains but if it’s hot outside your hands will burn. Was not expecting this hike to be so intense. I want to try it again but it’s more than an effort

name: Summit Valley Edmund D. Edelman Park
formatted_address: Topanga, CA 90290, USA

google_reviews.text: I love this trail all year round, but especially when winter turns to spring and the creek is flowing and all the native plants are in bloom. It’s an easy hike with little gems along the way — such as tree swings in several of the ancient oaks, a bridge to travel across Topanga Creek (loads of frogs sing during rainy season), a favorite Buddha where you can leave a little offering, a labyrinth made of small stones tended by locals, and a 100 year old car and stove that tumbled down the creek and rusted into the landscape. Note that there are often horses and/or children on this trail. Please use a doggie bag for poop if you bring a dog — it’s a narrow, single track trail. If you’re with a friend, stop at a viewpoint or a tree swing for conversation and a snack.

This is a fantastic area with convenient access. You can easily pull off Topanga Canyon and find parking, which costs $5.

The trail was populated with hikers and mountain bikers, yet it didn't feel overcrowded. The panoramic views were spectacular, and there was a rich variety of birds to watch and numerous types of wildflowers. Fortunately, there was no poison oak, which was a relief. There was plenty of black mustard along the trail to snack on.

Overall, this is an excellent trail, and I'll certainly visit again.

underrated hiking spot! you get to see the beauty of nature and the view is impeccable! just keep in mind that the hike is steep but when you reach the top it is amazing! you get a view of the city to the right and the mountains to the left!
Peaceful nature reserve with rolling hills. Trails are easy and great for beginners. Not very crowded, even on weekends. Parking is free. Feels like a hidden escape.

Absolutely amazing hike! Though it is a little crowded by normal trail standards, there are plenty of trails. I would highly recommend doing the Eagle Rock loop (roughly 3.75 miles) for an incredible view. Make sure you bring water and a camera! There is a more direct route to Eagle Rock which is only 1.2mi each way if needed.

name: Calabasas Peak Trailhead
formatted_address: 1698 Stunt Rd, Calabasas, CA 91302, USA

google_reviews.text: Great cardiovascular workout on a pleasant  day, but avoid it when hot as it’s all uphill and mostly exposed with minimal shade. There’s also 3 different trailheads to Peak so choose wisely. Stunt Rd is probably the best bet but also accessible from Red Rock Canyon.

Pretty cool little hike, not much to see at the top. Mostly would recommend it for the mountain bikers or cyclists, but not much pay off for a hiker. It was pretty easy with some steep parts but nothing out of the ordinary. Be wary as sandstone paths can be slippery.  I saw a few hikers slip including myself.

Also there are some harmless gopher snakes, spiders, lizards, and butterflies. Some horse flies as well.

Regular route seems pretty straightforward, if by chance you ever wonder about climbing up the rock formations far left,  shown on map, don’t do it. It’s ridiculously stupid and possible injury or death. We just couldn’t resist. The middle section is much better and easy. It will connect back to main fire road.
Great for road trail with amazing views. About 1100ish get off elevation change over 1.75 miles. During the summer months I would recommend getting there early to beat the heat.

Nice walking park, not just a dog park.  Also, easy access to Kobe Bryant’s  Memorial site.  Easy to get to, just park at the Dog park.  There's a trail that leads up the mountain.  The walk is all uphill.  Memorial site approx 1.2miles up the hill.  From there you can continue another 2.2mi or take the longer 7mi trail.  We did the 2.2mile.  Total of 6miles.  Again nice trail to walk.. not very busy either.

name: Glendale Peak
formatted_address: Los Angeles, CA 90027, USA

google_reviews.text: This prak is just an alright peak, it has some nice views, and is accessible from the Hogback Trail by a large sign pointing to a trail. It just has a benchmark that marks the peak.

I used Google to find this trail and it was kind of confusing. There’s no parking area so we parked on the street. It was a paved path that branches off into other paths. We took a dirt path so we could get some shade but the eventual decline was extremely steep so we turned around. Nice views

This has become my favorite hike in Griffith Park. Park at the “ponies”, cross Riverside Drive, and take the hard way up (trust me, the views are worth it!) Spectacular vistas, especially around sunset time.

Glendale Peak is a really fun hike with great vistas overlooking the city. Grange Stables will be the starting point for this rewarding climb. All abilities can take it on, with both shade and open areas. Good family-friendly and dog-friendly views are seen from the peak, while parking is free.

Less crowded than other Griffith Park hikes, this one offers amazing city views with fewer people. It’s a bit steep in parts but manageable for most fitness levels. There’s also a bench at the top where you can rest and enjoy the skyline. You pass through some shaded groves too. Bring water—there’s no fountain nearby.

name: La Tuna Canyon Park
formatted_address: 8000 La Tuna Canyon Rd, Sun Valley, CA 91352, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a decent hiking area with close proximity to LA. I stuck with the La Tuna Canyon Loop but there’s lots of trail options all around. Watch out for some steep sections and the recent rains eroded some sections - sticking with the main road is probably the best option until repairs are done. There were a moderate number of other hikers and mountain bikers. Seems like a popular spot for the nearby neighborhoods. Lots of dogs!

I really like la tuna canyon. It has terrific views (once u reach the top) and different styles of paths you can take depending on your style of hiking or walking. I use this area for mountain biking and training for Spartan races.

If you're into hiking, whether you are a beginner or an Intermediate hiker you will love this trail. It's a steay climb to the top by the towers. You can hike to Burbank on the right or hike to Glendale to the left and end up in Brand Park.

I did an easy mountain bike ride up the La Tuna Canyon fire road. Mid week it was farley deserted. From the parking lot to the top was a little over 3.5 miles and 1700 feet of climbing. So not an easy walk. Lots of views into La Canada and from the top down into Burbank.

one of the best outdoor places in the valley! Amazing scenic views of Glendale, Burbank, Down Town LA, and even the ocean on a clean sky. Around 1300 ft elevation and 3 miles to the top (if you start off the 210 Freeway). Great for mountain bike training and keep your heart beat UP! (no need for full suspension or super grippy tires).

name: Nancy Hoover Pohl Overlook at Fryman Canyon
formatted_address: 8401 Mulholland Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90046, USA

google_reviews.text: Great hike and most shaded. Dog friendly and easy to moderate depending on the route you take.
The Nancy Hoover Pohl Overlook Park is a great place to stop for a view or a hike on Mulholland Drive. There is a parking lot for easy access and parking.
Such a beautiful spot to be at night very romantic or even just alone time for your self the view is amazing

When driving magical Mulholland drive, there's a number of small overlooks where you can pull off and look out over the ravine, or begin hiking through the Laurel canyon and Hollywood Hills area. Gorgeous views. Slightly mystical. Watch out for rattlesnakes!
Easy access from Mulholland with parking. Dog friendly. Dirt trail, not carriage trails. Pretty vistas. The trail seemed to get more torn up the lower you went.

name: King Gillette Ranch, Mountains Recreation & Conservation Authority
formatted_address: 26800 Mulholland Hwy, Calabasas, CA 91302, USA

google_reviews.text: The Night of The Jack event was a good time with the family! I'm a wheelchair user/paraplegic so getting around the the parking lot and main concessions area was a little challenging with the rocks and gravel. Otherwise the rest of the layout and event was enjoyable! Our 2 year old had a great time.

We bought tickets for "Night of the Jake" and  had a great time. They had food trucks drinks and two bars. The light show was fantastic. Will do this again.  I heard that they have a Christmas event also, can't wait to go to one.

This review is only for the Christmas lights festivities. Unfortunately,  this was a big let down. The majority of the displays looked terrible and cheap and not much to it. We pick a new place every year and this was by far the most underwhelming installation of all.  There were a few nice installations,  but not worth the drive nor price.

Night of the Jack is pure Halloween magic! The trail at King Gillette Ranch in Calabasas is beautifully lit with thousands of hand-carved pumpkins, creative themed displays, and a fun, family-friendly atmosphere. From the glowing jack-o’-lantern art to the spooky music and food trucks, every detail feels immersive and festive. Perfect for kids and adults alike — definitely a must-see fall experience in SoCal! 👻

Went for their “holiday Road“ event and it was spectacular. Everything under the sun when it comes to food trucks, snacks, and the Christmas spirit within an amazing trail of lights to walk through with your honey or your family.

name: Junipero Beach
formatted_address: Junipero Beach, 1 Junipero Ave, Long Beach, CA 90803, USA

google_reviews.text:
A wonderful place for a sunset walk.
The golden light fills everything around, and the ocean view feels absolutely unreal. The atmosphere is very romantic and somehow reminiscent of Florence at sunset. Highly recommended to visit.

Junipero Beach is a beautiful and relaxing spot with wide sandy shores and stunning ocean views. It’s clean, well-kept, and perfect for a peaceful walk, bike ride, or just sitting back to enjoy the breeze. The beach has convenient access points, plenty of space to spread out, and a family-friendly atmosphere. I especially loved how calm and welcoming it felt compared to busier LA beaches. Parking is very convenient. A lovely place to unwind and enjoy the California coast.

Beautiful beach with wide sandy areas and great views of the ocean. It’s usually less crowded than other spots, which makes it perfect for a peaceful walk or just relaxing. Clean and well-kept, with plenty of space to enjoy the water or just hang out. A nice hidden gem in Long Beach! 🌊☀️

Kind of an average beach. There are some nice amenities and it’s large, but it’s also pretty dirty with a lot of trash and debris laying around. It looks like the city has partially renovated it with some features looking very new and others practically falling apart.

The offshore drilling islands with artificial decorations are amusing to see.

Junipero Beach is a fantastic spot for a relaxing day by the sea. The long, spacious beach is perfect for sunbathing, and there's a great playground for kids. It also features well-maintained cycling and running trails with stunning coastal views. The peaceful atmosphere is ideal for unwinding, and the pier offers a nice spot for fishing or a scenic walk. Overall, Junipero Beach is a gem with something for everyone, whether you're there for family fun, exercise, or some peace and quiet.

name: Point Dume State Beach
formatted_address: Point Dume State Beach, 6800 Westward Beach Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Gorgeous views especially from the top of the hiking trail.
