Come March and early April you will see the sea of flowers on the trail, overseeing the blue ocean while the seagulls flying in a line overhead. Yes I know it’s a blessing living only twenty five minutes away by driving. In summer it’s always busy in the day but if you come in just to watch the sunset you will have a lot of room. Parking after 4:00pm is a bit cheaper than during the day. We had lots of great memories here. Highly recommended for all who enjoy the beach. Sorry I don’t think dogs are allowed.

Quiet and clean beach. You can relax here with less noise around. We were there on Sunday morning till 3 pm and it was not too much crowded. Clean restrooms and active lifeguards. Plenty of parking for paid parking lot.
What a sparkling clean beach! It was generally empty on a Friday. A bit chilly the day I went but not cold. Seems like a perfect place to take photos since there aren't a lot of people.

This is probably one of my most favorite places on planet earth. I made it a point to come back here and visit when I was in LA and I havent been here since 2017. It’s so magical. The view is incredible. MUST SEE while in LA.

I had such an amazing time at Point Dume! The location is absolutely stunning, with beautiful, clean water that’s perfect for a refreshing swim or just relaxing on the shore. It’s a fantastic spot for families too—our kids had a blast! While parking can be a bit challenging, if you arrive early enough, there’s public parking available that makes it manageable. What I loved most is how peaceful it felt compared to the busier beaches in LA. We saw an Orca happily swimming in the water. It’s the perfect escape if you’re looking for a more tranquil beach day. Highly recommend this spot, 100%!

name: Torrance County Beach
formatted_address: Torrance County Beach, 289 Paseo De La Playa, Torrance, CA 90505, USA

google_reviews.text: Torrance Beach is one of the best hidden gems in the South Bay! Unlike other crowded beaches, this spot feels peaceful and offers breathtaking ocean views, perfect for a relaxing stroll or a photoshoot. The soft golden light at sunset creates a magical atmosphere, making it an ideal location for capturing warm and natural family moments. I recently did a family session here, and the backdrop was absolutely stunning! Sharing one of my favorite shots from that day

Nice atmosphere here--people walking, running, playing volleyball, but it also was not crowded at all. Very nice views of coastline and blue water.  Calm and relaxing.  It wasn't warm emough for me to get into the water though it was pleasant walking on the sand.

Good beach to bring the kids for a swim. Weekends it gets a bit busy but that is to be expected. Parking on the streets or in the parking lot near Merrimac park entrance for $7.

Beach was rather clean. Volleyball nets in front of the beach homes at the top of the hill. Nice view and the ride was pretty nice the day I came, on Sunday. Lifeguards throughout and recycling and trash cans everywhere. Luckily most people kept their area clean so its perhaps one of the cleanest beaches I've been to.

Great place to come back again throughout the summer. It does get busy with folks with boards  so you kind of have to be careful when they find a good spot with some waves to ride, no matter how small they may be. Had a good time with the family.

I went to visit my sister today and just typed in beaches near me and this beach popped up. This beach is out of a movie it felt so right being there supper romantic for date night. I parked my car and just took in the breeze there were a few people out just relaxing I loved it

Nice beach, well maintained. Saw a lifeguard make someone pack up and leave cuz they had their dog with them, so be aware that no dogs are allowed on the beach. Other than that it was good. $6 for parking in the lot.

name: Topanga Beach
formatted_address: Topanga Beach, Topanga, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: My favorite place to playin the tide pools and football w my dog and watch surfers. Remote-ish w mostly locals and more surfers than spectators. Lifeguards leave at golden hour so know ure on ur own out there after that! Be sure to use buddy system. I always stay until last surfer comes in but im not always there either.

The beach is great with a lot of street parking (typical for LA). The walk to the beach was stinking and I couldn't find any restroom nearby which was surprising for me. Noticed may surfers here as the waves were kind of supportive. They do have handicapped parking available.

Nice beach, almost always easy to park. There are paid and free parking as well.
Perfect place for surfing, because you can see good waves almost every day. But there is no rental surf shops, so better Google it where to rent before heading to the beach. If you need it, of course. Even at 6 am you can see somebody surfing already.
Usually families love spending time on the beach as well. But bring snacks and drinks with you, closest groceries like 5 min drive. But fish restaurant across the road is pretty good.
During winter sunsets are magical. Love it!

Beautiful spot right off the road to watch the sunset and the surfers. There’s a parking lot by the beach, but if that’s full, there’s also a lot on the other side of the road. There’s a pedestrian underpass that lets you cross the highway - please take that rather than running across a busy road. It’s faster and safer. There are also bathrooms nearby.
Such a good place in LA! A lot of surfers here! The waves are not very strong, so very suitable for fun surfing. I would really want to have a surf board since I came here. Will come back again!

name: Las Tunas Beach
formatted_address: 19444 Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Went from the mountains to the sea this Sunday. And saw the fire destruction on PCH so sad. But what a view now we all can enjoy. So no houses back along coast please.

This is a bias review simply because I love the beach. I especially loved the little turnout patch of sand I was able to park to enjoy the sunshine, ocean sounds and scenic views. It was peaceful people were friendly and just like I enjoying the waves crashing into the rocks below. The spot isn't that large and that turnout is free parking its literally along the shoreline just keep and eye out for it. If you find this little gem give me a thumbs up. I literally fell asleep in my car unbothered listening to the waves and sea life, oh can't forget the sounds of foreign muscle and import cars occasionally ripping down pch but who am i kidding i loved it all.😁👍🏼
Water was a study of blues and green. Great place to center in on thoughts, especially after a Santa Monica mountain hike. I do so love this city.

This is a great beach in the coastal city of Malibu and summer is a great time to visit as it gives you a respite from the heat because of the close proximity to the Pacific Ocean. It is not too far from Santa Monica and you can plan on covering both on the same day.
It's a nice stop to breathe once and awhile, also a warning to others with loose pockets, be careful cause your things will fall into a hole in the rocks. Bad for you and the environment.

name: Corral State Beach
formatted_address: Corral State Beach, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text:
Stopped somewhere between here and Solstice Canyon. Lots of spots to pull out onto along the eastbound direction.

Late January 2026, California bush sunflowers were blooming. It was lovely--probably even better at sunset.

Lovely little hidden beach with amazing sunsets! Only street parking is available, and it can be hard to find a spot on weekends. Be aware that you may get fined for bringing dogs if the state beach rangers are so inclined.

This is actually a nice beach you can spend time with your family and friends along PCH. It is not crowded and not as popular as other beaches. You can get food and drinks from Malibu Seafood which is across the street and eat here. This place is across a hiking trail as well so you can two best of both worlds. Will surely come back.
Corral State Beach was amazing! My family and I had a relaxing time! The beach was clean, beautiful, and peaceful. I would recommend you visit this beach!
Good spot to snap some sunset photos.

name: Dan Blocker Beach Malibu
formatted_address: 26000 Pacific Coast Hwy, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text:
Dan Blocker Beach is one of Malibu’s quietest ocean-view spots — perfect if you want the coastline without the crowds.
There’s plenty of parking, clean restrooms, and a pay station right at the entrance. While this spot doesn’t have direct beach access (important to know), the clifftop overlook is absolutely beautiful — clear views down the coast, peaceful landscaping, and several sturdy picnic tables that are great for a snack break or just enjoying the ocean breeze.

It’s a really nice stop for anyone driving the PCH who wants an easy pull-in with facilities, open space, and a place to take in that classic Malibu blue water. A very underrated viewpoint along the coast!

Great little stop on our way back into Santa Monica!  Porta John's is available as the restroom facility is closed.  Nice view of the ocean and plenty of seating areas.  Benches and picnics tables for all to enjoy.  Our pups loved it!

Great beach with amazing views. Carefully when walking on the beach, some people leave their class bottles. Bring lunch and have a seat on the sand. Small waves, not too crowded, and beautiful sunsets and sunrises. Come on down!
Had a great time this place is very beautiful, I loved it, the air was clean you can really enjoy the fresh , cool air, the water was so cold at first. Then you get used to it, nice place to visit.
It's been my spot for 30 years.Easy parking easy beach access

name: Little Dume Beach
formatted_address: 29208 Cliffside Dr, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: I got access to this fabulous beach from Point Dume Natural Preserve, it's a little bit of a challenge to go down but it's absolutely worth it, especially for surfers, it's less crowded when other beach are busy , if you are more of beach walking, head out on this for about 2.7 miles and you are at Paradise Cove beach,

This is the most fascinating, intimate beach I've ever been to. It is private access, and I went with a friend, Arnie, who lives in the neighborhood above the cliffs, as well as with Nick, the friend I was staying with in Culver City. Not crowded at all. Only about 25 people on the whole beach, since the access is private. As you can see from the entry pic, you ain't getting in from the land access. You'll have to walk on the beach from a public access to get to use it. It was my first experience with the Pacific ocean, as well as my first time in California. I lost my footing once I got waist deep and the surf rolled me back up onto the beach like a fat, wet burrito. Even with that, the 20 minutes i spent on this beach will always be one of my fondest memories.

On Friday, we had originally been planning to do a hike, but two-thirds of our party was extremely exhausted from activities earlier in the week. It was a shame since I’d been looking forward to doing the dawn mine hike. So, instead, we went to Malibu and relaxed on the beach for a few hours. This wound up being a great way to unwind after such an incredibly packed week. The waters were quite cold, that day, and the waves were remarkably strong--strong enough that I didn’t go in, despite how much I’d enjoyed my time in the ocean on Tuesday. Instead, I just sat on a towel in the sand with my friends for a few hours, enjoying one of our last few days in Los Angeles.

We were going to split off into pairs, that night, so we left the beach early to get ready for our planned evenings. I didn’t get to experience sunset or evening at this beach, but I can imagine that it would have been absolutely mesmerizing. I hope to go and see it again during the golden hours, someday.
If you can find your way to Little Dume Beach you will be in Shangrala. LD is secluded only open during low tide and a surfers delight.
Great beach to use if you've got the motivation to hike the three miles from the public access point. Johnny Carson's house is right there up the steep cliff. 1985 - 2005 (his passing).

Wonderful area to walk and enjoy the Seabreeze

There's a big field for kids to run and play in, shade for parents and two excellent playgrounds as well as workout equipment and sand to play in. You probably wouldn't want to run around in the sand barefoot (there seems to be bits of sticks and stuff that may hurt), plus there's a few ants around.  Not tons, but noticeable enough, so I wouldn't let the kiddos run around barefoot.

name: Bixby Park
formatted_address: 130 Cherry Ave, Long Beach, CA 90802, USA

google_reviews.text:
3 mins walk to Bluff park, Jogging and walking dogs.

Stretching along the bluff above the shoreline, Bluff Park offers one of the best panoramic ocean views in Long Beach.

This linear park is perfect for jogging, walking your dog, or simply sitting on a bench to watch the sunset paint the horizon. With its historic lampposts, gentle sea breezes, and a peaceful, uncluttered vibe, Bluff Park is the ideal spot for quiet reflection—or for gazing at passing sailboats and dolphins in the distance.

However I guess my soul does not connect here. It’s connected more with Venice, Malibu, and Laguna.

Nice though.

This park is amazing. It’s all cute cuz it’s by the beach. There’s a farmers market here sometimes.  Depends what day it is, so either Tuesday or Saturday.  I think.  I am not sure, you can use your google app to figure that out.  Just know that it’s cute.  Okay.  Enjoy.

Great dog park plenty of space for your doggies to run around there's a small dog area and a large dog area. Other than that the park is huge there's plenty of grass and plenty of place to walk around. It's probably the largest park in Long Beach. If you need to get away from work or blow off some steam come down here and take a walk through the park It's beautiful. Close enough to the ocean to smell the sea breeze.

Bixby Park is a true Long Beach gem with ocean breezes, open green space, and amazing views. It’s perfect for walking, jogging, picnics, or just sitting and relaxing near the water. The park feels lively but calm, with plenty of space for families, kids, and people bringing their dogs.

I really appreciate how clean and well-maintained the grounds are, and there’s always something happening—from yoga and community events to casual hangouts. It’s an easy place to unwind, get fresh air, and enjoy the neighborhood vibe. Whether you’re a local or visiting, Bixby Park is absolutely worth the stop.
One of the best neighborhood parks around. And they have cleaned it up immensely with cameras. It is a safer place

name: Mason Park

google_reviews.text: This park is fine I guess, but it's not as good as other parks. There have been multiple times that items on the playground are taped off because they're broken. I've seen one thing get fixed, but the bounce horses have have signs and chains covering them since I moved here. The swings are, for some strange reason, over sand and fully in the sun with no shade. I've also had my toddler fall fully out of the bucket swing, which only has a chain on the front instead of a fully closed seat. Upsides are that there's a splash pad which is usually open and running during summer months. The last equipment (that's not broken) includes separate areas for younger kids and older kids. There's huge wide open green spaces surrounding the playground.

Mason Park is a clean, spacious, and family-friendly park that’s perfect for a day outdoors. The playground is well-kept and safe, making it a great spot for kids to enjoy. There are picnic areas with plenty of shade, so it’s easy to set up for gatherings or just a relaxing afternoon.
The restrooms are available and decently maintained, which is always a plus. The park is also dog-friendly, with lots of open space and walking paths that make it enjoyable for pets and their owners.

name: North Atwater Park
formatted_address: 3900 Chevy Chase Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90039, USA

google_reviews.text:
🌿 North Atwater Park & LA River Trail (Atwater Village) Peaceful Urban Nature Walk with Iconic Bridge Views

North Atwater Park is a calm, underrated green space along the LA River that’s perfect for a relaxed walk, light exercise, or sunset stroll especially if you’re in Atwater Village or nearby Glendale.

The park connects directly to the LA River Trail, offering flat, easy paths suitable for walking, jogging, or cycling. The scenery is a mix of urban nature and infrastructure, with open views of the river, surrounding hills, and the modern pedestrian bridge that looks great at golden hour and dusk. It’s quiet compared to busier LA parks, which makes it ideal if you’re looking for some breathing room without leaving the city.

There are clear signs, restrooms nearby, and open grassy areas, plus a sports field and shaded sections. The educational signage about water quality and river restoration adds an interesting layer if you like learning while you walk. Just note the posted warnings about river water quality stick to the paths and enjoy the views.

Best time to visit is late afternoon or sunset for cooler temperatures and beautiful light. Overall, this is a simple, peaceful park that’s great for locals, photographers, and anyone wanting an easy nature break in LA.

Small recreation park with a baseball field, a basketball court, a playground and a picnic area. It's next to the La river bike path. Restrooms onsite.
The location isn't central and the park doesn't look like a popular one. When I passed the park in the morning, only a couple of homeless people were roaming around.
A gorgeous backdrop while playing basketball. We enjoyed our time tremendously and there was a playground for my son to play at while we hooped.

A little park adjacent to the LA River. You can walk near the river and see a wide variety of birds: stilts, great blue herons, black-crowned night herons, and kingfishers. The current state of the river is sad, but the wildlife is a testament to the resilience of nature, even in a big dirty city like LA.
Nice little Park, and it's adjacent to the LA River walking path and the new North Atwater pedestrian bridge. Is an equestrian path also so watch your step :-)

name: Los Cerritos Park

Skatepark review-

The fact that this little community park nestled away between rows of houses even has a skatepark is a huge plus. It's not much but it's well made and covers most of the basics.

The bank is a perfect angle and the tiny quarter pipe is fun for beginners and old farts alike. The flat ledge is a little tall for me but the flatbar is perfect.

#1000skateparkchallenge #cementurians

3/25/25 Unfortunate that city parks & rec has allowed this once lovely little park to become unusable for the public. Go visit or just stop by to see what I mean.

3/6/24 There's no longer an encampment on Selma Ave in front of the school. Yah. But Selma Park.... the homeless hang out in the park

Update 1/23/24 Parents tell me tents pop up on Selma every now & then and they report it to lapd and they're promptly moved elsewhere. Unfortunately the park continues to be a mess and residents are afraid to use the park. Efforts to save the park haven't gotten far. CD13 continues to allow encampments to ruin our neighborhoods & businesses.

10/11/23 Encampment hasn't returned but efforts need to be made to "save the park"

8/22 the mayors Inside Safe program was used here and this encampment has been removed and Lapd Hollywood division is enforcing an ordinance the prohibits encampments from along this stretch of Selma Ave. from the park at Schrader to Cherokee Ave.

7/18/23: Last 4 photos are most recent, took them 7/16. The encampment has doubled in size and surely impacts the nearby businesses and the school when students return from summer break in August.

7/16/23This park is even worse than it was when I first posted photos here. I would consider it a DANGEROUS park to visit because of the growing encampment here. I will be contacting LAParks & our council district. It has been promised the encampment will be removed by 8/1 when students return to the school nextdoor, Larchmont Charter & Selma Elementary.

----------------
Beautiful little pocket park & childrens playground, not sure if neighbors go there now with the growing encampment on the sidewalk next to the park. Sidewalk there is totally blocked.  Hopefully our city council will begin working on this issue soon. Encampments been on the news lately since there's a school next door to the park. Sidewalk is blocked with tents & students have to walk in the street to get past the encampment. Now LAPD is at the gate to provide extra security during morning drop offs & afternoon pickups after a parent was attacked by someone from the encampment.

Unfortunately, this area is moderately unsafe. The city built a very tall wrought iron fence around the park, which means there is only one entrance/exit to the area. There are many unhoused people living in the park. This is not uncommon in LA and usually doesn't bother me much, but I was far outnumbered and wasn't comfortable with the fact that there was only one exit that could be easily blocked by a person. However, the park itself is very shady and beautiful. There is a smaller area in the back with a children's playground and three cement chess tables. This park is also right behind the touristy area of Hollywood, and is easily walkable from the Hollywood/Highland stop on the Red Line train.

The homeless community has taken over and destroyed this park. Pass by any hour during daytime or daylight hours and you will see nearly every person inside the park smoking or injecting some form of street drug and it's shocking seeing the police drive by, watch it occur, and do absolutely nothing to clean up this sad pandemic ESPECIALLY with the park being next door to an elementary school! The children can't even use this park. Our tax dollars hard at work, huh!

The playground iscovered in tags, broken glass, and needles. No children come here, only adults looking for a place to hang during the day. As the guy who encouraged us to leave described it: “this place is ghetto af”

I love this park. It is a haven for the Homeless. I love being able to go there and relax on the days I have nothing to do.  Used to be a lot of drama, but now the homeless are working together to make it safer. Trying to make it clear that we aren't dangerous. Most of us over there have kids of our own, we just cant see them.

name: Hermon Park
formatted_address: 5566 Via Marisol, Los Angeles, CA 90042, USA

google_reviews.text: Love this place and the dog park is amazing. Definitely recommend! All the dogs are still nice 😊

Clean park with a very large open field, swimming pool, tennis courts, baseball court , top of the hill where the gym reside, restroom next to kids Play ground and plenty of parking areas. Good walking around park.

Beautiful space, and cool venue for large shows. Unfortunately, large shows mean lots and lots of the general public. The general public don't know how to behave themselves when out in these spaces. They drink too much, and don't take care of themselves or anyone around them.
Are we in LA ? No way, it can't be!! As if you are in Indonesian jungle or south American forest. I sat down and enjoyed the sceneries. Bring your dog and go for a hike you won't forget it

Ive been coming since the 70s. I've seen the changes and I like them. There used to be lots of drugs and alcohol going on. It was dangerous and scary sometimes, gang violence and killing.  It's all cleaned up now and it's a whole new atmosphere. I got some great pictures last time I was there.

.

name: Lugo Park
formatted_address: 7810 Otis Ave, Cudahy, CA 90201, USA

google_reviews.text: It's a nice, small park. Has a gazebo with 5 long tables where you can have a small party. You can play in soccer field if there is no game going on.
Went out to Lugo park to play some Futbol and had a blast.

Small park, not much room for kids to play. It is packed on weekends when the bench area is reserved. It has improved a lot since the soccer field was added. Didn't feel that safe in the past, but since the field was added more patrol vehicles pass by and the park has more people now.

It's very very nice. The turf is well tended and kept clean. The paint is continuously repainted and the goal post are very nice. However, it's very small and the water fountains are very faulty. Lacking good hygiene and proper maintenance as the fountain tends to not work.

They have a really nice and pretty spacious turf field for soccer. They have a gym for boxing as well and to put it all together they would have a playground for the kids. I went there with my son soccer team and it was pretty nice. And spacious.

name: Bixby Knolls Park
formatted_address: 1101 E San Antonio Dr, Long Beach, CA 90807, USA

google_reviews.text: Small and quaint park, but spacious enough to have your own space. Grounds kept clean.

It is a clean and quiet park from what I experienced.  I think it's funny that it says it closes at 10:00pm. It's an open park no fences or gates or anything.  I don't get it. Anyway it's one of the cleaner ones.

Nice little neighborhood park.  It's older but well kept. Mostly clean playground and not very crowded on a Saturday when we went.  No homeless people hanging around and felt safe over all.  I would recommend it if you live within walking distance. Probably not worth a long drive to get here however.

Had our family reunion there.  Park was nice.  Restrooms were atrocious!! Extremely filthy and without necessities like tissue for example.  I walked in and walked right back out.  It smelled horrid. I couldn't use the restroom until I got home.

I enrolled my daughter in a pre-k class through the city programs & the classes were moved to this location. I had never been to this park before. It’s in a nice little area & it’s got a ton of grass area. There’s a little gated play area & some swings. Inside the play area is the little classes center.

With that being said it’s getting a lower rating because when I first got there I had to wait for the class to open & while waiting I saw 3 grown individuals arguing over money being owed. One of the men had no shirt on. Then after they separated 2 of them on bikes met at the picnic table with another shirtless man on a bike to exchange more words about money being owed.

name: New York Park
formatted_address: 4525 New York Ave, Glendale, CA 91214, USA

google_reviews.text: It's a quaint park with a jungle gym, basketball court, & picnic benches. Rest room provided for public use.

Love this little park.  There’s a small but new apparatus.  The grass is always green.  There are about four picnic tables.  Other than certain holidays (e.g. Easter) it is never crowded.
UPDATE: The city of Glendale stopped watering the park.  The trees are still nice, but it’s mostly dirt now.  - September 2024

Great park for it's size. It is small, but has everything you would need for a small child. Bark in play area is deep, insuring the safety of your child if they fall. Nice little shaded hiking trail does a horseshoe shape around the park. Nice playset with two slides and swings. The slides are tunnel slides, which are fun and keep the surface from getting hot. Full-court basketball allows you to shoot hoops while your child is playing. Overall great experience. For a small park, it gets an A+.

If I lived within walking distance to this park I would use it, but if I'm getting in my car I would visit almost any of the other La Crescenta playgrounds - they're all bigger, with better views, and more playground equipment. It's not so much that anything is wrong with this park as that the other parks have more to offer.
Nice and friendly park. Upper level of park has a short trail for walking my hairy babies....away from children playing on equipment on lower level. Also park had a basketball court and bathrooms.

name: Valley Park
formatted_address: 2521 Valley Dr, Hermosa Beach, CA 90254, USA

google_reviews.text:
Lovely neighborhood park in Hermosa Beach — decent size and beautifully maintained. There are clean bathrooms, a nice playground (with equipment for younger and older kids), and a basketball court with metal backboards and rigid hoops. The court doesn’t have lights, though the promenade/path around the park is lit at night.
There’s also a very well-kept picnic area with picnic tables and even two sinks, which is a great touch. Street parking is free, though it can fill up quickly depending on the time of day.
Dogs on leash are welcome, and it's a beautiful neighbourhood to walk through as well. Few blocks down, you can reach the beach too and continue with a nice stroll there.

Nice open park with a playground, basketball court, large grassy area, and mature trees offering good shade. There are picnic tables, plenty of parking, and restrooms on site. I've also seen dogs enjoying the open space, running freely on the grass. Great spot to relax or play.

Share your love with the neighborhood.  Play and grill out. I've spent many mornings basking in the early sun, still a little green from the night before. It's a good cheerful place with small happy voices to lay around and get your sea legs back.

Awesome Park
Super Clean
Plenty to do
Ton of open fresh grass
So much shade everywhere
Minutes from the beach bike trails through Manhattan beach so beautiful and safe for kids we had a blast!!

Valley Park, located in a residential area of Hermosa Beach is just adorable. It it not huge, but a good size. It is known well by the locals. My grandson and I happened upon it on a drive one day.

It is so pretty. It has lovely trees and a full area of kids things to do. The place is kept super clean and it is a very safe park. What is super cool is the ice cream truck comes by every day and it will take you back to childhood.

There is something to do for everyone and if you just want to relax, there is plenty of space to lay out a blanket and have lunch or do whatever you want.

We love this place.

name: Patton Street Park
formatted_address: 317 Patton St, Los Angeles, CA 90026, USA

google_reviews.text: Park with lots of potential! They have fitness equipments and playground. Nice, small yet peaceful place in the neighborhood. I feel that if they were to maintain the park a little better, it would be an asset to the community. No one was there when I went. Machines weren’t cleaned, had cobwebs as if it has never been cleaned. They did a great job of making a friendly little park. It’s too bad it’s not well maintained for the community.

Haven't actually been there yet. But since we have Nuggets it's only a matter of time. Because he needs #1, to be able to go out and get some exercise.  &#2,  he needs to get out there and make some friends.

The range of the exercise machines are not as good as they can be. They definitely need some lubing up. There’s a little construction happening at the end of the slide but nothing too crazy. OTHERWISE, this park is haven! It’s so quaint. And I love the walking path.
Super Nice!!! Clean and modern
It’s a perfectly fine park. Good slides and play structure. However, no swings or jungle gym.

name: Pan Pacific Park
formatted_address: 7600 Beverly Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90036, USA

google_reviews.text:
Not much to see.

But perfect after diner at the grove. Walking here to digest. Don’t you feel the afternoon sunlight is magical especially in the greenery? When you walk into that afternoon sunlight, it felt there was a time channel that could take you away for a while, and land in a magical forest.

All the sunny days, nature looks more beautiful.
No need filter, no edit. Just a little natural light, its  beauty is impressively touching.

I love walking my dog through this park because their side-walks are big enough for the amount of people that walk there. Lots of benches along the way. The grass is well maintained and bushes are beautiful. Great place to just get away from it all. Enjoy

Beware of the atrocious parking lot here. I got stuck on a parking spot car bumper. The concrete was broken and the metal pieces were sticking out. Needless to say I had to call triple a for assistance, leaving me stranded here for 3 hours. The city wont take care of the parks and the citizens are forced to pay the price of damages and then some with all the taxes we already pay. Huge thanks to Newsom and Bass great way to spend my morning.

I attended a Black History event, which was wonderful, and the park is lovely. While I enjoy and am empowered by the poems, informative and motivational speeches, and got some great merch; my favorite part of the of these events is the food. The Jamaican jerk chicken with rice and peas was really good and the freshly made lemonade was to die for. However, an angry individual slashed one of my tires over a parking spot. It seems this person is a regular at the park. An image of their car with the license plate visible is posted.

I love going here on morning runs. It’s quiet, the sun looks lovely and there are beautiful dogs playing everywhere❤️. My only ISSUE is with the pool. They say it’s open but every time I check it out, it’s drained. The number they gave doesn’t work. But other than that, pretty solid park.

However, ladies especially, I would not go there alone after the sun has set. Stay safe 💕

name: The Sports Park
formatted_address: 13196 Bluff Creek Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90094, USA

google_reviews.text:
A favorite playground of my 4 year old, he absolutely loves playing here and making friends.

It’s a wonderful playground with lots of neat features like a Sway deck and a spinning round-about. There’s a musical instrument section along the side of the playground and another built into the playground structure.

The playground has various levels and balancing structures. It’s got swings for all kid sizes and a nice foamy surface to run along. There’s a paved path along the outskirts of the playground for kids to ride scooters or bikes on.

Plenty of benches, seating areas and grassy slopes to throw a blanket down on and get cozy.

It’s enclosed, which is nice, and there are bathrooms and an ample amount of parking spots. Parking gets pretty packed on weekends.

You’ll find an open basketball court next to the playground with 2 hoops, and tennis courts.

A great collection of athletic facilities in one park: a football field, a baseball field, basketball courts, tennis courts, and a playground. Public restrooms are onsite too.

When I visited the park, an event was held at the park with lots of happy kids and their parents. It was nice to see that the park is used and loved by the community.

We just parked the car on the street adjacent to the park. So there's little I actually know about the actual park. It did have ample street parking! The park was fairly large and beautifully maintained. It was a serene environment.

Awesome. It is big and has tons of things for kids to do. It does get crowded during the weekends because of the soccer games that happen during the morning/afternoon. I love that it has gates all around so kids do not run out. Recommend.
Scott soccer field basketball courts trail to walk and take your kids lot of helpful useful signs disabled parking playground as well Good place to bring the family

name: Cameron Nature Preserve at Puerco Canyon
formatted_address: 3501 Puerco Canyon Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Walked up to the abandoned pig farm and looked at birds on a drizzly day. It was lovely. Easy hiking, pretty secluded (on a Saturday afternoon).
Nice hike! Be sure to check out what’s left of the buildings at the old De Bell Ranch since the Woolsey Fire. New vegetation has started to grow but right now the trails are nice and clear.

If you have the ❤️‍🔥 your 👀's will be in for an exotic view🌄
I'm the type of person to not know when to turn back.. this trail taught me that once you've made it to the very top and the next trail transitions downward, it's pretty much time to head back (1 hour and 30min walk uphill and about the same to get back to the car). Savor the beauty and maintain the area litter free!
It truly is breathtaking coming back down! Only been there once but definitely need to go another  5 times to get the layout of the land. Be cautious and respectful of mother nature! ❤️‍🔥
Absolutely beautiful trails. Views of point dume and palos verdes. Used to hike this all the time June -nov 2014. Thank you James. Sorry.

name: Chatsworth Nature Preserve
formatted_address: Chatsworth, CA 91311, USA

google_reviews.text: While you can drive past the preserve every day, getting inside, for the public, happens once a year for Earth Day.  This year, due to the rain, that celebration was in June and it was a beautiful day to walk around the preserve, to spot various birds and native plants, and to enjoy being out in nature in the middle of the San Fernando Valley.
A nice 1-mile loop hike. Lots of docents and information booths to provide information on the preserve and local wildlife. Plenty of free parking, no admission charge. Only open 1 or 2 days/year.

We went today 04/23/2022 to Celebrate Earth Day. We had a blast, we especially Loved the Guided Nature Tour. Thank You to everyone who was there helping us out and everyone who went to this Earth Day Event.

I'm sure this place is awesome WHEN ITS OPEN. Closed year round except for Earth Day celebrations, from what the internet says. Google and Apple maps both take you to the wrong place some random stretch of the road. Go and try to check it out at your own risk 🤷🏽‍♂️
We love to visit preserve every one should visit take the kid out of a day family fun or picnic enjoy nature

name: Madrona Marsh Preserve and Nature Center
formatted_address: 3201 Plaza del Amo, Torrance, CA 90503, USA

google_reviews.text:
This is a very lowkey spot for bird watching. I went there for the first time, no traffic, easy parking, and even people there to welcome you in. I am not going to lie, the bird watching isn’t amazing. This is a list of what I saw there (I spent probably 1.5 hours): Allan and Anna’s hummingbird, Black head pheobe, Lesser Goldfinch, dark eyed junco ,song sparrow, house sparrow, house finch, western meadowlark, bushtit, oak bushtit, and either a tree or barn swallow. That being said, I also accidentally get within 3 feet of a coyote when i was wading through some bush which was awesome. There is tons of other animals there like lizards and bunnies. I would definitely reccomend this to a friend. It is also the first place i’ve been (naturally) where I have seen both Allan and Anna’s Hummingbirds together so that was cool. There was also like now one there so you can basically have the bush to yourself. My only issue with this place was the ambient noise. The perimeter are well used roads and planes fly over quite frequently so Merlin is hard to use at a times. But you should defo check it out.

-Avi

Closed Sun/Mon. Opens at 10 am. The volunteers were super nice. I went for a walk the outer path is .92 miles around. Lots of small animals. Very nice place to get some exercise  or just connect  with nature. Free parking across  the street, free to get in. A gift shop etc. Couple  field trips the day I went. Not a lot of shade.
This nature center is great! Part museum, part school with various types of nature and conservation related classes and walking tours of the adjacent marsh

First thing first this place is so beautiful. My sister and I both found this place so healing for the soul. We went through every trail that we could until we got hungry. But let me tell you it is splendid that Torrance has this gem. It has a indoor space that has information for all to read and interact with. I really am happy that this place exists. It is great for all ages. I ran into young and old visiting this place. If I come back I will love to come when ducks are in the marsh. I was told that there are ducks there.

I'm so happy to have such a space near my home. For my child, a place that encourages connection with insects and animals is emotionally beneficial and helps make the day more fulfilling and enjoyable. There’s no shortage of high-quality puzzles, dolls, and coloring activities to enjoy together. The beautiful murals and windows, in particular, provide emotional healing for me as well. There's ample parking, and admission is free.

name: Compton Creek Natural Park
formatted_address: 941 W Cressey St, Compton, CA 90222, USA

google_reviews.text: I rode the Compton Creek path early Saturday and enjoyed how car-free it felt compared to busier bike routes. The path winds through a surprising patchwork of parks and community murals; it felt more local than touristy. Some areas need maintenance, so watch for uneven pavement. It’s a great short ride for families or a calm hour of exercise. Bring sunscreen and water.

Small, thoughtfully designed green space along the creek. Native plants bring in butterflies and birds. It’s a nice place for a quick walk or sit. Kids ride scooters on the smooth path. Feels like a neighborhood success story
Clean and cute lots of open space and not a lot of people   Grounds keepers are always there and friendly
A beautiful, hidden gem. An oasis in the city. Thank you LA Conservation Corps for maintaining such an amazing community resource.
Very nice.  Maybe get a group goin'.

name: Malibu Creek State Park Campground
formatted_address: Agoura Hills, CA 91301, USA

google_reviews.text: Had a wonderful time at the group campground which is right before the general individual campsite. There were two clean bathrooms with clean showers, with a running nice hot water. Also, they had sinks which was handy for cleaning up, washing dishes, or hygiene.  There'a nice area by the sinks to handle dishes or other gear.  The highlight is the picnic table area that was big enough for 50+ people who came for this group. Remarkably, there were no ants, little mosquitos, nor bugs at the picnic area and by the tents.  The scenery by the campground to the parking area was so rustic and captivating with deer nearby. Of course, we had our privacy too as we had a great dance party with a DJ before quiet time at 10pm before going to bed. In the morning, by the grill, we had hot bacon, eggs, griddle, coffee and tea before departing by 11am.  Altogether, it was well worth it.

This campsite is well run with serious employees at the gate. They also work hard maintaining the space. This is a get-what-you-pay-for thing, as the campground is outrageously priced at double the cost of similar campgrounds that offer you more space and free showers. However, you are on Malibu's doorstep and you have a good staff.

Because it is so close to the city, you are likely to have those sounds alongside nature. In one night I heard a helicopter and two ambulances or cop cars. These definitely broke the atmosphere, as well as my nice sleep. You don't get general road noise.

Restrooms and showers are well spaced throughout the campground. You have to go and get tokens for the showers, which is annoying, but it works. It would certainly be nicer if you could just pay there. The showers themselves are nice, and the restrooms are clean.

Sites themselves are pretty level and come with the table and fire ring. We camped at a tent site. The size of your pad is smaller than many other locations. It is still plenty for 3-4 tents (up to 8 people), but not much room to spare.

Given the location, be ready for wet conditions. Condensation will be a bear, so it's like having rain every day. You'll want to towel down all your gear in the morning, as well as make sure you rain fly is well staked and off the main tent.

The area is quite beautiful, with excellent hiking and a short scenic drive to the beach. Dume point is well worth a visit, or you can find your way to a beach.

Be warned that poison oak is everywhere, so educate and monitor younger kids if you camp here!

A very good place for camping. The area was clean, the staff constantly traveled and kept order. There is a river nearby and a great trail, you can take a walk with a baby carriage. Good experience. It is convenient to book on the website, everything is fast and clear.

This campground was wonderful. It had an amazing pool in the creek, but be forewarned. It's really cold. The campsites are all conveniently close to the restrooms, and they all have fire pits. There is lots of nature, such as toads, rabbits, and squirrels. I highly recommend this campground. 5/5.

Campsite price: $47. A little bit steep, but worth it.

Just like the reviews say.
Perfect place to be!!
it couldn’t be a better spot for a quick escape from the city.
Everything you need is nearby, but you still feel far away.
Bring someone new to camping. you might just be the first to introduce them to the perfect getaway only 30 minutes from the city. Enjoy all.

name: Musch Trail Camp - Topanga State Park
formatted_address: Topanga, CA 90290, USA

google_reviews.text: Small little trail. A easy 1 mile from trail head to Musch Camp. The foot traffic past the camp is alot, especially during the morning hours. If you continue up to eagle rock make sure to wear pants. They have this brutal flower plant that is super sharp & hurts pretty bad when U walk into it/brush past it. The path to eagle rock is very over grown so bring poles to push the weeds out of the way. Starts off with shade but u will hit a area that ur in consistent sun. The camps get pretty packed mid afternoon. Lots of foot traffic with people bringing things back and forth. Nice clean bathroom with a Spicket & water fountain. Nice little trail for a beginner backpacker. Tables & lots of logs to place things on at Musch Camp.

Running water, wash basins, clean flushing toilets, very reasonable price and a nice, quiet part of Topanga SP. The rangers in the park were really hospitable and the camp was easy to access. $10 day parking fee, $10 overnight parking fee and a $7 camping fee is almost $20 cheaper than Malibu Creek SP - and to me - this is a nicer site as it's hike in only. Would for sure visit again.
Musch trail is very nice and pretty :) at the camp there are stalls for horses and picnic area.
Very nice trail !!! Great night hiking, kid friendly my 9 yr.& 11 yr old Daughter's Loved this hike!! Beautiful views,even saw a deer!!

We backpack to this place with the boyscouts and we always have a good experience and a good adventure....
In general is good...

name: Escondido Falls
formatted_address: Escondido Canyon Trail, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text:
Very nice trail! I crossed 5 small streams to get to the lower park of the waterfall. There are beautiful views of the beach (from the parking lot to the beginning of the trail).

If you are driving, you can park at 27751 Pacific Coast Highway, Malibu CA 90265. Parking as of 1/15/26 is $12 with card (I didn't see an apple pay or cash option:/). But if you want free parking, you can park along Route 1. I called the sheriff's department and confirmed that I can. Look out for signs of where not to park. I attached a video to show you where you can and cant park!

Directions to the falls: Park in the lot or street. Walk up the street Winding Way towards the east direction. Then there will be a point: three car roads and one dirt path to the left. There is a sign for the escondido falls, looking downwards. Head there and start walking :) follow the trail markers. Theres 5 small creeks you will need to cross. Once you see a bench, you made it! The falls will he to your left :)

Tips: Wear shoes with grip. Use a walking stick to help cross the streams. When in doubt, follow the creek!

Always practice "Leave no trace." I saw a bunch of plastic water bottles :(

I was able to hike to the lower part of the fall in 30 minutes (starting from the sign), but 45 minutes starting from the parking lot. Haha in my opinion, the walk from the parking lot to the trail was harder. As of 1/15, the trail wasn't slippery. I just walked through the creeks since the water level was low (maybe 6inches deep max)

A wonderful place, from the parking lot to the waterfall itself you need to walk about 40-55 minutes. When you go to the waterfall, on the way you will come across small rivers that you need to cross (there is not much water there), but you need to wear comfortable shoes and not be afraid to get wet. The parking lot is at the very bottom (right next to the road). You can't drive straight to the waterfall, because you'll get a fine, parking there is prohibited (rangers monitor this)

There's a very tiny PAID parking lot at the trailhead with bathrooms and trash cans. Many park along the roads at the entrance of the street. (I'm not sure how many tickets or not.)

Your first walk along a road in between stunning mansions. Then you get to the entrance to the trail. You will likely have to cross many streams to get there, so be prepared that your shoes may get wet. It's difficult, because you want hiking boots that have really good grip and traction especially if you're ignoring the warning sign (not recommending) and you also want water shoes because you may have to go through streams and into the pond at the bottom of the waterfall.

It is a gorgeous hike and a gorgeous destination though, so definitely recommended. You know your physical capabilities, make intelligent decisions.

This has actually reopened. We went there yesterday, July 2025.
Gorgeous hike with a great mix of canyon and ocean views. You’ll feel like you’re walking through a peaceful little forest—there’s plenty of shade thanks to the trees.
There’s a small parking lot with a $12 fee, but you can also try parking on PCH just before or after the trailhead (if you’re lucky). A porta potty is available at the start.
The first stretch goes through a residential area, but you’ll hit the actual trail soon after. Along the way, you’ll cross a few small streams, but it’s easy to stay dry with a bit of care.
The highlight is the waterfall about an hour in—a perfect stopping point. Don’t bother trying to go further; there’s no real trail beyond that, and nothing more to see.
Highly recommend for a light-to-moderate hike.
** January 2026 update **
Since reopening, this hike has become extremely popular. Parking is now very difficult — even early in the day, the lot fills up quickly, and the PCH is often packed with cars. Be careful, as some areas along the highway are no-parking zones and you can get fined.
Because this is an out-and-back trail with a single main path, it can feel quite overcrowded on weekends. The river crossings especially tend to bottleneck, and you may end up waiting behind groups to cross.

Beautiful, breath taking,  green, lush, dangerous and unapologetically raw; our December morning swim was a well deserved cold splash and a refreshing treat! I spent my morning climbing up my Malibu Mountains, Escondido Canyon Trail, Malibu. We had to hike about 2.5 miles first  just to get to the trail. Then climb the ridge on our hands and feet - BEAR CRAWL- most ways by grappling rocks and trees.

No ropes, harnessing nor clamps! But I wish we had them though because it was very hard and perilous in so many areas. Sliding rocks, mud, water, difficult terrain, slippery trails; many in our party of 10 were highly triggered. Dressed in yoga pants, we thought this would be just a little nature hike for the kids to enjoy. Many had to be helped, consoled and hyped up  just to continue. But it just reminded me of STL 😊 and our river trips so I just sang in the sunshine . This made the kids relax and climb while singing.  You can't cry and sing at the same time!  We taught them it was ok to be scared but it's not ok to let fear keep you from what you want to accomplish. So- Yes we scaled two waterfalls with a 10 month old baby!

Sure we had to slide back down that mountain on our butts but I have good padding there!  Yes, I ripped my pants wide open! Yes those rocks made me their wife!  You should have seen the amount of rocks, twigs, flora, fauna and dirt embedded there🙃.  Super sore ABSOLUTELY everywhere  and yet invigorated and empowered! Hey! I'm am good shape y'all! Let's go do it again next weekend!

name: Topanga Lookout Trailhead
formatted_address: Saddle Peak Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Pretty easy trail :) It took around 30 minutes to get to the lookout (1hr roundtrip). The last part to get to the graffiti is steep but short. Very nice place to catch the sunset/ sunrise! There is parking on the sides of the road or at Lois Ewen Overlook Trailhead sign (theres few spots in that lot). Parking is allowed past 6am in that small lot.
Drive about 1 hour from LA to there have 3 spots for parking that I see but not many ppl there that time only me and my friend on trails so nice for photography 📸

Easy trail with in & out access. We took our hiking stroller & was definitely a struggling. Hard to get the real view due to inconsistent weather. I could see this place as a great sunrise / sunset spot. Parking wasn’t bad, but definitely limited.

Highly recommend- short (about an hour from beginning to end) but scenic and peaceful. The continuation after the first peak is the best part of the trail. Lovely brush to walk through until you reach the last plateau.

Take your friends, family or children with you for this one.

The trail is nice with enough places where you can walk in the shade. Just watch out for broken glass. For some reason too much broken glass on the trail. Other than that, the lookout is a great place to see the valley and the ocean from a single spot. There is no fee for entering, no restrooms. Parking is free and it's along the road.

name: Sycamore Canyon Falls
formatted_address: Potrero Open Space Trail, Thousand Oaks, CA 91320, USA

google_reviews.text: Absolutely gorgeous! Due to all the recent rains the streams flow with water and the waterfall has lots of water making for a beautiful sight! The trails are muddy in places and you will find poison oak along the trail, especially by the waterfall and streams. I suggest bringing a picnic lunch, sitting on a rock and enjoying the beautiful sounds of the waterfall.
Beautiful hike under 4 miles. Smart to have hiking shoes for certain areas (with good grip vs trail shoes).

I love taking the family here for hiking.  The view is breathtaking. I see a lot of people taking their dogs for walks/run, families with kids, older couples just out for a stroll,  etc. There are a lot of trails to choose from. I recommend to park at wendy and Potrero. There's a map of all the trails there.

This was an exceptionally pretty place during dry times. We've been in a drought for a couple of years and only had one major rain storm, and the falls are flowing beautifully! Get here early enough and you might even catch the gorgeous sunrise and the cold frost ❄ during winter mornings!

Easy to get to from fwy.
Easy to park...free!!
Friendly hikers, bikers, and runners!

Went in mid-August. Not much shade at the beginning of the trail, i.e., not much on your return journey, so be mindful if you are sun sensitive! 🥵

Lots of overgrowth on the trail to the waterfall.

**Beware of Ticks!
My dog came out without a hitch...Unfortunately, I had one attached to the bottom of my neck/shoulder! Had to go to Urgent Care to get it removed because I couldn't reach that sucker! 🫣😲

Hope this helps!

name: Los Robles Trail and Open Space system
formatted_address: Thousand Oaks, CA 91361, USA

google_reviews.text: Great trails for hiking or mountain biking.  It's about 2 miles up the switchbacks to the top of the hill and 2 miles back down to the parking lot.  But you can continue this trail from the top of the hill towards rasnow Peak road all the way to ventu Park Road. You can also take this Trail to Trimpho Park.
Absolutely wonderful place to walk and enjoy the nature!

Great trail system with lots of options for hikers and mountain bikers.  Many miles of trails with various skill levels and side trails, all well maintained.

End to end, you can hike 9+ miles from Westlake to Newbury Park, with myriad side trails and areas to explore and add mileage.  Highlights on the system include Angel Vista, Space Mountain, and the White Horse trail.  A great system of trails with incredible views and many animals and plants to enjoy.

This is an easy find with good parking, and a small but nice public bathroom that's easy to access,  plenty of quiet spaces you can take an easy stroll through when your looking for a place to chill, or sit down with a friend and share a sandwich at one of the picnic tables you'll find along the way, really a very cool place.
Great open space in Thousand Oaks. Right now, everything is as green as can be thanks to the recent rains. Wonderful colorful wild flowers in full bloom. Quiet and very serene.

name: Topanga State Park
formatted_address: 20828 Entrada Rd, Topanga, CA 90290, USA

google_reviews.text:
Topanga State Park is a peaceful escape into nature right in Los Angeles.
With hiking trails, scenic overlooks, and lush greenery, it’s perfect for a morning walk, picnic, or photography.
The fresh air and stunning views make it a great spot to recharge and disconnect from city life.
Ideal for nature lovers, hikers, and anyone seeking calm and adventure.

Wow is it fun to absolutely rip through the canyon roads on the ways to trails! The trails themselves are very beautiful and I went up there to photograph birds and will attach pics.  there was a landslide so if you are coming from the PCH you have to wait a little but no matter what it’s going to be a bit of a drive because you really get up there. I picked up a hitchhiker who was skateboarding down the canyon trails which was sick dude. Definitely a great place to hike but this is sort of a unique and singular spot in the sense that once your there, you may as well spend the day; Or, go down to the PCH and the beach after.

Main entrance closed due to wildfires, but we were able to hop on the trail via dead horse lot. Gorgeous hike! We spent about 4 hours. Crossed a cool little bridge (would've been amazing to see the "river" during the rainy season!). No water features, but the punch bowls I wanted to hike were too far for this trip. Gorgeous views, nice little elevation change. Tons of lizards, too! I probably won't do this hike again, but I'm glad we got a chance to see the views!

The Topanga canyon side of the trails are open! It's beautiful as always. Tippet ranch parking lot is under construction but the trails are accessible from the dead horse parking lot and entrada road via 92 trial.

Great hike with lots of trails all over. The views are breathtaking of the whole ocean,  Malibu and Los Angeles areas. Moderate hike depending on how far you go and what trails you choose. We started at the Los Leones trail head. It is very busy on the weekends. Hiking boots are not needed, it's a gradual climb with many switch backs. No bathrooms close by and parking can be hard to find on busy days. Trails are narrow at the beginning of the first mile, but it gets wider as you get to the top and then you can hike further up on a wider trail/road. There are lots of lookout points and one park bench at the first upper stopping point. Watch for snakes on hot days. Bring plenty of water and sun protection if you are worried about heat as there isn't shade.
There's so much to explore and many different starting points so plan and prepare and have fun.

name: Sandstone Peak Trailhead Parking
formatted_address: 12860-, 12896 Yerba Buena Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to hike. Amazing views, great weather, easy trail. A little slippery if you are not wearing the right shoes but a great trail regardless. Highly recommend for beginners and also to take your pets. Parking is a little small so plan to either show up really early or walk from the other areas where cars can park. Great place either way.

By far one of my best hikes. I love the beach and being able to hike and able to see the ocean is by far a beautiful experience. Bring snacks because you are going to want to be there longer then expected

A very beautiful place, away from all the noise. The ideal is to go early in the morning during the summer if you don’t want to be affected too much by the sun, because it can get very hot. But the view from the top is stunning, and the hike isn’t very long. Bring plenty of water and a small snack. I also found it interesting that at the top there’s a little box with notebooks and pens to write something down.

Excellent trail for a quick thrill to the highest point in the Santa Monica Mountains. The distance is approximately 3 miles out and back. Average grade is about 10° to 15°. Near the top, there needs to be blazing or markings to minimize human erosion.

Amazing hike! They have free parking and bathrooms. Dog friendly, bring water there’s not much shade on the hike & sun glasses it gets sunny at the peak and a big flew I. My eye.

The hike is a lot of incline but it’s beautiful seeing the road below and hiking in the clouds. It was super cloudy/gloomy all week in LA but when I got to the peak there were no clouds in the sky but as I made my way back down you could see the clouds forming again and passing you by.

name: Garden of the Gods
formatted_address: Santa Susana Pass at, Redmesa Rd, Chatsworth, CA 91311, USA

google_reviews.text: Short easy tails with amazing views! Only spent about an hour there because it had been raining all weekend so there might be some longer trails that we didn't explore.

It's a place to go walking around in natural socal  landscape. There are tons of giant boulders, which are iconic to the area. Not the prettiest place, but then again it's Southern California not a tropical rainforest.

Some people rock climb (specifically, they 'boulder') here.

Street parking. There is almost no one here ever. It's free. Super short walk. This is not a strenuous hike.
nice little neighbor hike with a great city view.

Yesterday morning my husband and I took my daughter Jasmine to this location before she flew back to Nashville Tennessee !  This was one of the last locations tha my father had visited before he passed away almost 6 years ago ! My father was really into western movies and you could just feel my father's  presence here yesterday ! A nice location to come to and have a picnic or just walk around and have a little adventure especially during the winter
Nice place but not really a hiking trail.  Lots of rocks/boulders to climb on... very close to residential area.  Great views from some spots.  Plenty of street parking.

name: Red Rock Canyon Park, Mountains Recreation & Conservation Authority
formatted_address: 23601 W Red Rock Rd, Topanga, CA 90290, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to go hiking and take in some nature!  Amazing views and very peaceful.  When I went, not too many people on the trail!  Go out and explore this gem in SoCal!

The road to get to the park takes you through a residential canyon where at times it looks like you're about to pull into someone's driveway because you can't see how the road curves at the last minute. It becomes one-lane through the neighborhood with a lot of blind curves. When you reach the park entrance, the road turns into an unmaintained gravel road with many ruts and potholes; I definitely recommend a high-clearance vehicle. We didn't have to use 4-wheel drive, but it wasn't particularly muddy that day.

The actual park feels incredibly remote for being in the middle of the Santa Monica Mountains, surrounded by greater Los Angeles.   The main trail out of the parking area is a wide fire road, but the trails that go off of it are poorly marked. We missed the trail we meant to take, which is supposed to have all the cool geological features. I'd like to go back sometime to do that trail, but there are many other parks/trails that are easier to get to in the L.A. metro area.

The road is no joke. Not sure how some of the lower cars made it there. You stay on the road and keep going to get to the lot/entrance. Unfortunately there really aren't any parking options before the parking lot (resident parking only). Views are beautiful and seems like a great place to explore. Very unique and wonderful park. Parking is $5

Solid hiking trail that will take you to the Calabasas peak in roughly 50 minutes from the parking lot. The views are beautiful all along the hike. Keep in mind that the park "closes" 30 minutes after sunset, but gets pretty dark right at sunset time - if not a little earlier, because of the mountains and rocks surrounding you.
Getting to the parking lot for the start of the hike and the Red Rock park can be challenging, there is an off road for probably 1/2 mile, and it was quite rough driving on that when we went (December 2024), so take extra caution with low asset vehicles.

This place is a hidden gem. So much to explore. Definitely be prepared with comfortable hiking shoes and water. A cloudy somewhat cold day was perfect as we quickly worked out a sweat. I recommend taking the trail to the Teppe which leads to the Waterfall. You can go past the waterfall and follow the stream for beautiful shaded spots. We will definitely have to come back.

name: Upper Canyonback Trailhead
formatted_address: 17024 Mulholland Dr, Encino, CA 91436, USA

google_reviews.text:
Easy, dog-friendly off-leash hike that’s perfect if you want great views without a brutal climb. We went on a Sunday morning around 8:30, which turned out to be a sweet spot—busy enough to feel lively, but never crowded or chaotic. There was plenty of space to walk comfortably, let dogs roam and explore, and still enjoy the trail without constantly stopping or stepping aside.

The weather couldn’t have been better. Clear skies made all the difference, and the views were honestly spectacular. From several points along the trail, you could see all the way out to the Santa Ana Mountains, across Long Beach and Palos Verdes, and even Catalina Island sitting on the horizon. Watching planes take off and land at LAX (binoculars required)in the distance added an unexpected and fun visual element that made the landscape feel even more expansive.

What really sets this hike apart is how accessible it is. You don’t need to be an experienced hiker to enjoy it, and it’s especially great for dogs that love open space and freedom. The trail is well-suited for a relaxed pace, plenty of stops to take in the scenery, and moments where your dog can just be a dog.

All in all, it’s a fantastic option for a weekend morning—easy, scenic, and incredibly rewarding for the effort. If you catch it on a clear day, the views alone make it more than worth the trip.

If you're looking for an outdoor place to exercise that's accessible from the city/valley, this is a good spot.
On a clear day, the views are nice.
I recommend getting here as the sun comes up.
Be mindful of mountainbikers who have the right of way.
There are dogs off leash - which I wouldn't recommend considering the number of rattlesnakes in the area.
Bring plenty of water.
It was a nice hike with some great views free parking at the small lot,seen quite a few bikers, it wasn't too crowded.I gave it a 10/10

Hiking the beast trail from Whitney Canyon park to Camp 9 Fire station. Total distance 15 miles out and back.
Total Ascent - 5,951 ft.

A 6 mile gravel trail that has scenic views. Right now Spring is just starting to show with yellow and pink wildflowers already sprouting. We went a few days after the rain and there were small areas of mud and a rather strong water current flowing on a neighborhood roadside. The road up to parking is a bit rocky with large potholes

name: Point Dume
formatted_address: Cliffside Dr &, Birdview Ave, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Very peaceful place to enjoy the sunset and beach. Overall, the beach was not too crowded and the sunset was majestic. There is a short hiking trail that offers some pretty nice coastal views.  Also if you are lucky you can spot some seals on the rocks. Highly recommended if you are in the area and want a chill place to watch the sunset, short hike, or some scenic views.

Easy hike with Scenic views. We parked in the beach parking and started hike from beach. The trail itself is beautiful with all yellow flowers all around and then the stunning views of pacific ocean. The trail is well maintained and easy to walk. There are a couple of viewpoints on the trail where you can sit and enjoy the view. You can also spot sea lions from trail.

It’s beautiful, the short trail, the view, the cliff, the wild flowers after the rain, gorgeous sunset. Go to the parking lot in front of Westward beach, this parking lot is huge, the one in front of the destination where google map took us to only has less than 10 spots. We also came across some high profile people during the trail as it’s a celeb-packed neighborhood.

Parking can be challenging right by the trailhead, however, parking is available on the side streets but just be careful to park fully off the street and not in a NO PARKING zone (you will get a ticket!). Trails are easy and marked around the bluff, however, watch your step by the edge. Great place to take in the views and catch the sunset!

Very cool and historic location. We ended up spending hours here exploring all of the trails, coast and watching the rock climbers. We didn't realize there were other parking options and waited 15 minutes for a vehicle to leave. While waiting, we watch 4 vehicles get tickets for sharing spaces outside the white boxes and or parking in the red zones. Also, these parallel spaces are tight and bad drivers play bumper cars to squeeze in. I do not recommend waiting or using the 5 parallel parking spots unless you have a handicap pass. Instead, simply pay and park at the beach where the restrooms are also located. All trails connect from the paid parking lot.

name: Charmlee Wilderness Park
formatted_address: 2577 Encinal Canyon Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a very solid, easy hike. The trail is enjoyable, the views are as expected (stellar) and the big bonus for me, was the wide variety of very interesting native plants. I was constantly tickled to see plants I knew and loved, plus many that I was curious to learn more about. If I had a complaint it would be that there are quite a few unofficial trails that can lead you astray. But at the end of the day it’s a small enough space that you can’t get yourself into a bad situation

Beautiful hike and easy to navigate with wonderful views from every direction. Wild flowers are in bloom. Lovely for a family outing since the trail is mostly flat; also many wonderful spots for a picnic for a romantic evening sunset hike. Loved this trail! And it was not crowded!

Great little hiking loop with multiple trails and outlooks. Just keep an eye open for wildlife as there is a lot of deer prints, coyote scat, lizards, and an occasional rattlesnake on the trail. I did run into a coyote on the trail who was kind of a pain. The bathrooms are clean and there are eating areas.

Beautiful, easy hiking for the family. There are a couple of trails that branch off of each other, for a couple miles of trail in total.  The Mike  walk we took was nice, and at the end of our walk (we went to the end of the Ocean View trail), the view of the ocean and parts off Malibu made for a beautiful place to rest for a bit before coming back the way we came. We did see a rattlesnake just off the trail (it totally ignored us) so keep an eye out.
A really peaceful and relaxing place to be. And if you want to go for a jog or run…you need to be ready pivot where there are areas with gravel and uneven surfaces created by Mother Nature.

name: Autumn Ridge Trail
formatted_address: Autumn Ridge Trail, Thousand Oaks, CA 91362, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful hike! Followed the All Trails route (trailhead to Sunrise & loop back on Autumn Ridge trail). Started around 8AM, plenty of street parking when we arrived. Would love to come back to explore all the different trails & routes. Very warm once the sun isn’t hiding behind the ridges, so be mindful of the heat & sun protection.
Beautiful starting point to explore the Simi hills. It's a steep climb at the beginning but a nice way to get warmed up. Beautiful views. Throughout the park, especially at sunset.

It's a nice trail that you can customize to your liking! If you like you can do a 3.5 mile loop or go to Simi Peak or China Flats for extra workout making it about 7 miles round trip. Vista keeps changing making it interesting.

Update 5/9/2023
This year with extra rain this trail is even more beautiful. A pond has sprung up with a few Mallard ducks. Wild flowers give different colors to the landscape. See the pictures! This trail provides a few options to take either the easy or moderate trail.

Great trail open to any skills level. Wide trail, well kept, not super steep but there are steep parts you'll want good traction shoes for. Saw families, trail runners, and folks with dogs. Most if the larger plants along the path have been burned away in the recent fires but lots of rolling hills views. Beautiful especially with the green after a rain. Parking is on the street and it's plentiful.

Beautiful Trail that goes up to 20 miles long. Tons of ocean fossils, water features ( dry mostly year round). Stay away from the far back of the property as it leads to the toxic Santa Susana Field Lab. Also avoid this area on windy days as the dust contains radiation and TCE.

name: Sullivan Canyon Trailhead
formatted_address: 2118-2100, Queensferry Rd, Los Angeles, CA 90049, USA

google_reviews.text: Sullivan Canyon is located right off Sunset with a climb into a neighborhood where you can find the trailhead. This is not a climbing type of hike and perfect for trail bikes. It is a long trail with plenty of tree coverage in areas. It is beautiful in the fall, winter, and spring but can get hot in the summer. There are some swings located on the trail...and it is a beautiful hike. Parking can be tough since you have to park in the neighborhood. Be careful some neighbors don't like it if you park by their homes. It is very dog friendly and the people who hike the trail are friendly.

Great trail for MTB (bring your bell(s)). Watch where you park. Enforcement is highly monitored. Sullivan canyon trail takes you up to Dirt Mulholland and Nike Missile tower where there are great views. There is a restroom near the tower with a working payphone. Take a trip back in time on this journey to the top. Enjoy!

Sick little gravel trail for the first 5 miles or so then you get to ride this beautiful paved path the rest of the way up. A nice punishing climb for the last mile that will make you contemplate life but will be super satisfied at the top. Great for all levels.
Unbelievably beautiful. It’s a easy level trail. Trail is beside the seasonal river. Several times you should pass the small river. Pictures really can’t show the true beauty
Great spot to start and explore the canyon. No facilities at a - just a lot of nature. Watch out for the mountain bikers - they are NOT looking out for you!

name: Corral Canyon Park
formatted_address: Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Great little hike. Very enjoyable, steep at first but more gradual 75 percent of hike. The full loop is about 90 minutes. Great at sunset time. Very green right now. ✌️✌️

I would definitely recommend reading a few reviews before hiking at this location.  We got things figured out before we left, but it would have gone better had we done our homework first.

We paid our $12 (mistake #1) and we were excited to get moving, so we initially took the "trail" right behind the signage near the parking kiosk.  This path was steep, overgrown and somewhat dead-ended us into a steep, muddy creek embankment which we did not want to try to navigate.

We figured we did something wrong and read a few reviews with the bar or two of connectivity we had.  This is how we learned the correct path to take was back down the parking lot, along the PCH for 40 yards or so and then into the wild.  This path was wider and better cared for, which made the rest of our hike more pleasant.

Beautiful views. Good elevation gains. The trail is very overgrown. To pay for parking is ridiculous when you can park for free on the PCH. I usually like to hike further away from civilization, but for my situation at the time, the hike was entertaining enough.

Very nice views. Nice for hiking. Mountain biking is nice but mainly fire roads. There is at least one cool singletrack downhill. It gets hot. Take plenty of water and sun protection. Not many shade areas

The loop here gives ocean and canyon views without a massive climb. Wild sage and coastal chaparral make the whole hike smell amazing. There’s minimal shade, so sunscreen is a must. We snagged a roadside spot and started clockwise—views came faster that way. Great stop paired with a beach picnic

name: Circle X Ranch, Grotto Trailhead
formatted_address: 12896 Yerba Buena Rd, Malibu, CA 90265, USA

google_reviews.text: Just taking a drive today which is fun. Up to circle x ranch. I've hiked the grotto many times and it's so beautiful, especially this time of year after the rains. Definitely a must see even if you just want to go for a scenic drive. Be careful on the roads. They're very narrow and rocks are common. There are restrooms available. And if you just want to have a picnic here, it's a great spot.

A popular place to hike. The grotto trail and happy hollow trails are moderate. The canyon view trail is easy. If you start at Circle C, Sandstone Peak trail is somewhat strenuous. If you want to hike the Backbone trail you should park at Mishe Mokwa trailhead. There are excellent views on all these trails. Waterfall is seasonal.

Really good hike. Took the kids. So many trails to choose from. Just beware to wear good shoes and watch out for poison oaks near water area. Trail starts at the camping area. Had a hard time finding out where the starting point was lol. They have more parking areas below the cabin with the trail maps

The trails are well maintained and user friendly for any age group.  The hike is fairly easy. If you can walk, then you can definitely do this hike with ease. However,  if you venture off to the Grotto,  you definitely need to have a bit more skill and balance.  I wouldn't suggest visiting the grotto with little kids. They're likely to fall or slip and get hurt because at that point you go off trail and climb down boulders and hold onto the trees and their roots. At this time of the year (summer), the waterfall isn't quite active.  There's more of a drizzle than an actual waterfall. The water in the creek is minimal but still nice. I would recommend the hike.

The grotto trail was pretty chill, not a long hike at all but it was enjoyable.

The start of the hike is pretty much 90% descending which is super easy just make sure you watch your step, the loose dirt can make you slip.

Once you get closer to the grotto there is a lot of boulders you have to scale down and then a woody slippery area which is easy if you remember to grab onto nearby trees or rocks.
