It’s a hike! The view is spectacular!!! Perfect birthday hike. It’s not easy or flat. Bring a lot of cold water and good hiking shoes. There is an opportunity to cool off in the river and the waterfalls are spectacular!

Great trail system with breathtaking views! We did about a mile in, but saw a lot of great views along the way. The trail was  easy-moderate for the section that we did. You can either take the cold spring trail to Montecito Peak, or take the Tangerine Trail to Tangerine Falls.

name: Hot Springs Canyon Trailhead
formatted_address: 1202-1298 E Mountain Dr, Montecito, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: I'm glad we decided to go. When I read the reviews online, everyone said they had problems with parking. We were downtown Santa Barbara so we decided to take a Lyft up there. It was less than half an hour and cost less than $20. And did the same thing back. I definitely recommend that route rather than driving. Or you can walk if you live in the neighborhood. The hike was about an hour. Be sure to follow the signs or you will miss it. The hike was fun but can be rough if the ground is wet from rain. Luckily we were there on a dry day. We went in late January but the temperature was in the mid 60s. It was perfect because after the hike you will sweat and don't want to get into the springs when you're hot. The water was hot, the higher you go up. A few small pools for 1 or 2 people to use, if you don't want to sit in the bigger pool with strangers. The hike itself was also beautiful. Lots of lookout spots. This is by far one of the best natural hot springs I have been to. And I have been to New Zealand, and Iceland. Grap your walking shoes, and snacks and enjoy what nature has to offer!

Montecito Hot Springs is an absolute hidden gem. The hike in is beautiful and peaceful, and the reward at the end is more than worth it. The trail can be a little tricky at first, but there are plenty of helpful YouTube videos that walk you through it step by step. The biggest key is to just follow the creek and listen for the sound of the water — once you do that, it’s hard to go wrong. The hot springs themselves are incredible, relaxing, and surrounded by nature. Definitely one of the best outdoor experiences in the area and well worth the effort to get there.

I got lost on the way to the hot springs, but luckily some friendly hikers pointed me in the right direction. I eventually heard laughter echoing through the trees — that’s when I knew I was close. The place is absolutely beautiful and feels tucked away from the world. There isn’t a lot of room, so you really need to come early in the morning if you want a spot. Most people there are fellow hikers — open-minded, laid-back, and usually happy to make a bit of space.

The pools themselves are warm at the top, and they get cooler as you move down — still nice, just not as hot. But be careful: the trail can be confusing, and I nearly ended up at the top of the mountain by mistake. Totally worth the trip, just plan ahead and bring a good sense of direction.
Really nice trail - it was kinda steep at some parts but doable. We walked like 50-60min up there but it was fun.

I absolutely love this hike! There are many hot pots starting at the top being the hottest and trickling down to different little spots you can sit in. Going up is a bit of a climb but not too bad. Going down seems quick. I definitely will do this hike again.

name: Stevens Park
formatted_address: 258 Canon Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice little park. The parking lot is small. The playground is cute. The bathroom functions well enough. There’s a drinking fountain. There’s a little bridge that crosses the creek and there are hiking trails that go out the back of the park. They go all the way up the mountain. One of the quieter parks in Santa Barbara.

Lovely gem of a park in a beautiful narrow arroyo.   Wooded trail follows the stream, heads north to the mountains and eventually meets up with the Jesusita Trail.     Lots of wildlife and plants.   Decent playground for the kids, clean restrooms.   Good parking.
It's still beautiful and quiet. I haven't been here since I was a kid. There's a playground, bathrooms & a bridge if you don't want to walk through the creek.

Water was very high during storm , in the picture you can see the dark horizontal line  which was the water line. In other picture with the 2 boulders in middle, the water was 2feet over them, never seen that much water there.i would say it was about 10 feet deep there

Great neighborhood park with a large grassy open space, large playground, a public restroom, along with many picnic tables and bbq's. There is a running creek during the winter months but it's dry during the summer.

name: Inspiration Point Trailhead
formatted_address: Spyglass Ridge Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Some trails appear on the maps at the beginning of the park, but are actually private property. So our hike turned out to be twice as long because of it. Nonetheless, the scenery was beautiful and the hike was very enjoyable.
Great hike! Especially for the sunset. Loved the view and you could hear the frogs in the river down below. 10/10, would do again.

Went up the Mission Canyon Catway trail - out and back. Total of 5.6 miles with steep climb of 1,300 ft gain over 2 miles. At 7 pm the views were magnificent except for the ocean fully covered with clouds.

This is a great hike and if you’re in the area, I definitely recommend setting aside at least a few hours and you’ll be rewarded with an amazing scenic view of the area. I was throughly exhausted but the effort was worth it for the view!
Busy hike,  too busy for my taste. Parking on Tunnel Road can be a pain. 3.5 miles from trailhead to viewpoint. Not much gain going up, maybe half mile up hill but that's it.

name: Douglas Family Preserve
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: I was just passing through the area while on a business trip and saw this place as a recommended stop for my dog. Even on a Friday afternoon, the parking was plentiful. The trails were nice and secluded. There were many other dog owners around with their pups off-leash and not a single problem. It was a great day!

Beautiful spot with very mild hikes and spectacular ocean views from the cliffs. Loved seeing so many dogs running around and having a great time — it really adds to the atmosphere!

Also a great place for trail running or just an easy walk with amazing scenery. Highly recommend!

Nice tail smoking the coast with great views, especially during sunset. It's a bit hard to find, doesn't have dedicated parking. You can park in the residential blocks near the trail. Is dog friendly so that's a plus. Glad we came here.

The Douglas Family Preserve was an excellent park to go walk around. It is generally an easy place to walk around with lots of pretty scenery over the water. There were multiple people walking and with dogs (many off leash). It was definitely worth the trip and one of our favorite places to walk in Santa Barbara.
That was the best experience ever literally heaven on earth. The pups had a blast. The views were amazing. We met a lot of nice people. The energy is unmatched.

name: Red Rock Trailhead
formatted_address: Gibraltar Trail, Forest Rte 5N18, Santa Barbara, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: We have been here for about 10 years, the staff is always friendly and accommodating. I would suggest booking a spot at least a month before to get secure spot. They do have day passes to just visit the lake if you don't want to spend the night. The campsites have a water fountain, a fire pit and a grill. They also have bathrooms that are maintained well.

Good place for hiking. You have also the option to swim in the river. If you follow the river until you reach the Gibraltar reservoir and then walk back on the dirt road it will take you approximately 4 hours if you make stops in between.

i’m writing this because i noticed that there aren’t many recent reviews for red rock, which made my partner and i apprehensive when we commuted into los padres forest last friday without much information. thankfully,  everything turned out much better than we had expected!

it was above 90 degrees the day that we went, which was perfect temperature for the cold water of the red rock pools. the drive was fairly simple, with a few narrow roads and dirt paths that could make some uneasy, but there were many signs directing us to the correct locations as we delved deeper into the mountains. there was ample parking at the trailhead, as well as a small pool to the left of the lot for those who do not want to hike to the “official” pools! we only traveled to the first pool (we were told it was the biggest), which was a very easy 0.5 mile hike. the view that we were met with was breathtaking! i am still shocked that a place like this exists in santa barbara. the water was cool and a perfect level to swim, with some pretty deep areas. i highly recommend going on sb’s next hot day if you can!

the only negative is that it seemed like the park was devoid of all rangers; the pay station was deserted and we were quite confused on how to pay the $10 entrance fee. a local we spoke to informed us that the pay station has been this way for quite some time. so if you’re confused once you reach the entrance, we promise it’s not abandoned! there were many people hiking and enjoying the pools.

here are some pics of the sights :)
I didn't want to leave. I could go there everyday or just stay. It one of the most beautiful places I ever been
The single tracks are totally unmaintained, but the hike along the river is kind of cool

name: Arlington Peak
formatted_address: Cathedral Peak Trail, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Exploring the area. I just realized I did Arlington and Cathedral peak. Start early morning with camel back and food. Make sure to bring a gps or compass as I got lost twice. Take pictures to remember. The first time I went off trail was bad as I was free soloing down which was dangerous . Had to backtrack.

Started the hike down at 7 falls around 1pm and ended up at the peak by sun down 5:30pm. Make sure to bring a power bank and lots of water!! And flashlights just in case. We ended up walking down another trail from the peak for 3 hours all in the dark. Made it out of 7 falls but 9pm. Alot of rock and boulder hopping and some rock sliding! The gound had some loose sand and we kept slipping. So many amazing veiws, but had to rush down. Possibly saw a mountain lion right below us, as we were taking a brake in the dark. The trails are pretty clear and small arrows are kinda placed around, but you definitely need a gps. Our phones died around 7pm in the dark. Our (one) flashlight was going out too the last 2 hours. But fun over all.

I had so much fun on this hike or scramble. Lots of jumping over boulders and rocks.  There is a path but not well marked so looking at a map ahead of time might be helpful.  The views are all worth it.

This was a challenging, beautiful and unique trail. Beautiful views! Half the trail you’ll be climbing boulders, so some good lower and upper body strength is required. Make sure you’re wearing some good hiking shoes :)
Love it!!! One of the must beautiful hikes here in Santa Barbara! Rock climbing half of the way, so plan a lot of effort, water and time going up and down!

name: Skofield Park
formatted_address: 1783 Las Canoas Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Fantastic camping ground ideal for walks and practicing rock climbing! Dog-friendly and reasonable parking. Paved paths, as well as dirt trails, allow you to choose your hike difficulty! Fifteen minutes away from downtown Santa Barbara a fantastic local gateway!

LOVE Skofield Park! They have huge, magnificent boulders that fell and left just enough space for even tall toddlers to crawl through! It feels like my new happy place in Santa Barbara. AND it’s right around the corner from the Rattlesnake trail which I have enjoyed before.

Super clean nice and safe area to get some recreation in. We parked and then did the rattlesnake trail. It's pretty easy going up until about the 2 mile mark and then finally gains some ascent. You really have to commit to connecting at the top of the trail to get to the top of the ridge for the views. Overall though really like the Rocky trail and as it was a bit technical.

Really nice and quiet area. Beautiful creek with some nice hidden places. Usually not too busy. There is a lot of critters which is important if you have a puppy or relatively hyperactive dog. Now that my dog knows where the squirrels and rabbits are it can get hard to leave play fetch without distractions
Incredible views of the mountains from throughout the park. Great paved main walking trails and additional dirt trails leading to massive rock formations. Peaceful and quiet. Free parking.

name: San Marcos Foothills Preserve
formatted_address: CA-154, Santa Barbara, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful sprawling mountain landscape with a mix of trails both improved and unimproved. Plenty of parking at the trailhead. No water or bathrooms. Dog friendly.

Need a quick hike and don't have time to drive up winding hills to one of Santa Barbara or Montecito's other trailheads? Then San Marcos Foothills Preserve might be the spot for you! There's a nice quick loop that includes enough elevation gain to get the heart pumping and the dogs (if you so chose to bring them), panting for water at the end of the excursion. Be weary of snakes who might snap at your ankles leaving you left for dead, especially since there's quite a few dead zones (phone-wise) in the preserve - at least if you have Verizon.
Great place for a walk or trail run. Beautiful views and lots of rabbits on the trails before sunset.
Beautiful hike. Primary hike during spring time because there is very little poison oak compared to say tuckers grove
Steep and muddy in places, we lost the trail a few times and needed to backtrack, but the view is spectacular!

name: Rocky Nook Park
formatted_address: 610 Mission Canyon Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Pretty park. Lots of individuals enjoying their Sunday by grilling food. We came for the park and the creek. The creek can be difficult to get to and wouldn’t be easy for individuals with mobility difficulties.
This is an incredibly beautiful park nestled right in the Mission district. It's serene and perfect for a short, easy hike.

Very pretty place for a dog walk. Easy to walk around. A bit crowded though and people drove really fast past pedestrians and there's some private property adjacent you need to be aware of. But otherwise it's very pretty and worth a stop if you're in the area.

Love this park. It's so lush and green, and it's nice to listen to the flow of the river. It's also very peaceful with very few people most of the time. It's a great spot to connect to oneself and get grounded.
Not your typical park, albiet beautiful in it's own right. Dominated by oak trees and large boulders. There is a playground but no other general open space or grass. Plenty of picnic tables and BBQs.

name: Gould Park
formatted_address: Cold Spring Rd, Montecito, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: Good place. Still weird about parking although the sign says parking open after July. Trail condition is actually way better than I thought it would be. Small water flow on 8/24
Beautiful views and a nice and challenging trail loop
Hiked cold springs trail with lots if climbing and creek hoping , spectacular flora and fauna.

Lovely panoramic views of Santa Barbara. Popular with locals when water is running. Pools and waterfalls. Dogs, too, on leash. Evidence of the last fire everywhere (charred trees) and sobering. Lots of well maintained trails.
Beautiful hike with beautiful views and amazing waterfalls!

name: Inspiration Point
formatted_address: Tunnel Road, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: I've hiked up to Inspiration Point from Stevens Park twice. It is a nice hike with very beautiful views at the top. Shade is plentiful on the trail, except for the last half-mile or so. Also, there is a creek that you will need to cross a few times, and the trail can be muddy in places, so I recommend wearing proper hiking boots or shoes that you don't mind getting muddy/wet.

Great place for hiking.
Enough trees and plants around, so I don't need to worry about the sun. There are also a few creeks along the trails.

Highly recommend!

Classic Santa Barbara hike. Outstanding views along the entire way, especially when you get to Inspiration Point itself. First quarter of the hike is fairly easy, slight incline, but on a paved road. After crossing the bridges, terrain is more traditional. The hike itself is moderate difficulty. Heart and breath rate will climb a bit, but nothing extreme. Best to come on a weekday or early morning to grab parking and avoid crowded trails. Highly recommend!

Easy to moderate hike with plenty of vistas along the way.  Midway through the hike is a tree-shrouded Canyon that offers a relaxing and tranquil experience before the steepest part of the ascent to the point where all your effort is  rewarded with  spectacular views of the sweeping costal mountain range, Santa Barbara, Pacific Ocean and the Channel Islands.

A little less than 4 miles total to/back Inspiration Point from Tunnel rd trailhead.  First mile was open fire/paved road, then then 2nd mile was single-track with surprisingly quite a lot of shade (75%), so even though it’s a bit steep, it’s not actually that difficult even for kids.  The view at top is sudden and sweeping, you know you’re nearly there when you near the power line towers.

name: Tuckers Grove Park
formatted_address: 4800 Cathedral Oaks Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93111, USA

google_reviews.text: At the end of the parking lot up the canyon a ways there’s a beautiful nature trail that heads out from the dog park. It’s well shaded and great for people, dogs, and skilled mountain bikers alike. After rains the creek is full and babbling away. Went climbing over the boulders and rocks with my kids and we all had a great time!

Beautiful park, lots of shade for activities. Water streams. Dog park and there's actually a place to give your dog a bath. The machine charges for that. It has options for the wash. Just a really nice place.
One of my favorite paths to take my dogs for a walk.

I have been coming to Tucker's Grove since I was a little kid. This park has always been my neighborhood park. I have been to weddings, birthdays, and gatherings here for years. The park is big. There are all kinds of great serene spaces. There are horseshoe pits and a playground area. Loads of picnic tables and areas. You can rent spaces or just set up. In the back, there are trails and water in the creek. It's picturesque.  Loads of free parking. During Covid, we used to come and hike, it was always pretty empty.  Not now though.  We had a nice area near the horseshoe pits and the playground.  It was a perfect day, kind of hazy and around 72 °.
If you're looking for a place to meditate, picnic, read a book, have a day hike or just gather with friends, check out Tucker's Grove.  It is located where Turnpike meets Cathedral Oaks (4800 Cathedral Oaks).

The dog park is in the very back of the park. Follow the road until it ends then walk to the end of the side walk.The Dog park is what I visited this park for. It doesn't have much grass and for the most part is fine dirt. It does have water available for the dogs. And there is a dog wash machine I have never seen before. But sure looks cool, it's by the restrooms. The park itself seems like a fun place to walk and bbq during the winter months. Play grounds are scattered throughout.

name: Cachuma Lake Recreation Area
formatted_address: 1 Lakeview Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: This campground is really nice and reasonably priced! Several lake front sites (no hook ups) and many other partial and full hook up sites that are not far from the lake. Has a playground, pool, a store and decent bathrooms. They also have a restaurant and boat dock/launch ramp with boat rentals as well. Not a swimming lake however. We will definitely be coming back!

Beautiful and peaceful spot!
I had an amazing time here — the lake views are incredible, and the sunset was absolutely stunning. The weather was perfect, and it’s a great place for a relaxing BBQ with friends. Everything felt calm and refreshing, away from the city noise. Definitely one of my favorite places around Goleta! 🌅🔥💧
Hiking trails were great the sites as well. Showers clean and laundry on site. Loved the cafe and restaurant at the marina.

Beautiful scenery but a poorly maintained campground. Dingy bathrooms and three different showers took quarters and no water came out at all. Rude gatehouse woman rolled her eyes like it was an inconvenience to help us. Could be great but it's not.

I had a very disappointing experience with this service. The staff was unprofessional and unhelpful, and it felt like all they want to do is charge you more money. I waited far longer than expected, received poor communication, and when I tried to address the issue, no one took responsibility or offered a solution. Overall, the service was inefficient and frustrating. I would not recommend this place to anyone looking for reliable or respectful service.

name: San Antonio Canyon Park
formatted_address: San Antonio Creek Trail, Santa Barbara, CA 93111, USA

google_reviews.text: It is so green, lush and beautiful from the Winter's rains. The creek has a beautiful stream. If you walk up the hike path there are many areas you can stop in and dip your feet in whilst there is still water flowing.

Great space for dogs. Fenced area, but if you have a dog that jumps fences, the fenced are easily jumped as they're only about 4.5-5 feet high. Lots of balls in the park too if you need something to throw around. lastly,  there is a faucet with a big bowl of water for the doggos!

This is a lovely, easy trail for a relaxing walk. The trail is mostly shaded by oaks and follows to the San Antonio creek. There are several creek crossings on the trail. The beginning of the path goes past the dog park, and continues on to end at highway 154. The whole trail took me a very leisurely hour one way. It probably could be done in 40-45 mins, so ~1.5 hrs total, if you're walking at a brisk pace. I took these photos on 4/5/16, and there are lots of beautiful wildflowers and green scenery. Gorgeous!!
I enjoy this fairly shaded walk/run that passes thru the creek several times. Great picnic & BBQ spots, kids playground, and a dog park in the back.

A great little park to get away from civilization not far from Goleta or Santa Barbara. This is a county Park connected to Tucker's Grove Park (which closes at twilight every day). There is a dog park, some picnic areas, and a 1.5 mile trail that follows the creek up to the 154 (where there is another access point). The trail is usually well kept, not very strenuous, and very relaxing when the creek is flowing. Be careful of mountain bikers on the trail. Dogs should be kept on their leashes.  Please be sure to keep the park clean by not littering!

name: Santa Barbara Shores Park
formatted_address: Hollister Ave, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful sights and views at the end of groomed trails. So many walking/hiking options with gorgeous views of the water and Bluffs at the end. Worth the trek back to the beach to hang out for the day.  I could have spent the day here, but was not prepared as thought it was just a hike out and back.
An absolutely incredible park. So many trails, so many amazing views. Genuinely was blown away by this place.

Great place to site the moon for Ramadan or Eid. Relatively easy trails but be careful of damage caused by winter rains.
No bathrooms.

While this spot is beautiful, I was so sadden to see the beach covered with globs of tar, no doubt from the oil refinery just off shore. The smell of the tar was awful but the fact that my dogs paws were covered in the stuff is probably the worst part. So sad to see an absolutely beautiful beach ruined by humans..

Wonderful for dogs (must be on leash) and children. We spent several hours playing on the plentiful eucalyptus trees and making forts and other “make believe” games with the kids. The hike down to the beach was absolutely breathtaking- maybe it was 2 miles there and back to the parking lot. Take water and snacks and charge up your phone for picture.

name: Cathedral Peak
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: I have seen this peak from the parking lot for more than a year. I try going there 3 times. Finally made it. It was very difficult not for the faint of heart. I am so grateful to have made it all the way to the top. This rock has been calling me for almost 2 years. I made it. Thank you to everyone who had been there before me. If you try this trail, make sure that you are in excellent shape and have enough water and time. Enjoy the path.
Hiked the peak from La Cumbre, not from the bottom.  Trail is somewhat crazy.  Very steep down and up.  Is very loose in places.  Nice destination, beasy hiking. hiking.
Quite a hard trail to climb, offers beautiful views of Santa Barbara. Can be extended further to climb La Cumbre peak - that part of the trail is easy to follow.
Hard hike (from inspiration trailhead). Bring a lot of water, electrolytes and snacks. I wasn’t prepared like I should have been but some nice hikers helped me on the way up.
Great hike! Was well worth the extra walk from Arlington peak, felt like nothing compared to the arduous hike to Arlington

name: San Marcos Foothills Preserve
formatted_address: 1155 Via Gaitero, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful sprawling mountain landscape with a mix of trails both improved and unimproved. Plenty of parking at the trailhead. No water or bathrooms. Dog friendly.
Noisy hot and Dusty. No bikes. No dogs, no fun claims to be some sort of preserve and yet most of the grasses and shrubs are non-native weeds. Probably Santa Barbara's least appealing open space
Awesome trail with varied terrain. Excellent workout.  Nice vistas of the mountains and the sea.
Gorgeous sweeping views from the Santa Barbara foothills. Great place to hike. Bring your dog.
Great Natural Preserve so close to town! Lots of little trails beyond the dirt roads make it fun to explore and get some good exercise. Also spectacular views at Sunrise and sunset.

name: Carpinteria Seal Sanctuary
formatted_address: 5669 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Really cool to see the seals.  No pups when we were there.  We took a route which we had to walk across the railroad tracks to access this area but it looked like there were better ways to get there.  Worth the walk and we took the hike down to the beach (not where the seals were) which was also really cool.

cool little stop on the way to our next destination. if I wasn't with kiddos, I would lace up some running shoes, snacks and a cold one and just let the trails take me to a cool spot to camp out and soak in the views. the actual sanctuary is just a small little perch you can view the seals and their pups from afar. there are other spots along the trail you can be super close on the cliff and view other seals swimming and playing in the general area as well. if you want to head to the beach too, there is a spot on the left side of this, although we didn't go but saw others and their dogs running on the beach. it's rocky so it's not a swimming type of beach, but just chill vibes.

Awesome to see sea lions in natural habitat. We are from Ohio so it was a new sight for us! Love that they are given this space as well. The trail is also nice to walk on and we enjoyed that on the way to the sanctuary!

We happened to meet one of the volunteers while we were there, and he shared a lot of great information about the seals and the area, which made the visit even better. The park is beautiful and well maintained, with an easy, beginner friendly walk that’s perfect for families. Peaceful, educational, and absolutely worth visiting. Highly recommend.

We had such a wonderful time at the Seal Sanctuary in Carpinteria! The secluded beach is perfect for a peaceful family outing, and we loved exploring the area. Our kids had a blast searching for seashells and watching the seals in their natural habitat. It’s such a fun and unique place to connect with nature. The short hike to the beach added to the adventure, offering beautiful views along the way. We could easily imagine this as a perfect spot for a secluded family photoshoot—picturesque and quiet. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a relaxing day out in nature!

Heads up you have to cross an active train track, hike a bit, but well worth it!

name: Lake Los Carneros Park
formatted_address: N Los Carneros Rd, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text:
This small lake is an absolute gem, perfect for a quick escape into nature. The walking loop around the lake is well-maintained and offers a serene stroll with lovely scenery at every turn. Ducks and various other birds are plentiful, adding life and charm to the experience.

It’s dog friendly - dogs must stay on leash. Your furry friend will love it and the trail around the back of the lake is a really nice natural path that winds its way back in a loop. It’s the perfect little outing to refresh your mind and connect with nature. Highly recommend!

What a nice and peaceful place to walk or just sit and enjoy. Many people come to hang out or to exercise. The lake landscape is gorgeous and the ducks are super cute. We parked in the small lot next to the fire station and train museum and walked along the wooden gate. Just followed the pathway in the grass that's kind of paved. A big plus, dogs are allowed.. yay!

Excellent park for a stroll. Views of lakes and mountains as well as wildlife.
Attractions available for children include a train, tractor ride and petting zoo.

Comfortably walk around the lake in 1 your but two hours to really enjoy yourself is recommended.

I don't believe bathrooms are available other than a portable restroom.

On Saturdays and Sundays there are a few small museums available about Goleta history.
Wonderful stroll for bird watching and those who just want to enjoy a nice day out. Several trails, one main paved path, and a central lake. No you cannot swim. Yes, you cannot swim. No swimming.
Beautiful park with great views of the mountains. Parking is a little tricky as there’s not really a designated lot but rather multiple entrance points in neighborhoods. A great place to walk!

name: Lookout Park Summerland, CA
formatted_address: Summerland, CA 93067, USA

google_reviews.text: Such a cute park with amazing views! I greatly appreciate the dog washing station ($10 for 10 minutes of washing/blowdry - but soap is provided in the machine!). Super nice after my dog rolled in the sand at the beach below!
Nature at its best on the Trail down to the beach. Beautiful flowers and and trees along the way.  Lovely beach with few people.
Lovely little park with picnic areas, play area for kids, ocean views, walking path to  the beach & restrooms. Easy on-off for bathroom stop off the highway too!
This park is so beautiful. Never seen a dog shower in a park! It was nice and clean and great views
Gorgeous clean park with nice bathrooms with soap and dryer and even a doggy wash. Dog friendly. Playground is very nice with lots of climbing equipment.

name: Honda Valley Park
formatted_address: 961 Miramonte Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: This place had an entrance at the end of a dead-end road. It’s got another entrance point at the top of the hill that I noticed as I was hiking on the trail.beautiful view once you get to the top of the steep hill/Short hike. Dogs loved it, bring water.I didn’t see a bathroom or benches it is only An open space with some trails
I LOVE RIDING MY EB VF1+ IN SANTA BARBARA HONDA VALLEY PARK 🌳🌳🌳

Recently went to Honda Park for the first time after the rain in January with a friend and our dogs.  My dog ended up with 8 ticks but my friend who has an larger dog ended up having ticks all her body.  Not sure if it was because of the recent participation?
A small, rarely-used, slightly hilly park. No amenities - just some serenity in the middle of a residential area in Santa Barbara. Picture is a bat box in the middle of the park.
Epic sunrise to sunset, expensive real estate for the big bailers!

name: West Beach
formatted_address: West Beach, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: What a great little beach. We were at the beach for the day and we loved it. A close walk from the train station, and plenty of room. There is a nice walking path up and down the beach, as well as multiple recreational spots that include volley nets and children playgrounds. There are bathroom facilities nearby as well however they have no doors on the stalls. So many restaurant choices within walking distance. They have a pier that has a wonderful lookout to the ocean and fishing access. We will definitely go back again.

Beautiful day today. Waves were pretty meh, but this beach is gorgeous and in the heart of Santa Barbara where State street meets the pier. Check it out or go for a walk along the beach path lines with Palm trees.

Great beach in Santa Barbara surrounded by mountains, palm trees and Pacific Ocean 🌊🌊🌊 nice place for a walk, stroll or just enjoying the ocean. Not crowded, but lots of people running, biking and walking their pets. There are also clean restrooms in the walking distance from the beach.

This spot is a favorite for both visitors and locals, with tons of fun vibes. People love working out on the beach, which is long, spacious, and perfect for hanging out. There’s plenty of parking, and the whole area has that warm, laid-back California energy.

Nice beach and pier.  Lots of people, but doesn't feel too crowded. There are a couple restaurants on the pier and a big parking lot as well.

We would come back if we were in the area.

name: Mesa Lane Beach
formatted_address: Mesa Lane Beach, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: A very picturesque place with stunning views of the ocean and hills. Perfect for a peaceful walk, relaxing, and enjoying nature. Whether you’re looking for a quiet place to relax, take some photos, or simply connect with nature, this location has it all.  Highly recommend visiting if you’re in Santa Barbara.

Conquered these steps June 2020. A fun activity anytime if the day. Highly trafficked.

Steps are the best workout.
450 round trip, give or take.
Flat landings every 25 steps.
Steps. Plenty of photo ops.

Steps lead to the beautiful beach. All ages and activity levels.

Beautiful beach, with many walkers and local pups enjoying the sunshine. A few surfers were riding the small ripples that came in this morning at low tide. There is NO handicap access or parking down the many, MANY stairs. General parking is in the neighborhood, near the the top of the cliff stairs.

This is a local's beach, and it is truly one of the finest strands in the entire world, (my favorite anyway)
It is a semi remote and very beautiful and magical place. There's an unsurpassed view of the purple channel islands and the perfect bluegreen Pacific Ocean and pale blue sky above the turning waves of foam. Not bad, for perfect.

With that said, this beach is a gem, and I don't want too many people to come visit so....

BEWARE... There are gigantic man-eating sabertooth bats that come out at night and steal people to feed their young. And of course the sea monsters are everywhere.

Fantastic beach! The steps leading down are very beautiful, and the beach isn't crowded at all. Be aware that at high tide, the water may completely cover the beach. We went at low tide and there was plenty of room and dry sand.

name: 1000 Steps Beach
formatted_address: 1429 Shoreline Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: Love easily accessible it is to the beach with just climbing down the stairs, that are not many and not steep. Parking can be an issue as it's int he residential area and is a culdesac. But the views itself is worth a visit.

This beach is absolutely beautiful. It’s a special spot to watch the sunset — the colors are just stunning. It’s never too crowded, which makes it feel peaceful and calm. If you’re lucky, you might even spot a sea otter playing in the waves. I think that’s what I saw — could’ve been an otari, but pretty sure it was a sea otter. Either way, it was magical. The whole place has a quiet charm that’s hard to beat.
This is a coolest beach along the shoreline park and has one of the beautiful sunset view from the steps.
Such a beautiful place! Nice people around and Dog friendly! 🩵🐾

We discovered this Santa Barbara beach as a result of a recommendation from a hotel clerk. It wasn’t the easiest to reach, but it was totally worth it. Located at the end of Santa Cruz Blvd. off of Shoreline Drive, you can park for free on Shoreline Drive and just walk down to the stairs. Don’t let the name fool you though, there are only about 250 steps to get to the bottom, but wear a good soled shoes because it does get slippery on the steps the farther down you go. You should also check the daily tide before you head out because during high tide you can’t even get down there. We went at 11am which was low tide and you could easily bring stuff and enjoy a few hours as high tide was not expected at 4:23 that day.

The beach itself is a bit unique as the photos will show. Not a ton of shells, but loads of unique rocks! And so many lobster and crab shells so clearly it’s a good location for predator food! The sand was ultra soft and its even, making it extremely walkable which is what we did!

Yes it was a bit tiring going back up the stairs, but you can take your time and we thought it was worth it for the stunning views and unique opportunity to walk down the cliffs and check out a gorgeous beach with very few visitors! We will definitely be back and plan to stay longer next time!

name: East Beach
formatted_address: East Beach, Santa Barbara, CA, USA

google_reviews.text: The beach is beautiful, with melodious wave sounds accompanied by seagulls. You can capture beautiful moments here.

Santa Barbara beach is a superb long sweeping stretch of sand. It is well maintained and a peaceful place where locals and tourists clearly like to spend time whether that be during the day or, catching the sunset.

Lifeguard stations are dotted along the beach as a precaution for any unplanned incidents..!

A nice place to visit or simply wall along if visiting Santa Barbara.

East Beach was great for taking a walk and enjoying the scene. The sand was clean, unlike some of the other beaches in the area that leave your feet covered in a sticky, black tar. There was plenty of parking and easy access to the beach.

Beautiful beach adjacent from West Beach. East Beach seems to be calmer and quieter than West Beach in my opinion. Been to Santa Barbara many times and this part is absolutely brilliant. There are a few hotels /motels across the street on Cabrillo. Volleyball courts are available,  the parking lots for the beach are $3 an hour per car. The Zoo and historic cemetery are are just a few blocks away. Definitely would recommend a stroll or bike ride this way!

Stunning stunning stunning beach! Especially for sunset. We were walking distance from our hotel so this was the go-to beach while in the area. Soft sand, clean coastline. Highly recommend checking out East Beach!

name: Sands Beach
formatted_address: Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text:
Yes. Sunset is very visible here.
I was upset that mountains were in the way around this side of the coast. But we found sands beach and it was marvelous. Sun setting over the mountain over the distance was a beautiful way to experience this sunset.

Memorable beach walk at low tide. We were practically the only ones except for the Snowy Plover which, as the signs say,  are endangered. Its one of the few Santa Barbara beaches where you can walk for miles without worrying about a high tide.
There is a bluff walk above the beach which must be spectacular. Parking was remote and confusing but keep heading toward the end as Google maps shows and there is a metered lot there.
Definitely a hiking highlight in the area.

Coming from a Chicagoan, this beach is so pretty, clean, and full of life with the occasional surfers and students. Understandable why. The sunsets here emasculate!! 🌅

If you’re ever in the area visiting or residing, please visit at least once to experience the sunset here, and if you’re lucky, you can see stars as well!

A very nice beach, but beware of the oil spills that have been accumulating there lately.
I don't know where it comes from but once you step in butter will help to remove the oil from your feet or from the shower.
Perfect sunset!! Not too crowded but there’s a bit of tar. Parked somewhere in UCSB housing area and walked a bit.

name: El Capitán State Beach
formatted_address: El Capitán State Beach, California 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Very beautiful beach, with a lot of great opportunities to take pics.

Beautiful park & campground. Plenty of sites open in the off season. I thought the rates were a little high for winter & no hookups at all. The female camp host was very rude about us parking our truck in one of the 50 open sites. She could have been a bit nicer, we were happy to move it. It was fine for one nights stay on our way up the coast.

This beach was recommended to us by some family friends so we made the drive up.  The beach itself was not too crowded and had some beautiful views.  There were lots of rock areas to explore.  Upon leaving we discovered this beach was covered in tar and so were our feet!  The lifeguard recommended using sunscreen to remove it.  We went to the parking lot where the showers were not working and the bathrooms were in desperate need of repair and updating. After about an hour of rubbing sunscreen on our feet we finally got clean enough to drive back to our hotel.

At the entrance you need to pay $ 10.  It's not clear what you're paying for.  There is nothing special on the beach.  A toilet, a shop with beach paraphernalia and souvenirs, and one working lifeguard booth.  The beach is narrow, there is not enough space, plus if you go further there are a lot of stones and generally nowhere to lie.  We were on a weekday at 3 pm and there was practically no place.  I do not recommend this beach, just throw away 10$

Small rocky shore beach.

You can camp here, on the main grounds not on the beach itself.

Ten dollars entry allows you to visit all three beaches in one day with one pass (Refugio, El Capitan, and Gaviota).

Many trails to explorer.  There are tide pools on the beach and sycamore and oak trees in the campground area.

Great place to end day at sunset

name: Devereux Beach
formatted_address: Devereux Beach, Slough Rd, University of California-Santa Barbara, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: BEautiful walk from parking (about a mile .People are mostly friendly along the way.You Can  ride your bke from parking,there are racks to Lock itwith your lock.I was there Sunday it was busy not crowded.
Beautiful beach with great waves.  Hidden in plain sight. It's a bit of a walk from the nearest parking lot, but not bad.

Best tidepool beach. From nudibranchs to octopuses, sea stars and sea urchins and sea hares and so much more. So much diversity in this area. I love this beach. It's a sanctuary to hide from humanity and the cruelty of the world.
Beautiful place to visit.
Devereaux has got great views of the Channel Islands as well as Isla Vista and Campus Point. Like all beaches in the UCSB area, it’s beautiful and is imbued with youthful spirit.

name: Summerland Beach
formatted_address: Beach Access, Summerland, CA 93067, USA

google_reviews.text: This beach has so much potential but unfortunately there is still oil present. The smell is strong as soon as you get to the water. It is dog friendly off leash, but you have to be extremely careful not to let them step on the oil patties. The upper grassy park area is nice. There is also a dog wash station which was cool. The views from this vantage point are amazing. I recommend checking out Loon point further south as the oil seems to clear up there.

Summerland Beach and Lookout Park is located right off the 101 freeway.  Railroad track is present at the park entrance.

This is a dog friendly park located along the Summerland Beach. Access down to the beach is permitted via asphalt-paved step road from a lookout point. The admission-free park appears to be well maintained and features a paved parking lot with ADA accessible spaces, playground area for kids, grassy lawn area with picnic tables and benches, a volleyball court, restrooms, foot showers, paid dog wash area, and beautiful spots to view the beach and sunset.  The beach is clean and quiet with sandy and rocky areas.
Beautiful beach right between San Luis Obispo and Los angeles. A nice place to stop, stretch and use the restroom and let your kids run around a bit. And the view is fantastic of the ocean.

I love the Lookout Park by the beach. It’s well maintained with beautiful wildflowers and grapes, Volleyball court on the A side and ping pong tables on the B side, and picnic tables & BBQ grills throughout. Nice green lawn area, playground for kids, and foot showers to clean off the sand, as well as a special dog washing area!

The beach is clean and quiet with both sandy and rocky areas

Great spot for sunset.

They have tons of benches and picnic tables with exquisite views.  Clear day you can see all the Channel Islands.

BBQ grills, restrooms, and a self serve dog wash station!

Saw dogs running on the beach and have a pet station for poop bags.

Great spot just off the freeway.

name: Butterfly Beach
formatted_address: Butterfly Beach, Montecito, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: Cute little beach with beautiful views! Not too busy and plenty of parking along the street.

Was swayed by tiktok to go to this beach and it was beautiful but the water has tar in it that took a while to scrub off my feet and totally ruined my sandals. I’m new to the pacific coast beaches but it was quite unpleasant. Otherwise great views and free street parking.

Butterfly beach is a beautiful beach in Santa Barbara with some of the most best views of the water and many other houses/nice properties. It is definitely worth it to come walk/sit in this area at some point if you visit Santa Barbara.
Beautiful beach with free street parking. Some paved walking path choices and space on the beach to walk as well. A gorgeous place to see the sunset!

Relatively not crowded beach with easy access. We witnessed a wedding solemnization. My boyfriend and I spent all afternoon painting 🖼️!I totally recommend this beach for a chill time by the water. Very family friendly and very clean. I will be sure to return the next time I am in the area!

name: Oil Piers Beach
formatted_address: Oil Piers Beach, California 93001, USA

google_reviews.text: Public parking for access to Pier Shoals Public Beach.  But be careful how late you are out on the beach because it is not overnight parking and the signs that state this are hidden in the brush along side the roadway.  Not visible unless you look for them and you will get a parking ticket by the local authorities.
Hidden little beach spot on the way to Santa Barbara.
I wish the Oil Piers were still out there because the Sandbar at the end of the Pier made Perfect Waves almost all year round and us Ventura Locals Ran the Place & kept all the Kooks from going out.
Love this beach very much as it has a semi private feel to it. The only downside is the oil/tar in the sand that sticks to your shoes & feet.
Sandy beaches. Awesome at low tide.  Also one of my peaceful places.

name: Leadbetter Beach
formatted_address: Leadbetter Beach, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text:
Leadbetter Beach is an absolutely beautiful and relaxing spot in Santa Barbara. The beach is clean, spacious, and perfect for enjoying the ocean breeze, sunset views, and peaceful vibes.

It’s a great place for family time, walking, relaxing, and taking photos. Compared to crowded beaches, this one feels calm and well maintained. The views of the ocean and surrounding area are simply stunning.

If you’re visiting Santa Barbara and looking for a peaceful beach with amazing scenery, Leadbetter Beach is highly recommended. Definitely worth a visit!

Nice beach! This is a great beach in Santa Barbara. Nice sand, relatively calm water, plenty of paid parking, and bathrooms. There is also a cafe/restaurant right on the beach. That being said we slightly prefer the beaches west of the harbor.

Visited this beach for the first time, and was definitely not disappointed! The beach was clean, the water was nice, and there's a cute little cafe on the beach with delicious food. Would definitely recommend visiting!
Great beach to relax and enjoy the beach with my Nala B. Not too crowded, paid parking, nice shoreline.

I have been here a few times and have enjoyed it! Across from SBCC, leadbetter is a beach side park that has grills and a small restaurant on the beach. Good place to hang out! Parking is paid but is cheap and more private. FOR SBCC STUDENTS, if you get a student parking permit from the school, you can park at the school or leadbetter for free!!

name: Hendrys Beach
formatted_address: Hendrys Beach, California 93109, USA

google_reviews.text:
Best Dog Beach!
Restaurant is nice right on the beach. Food is excellent.
Parking is limited only draw back, however Parking on street is available if overflow is full.
Breath taking dog friendly,  beach cove! Not crowded. Dogs allowed to be off leash in designated area of beach. Small tide pools. Highly recommended!
I have loved this beach since I was a kid (our St Bernard loved it too) in order to enjoy it now I only go late afternoons when the weather is moody.... Still beautiful but not so crowded.

Beautiful beach! Maybe not one I’d lay / lounge at but a perfect beach for walking and especially searching for sea glass! We found the most sea glass we’ve ever found at a beach here. Also love how dog friendly this beach is! Would come again!!

Great beach area. The Boathouse has an  excellent happy hour. Lots of people at times, but you can also get lucky and see no crowd at all. Lots of shore to walk on depending on tides. Lots of rocks and shells to look at.

name: El Capitán State Beach
formatted_address: 1 El Capitan State Beach Rd, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Had a great time walking the short trail along the beachside. Its a small beach. Parking is $10, restrooms are decent. Shoreline is super pretty but tons of piled up beach wood, which is fine if that's a thing you enjoy. Two small picknick areas with with benches and grills. Lots of rocks, not a lot of rock free sand (which again, totally fine).

great campgrounds, perfect for families and nature lovers. We joined a group of friends to spend the weekend at El Capitan and we were happy with the location, cleanliness, water availability and overall site condition.

went along with group, was pretty secluded! went for 2 days and it was good. my group had a small part of the beach secluded that was connected to the campsite which made the experience 10 times more better! would go again definitely

Beautiful beach and well maintained amenities like parking lot and restrooms. The main issue though is the amount of oil pollution. We walked on the beach and came back with black, sticky feet. It can be cleaned off with butter and vigorous rubbing, but that’s quite a project.

Only spent a few minutes here to take some pics after a long road trip through California. Looks like a very nice park with outstanding views of the Pacific, though said views may be limited to a small number of sites. There was plenty of parking and overall seemed very safe, with on-site staff.

Olympic Otter

name: Arroyo Burro Beach County Park
formatted_address: 2981 Cliff Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: Very nice dog Beach. You can keep walking south for miles to find beautiful desolate beaches and caves. Dogs are able to be off leash. Parking is limited though so you have to get lucky to find a spot if arriving after the morning. There is a cool look out spot at the top of the stairs. That's where I took my pictures. I would highly recommend this place.
Great place to let your dog run free and be a dog. Easy access to the beach . Bathroom,  water fountain available.   Parking is limited,  but free

Great place!
Very good for families with kids.
There are bathrooms, beach showers.
The park has picnic area, grills, place for games.
Left side of the beach allows dogs. There is a self service dog wash.
It has a beautiful overview (the stairs to it are behind the restaurant). The restaurant is pretty good with indoor and outdoor seating with good view.
Free parking. During high season might be difficult to find a parking spot.
Dog heaven. My dog starts to cry as we pull into the parking lot because he’s so excited for this place! Have only experienced friendly people and wonderful dogs off leash.

Beautiful beach, we sat and saw dolphins going up and down.
Great waves for body boarding, I tried body surfing lol
Nearby parking and restaurant (awesome burger btw 🍔) plus a reasonable free public WC

name: Hammond’s Beach
formatted_address: Hammond’s Beach, Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text:
A beautiful beach day. Walking through the path: wow: many beautiful flowers; the creek; 73F. Perfect.
Easy walk on the beach till Butterfly Beach and after that opposite direction toward Miramar Resort. I enjoyed there was no crowd (Covid19 - 6 feet apart!) Enjoy it and stay safe.

There is a river mouth on this beach and it is ussually flowing dirty water into the ocean, me not knowing this at the time because I did not have local knowledge of this beach went swimming in the water and got very sick.  If there is water in the river then swimming at this beach is out of the question, I would recommend going to a beach like East Beach, Santa Clause, or Mesa Lane because these beaches do not have the same problem that Hammonds has. Enjoy!
Nice beach, great views. No parking but a short walk from Channel drive near the Biltmore hotel.
Best at low tide.
You can bring your dog to this wonderful beach (but you must bring doggie poop bags)

name: El Capitan Beach
formatted_address: El Capitan Beach, California 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Nature at its best!!!   Places like El Capitan Beach are why people love California.  It's so beautiful, magical and the perfect place to go for the day or camp.   We went for the weekend and had the best campsite where the ocean was our view and dolphins playing were our entertainment.   One day, while on the beach we took a secret trail and stumbled upon the most magical nasturtium garden!  I mean, it felt so magical I could have sworn there were little gnomes and fairies watching us!!   This place is beyond special!!!
Very nice park

name: Rincon Beach
formatted_address: Rincon Beach, California 93001, USA

google_reviews.text:
Shoreside parallel RV campsites operated by Ventura County Parks.

Great views on the Channel Islands.  Hard surface sites that are generally level with dirt/sand area outside your RV door. Services limited to: Trash Containers  & Portable Bathrooms (that needed service & cleaned when we visited in early December ‘25).

Sites 1-15 have a seawall with the rest generally boarded by large stoneworks at the high tide mark.

Rail line runs along the entire camping area and traffic sounds from US-101 should be expected.

Again, views are outstanding and the evening breeze was wonderful.

Come to Rincon Beach for a nice long walk and the ability to explore the pools when the tide is out. We found lots of interesting seashells and saw a starfish. The only issue is that the beach has a lot of tar that gets on your feet (easy to clean off) and sneakers (not easy to clean off).

It is beautiful here, no doubt. However, a short walk on the beach left us with tar all over our feet. It is hard to get off and not a fan of petroleum products stuck to our skin.
Positives- Great views of the sea and lots of dolphins 🐬

I recently visited this beautiful and clean beach. The scenery was stunning, and the cleanliness was impressive. However, make sure to bring a mat, chair, and umbrella, as there are no facilities to rent them. Parking is also very limited, so plan accordingly.

Great place for a beach walk and exploring.
There are tide pools and shells, and these blooby things called velella velella ( by the wind sailors ). Also watch out for oil in/on sand. I got a bunch on my shoes.
Hang gliders take off from the cliff up above
And there’s lots of drift wood to create cool sculptures. We also saw surfers and a seal This time we saw lots of these little blobby things with jellyfish type sails washed up on the beach called velella velella. Great way to spend two or three hours and it’s so big that it’s not crowded.
There are bathrooms and the Covered picnic area and the parking lot is up above on the cliff,. There’s also a paved path that you can take to come down to the beach.

name: More Mesa Beach
formatted_address: More Mesa Beach, Eastern Goleta Valley, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: All of the bugs and the sand critters made this a pretty un enjoyable experience other than that pretty great

What makes More Mesa Beach so amazing is how secluded and quiet it is compared to other beaches nearby. Clothing optional further north of the beach once you reach the bottom of the stairs. Truly a hidden gem.

I personally enjoy the solitude of this beach. Some rocks, some nude sun bathers, I think it’s all part of the rugged experience that is coastal California. Going left takes you to a more traditional beach setting with wholesome vibes (Hope Ranch beach) and if you go to the right you can pass the nude beach and eventually get to some beautiful open areas where you’ll see seals basking out on enormous rocks in the water. Locals have told me it’s called “seal” island. The walk between the More Mesa steps and Goleta beach is the prettiest most serene and untouched beach walk I’ve taken in Santa Barbara. I think it’s because there aren’t many people to disrupt nature.

Wonderful beach all around, but if you want something easier and more people try Goleta, Hendrys, or Leadbetter beaches.

Nice stroll down to the semi-secluded beach. One has to park pretty far off from the trail that leads to More Mesa Beach as the neighborhood has legislated for a lot of restricted parking close to the beach.

Google takes you to a wrong place. rich people bought all the property blocking most of the trails to the beach. Can't park there. Go to vieja drive to park your car and walk or ride bicycle to the beach. 20 min walk to the cliff, take stairs down to beach. tiny beach full of boulders. Not really suitable for sitting or bathing

name: Depressions Beach IVKC
formatted_address: Depressions Beach IVKC, Santa Barbara, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: My son absolutely loves coming to this beach since there's never a lot of people. You can find a good amount of shells just laying around if you want to collect some. Great place overall.
Beautiful beach accessible from UCSB campus. Park at a nearby lot for the school and walk there. Only downside is the throngs of students who you feel old for being there. Otherwise awesome
nice beach but you always get tar (something sticky black ish) on your feet

Beautiful beach not far from the University but very mellow.
Not a lot of people here, very soothing for the soul
I used to smoke weed here with my friend and scare high school kids who would come for campus visits. 10/10

name: East Beach (Santa Barbara, CA)
formatted_address: 533-587 E Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful beach with nice walking & biking track, grassy park in the middle, very clean shower station and restrooms . It’s nice whether you can sit down and enjoy the scenery or for doing some activities

It's beauty is priceless...a land like no other, my home away from home. Took some time to appreciate the gift that it is. I'm sharing this, so that, if you do come visit, please remember, it's not for sale, that you are visiting someone's home, mind your manners, please pick up after yourself and be safe for others.  ❤️ Thank you!
It rained the day I was and very windy. But very beautiful  beach
Nice open beach with a long path for walking or running. Some palm trees are close enough to hang a hammock. Many people bring their dogs here, and respecting the leash rules seem to be optional.

Nice beach, but the sea (at least the part where I entered the sea) was apparently drenched with oil, so when I came out, my entire body and clothes were full of oil. It took me hours to clean myself up and I had to throw away my clothes. Nowhere were any signs of this oil danger. I think it has something to do with the oil rigs you can see in the distance in the ocean.

name: East Beach Park
formatted_address: 1400 E Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to have a picnic, to enjoy the ocean! The waves are strong depending on the time or day so make sure to check it out before letting small children play in it!
Very nice long, white sand beach.
Beautiful spot for a night stroll asking the ocean.
Love this beach, especially during the summer when all the tourists come.
Even though we just lost 16 courts this year; it's still the BEST beach for volleyball in CA.

name: Hope Ranch Private Beach
formatted_address: Hope Ranch Private Beach, Eastern Goleta Valley, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: There are no private beaches in California. Friends, please visit and take this beach back for the people. The rich think they get to own everything but they’re really stealing from us.
This is Public Beach, walk or bike there from Arroyo Burro County Park. It is a nice place.
No such thing as private beach in California

There are no “private beaches” in California! They are prohibited. This is just an elitist exclusionary privileged attitude that I am well familiar with. This is a lovely wild beach, and thankfully the beautiful but excessive mansions above the cliffs are nearly invisible. Ride your bike here from either Goleta pier or Arroyo Burro Beach parks, or take the hike.

Private beaches in Cali work this way. The beach is public up to the mean high tide line. When its low tide then you have lots of beach to play with and can even put a towel or umbrella up within the high tide line... but when its high tide you're screwed and don't legally have much sand to work with. You can always walk, swim or boat to a private beach from a public access point and play in the surf.... laying around on the sand can be a tricker issue at high tide.Of course, even if you are within the high tide line, security guards/ranchers are going to try and bluff you and tell you its against the law to be on that beach.... know your rights, but of course in the middle of nowhere that at times will only go so far.

name: Rincon Point Beach
formatted_address: Rincon Point Rd, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: We were on the left parking lot. Short hike to the beach. Waves were calm, but big enough for kiddos to ride. Calm wind day with full sun. Perfect 4 hour beach day

Great place for beachcombers and surfers!
Tide pools are always fascinating and fun to observe! The shells and driftwood are a‘plenty too!  This is a popular spot off of hwy1/ventura hwy….especially in the evening. Parking lot with bathrooms available at the trailhead down to the beach.
When it's on one of the best serve spots in the world and also just fun to walk along the beach.

Right on the ocean 🌊 It was a beautiful day the beach was packed. Plenty of families and furry friends enjoying the sun. Several youngsters out surfing the waves. It's a small beach but was a nice stop to my travels so I could stretch my legs and defuse from the traffic.

Beautiful peaceful camping beach ⛱ and it's only located about 20min away from Port Hueneme.  🥰💕💕💕🙌
Free beach automobile, trailer/motor home parking fm 7am-9pm. Bbq all day n evening 😋 👌 😎 🙌

name: Bates Beach
formatted_address: 6713 Rincon Point Ln, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Free parking, clean restrooms and showers, and covered picnic tables. Nice walk down a long ramp to a soft sand beach with lots of driftwood; some of it used to make very creative structures. Watch out for small blobs of tar that may stick to your feet. Top tip: bring an old rag or washcloth and baby oil. That will clean off the oil. Be mindful of nude sunbathing beyond the long beach wall. It has historically been posted with signage.

This is a quiet beach for swimming, surfing and picnics, with an ample parking lot. Which is free...!
Restrooms are few, and although serviced regularly, they are not in good shape most of the time.
The parking lot is much higher than the beach, which does not stop the people from walking there with their carry-ons.
The seating area is quite large and accommodating. A unique place for family gatherings and parties.
Reservations may be required.
Overall an amazing hideaway, where you can have a peaceful beach experience.

Been coming to this beach since 1986 when I came with some friends and we went to the nude section.  The nude portion has returned and it is still great.  Long stretch of beach to enjoy the water, walks, and to play while nude. Wife and I spent two days here on the beach. Weather was great.
A little gem! Little crabs everywhere! It’s lovely there.
Beautiful.  Lots of beach to walk on. Free parking. Restrooms.  Playground. Nice swells. Picnic area.

name: Miramar Beach
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: One of the best beach in Santa Barbara for kids and adults to play around and to hang out for sunset.
Lovely beach open to the public. Good sandy bottom for most of it which makes swimming easy. Dog friendly. Home of the Miramar hotel.
Gorgeous, but expensive to stay at the hotel.  Fortunately, access is open so a day visit is a joy!  Enjoy the beach, bocce, food and drink.  Do check out my the "secret" lounge off of the lobby!
Small amazing beach
A beautiful beach, although a bit narrow. Good location. Peaceful and uncrowded. Perfect for a weekend getaway.

name: Padaro Beach
formatted_address: 2825 Padaro Ln, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

name: Ledbetter Beach
formatted_address: C879+XV, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice beach, but can get crowded during hot days.  Arrive EARLY if you want a picnic table.  Don't forget to pay for parking (not expensive,  but they write a lot of tickets)
Nice beach not too busy, great to bring the kids to and has a good cafe.
Great place, been here many times.
Very beautiful!

name: El Capitán State Beach Campground
formatted_address: Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text:
El Capitan State Beach campground is a beautiful place.
There are beautiful views of the ocean from many sites.
There's a very nice beach, walking paths and trails, with one that goes by a little stream. (Amount of water in it varies depending on time of year, and if there's been rain.)
There are very nice sized campsites where you are not on top of each other, and  are not too close together, like so many other Southern California campgrounds.
It's a nice place to get away from it all.
The restrooms near our site were out of order on our last visit here, but hopefully they will be fixed soon. They put up a temporary outhouse for now.
There is a cute little store with cute souvenir items.  It would be nice if the store also carried a few items that people often run out of or forget, but it's not that kind of store.

Great location and campground with stunning views over the ocean. However, we were on the upper campground and the PCH being close by, our spot was extremely noisy. Trucks passing by all night next to our tent… not pleasant at all.

The bathrooms were pretty gross, the drain in the showers did not work and there was toilet paper everywhere.

Also, there is a ton of raccoons in the park at night trying to eat people’s food so please be mindful and remember to put away your trash and food before sundown. Our neighbors didn’t…

We still had a great time but beware of the noise level, that was not a fun experience.

We camped in the upper section, site # 114. Very nice campground on the bluffs above the beach.  Walk and bike paths  make it easy to get to each section of the campground. Beach access is by staircases or paths. Rocky beach with drift wood on one end, open sandy beach on the other end of the campground. Enjoy mature sycamore trees on the nature trails. Traffic on the 101 and trains can be heard in northern campsites. Best sites have ocean views.

Great campground with lots of amenities. Bring QUARTERS for the hot showers. People were nice and wore masks.

Awesome camping experience with my dog. Definitely windy and cold but we were prepared. People were courteous and we had no issues with our neighbors.

Beautiful! 2 of the lots were open on first come first serve basis. Plenty of spots available on a Sunday. We got a nice spot in lot d, pretty private for the number of spots around. Great walking path right next to the campground. Coin operated showers. It was dry camping for $45, but the view was worth it!

name: Ortega Park
formatted_address: 604 E Ortega St, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: It's  a nice place to go to exercise or play any sports or go with families to have fun

Very spacious, but not fenced in because it is a literal public park, but people who have well trained dogs, play with them here all the time and they stay within the boundaries of their play space. Super nice.
Fact:  spending time in nature is good for you.

The slide is dangerous. Has a turn at the bottom my kids hit themselves on dangerously. Honestly meant for older kiddos. Bathrooms are easily accessible though which is nice. Park also stays pretty wet most mornings since it's metal.

This park was a blast to the past for me as I remember playing here as a kid! The park was large with a baseball diamond, basketball courts and soccer fields, an amphitheater, swings and a play structure. It has a great Santa Barbara flavor with murals. It is located on the Eastside near the Girls Inc building.

Pros
There is an awesome structure for the bigger kids (there is no sign, but I would guess the older 5-12 age range).
There are bathrooms in the center.
It was not busy in the morning hours.

Cons
There was really nothing for the littlest kids.
Parking was not easy to find during the day.
There were some homeless people in the park (though not close to the play area) if this would concern you.

name: Stow Grove Park
formatted_address: 580 N La Patera Ln, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Stow Grove is a great park Goleta. There are tall redwoods or pine trees that make you feel like you are in the redwoods national park. There are 2 large open areas for playing and game. There are multiple playgrounds, a baseball back stop, volleyball courts and bbq/picnic areas which are great for parties and events

It’s a small Park with many redwoods (trees).
This small “forest”,, / park smells so good.

You can walk around. Also there is a nice size picnic area.

I saw many acorn-woodpeckers.
Very quiet and peaceful park located across from LA Patera Elementary School. Restrooms are well kept and playground w swings for the kids. Dog welcome on leash. Wife open spaces to roam about!

A sweet park with lots of park benches and a little redwood forest that is next to the live oak so they're not going to live very long. Totally different climates silly people! Of course star loved it and she met a new friend!
Lovely, shady park with stands of redwood trees. Great place to walk your dog.

name: Willow Glen Park
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to take dogs
Nice and quiet.  Excellent for the whole family.

This is a cute little neighborhood park. There’s a small playground with slides and swings. There’s also a nice size green space for kids and dogs to run and play. It’s surrounded by beautiful trees on a quiet street. Lots of locals walk here :)

Attended a birthday party here.  Nice and clean. Kiddo had a blast riding his bike on the concrete and playing with the unique leaves of a certain tree there. Only drawback is the lack of a restroom. Other than that, absolutely great.
time and money re-seeding the grass to let it ho right back to where it was two years ago. Been coming here every day for over 20 years and its a great place to hang out or throw a ball around.

name: Girsh Park
formatted_address: 7050 Phelps Rd, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Wonderful community/neighborhood park and sports fields. The park is run as nonprofit to serve the needs of local residents. They have a number of baseball fields, soccer fields, restrooms, and picnic tables and a small community center, basketball courts, and a playground . The Goleta Lemon Festival is also held here. It is a wonderful resource for the local community and is enjoyed by thousands of children during the various sporting seasons.  They have free parking but it can be difficult to find parking during peak usage.
The field is of good quality.

Great location for a photo shoot.  Large and spacious area with all commodities you could want at a park.  Playground, soccer field, large parking lot, lots of green open spaces, trees, flowers, and nearby to the ice skating rink.

This is the best park in Goleta. It's huge and options for kids play arena, basketball, soccer, skate and just walk around. Onsite free parking is always a convenient option. There is a dedicated soccer practice ground adjacent to that, which requires prior booking. Adjacent shopping place is another attraction of this area. After a tiring shopping, you can just spend few minutes here for a fresh air.

I went to this park few weeks ago for the Lemon Festival!
It was a great community activity. Well organized, and the park facilities helped to have a smooth event.

name: Escondido Park
formatted_address: 1306 Flora Vista Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text:
As its name implies it is indeed hidden, a tiny little hidden gem. There's a playground for the little ones, a drinking fountain and a small walk leads you to a place to see some horses from. A great for little kids. On top of the hill there is a half basketball court and an amazing view of Santa Barbara Foothills in the mountains.
Only bad point is there is no bathrooms.
Great neighborhood park with a few short walking trails, a small playground, and half-court basketball up top. There are a 6-7 picnic tables, a drinking fountain, and few bbq's, but no bathroom.
Nice park with a small picnic area, only one grill, and a small basketball court. Good spot for relaxation.

This Mesa area park is shady for your warm summer-in-fall days. It has a 2-5 year old play structure, 2 toddler swings and a basketball court. It is located off Cliff Dr on Flora Vista Dr.

Pros
This park has lots of trees and is shady on a warm day.
The structure is toddler friendly.

Cons
There are no bathrooms.
Beside the basketball court, there is no play equipment for the older kids.
Nice little park. Playground equipment is geared towards smaller ones, perhaps 2/3+ yr. old. No restroom. Nice views from the basketball court at the top of the hill.

name: Bohnett Park
formatted_address: 1251 San Pascual St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text: Located deep in the heart of the SB Westside. Equipped with a public restroom, a playground, and picnic areas. Tends to attract unsavory characters who loiter along the split rail fence bordering the creek.

Clean and safe space with public toilets.
Not too far from a bakery that sells yummy goods and coffee. Good spot for a cloudy day
Very small park with a chair swing small Creek that runs through it and basketball courts and kids playground that is currently closed due to covid

The playground is good, but the park is ok at best and has the feeling of being gritty... Like maybe a place you wouldn't be surprised to find broken glass/40oz bottles or a used needle on the ground. There are regularly folks hanging out drinking booze (often juveniles) of one type or another. Overall, there are plenty of better options if you don't mind a 5 minute drive.
Awesome place to take our children to play and have family get togethers and bbq

name: La Mesa Park
formatted_address: 295 Meigs Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: Mesa Park in Santa Barbara is absolutely stunning. I’ve never walked on grass so perfectly trimmed — it feels almost unreal. The weather was incredible, and the fact that the park sits right above the beach makes it even more special. The views are fantastic, and the atmosphere is peaceful and relaxing. I was lucky enough to catch a SpaceX launch while I was there, and that was just mind-blowing. An unforgettable experience in a truly amazing spot.

Beautiful and quiet.  You will need to descend to get to the beach that has a rocky part but manageable - just be careful when walking.  We visited on 1/2/23 so the weather wasn't so great due to the pineapple express system that was moving through, but it was still beautiful!
The perfect park for families, with its great play area and structure. It’s right near the beach, too. Dogs can fetch and others can relax in the sun or shade.

Came here to get the family out of the house. Decent size park. Kids play place Currently closed due to covid. Nice view of the ocean. Clean and fresh air.  There is a parking lot here and street parking

Small, grassy park with small playground facility and good off-street parking. Great for family and for short breaks and walking areas...  Park lies between the Mesa shopping area above and an oceanside Coast Guard compound w/automatic lighthouse below along with a trail & pedestrian bridge across an arroyo to a beach-cliffside residential area on the other side. From there, continuing west ~6 blocks into it is Mesa Lane Steps, a Coastal Access point with stairs down the cliff, and continuing west again, beyond Mesa Lane itself, another ~5 blocks on top of the cliff is Douglas Family Preserve.

name: Chase Palm Park
formatted_address: 323 E Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text: What a perfect spot to relax, stroll, fly a kite, read a book or just be with nature. The sound of the waves, the expansive grass area with so much green, and of course...the people watching. First thing we did after arriving in town, was to stroll along this path and take in the sights and sounds while loosening up our joints!

The beach have a great view of the ocean,  palm tree lined and the pier.
An Art & Craft Show was running at the time.
Vendors were very nice.
