Is a great place to walking on the sand and having a relaxing time with sound of the waves, just be careful not to walk over the green algaes, your foot will end up with a slime and black substance that's hard to go away.
You will need water to apply on it (not Ocean Water).
I'll visiting back again in the future.
It have a very big playground and a very good environment but it does not have a swing,and kids love swings.
Nice park and beach. Beach has a bit of driftwood, but not a problem. Water is swimmable, surf was much lower than other beaches closer to Malibu. Plenty of parking available - $18 per day.
Very chilled and relaxed atmosphere. Had a picnic in the park which was very nice.

name: Hidden Valley Park
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Great small neighborhood park with a toddler playground, a few picnic tables and benches, but no bathroom.
Short lovely walk along the Arroyo Burro Creek which you can hardly see because of growth.  Liked the fragrance of being "in the woods."
Beautiful green landscape. A safe place for children to play. Parents can relax on the picnic tables or benches and allow their children to play freely
This is a great toddler playground, but there isn't as much for big kids. There is a nice path for scootering and biking (after walking them down the hill-it's pretty steep). No bathrooms.

Tucked away into hidden valley this park is a treasure. Good for little ones as the park is semi spacious and has nice playground equipment.  There is also a walking path next to the park that follows the river that is also splendid.

name: Orpet Park
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text:
Secluded and under-visited park on the Lower Riviera, located directly below El Encanto and Riviera Business Park. Orpet Park is divided in half by Alameda Padre Serra and has ocean views, a large lawn area and a few benches with BBQ pits.

Because Orpet Park is on a steep incline, it doesn't lend itself well to large groups and activities. It is however a great place for a picnic, a solo outing or a dog walk.

The vegetation upkeep has its ups and downs during the year, however the lower part of Orpet has a Jade Plant forest that is beautiful year-round.
I love walking dogs here. It is so convenient for my dog walking business on the Riviera 🐾💕 Jen’s Pet Sitting of Santa Barbara

Beautiful little park nestled in the middle of the American Riviera. The setting is the perfect place for a smaller fido to stretch his legs, enjoy a romantic shaded picnic, or to just relax and get away from the stresses of life for a couple minutes. A couple blocks away from the mission and the road it is on is a straight shot between there and the 101 Freeway North, so well worth the drive if you are passing through too.

I was in Santa Barbara 2 weeks ago. I haven't been to Orpet Park since roughly 1965! I thought it was right off of State Street. They haven't moved anything, have they? I remember it being very cool and green, a nice place to rest before the drive back to the San Fernando Valley. I will come back and add a star after I visit it again!

Very nice for walks and picnics. Not much to it, just grass and a picnic table. There are two grassy areas, one on each side of the street. Also, you are basically in someone's backyard. Would recommend for locals who want to enjoy a book on the grass or a nap in the shade of a tree. We just went at the end of July and the grass was surprisingly still very green. Last year it was pretty yellow with the drought. All good now!

name: Pershing Park
formatted_address: 100 Castillo St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice park for a softball game but they charge $10 to see the game in the stand.
Incredible views and a nice walk to the top!
Historical. Amphitheater listen to the music as you sit on the lawn under a shady tree. Street parking and a few homeless. Santa Barbara Foresters baseball ⚾🧢🍻. Views of sunset or marine layer.

This is a very nice park near the beach.  My visits here are for SBCC baseball games.  The facilities are well groomed and clean.  There is almost always a small contingent of homeless in the park during the day but I have never seen them bother anyone.

Update 5/2021:
Not what it used to be....hordes of tents, garbage and shoddy, parked vehicles populate this place in the evenings now...indicative of the lack of city policy and management for homeless. Probably okay during the day, but after hours there are probably far safer parks elsewhere.

I love this place for the tennis courts and proximity to hotels and other mainstays in Santa Barbara. There are lighted walking paths above the tennis courts.

name: Little Acorn Park
formatted_address: 901 El Embarcadero Rd, Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Charming little place to hang out with friends. They have picnic tables and benches for sitting and talking.
Well kept park with nice picnic tables. Perfect to  grab lunch close by, and enjoy the sunshine.
Cute lil park, adds to charm of the community. clean up the trash cans though!
Hold flea markets here on Saturdays
It's a park in the middle of the madness!

name: Evergreen Park
formatted_address: 7524 Padova Dr, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Safe and fun convenient right around the corner park
Super challenging disc golf. Some holes are  risky to play based on thick trees. Not too many long holes.

Evergreen Park has a challenging disc golf course,  the best in the county. It also has tennis courts, playing fields and a newer children's playground.  I walk around the park several times a week, being careful to not impede the disc golf  players. I'm  beginning to practice disc golf myself...it looks like so much fun!

Evergreen is a fairly large, very nice park.  There are tennis courts, a challenging disc golf course, lots of grassy areas to run in, quite a bit of forested area and a mediocre kids play equipment area.  There are porta-potties scattered around the park, sometimes clean and sometimes, not so much...

I go for the disc golf course located here.  Played the course for over a decade.  Some of the holes have changed due to course redesign by pros visiting the course and keeping locals happy involving lost discs flying in their yard.  It's always been a challenge but if you're good enough you can still score low here.

name: Eastside Neighborhood Park
formatted_address: 118 N Soledad St, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Eastside Neighborhood Park is a great little park for small children.  There is a small playground and a large grassy field.  There are also restrooms (that were quite clean when we visited on a Sunday morning).  However, I docked a star because the playground itself had a bit of trash on and around it and a swing was missing.  The park is also popular with homeless individuals — they tend to congregate near the restrooms.

We are lucky to have a house on the Eastside and walk through this park at least twice a day.  It's very shady which is a plus when it's hot as it isn't easy to find shade in a public area.  It's kept very clean and the restrooms have just been redone.  The murals done by Manuel are colorful and happy.  One can walk the dog through the park and meet many others doing the same.  On Sunday's, there are fun parties and lots of music.  There is playground equipment for children and I see at least ten kids a day playing with supervision.  There is a large public garden which is quite popular.  Since this area has alluvial soil, everything grows well.  We love this park and give it 5 stars.
Great park for all ages small park and a larger park for older kids. Beautiful large trees to sit under in the grass. Also a public bathroom (closed at dusk).
No seats. No tables. No bbqs.
Love this park for exercising!!! The machines they put out there!! Weight machines! There neat!!

name: Estero Park
formatted_address: 889 Camino Del Sur, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to hang out. People were playing basketball and there was a yard sale when i went
Not too bad for walk or play basketball
Nice park, nice people, nice dogs
Cleanest porta potty in the land.
The pump track is pretty fun

name: San Roque Park
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: This is a beautiful park with mature trees, nestled among homes in the San Roque neighborhood. It is so peaceful there with views of the mountains, and a great place to sit on the park bench, listen to the birds, and relax.

It’s a very small park. But it’s very clean and it’s tucked away in a quiet neighborhood not to far away from a bunch of restaurants on upper state st/de la vina st. Usually has a person or two cruising through but for the most part it’s quiet and few people. This means that it’s an excellent 5-star location to bring your snuggum-pie-honey-bunch for a romantic picnic date. Bring a blanket because there’s not really any benches or tables but other than that it’s perfect!

Ok not a big park. This is a triangle between streets. It's actually a pump station for underground water 💧.  It does have a disc golf goal(just one) but creative people can make a course(it's movable). Never meet a rude person here.
All I needed was a nice park bench, and some peace and quiet... and this small, residential greenspace came through. Perfect for meeting up with a friend. 🧡

Cute little park,if you live in the little rich area! Tried to walk our dog but,alot of there dogs were not on the leash and I was afraid that my dog would be bitten! If you don't live there don't go! Also no bathrooms! What kind of city has non-welcoming Park's?

name: Hale Park
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: Lovely park where dogs can run off leash. Clean and grassy with a well kept trail. Very little poison oak (only if you wander off the trail into the dryer areas) which is a big bonus for Montecito!

May 2023 there were a couple of fallen (or possibly purposely cut) trees blocking what seemed like the original pathway but then I noticed new paths going around the trees. Trail is easy level and short (might be challenging for smaller dogs). Earlier this year we had heavy rains so the grass is tall so pants and long sleeves and very sturdy shoes are recommended. Off leash ok inside park, ranch style fence at entrance that is right next to the road so I kept leash on until we were a good distance inside. Parking very limited. Enjoy!

Underwhelming, especially after looking at the other reviews! This "hike" most are referring to is a tiny loop that took me and my toy poodle about 10 minutes to complete (including pausing often to sniff). Two hawks were circling overhead as well which was cool but also intimidating for my tiny dog.
The entrance was not inviting, just a field, I actually drove past it not realizing that it was the park. Very limited parking, maybe 3 cars can fit in the dirt pull out.
Next time I drive out to a park, it will not be this one.
Meh... not much of a park, at least in summer. Mostly dried grass but better in the back. There are much better wooded areas nearby.
Wonderful park and dogs off leash doing dog things makes my heart soar.

name: Hilda McIntyre Ray City Park
formatted_address: 1420 Kenwood Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: I had my fifth birthday party there, I'm over 60 now. It's just a truly unique and beautiful place. The view of SB and are beautiful mountains is unprecedented. The live oak trees have been there since long long before I was even born. This park is historical, beautiful and family-oriented. A jewel.

A hidden gem. Hilda Ray is a great neighborhood park on a steep slope with a small parking lot, playground, picnic tables, bbq's, a group picnic site, and bathrooms. All of which comes with an impressive view of the Santa Barbara valley.
Nice little community park with an exceptional and unusual view of Santa Barbara.
This tucked away, not so known Mesa park has amazing city views! Great for bringing a picnic too.
Fantastic little park! Parking is good and bathroom on site!

name: Laurel Canyon Park
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: There are coyotes present at the base of the park. One sprung from tall grass toward my off-leash dog. I met a hiker who showed me how his dog was attacked three weeks ago by a group of four. Use caution!

Not a park.  A virtually unmaintained, gopher infested empty lot.  The city shows up once a year to mow the waist high weeds, but that's it.  When the neighbors try to set up play structures, the city removes them.  When the neighbors use the empty area to park, the city puts boulders in the way.  Nice view if you climb up the nearby hill, but watch your ankles on the way up.

Santa Barbara has some of the best parks I have ever been to. The old growth trees are amazing. I love hanging out with friends and family, relaxing in the shade enjoying the wonderful California central coast weather. I feel very blessed to live here. If visiting be sure to literally stop and smell the roses!

This is a neighborhood park with no amenities; there are gopher holes. There is a baseball backdrop but no play structure there's not really anything for kids to do. There is a short hike which is kind of fun but other than that it's not really worth a stop.
Beautiful and tranquil park with a nice bench at the top of the hill.

name: Los Robles Park
formatted_address: 4010 Via Diego, Santa Barbara, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: The fact that this shows as a park is crazy but lets assume it is, it got two dirty benches, grass is non existent, too many flies im assuming bc people use itas a dog path... the only good thing here are the two trees that provide nice shade.

It's such a little patch of nothing in a neighborhood full of overflowing trees. There are no trees in this little patch they want to call a park. The neighborhood is lined with shading trees that keep out the sun and heat. The most this patch is good for is just to sit down on the grass late in the evening.
A nice neighborhood greenspace, but no playground or play structures. Gophers have made lots of holes in the grass.
Wild grass, no playground or ball diamond. Maybe good for dogs? Might as well be used for more houses
Simple open space. No amenities
Such a sweet little park for all ages. Lots of shade for parents and many levels of toys for kids. I was there with some teens who had as much fun as little ones.
Simple neighborhood park with lots of open space, 3 swings, a slide, and 1 picnic table. No bathroom.
Love this park! I see kids from ages 1-15 finding fun ways to engage with the playground equipment. Nice open grass space surrounding the playground.

We would come here while camping at El Cap because they had great swings but this year we got here to discover they tore down the swings and put up a modern plastic play area for little kids. Probably a cool park for the under 7 crowd. Just a disappointment because the former swings were cool.
Awesome neighborhood park for young kids. Let em run.

name: Old Coast Park
formatted_address: 1414 Park Pl, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place to bring the children

name: Mission Historical Park
formatted_address: 502 E Los Olivos St, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Mission Historical Park is such a peaceful, beautiful and underrated spot in Santa Barbara. It's surrounded by history being right across from the Old Mission, with open lawns, old stone aqueducts, and pieces of early California history scattered throughout. The park is quiet and well-kept — perfect for picnics, walks, or just relaxing under the trees after exploring the Mission and Rose Garden. You can feel the history here, yet it still feels like a true neighborhood park. Free parking, easy access, and gorgeous scenery make it a must-visit for anyone exploring the Mission area.

It’s very beautiful park. Really enjoyed this park. It’s must go place. There are multiple sections of rose with different colors. You can easily spent 1 hr at this place. And going to enjoy different roses. Would suggest to visit at evening time to enjoy different sheds of flowers.
Fantastic park across from the beautiful Santa Barbara Mission. Great place to picnic, stroll amongst the rose garden paths, or just lay on the grass with you dog.

Beautiful grounds and rose gardens around the Old mission Church.
It's a must for sight seeing in Santa Barbara. The volunteers that take care of the roses will have a special place in heaven. Wow how beautiful. 😊

Side street parking across from the Mission admiring the majestic colorful roses.
Art gathering down to the right and on the left side a few young ladies preparing a celebration 🍾 of sorts with an elegant long ground table surrounded with peach pillows for the guests.

name: Alice Keck Park Memorial Garden
formatted_address: 1500 Santa Barbara St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text: What a Beautiful park so close to State Street! Wonderful paths surrounded by nature. A pond with ducks & turtles is sweet. The trees and flowers unique and so pretty. We discovered it in the Fall , and I can only imagine how much more beautiful it would be in Spring!! Perfect place for a picnic or stroll.

This is such a beautiful park. It’s small, but it has so many different types of wildflowers and trees. There’s even a pond with lots of mallard ducks, and some turtles. There’s a gazebo and several park benches for you to rest. Across the street is an enclosed playground for children. It’s quite large and clean.

Alice Keck Park Memorial Garden, located just across the street from Kids World Playground, creates the perfect balance for a well-rounded family outing. But beyond its convenient location, this park is truly one of California’s finest. Home to an impressive variety of lush, vibrant plants, it stands out even in a botanical paradise like Santa Barbara. What truly makes the garden come alive, however, are its serene ponds, teeming with wildlife. From turtles to ducks and geese, the park hosts these familiar favorites in remarkable abundance, adding a dynamic, natural charm to the tranquil setting.

We just had our wedding ceremony at the arbor area in June and it was magical. This park is so special to us and I highly recommend it. We had about 80 guests and a violinist and everyone commented on the beauty of the park!
Beautiful grounds! Peaceful and calm surroundings.

name: Gaffney Park
formatted_address: Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Splendid place to share a bite with a friend overlooking the ocean. Breathtakingly beautiful.

name: Ambassador Park
formatted_address: Burton Cir, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text: Small neighborhood grassy open space lined with palm trees. Beautiful view of West Beach and the mouth of Santa Barbara Harbor.
Nice a little park.
Visited this park for the historical site "Burtons Mound" (CHL #308).
Really nice patch of grass
Calm quiet and beautiful

name: Sylvan Park
formatted_address: 1321 Dover Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Really interesting park with what looks like the ruins of a cascading hillside water feature (even a tiny bridge!) but there’s no water now. Also one single lonely bench and a trash can. Street parking and the streets are narrow and hilly.
Beautiful town must visit a historic place
Very good view! But it's basically a dirt lot in the middle of some very fancy homes. Desperately needs some grass. Pretty good view of the fireworks for the Fourth of July too!
What is the history of this interesting micro park?  And what interesting things could be done to activate this sweet space, I wonder?
Small nice park located on hill side.. parking around may be tricky. Beautiful views of santa barbara can be seen from here

name: Cabrillo Ball Park
formatted_address: 800 E Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: I feel like the exercise equipment would get more use — by an order of magnitude — if it was installed on the ocean side where people walk. I love this type of equipment to encourage fitness, but i think it’s better placed where people are already regularly passing it.  I could be very wrong! Time will tell.  I am about to go try the upper body equipment but I’m a little leery because there’s a man sleeping right near it and, unlike on the ocean side of the street, I am alone here with him.  🤷🏻‍♀️

Very nice and clean, but bathrooms here are locked so don't expect for them to be open unless there is a game or event. There is exercise equipment here for use, and almost 0 homeless people, 1 maybe 2 but otherwise almost none. I think having the bathroom locked is a good idea because it keeps the homeless people away.

This park is cool. It has a baseball field, space to kick around a soccer ball, green grass for a picnic, it is right across the street from the beach and it has state of the art exercise equipment stations set up along the outer rim of the park. The only missing piece was that there were no trees, therefore no shade. Still, this was a fun, little park.

Santa Barbara did a great job revamping the fields.  Exercise structures look sharp.  Field is missing the previously abundant holes.  Restroom situation is still blocked off, but the quality of the field is exceptional.  I hope the integrity can be maintained.

A fabulous location to play organized softball! Great view of the Pacific beyond the outfield across Cabrillo Blvd. Recent upgrades have added exercise equipment & a walking path. This place is a gem in SB.

name: Sea Lookout Park
formatted_address: 6787 Del Playa Dr, Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful view of the sunset and Channel Islands from Isla Vista.
Excellent park that connects to an amazing walking path along the cliffs overlooking the ocean. Fantastic experience at sunset. Loved this place!
Small park. There were nearby porta potties. Not a place I would think of for a run, but definitely a spot for parties or a view of the beach.

Beautiful view! I was in town on business and walked the 1.5 miles (great walk) and took in the gorgeous cliff view of the Pacific. Despite the cool, rainy weather, it was a great area to enjoy natural beauty.
Great view.  Grab a coffee and get here!  Needs more benches otherwise it would get 5 stars.

name: Mathilda Park
formatted_address: 321 Mathilda Dr, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: It’s place for younger children

name: Armitos Park and Community Gardens
formatted_address: Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: I would give this park 5 stars but it has no restroom! My 4 star rating though is a high 4 because I realize potential of this park. I can put up a tent and sleep! It would be great if it is allowed. Playground needs to be taped off or taken out. No one should be on them due to covid-19.
This park has great climbing equipment and a structure for bigger kids with a slide and monkey bars as well as swings. There are no bathrooms here but it is never crowded.
Nice and clean!!
If it's fun you're after, this is the best place in the heart of Goleta. It's suitable for all ages, regardless of origin, color, or religion.

name: Carpinteria Community Garden Park
formatted_address: 4855 5th St, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful community garden! Ton of classes for beginner and advanced gardeners. Beautifully designed and well taken care of.
A nice community garden with many beds to see.
It's great beautiful

name: Oceanview Park
formatted_address: 100 Greenwell Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice spot to chill a few or walk along upper shoreline park. Can be full, so may need to use one of the other lots or parked along the streets opposite in the Mesa neighborhood. Some benches to sit nearby and can walk down to Ledbetter Beach if you like to go back uphill.
Nice little park across from Sacred Spaces. Saw 5 Hummingbirds.
Well maintained, almost always empty.  Great place to bring your lunch and take in the beautiful setting and ocean view.
Very clean fun place to walk dogs or hang out. Only thing is sometimes the waterfountain does not work.
My new fixation is to check out all the many parks along the coast.

name: Tarragona Open Space
formatted_address: 635 N San Marcos Rd #551, Goleta, CA 93111, USA

google_reviews.text: I love open public spaces. No matter how beautiful the house and the backyard you have, you should have a place to walk. The park is beautifully done, very clean at all entrances there are trash cans and bags for dogs. I liked the presence of benches, you don’t always want to sit  on the grass. Unfortunately, there is not enough fenced area for dogs, because sometimes a dog needs freedom for a walk.  In general, the place is wonderful, we need to do more of these small parks.

It was a decent spot and it looks like the history of it could go way back. It's a natural space so most of the following is expected: Unfortunately there are tons of ants everywhere and by the benches.  There's a palm tree that has multiple holes that are filled with bees.
This open space and grass area with paths is lovely for a walk. No playground equipment here, but plenty of room for the kids to run around.
I love taking a walk through these wide open spaces...there are great views to be seen and great trees to sit beneath.
This a green space for walking dogs with a short trail through it. There is no playground, but plenty of room to run and play.

name: Anisq'Oyo Park
formatted_address: 950 Embarcadero del Mar, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Love how it's never packed. It has a really cool dinosaur which my toddler loves! the play structure is pretty cool too. When I first started taking my son it didn't have swings installed but now it does! The best part would have to be all the ducks that live in the pond, they just casually walk around the Park trying to steal some food 😅

Beautiful park to walk around. Very clean and very calm. We sat down and had a relaxing time. We were at this park during the  students earth day event. Enough space to walk around and enjoy yourself.
Decent park with a nice duck pond, but it's not in a great place.  The park is pretty dirty and it smells greasy from all the college fast food places around.
I love the meals for the homeless offered here by the church. California's best hangout for the chronically homeless.  Can't wait to sleep there again.
We attended Halloween party in this park and it was well organised.

name: Sun Outdoors Santa Barbara
formatted_address: 100 El Capitan Terrace Ln, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: I have spend approx 10 days within 3 separate stays at Goleta location. Service has been nothing but perfect. Place has always been clean. Staff have been respectful and location is lovely. Only reason it gets a 4 start instead of 5 is WiFi is terrible (all 3 stays). They really need to fix it. This last time which was late Feb 2026 it was so bad I couldn’t even google anything. Will change to 5 Star once WiFi is fixed.

With a per-night rate of $160 we expected an RV Resort but experienced a standard campground.  Sites were generally level and while there is vegetation between the sites you are not widely spaced out from your neighbors.

Bathrooms are separate from showers.  Showers were warm at best (not hot) and lacked effective ventilation. Expect considerable road noise from US-101, earplugs should be considered.

Trash containers were very convenient to your site. Staff were friendly & courteous.  Our site did not have ocean views.

This is a nice little RV park with level concrete parking pads. Spaces are fairly close together but nice vegetation exists to separate the spaces. There are a number of tent sites available as well. The location overall is very close to 101 but there are some raised earthen sound barriers that keep the highway sound down pretty effectively.  The pool and jacuzzi are nice and all common areas are kept clean.

We loved our stay at Sun Outdoors in Santa Barbara! We pulled in late Friday night and people were up, not being loud, but just hanging out and having a good time at their sites. It just felt really nice and chill.

Our tent site was up against the meadow and the views were insane.

The bathroom facilities were clean and the showers were hot and free! I hate paying good money for a campsite and then having to fork over quarters for a shower as well.

Kids had fun and so did the adults!

10/10 would recommend!

We are currently at the campground. On our 3 week trip, this is the most expensive campground. When we arrived they told us that the beach was closed because of maintenance. We could go to another beach 3 miles further down the road…. I know that maintenance is necessary but tell me in advance so I can decide if I am willing to go or not., at least put it on your website or whatever so people are informed! The price is too high for that kind of service.

name: Outdoors Santa Barbara Visitor Center
formatted_address: 113 Harbor Way, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: So many people including locals don’t know about this lovely spot for which you can get the best view of the harbor and mountains. It’s free and it’s on the fourth floor. When the elevator opens, there’s beautiful ocean themed tiles in the floor and walls with all kinds of information. There’s also a staff person who is there to answer Ny questions. The best part is to go out onto the balcony with comfy seating and look at the beauty of Santa Barbara.
Small but very friendly visitor center with one of the best views of the harbor and Santa Barbara. Worth stopping up here just for the view.
Santa Barbara wharf vibe
Nice historical place
It was a really good place

name: Santa Barbara Adventure Company
formatted_address: 520 E Haley St Suite A, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Amazing day. We were very fortunate to observe lots of migrating marine life.   Gorgeous island, and our guide Jeremy was so passionate about conservation, marine biology and the history of the island!

We did the 3 hr Sea Cave Kayak Tour in early Nov and had an adventurous time! Cullen was a fantastic guide: funny, knowledgeable, made us feel safe/prepared and pointed out all the cool features along the journey. We booked our tickets pretty last min but there was availability since it's the slower season. We booked our ferry tickets separate but you can do it through the guiding service too. They provide splash guards, wet suits, helmets and all the kayak gear. At this time of year, I recommend a splash guard to keep your core drier and wearing shorts as most of the tour is in the shade and you are going to get wet as the water was more choppy. Definitely bring a change of clothes to change into after the tour so you're not wet and cold on the ferry ride back! They have changing rooms and outdoor fox proof bins to store your belongings while on the tour. We saw lots of cool sea caves, pelagic birds and kelp beds.  Cullen provided adventurous and chill options to go through or around sea cave features and my favorite challenge was going through the Marge Sea Cave. I also wasn't expecting it but we saw the small foxes too! If you're up for the challenge, it was a fun and interesting way to explore the Channel Islands in Nov but would probably recommend to go in the summer when the water is more calm/warmer.

We used this company to book our ferry and sea kayak adventure tour on Santa Rosa in the Channel Islands. They were excellent to deal with. Very thorough and communicative with instructions, what you need to pack, etc. they even have a good video to help.

Our guide Carly was amazing. She was so knowledgeable about the area , the wildlife, the caves and kayaking. She was so respectful of her guests. We don’t get those type of guides often! Thank you Carly.

I highly recommend this company

We took a Sea Cave Kayak tour last week with Santa Barbara Adventure Co and we had the best time!! Our guide Luke was the BEST and even though I was nervous, he was patient and encouraging. He had great stories and told us a lot about the area and wildlife as well. It was so much fun! Definitely recommend!

Dustin was a fun and knowledgeable guide. He gets 5 stars. Won the lotto on the most perfect weather. Expensive experience? Yes. Worth it? Absolutely. I think this is the best way to see Channel Islands NP. Take an early or late ferry to make sure you have time to do a little hiking while you’re there.

name: Coronado Butterfly Preserve, The Land Trust for Santa Barbara County
formatted_address: Coronado Dr, Goleta, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: I enjoyed a leisurely walk through the Coronado Butterfly Preserve in Goleta during a trip to Santa Barbara in late February 2025. Its trails lead to the coastal bluffs and Goleta Monarch Butterfly Grove at Ellwood Mesa. When I visited, the area had recently flooded and it was a bit too late to see the butterflies. It's a very beautiful area full of eucalyptus trees and great place for a leisurely hike.

Wow, what a beautiful, breathtaking place. You can literally spend hours here. It's perfect for kids and dogs. We did miles of walking on the oceanside trails and then went into the forest area to see the monarch butterfly nesting areas. There's a designated area to go sit and observe. Such an awesome place to visit. Street parking but plenty.
Really good i recommend to take a few hours to check all the trails enjoy with your family at the shore you can see some dolphins and maybe more the sunset its really amazing in this location

Nice place to take
A hike and enjoy the beauty of Santa Barbara. It's a great walk, and the trails are not too hard to handle.
Great place for butterfly spotting during Nov, Dec, Jan. They are plenty and all pictures taken on 23rd Nov 2022, they are just showing ip.

name: Modoc Preserve
formatted_address: Modoc Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful hikeing area, lots of planted flowers with info signs. Nice little slice of nature.
No entrance or parking.
This is the best work commute anyone could ask for!
Beautiful views, great place for dog walking.
Sweet little nature spot to walk and enjoy!

name: Andree Clark Bird Refuge
formatted_address: 34a Los Patos Way, Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text:
A hidden gem for bird enthusiasts and nature lovers alike, the Andree Clark Bird Refuge offers a peaceful escape just minutes from downtown Santa Barbara. This 42-acre saltwater marsh might not look like much from the road, but it's a vital stopover on the Pacific Flyway and home to over 200 species of birds.

The walking path around the lake provides scenic views and plenty of opportunities for wildlife spotting.

What I loved most was the tranquil atmosphere, especially in the early morning when the light is perfect for photography and the birds are most active. The benches scattered along the path offer perfect spots for quiet contemplation or bird watching.

This place is really lovely. It's in the middle of the city, but it has is so much diversity of bird life. I don't even consider myself a huge bird lover, and I went on a whim, but the sounds these birds were making, and seeing them wade or fly around was amazing. It feels like you are somewhere much more remote, even though the sound of traffic is constantly in your ear. You can walk on a path around the whole place and end up the beach to make it a combo trip, then there's a cute French restaurant where you can have a glass of wine to wind down the day.

Back in the 90s me and my friend Stewy camped on the island in the middle of the Bird Refuge. It was a cold night but definitely a blast! I recommend those seeking adventure to grab a paddle board and take it out to the island. Have fun and watch out for spiders!

Andrew Clark Bird Refuge is a peaceful place for a walk. If going off the paved path, bring shoes you don't mind getting dirty. Wildlife is flourishing and you might see a brush rabbit or two. Binoculars might be a good idea. No proper restrooms so go before you go. Please keep this place clean.

Was excited to come here and walk about. Did see several wayer bird species and turtles. But the smell was nauseating, maybe if i walked the other side i would have been upwind. Also a pair of really creepy guys lurking under a tree with their clothes hanging on a rope. Hope they weren't swimming in that murk! Anyway walked on and came to a locked fence. Was hoping to be able to hike around the lake but no luck. Had to backtrack. 😞  past the creepies who stared at me the whole time. Was so disappointed. Looks like at one time this was a lovely place.

name: Nature's Own Gallery
formatted_address: 217 Stearns Wharf Ste C, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text:
🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
A Hidden Gem on the Wharf!

Nature’s Own Gallery is such a unique and beautiful shop—it’s a must-visit if you’re strolling the Santa Barbara Wharf! The store is full of treasures from the natural world, from stunning crystals and polished stones to delicate shell art and even real megalodon fossils. You can tell everything is thoughtfully curated, and there’s something here for all ages.

I especially loved the jewelry—each piece felt one-of-a-kind, and many incorporate natural elements like raw stones or shells. It’s the kind of place where you’ll find something special, whether you’re a geology buff, shopping for a gift, or just love beautiful things.

The staff were friendly and knowledgeable, happy to answer questions about the items and their origins. Definitely one of my favorite stops in Santa Barbara. Highly recommend!

Good vibes only in this special sacred space 🔮🪷🌜🧚🏼🌊 love the jewelry and also got some great additions to my crystal collection. And a turquoise ring, so hard to pick just one! Love 💕 the energy and environment you’ve created

🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Absolutely magical shop!
Nature’s Own Gallery is hands-down one of the most beautiful and thoughtfully curated places I’ve ever stepped into. Every piece feels like it has a story—whether it’s a gemstone, a fossil, or a handcrafted piece of jewelry. You can feel the love and care that goes into everything here. It’s more than a store—it’s an experience. If you’re in Santa Barbara, don’t miss it!

I was looking for a few specific pieces to complete a display project. I’ve been looking for several weeks up and down the coast and remembered visiting the store several years ago, I had a great experience then.  If you’re in the market for high-quality stones and shells look no further! Their prices are very reasonable their selection is outstanding, and their staff knowledgeable polite and accommodating. Even though the shop Is usually very busy at midday, The staff was patient while I decided on the pieces that would work right. Bravo! It was an outstanding experience from top to bottom. I look forward to my next visit.
Installation Artist
Jaxon

A truly magical shop filled with beautiful gems, wonderful gifts and mementos. You can feel the good vibes and energy the second you walk in the door and there’s so much to explore. The friendly and knowledgeable shop owners and staff are so kind and helpful guiding  you through your visit to find the perfect item for you and/or someone special!
The location on Stearn’s wharf is also incredible !!!! Anything  you bring home will always remind you of this incredibly breathtaking spot in Santa Barbara! Don’t miss it!

name: Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History
formatted_address: 2559 Puesta Del Sol, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text:
I visited the Natural History Museum during summer break and bought our tickets, paying a little extra for the Butterfly Pavilion—it was a bit “extra,” but absolutely worth it. The first thing that caught my attention was the planetarium. Small, yes, but incredibly immersive! Even though some little ones weren’t enjoying the show and made a lot of noise, it was still amazing and almost changed how I view Earth and life.

After that, we headed to the Butterfly Pavilion. The line was short and easygoing, though it was pretty hot—some fans nearby would have been nice. Once inside, it was magical: butterflies were everywhere, and three even landed on me! It felt like being in a Disney movie. My visit was a little more expensive than expected, but the mix of the immersive planetarium and the enchanting butterflies made it fascinating, fun, and unforgettable.

The Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History is a great place to visit and bring your children to. Offers a good amount of history and information on mammals, birds, science, prehistoric animals, Chumash people and many, many more things to learn about. They have a beautiful butterfly garden and a live bird area. It’s truly a fun place to visit.

What an amazing Museum to visit we had no idea the history we would see with the largest whale skelton in the world.  There is an indoor area you can visit to check out the history and animals around Santa Barbara, learn about the Missions along the coast.  You can also walk around outside via the nature area and see some really cool dinosaurs and learn some history.

The staff are very friendly and the museum is well kept and clean. Fun exhibits in the museum part and it also has an outside backyard area that's great for kids. The butterflies are also out right now, and are very well maintained. It also has an observatory and planetarium where we got a pretty cool show.

The animatronic dinos were awesome my kids thought they were amazing. It was a highlight of our visit. Seeing Chad as we walked up was amazing to learn those are his real bones showing g how massive blue whales are. Definitely going to go back on a week day and check out the planetarium next time.

name: Dune Swale Pond
formatted_address: Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text:
This nice swale attracts many species of resident and migratory birds.   It is also home to several Western Pond Turtles. It is located on one of the many trails in the Coal Oil Point Reserve. It is managed by the University of California Santa Barbara and lies just outside the protected critical habitat of the Snowy Plover and near Sands Beach and Coal Oil Point.
There are several benches to sit and relax and enjoy the scenery. Do yourself a favor and explore this gem of a place.
This is a great pond. Also, there is a secret UCSB treasure chest underneath the bridge. It’s a must see.

name: Snowy Plover Critical Habitat
formatted_address: Slough Rd, Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: A beautiful area that's rather delicate.  Please read the signs and do not go beyond the fence. Don't be like me and walk blindly amongst the poor plovers.  A docent had to come up to me, poor girl was running, to tell me to get out of the area. Apologies!!!

From the moment I saw this place, I knew it was perfect for my orchard! After planting my green union's in the gravely sand, I laid back and had a bottle of wine (or 3). All these college kids laughed and pointed at me! Not my fault green onion husks create the best trench coats! After it began to dim, I started to go back to my Vespa when I suddenly got lost! The paths diverged, and I came upon a quaint pottery studio. A portly man came to greet me and tried to throw a vase at mean. Anyway, snowy plovers just love green onions.
--Buttery Von Vilbert Vaseline
Wish people would follow the beach rules to protect the snowy plovers
Nice spot to see plovers

name: Goleta Slough Ecological Reserve

google_reviews.text: Great for dogs, but in the summer it gets very hot because there is little shade. You can also link the trails here to the ones that go to IV, or in the other direction to the Butterfly Preserve and beaches.
Like I was back in time.

name: Sheffield Reservoir
formatted_address: 530 Mountain Dr #490, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text:
Good paved walk, flat for the first stretch and a very mild down/up after that. Great for kids or elders. Also some narrower dirt trails to mix it up. Directly adjacent to the firehouse demonstration garden.

Note the reservoir itself is underground.

This place is literally, my front yard, and has been for 50 years. When I was a kid, all my friends thought I had the biggest swimming pool in town! That was back when the reservoir was still ABOVE ground... now that it's all BELOW ground, in my opinion, it's the best (and biggest, dog park in town! My dog, Chester, the cutest golden retriever you'll ever meet, visits this park at least twice per week, and he can enjoy an off leash experience, due to the vast openness that's available, in a truly natural setting, without too much worry of invading other's experience.
Inspite of the lack of bathroom facilities, I still give it 5-stars! Get out and experience nature first hand, with its native foliage and birds alike, for yourself and shake off the urban stresses, by taking your own nature walk at Sheffield Reservoir. It'll do your mind, body and spirit a ton of good!
A maze of trails to enjoy with your dog! Lots of wildflowers.

Beautiful park to walk day or night. The only downside is even with the signs posted it appears no one knows how to read that dogs must be on leash. I understand the dogs that over and are sight and sound trained but even then you don't know how the other dog is going to react. Several times I've taken my German Shepard for a walk and had an incident because people don't know how to leash they're dogs. Other than that it's a beautiful park

Nice place for quick pretty walk with the dogs. Was hoping to avoid foxtails, instead there were those round things with little spikes on the path that kept getting into my dog's paws. Better during non sticker/foxtail season.  Could not extend the walk at this time due to stickers and burrs, darn.  All depends on the type of hair or fur your pup has.

Worst however were several semi-agressive dogs not under good control. We avoided any entanglements but would like to see other walkers with stressed dogs keep them on leash and practice training to help these canines be more relaxed.

name: North Campus Open Space

google_reviews.text: Beautiful natural reserve with lots of biodiversity! Many different loops and trails so you can spend as much or as little time as you’d like.
I LOVE this hike! The be nature preserve is beautiful, you're near the beach, parking is easy and it isn't over crowded.

Nice local walking area. The area is dedicated to cultivating local plants in this space to encourage local birds and wildlife to return and thrive. I live near this space, and it is a lot of fun to walk, jog, or bike in this area. It has a mix of dirt trail, sand trail, paved service roads, and wide gravel paths.
It’s a nice place for jogging and having a stroll. You can see ducks and some small animals here! This place is natural and environment friendly.
A wonderful place for bird watchers,  a wonderful sanctuary for wildlife and a serene place to visit and stroll through. There are several endangered species present. Make sure your dog is leashed.

name: Thunderbird Open Space

google_reviews.text: Simple open space in a quiet neighborhood. No playground, no bathroom, just trees around the perimeter and open grass area in the middle.

Exactly what it is, a nice sized open space for dogs or little kids sports. Speckled with an assortment of different trees for shade and soft patches of grass that are maintained by the city. No bathrooms or amenities, but good walking distance to food and a practical little gem of an open space for the neighborhood.
Grassy open space with trees. No bathrooms or playground, but nature play and open space for sports.
Nice open green space to lay in the grass or play with a dog.
Small but still a great place to relax

name: Goleta Monarch Butterfly Grove at Ellwood Mesa

google_reviews.text: Muddy trails from the rain 11/22/25, with some impassable sections. We only saw three monarchs, but I know there can be more later in the season (December-January). As always, spectacular views from the bluffs, with a little trail you can take down to tide pools. Dogs on leash allowed, and there is a poop bag dispenser at the trailhead. Dogs allowed on the beach, but please mind the restrictions for the snowy plover nesting season.
I saw a sign to visit Ellwood MESA in Goleta, where it was nice to have a walk and ride my bike around! Indeed, after waiting for a while during the nature trail, I saw some butterflies!

There were no butterflies 🦋 today but we had a great time walking around in the shaded areas and exploring the general area. It feels very residential until you start walking on these trails. It then becomes absolutely gorgeous.

It’s a beautiful little hike! Though don’t expect to see many butterflies as the population is sadly dwindling. There is parking just on the main road or in the neighborhood. The parking has a donation box. There is a guide explaining everything at the small clearing where the butterflies are.

- place easy to find

-people makes too much noise, please respect the butterfly’s home and. Be silence

name: Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park
formatted_address: CA-154, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park is a small but a special spot, with colorful rock art that feels surprisingly powerful when you see it up close. The road up is narrow and a bit of an adventure, but the quiet setting and ancient paintings make it absolutely worth the trip.

Five stars for the significance of this place to people from ancient times to the present and its location. The security is decent for what is trying to protect.

However, four to three stars are given to the contemporary human activities, which still make this place important, but it would be nice if people could honor the past and respect the current state of the cave. The petroglyphs created this year are in the form of names with plus signs, dates, and all surrounded by a roughly shaped heart.

Tiss the impulses of  contamporary beings that drive then to continue marking this site. Please be kind and resist the urge to carve a name or brush off the lichen and moss in order to leave a mark. I suspect that those who will read this are more aware of the historic value of this place and will leave things as they are.

Maybe having a ranger that charges for the visit or having a form of monitoring visitors ought to be set up to catch these modern guerrilla shamans.

All in all, it's worth visiting.

Native American archaeological sites deserve more respect than they get in California. I'm glad this exists, that it's reasonably protected, and the setting is quite beautiful. But there's only parking space for 2-3 cars on the roadside next to the site, and passing California drivers on a narrow road was a comedy of errors. On the downhill side of the road is a pretty creek with boulders when it's running.

I found it fascinating! The pictographs in the cave were awesome. You can't go in the cave, but rather look at it through metal mesh barrier.
The cave is 2 miles up a pretty steep winding road barely enough for two vehicles.
At the site there is parking for two vehicles top barely.
The site is clearly marked and just a short climb up a rock staircase.
The surrounding rock formations are awesome! And once you look inside the cave through the grating, you go WOW!
Is it worth it? Yes, it is just for the archeological aspect of it!

Very cool stop off a very cool road. The journey is more impressive than the destination if you don't really know what you're looking at. The drive may be stressful. Only a few spots to pull over to check it out. Easy to drive past (though there's a helpful sign to let you know you have).

name: More Mesa
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93110, USA

google_reviews.text: We have been to this area over the years when we are visiting Santa Barbara/Goleta.  It is an easy walk to the bluff and the views are spectacular.  It is also a great place for dogs to run free when not busy.

LOVED IT!!!!!! Absolutely STUNNING sunset from this vantage point!!!! It's definitely unstable as the cliffs are made of sand so be prepared and wear good shoes. The path leads from the top of the cliff all the way down to the beach and it was amazing!!!!! Definitely 100% recommend stopping by if you're in the area for incredible Cali views 💛

This place is beautiful. There are steps where you can get down to the beach. Oh there are nudist at the beach. Unfortunately I did not have enough time to walk to the other end. Will come back again if I am in Santa Barbara.

Large, wild natural beachfront park. Great spot for short 2-3 mi walk and/or run. Beach is often clothes optional to west of stairs. Harrier hawks, owls and swallows are common in park, plovers, sandpipers are common on beach. Fishing in kelp just off shore. Need to ride bike or park on Puente and walk in.
Olive approves of the trails at More Mesa. Great for dogs and their humans alike!

Great park to take young toddlers and older kids. Has two large  picnic benches and plenty of shade. The only reason for 4 stars is there are no bathrooms.

If you choose to visit this area please pick up after your dog, so many people who use this area as a dog exercise place do not. There also is no off-leash dog park in this area and despite the many people who treated as such it is not a good place for that.
Great for a nature walk along the creek. Nice playground for the kids as well.

name: Cachuma Lake Campground
formatted_address: 2225 CA-154, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: I have to say I was a little worried before arriving, some of the reviews stated that the grounds and bathrooms were disgusting. I was so pleasantly surprised with the cleanliness of both grounds and bathrooms. I really enjoyed our stay here. Hot showers clean restrooms, they have a  cleaner crew come every morning and clean the restrooms. They views were amazing and we had alot of privacy.

We come here as a family every year during the summer and we love. Some family stay in a cabin and the rest of us camp out in our tents. I love here because of the little amenities it has to offer like, coin hot showers, flush toilets, swimming pool, a small convenience store, and there bar and grill that serves amazing food and drinks not to mention the people there are so nice and hospitable. We have come to a few problems the last couple of years though, last year the pool was not in service and we weren't notified ahead of time, also normally they are on it so that people respect the quiet time last year not so much another issue was they let the restrooms stay dirty a whole weekend which was left pretty bad, this year we had a power outage and not once did the ranger come to talk to us or give us some kind of information on the situation, because of the power outage you are unable to use some of there amenities like shower, or the store, we drove 30 minutes out to the nearest store to get ice, and we drove over an hour out trying to find a place to shower, thankfully we decided to call around 6:30pm when they told us the power was back on. We never got a sorry or anything, it was very disappointing but it is what it is. This year's there were a lot of bugs so bring bug spray.

Had a great time camping at Lake Cachuma! Our site was clean and well maintained, and it was awesome to be able to have a campfire at night. We had some great luck fishing and the star watching was absolutely incredible. You can see so many stars out there away from the city lights.

The only things to be aware of are the market prices, which are a bit high, so it's a good idea to bring your own groceries. Also, it can get pretty dusty,, so be prepared for that. Overall, a really fantastic camping trip and we'll definitely be back!

Bathrooms were disgusting, doesn't anyone own a power washer? The campground is beautiful but sites are poorly maintained and rundown. A little creepy all around,  did not see any patrols or staff the entire time we were there. The only high note is that views of the lake are beautiful.

Our families come here every year, and we started off with a tent, and now my wife prefers the Trailer from Campos Campers.
This is the second year with the trailer, and now we have added a projector. The Park Ranger is always out, and about so there are no real issues. You can rent a boat we did that held 12 passengers and it ran about $65.00 per family. The General Store is a bit pricey but expected oh and the have gasoline $5.49 per gallon.
All together, not a bad spot for the family and friends.

name: Paradise Campground
formatted_address: 301 Paradise Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Great place. We camp out there during the 4th of July holiday.

I had a wonderful experience, it’s super quiet, and very basic. There are toilets( no sinks) which are immaculately clean, and water spigots throughout. I went for a swim in the river (Nov 2024) across the road, was about a 10 min walk to where it’s deep enough to soak/wash. There are tables/BBQs and fire pits at every site, plus loads of shade at outer sites as well. I LOVE this place, super helpful, friendly people that live there and I highly recommend it if you are an experienced camper, which means you have everything you need re showers etc. Oh and they also sell firewood there too. 💥❤️‼️🙏

Awesome campsite! It was beautiful surroundings and water to go swimming, especially if you're up to taking the hike out there
Is car accessible part of the way through so you don't have to hike out as far
Always a good time
Nice place to spend a few days. Sort of hidden but well worth the excursion. They have all the same amenities as a normal campground. Showers are coin operated , public bathrooms are clean daily.
Nice campground. Mix of tents and RVs, no hookups. Beautiful huge Oak trees provide great shade. We enjoyed #12. There is a tunnel under the road that leads to some trails that were nice to walk.

name: Los Prietos Campground
formatted_address: 111 Paradise Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text:
We hiked to this campground from ours using the back roads. It is on a steep hill. Some sites high up look more secluded but  be careful which you chose to make sure you are not sliding off your bed all night!
LOTS of trees so more shade and ability to use a hammock! Some sites are large so you can still use very large tents.
Beautiful campground. Great hosts. So much to explore.
Is ok... No Shower!  The ground is slope so make sure to set your tent correctly or you will roll off your blowup bed. Dried up river. There's a water spout but you can't hook up the water hose.

Stayed on site 28. Good distance between other sites. Plenty of room and dog friendly. They provide bathrooms and running water. I suggest bringing a bucket to fill water to take back to your site. The site has a fire pit and grill. They sell wood too which came in handy. Deer came pretty close to our site and we constantly heard turkeys. There is a hiking trail about half a mile from the campground called Snyder trail. It's 3.6 miles long but the views of the hike are amazing and totally worth it. Definitely suggest the hike while you are there. The hiking trail is also the only place I got reception.

Pros: Shady oaks (at most sites) and wild turkeys wander the campground. Near Cachuma Lake and Santa Ynez River. Some of the sites are nice and roomy.
CONS:
-Site #13 looks like a massive branch from the oak tree fell off and cracked the table in half. The table hasn't been repaired and the site is small and in full sun.
-Site #12 has no spigot (you have to access the spigot smack dab in the middle of Site #13 and it barely functions, the back of Site #11, or across the way...it's a little awkward walking through people's sites).
-The bathroom nearest Sites 11, 12 and 13 shares with a couple of other sites and one of the two toilets was out of order. This causes occasional lines. NO SINKS.
-The fire pit was halfway full of ash and hadn't been cleaned in between reservations.
-Poison oak is part of the natural habitat so keep a lookout. The back side of our site was full of it.

name: Live Oak Camp
formatted_address: 4600 CA-154, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Our Ski Club group uses this campground annually for one huge adult camping weekend! We always have a great time. The group site is large and flat. No desiginated sites. No hookups but some electrical outlets are available if you have a RV.
What a lovely campground!! So special and really nice facilities. Very accessible and peaceful. Cell service and WiFi are good there too if you don’t want to go totally off the grid.

This lovely site has plenty of camping and activity space under beautiful,  mature oak trees.  There is a large on site kitchen with walk in fridge and small freezer, which is directly next to the covered gathering area.  This area has a small stage,  many picnic tables,  and lights to use at night.  The bathrooms were upgraded a few years ago,  and now include plenty of flush toilets and sinks with mirrors in a well lit structure. The open air showers are still there,  now with slightly more roof.  Really,  although it's cold this time of year (dress in layers,  CA people!), it's really one of the most delightful event spaces in the Santa Barbara area.

Three day eclectic music festival benefiting  local public radio in Santa Barbara every father's  day weekend at live oak camp grounds called LIVE OAK. Great for families with children  including young adults. Really eclectic crowd also of all ages This is the thirtieth year. Come for a day or camp all three days you won't regret  it.
Great Group Camp area. Great facilities and close to Cachuma Lake, Wineries and Coldcreek Tavern.

name: Sage Hill Campground
formatted_address: Forest Rte 5N43, 3585 Paradise Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice trail, easy to walk. Did saw 2 large rattle snakes half the distance and desided not to cross their path and walked back. But it was nice and one day I will try to finish the whole round trip

If you can get some friends together to chip in for a group camping area, this is the way to do it. No partying going on until all hours, no screaming kids running through your site, no drunks to deal with. Unless the parties, kids, and drunks are members of your group, anyway.

This was the first time I've had a quiet night camping on Paradise Road unless it was during the winter with nobody else around. Wonderful!

Our camp host was awesome, the restrooms were kept clean and fully stocked, and it was a great experience all the way around.

Normally you have to cross over a stream to get to the campground, but at the moment it is bone dry. When there's enough water the stream is a great place for your kids to play.

This place used to be my Uncle Marvin Morris's ranch from 1956 to 1975. It was called Los Prietos. I'm 15 in the photo taken 60 years ago.

The girls are Carol Morris, my cousin, and her best friend, Leslie Stewart.
Beautiful spot in the Los padres. Nice group site. Host was awesome!

I camped here in June, and it was wonderful!  The weather was perfect.  The bathrooms were very clean, and a water tap was conveniently located within the group campground.  The hills were gorgeous, dotted with blooming yuccas and other plant life.  Unfortunately the river had dried out, but it was interesting to walk along the riverbed and check out the rocks.

name: Upper Oso Campground
formatted_address: 10-14 Paradise Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text: It is much more isolated than other campgrounds in the area and not along the side of the road. They also have an ATV road at the back of the campsite and a trail. Not sure if you are supposed to walk on the ATV trail but it has really pretty views of the mountains. The toilets were fine, for a campground they were pretty good! It got COLD at night, my car said it was 37 degrees! the stars were INCREDIBLE and I highly recommend going on a night with a new moon!
Great secluded spot, not many trees on sites but excellent camp hosts. There are many horse, hiking, and ATV trails nearby.

Great camping. Great hiking trails. Very courteous staff and host. Enjoyable scenery all around. Only reason for 4 stars is the bathrooms were a bit gross. 1 of them being locked, meaning almost always having to wait. If you go here, make sure to note there are no showers. We brought a Ozark trail two room shower tent with a portable shower and portable toilet so wasn't as inconvenient for us. Also, lots of ants all over. But hey, that's camping :)

Good: The mountain landscape, flush toilets (not too grimy), drinking water, hiking trails

BAD: Experience with camp host Bob! He pointed us twice in the wrong direction to set up our tent- the first time he came around AFTER we set up and said that area's reserved (ok we should have known, it had a grill and it's own parking spot). The second time, I asked him where was NOT reserved, and he pointed downhill to a patch of grass without amenities, so we walked down there and set up, he passed by and it seemed fine.

The next morning, I went to ask him if we could use a grill to boil water and he said we were lucky not to have gotten a fee overnight- WHAT? He told us the area we camped in was for day use only, and if the ranger had seen, we'd have gotten a ticket... So he chose not to tell us that until the next day? So I asked him where did he want us to go, and he SHRUGGED. I explained that we had gotten two day passes, and he said oh okay, that's fine then...

Except that 15 minutes later he came by and yelled "You people are illegally camping, I won't tell you again!"- so I have no idea what his problem was, and why he couldn't be clear with us as to where to go/what he wanted from us. He was so abrupt and talking with him was so frustrating we left immediately rather than struggle to get any kind of help!

Ensure you have a reservation so you don't have to deal with Bob!

Fire ripped through last year.  So it's dry and in some areas burnt. But it's a great place to checkout with trailheads for horses and dirt bikes.  Also great place to stay with horses because it has corals. The host name is Bob and he  was very helpful and nice. Restrooms need work

name: Fremont Campground
formatted_address: 2525 Paradise Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text:
Beautiful and clean campgrounds for the true outdoors man.

💰 Cost $40 per day or $80 for a double site

🚗 Parking free for 1 car and $10 per car per day

💧 No natural water here but there is nearby. There are spickets here but plan to bring your own water.

🌳 Plenty of Shade from the trees

🐍 Past reviews say rattlesnakes can often be found at the base of the trees

☣️Poison Oak is plentiful nearby but the grounds are cleared of any

🚻 Restrooms

🗑 Trash on-site

Reservations recommended. 2-day minimum online. Group sites available. Campgrounds easily booked out so schedule ahead.

On-site Manager, Sunny, was very friendly and familiar with the area.

Been planning this trip for a while and it did not disappoint.
Pros: beautiful area. Lots of wildlife, we saw wild turkeys and heard frogs and owls at night. Plenty of space in each site and the sites are spread out so you don't feel like you're on top of each other with your campsite neighbors. Lively enough but people are generally respectful of being quiet late at night. Dog friendly! Has picnic table, fire pit, bench, bbq and plenty of shade trees. Very close to things to do such as the beach, Solvang etc, camp hosts were friendly and made sure to clean each site between guests.

Cons: biggest complaint I have is that the restrooms were closed and the only available toilets were porta pottys. I know that's a covid thing but the porta pottys were absolutely rancid. I had to cover my face to keep the smell down and still physically gagged. No toilet paper either. Another thing and it's not really the campgrounds fault but it was a huge shock in temperature from the beach to the campsite. If I had to guess I'd say 20 degrees more hot. We set up the tent quickly and went back to the beach until evening. So just be aware. Keep in mind there's lots of poison oak to avoid and no cell reception. Not a big deal but just so you know.

The campground has a lot of nice tree coverage.  It's definitely a worn campground, but ok, if you just need a place to sleep. The table tops are not clean, so be sure to have wet wipes on hand. The bathrooms are portable potties and are fairly clean. At night is when the campground is best. It gets really dark and quiet about an hour after sunset. The morning rise is really nice for the only noise you hear are birds chirping away. There's a wild turkey that roams around and is pretty friendly.  For the cost of $40.00, I've stayed in nicer ones for less cost. But hey, it served the purpose.  I would recommend it and would stay there again to see the turkey.

I very much enjoyed it and so did my dogs. Make sure to reserve a spot online beforehand so that way you can get everything in order. The only problems were the bugs (but what can you expect?) and the walk from the car. Campground #11 has a far walk from literally everything and it’s uphill! At least it gave a good leg workout!

Camped here 4/11/2025. Beautiful setting right off Paradise Road. The host is nice. It wasn’t too buggy but there were lots of mosquitoes at dusk. Trash, water, and recycling were good. The campground was quiet. The host sells wood, $10 per bundle. The bathrooms are closed and porta potties are setup instead. The porta potties are clean but, still, there should be good serviceable bathrooms at all of the campgrounds along this road — I don’t think any of the campgrounds had useable restrooms with flush or pit toilets. Most sites are somewhat sloped — not the worst, not the best. Ample space between sites is nice. This part of the Santa Ynez Valley and river is very beautiful, especially in the spring. All-in-all a nice campground in a beautiful part of CA with facilities that have seen better days. Your tax dollars hard at work barely keeping the public facilities in working order!

name: Thousand Trails Rancho Oso
formatted_address: 3750 Paradise Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text:
This park is really beautiful, with a pool, jacuzzi, and large playground area, however, the administration failed to show competence in an extreme way.  Additionally, their "wi-fi" was on a low-range antenna at the club-house, not reaching even the closest RVs during my stay, and there is virtually 0 cell signal there.  I was able to use a cell signal booster to receive a tolerable cell signal, however, and of course, Starlink will likely work most of the time, though it is situated in a valley with a bridge passing over nearby.
The major issue I ran into was because I had auto-billing set up, and had them just auto-renewing my contract with them continually so that I could stay there indefinitely.  While this worked most of the time, at one point they lost my billing info somehow, and then, when I had guests who used their credit card to pay the gate fee, they charged my $700 rent to my guests credit card.  Needless to say, my friends, who did not have $700 to spare until the credit card would refund it, freaked out, and the park was not helpful when we contacted them about the error.  That being bad enough, several months later, the manager failed to set my lot to auto-renew, as it had the previous 6 months, and then contacted me on a Friday telling me I had something like 3 days to remove all of my possessions from the park.. I was out of town at the time, and if you've ever expanded a pop-out camper to live in indefinitely, you would know that it takes a good bit of preparation to be ready to relocate again.  I ended up leaving that park in an undue rush, with the manager indicating they couldn't resolve their error for me because they had booked the spot out to someone else.
It's hard not to give this location 1 star for these reasons, but it was quite beautiful and peaceful.. though the management operated as though their operation is part of the filming of "trailer park boys".

I was really looking forward to staying at this beautiful place, but unfortunately, my plans were disrupted. I had booked it for July 4th, but a month prior, I received a cancellation notice due to renovations. What's disappointing is that I was allowed to make the booking in the first place, despite the planned closure. As a result, my family and I will now have to spend our long weekend at home, which is really frustrating

Beautiful scenery and the unique opportunity to go horse back riding on a trail with a guide! Make sure to make reservations for your ride at least the day before. Laura and Alexis at the barn and stables were very friendly and knowledgeable. The horses were well taken care of and very well trained. I've never heard of campgrounds offering activities like horse back riding, so this campground really stands out. In the barn you can also go see other animals like goats, pigs, and bunnies if you're not keen on riding horses. Either way, I totally recommend the horse back riding experience and staying here! We had an amazing time and would love to visit again in the future ✨

It was kind of a first come first serve when it came to RV spots which was nice. Staff at the front gate immediately tried selling us a membership before we even pulled in. It was very very hot. Make your horse riding reservations before you even get there. It books up very fast. The pool. Where do I start. No one monitors the pool. Ever.  The rules are very clear at the pool. Common sense rules. Every day there was dogs, glass bottles, alcohol and food. I even heard about a near drowning of a 2 year old during the 4th of July weekend due to the mom being too under the influence to properly take care of her child. The pool was crowded and kids running. I was just waiting for someone to slip and fall. I spoke with the store employee and she said there wasn't enough staff to monitor the pool and if I saw a problem to go to the start of the park and call a ranger. She stated that the other thousand trails have someone watching the pool, but they don't have the staff. Even after talking to the store employee and making my concerns known on the first day we were there, I never once saw a ranger and we were there for 4 days. The worst. My husband took my 9 year old daughter to the bathroom. Waited outside and while she was in there a couple,a man and a young woman came out of the bathroom together. Maybe he was just changing or maybe something worse, I can't take that chance with my daughter. The bathroom next to us was not kept up either. 2 out of the 4 days it was not cleaned. It showed and was obvious. I can definitely say I'm glad we did not sign up for a membership and we will never go back to this site.

I really enjoyed my 4 days in the Western Cabin...it was clean and tidy, and conducive to curling up with a good book in the evenings!  The horses were well looked after by guide, Torrey, who was so friendly and pleasant.  The barn was "spit spot."  I would recommend this place to anyone looking for a retreat in mountains.

name: Montecito Peak
formatted_address: Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: I climbed the mountain thinking that I would sweat a bit before entering the hot spring.  Contrary to the crowdedness of the hot spring, it was quiet here and I barely passed three groups of hikers.  Expansive views of sea, sky and rocks.

Montecito Peak is a great peak to climb and offers amazing views of the California coastline from Santa Barbara to Ventura on a clear day. Best to hike down from E. Camino Cielo Road up on the ridgeline, and the hike down and back up is about 2.5 miles round trip. The views from the peak are amazing and well worth the steep ascent to reach at the end.
Great hike, strenuous long and quiet trail. Bring your hiking poles it helps a lot to have them.

Amazing Views!
The top of Montecito Peak is a great hiking destination for experienced hikers. From the Cold Spring Trailhead this is a 8 mile round trip trek, up moderate to difficult terrain. The majority of this hike is in direct sunlight, so bring lots of water and sunscreen. Depending on how fast you typically hike, this will take around 4 hours round trip.
If you make it to the top, the views are incredible! #hike #mountain #santabarbara #montecito #trail
Moderately challenging hike with ample shade in early morning. Last 100m is loose gravel and a little more strenious than the rest of the trail so be ready to finish strong. Amazing views.
